1:1.text: In the Name of Allah‚ÄĒthe Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

1:2.text: All praise is for Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Lord of everything in existence including angels, humans, and animals.

1:4.text: Master of the Day of Judgment.

1:5.text: You ňĻaloneňļ we worship and You ňĻaloneňļ we ask for help.

1:6.text: Guide us along the Straight Path,

1:7.text: the Path of those You have blessed‚ÄĒnot those You are displeased with, or those who are astray.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†It is customary to say ‚Äú√ām√ģn‚ÄĚ or ‚ÄúAmen‚ÄĚ at the end of this s√Ľrah.

10:1.text: Alif-L√£m-Ra. These are the verses of the Book, rich in wisdom.

10:2.text: Is it astonishing to people that We have sent revelation to a man from among themselves, ňĻinstructing him,ňļ ‚ÄúWarn humanity and give good news to the believers that they will have an honourable status with their Lord.‚ÄĚ? Yet the disbelievers said, ‚ÄúIndeed, this ňĻmanňļ is clearly a magician!‚ÄĚ

10:3.text: Surely your Lord is Allah Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then established Himself on the Throne, conducting every affair. None can intercede except by His permission. That is Allah‚ÄĒyour Lord, so worship Him ňĻaloneňļ. Will you not then be mindful?
footnotes: ¬†See footnote for 7:54.

10:4.text: To Him is your return all together. Allah‚Äôs promise is ňĻalwaysňļ true. Indeed, He originates the creation then resurrects it so that He may justly reward those who believe and do good. But those who disbelieve will have a boiling drink and a painful punishment for their disbelief.

10:5.text: He is the One Who made the sun a radiant source and the moon a reflected light, with precisely ordained phases, so that you may know the number of years and calculation ňĻof timeňļ. Allah did not create all this except for a purpose. He makes the signs clear for people of knowledge.

10:6.text: Surely in the alternation of the day and the night, and in all that Allah has created in the heavens and the earth, there are truly signs for those mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

10:7.text: Indeed, those who do not expect to meet Us, being pleased and content with this worldly life, and who are heedless of Our signs,

10:8.text: they will have the Fire as a home because of what they have committed.

10:9.text: Surely those who believe and do good, their Lord will guide them ňĻto Paradiseňļ through their faith, rivers will flow under their feet in the Gardens of Bliss,

10:10.text: in which their prayer will be, ‚ÄúGlory be to You, O ¬†Allah!‚ÄĚ and their greeting will be, ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ and their closing prayer will be, ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds!‚ÄĚ

10:11.text: If Allah were to hasten evil for people<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as they wish to hasten good, they would have certainly been doomed. But We leave those who do not expect to meet Us to wander blindly in their defiance.
footnotes: ¬†When they pray against themselves or their children, or when they demand to be destroyed.

10:12.text: Whenever someone is touched by hardship, they cry out to Us, whether lying on their side, sitting, or standing. But when We relieve their hardship, they return to their old ways as if they had never cried to Us to remove any hardship! This is how the misdeeds of the transgressors have been made appealing to them.

10:13.text: We surely destroyed ňĻotherňļ peoples before you when they did wrong, and their messengers came to them with clear proofs but they would not believe! This is how We reward the wicked people.

10:14.text: Then We made you their successors in the land to see how you would act.

10:15.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, those who do not expect to meet Us say ňĻto the Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúBring us a different Quran or make some changes in it.‚ÄĚ Say ňĻto themňļ, ‚ÄúIt is not for me to change it on my own. I only follow what is revealed to me. I fear, if I were to disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.‚ÄĚ

10:16.text: Say, ‚ÄúHad Allah willed, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you. I had lived my whole life among you before this ňĻrevelationňļ. Do you not understand?‚ÄĚ

10:17.text: Who does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah or deny His revelations? Indeed, the wicked will never succeed.

10:18.text: They worship besides Allah others who can neither harm nor benefit them, and say, ‚ÄúThese are our intercessors with Allah.‚ÄĚ Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúAre you informing Allah of something He does not know in the heavens or the earth? Glorified and Exalted is He above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!‚ÄĚ

10:19.text: Humanity was once nothing but a single community ňĻof believersňļ, but then they differed.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†their differences would have been settled ňĻat onceňļ.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they split into believers and disbelievers.
¬†That He will delay their judgment until the Hereafter.

10:20.text: They ask, ‚ÄúWhy has no ňĻotherňļ sign been sent down to him from his Lord?‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻThe knowledge ofňļ the unseen is with Allah alone. So wait! I too am waiting with you.‚ÄĚ

10:21.text: When We give people a taste of mercy after being afflicted with a hardship, they swiftly devise plots against Our revelations! Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah is swifter in devising ňĻpunishmentňļ. Surely Our messenger-angels record whatever you devise.‚ÄĚ

10:22.text: He is the One Who enables you to travel through land and sea. And it so happens that you are on ships, sailing with a favourable wind, to the passengers‚Äô delight. Suddenly, the ships are overcome by a gale wind and those on board are overwhelmed by waves from every side, and they assume they are doomed. They cry out to Allah ňĻaloneňļ in sincere devotion, ‚ÄúIf You save us from this, we will certainly be grateful.‚ÄĚ

10:23.text: But as soon as He rescues them, they transgress in the land unjustly. O ¬†humanity! Your transgression is only against your own souls. ňĻThere is onlyňļ brief enjoyment in this worldly life, then to Us is your return, and then We will inform you of what you used to do.

10:24.text: The life of this world is just like rain We send down from the sky, producing a mixture of plants which humans and animals consume. Then just as the earth looks its best, perfectly beautified, and its people think they have full control over it, there comes to it Our command by night or by day, so We mow it down as if it never flourished yesterday! This is how We make the signs clear for people who reflect.

10:25.text: And Allah invites ňĻallňļ to the Home of Peace and guides whoever He wills to the Straight Path.

10:26.text: Those who do good will have the finest reward<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and ňĻevenňļ more.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Neither gloom nor disgrace will cover their faces. It is they who will be the residents of Paradise. They will be there forever.
footnotes: ¬†Paradise.
¬†Seeing Almighty Allah in the Hereafter.

10:27.text: As for those who commit evil, the reward of an evil deed is its equivalent. Humiliation will cover them‚ÄĒwith no one to protect them from Allah‚ÄĒas if their faces were covered with patches of the night‚Äôs deep darkness. It is they who will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.

10:28.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day We will gather them all together then say to those who associated others ňĻwith Allah in worshipňļ, ‚ÄúStay in your places‚ÄĒyou and your associate-gods.‚ÄĚ We will separate them from each other, and their associate-gods will say, ‚ÄúIt was not us that you worshipped!

10:29.text: Allah is sufficient as a Witness between each of us that we were totally unaware of your worship.‚ÄĚ

10:30.text: Then and there every soul will face ňĻthe consequences ofňļ what it had done. They all will be returned to Allah‚ÄĒtheir True Master. And whatever ňĻgodsňļ they fabricated will fail them.

10:31.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWho provides for you from heaven and earth? Who owns ňĻyourňļ hearing and sight? Who brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living? And who conducts every affair?‚ÄĚ They will ňĻsurelyňļ say, ‚ÄúAllah.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúWill you not then fear ňĻHimňļ?

10:32.text: That is Allah‚ÄĒyour True Lord. So what is beyond the truth except falsehood? How can you then be turned away?‚ÄĚ

10:33.text: And so your Lord‚Äôs decree has been proven true against the rebellious‚ÄĒthat they will never believe.

10:34.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúCan any of your associate-gods originate creation and then resurrect it?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúňĻOnlyňļ Allah originates creation and then resurrects it. How can you then be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?‚ÄĚ

10:35.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúCan any of your associate-gods guide to the truth?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúňĻOnlyňļ Allah guides to the truth.‚ÄĚ Who then is more worthy to be followed: the One Who guides to the truth or those who cannot find the way unless guided? What is the matter with you? How do you judge?

10:36.text: Most of them follow nothing but ňĻinheritedňļ assumptions. ňĻAndňļ surely assumptions can in no way replace the truth. Allah is indeed All-Knowing of what they do.

10:37.text: It is not ňĻpossibleňļ for this Quran to have been produced by anyone other than Allah. In fact, it is a confirmation of what came before, and an explanation of the Scripture. It is, without a doubt, from the Lord of all worlds.

10:38.text: Or do they claim, ‚ÄúHe<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†made it up!‚ÄĚ? Tell them ňĻO Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúProduce one s√Ľrah¬†like it then, and seek help from whoever you can‚ÄĒother than Allah‚ÄĒif what you say is true!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

10:39.text: In fact, they ňĻhastilyňļ rejected the Book without comprehending it and before the fulfilment of its warnings. Similarly, those before them were in denial. See then what was the end of the wrongdoers!

10:40.text: Some of them will ňĻeventuallyňļ believe in it; others will not. And your Lord knows best the corruptors.

10:41.text: If they deny you, then say, ‚ÄúMy deeds are mine and your deeds are yours. You are free of what I do and I am free of what you do!‚ÄĚ

10:42.text: Some of them listen to what you say, but can you make the deaf hear even though they do not understand?

10:43.text: And some of them look at you, but can you guide the blind even though they cannot see?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This refers metaphorically to the pagans who fail to see and hear the truth.

10:44.text: Indeed, Allah does not wrong people in the least, but it is people who wrong themselves.

10:45.text: On the Day He will gather them, it will be as if they had not stayed ňĻin the worldňļ except for an hour of a day,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻas though they were onlyňļ getting to know one another. Lost indeed will be those who denied the meeting with Allah, and were not ňĻrightlyňļ guided!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., only for a short period of time.

10:46.text: Whether We show you ňĻO Prophetňļ some of what We threaten them with, or cause you to die ňĻbefore thatňļ, to Us is their return and Allah is a Witness over what they do.

10:47.text: And for every community there is a messenger. After their messenger has come,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†judgment is passed on them in all fairness, and they are not wronged.
footnotes: ¬†Either in this life to deliver the message or on the Day of Judgment to testify for or against them.

10:48.text: They ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

10:49.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI have no power to benefit or protect myself, except by the Will of Allah.‚ÄĚ For each community there is an appointed term. When their time arrives, they cannot delay it for a moment, nor could they advance it.

10:50.text: Tell them ňĻO Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúImagine if His torment were to overcome you by night or day‚ÄĒdo the wicked realize what they are ňĻreallyňļ asking Him to hasten?

10:51.text: Will you believe in it only after it has overtaken you? Now? But you always wanted to hasten it!‚ÄĚ

10:52.text: Then the wrongdoers will be told, ‚ÄúTaste the torment of eternity! Are you not rewarded except for what you used to commit?‚ÄĚ

10:53.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúIs this true?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúYes, by my Lord! Most certainly it is true! And you will have no escape.‚ÄĚ

10:54.text: If every wrongdoer were to possess everything in the world, they would surely ransom themselves with it. They will hide ňĻtheirňļ remorse when they see the torment. And they will be judged in all fairness, and none will be wronged.

10:55.text: Surely to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Surely Allah‚Äôs promise is ňĻalwaysňļ true, but most of them do not know.

10:56.text: He ňĻis the One Whoňļ gives life and causes death, and to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

10:57.text: O humanity! Indeed, there has come to you a warning from your Lord, a cure for what is in the hearts, a guide, and a mercy for the believers.

10:58.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIn Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy let them rejoice. That is far better than whatever ňĻwealthňļ they amass.‚ÄĚ

10:59.text: Ask ňĻthe pagans, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúHave you seen that which Allah has sent down for you as a provision, of which you have made some lawful and some unlawful?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ‚ÄúHas Allah given you authorization, or are you fabricating lies against Allah?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to 6:138-139.

10:60.text: What do those who fabricate lies against Allah expect on Judgment Day? Surely Allah is ever Bountiful to humanity,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but most of them are ungrateful.
footnotes: ¬†By delaying their punishment and allowing them time to repent.

10:61.text: There is no activity you may be engaged in ňĻO Prophetňļ or portion of the Quran you may be reciting, nor any deed you ňĻallňļ may be doing except that We are a Witness over you while doing it. Not ňĻevenňļ an atom‚Äôs weight is hidden from your Lord on earth or in heaven; nor anything smaller or larger than that, but is ňĻwrittenňļ in a perfect Record.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 6:38.

10:62.text: There will certainly be no fear for the close servants of Allah, nor will they grieve.

10:63.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who are faithful and are mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

10:64.text: For them is good news in this worldly life and the Hereafter. There is no change in the promise of Allah. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.

10:65.text: Do not let their words grieve you ňĻO Prophetňļ. Surely all honour and power belongs to Allah. He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

10:66.text: Certainly to Allah ňĻaloneňļ belong all those in the heavens and all those on the earth. And what do those who associate others with Allah really follow? They follow nothing but assumptions and do nothing but lie.

10:67.text: He is the One Who has made the night for you to rest in and the day bright. Surely in this are signs for people who listen.

10:68.text: They<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†say, ‚ÄúAllah has offspring.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Glory be to Him! He is the Self-Sufficient. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. You have no proof of this! Do you say about Allah what you do not know?
footnotes: ¬†The Christians, pagans, etc.
¬†Jesus in Christianity, the angels in pagan Arab mythology, etc.

10:69.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIndeed, those who fabricate lies against Allah will never succeed.‚ÄĚ

10:70.text: ňĻIt is onlyňļ a brief enjoyment in this world, then to Us is their return, then We will make them taste the severe punishment for their disbelief.

10:71.text: Relate to them ňĻO Prophetňļ the story of Noah when he said to his people, ‚ÄúO my People! If my presence and my reminders to you of Allah‚Äôs signs are unbearable to you, then ňĻknow thatňļ I have put my trust in Allah. So devise a plot along with your associate-gods‚ÄĒand you do not have to be secretive about your plot‚ÄĒthen carry it out against me without delay!

10:72.text: And if you turn away, ňĻrememberňļ I have never demanded a reward from you ňĻfor delivering the messageňļ. My reward is only from Allah. And I have been commanded to be one of those who submit ňĻto Allahňļ.‚ÄĚ

10:73.text: But they still rejected him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark and made them successors, and drowned those who rejected Our signs. See then what was the end of those who had been warned!

10:74.text: Then after him We sent ňĻotherňļ messengers to their ňĻownňļ people and they came to them with clear proofs. But they would not believe in what they had rejected before. This is how We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

10:75.text: Then after these ňĻmessengersňļ We sent Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his chiefs with Our signs. But they behaved arrogantly and were a wicked people.

10:76.text: When the truth came to them from Us, they said, ‚ÄúThis is certainly pure magic!‚ÄĚ

10:77.text: Moses responded, ‚ÄúIs this what you say about the truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? Magicians will never succeed.‚ÄĚ

10:78.text: They argued, ‚ÄúHave you come to turn us away from the faith of our forefathers so that the two of you may become supreme in the land? We will never believe in you!‚ÄĚ

10:79.text: Pharaoh demanded, ‚ÄúBring me every skilled magician.‚ÄĚ

10:80.text: When the magicians came, Moses said to them, ‚ÄúCast whatever you wish to cast!‚ÄĚ

10:81.text: When they did, Moses said, ‚ÄúWhat you have produced is mere magic, Allah will surely make it useless, for Allah certainly does not set right the work of the corruptors.

10:82.text: And Allah establishes the truth by His Words‚ÄĒeven to the dismay of the wicked.‚ÄĚ

10:83.text: But no one believed in Moses except a few youths of his people, while fearing that Pharaoh and their own chiefs might persecute them. And certainly Pharaoh was a tyrant in the land, and he was truly a transgressor.

10:84.text: Moses said, ‚ÄúO my people! If you do believe in Allah and submit ňĻto His Willňļ, then put your trust in Him.‚ÄĚ

10:85.text: They replied, ‚ÄúIn Allah we trust. Our Lord! Do not subject us to the persecution of the oppressive people,

10:86.text: and deliver us by Your mercy from the disbelieving people.‚ÄĚ

10:87.text: We revealed to Moses and his brother, ‚ÄúAppoint houses for your people in Egypt. Turn these houses into places of worship, establish prayer, and give good news to the believers!‚ÄĚ

10:88.text: Moses prayed, ‚ÄúOur Lord! You have granted Pharaoh and his chiefs luxuries and riches in this worldly life, ňĻwhich they abusedňļ to lead people astray from Your Way! Our Lord, destroy their riches and harden their hearts so that they will not believe until they see the painful punishment.‚ÄĚ

10:89.text: Allah responded ňĻto Moses and Aaronňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúYour prayer is answered! So be steadfast and do not follow the way of those who do not know.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Moses prayed while Aaron said, ‚ÄúAmen.‚ÄĚ So it is as if both of them prayed.

10:90.text: We brought the Children of Israel across the sea. Then Pharaoh and his soldiers pursued them unjustly and oppressively. But as Pharaoh was drowning, he cried out, ‚ÄúI believe that there is no god except that in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am ňĻnowňļ one of those who submit.‚ÄĚ

10:91.text: ňĻHe was told,ňļ ‚ÄúNow ňĻyou believeňļ? But you always disobeyed and were one of the corruptors.

10:92.text: Today We will preserve your corpse so that you may become an example<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for those who come after you. And surely most people are heedless of Our examples!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., sign.

10:93.text: Indeed, We settled the Children of Israel in a blessed land,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and granted them good, lawful provisions. They did not differ until knowledge came to them.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their differences.
footnotes: ¬†In Egypt, Jerusalem, and other places.
¬†They did not split into believers and disbelievers until they received the knowledge given to their prophets.

10:94.text: If you ňĻO Prophetňļ are in doubt about ňĻthese storiesňļ that We have revealed to you, then ask those who read the Scripture before you. The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord, so do not be one of those who doubt,

10:95.text: and do not be one of those who deny Allah‚Äôs signs or you will be one of the losers.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†When these verses were revealed, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) responded, ‚ÄúI am not in doubt and I will not ask ňĻanyoneňļ.‚ÄĚ

10:96.text: Indeed, those against whom your Lord‚Äôs decree ňĻof tormentňļ is justified will not believe‚ÄĒ

10:97.text: even if every sign were to come to them‚ÄĒuntil they see the painful punishment.

10:98.text: If only there had been a society which believed ňĻbefore seeing the tormentňļ and, therefore, benefited from its belief, like the people of Jonah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†When they believed, We lifted from them the torment of disgrace in this world and allowed them enjoyment for a while.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Initially, the people of Jonah rejected his message. When he was told they would be punished after three days for their defiance, he left his city without Allah‚Äôs permission before its destruction. Jonah‚Äôs people became convinced that they were going to be destroyed when he left them and they saw signs of the imminent torment, so they felt remorseful and cried out for forgiveness before the coming of the punishment. Therefore, Allah accepted their repentance and the torment was retracted.
¬†i.e., until the end of their term.

10:99.text: Had your Lord so willed ňĻO Prophetňļ, all ňĻpeopleňļ on earth would have certainly believed, every single one of them! Would you then force people to become believers?

10:100.text: It is not for any soul to believe except by Allah‚Äôs leave, and He will bring His wrath upon those who are unmindful.

10:101.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúConsider all that is in the heavens and the earth!‚ÄĚ Yet neither signs nor warners are of any benefit to those who refuse to believe.

10:102.text: Are they waiting for ňĻanythingňļ except the same torments that befell those before them? Say, ‚ÄúKeep waiting then! I too am waiting with you.‚ÄĚ

10:103.text: Then We saved Our messengers and those who believed. For it is Our duty to save the believers.

10:104.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO humanity! If you are in doubt of my faith, then ňĻknow thatňļ I do not worship those ňĻidolsňļ you worship instead of Allah. But I worship Allah, Who has the power to cause your death. And I have been commanded, ‚ÄėBe one of the believers,‚Äô

10:105.text: and, ‚ÄėBe steadfast in faith in all uprightness, and do not be one of the polytheists,‚Äô

10:106.text: and ‚ÄėDo not invoke, instead of Allah, what can neither benefit nor harm you‚ÄĒfor if you do, then you will certainly be one of the wrongdoers,‚Äô

10:107.text: and ‚ÄėIf Allah touches you with harm, none can undo it except Him. And if He intends good for you, none can withhold His bounty. He grants it to whoever He wills of His servants. And He is the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

10:108.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO humanity! The truth has surely come to you from your Lord. So whoever chooses to be guided, it is only for their own good. And whoever chooses to stray, it is only to their own loss. And I am not a keeper over you.‚ÄĚ

10:109.text: And follow what is revealed to you, and be patient until Allah passes His judgment. For He is the Best of Judges.

100:2.text: striking sparks of fire ňĻwith their hoofsňļ,

100:5.text: and penetrating into the heart of enemy lines!

100:6.text: Surely humankind is ungrateful to their Lord‚ÄĒ

100:7.text: and they certainly attest to this‚ÄĒ

100:8.text: and they are truly extreme in their love of ňĻworldlyňļ gains.

100:9.text: Do they not know that when the contents of the graves will be spilled out,

100:10.text: and the secrets of the hearts will be laid bare‚ÄĒ

100:11.text: surely their Lord is All-Aware of them on that Day.

101:3.text: And what will make you realize what the Striking Disaster is?

101:4.text: ňĻIt isňļ the Day people will be like scattered moths,

101:5.text: and the mountains will be like carded wool.

101:6.text: So as for those whose scale is heavy ňĻwith good deedsňļ,

101:7.text: they will be in a life of bliss.

101:8.text: And as for those whose scale is light,

101:9.text: their home will be the abyss.

101:10.text: And what will make you realize what that is?

102:1.text: Competition for more ňĻgainsňļ diverts you ňĻfrom Allahňļ,

102:2.text: until you end up in ňĻyourňļ graves.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., visited the graves, since the stay in the grave is brief, followed by eternal life either in Paradise or Hell.

102:3.text: But no! You will soon come to know.

102:4.text: Again, no! You will soon come to know.

102:5.text: Indeed, if you were to know ňĻyour fateňļ with certainty, ňĻyou would have acted differentlyňļ.

102:6.text: ňĻButňļ you will surely see the Hellfire.

102:7.text: Again, you will surely see it with the eye of certainty.

102:8.text: Then, on that Day, you will definitely be questioned about ňĻyour worldlyňļ pleasures.

103:2.text: Surely humanity is in ňĻgraveňļ loss,

104:2.text: who amasses wealth ňĻgreedilyňļ and counts it ňĻrepeatedlyňļ,

105:1.text: Have you not seen ňĻO Prophetňļ how your Lord dealt with the Army of the Elephant?

105:4.text: that pelted them with stones of baked clay,

106:2.text: secure in their trading caravan ňĻto Yemenňļ in the winter and ňĻSyriaňļ in the summer‚ÄĒ

footnotes: ¬†Blessing the people of Quraysh with provision and security is probably the answer to Abraham‚Äôs prayer in 14:35-37.

107:1.text: Have you seen the one who denies the ňĻfinalňļ Judgment?

107:2.text: That is the one who repulses the orphan,

107:3.text: and does not encourage the feeding of the poor.

107:4.text: So woe to those ňĻhypocritesňļ who pray

107:5.text: yet are unmindful of their prayers;

107:7.text: and refuse to give ňĻeven the simplestňļ aid.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†They refuse to help others with small things like salt or water, let alone alms-tax.

108:1.text: Indeed, We have granted you ňĻO Prophetňļ abundant goodness.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This includes a heavenly river as well other favours in both worlds.

108:2.text: So pray and sacrifice to your Lord ňĻaloneňļ.

108:3.text: Only the one who hates you is truly cut off ňĻfrom any goodnessňļ.

109:2.text: I do not worship what you worship,

109:3.text: nor do you worship what I worship.

109:4.text: I will never worship what you worship,

109:5.text: nor will you ever worship what I worship.

11:1.text: Alif-L√£m-Ra. ňĻThis isňļ a Book whose verses are well perfected and then fully explained. ňĻIt isňļ from the One ňĻWho isňļ All-Wise, All-Aware.

11:2.text: ňĻTell them, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWorship none but Allah. Surely I am a warner and deliverer of good news to you from Him.

11:3.text: And seek your Lord‚Äôs forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance. He will grant you a good provision for an appointed term and graciously reward the doers of good. But if you turn away, then I truly fear for you the torment of a formidable Day.

11:4.text: To Allah is your return. And He is Most Capable of everything.‚ÄĚ

11:5.text: Indeed, they<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†enfold ňĻwhat is inňļ their hearts, ňĻtryingňļ to hide it from Him! But even when they cover themselves with their clothes, He knows what they conceal and what they reveal. Surely He knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.
footnotes: ¬†The hypocrites or the disbelievers.

11:6.text: There is no moving creature on earth whose provision is not guaranteed by Allah. And He knows where it lives and where it is laid to rest. All is ňĻwrittenňļ in a perfect Record.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 10:61.

11:7.text: He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒand His Throne was upon the waters‚ÄĒin order to test which of you is best in deeds. And if you ňĻO Prophetňļ say, ‚ÄúSurely you will ňĻallňļ be raised up after death,‚ÄĚ the disbelievers will certainly say, ‚ÄúThat is nothing but pure magic!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:54.

11:8.text: And if We delay their punishment until an appointed time, they will definitely say, ‚ÄúWhat is holding it back?‚ÄĚ Indeed, on the Day it overtakes them, it will not be averted from them, and they will be overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.

11:9.text: If We give people a taste of Our mercy then take it away from them, they become utterly desperate, ungrateful.

11:10.text: But if We give them a taste of prosperity after being touched with adversity, they say, ‚ÄúMy ills are gone,‚ÄĚ and become totally prideful and boastful,

11:11.text: except those who patiently endure and do good. It is they who will have forgiveness and a mighty reward.

11:12.text: Perhaps you ňĻO Prophetňļ may wish to give up some of what is revealed to you and may be distressed by it because they say, ‚ÄúIf only a treasure had been sent down to him, or an angel had come with him!‚ÄĚ You are only a warner, and Allah is the Trustee of All Affairs.

11:13.text: Or do they say, ‚ÄúHe<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†has fabricated this ňĻQuranňļ!‚ÄĚ? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúProduce ten fabricated s√Ľrahs like it and seek help from whoever you can‚ÄĒother than Allah‚ÄĒif what you say is true!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

11:14.text: But if your helpers fail you, then know that it has been revealed with the knowledge of Allah, and that there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him! Will you ňĻnotňļ then submit ňĻto Allahňļ?

11:15.text: Whoever desires ňĻonlyňļ this worldly life and its luxuries, We will pay them in full for their deeds in this life‚ÄĒnothing will be left out.

11:16.text: It is they who will have nothing in the Hereafter except the Fire. Their efforts in this life will be fruitless and their deeds will be useless.

11:17.text: ňĻCan these people be compared toňļ those ňĻbelieversňļ who stand on clear proof from their Lord, backed by ňĻthe Quran asňļ a witness from Him, and preceded by the Book of Moses ňĻwhich was revealedňļ as a guide and mercy? It is those ňĻbelieversňļ who have faith in it. But whoever from the ňĻdisbelievingňļ groups¬†rejects it, the Fire will be their destiny. So do not be in doubt of it. It is certainly the truth from your Lord, but most people do not believe.

11:18.text: Who does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah? They will be brought before their Lord, and the witnesses<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†will say, ‚ÄúThese are the ones who lied against their Lord.‚ÄĚ Surely Allah‚Äôs condemnation is upon the wrongdoers,
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the angels and the prophets.

11:19.text: who hinder ňĻothersňļ from Allah‚Äôs Path, striving to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked, and disbelieve in the Hereafter.

11:20.text: They will never frustrate Allah on earth, and they will have no protector besides Allah. Their punishment will be multiplied, for they failed to hear or see ňĻthe truthňļ.

11:21.text: It is they who have ruined themselves, and whatever ňĻgodsňļ they fabricated will fail them.

11:22.text: Without a doubt, they will be the worst losers in the Hereafter.

11:23.text: Surely those who believe, do good, and humble themselves before their Lord will be the residents of Paradise. They will be there forever.

11:24.text: The example of these two parties is that of the blind and the deaf, compared to the seeing and the hearing. Can the two be equal? Will you not then be mindful?

11:25.text: Surely We sent Noah to his people. ňĻHe said,ňļ ‚ÄúIndeed, I am sent to you with a clear warning

11:26.text: that you should worship none but Allah. I truly fear for you the torment of a painful Day.‚ÄĚ

11:27.text: The disbelieving chiefs of his people said, ‚ÄúWe see you only as a human being like ourselves, and we see that no one follows you except the lowliest among us, who do so ňĻhastilyňļ without thinking.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We do not see anything that makes ňĻall ofňļ you any better than us. In fact, we think you are liars.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Typically, the poor and the helpless are the first to believe in prophets, taking them as saviours, whereas the rich and powerful are hesitant to believe, perceiving the new faith as a threat to their influence.

11:28.text: He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Consider if I stand on a clear proof from my Lord and He has blessed me with a mercy from Himself,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†which you fail to see. Should we ňĻthenňļ force it on you against your will?
footnotes: ¬†Mercy here means prophethood.

11:29.text: O ¬†my people! I do not ask you for a payment for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from Allah. And I will never dismiss the believers, for they will surely meet their Lord. But I can see that you are a people acting ignorantly.

11:30.text: O ¬†my people! Who would protect me from Allah if I were to dismiss them? Will you not then be mindful?

11:31.text: I do not say to you that I possess Allah‚Äôs treasuries or know the unseen, nor do I claim to be an angel, nor do I say that Allah will never grant goodness to those you look down upon. Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ within them. ňĻIf I did,ňļ then I would truly be one of the wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

11:32.text: They protested, ‚ÄúO Noah! You have argued with us far too much, so bring upon us what you threaten us with, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

11:33.text: He responded, ‚ÄúIt is Allah Who can bring it upon you if He wills, and then you will have no escape!

11:34.text: My advice will not benefit you‚ÄĒno matter how hard I try‚ÄĒif Allah wills ňĻforňļ you to stray. He is your Lord, and to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.‚ÄĚ

11:35.text: Or do they say, ‚ÄúHe<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†has fabricated this ňĻQuranňļ!‚ÄĚ? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf I have done so, then I bear the burden of that sin! But I am free from your sinful accusation.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

11:36.text: And it was revealed to Noah, ‚ÄúNone of your people will believe except those who already have. So do not be distressed by what they have been doing.

11:37.text: And build the Ark under Our ňĻwatchfulňļ Eyes and directions, and do not plead with Me for those who have done wrong, for they will surely be drowned.‚ÄĚ

11:38.text: So he began to build the Ark, and whenever some of the chiefs of his people passed by, they mocked him. He said, ‚ÄúIf you laugh at us, we will ňĻsoonňļ laugh at you similarly.

11:39.text: You will soon come to know who will be visited by a humiliating torment ňĻin this lifeňļ and overwhelmed by an everlasting punishment ňĻin the nextňļ.‚ÄĚ

11:40.text: And when Our command came and the oven burst ňĻwith waterňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We said ňĻto Noahňļ, ‚ÄúTake into the Ark a pair from every species along with your family‚ÄĒexcept those against whom the decree ňĻto drownňļ has already been passed‚ÄĒand those who believe.‚ÄĚ But none believed with him except for a few.
footnotes: ¬†Noah was given a signal that once the water gushes out of a particular oven then the Flood is about to begin. It can also be translated as: ‚ÄúThe fountains of the earth gushed forth.‚ÄĚ

11:41.text: And he said, ‚ÄúBoard it! In the Name of Allah it will sail and cast anchor. Surely my Lord is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ

11:42.text: And ňĻsoňļ the Ark sailed with them through waves like mountains. Noah called out to his son, who stood apart, ‚ÄúO my dear son! Come aboard with us and do not be with the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ

11:43.text: He replied, ‚ÄúI will take refuge on a mountain, which will protect me from the water.‚ÄĚ Noah cried, ‚ÄúToday no one is protected from Allah‚Äôs decree except those to whom He shows mercy!‚ÄĚ And the waves came between them, and his son was among the drowned.

11:44.text: And it was said, ‚ÄúO earth! Swallow up your water. And O ¬†sky! Withhold ňĻyour rainňļ.‚ÄĚ The floodwater receded and the decree was carried out. The Ark rested on Mount Judi, and it was said, ‚ÄúAway with the wrongdoing people!‚ÄĚ

11:45.text: Noah called out to his Lord, saying, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Certainly my son is ňĻalsoňļ of my family, Your promise is surely true, and You are the most just of all judges!‚ÄĚ

11:46.text: Allah replied, ‚ÄúO Noah! He is certainly not of your family‚ÄĒhe was entirely of unrighteous conduct. So do not ask Me about what you have no knowledge of! I warn you so you do not fall into ignorance.‚ÄĚ

11:47.text: Noah pleaded, ‚ÄúMy Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking You about what I have no knowledge of, and unless You forgive me and have mercy on me, I will be one of the losers.‚ÄĚ

11:48.text: It was said, ‚ÄúO Noah! Disembark with Our peace and blessings on you and some of the descendants of those with you. As for the others, We will allow them ňĻa briefňļ enjoyment, then they will be touched with a painful punishment from Us.‚ÄĚ

11:49.text: This is one of the stories of the unseen, which we reveal to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. Neither you nor your people knew it before this. So be patient! Surely the ultimate outcome belongs ňĻonlyňļ to the righteous.

11:50.text: And to the people of ‚Äô√ād We sent their brother H√Ľd. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. You do nothing but fabricate lies ňĻagainst Allahňļ.

11:51.text: O ¬†my people! I do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from the One Who created me. Will you not then understand?

11:52.text: And O ¬†my people! Seek your Lord‚Äôs forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance. He will shower you with rain in abundance, and add strength to your strength. So do not turn away, persisting in wickedness.‚ÄĚ

11:53.text: They argued, ‚ÄúO H√Ľd! You have not given us any clear proof, and we will never abandon our gods upon your word, nor will we believe in you.

11:54.text: All we can say is that some of our gods have possessed you with evil.‚ÄĚ He said, ‚ÄúI call Allah to witness, and you too bear witness, that I ňĻtotallyňļ reject whatever you associate

11:55.text: with Him ňĻin worshipňļ. So let all of you plot against me without delay!

11:56.text: I have put my trust in Allah‚ÄĒmy Lord and your Lord. There is no living creature that is not completely under His control.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely my Lord‚Äôs Way is perfect justice.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., there is no moving creature that He does not hold by its forelock.
¬†lit., my Lord is on the Straight Path.

11:57.text: But if you turn away, I have already delivered to you what I have been sent with. My Lord will replace you with others. You are not harming Him in the least. Indeed, my Lord is a ňĻvigilantňļ Keeper over all things.‚ÄĚ

11:58.text: When Our command came, We rescued H√Ľd and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us, saving them from a harsh torment.

11:59.text: That was ‚Äô√ād. They denied the signs of their Lord, disobeyed His messengers, and followed the command of every stubborn tyrant.

11:60.text: They were followed by a curse in this world, as they will be on the Day of Judgment. Surely ‚Äô√ād denied their Lord. So away with ‚Äô√ād, the people of H√Ľd.

11:61.text: And to the people of Tham√Ľd We sent their brother ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. He ňĻis the One Whoňļ produced you from the earth and settled you on it. So seek His forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance. Surely my Lord is Ever Near, All-Responsive ňĻto prayersňļ.‚ÄĚ

11:62.text: They argued, ‚ÄúO ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•! We truly had high hopes in you before this.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†How dare you forbid us to worship what our forefathers had worshipped? We are certainly in alarming doubt about what you are inviting us to.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†They believed that ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• had the potential to be their future leader.¬†

11:63.text: He responded, ‚ÄúO my people! Consider if I stand on a clear proof from my Lord and He has blessed me with a mercy from Him. Who could help me against Allah if I were to disobey Him? You would only contribute to my doom.

11:64.text: And O ¬†my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So leave her to graze ňĻfreelyňļ on Allah‚Äôs earth and do her no harm, or a swift punishment will overtake you!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The she-camel came out of a mountain as a sign for them.

11:65.text: But they killed her, so he warned ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúYou have ňĻonlyňļ three ňĻmoreňļ days to enjoy life in your homes‚ÄĒthis is an unfailing promise!‚ÄĚ

11:66.text: When Our command came, We saved ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us and spared them the disgrace of that Day. Surely your Lord ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Powerful, Almighty.

11:67.text: And the ňĻmightyňļ blast overtook the wrongdoers, so they fell lifeless in their homes,

11:68.text: as if they had never lived there. Surely Tham√Ľd denied their Lord, so away with Tham√Ľd!

11:69.text: And surely Our messenger-angels came to Abraham with good news ňĻof a sonňļ. They greeted ňĻhim withňļ, ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ And he replied, ‚ÄúPeace ňĻbe upon youňļ!‚ÄĚ Then it was not long before he brought ňĻthemňļ a ňĻfat,ňļ roasted calf.

11:70.text: And when he saw that their hands did not reach for the food, he became suspicious and fearful of them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They reassured ňĻhimňļ, ‚ÄúDo not be afraid! We are ňĻangelsňļ sent ňĻonlyňļ against the people of Lot.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†In ancient Middle Eastern culture, if a guest refused to eat the food provided by their host, it was a sign of ill-will.

11:71.text: And his wife was standing by, so she laughed,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then We gave her good news of ňĻthe birth ofňļ Isaac, and, after him, Jacob.
footnotes: ¬†She laughed after her husband was assured that the guests intended no harm or when she heard the news of the imminent destruction of the sinful people of Lot.

11:72.text: She wondered, ‚ÄúOh, my! How can I have a child in this old age, and my husband here is an old man? This is truly an astonishing thing!‚ÄĚ

11:73.text: They responded, ‚ÄúAre you astonished by Allah‚Äôs decree? May Allah‚Äôs mercy and blessings be upon you, O ¬†people of this house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy, All-Glorious.‚ÄĚ

11:74.text: Then after the fear had left Abraham, and the good news had reached him, he began to plead with Us for the people of Lot.

11:75.text: Truly, Abraham was forbearing, tender-hearted, and ever turning ňĻto his Lordňļ.

11:76.text: ňĻThe angels said,ňļ ‚ÄúO Abraham! Plead no more! Your Lord‚Äôs decree has already come, and they will certainly be afflicted with a punishment that cannot be averted!‚ÄĚ

11:77.text: When Our messenger-angels came to Lot, he was distressed and worried by their arrival.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He said, ‚ÄúThis is a terrible day.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Since the angels came in the form of handsome men, Lot was worried for the dignity and safety of his guests among his people‚ÄĒnot knowing that they were angels.

11:78.text: And ňĻthe men ofňļ his people‚ÄĒwho were used to shameful deeds‚ÄĒcame to him rushing. He pleaded, ‚ÄúO my people! Here are my daughters<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻfor marriageňļ‚ÄĒthey are pure for you. So fear Allah, and do not humiliate me by disrespecting my guests. Is there not ňĻevenňļ a single right-minded man among you?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Single women of his community.

11:79.text: They argued, ‚ÄúYou certainly know that we have no need for your daughters. You already know what we desire!‚ÄĚ

11:80.text: He responded, ‚ÄúIf only I had the strength ňĻto resist youňļ or could rely on a strong supporter.‚ÄĚ

11:81.text: The angels said, ‚ÄúO Lot! We are the messengers of your Lord. They will never reach you. So travel with your family in the dark of night, and do not let any of you look back, except your wife. She will certainly suffer the fate of the others.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Their appointed time is the morning. Is the morning not near?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Lot‚Äôs wife disbelieved in his message. It is believed that she is the one who informed the people of Lot‚Äôs handsome guests.

11:82.text: When Our command came, We turned the cities upside down and rained down on them clustered stones of baked clay,

11:83.text: marked by your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ. And these stones are not far from the ňĻpaganňļ wrongdoers!

11:84.text: And to the people of Midian We sent their brother Shu‚Äôaib. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. And do not give short measure and weight. I do see you in prosperity now, but I truly fear for you the torment of an overwhelming Day.

11:85.text: O ¬†my people! Give full measure and weigh with justice. Do not defraud people of their property, nor go about spreading corruption in the land.

11:86.text: What is left ňĻas a lawful gainňļ by Allah is far better for you if you are ňĻtrulyňļ believers. And I am not a keeper over you.‚ÄĚ

11:87.text: They asked ňĻsarcasticallyňļ, ‚ÄúO Shu‚Äôaib! Does your prayer command you that we should abandon what our forefathers worshipped or give up managing our wealth as we please? Indeed, you are such a tolerant, sensible man!‚ÄĚ

11:88.text: He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Consider if I stand on a clear proof from my Lord and He has blessed me with a good provision from Him. I do not want to do what I am forbidding you from. I only intend reform to the best of my ability. My success comes only through Allah. In Him I trust and to Him I turn.

11:89.text: O ¬†my people! Do not let your opposition to me lead you to a fate similar to that of the people of Noah, or H√Ľd, or ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•. And the people of Lot are not far from you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Geographically and chronologically.

11:90.text: So seek your Lord‚Äôs forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance. Surely my Lord is Most Merciful, All-Loving.‚ÄĚ

11:91.text: They threatened, ‚ÄúO Shu‚Äôaib! We do not comprehend much of what you say, and surely we see you powerless among us. Were it not for your clan, we would have certainly stoned you, for you are nothing to us.‚ÄĚ

11:92.text: He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Do you have more regard for my clan than for Allah, turning your back on Him entirely? Surely my Lord is Fully Aware of what you do.

11:93.text: O ¬†my people! Persist in your ways, for I ňĻtooňļ will persist in mine. You will soon come to know who will be visited by a humiliating torment and is a liar! And watch! I too am watching with you!‚ÄĚ

11:94.text: When Our command came, We saved Shu‚Äôaib and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us. And the ňĻmightyňļ blast overtook the wrongdoers, so they fell lifeless in their homes,

11:95.text: as if they had never lived there. So away with Midian as it was with Tham√Ľd!

11:96.text: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs and compelling proof

11:97.text: to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and Pharaoh‚Äôs command was not well guided.

11:98.text: He will be before his people on the Day of Judgment and will lead them into the Fire. What an evil place to be led into!

11:99.text: They were followed by a curse in this ňĻlifeňļ and ňĻwill receive anotherňļ on the Day of Judgment. What an evil gift to receive!

11:100.text: These are accounts, We relate to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, of the ňĻdestroyedňļ cities. Some are still standing ňĻbarrenňļ, while others have been mowed down.

11:101.text: We did not wrong them, rather they wronged themselves. The gods they invoked beside Allah were of no help at all when the command of your Lord came, and only contributed to their ruin.

11:102.text: Such is the ňĻcrushingňļ grip of your Lord when He seizes the societies entrenched in wrongdoing. Indeed, His grip is ňĻterriblyňļ painful and severe.

11:103.text: Surely in this is a sign for those who fear the torment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which humanity will be gathered and a Day ňĻthat will beňļ witnessed ňĻby allňļ.

11:104.text: We only delay it for a fixed term.

11:105.text: When that Day arrives, no one will dare speak except with His permission. Some of them will be miserable, others joyful.

11:106.text: As for those bound for misery, they will be in the Fire, where they will be sighing and gasping,

11:107.text: staying there forever, as long as the heavens and the earth will endure, except what your Lord wills.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely your Lord does what He intends.
footnotes: ¬†Sinful believers will eventually be removed from Hell after receiving their punishment.

11:108.text: And as for those destined to joy, they will be in Paradise, staying there forever, as long as the heavens and the earth will endure, except what your Lord wills<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒa ňĻgenerousňļ giving, without end.
footnotes: ¬†Except the time they spend on earth and in the grave, or except the time the sinful believers spend in Hell before being transferred to Paradise.

11:109.text: So do not be in doubt ňĻO Prophetňļ about what those ňĻpagansňļ worship. They worship nothing except what their forefathers worshipped before ňĻthemňļ. And We will certainly give them their share ňĻof punishmentňļ in full, without any reduction.

11:110.text: Indeed, We had given Moses the Scripture, but differences arose regarding it. Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†their differences would have been settled ňĻat onceňļ. They are truly in alarming doubt about it.
footnotes: ¬†That He will delay their judgment until the Hereafter.

11:111.text: And surely your Lord will fully pay all for their deeds. He is certainly All-Aware of what they do.

11:112.text: So be steadfast as you are commanded ňĻO Prophetňļ, along with those who turn ňĻin submission to Allahňļ with you. And do not transgress. Surely He is All-Seeing of what you ňĻbelieversňļ do.

11:113.text: And do not be inclined to the wrongdoers or you will be touched by the Fire. For then you would have no protectors other than Allah, nor would you be helped.

11:114.text: Establish prayer ňĻO Prophetňļ at both ends of the day and in the early part of the night.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely good deeds wipe out evil deeds. That is a reminder for the mindful.
footnotes: ¬†This verse talks about the five daily prayers. The prayers at the two ends of the day are the dawn prayer (Fajr) at one end and the afternoon prayers (Šļíuhr¬†and ‚ÄôAŠĻ£r) at the other. Prayers in the early part of the night include the sunset (Maghrib) and late evening (‚ÄôIsh√Ę') prayers.

11:115.text: And be patient! Certainly Allah does not discount the reward of the good-doers.

11:116.text: If only there had been among the ňĻdestroyedňļ peoples before you, ňĻO believers,ňļ virtuous individuals who forbade corruption in the land‚ÄĒother than the few We had saved ňĻfrom the tormentňļ. But the wrongdoers ňĻonlyňļ pursued their ňĻworldlyňļ pleasures, becoming wicked.

11:117.text: And your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ would never destroy a society unjustly while its people were acting rightly.

11:118.text: Had your Lord so willed, He would have certainly made humanity one single community ňĻof believersňļ, but they will always ňĻchoose toňļ differ‚ÄĒ

11:119.text: except those shown mercy by your Lord‚ÄĒand so He created them ňĻto choose freelyňļ. And so the Word of your Lord will be fulfilled: ‚ÄúI will surely fill up Hell with jinn and humans all together.‚ÄĚ

11:120.text: And We relate to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the stories of the messengers to reassure your heart. And there has come to you in this ňĻs√Ľrahňļ the truth, a warning ňĻto the disbelieversňļ, and a reminder to the believers.

11:121.text: Say to those who disbelieve, ‚ÄúPersist in your ways; we will certainly persist in ours.

11:122.text: And wait! Surely we ňĻtooňļ are waiting.‚ÄĚ

11:123.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the knowledge of what is hidden in the heavens and the earth. And to Him all matters are returned. So worship Him and put your trust in Him. And your Lord is never unaware of what you do.

110:1.text: When Allah‚Äôs ňĻultimateňļ help comes and the victory ňĻover Mecca is achievedňļ,

110:2.text: and you ňĻO Prophetňļ see the people embracing Allah‚Äôs Way in crowds,

110:3.text: then glorify the praises of your Lord and seek His forgiveness, for certainly He is ever Accepting of Repentance.

111:1.text: May the hands of Abu Lahab perish, and he ňĻhimselfňļ perish!

111:2.text: Neither his wealth nor ňĻworldlyňļ gains will benefit him.

111:3.text: He will burn in a flaming Fire,

111:4.text: and ňĻso willňļ his wife, the carrier of ňĻthornyňļ kindling,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Since she used to carry thorny branches and throw them in the way of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), she will be carrying firewood in Hell.

111:5.text: around her neck will be a rope of palm-fibre.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This was one of the earliest s√Ľrahs¬†revealed of the Quran. Paradoxically, for Abu Lahab to disprove the Quran, all he had to do was accept Islam because this verse condemns him to Hell.

112:1.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHe is Allah‚ÄĒOne ňĻand Indivisibleňļ;

112:3.text: He has never had offspring, nor was He born.

112:4.text: And there is none comparable to Him.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†As mentioned in the Introduction, the Quran has three main themes: 1. Stories. 2. Muslim teachings. 3. And belief in the unseen. Since S√Ľrah 112 covers the third theme, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) says in a Šł•ad√ģth collected by Bukh√Ęri and Muslim that reading this s√Ľrah¬†equals reading one third of the Quran.

113:1.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI seek refuge in the Lord of the daybreak

113:2.text: from the evil of whatever He has created,

113:3.text: and from the evil of the night when it grows dark,

113:4.text: and from the evil of those ňĻwitches casting spells byňļ blowing onto knots,

113:5.text: and from the evil of an envier when they envy.‚ÄĚ

114:1.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI seek refuge in the Lord of humankind,

114:4.text: from the evil of the lurking whisperer‚ÄĒ

114:5.text: who whispers into the hearts of humankind‚ÄĒ

12:1.text: Alif-L√£m-Ra. These are the verses of the clear Book.

12:2.text: Indeed, We have sent it down as an Arabic Quran<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so that you may understand.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Quran literally means recitation.
¬†The address here is to the Arabs and, by extension, all of humanity.

12:3.text: We relate to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the best of stories through Our revelation of this Quran, though before this you were totally unaware ňĻof themňļ.

12:4.text: ňĻRememberňļ when Joseph said to his father, ‚ÄúO my dear father! Indeed I dreamt of eleven stars, and the sun, and the moon‚ÄĒI saw them prostrating to me!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This dream came true at the end of the story (see¬†12:100).

12:5.text: He replied, ‚ÄúO my dear son! Do not relate your vision to your brothers, or they will devise a plot against you. Surely Satan is a sworn enemy to humankind.

12:6.text: And so will your Lord choose you ňĻO Josephňļ, and teach you the interpretation of dreams, and perfect His favour upon you and the descendants of Jacob‚ÄĒňĻjustňļ as He once perfected it upon your forefathers, Abraham and Isaac. Surely your Lord is All-Knowing, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ

12:7.text: Indeed, in the story of Joseph and his brothers there are lessons for all who ask.

12:8.text: ňĻRememberňļ when they said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúSurely Joseph and his brother ňĻBenjaminňļ are more beloved to our father than we, even though we are a group of so many.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Indeed, our father is clearly mistaken.
footnotes: ¬†Unlike Joseph‚Äôs other brothers, Benjamin was Joseph‚Äôs full brother. They lost their mother at a young age, so their half-brothers thought that their father loved them more.

12:9.text: Kill Joseph or cast him out to some ňĻdistantňļ land so that our father‚Äôs attention will be only ours, then after that you may ňĻrepent andňļ become righteous people!‚ÄĚ

12:10.text: One of them said, ‚ÄúDo not kill Joseph. But if you must do something, throw him into the bottom of a well so perhaps he may be picked up by some travellers.‚ÄĚ

12:11.text: They said, ‚ÄúO our father! Why do you not trust us with Joseph, although we truly wish him well?

12:12.text: Send him out with us tomorrow so that he may enjoy himself and play. And we will really watch over him.‚ÄĚ

12:13.text: He responded, ‚ÄúIt would truly sadden me if you took him away with you, and I fear that a wolf may devour him while you are negligent of him.‚ÄĚ

12:14.text: They said, ‚ÄúIf a wolf were to devour him, despite our strong group, then we would certainly be losers!‚ÄĚ

12:15.text: And so, when they took him away and decided to throw him into the bottom of the well, We inspired him: ‚ÄúňĻOne dayňļ you will remind them of this deed of theirs while they are unaware ňĻof who you areňļ.‚ÄĚ

12:16.text: Then they returned to their father in the evening, weeping.

12:17.text: They cried, ‚ÄúOur father! We went racing and left Joseph with our belongings, and a wolf devoured him! But you will not believe us, no matter how truthful we are.‚ÄĚ

12:18.text: And they brought his shirt, stained with false blood.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He responded, ‚ÄúNo! Your souls must have tempted you to do something ňĻevilňļ. So ňĻI can only endure withňļ beautiful patience!<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†It is Allah‚Äôs help that I seek to bear your claims.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†They stained Joseph‚Äôs shirt with blood from a sheep, but forgot to tear his shirt. So Jacob became suspicious when he saw the intact shirt.
¬†i.e., patience without complaining.

12:19.text: And there came some travellers, and they sent their water-boy who let down his bucket into the well. He cried out, ‚ÄúOh, what a great find! Here is a boy!‚ÄĚ And they took him secretly ňĻto be soldňļ as merchandise, but Allah is All-Knowing of what they did.

12:20.text: They ňĻlaterňļ sold him for a cheap price, just a few silver coins‚ÄĒonly wanting to get rid of him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Other possible translations: 1. ‚Äú‚Ä¶ so little did they value him.‚ÄĚ 2. ‚Äú‚Ä¶ they had no interest in him.‚ÄĚ In any case, they wanted to sell him immediately before someone claimed him. According to some Quran commentators, it was Joseph‚Äôs brothers who sold him to the travellers after he was picked up from the well. The verse states that Joseph was sold for a few worthless coins. Ironically, in 12:88 Joseph‚Äôs brothers came to him after he became Egypt‚Äôs Chief Minister, begging for supplies and saying that the only money they could afford was a few worthless coins. This is when Joseph revealed his true identity to them.

12:21.text: The man from Egypt<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who bought him said to his wife, ‚ÄúTake good care of him, perhaps he may be useful to us or we may adopt him as a son.‚ÄĚ This is how We established Joseph in the land, so that We might teach him the interpretation of dreams. Allah‚Äôs Will always prevails, but most people do not know.
footnotes: ¬†Potiphar, the Chief Minister of Egypt (Al-‚ÄôAz√ģz).

12:22.text: And when he reached maturity, We gave him wisdom and knowledge. This is how We reward the good-doers.

12:23.text: And the lady, in whose house he lived, tried to seduce him. She locked the doors ňĻfirmlyňļ and said, ‚ÄúCome to me!‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúAllah is my refuge! It is ňĻnot right to betrayňļ my master, who has taken good care of me. Indeed, the wrongdoers never succeed.‚ÄĚ

12:24.text: She advanced towards him, and he would have done likewise, had he not seen a sign from his Lord.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is how We kept evil and indecency away from him, for he was truly one of Our chosen servants.
footnotes: ¬†Joseph (Ô∑ļ) received warning against fornication either through divine inspiration or a vision of his father.

12:25.text: They raced for the door and she tore his shirt from the back, only to find her husband at the door. She cried, ‚ÄúWhat is the penalty for someone who tried to violate your wife, except imprisonment or a painful punishment?‚ÄĚ

12:26.text: Joseph responded, ‚ÄúIt was she who tried to seduce me.‚ÄĚ And a witness from her own family testified: ‚ÄúIf his shirt is torn from the front, then she has told the truth and he is a liar.

12:27.text: But if it is torn from the back, then she has lied and he is truthful.‚ÄĚ

12:28.text: So when her husband saw that Joseph‚Äôs shirt was torn from the back, he said ňĻto herňļ, ‚ÄúThis must be ňĻan exampleňļ of the cunning of you ňĻwomenňļ! Indeed, your cunning is so shrewd!

12:29.text: O ¬†Joseph! Forget about this. And you ňĻO wifeňļ! Seek forgiveness for your sin.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It certainly has been your fault.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†She was urged to seek forgiveness from her husband.

12:30.text: Some women of the city gossiped, ‚ÄúThe Chief Minister‚Äôs wife is trying to seduce her slave-boy. Love for him has plagued her heart. Indeed, we see that she is clearly mistaken.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The women‚Äôs only objection was because the wife of the Chief Minister was in love with someone raised in her house like a son.

12:31.text: When she heard about their gossip,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†she invited them and set a banquet for them. She gave each one a knife, then said ňĻto Josephňļ, ‚ÄúCome out before them.‚ÄĚ When they saw him, they were so stunned ňĻby his beautyňļ that they cut their hands,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and exclaimed, ‚ÄúGood God! This cannot be human; this must be a noble angel!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., ‚Äėtheir cunning‚Äô because the women themselves were probably interested in Joseph.
¬†The women were cutting fruit, and when Joseph came out they were so impressed by his beauty that they sliced through the fruit and into their hands without knowing.

12:32.text: She said, ‚ÄúThis is the one for whose love you criticized me! I did try to seduce him but he ňĻfirmlyňļ refused. And if he does not do what I order him to, he will certainly be imprisoned and ňĻfullyňļ disgraced.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The women tried to convince him to obey the Chief Minister‚Äôs wife, so Joseph prayed to Allah to keep him away from them.

12:33.text: Joseph prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I would rather be in jail than do what they invite me to. And if You do not turn their cunning away from me, I might yield to them and fall into ignorance.‚ÄĚ

12:34.text: So his Lord responded to him, turning their cunning away from him. Surely He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

12:35.text: And so it occurred to those in charge, despite seeing all the proofs ňĻof his innocenceňļ, that he should be imprisoned for a while.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†To stop women from being charmed by Joseph‚Äôs beauty, or to put an end to the rumours, or to keep him away from the Minister‚Äôs wife.

12:36.text: And two other servants went to jail with Joseph. One of them said, ‚ÄúI dreamt I was pressing wine.‚ÄĚ The other said, ‚ÄúI dreamt I was carrying ňĻsomeňļ bread on my head, from which birds were eating.‚ÄĚ ňĻThen both said,ňļ ‚ÄúTell us their interpretation, for we surely see you as one of the good-doers.‚ÄĚ

12:37.text: Joseph replied, ‚ÄúI can even tell you what kind of meal you will be served before you receive it. This ňĻknowledgeňļ is from what my Lord has taught me. I have shunned the faith of a people who disbelieve in Allah and deny the Hereafter.

12:38.text: I follow the faith of my fathers: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. It is not ňĻrightňļ for us to associate anything with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ. This is part of Allah‚Äôs grace upon us and humanity, but most people are not grateful.

12:39.text: O ¬†my fellow-prisoners! Which is far better: many different lords or Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme?

12:40.text: Whatever ňĻidolsňļ you worship instead of Him are mere names which you and your forefathers have made up<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒa practice Allah has never authorized. It is only Allah Who decides. He has commanded that you worship none but Him. That is the upright faith, but most people do not know.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, ‚ÄúYou call them gods while in fact they are not gods.‚ÄĚ

12:41.text: ‚ÄúO my fellow-prisoners! ňĻThe firstňļ one of you will serve wine to his master, and the other will be crucified and the birds will eat from his head. The matter about which you inquired has been decided.‚ÄĚ

12:42.text: Then he said to the one he knew would survive, ‚ÄúMention me in the presence of your master.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĚ But Satan made him forget to mention Joseph to his master, so he remained in prison for several years.
footnotes: ¬†The King of Egypt.

12:43.text: And ňĻone dayňļ the King<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†said, ‚ÄúI dreamt of seven fat cows eaten up by seven skinny ones; and seven green ears of grain and ňĻsevenňļ others dry. O ¬†chiefs! Tell me the meaning of my dream if you can interpret dreams.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†In the Bible (the Book of Genesis), this ruler is referred to as a pharaoh, while in the Quran he is referred to as a king. Typically, Egypt was ruled by pharaohs, but there existed a brief period in Egyptian history in which Egypt was ruled by the Hyksos invaders (1700-1550 B.C.E.). Hyksos rulers were called kings, not pharaohs. Joseph entered Egypt during the reign of the Hyksos, who used to appoint some foreigners to prominent positions in Egypt. According to The Jewish Encyclopedia, ‚ÄúThose who regard the Joseph stories as historical generally hold that the Pharaoh by whom Joseph was made the practical ruler of Egypt was one of the Hyksos kings.‚ÄĚ "Joseph,"¬†The Jewish Encyclopedia, Volume VII (London: Funk and Wagnalls Company, 1916), p. 252.

12:44.text: They replied, ‚ÄúThese are confused visions and we do not know the interpretation of such dreams.‚ÄĚ

12:45.text: ňĻFinally,ňļ the surviving ex-prisoner remembered ňĻJosephňļ after a long time and said, ‚ÄúI will tell you its interpretation, so send me forth ňĻto Josephňļ.‚ÄĚ

12:46.text: ňĻHe said,ňļ ‚ÄúJoseph, O ¬†man of truth! Interpret for us ňĻthe dream ofňļ seven fat cows eaten up by seven skinny ones; and seven green ears of grain and ňĻsevenňļ others dry, so that I may return to the people and let them know.‚ÄĚ

12:47.text: Joseph replied, ‚ÄúYou will plant ňĻgrainňļ for seven consecutive years, leaving in the ear whatever you will harvest, except for the little you will eat.

12:48.text: Then after that will come seven years of great hardship which will consume whatever you have saved, except the little you will store ňĻfor seedňļ.

12:49.text: Then after that will come a year in which people will receive abundant rain and they will press ňĻoil and wineňļ.‚ÄĚ

12:50.text: The King ňĻthenňļ said, ‚ÄúBring him to me.‚ÄĚ When the messenger came to him, Joseph said, ‚ÄúGo back to your master and ask him about the case of the women who cut their hands. Surely my Lord has ňĻfullňļ knowledge of their cunning.‚ÄĚ

12:51.text: The King asked ňĻthe womenňļ, ‚ÄúWhat did you get when you tried to seduce Joseph?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúAllah forbid! We know nothing indecent about him.‚ÄĚ Then the Chief Minister‚Äôs wife admitted, ‚ÄúNow the truth has come to light. It was I who tried to seduce him, and he is surely truthful.

12:52.text: From this, Joseph should know that I did not speak dishonestly about him in his absence, for Allah certainly does not guide the scheming of the dishonest.

12:53.text: And I do not seek to free myself from blame, for indeed the soul is ever inclined to evil, except those shown mercy by my Lord. Surely my Lord is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ

12:54.text: The King said, ‚ÄúBring him to me. I will employ him exclusively in my service.‚ÄĚ And when Joseph spoke to him, the King said, ‚ÄúToday you are highly esteemed and fully trusted by us.‚ÄĚ

12:55.text: Joseph proposed, ‚ÄúPut me in charge of the store-houses of the land, for I am truly reliable and adept.‚ÄĚ

12:56.text: This is how We established Joseph in the land to settle wherever he pleased. We shower Our mercy on whoever We will, and We never discount the reward of the good-doers.

12:57.text: And the reward of the Hereafter is far better for those who are faithful and are mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.

12:58.text: And Joseph‚Äôs brothers came and entered his presence. He recognized them but they were unaware of who he really was.

12:59.text: When he had provided them with their supplies, he demanded, ‚ÄúBring me your brother on your father‚Äôs side.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Do you not see that I give full measure and I am the best of hosts?
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Benjamin. When Joseph hosted his half-brothers, they told him the number of people in their household for future supplies.

12:60.text: But if you do not bring him to me ňĻnext timeňļ, I will have no grain for you, nor will you ever come close to me again.‚ÄĚ

12:61.text: They promised, ‚ÄúWe will try to convince his father to let him come. We will certainly do ňĻour bestňļ.‚ÄĚ

12:62.text: Joseph ordered his servants to put his brothers‚Äô money back into their saddlebags so that they would find it when they returned to their family and perhaps they would come back.

12:63.text: When Joseph‚Äôs brothers returned to their father, they pleaded, ‚ÄúO our father! We have been denied ňĻfurtherňļ supplies. So send our brother with us so that we may receive our measure, and we will definitely watch over him.‚ÄĚ

12:64.text: He responded, ‚ÄúShould I trust you with him as I once trusted you with his brother ňĻJosephňļ? But ňĻonlyňļ Allah is the best Protector, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.‚ÄĚ

12:65.text: When they opened their bags, they discovered that their money had been returned to them. They argued, ‚ÄúO our father! What more can we ask for? Here is our money, fully returned to us. Now we can buy more food for our family. We will watch over our brother, and obtain an extra camel-load of grain. That load can be easily secured.‚ÄĚ

12:66.text: Jacob insisted, ‚ÄúI will not send him with you until you give me a solemn oath by Allah that you will certainly bring him back to me, unless you are totally overpowered.‚ÄĚ Then after they had given him their oaths, he concluded, ‚ÄúAllah is a Witness to what we have said.‚ÄĚ

12:67.text: He then instructed ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúO my sons! Do not enter ňĻthe cityňļ all through one gate, but through separate gates.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†I cannot help you against ňĻwhat is destined byňļ Allah in the least. It is only Allah Who decides. In Him I put my trust. And in Him let the faithful put their trust.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†He told them that every three or four of them should enter through a different gate. His desire was to protect them from envy and harm.

12:68.text: Then when they entered as their father had instructed them, this did not help them against ňĻthe Will ofňļ Allah whatsoever. It was just a desire in Jacob‚Äôs heart which he satisfied. He was truly blessed with ňĻgreatňļ knowledge because of what We had taught him, but most people have no knowledge.

12:69.text: When they entered Joseph‚Äôs presence, he called his brother ňĻBenjaminňļ aside, and confided ňĻto himňļ, ‚ÄúI am indeed your brother ňĻJosephňļ! So do not feel distressed about what they have been doing.‚ÄĚ

12:70.text: When Joseph had provided them with supplies, he slipped the royal cup into his brother‚Äôs bag. Then a herald cried, ‚ÄúO people of the caravan! You must be thieves!‚ÄĚ

12:71.text: They asked, turning back, ‚ÄúWhat have you lost?‚ÄĚ

12:72.text: The herald ňĻalong with the guardsňļ replied, ‚ÄúWe have lost the King‚Äôs measuring cup. And whoever brings it will be awarded a camel-load ňĻof grainňļ. I guarantee it.‚ÄĚ

12:73.text: Joseph‚Äôs brothers replied, ‚ÄúBy Allah! You know well that we did not come to cause trouble in the land, nor are we thieves.‚ÄĚ

12:74.text: Joseph‚Äôs men asked, ‚ÄúWhat should be the price for theft, if you are lying?‚ÄĚ

12:75.text: Joseph‚Äôs brothers responded, ‚ÄúThe price will be ňĻthe enslavement ofňļ the one in whose bag the cup is found. That is how we punish the wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

12:76.text: Joseph began searching their bags before that of his brother ňĻBenjaminňļ, then brought it out of Benjamin‚Äôs bag. This is how We inspired Joseph to plan. He could not have taken his brother under the King‚Äôs law, but Allah had so willed. We elevate in rank whoever We will. But above those ranking in knowledge is the One All-Knowing.

12:77.text: ňĻTo distance themselves,ňļ Joseph‚Äôs brothers argued, ‚ÄúIf he has stolen, so did his ňĻfullňļ brother before.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But Joseph suppressed his outrage‚ÄĒrevealing nothing to them‚ÄĒand said ňĻto himselfňļ, ‚ÄúYou are in such an evil position,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and Allah knows best ňĻthe truth ofňļ what you claim.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Joseph was falsely accused of theft when he was young.
¬†He meant: I did not steal anything, nor did my brother ňĻBenjaminňļ. You are the real thieves; you stole me from my father and threw me into the well.

12:78.text: They appealed, ‚ÄúO Chief Minister! He has a very old father, so take one of us instead. We surely see you as one of the good-doers.‚ÄĚ

12:79.text: Joseph responded, ‚ÄúAllah forbid that we should take other than the one with whom we found our property. Otherwise, we would surely be unjust.‚ÄĚ

12:80.text: When they lost all hope in him, they spoke privately. The eldest of them said, ‚ÄúDo you not know that your father had taken a solemn oath by Allah from you, nor how you failed him regarding Joseph before? So I am not leaving this land until my father allows me to, or Allah decides for me. For He is the Best of Judges.

12:81.text: Return to your father and say, ‚ÄėO our father! Your son committed theft. We testify only to what we know. We could not guard against the unforeseen.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†We did not know when we gave you our solemn oath that our brother was going to steal.

12:82.text: Ask ňĻthe people ofňļ the land where we were and the caravan we travelled with. We are certainly telling the truth.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

12:83.text: He cried, ‚ÄúNo! Your souls must have tempted you to do something ňĻevilňļ. So ňĻI am left with nothing butňļ beautiful patience!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†I trust Allah will return them all to me. Surely He ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Knowing, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., patience without complaining.

12:84.text: He turned away from them, lamenting, ‚ÄúAlas, poor Joseph!‚ÄĚ And his eyes turned white out of the grief he suppressed.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Jacob (Ô∑ļ) cried for so long that he became very weak-sighted. Some scholars suggest that he lost his sight completely. This does not contradict Jacob‚Äôs beautiful patience because he did not complain to anyone, but expressed his sorrow only to Allah.

12:85.text: They said, ‚ÄúBy Allah! You will not cease to remember Joseph until you lose your health or ňĻevenňļ your life.‚ÄĚ

12:86.text: He replied, ‚ÄúI complain of my anguish and sorrow only to Allah, and I know from Allah what you do not know.

12:87.text: O ¬†my sons! Go and search ňĻdiligentlyňļ for Joseph and his brother. And do not lose hope in the mercy of Allah, for no one loses hope in Allah‚Äôs mercy except those with no faith.‚ÄĚ

12:88.text: When they entered Joseph‚Äôs presence, they pleaded, ‚ÄúO Chief Minister! We and our family have been touched with hardship, and we have brought only a few worthless coins, but ňĻpleaseňļ give us our supplies in full and be charitable to us. Indeed, Allah rewards the charitable.‚ÄĚ

12:89.text: He asked, ‚ÄúDo you remember what you did to Joseph and his brother in your ignorance?‚ÄĚ

12:90.text: They replied ňĻin shockňļ, ‚ÄúAre you really Joseph?‚ÄĚ He said, ‚ÄúI am Joseph, and here is my brother ňĻBenjaminňļ! Allah has truly been gracious to us. Surely whoever is mindful ňĻof Allahňļ and patient, then certainly Allah never discounts the reward of the good-doers.‚ÄĚ

12:91.text: They admitted, ‚ÄúBy Allah! Allah has truly preferred you over us, and we have surely been sinful.‚ÄĚ

12:92.text: Joseph said, ‚ÄúThere is no blame on you today. May Allah forgive you! He is the Most Merciful of the merciful!

12:93.text: Go with this shirt of mine and cast it over my father‚Äôs face, and he will regain his sight. Then come back to me with your whole family.‚ÄĚ

12:94.text: When the caravan departed ňĻfrom Egyptňļ, their father said ňĻto those around himňļ, ‚ÄúYou may think I am senile, but I certainly sense the smell of Joseph.‚ÄĚ

12:95.text: They replied, ‚ÄúBy Allah! You are definitely still in your old delusion.‚ÄĚ

12:96.text: But when the bearer of the good news arrived, he cast the shirt over Jacob‚Äôs face, so he regained his sight. Jacob then said ňĻto his childrenňļ, ‚ÄúDid I not tell you that I truly know from Allah what you do not know?‚ÄĚ

12:98.text: He said, ‚ÄúI will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He ňĻaloneňļ is indeed the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Jacob (Ô∑ļ) delayed the prayer for his children to be forgiven until pre-dawn, which is a blessed time for supplication.

12:99.text: When they entered Joseph‚Äôs presence, he received his parents ňĻgraciouslyňļ and said, ‚ÄúEnter Egypt, Allah willing, in security.‚ÄĚ

12:100.text: Then he raised his parents to the throne, and they all fell down in prostration to Joseph,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who then said, ‚ÄúO my dear father! This is the interpretation of my old dream. My Lord has made it come true. He was truly kind to me when He freed me from prison, and brought you all from the desert after Satan had ignited rivalry between me and my siblings.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Indeed my Lord is subtle in fulfilling what He wills. Surely He ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Knowing, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Joseph‚Äôs parents and his eleven brothers prostrated before him out of respect, not as an act of worship. This was permissible in their tradition, but in Islam, Muslims prostrate only to Allah.
¬†Joseph (Ô∑ļ) did not mention how Allah saved him from the well because he did not want to embarrass his brothers after forgiving them.

12:101.text: ‚ÄúMy Lord! You have surely granted me authority and taught me the interpretation of dreams. ňĻOňļ Originator of the heavens and the earth! You are my Guardian in this world and the Hereafter. Allow me to die as one who submits<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and join me with the righteous.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., as a Muslim.

12:102.text: That is from the stories of the unseen which We reveal to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. You were not present when they ňĻallňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†made up their minds, and when they plotted ňĻagainst Josephňļ.
footnotes: ¬†This includes Joseph‚Äôs brothers, the travellers who picked him up from the well and sold him into slavery, and the Chief Minister‚Äôs wife and other women in the city.

12:103.text: And most people will not believe‚ÄĒno matter how keen you are‚ÄĒ

12:104.text: even though you are not asking them for a reward for this ňĻQuranňļ. It is only a reminder to the whole world.
