12:107.text: Do they feel secure that an overwhelming torment from Allah will not overtake them, or that the Hour will not take them by surprise when they least expect ňĻitňļ?

12:108.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is my way. I invite to Allah with insight‚ÄĒI and those who follow me. Glory be to Allah, and I am not one of the polytheists.‚ÄĚ

12:109.text: We only sent before you ňĻO Prophetňļ men inspired by Us from among the people of each society. Have the deniers not travelled through the land to see what was the end of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them? And surely the ňĻeternalňļ Home of the Hereafter is far better for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ. Will you not then understand?

12:111.text: In their stories there is truly a lesson for people of reason. This message cannot be a fabrication, rather ňĻit isňļ a confirmation of previous revelation, a detailed explanation of all things, a guide, and a mercy for people of faith.

13:1.text: Alif-L√£m-Mń©m-Ra. These are the verses of the Book. What has been revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ from your Lord is the truth, but most people do not believe.

13:2.text: It is Allah Who has raised the heavens without pillars‚ÄĒas you can see‚ÄĒthen established Himself on the Throne. He has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term. He conducts the whole affair. He makes the signs clear so that you may be certain of the meeting with your Lord.

13:3.text: And He is the One Who spread out the earth and placed firm mountains and rivers upon it, and created fruits of every kind in pairs.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He covers the day with night. Surely in this are signs for those who reflect.
footnotes: ¬†Males and females, sweet and bitter, etc.

13:4.text: And on the earth there are ňĻdifferentňļ neighbouring tracts, gardens of grapevines, ňĻvariousňļ crops, palm trees‚ÄĒsome stemming from the same root, others standing alone. They are all irrigated with the same water, yet We make some taste better than others. Surely in this are signs for those who understand.

13:5.text: ňĻNow,ňļ if anything should amaze you ňĻO Prophetňļ, then it is their question: ‚ÄúWhen we are reduced to dust, will we really be raised as a new creation?‚ÄĚ It is they who have disbelieved in their Lord. It is they who will have shackles around their necks. And it is they who will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.

13:6.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ to hasten the torment rather than grace, though there have ňĻalreadyňļ been ňĻmanyňļ torments before them. Surely your Lord is full of forgiveness for people, despite their wrongdoing, and your Lord is truly severe in punishment.

13:7.text: The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúIf only a sign could be sent down to him from his Lord.‚ÄĚ You ňĻO Prophetňļ are only a warner. And every people had a guide.

13:8.text: Allah knows what every female bears and what increases and decreases in the wombs.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And with Him everything is determined with precision.
footnotes: ¬†He knows whether the egg will be fertilized or not, whether the baby will be born before or after nine months, whether the pregnancy will end with delivery or miscarriage, and whether there will be one baby or more.

13:9.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Knower of the seen and the unseen‚ÄĒthe All-Great, Most Exalted.

13:10.text: It is the same ňĻto Himňļ whether any of you speaks secretly or openly, whether one hides in the darkness of night or goes about in broad daylight.

13:11.text: For each one there are successive angels before and behind, protecting them by Allah‚Äôs command. Indeed, Allah would never change a people‚Äôs state ňĻof favourňļ until they change their own state ňĻof faithňļ. And if it is Allah‚Äôs Will to torment a people, it can never be averted, nor can they find a protector other than Him.

13:12.text: He is the One Who shows you lightning, inspiring ňĻyou withňļ hope and fear,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and produces heavy clouds.
footnotes: ¬†Hope of rain and fear of torment.

13:13.text: The thunder glorifies His praises, as do the angels in awe of Him. He sends thunderbolts, striking with them whoever He wills. Yet they dispute about Allah. And He is tremendous in might.

13:14.text: Calling upon Him ňĻaloneňļ is the truth. But those ňĻidolsňļ the pagans invoke besides Him ňĻcanňļ never respond to them in any way. ňĻIt isňļ just like someone who stretches out their hands to water, ňĻasking itňļ to reach their mouths, but it can never do so. The calls of the disbelievers are only in vain.

13:15.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ bow down ňĻin submissionňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†all those in the heavens and the earth‚ÄĒwillingly or unwillingly‚ÄĒas do their shadows, morning and evening.
footnotes: ¬†lit., prostrate. Meaning, all beings submit to His Will.

13:16.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWho is the Lord of the heavens and the earth?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ Ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúWhy ňĻthenňļ have you taken besides Him lords who cannot even benefit or protect themselves?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúCan the blind and the sighted be equal? Or can darkness and light be equal?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Or have they associated with Allah partners who ňĻsupposedlyňļ produced a creation like His, leaving them confused between the two creations? Say, ‚ÄúAllah is the Creator of all things, and He is the One, the Supreme.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Blindness and darkness are commonly used as an analogy for disbelief, whereas the ability to see and light refer to true faith in Allah.

13:17.text: He sends down rain from the sky, causing the valleys to flow, each according to its capacity. The currents then carry along rising foam, similar to the slag produced from metal that people melt in the fire for ornaments or tools. This is how Allah compares truth to falsehood. The ňĻworthlessňļ scum is then cast away, but what benefits people remains on the earth. This is how Allah sets forth parables.

13:18.text: Those who respond to ňĻthe call ofňļ their Lord will have the finest reward. As for those who do not respond to Him, even if they were to possess everything in the world twice over, they would certainly offer it to ransom themselves. They will face strict judgment, and Hell will be their home. What an evil place to rest!

13:19.text: Can the one who knows that your Lord‚Äôs revelation to you ňĻO Prophetňļ is the truth be like the one who is blind? None will be mindful ňĻof thisňļ except people of reason.

13:20.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who honour Allah‚Äôs covenant, never breaking the pledge;

13:21.text: and those who maintain whatever ňĻtiesňļ Allah has ordered to be maintained, stand in awe of their Lord, and fear strict judgment.

13:22.text: And ňĻthey areňļ those who endure patiently, seeking their Lord‚Äôs pleasure,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†establish prayer, donate from what We have provided for them‚ÄĒsecretly and openly‚ÄĒand respond to evil with good. It is they who will have the ultimate abode:
footnotes: ¬†lit., seeking their Lord‚Äôs Face.

13:23.text: the Gardens of Eternity, which they will enter along with the righteous among their parents, spouses, and descendants. And the angels will enter upon them from every gate, ňĻsaying,ňļ

13:24.text: ‚ÄúPeace be upon you for your perseverance. How excellent is the ultimate abode!‚ÄĚ

13:25.text: And those who violate Allah‚Äôs covenant after it has been affirmed, break whatever ňĻtiesňļ Allah has ordered to be maintained, and spread corruption in the land‚ÄĒit is they who will be condemned and will have the worst abode.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Hellfire.

13:26.text: Allah gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. And the disbelievers become prideful of ňĻthe pleasures ofňļ this worldly life. But the life of this world, compared to the Hereafter, is nothing but a fleeting enjoyment.

13:27.text: The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúIf only a sign could be sent down to him from his Lord.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIndeed, Allah leaves to stray whoever He wills, and guides to Himself whoever turns to Him‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†The disbelievers demanded a tangible miracle such as the staff of Moses.

13:28.text: those who believe and whose hearts find comfort in the remembrance of Allah. Surely in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find comfort.

13:29.text: Those who believe and do good, for them will be bliss and an honourable destination.‚ÄĚ

13:30.text: And so We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ to a community, like ňĻWe did withňļ earlier communities, so that you may recite to them what We have revealed to you. Yet they deny the Most Compassionate.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ‚ÄúHe is my Lord! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. In Him I put my trust, and to Him I turn ňĻin repentanceňļ.‚ÄĚ

13:31.text: If there were a recitation that could cause mountains to move, or the earth to split, or the dead to speak, ňĻit would have been this Quranňļ. But all matters are by Allah‚Äôs Will. Have the believers not yet realized that had Allah willed, He could have guided all of humanity? And disasters will continue to afflict the disbelievers or strike close to their homes for their misdeeds, until Allah‚Äôs promise comes to pass. Surely Allah never fails in His promise.

13:32.text: Other messengers had already been ridiculed before you, but I delayed the disbelievers ňĻfor a whileňļ then seized them. And how ňĻhorribleňļ was My punishment!

13:33.text: Is ňĻthere any equal toňļ the Vigilant One Who knows what every soul commits? Yet the pagans have associated others with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúName them! Or do you ňĻmean toňļ inform Him of something He does not know on the earth? Or are these ňĻgodsňļ just empty words?‚ÄĚ In fact, the disbelievers‚Äô falsehood has been made so appealing to them that they have been turned away from the Path. And whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.

13:34.text: For them is punishment in this worldly life, but the punishment of the Hereafter is truly far worse. And none can shield them from Allah.

13:35.text: The description of the Paradise promised to the righteous is that under it rivers flow; eternal is its fruit as well as its shade. That is the ňĻultimateňļ outcome for the righteous. But the outcome for the disbelievers is the Fire!

13:36.text: ňĻThe believers amongňļ those who were given the Scripture rejoice at what has been revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, while some ňĻdisbelievingňļ factions deny a portion of it. Say, ‚ÄúI have only been commanded to worship Allah, associating none with Him ňĻin worshipňļ. To Him I invite ňĻallňļ, and to Him is my return.‚ÄĚ

13:37.text: And so We have revealed it as an authority in Arabic. And if you were to follow their desires after ňĻallňļ the knowledge that has come to you, there would be none to protect or shield you from Allah.

13:38.text: We have certainly sent messengers before you ňĻO Prophetňļ and blessed them with wives and offspring. It was not for any messenger to bring a sign without Allah‚Äôs permission. Every destined matter has a ňĻsetňļ time.

13:39.text: Allah eliminates and confirms what He wills, and with Him is the Master Record.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 6:38.

13:40.text: Whether We show you ňĻO Prophetňļ some of what We threaten them with, or cause you to die ňĻbefore thatňļ, your duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ. Judgment is for Us.

13:41.text: Do they not see that We gradually reduce their land from its borders? Allah decides‚ÄĒnone can reverse His decision. And He is swift in reckoning.

13:42.text: Those ňĻdisbelieversňļ before them ňĻsecretlyňļ planned, but Allah has the ultimate plan. He knows what every soul commits. And the disbelievers will soon know who will have the ultimate outcome.

13:43.text: The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúYou ňĻMuŠł•ammadňļ are no messenger.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah is sufficient as a Witness between me and you, as is whoever has knowledge of the Scripture.‚ÄĚ

14:1.text: Alif-L√£m-Ra. ňĻThis isňļ a Book which We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ so that you may lead people out of darkness and into light, by the Will of their Lord, to the Path of the Almighty, the Praiseworthy‚ÄĒ

14:2.text: Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And woe to the disbelievers because of a severe torment!

14:3.text: ňĻThey areňļ the ones who favour the life of this world over the Hereafter and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, striving to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked. It is they who have gone far astray.

14:5.text: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs, ňĻordering him,ňļ ‚ÄúLead your people out of darkness and into light, and remind them of Allah‚Äôs days ňĻof favourňļ.‚ÄĚ Surely in this are signs for whoever is steadfast, grateful.

14:6.text: ňĻConsiderňļ when Moses said to his people, ‚ÄúRemember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you when He rescued you from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with dreadful torment‚ÄĒslaughtering your sons and keeping your women. That was a severe test from your Lord.

14:9.text: Have you not ňĻalreadyňļ received the stories of those who were before you: the people of Noah, ‚Äô√ād, Tham√Ľd, and those after them? Only Allah knows how many they were. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they put their hands over their mouths<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and said, ‚ÄúWe totally reject what you have been sent with, and we are certainly in alarming doubt about what you are inviting us to.‚ÄĚ

14:10.text: Their messengers asked ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúIs there any doubt about Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth? He is inviting you in order to forgive your sins, and delay your end until your appointed term.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They argued, ‚ÄúYou are no more than humans like us! You ňĻonlyňļ wish to turn us away from what our forefathers worshipped. So bring us some compelling proof.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†It is out of Allah‚Äôs mercy that He does not hasten your punishment but delays your death until your destined time.

14:11.text: Their messengers said to them, ‚ÄúWe are ňĻindeedňļ only humans like you, but Allah favours whoever He chooses of His servants. It is not for us to bring you any proof without Allah‚Äôs permission. And in Allah let the believers put their trust.

14:12.text: Why should we not put our trust in Allah, when He has truly guided us to ňĻthe very best ofňļ ways? Indeed, we will patiently endure whatever harm you may cause us. And in Allah let the faithful put their trust.‚ÄĚ

14:13.text: The disbelievers then threatened their messengers, ‚ÄúWe will certainly expel you from our land, unless you return to our faith.‚ÄĚ So their Lord revealed to them, ‚ÄúWe will surely destroy the wrongdoers,

14:14.text: and make you reside in the land after them. This is for whoever is in awe of standing before Me and fears My warning.‚ÄĚ

14:15.text: And both sides called for judgment, so every stubborn tyrant was doomed.

14:16.text: Awaiting them is Hell, and they will be left to drink oozing pus,

14:17.text: which they will sip with difficulty, and can hardly swallow. Death will overwhelm them from every side, yet they will not ňĻbe able toňļ die. Awaiting them still is harsher torment.

14:18.text: The parable of the deeds of those who disbelieve in their Lord is that of ashes fiercely blown away by wind on a stormy day. They will gain nothing from what they have earned. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the farthest one can stray.

14:19.text: Have you not seen that Allah created the heavens and the earth for a reason? If He wills, He can eliminate you and produce a new creation.

14:20.text: And that is not difficult for Allah ňĻat allňļ.

14:21.text: They will all appear before Allah, and the lowly ňĻfollowersňļ will appeal to the arrogant ňĻleadersňļ, ‚ÄúWe were your ňĻdedicatedňļ followers, so will you ňĻthenňļ protect us from Allah‚Äôs torment in any way?‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúHad Allah guided us, we would have guided you. ňĻNowňļ it is all the same for us whether we suffer patiently or impatiently, there is no escape for us.‚ÄĚ

14:22.text: And Satan will say ňĻto his followersňļ after the judgment has been passed, ‚ÄúIndeed, Allah has made you a true promise.¬†I too made you a promise, but I failed you. I did not have any authority over you. I only called you, and you responded to me. So do not blame me; blame yourselves. I cannot save you, nor can you save me. Indeed, I denounce your previous association of me with Allah ňĻin loyaltyňļ. Surely the wrongdoers will suffer a painful punishment.‚ÄĚ

14:23.text: Those who believe and do good will be admitted into Gardens, under which rivers flow‚ÄĒto stay there forever by the Will of their Lord‚ÄĒwhere they will be greeted with ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ

14:24.text: Do you not see how Allah compares a good word to a good tree? Its root is firm and its branches reach the sky,

14:25.text: ňĻalwaysňļ yielding its fruit in every season by the Will of its Lord. This is how Allah sets forth parables for the people, so perhaps they will be mindful.

14:26.text: And the parable of an evil word is that of an evil tree, uprooted from the earth, having no stability.

14:27.text: Allah makes the believers steadfast with the firm Word ňĻof faithňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†in this worldly life and the Hereafter. And Allah leaves the wrongdoers to stray. For Allah does what He wills.
footnotes: ¬†That there is only one God worthy of worship.

14:28.text: Have you not seen those ňĻdisbelieversňļ who meet Allah‚Äôs favours with ingratitude and lead their own people to their doom?

14:29.text: In Hell they will burn. What an evil place for settlement.

14:30.text: They set up equals to Allah to mislead ňĻothersňļ from His Way. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEnjoy yourselves! Surely your destination is the Fire.‚ÄĚ

14:31.text: Tell My believing servants to establish prayer and donate from what We have provided for them‚ÄĒopenly and secretly‚ÄĒbefore the arrival of a Day in which there will be no ransom or friendly connections.

14:32.text: It is Allah Who created the heavens and the earth and sends down rain from the sky, causing fruits to grow as a provision for you. He has subjected the ships for your service, sailing through the sea by His command, and has subjected the rivers for you.

14:33.text: He has ňĻalsoňļ subjected for you the sun and the moon, both constantly orbiting, and has subjected the day and night for you.

14:34.text: And He has granted you all that you asked Him for. If you tried to count Allah‚Äôs blessings, you would never be able to number them. Indeed humankind is truly unfair, ňĻtotallyňļ ungrateful.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†In this context, ‚Äúhumankind‚ÄĚ refers only to those who deny Allah‚Äôs favours.

14:35.text: ňĻRememberňļ when Abraham prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Make this city ňĻof Meccaňļ secure, and keep me and my children away from the worship of idols.

14:36.text: My Lord! They have caused many people to go astray. So whoever follows me is with me, and whoever disobeys me‚ÄĒthen surely You are ňĻstillňļ All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

14:37.text: Our Lord! I have settled some of my offspring in a barren valley, near Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may establish prayer. So make the hearts of ňĻbelievingňļ people incline towards them and provide them with fruits, so perhaps they will be thankful.

14:38.text: Our Lord! You certainly know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on earth or in heaven is hidden from Allah.

14:39.text: All praise is for Allah who has blessed me with Ishmael and Isaac in my old age. My Lord is indeed the Hearer of ňĻallňļ prayers.

14:40.text: My Lord! Make me and those ňĻbelieversňļ of my descendants keep up prayer. Our Lord! Accept my prayers.

14:41.text: Our Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day when the judgment will come to pass.‚ÄĚ

14:42.text: Do not think ňĻO Prophetňļ that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them until a Day when ňĻtheirňļ eyes will stare in horror‚ÄĒ

14:43.text: rushing forth, heads raised, never blinking, hearts void.

14:44.text: And warn the people of the Day when the punishment will overtake ňĻthe wicked amongňļ them, and the wrongdoers will cry, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Delay us for a little while. We will respond to Your call and follow the messengers!‚ÄĚ ňĻIt will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúDid you not swear before that you would never be removed ňĻto the next lifeňļ?‚ÄĚ

14:45.text: You passed by the ruins of those ňĻdestroyed peoplesňļ who had wronged themselves.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It was made clear to you how We dealt with them, and We gave you ňĻmanyňļ examples.
footnotes: ¬†Arab traders used to pass by the homes of some destroyed nations on their journeys to Syria and Yemen (such as ‚Äô»ād and Tham√Ľd, respectively), stopping shortly for rest.

14:46.text: They devised every plot, which was fully known to Allah, but their plotting was not enough to ňĻevenňļ overpower mountains ňĻlet alone Allahňļ.

14:47.text: So do not think ňĻO Prophetňļ that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His messengers. Allah is indeed Almighty, capable of punishment.

14:48.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the earth will be changed into a different earth and the heavens as well, and all will appear before Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme.

14:49.text: On that Day you will see the wicked bound together in chains,

14:50.text: with garments of tar, and their faces covered with flames.

14:51.text: As such, Allah will reward every soul for what it has committed. Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.

14:52.text: This ňĻQuranňļ is a ňĻsufficientňļ message for humanity so that they may take it as a warning and know that there is only One God, and so that people of reason may be mindful.

15:1.text: Alif-L√£m-Ra. These are the verses of the Book; the clear Quran.

15:2.text: ňĻThe day will come whenňļ the disbelievers will certainly wish they had submitted ňĻto Allahňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., had been Muslims.

15:3.text: ňĻSoňļ let them eat and enjoy themselves and be diverted by ňĻfalseňļ hope, for they will soon know.

15:4.text: We have never destroyed a society without a destined term.

15:5.text: No people can advance their doom, nor can they delay it.

15:6.text: They say, ‚ÄúO you to whom the Reminder<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is revealed! You must be insane!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Quran.

15:7.text: Why do you not bring us the angels, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

15:8.text: We do not send the angels down except for a just cause, and then ňĻthe end ofňļ the disbelievers will not be delayed.

15:9.text: It is certainly We Who have revealed the Reminder, and it is certainly We Who will preserve it.

15:10.text: Indeed, We sent messengers before you ňĻO Prophetňļ among the groups of early peoples,

15:11.text: but no messenger ever came to them without being mocked.

15:12.text: This is how We allow disbelief ňĻto steepňļ into the hearts of the wicked.

15:13.text: They would not believe in this ňĻQuranňļ despite the ňĻmanyňļ examples of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before.

15:14.text: And even if We opened for them a gate to heaven, through which they continued to ascend,

15:15.text: still they would say, ‚ÄúOur eyes have truly been dazzled! In fact, we must have been bewitched.‚ÄĚ

15:16.text: Indeed, We have placed constellations in the sky, and adorned it for all to see.

15:17.text: And We protected it from every accursed devil,

15:18.text: except the one eavesdropping, who is then pursued by a visible flare.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Some jinn used to eavesdrop on the angels in heaven so they could disclose this information to their human associates. But this practice came to an end when the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was sent with the message of Islam. See¬†72:8-10.

15:19.text: As for the earth, We spread it out and placed upon it firm mountains, and caused everything to grow there in perfect balance.

15:20.text: And We made in it means of sustenance for you and others, who you do not provide for.

15:21.text: There is not any means ňĻof sustenanceňļ whose reserves We do not hold, only bringing it forth in precise measure.

15:22.text: We send fertilizing winds, and bring down rain from the sky for you to drink. It is not you who hold its reserves.

15:23.text: Surely it is We Who give life and cause death. And We are the ňĻEternalňļ Successor.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Allah will remain eternally after all pass away.

15:24.text: We certainly know those who have gone before you and those who will come after ňĻyouňļ.

15:25.text: Surely your Lord ňĻaloneňļ will gather them together ňĻfor judgmentňļ. He is truly All-Wise, All-Knowing.

15:26.text: Indeed, We created man from sounding clay moulded from black mud.

15:27.text: As for the jinn, We created them earlier from smokeless fire.

15:28.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophetňļ when your Lord said to the angels, ‚ÄúI am going to create a human being from sounding clay moulded from black mud.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This does not contradict other verses that say Adam was created out of dust and those that say he was made out of mud. Adam was created in stages: first from dust, which was later turned into mud, then clay. This is similar to saying: I made a loaf of bread out of grain, or flour, or dough.

15:29.text: So when I have fashioned him and had a spirit of My Own ňĻcreationňļ breathed into him, fall down in prostration to him.‚ÄĚ

15:30.text: So the angels prostrated all together‚ÄĒ

15:31.text: but not Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who refused to prostrate with the others.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:34.

15:32.text: Allah asked, ‚ÄúO Ibl√ģs! What is the matter with you that you did not join others in prostration?‚ÄĚ

15:33.text: He replied, ‚ÄúIt is not for me to prostrate to a human You created from sounding clay moulded from black mud.‚ÄĚ

15:34.text: Allah commanded, ‚ÄúThen get out of Paradise, for you are truly cursed.

15:35.text: And surely upon you is condemnation until the Day of Judgment.‚ÄĚ

15:36.text: Satan appealed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Then delay my end until the Day of their resurrection.‚ÄĚ

15:39.text: Satan responded, ‚ÄúMy Lord! For allowing me to stray I will surely tempt them on earth and mislead them all together,

15:40.text: except Your chosen servants among them.‚ÄĚ

15:41.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúThis is the Way, binding on Me:

15:42.text: you will certainly have no authority over My servants, except the deviant who follow you,

15:43.text: and surely Hell is their destined place, all together.

15:44.text: It has seven gates, to each a group of them is designated.‚ÄĚ

15:45.text: Indeed, the righteous will be amid Gardens and springs.

15:46.text: ňĻIt will be said to them,ňļ ‚ÄúEnter in peace and security.‚ÄĚ

15:47.text: We will remove whatever bitterness they had in their hearts.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†In a friendly manner, they will be on thrones, facing one another.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the bitterness they had in their hearts towards other believers who wronged them in the worldly life.

15:48.text: No fatigue will touch them there, nor will they ever be asked to leave.

15:49.text: Inform My servants ňĻO Prophetňļ that I am truly the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful,

15:50.text: and that My torment is indeed the most painful.

15:51.text: And inform them ňĻO Prophetňļ about Abraham‚Äôs guests

15:52.text: who entered upon him and greeted ňĻhim withňļ, ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ He ňĻlaterňļ said, ‚ÄúSurely we are afraid of you.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†As mentioned in 11:69-70, the angels came to Abraham in the form of men and when he offered them food, they did not eat. In ancient Middle Eastern culture, if a guest refused to eat the food provided by their host, it was a sign of ill-will.

15:53.text: They reassured ňĻhimňļ, ‚ÄúDo not be afraid! Surely we give you good news of a knowledgeable son.‚ÄĚ

15:54.text: He wondered, ‚ÄúDo you give me good news despite my old age? What unlikely news!‚ÄĚ

15:55.text: They responded, ‚ÄúWe give you good news in all truth, so do not be one of those who despair.‚ÄĚ

15:56.text: He exclaimed, ‚ÄúWho would despair of the mercy of their Lord except the misguided?‚ÄĚ

15:57.text: He ňĻthenňļ added, ‚ÄúWhat is your mission, O ¬†messenger-angels?‚ÄĚ

15:58.text: They replied, ‚ÄúWe have actually been sent to a wicked people.

15:59.text: As for the family of Lot, we will certainly deliver them all,

15:60.text: except his wife. We have determined that she will be one of the doomed.‚ÄĚ

15:61.text: So when the messenger-angels came to the family of Lot,

15:63.text: They responded, ‚ÄúWe have come to you with that ňĻtormentňļ which they have doubted.

15:64.text: We come to you with the truth, and we are certainly truthful.

15:65.text: So travel with your family in the dark of night, and follow ňĻcloselyňļ behind them. Do not let any of you look back, and go where you are commanded.‚ÄĚ

15:66.text: We revealed to him this decree: ‚ÄúThose ňĻsinnersňļ will be uprooted in the morning.‚ÄĚ

15:67.text: And there came the men of the city, rejoicing.

15:68.text: Lot pleaded, ‚ÄúIndeed, these are my guests, so do not embarrass me.

15:69.text: Fear Allah and do not humiliate me.‚ÄĚ

15:70.text: They responded, ‚ÄúHave we not forbidden you from protecting anyone?‚ÄĚ

15:71.text: He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Here are my daughters<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻso marry themňļ if you wish to do so.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Single women of his community.

15:72.text: By your life ňĻO Prophetňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†they certainly wandered blindly, intoxicated ňĻby lustňļ.
footnotes: ¬†This is the only time in the Quran where Allah swears by the life of a human being. Elsewhere Allah swears by the sun, the moon, the stars, and other marvels of His creation.

15:73.text: So the ňĻmightyňļ blast overtook them at sunrise.

15:74.text: And We turned the cities ňĻof Sodom and Gomorrahňļ upside down and rained upon them stones of baked clay.

15:75.text: Surely in this are signs for those who contemplate.

15:76.text: Their ruins still lie along a known route.

15:77.text: Surely in this is a sign for those who believe.

15:78.text: And the residents of the Forest were truly wrongdoers,

15:79.text: so We inflicted punishment upon them. The ruins of both nations still lie on a well-known road.

15:80.text: Indeed, the residents of the Stone Valley also denied the messengers.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., messengers, because denying ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• (Ô∑ļ) was equal to denying all of Allah‚Äôs messengers.

15:81.text: We gave them Our signs, but they turned away from them.

15:82.text: They carved their homes in the mountains, feeling secure.

15:83.text: But the ňĻmightyňļ blast overtook them in the morning,

15:84.text: and all they achieved was of no help to them.

15:85.text: We have not created the heavens and the earth and everything in between except for a purpose. And the Hour is certain to come, so forgive graciously.

15:86.text: Surely your Lord is the Master Creator, All-Knowing.

15:87.text: We have certainly granted you the seven often-repeated verses<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and the great Quran.
footnotes: ¬†S√Ľrah¬†1 of the Quran.

15:88.text: Do not let your eyes crave the ňĻfleetingňļ pleasures We have provided for some of the disbelievers, nor grieve for them. And be gracious to the believers.

15:89.text: And say, ‚ÄúI am truly sent with a clear warning‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒ

15:90.text: ňĻa warningňļ similar to what We sent to those who divided ňĻthe Scripturesňļ,

15:91.text: who ňĻnowňļ accept parts of the Quran, rejecting others.

15:92.text: So by your Lord! We will certainly question them all

15:93.text: about what they used to do.

15:94.text: So proclaim what you have been commanded, and turn away from the polytheists.

15:95.text: Surely We will be sufficient for you against the mockers,

15:96.text: who set up ňĻotherňļ gods with Allah. They will soon come to know.

15:97.text: We certainly know that your heart is truly distressed by what they say.

15:98.text: So glorify the praises of your Lord and be one of those who ňĻalwaysňļ pray,

15:99.text: and worship your Lord until the inevitable<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†comes your way.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., lit., what is certain‚ÄĒdeath.

16:1.text: The command of Allah is at hand, so do not hasten it. Glorified and Exalted is He above what they associate ňĻwith Him in worshipňļ!

16:2.text: He sends down the angels with revelation by His command to whoever He wills of His servants, ňĻstating:ňļ ‚ÄúWarn ňĻhumanityňļ that there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Me, so be mindful of Me ňĻaloneňļ.‚ÄĚ

16:3.text: He created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. Exalted is He above what they associate with Him ňĻin worshipňļ!

16:4.text: He created humans from a sperm-drop, then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒthey openly challenge ňĻHimňļ.

16:5.text: And He created the cattle for you as a source of warmth, food, and ňĻmany otherňļ benefits.

16:6.text: They are also pleasing to you when you bring them home and when you take them out to graze.

16:7.text: And they carry your loads to ňĻdistantňļ lands which you could not otherwise reach without great hardship. Surely your Lord is Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.

16:8.text: ňĻHe also createdňļ horses, mules, and donkeys for your transportation and adornment. And He creates what you do not know.

16:9.text: It is upon Allah ňĻaloneňļ to ňĻclearlyňļ show the Straight Way. Other ways are deviant. Had He willed, He would have easily imposed guidance upon all of you.

16:10.text: He is the One Who sends down rain from the sky, from which you drink and by which plants grow for your cattle to graze.

16:11.text: With it He produces for you ňĻvariousňļ crops, olives, palm trees, grapevines, and every type of fruit. Surely in this is a sign for those who reflect.

16:12.text: And He has subjected for your benefit the day and the night, the sun and the moon. And the stars have been subjected by His command. Surely in this are signs for those who understand.

16:13.text: And ňĻHe subjectedňļ for you whatever He has created on earth of varying colours.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely in this is a sign for those who are mindful.
footnotes: ¬†The verse refers to the diverse creatures that have been created for the service of humanity.

16:14.text: And He is the One Who has subjected the sea, so from it you may eat tender seafood and extract ornaments to wear. And you see the ships ploughing their way through it, so you may seek His bounty and give thanks ňĻto Himňļ.

16:15.text: He has placed into the earth firm mountains, so it does not shake with you, as well as rivers, and pathways so you may find your way.

16:16.text: Also by landmarks and stars do people find their way.

16:17.text: Can the One Who creates be equal to those who do not? Will you not then be mindful?

16:18.text: If you tried to count Allah‚Äôs blessings, you would never be able to number them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

16:19.text: And Allah knows what you conceal and what you reveal.

16:20.text: But those ňĻidolsňļ they invoke besides Allah cannot create anything‚ÄĒthey themselves are created.

16:21.text: They are dead, not alive‚ÄĒnot even knowing when their followers will be resurrected.

16:22.text: Your God is ňĻonlyňļ One God. As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, their hearts are in denial, and they are too proud.

16:23.text: Without a doubt, Allah knows what they conceal and what they reveal. He certainly does not like those who are too proud.

16:24.text: And when it is said to them, ‚ÄúWhat has your Lord revealed?‚ÄĚ They say, ‚ÄúAncient fables!‚ÄĚ

16:25.text: Let them bear their burdens in full on the Day of Judgment as well as some of the burdens of those they mislead without knowledge. Evil indeed is what they will bear!

16:26.text: Indeed, those before them had plotted, but Allah struck at the ňĻveryňļ foundation of their structure, so the roof collapsed on top of them, and the torment came upon them from where they did not expect.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This verse refers to a tyrant king who ordered the building of a tower so he can reach the heavens and fight Allah and the angels. Eventually the tower collapsed, leaving the tyrant crushed.

16:27.text: Then on the Day of Judgment He will humiliate them and say, ‚ÄúWhere are My ňĻso-calledňļ associate-gods for whose sake you used to oppose ňĻthe believersňļ?‚ÄĚ Those gifted with knowledge will say, ‚ÄúSurely disgrace and misery today are upon the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ

16:28.text: Those whose souls the angels seize while they wrong themselves will then offer ňĻfullňļ submission ňĻand say falsely,ňļ ‚ÄúWe did not do any evil.‚ÄĚ ňĻThe angels will say,ňļ ‚ÄúNo! Surely Allah fully knows what you used to do.

16:29.text: So enter the gates of Hell, to stay there forever. Indeed, what an evil home for the arrogant!‚ÄĚ

16:30.text: And ňĻwhenňļ it is said to those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, ‚ÄúWhat has your Lord revealed?‚ÄĚ They say, ‚ÄúAll the best!‚ÄĚ For those who do good in this world, there is goodness. But far better is the ňĻeternalňļ Home of the Hereafter. How excellent indeed is the home of the righteous:

16:31.text: the Gardens of Eternity which they will enter, under which rivers flow. In it they will have whatever they desire. This is how Allah rewards the righteous‚ÄĒ

16:32.text: those whose souls the angels take while they are virtuous, saying ňĻto themňļ, ‚ÄúPeace be upon you! Enter Paradise for what you used to do.‚ÄĚ

16:33.text: Are they only awaiting the coming of the angels or the command of your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ? So were those before them. And Allah never wronged them, but it was they who wronged themselves.

16:34.text: Then the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds overtook them, and they were overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.

16:35.text: The polytheists argue, ‚ÄúHad Allah willed, neither we nor our forefathers would have worshipped anything other than Him, nor prohibited anything without His command.‚ÄĚ So did those before them. Is not the messengers‚Äô duty only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly?

16:36.text: We surely sent a messenger to every community, saying, ‚ÄúWorship Allah and shun false gods.‚ÄĚ But some of them were guided by Allah, while others were destined to stray. So travel throughout the land and see the fate of the deniers!

16:37.text: Even though you ňĻO Prophetňļ are keen on their guidance, Allah certainly does not guide those He leaves to stray, and they will have no helpers.

16:38.text: They swear by Allah their most solemn oaths that Allah will never raise the dead to life. Yes ňĻHe willňļ! It is a true promise binding on Him, but most people do not know.

16:39.text: ňĻHe will do thatňļ to make clear to them what they disagreed on, and for the disbelievers to know that they were liars.

16:40.text: If We ever will something ňĻto existňļ, all We say is: ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And it is!

16:41.text: As for those who emigrated in ňĻthe cause ofňļ Allah after being persecuted, We will surely bless them with a good home in this world. But the reward of the Hereafter is far better, if only they knew.

16:42.text: ňĻIt isňļ they who have patiently endured, and in their Lord they put their trust.

16:43.text: We did not send ňĻmessengersňļ before you ňĻO Prophetňļ except mere men inspired by Us. If you ňĻpolytheistsňļ do not know ňĻthis alreadyňļ, then ask those who have knowledge ňĻof the Scripturesňļ.

16:44.text: ňĻWe sent themňļ with clear proofs and divine Books. And We have sent down to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the Reminder, so that you may explain to people what has been revealed for them, and perhaps they will reflect.

16:45.text: Do those who devise evil plots feel secure that Allah will not cause the earth to swallow them? Or that the torment will not come upon them in ways they cannot comprehend?

16:46.text: Or that He will not seize them while they go about ňĻtheir dayňļ, for then they will have no escape?

16:47.text: Or that He will not destroy them gradually? But your Lord is truly Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.

16:48.text: Have they not considered how the shadows of everything Allah has created incline to the right and the left ňĻas the sun movesňļ, totally submitting to Allah in all humility?

16:49.text: And to Allah ňĻaloneňļ bows down ňĻin submissionňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth of living creatures, as do the angels‚ÄĒwho are not too proud ňĻto do soňļ.
footnotes: ¬†lit., prostrates.

16:50.text: They<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†fear their Lord above them, and do whatever they are commanded.
footnotes: ¬†The angels.

16:51.text: And Allah has said, ‚ÄúDo not take two gods. There is only One God. So be in awe of Me ňĻaloneňļ.‚ÄĚ

16:52.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and to Him ňĻaloneňļ is the everlasting devotion. Will you then fear any other than Allah?

16:53.text: Whatever blessings you have are from Allah. Then whenever hardship touches you, to Him ňĻaloneňļ you cry ňĻfor helpňļ.

16:54.text: Then as soon as He removes the hardship from you, a group of you associates ňĻothersňļ with their Lord ňĻin worshipňļ,

16:55.text: only returning Our favours with ingratitude. So enjoy yourselves, for you will soon know.

16:56.text: And they ňĻevenňļ assign to those ňĻidolsňļ‚ÄĒwho know nothing‚ÄĒa share of what We have provided for them. By Allah! You will certainly be questioned about whatever ňĻliesňļ you used to fabricate ňĻagainst Allahňļ.

16:57.text: And they attribute ňĻangels asňļ daughters to Allah‚ÄĒglory be to Him!‚ÄĒthe opposite of what they desire for themselves.

16:58.text: Whenever one of them is given the good news of a baby girl, his face grows gloomy, as he suppresses his rage.

16:59.text: He hides himself from the people because of the bad news he has received. Should he keep her in disgrace, or bury her ňĻaliveňļ in the ground? Evil indeed is their judgment!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Some pagan Arabs used to bury their infant daughters alive for fear of shame or poverty. This practice was condemned and abolished by Islam. See¬†6:151 and 81:8-9.

16:60.text: To those who disbelieve in the Hereafter belong all evil qualities, whereas to Allah belong the finest attributes. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

16:61.text: If Allah were to punish people ňĻimmediatelyňļ for their wrongdoing, He would not have left a single living being on earth. But He delays them for an appointed term. And when their time arrives, they cannot delay it for a moment, nor could they advance it.

16:62.text: They attribute to Allah what they hate ňĻfor themselvesňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and their tongues utter the lie that they will have the finest reward. Without a doubt, for them is the Fire, where they will be abandoned.
footnotes: ¬†They say that Allah has daughters (i.e., the angels), although they themselves do not like to have daughters.

16:63.text: By Allah! We have surely sent messengers to communities before you ňĻO Prophetňļ, but Satan made their misdeeds appealing to them. So he is their patron today, and they will suffer a painful punishment.

16:64.text: We have revealed to you the Book only to clarify for them what they differed about, and as a guide and mercy for those who believe.

16:65.text: And Allah sends down rain from the sky, giving life to the earth after its death. Surely in this is a sign for those who listen.

16:66.text: And there is certainly a lesson for you in cattle: We give you to drink of what is in their bellies, from between digested food and blood: pure milk, pleasant to drink.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., pleasant for those who drink.

16:67.text: And from the fruits of palm trees and grapevines you derive intoxicants<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as well as wholesome provision. Surely in this is a sign for those who understand.
footnotes: ¬†Intoxicants were later prohibited in the Quran in three stages: 2:219, 4:43, and finally 5:90-91.

16:68.text: And your Lord inspired the bees: ‚ÄúMake ňĻyourňļ homes in the mountains, the trees, and in what people construct,

16:69.text: and feed from ňĻthe flower ofňļ any fruit ňĻyou pleaseňļ and follow the ways your Lord has made easy for you.‚ÄĚ From their bellies comes forth liquid of varying colours, in which there is healing for people. Surely in this is a sign for those who reflect.

16:70.text: Allah has created you, and then causes you to die. And some of you are left to reach the most feeble stage of life so that they may know nothing after having known much. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, Most Capable.

16:71.text: And Allah has favoured some of you over others in provision. But those who have been much favoured would not share their wealth with those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in their possession, making them their equals.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Do they then deny Allah‚Äôs favours?
footnotes: ¬†In other words, if the pagan slave-masters are not willing to be equal to their slaves, why would they make anyone equal to Allah‚ÄĒthe Supreme Master and Creator of all things?

16:72.text: And Allah has made for you spouses of your own kind, and given you through your spouses children and grandchildren.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And He has granted you good, lawful provisions. Are they then faithful to falsehood and ungrateful for Allah‚Äôs favours?
footnotes: ¬†Šł§afadah¬†means grandchildren, but can also mean children-in-law.

16:73.text: Yet they worship besides Allah those ňĻidolsňļ who do not afford them any provision from the heavens and the earth, nor do they have the power to.

16:74.text: So do not set up equals to Allah, for Allah certainly knows and you do not know.

16:75.text: Allah sets forth a parable: a slave who lacks all means, compared to a ňĻfreeňļ man to whom We granted a good provision, of which he donates ňĻfreely,ňļ openly and secretly. Are they equal? Praise be to Allah. In fact, most of them do not know.

16:76.text: And Allah sets forth a parable of two men: one of them is dumb, incapable of anything. He is a burden on his master. Wherever he is sent, he brings no good. Can such a person be equal to the one who commands justice and is on the Straight Path?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The two parables emphasize that Almighty Allah masterfully conducts the affairs of the heavens and the earth, whereas false gods are incapable of anything. If this is the case, these gods are not His equal and He is the only one worthy of worship.

16:77.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs ňĻthe knowledge ofňļ the unseen in the heavens and the earth. Bringing about the Hour would only take the blink of an eye, or even less. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

16:78.text: And Allah brought you out of the wombs of your mothers while you knew nothing, and gave you hearing, sight, and intellect so perhaps you would be thankful.

16:79.text: Have they not seen the birds glide in the open sky? None holds them up except Allah. Surely in this are signs for those who believe.

16:80.text: And Allah has made your homes a place to rest, and has given you tents from the hide of animals, light to handle when you travel and when you camp. And out of their wool, fur, and hair He has given you furnishings and goods for a while.

16:81.text: And Allah has provided you shade out of what He created, and has given you shelter in the mountains. He has also provided you with clothes protecting you from the heat ňĻand coldňļ, and armour shielding you in battle. This is how He perfects His favour upon you, so perhaps you will ňĻfullyňļ submit ňĻto Himňļ.

16:82.text: But if they turn away, then your duty ňĻO Prophetňļ is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.

16:83.text: They are aware of Allah‚Äôs favours, but still deny them. And most of them are ňĻtrulyňļ ungrateful.

16:84.text: ňĻConsider, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ the Day We will call ňĻa prophet asňļ a witness from every faith-community. Then the disbelievers will neither be allowed to plead nor appease ňĻtheir Lordňļ.

16:85.text: And when the wrongdoers face the punishment, it will not be lightened for them, nor will they be delayed ňĻfrom itňļ.

16:86.text: And when the polytheists see their associate-gods, they will say, ‚ÄúOur Lord! These are our associate-gods that we used to invoke besides You.‚ÄĚ Their gods will throw a rebuttal at them, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúYou are definitely liars.‚ÄĚ

16:87.text: They will offer ňĻfullňļ submission to Allah on that Day, and whatever ňĻgodsňļ they fabricated will fail them.

16:88.text: For those who disbelieve and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, We will add more punishment to their punishment for all the corruption they spread.

16:89.text: ňĻConsider, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ the Day We will call against every faith-community a witness of their own. And We will call you to be a witness against these ňĻpeople of yoursňļ. We have revealed to you the Book as an explanation of all things, a guide, a mercy, and good news for those who ňĻfullyňļ submit.

16:90.text: Indeed, Allah commands justice, grace, as well as generosity to close relatives. He forbids indecency, wickedness, and aggression. He instructs you so perhaps you will be mindful.

16:91.text: Honour Allah‚Äôs covenant when you make a pledge, and do not break your oaths after confirming them, having made Allah your guarantor. Surely Allah knows all you do.

16:92.text: Do not be like the woman who ňĻfoolishlyňļ unravels her yarn after it is firmly spun, by taking your oaths as a means of deceiving one another in favour of a stronger group. Surely Allah tests you through this. And on the Day of Judgment He will certainly make your differences clear to you.

16:93.text: Had Allah willed, He could have easily made you one community ňĻof believersňļ, but He leaves to stray whoever He wills and guides whoever He wills.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And you will certainly be questioned about what you used to do.
footnotes: ¬†He guides those who are sincere in their quest for guidance.

16:94.text: And do not take your oaths as a means of deceiving one another or your feet will slip after they have been firm. Then you will taste the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of hindering ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, and you will suffer a tremendous punishment.

16:95.text: And do not trade Allah‚Äôs covenant for a fleeting gain. What is with Allah is certainly far better for you, if only you knew.

16:96.text: Whatever you have will end, but whatever Allah has is everlasting. And We will certainly reward the steadfast according to the best of their deeds.

16:97.text: Whoever does good, whether male or female, and is a believer, We will surely bless them with a good life, and We will certainly reward them according to the best of their deeds.

16:98.text: When you recite the Quran, seek refuge with Allah from Satan, the accursed.

16:99.text: He certainly has no authority over those who believe and put their trust in their Lord.

16:100.text: His authority is only over those who take him as a patron and who‚ÄĒunder his influence‚ÄĒassociate ňĻothersňļ with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ.

16:101.text: When We replace a verse with another<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒand Allah knows best what He reveals‚ÄĒthey say, ‚ÄúYou ňĻMuŠł•ammadňļ are just a fabricator.‚ÄĚ In fact, most of them do not know.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:106.

16:102.text: Say, ‚ÄúThe holy spirit<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†has brought it down from your Lord with the truth to reassure the believers, and as a guide and good news for those who submit ňĻto Allahňļ.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The angel Gabriel.

16:103.text: And We surely know that they say, ‚ÄúNo one is teaching him except a human.‚ÄĚ But the man they refer to speaks a foreign tongue, whereas this ňĻQuranňļ is ňĻinňļ eloquent Arabic.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Some Meccan pagans claimed that the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) received the Quran from a non-Arab slave owned by an Arab pagan.

16:104.text: Surely those who do not believe in Allah‚Äôs revelations will never be guided by Allah, and they will suffer a painful punishment.

16:105.text: No one fabricates lies except those who disbelieve in Allah‚Äôs revelations, and it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ liars.

16:106.text: Whoever disbelieves in Allah after their belief‚ÄĒnot those who are forced while their hearts are firm in faith,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but those who embrace disbelief wholeheartedly‚ÄĒthey will be condemned by Allah and suffer a tremendous punishment.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to ‚ÄôAmm√Ęr ibn Y√Ęsser, an early revert to Islam, who was tortured to leave Islam. To save his life, ‚ÄôAmm√Ęr pretended to denounce Islam, but his heart was full of faith. When he told the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) about what happened, this verse was revealed, reassuring him that his faith was intact.

16:107.text: This is because they prefer the life of this world over the Hereafter. Surely Allah never guides those who ňĻchoose toňļ disbelieve.

16:108.text: They are the ones whose hearts, ears, and eyes are sealed by Allah, and it is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ heedless.

16:109.text: Without a doubt, they will be the losers in the Hereafter.

16:110.text: As for those who emigrated after being compelled ňĻto renounce Islamňļ, then struggled ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ, and persevered, your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful after all.

16:111.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ every soul will come pleading for itself, and each will be paid in full for what it did, and none will be wronged.

16:112.text: And Allah sets forth the example of a society which was safe and at ease, receiving its provision in abundance from all directions. But its people met Allah‚Äôs favours with ingratitude, so Allah made them taste the clutches of hunger and fear for their misdeeds.

16:113.text: A messenger of their own actually did come to them, but they denied him. So the torment overtook them while they persisted in wrongdoing.

16:114.text: So eat from the good, lawful things which Allah has provided for you, and be grateful for Allah‚Äôs favours, if you ňĻtrulyňļ worship Him ňĻaloneňļ.

16:115.text: He has only forbidden you ňĻto eatňļ carrion, blood, swine,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and what is slaughtered in the name of any other than Allah. But if someone is compelled by necessity‚ÄĒneither driven by desire nor exceeding immediate need‚ÄĒthen surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:173.

16:116.text: Do not falsely declare with your tongues, ‚ÄúThis is lawful, and that is unlawful,‚ÄĚ ňĻonlyňļ fabricating lies against Allah. Indeed, those who fabricate lies against Allah will never succeed.

16:117.text: ňĻIt is onlyňļ a brief enjoyment, then they will suffer a painful punishment.

16:118.text: To the Jews, We have forbidden what We related to you before.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We did not wrong them, but it was they who wronged themselves.
footnotes: ¬†What is mentioned in 6:146.

16:119.text: As for those who commit evil ignorantly ňĻor recklesslyňļ, then repent afterwards and mend their ways, then your Lord is surely All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

16:120.text: Indeed, Abraham was a model of excellence: devoted to Allah, ňĻperfectlyňļ upright‚ÄĒnot a polytheist‚ÄĒ

16:121.text: ňĻutterlyňļ grateful for Allah‚Äôs favours. ňĻSoňļ He chose him and guided him to the Straight Path.

16:122.text: We blessed him with all goodness in this world,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and in the Hereafter he will certainly be among the righteous.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., honourable mention and a righteous family.

16:123.text: Then We revealed to you ňĻO Prophet, sayingňļ: ‚ÄúFollow the faith of Abraham, the upright, who was not one of the polytheists.‚ÄĚ

16:124.text: ňĻHonouringňļ the Sabbath was ordained only for those who disputed about Abraham.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their disputes.
footnotes: ¬†This verse refers to the Jews who claimed that Abraham was Jewish. See¬†3:65-68.

16:125.text: Invite ňĻallňļ to the Way of your Lord with wisdom and kind advice, and only debate with them in the best manner. Surely your Lord ňĻaloneňļ knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

16:126.text: If you retaliate, then let it be equivalent to what you have suffered. But if you patiently endure, it is certainly best for those who are patient.

16:127.text: Be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ, for your patience is only with Allah‚Äôs help. Do not grieve over those ňĻwho disbelieveňļ, nor be distressed by their schemes.

16:128.text: Surely Allah is with those who shun evil and who do good ňĻdeedsňļ.

17:1.text: Glory be to the One Who took His servant ňĻMuŠł•ammadňļ by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque whose surroundings We have blessed, so that We may show him some of Our signs.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Indeed, He<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†alone is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
footnotes: ¬†Al-Isr√Ę' refers to the Prophet‚Äôs Night Journey from Mecca to Jerusalem about a year before his emigration (or Hijrah) from Mecca to Medina. This journey came as a comfort for the Prophet after several years of hardship and persecution, which included a 3-year siege by Meccan pagans, who drove the Muslims out of the city and forbade anyone from trading, marrying, or feeding them. This was followed by the ‚ÄúYear of Sadness,‚ÄĚ which included the death of the Prophet‚Äôs uncle Abu ŠĻ¨√Ęlib, the major defender of MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) despite his disbelief in his message, as well as the death of the Prophet‚Äôs beloved wife Khad√ģjah. The Prophet was carried overnight by a noble steed (called Bur√Ęq) from Mecca to Jerusalem where he met some earlier prophets and led them in prayer. He was later carried to the heavens (this journey is called Al-Mi‚Äôr√Ęj, or the Ascension) where he received direct orders from Allah to observe five daily prayers. The Ascension is referred to in 53:13‚Äď18.
¬†i.e., Allah.

17:2.text: And We gave Moses the Scripture and made it a guide for the Children of Israel, ňĻstating:ňļ ‚ÄúDo not take besides Me any other Trustee of Affairs,

17:3.text: ňĻOňļ descendants of those We carried with Noah ňĻin the Arkňļ! He was indeed a grateful servant.‚ÄĚ

17:4.text: And We warned the Children of Israel in the Scripture, ‚ÄúYou will certainly cause corruption in the land twice, and you will become extremely arrogant.

17:5.text: When the first of the two warnings would come to pass, We would send against you some of Our servants of great might, who would ravage your homes. This would be a warning fulfilled.

17:6.text: Then ňĻafter your repentanceňļ We would give you the upper hand over them and aid you with wealth and offspring, causing you to outnumber them.

17:7.text: If you act rightly, it is for your own good, but if you do wrong, it is to your own loss. ‚ÄúAnd when the second warning would come to pass, your enemies would ňĻbe left toňļ totally disgrace you and enter that place of worship as they entered it the first time, and utterly destroy whatever would fall into their hands.

17:8.text: Perhaps your Lord will have mercy on you ňĻif you repentňļ, but if you return ňĻto sinňļ, We will return ňĻto punishmentňļ. And We have made Hell a ňĻpermanentňļ confinement for the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ

17:9.text: Surely this Quran guides to what is most upright, and gives good news to the believers‚ÄĒwho do good‚ÄĒthat they will have a mighty reward.

17:10.text: And ňĻit warnsňļ those who do not believe in the Hereafter ňĻthatňļ We have prepared for them a painful punishment.

17:11.text: And humans ňĻswiftlyňļ pray for evil<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as they pray for good. For humankind is ever hasty.
footnotes: ¬†Some people are quick to pray against themselves or others in times of anger and frustration.

17:12.text: We made the day and night as two signs. So We made the sign of the night devoid of light, and We made the sign of the day ňĻperfectlyňļ bright, so that you may seek the bounty of your Lord and know the number of years and calculation ňĻof timeňļ. And We have explained everything in detail.

17:13.text: We have bound every human‚Äôs destiny to their neck.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And on the Day of Judgment We will bring forth to each ňĻpersonňļ a record which they will find laid open.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the deeds that one is destined to do of their own free will. Out of His infinite knowledge, Allah knows people‚Äôs choices even before they make them. In the Hereafter, He will raise them from the dead for judgment.

17:14.text: ňĻAnd it will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúRead your record. You ňĻaloneňļ are sufficient this Day to take account of yourself.‚ÄĚ

17:15.text: Whoever chooses to be guided, it is only for their own good. And whoever chooses to stray, it is only to their own loss. No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. And We would never punish ňĻa peopleňļ until We have sent a messenger ňĻto warn themňļ.

17:16.text: Whenever We intend to destroy a society, We command its elite ňĻto obey Allahňļ but they act rebelliously in it. So the decree ňĻof punishmentňļ is justified, and We destroy it utterly.

17:17.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many peoples We have destroyed after Noah! And sufficient is your Lord as All-Aware and All-Seeing of the sins of His servants.

17:18.text: Whoever desires this fleeting world ňĻaloneňļ, We hasten in it whatever We please to whoever We will; then We destine them for Hell, where they will burn, condemned and rejected.

17:19.text: But whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it accordingly, and is a ňĻtrueňļ believer, it is they whose striving will be appreciated.

17:20.text: We provide both the former and the latter from the bounty of your Lord. And the bounty of your Lord can never be withheld.

17:21.text: See how We have favoured some over others ňĻin this lifeňļ, but the Hereafter is certainly far greater in rank and in favour.

17:22.text: Do not set up any other god with Allah, or you will end up condemned, abandoned.

17:23.text: For your Lord has decreed that you worship none but Him. And honour your parents. If one or both of them reach old age in your care, never say to them ňĻevenňļ ‚Äėugh,‚Äô nor yell at them. Rather, address them respectfully.

17:24.text: And be humble with them out of mercy, and pray, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Be merciful to them as they raised me when I was young.‚ÄĚ

17:25.text: Your Lord knows best what is within yourselves. If you are righteous, He is certainly All-Forgiving to those who ňĻconstantlyňļ turn to Him.

17:26.text: Give to close relatives their due, as well as the poor and ňĻneedyňļ travellers. And do not spend wastefully.

17:27.text: Surely the wasteful are ňĻlikeňļ brothers to the devils. And the Devil is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

17:28.text: But if you must turn them down ňĻbecause you lack the means to giveňļ‚ÄĒwhile hoping to receive your Lord‚Äôs bounty‚ÄĒthen ňĻat leastňļ give them a kind word.

17:29.text: Do not be so tight-fisted, for you will be blameworthy; nor so open-handed, for you will end up in poverty.

17:30.text: Surely your Lord gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. He is certainly All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.

17:31.text: Do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Surely killing them is a heinous sin.

17:32.text: Do not go near adultery. It is truly a shameful deed and an evil way.

17:33.text: Do not take a ňĻhumanňļ life‚ÄĒmade sacred by Allah‚ÄĒexcept with ňĻlegalňļ right.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†If anyone is killed unjustly, We have given their heirs<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†the authority, but do not let them exceed limits in retaliation,<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†for they are already supported ňĻby lawňļ.
footnotes: ¬†For example, in retaliation for intentional killing through legal channels.
¬†Here the word wali,¬†‚Äúheir,‚ÄĚ actually means awliy√Ę' or ‚Äúheirs,‚ÄĚ which includes the closest relatives, both men and women.
¬†By killing anyone other than the killer, or killing others with the killer.

17:34.text: Do not come near the wealth of the orphan‚ÄĒunless intending to enhance it‚ÄĒuntil they attain maturity. Honour ňĻyourňļ pledges, for you will surely be accountable for them.

17:35.text: Give in full when you measure, and weigh with an even balance. That is fairest and best in the end.

17:36.text: Do not follow what you have no ňĻsureňļ knowledge of. Indeed, all will be called to account for ňĻtheirňļ hearing, sight, and intellect.

17:37.text: And do not walk on the earth arrogantly. Surely you can neither crack the earth nor stretch to the height of the mountains.

17:38.text: The violation of any of these ňĻcommandmentsňļ is detestable to your Lord.

17:39.text: This is part of the wisdom which your Lord has revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. And do not set up any other god with Allah ňĻO humanityňļ, or you will be cast into Hell, blameworthy, rejected.

17:40.text: Has your Lord favoured you ňĻpagansňļ with sons and taken angels as ňĻHisňļ daughters?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†You are truly making an outrageous claim.
footnotes: ¬†Some pagans had the belief that the angels are the daughters of Allah.

17:41.text: We have surely varied ňĻthe signsňļ in this Quran so perhaps they may be mindful, but it only drives them farther away.

17:42.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHad there been other gods besides Him‚ÄĒas they claim‚ÄĒthen they would have certainly sought a way to ňĻchallengeňļ the Lord of the Throne.‚ÄĚ

17:43.text: Glorified and Highly Exalted is He above what they claim!

17:44.text: The seven heavens, the earth, and all those in them glorify Him. There is not a single thing that does not glorify His praises‚ÄĒbut you ňĻsimplyňļ cannot comprehend their glorification. He is indeed Most Forbearing, All-Forgiving.

17:45.text: When you ňĻO Prophetňļ recite the Quran, We put a hidden barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter.

17:46.text: We have cast veils over their hearts‚ÄĒleaving them unable to comprehend it‚ÄĒand deafness in their ears. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Quran, they turn their backs in aversion.

17:47.text: We know best how they listen to your recitation and what they say privately‚ÄĒwhen the wrongdoers say, ‚ÄúYou would only be following a bewitched man.‚ÄĚ

17:48.text: See how they call you names ňĻO Prophetňļ!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So they have gone so ňĻfarňļ astray that they cannot find the ňĻRightňļ Way.
footnotes: ¬†They rejected you as a magician, a poet, a fabricator, and madman.

17:49.text: And they say ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen we are reduced to bones and ashes, will we really be raised as a new creation?‚ÄĚ

17:50.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻYes, even ifňļ you become stones, or iron,

17:51.text: or whatever you think is harder to bring to life!‚ÄĚ Then they will ask ňĻyouňļ, ‚ÄúWho will bring us back ňĻto lifeňļ?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúThe One Who created you the first time.‚ÄĚ They will then shake their heads at you and ask, ‚ÄúWhen will that be?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúPerhaps it is soon!‚ÄĚ

17:52.text: On the Day He will call you, you will ňĻinstantlyňļ respond by praising Him,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†thinking you had remained ňĻin the worldňļ only for a little while.
footnotes: ¬†When everyone is raised from the dead, they will all praise Allah, regardless of what they had believed in this life.

17:53.text: Tell My ňĻbelievingňļ servants to say only what is best. Satan certainly seeks to sow discord among them. Satan is indeed a sworn enemy to humankind.

17:54.text: Your Lord knows you best. He may have mercy on you if He wills, or punish you if He wills. We have not sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a keeper over them.

17:55.text: Your Lord knows best all those in the heavens and the earth. And We have surely favoured some prophets above others, and to David We gave the Psalms.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The reason David is singled out here is because some Jewish authorities at the time of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) claimed that no scripture had been revealed after Moses. So this verse refutes this claim by referring to the Psalms of David.

17:56.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúInvoke those you claim ňĻto be divineňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†besides Him‚ÄĒthey do not have the power to undo harm from you or transfer it ňĻto someone elseňļ.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Jesus, Ezra, and the angels. Idol worship is refuted in other passages such as 7:191-198 and 34:22.

17:57.text: ňĻEvenňļ the closest ňĻto Allahňļ of those invoked would be seeking a way to their Lord, hoping for His mercy, and fearing His punishment. Indeed, your Lord‚Äôs torment is fearsome.

17:58.text: There is not a ňĻwickedňļ society that We will not destroy or punish with a severe torment before the Day of Judgment. That is written in the Record.

17:59.text: Nothing keeps Us from sending the ňĻdemandedňļ signs except that they had ňĻalreadyňļ been denied by earlier peoples. And We gave Tham√Ľd the she-camel as a clear sign, but they wrongfully rejected it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We only send the signs as a warning.
footnotes: ¬†Or they did wrong by it.

17:60.text: And ňĻremember, O ¬†Prophetňļ when We told you, ‚ÄúCertainly your Lord encompasses the people.‚ÄĚ And We have made what We brought you to see<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as well as the cursed tree ňĻmentionedňļ in the Quran<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†only as a test for the people. We keep warning them, but it only increases them greatly in defiance.
footnotes: ¬†During the Night Journey mentioned in 17:1.
¬†The tree of Zaqq√Ľm¬†which grows in the depths of Hell as mentioned in 37:62-65. The pagans of Mecca used to make fun of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and say, ‚ÄúHow can a tree grow in Hell?‚ÄĚ

17:61.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We said to the angels, ‚ÄúProstrate before Adam,‚ÄĚ so they all did‚ÄĒbut not Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who protested, ‚ÄúShould I prostrate to the one You have created from mud?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:34.

17:62.text: Adding, ‚ÄúDo you see this one you honoured above me? If you delay my end until the Day of Judgment, I will certainly take hold of his descendants, except for a few.‚ÄĚ

17:63.text: Allah responded, ‚ÄúBe gone! Whoever of them follows you, Hell will surely be the reward for all of you‚ÄĒan ample reward.

17:64.text: And incite whoever you can of them with your voice, mobilize against them all your cavalry and infantry, manipulate them in their wealth and children, and make them promises.‚ÄĚ But Satan promises them nothing but delusion.

17:65.text: ňĻAllah added,ňļ ‚ÄúYou will truly have no authority over My ňĻfaithfulňļ servants.‚ÄĚ And sufficient is your Lord as a Guardian.

17:66.text: It is your Lord Who steers the ships for you through the sea, so that you may seek His bounty. Surely He is ever Merciful to you.

17:67.text: When you are touched with hardship at sea, you ňĻtotallyňļ forget all ňĻthe godsňļ you ňĻnormallyňļ invoke, except Him. But when He delivers you ňĻsafelyňļ to shore, you turn away. Humankind is ever ungrateful.

17:68.text: Do you feel secure that He will not cause the land to swallow you up, or unleash upon you a storm of stones? Then you will find none to protect you.

17:69.text: Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back to sea once again, and send upon you a violent storm, drowning you for your denial? Then you will find none to avenge you against Us.

17:70.text: Indeed, We have dignified the children of Adam, carried them on land and sea, granted them good and lawful provisions, and privileged them far above many of Our creatures.

17:71.text: ňĻBeware ofňļ the Day We will summon every people with their leader.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So whoever will be given their record in their right hand will read it ňĻhappilyňļ and will not be wronged ňĻeven by the width ofňļ the thread of a date stone.
footnotes: ¬†Other meanings include: with their record of deeds, or their prophet, or their scripture.

17:72.text: But whoever is blind ňĻto the truthňļ in this ňĻworldňļ will be blind in the Hereafter, and ňĻevenňļ far more astray from the ňĻRightňļ Way.

17:73.text: They definitely ňĻthought theyňļ were about to lure you away from what We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, hoping that you would attribute something else to Us falsely‚ÄĒand then they would have certainly taken you as a close friend.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The pagans of Mecca tried in vain to dissuade the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) from preaching the message of Islam. In some instances they offered to worship his Allah if he agreed to bow to their idols, and some even offered him riches and positions if he just stopped preaching in the city.

17:74.text: Had We not made you steadfast, you probably would have inclined to them a little,

17:75.text: and then We truly would have made you taste double ňĻpunishmentňļ both in this life and after death, and you would have found no helper against Us.

17:76.text: They were about to intimidate you to drive you out of the land ňĻof Meccaňļ, but then they would not have survived after you ňĻhad leftňļ except for a little while.

17:77.text: ňĻThis has beenňļ Our way with the messengers We sent before you. And you will never find any change in Our way.

17:78.text: Observe the prayer from the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night and the dawn prayer, for certainly the dawn prayer is witnessed ňĻby angelsňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This verse gives the times of the five daily prayers: the decline of the sun refers to the afternoon and late afternoon prayers, the darkness of night refers to sunset and late evening prayers, then the dawn prayer.

17:79.text: And rise at ňĻthe lastňļ part of the night, offering additional prayers, so your Lord may raise you to a station of praise.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the time when the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) will make intercession (shaf√Ę‚Äôah) on the Day of Judgment.

17:80.text: And say, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me an honourable entrance and an honourable exit<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and give me a supporting authority from Yourself.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This verse was revealed when the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) received the order to emigrate from Mecca to Medina. So here he (Ô∑ļ) is praying to leave Mecca honourably and enter Medina honourably. It can also apply to any worldly activity that a person starts and finishes.

17:81.text: And declare, ‚ÄúThe truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is bound to vanish.‚ÄĚ

17:82.text: We send down the Quran as a healing and mercy for the believers, but it only increases the wrongdoers in loss.

17:83.text: When We grant people Our favours, they turn away, acting arrogantly. But when touched with evil, they lose all hope.

17:84.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEveryone acts in their own way. But your Lord knows best whose way is rightly guided.‚ÄĚ

17:85.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ about the spirit. Say, ‚ÄúIts nature is known only to my Lord, and you ňĻO humanityňļ have been given but little knowledge.‚ÄĚ

17:86.text: If We willed, We could have certainly taken away what We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒthen you would find none to guarantee its return from Us‚ÄĒ

17:87.text: had it not been for the mercy of your Lord. Indeed, His favour upon you is immense.

17:88.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf ňĻallňļ humans and jinn were to come together to produce the equivalent of this Quran, they could not produce its equal, no matter how they supported each other.‚ÄĚ

17:89.text: And We have truly set forth every ňĻkind ofňļ lesson for humanity in this Quran, yet most people persist in disbelief.

17:90.text: They challenge ňĻthe Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWe will never believe in you until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us,

17:91.text: or until you have a garden of palm trees and vineyards, and cause rivers to flow abundantly in it,

17:92.text: or cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces, as you have claimed, or bring Allah and the angels before us, face to face,

17:93.text: or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into heaven‚ÄĒand even then we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we can read.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúGlory be to my Lord! Am I not only a human messenger?‚ÄĚ

17:94.text: And nothing has prevented people from believing when guidance comes to them except their protest: ‚ÄúHas Allah sent a human as a messenger?‚ÄĚ

17:95.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHad there been angels walking the earth, well settled, We would have surely sent down for them an angel from heaven as a messenger.‚ÄĚ

17:96.text: Say, ‚ÄúSufficient is Allah as a Witness between me and you. He is certainly All-Knowing, All-Seeing of His servants.‚ÄĚ

17:97.text: Whoever Allah guides is truly guided. And whoever He leaves to stray, you will find no guardians for them besides Him. And We will drag them on their faces on the Day of Judgment‚ÄĒdeaf, dumb, and blind. Hell will be their home. Whenever it dies down, We will flare it up for them.

17:98.text: That is their reward for rejecting Our signs and asking ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen we are reduced to bones and ashes, will we really be raised as a new creation?‚ÄĚ

17:99.text: Have they not realized that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, can ňĻeasilyňļ re-create them? He has ňĻalreadyňļ set for them a time, about which there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers persist in denial.

17:100.text: Say ňĻto them, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúEven if you were to possess the ňĻinfiniteňļ treasuries of my Lord‚Äôs mercy, then you would certainly withhold ňĻthemňļ, fearing they would run out‚ÄĒfor humankind is ever stingy!‚ÄĚ

17:101.text: We surely gave Moses nine clear signs.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻYou, O ¬†Prophet, canňļ ask the Children of Israel. When Moses came to them, Pharaoh said to him, ‚ÄúI really think that you, O ¬†Moses, are bewitched.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The nine signs of Moses are: the staff, the hand (both mentioned in 20:17-22), famine, shortage of crops, floods, locusts, lice, frogs, and blood (all mentioned in 7:130-133). These signs came as proofs for Pharaoh and the Egyptians. Otherwise, Moses had some other signs such water gushing out of the rock after he hit it with his staff, and splitting the sea.

17:102.text: Moses replied, ‚ÄúYou know well that none has sent these ňĻsignsňļ down except the Lord of the heavens and the earth as insights. And I really think that you, O ¬†Pharaoh, are doomed.‚ÄĚ

17:103.text: So Pharaoh wanted to scare the Israelites out of the land ňĻof Egyptňļ, but We drowned him and all of those with him.

17:104.text: And We said to the Children of Israel after Pharaoh, ‚ÄúReside in the land, but when the promise of the Hereafter comes to pass,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We will bring you all together.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This may also refer to the second warning in 17:7.

17:105.text: We have sent down the Quran in truth, and with the truth it has come down. We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner.

17:106.text: ňĻIt isňļ a Quran We have revealed in stages so that you may recite it to people at a deliberate pace. And We have sent it down in successive revelations.

17:107.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBelieve in this ňĻQuranňļ, or do not. Indeed, when it is recited to those who were gifted with knowledge<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†before it ňĻwas revealedňļ, they fall upon their faces in prostration,
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the faithful among the scholars of the Torah and the Gospel.

17:108.text: and say, ‚ÄėGlory be to our Lord! Surely the promise of our Lord has been fulfilled.‚Äô
