17:109.text: And they fall down upon their faces weeping, and it increases them in humility.‚ÄĚ

17:110.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúCall upon Allah or call upon the Most Compassionate‚ÄĒwhichever you call, He has the Most Beautiful Names.‚ÄĚ Do not recite your prayers too loudly or silently, but seek a way between.

17:111.text: And say, ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah, Who has never had ňĻanyňļ offspring;<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†nor does He have a partner in ňĻgoverningňļ the kingdom;<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†nor is He pathetic, needing a protector.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†And revere Him immensely.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Jesus, Ezra, or the angels.
¬†i.e., another god equal to Him.
¬†i.e., the idols.

18:1.text: All praise is for Allah Who has revealed the Book to His servant,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†allowing no crookedness in it,
footnotes: ¬†MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

18:2.text: ňĻmaking itňļ perfectly upright, to warn ňĻthe disbelieversňļ of a severe torment from Him; to give good news to the believers‚ÄĒwho do good‚ÄĒthat they will have a fine reward,

18:3.text: in which they will remain forever;

18:4.text: and to warn those who claim, ‚ÄúAllah has offspring.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Jesus in Christianity and the angels in Arab pagan tradition.

18:5.text: They have no knowledge of this, nor did their forefathers. What a terrible claim that comes out of their mouths! They say nothing but lies.

18:6.text: Now, perhaps you ňĻO Prophetňļ will grieve yourself to death over their denial, if they ňĻcontinue toňļ disbelieve in this message.

18:7.text: We have indeed made whatever is on earth as an adornment for it, in order to test which of them is best in deeds.

18:8.text: And We will certainly reduce whatever is on it to barren ground.

18:9.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ thought that the people of the cave¬†and the plaque<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†were ňĻthe onlyňļ wonders of Our signs?
footnotes: ¬†Ar-Raq√ģm¬†is the plaque that was placed at the entrance of the cave with the names and story of the People of the Cave. This is the story of a group of Christian youths who hid inside a cave outside the city of Ephesus around 250 C.E., to escape persecution at the hands of pagans during the reign of the Roman emperor Decius. The Quran does not give an exact number of the youths, although many scholars believe there were seven in addition to a dog. The youths slept for 300 years, plus nine (300 solar years equal 309 lunar years).

18:10.text: ňĻRememberňļ when those youths took refuge in the cave, and said, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Grant us mercy from Yourself and guide us rightly through our ordeal.‚ÄĚ

18:11.text: So We caused them to fall into a dead sleep<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†in the cave for many years,
footnotes: ¬†lit., We cast a cover of ňĻdeepňļ sleep over their ears.

18:12.text: then We raised them so We may show which of the two groups would make a better estimation of the length of their stay.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The People of the Cave themselves disagreed on how long they had slept in the cave. See¬†18:19.

18:13.text: We relate to you ňĻO Prophetňļ their story in truth. They were youths who truly believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

18:14.text: And We strengthened their hearts when they stood up and declared, ‚ÄúOur Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will never call upon any god besides Him, or we would truly be uttering an outrageous lie.‚ÄĚ

18:15.text: ňĻThen they said to one another,ňļ ‚ÄúThese people of ours have taken gods besides Him. Why do they not produce a clear proof of them? Who then does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah?

18:16.text: Since you have distanced yourselves from them and what they worship besides Allah, take refuge in the cave. Your Lord will extend His mercy to you and accommodate you in your ordeal.‚ÄĚ

18:17.text: And you would have seen the sun, as it rose, inclining away from their cave to the right, and as it set, declining away from them to the left, while they lay in its open space.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†That is one of the signs of Allah. Whoever Allah guides is truly guided. But whoever He leaves to stray, you will never find for them a guiding mentor.
footnotes: ¬†While they slept in the open space of the cave, fresh air blew through the cave, but they were protected from the heat of the sun.

18:18.text: And you would have thought they were awake,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†though they were asleep. We turned them over, to the right and left, while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance. Had you looked at them, you would have certainly fled away from them, filled with horror.
footnotes: ¬†This is because, during their long sleep, their eyes were open, their hair grew long, and their bodies rolled over to the right and left to prevent bedsores.

18:19.text: And so We awakened them so that they might question one another. One of them exclaimed, ‚ÄúHow long have you remained ňĻasleepňļ?‚ÄĚ Some replied, ‚ÄúPerhaps a day, or part of a day.‚ÄĚ They said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúYour Lord knows best how long you have remained. So send one of you with these silver coins of yours to the city, and let him find which food is the purest, and then bring you provisions from it. Let him be ňĻexceptionallyňļ cautious, and do not let him give you away.

18:20.text: For, indeed, if they find out about you, they will stone you ňĻto deathňļ, or force you back into their faith, and then you will never succeed.‚ÄĚ

18:21.text: That is how We caused them to be discovered so that their people might know that Allah‚Äôs promise ňĻof resurrectionňļ is true and that there is no doubt about the Hour.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†When the people disputed with each other about the case of the youth ňĻafter their deathňļ,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†some proposed, ‚ÄúBuild a structure around them. Their Lord knows best about them.‚ÄĚ Those who prevailed in the matter said, ‚ÄúWe will surely build a place of worship over them.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Their antique silver coins gave them away. People rushed to the cave to greet the youths, who finally passed away and were buried in the cave. The King decided to build a place of worship at the cave to commemorate their story.
¬†Some pagans suggested that a wall should be built to seal off the cave, whereas the believers decided to build a place of worship at the cave to honour those youths.

18:22.text: Some will say, ‚ÄúThey were three, their dog was the fourth,‚ÄĚ while others will say, ‚ÄúThey were five, their dog was the sixth,‚ÄĚ ňĻonlyňļ guessing blindly. And others will say, ‚ÄúThey were seven and their dog was the eighth.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúMy Lord knows best their ňĻexactňļ number. Only a few people know as well.‚ÄĚ So do not argue about them except with sure knowledge,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†nor consult any of those ňĻwho debateňļ about them.
footnotes: ¬†Based on what has been revealed in the Quran.

18:23.text: And never say of anything, ‚ÄúI will definitely do this tomorrow,‚ÄĚ

18:24.text: without adding, ‚Äúif Allah so wills!‚ÄĚ But if you forget, then remember your Lord, and say, ‚ÄúI trust my Lord will guide me to what is more right than this.‚ÄĚ

18:25.text: They had remained in their cave for three hundred years, adding nine.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Three hundred years in the Gregorian calendar equal three hundred and nine years in the Islamic lunar calendar.

18:26.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah knows best how long they stayed. With Him ňĻaloneňļ is ňĻthe knowledge ofňļ the unseen of the heavens and the earth. How perfectly He hears and sees! They have no guardian besides Him, and He shares His command with none.‚ÄĚ

18:27.text: Recite what has been revealed to you from the Book of your Lord. None can change His Words, nor can you find any refuge besides Him.

18:28.text: And patiently stick with those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking His pleasure.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Do not let your eyes look beyond them, desiring the luxuries of this worldly life. And do not obey those whose hearts We have made heedless of Our remembrance, who follow ňĻonlyňļ their desires and whose state is ňĻtotalňļ loss.
footnotes: ¬†lit., seeking His Face.

18:29.text: And say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻThis isňļ the truth from your Lord. Whoever wills let them believe, and whoever wills let them disbelieve.‚ÄĚ Surely We have prepared for the wrongdoers a Fire whose walls will ňĻcompletelyňļ surround them. When they cry for aid, they will be aided with water like molten metal, which will burn ňĻtheirňļ faces. What a horrible drink! And what a terrible place to rest!

18:30.text: As for those who believe and do good, We certainly never deny the reward of those who are best in deeds.

18:31.text: It is they who will have the Gardens of Eternity, with rivers flowing under their feet. There they will be adorned with bracelets of gold, and wear green garments of fine silk and rich brocade, reclining there on ňĻcanopiedňļ couches. What a marvellous reward! And what a fabulous place to rest!

18:32.text: Give them ňĻO Prophetňļ an example of two men. To ňĻthe disbelievingňļ one We gave two gardens of grapevines, which We surrounded with palm trees and placed ňĻvariousňļ crops in between.

18:33.text: Each garden yielded ňĻallňļ its produce, never falling short. And We caused a river to flow between them.

18:34.text: And he had other resources<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻas wellňļ. So he boasted to a ňĻpoorňļ companion of his, while conversing with him, ‚ÄúI am greater than you in wealth and superior in manpower.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The word ‚Äúthamar‚ÄĚ can mean fruits, gold and silver, etc.

18:35.text: And he entered his property, while wronging his soul, saying, ‚ÄúI do not think this will ever perish,

18:36.text: nor do I think the Hour will ňĻeverňļ come. And if in fact I am returned to my Lord, I will definitely get a far better outcome than ňĻallňļ this.‚ÄĚ

18:37.text: His ňĻbelievingňļ companion replied, while conversing with him, ‚ÄúDo you disbelieve in the One Who created you from dust,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then ňĻdeveloped youňļ from a sperm-drop, then formed you into a man?
footnotes: ¬†Created your father, Adam, from dust.

18:38.text: But as for me: He is Allah, my Lord, and I will never associate anyone with my Lord ňĻin worshipňļ.

18:39.text: If only you had said, upon entering your property, ‚ÄėThis is what Allah has willed! There is no power except with Allah!‚Äô Even though you see me inferior to you in wealth and offspring,

18:40.text: perhaps my Lord will grant me ňĻsomethingňļ better than your garden, and send down upon your garden a thunderbolt from the sky, turning it into a barren waste.

18:41.text: Or its water may sink ňĻinto the earthňļ, and then you will never be able to seek it out.‚ÄĚ

18:42.text: And so all his produce was ňĻtotallyňļ ruined, so he started to wring his hands for all he had spent on it, while it had collapsed on its trellises. He cried, ‚ÄúAlas! I wish I had never associated anyone with my Lord ňĻin worshipňļ!‚ÄĚ

18:43.text: And he had no manpower to help him against Allah, nor could he ňĻevenňļ help himself.

18:44.text: At this time, support comes ňĻonlyňļ from Allah‚ÄĒthe True ňĻLordňļ. He is best in reward and best in outcome.

18:45.text: And give them a parable of this worldly life. ňĻIt isňļ like the plants of the earth, thriving when sustained by the rain We send down from the sky. Then they ňĻsoonňļ turn into chaff scattered by the wind. And Allah is fully capable of ňĻdoingňļ all things.

18:46.text: Wealth and children are the adornment of this worldly life, but the everlasting good deeds are far better with your Lord in reward and in hope.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This refers to good deeds that will benefit the believer in the Hereafter such as prayers or praises of Allah such as saying: ‚ÄėSubŠł•√Ęna-All√Ęh‚Äô (Glory be to Allah), ‚ÄėAlŠł•amdulill√Ęh‚Äô (Praise be to Allah), ‚ÄėL√Ę il√Ęha illa-All√Ęh‚Äô (There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah), and ‚ÄėAll√Ęhu akbar‚Äô (Allah is the Greatest).

18:47.text: ňĻBeware ofňļ the Day We will blow the mountains away, and you will see the earth laid bare. And We will gather all ňĻhumankindňļ, leaving none behind.

18:48.text: They will be presented before your Lord in rows, ňĻand the deniers will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúYou have surely returned to Us ňĻall aloneňļ as We created you the first time, although you ňĻalwaysňļ claimed that We would never appoint a time for your return.‚ÄĚ

18:49.text: And the record ňĻof deedsňļ will be laid ňĻopenňļ, and you will see the wicked in fear of what is ňĻwrittenňļ in it. They will cry, ‚ÄúWoe to us! What kind of record is this that does not leave any sin, small or large, unlisted?‚ÄĚ They will find whatever they did present ňĻbefore themňļ. And your Lord will never wrong anyone.

18:50.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We said to the angels, ‚ÄúProstrate before Adam,‚ÄĚ so they all did‚ÄĒbut not Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who was one of the jinn, but he rebelled against the command of his Lord. Would you then take him and his descendants as patrons instead of Me, although they are your enemy? What an evil alternative for the wrongdoers ňĻto chooseňļ!
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:34.

18:51.text: I never called them to witness the creation of the heavens and the earth or ňĻevenňļ their own creation, nor would I take the misleaders as helpers.

18:52.text: And ňĻbeware ofňļ the Day He will say, ‚ÄúCall upon those you claimed were My associate-gods.‚ÄĚ So they will call them, but will receive no response. And We will make them ňĻallňļ share in the same doom.

18:53.text: The wicked will see the Fire and realize that they are bound to fall into it, and will find no way to avoid it.

18:54.text: We have surely set forth in this Quran every ňĻkind ofňļ lesson for people, but humankind is the most argumentative of all beings.

18:55.text: And nothing prevents people from believing when guidance comes to them and from seeking their Lord‚Äôs forgiveness except ňĻtheir demandňļ to meet the same fate of earlier deniers or that the torment would confront them face to face.

18:56.text: We do not send the messengers except as deliverers of good news and warners. But the disbelievers argue in falsehood, ňĻhopingňļ to discredit the truth with it, and make a mockery of My revelations and warnings.

18:57.text: And who does more wrong than those who, when reminded of their Lord‚Äôs revelations, turn away from them and forget what their own hands have done? We have certainly cast veils over their hearts‚ÄĒleaving them unable to comprehend this ňĻQuranňļ‚ÄĒand deafness in their ears. And if you ňĻO Prophetňļ invite them to ňĻtrueňļ guidance, they will never be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

18:58.text: Your Lord is the All-Forgiving, Full of Mercy. If He were to seize them ňĻimmediatelyňļ for what they commit, He would have certainly hastened their punishment. But they have an appointed time, from which they will find no refuge.

18:59.text: Those ňĻare theňļ societies We destroyed when they persisted in wrong,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and We had set a time for their destruction.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the peoples of ‚Äô»ād and Tham√Ľd.

18:60.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Moses said to his young assistant, ‚ÄúI will never give up until I reach the junction of the two seas, even if I travel for ages.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in an authentic narration collected by Bukh√Ęri that a man approached Moses after he gave a talk and asked him, ‚ÄúWho is the most knowledgeable person on earth?‚ÄĚ Moses responded, ‚ÄúThat would be me!‚ÄĚ So Allah revealed to Moses that he should not have said this and there was in fact someone who was more knowledgeable than him. Moses was commanded to travel to meet this man, named Al-KhaŠłćir, at the junction of the two seas (which could be the northern part of Sinai between the Red Sea and the Mediterranean Sea, or the Southern part of Sinai where the Rea Sea splits into the Gulf of Suez and the Gulf of Aqaba).

18:61.text: But when they ňĻfinallyňļ reached the point where the seas met, they forgot their ňĻsaltedňļ fish, and it made its way into the sea, slipping away ňĻwondrouslyňļ.

18:62.text: When they had passed further, he said to his assistant, ‚ÄúBring us our meal! We have certainly been exhausted by today‚Äôs journey.‚ÄĚ

18:63.text: He replied, ‚ÄúDo you remember when we rested by the rock? ňĻThat is whenňļ I forgot the fish. None made me forget to mention this except Satan. And the fish made its way into the sea miraculously.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúThe ňĻsaltedňļ fish must have made its way into the sea. How strange!‚ÄĚ

18:64.text: Moses responded, ‚ÄúThat is ňĻexactlyňļ what we were looking for.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So they returned, retracing their footsteps.
footnotes: ¬†Moses was given a sign: when he and his assistant Joshua (or Y√Ľsha‚Äô ibn N√Ľn) lost their food (a salted fish), this would be the place where they would find the man of knowledge.

18:65.text: There they found a servant of Ours, to whom We had granted mercy from Us and enlightened with knowledge of Our Own.

18:66.text: Moses said to him, ‚ÄúMay I follow you, provided that you teach me some of the right guidance you have been taught?‚ÄĚ

18:67.text: He said, ‚ÄúYou certainly cannot be patient ňĻenoughňļ with me.

18:68.text: And how can you be patient with what is beyond your ňĻrealm ofňļ knowledge?‚ÄĚ

18:69.text: Moses assured ňĻhimňļ, ‚ÄúYou will find me patient, Allah willing, and I will not disobey any of your orders.‚ÄĚ

18:70.text: He responded, ‚ÄúThen if you follow me, do not question me about anything until I ňĻmyselfňļ clarify it for you.‚ÄĚ

18:71.text: So they set out, but after they had boarded a ship, the man made a hole in it. Moses protested, ‚ÄúHave you done this to drown its people? You have certainly done a terrible thing!‚ÄĚ

18:72.text: He replied, ‚ÄúDid I not say that you cannot have patience with me?‚ÄĚ

18:73.text: Moses pleaded, ‚ÄúExcuse me for forgetting, and do not be hard on me.‚ÄĚ

18:74.text: So they proceeded until they came across a boy, and the man killed him. Moses protested, ‚ÄúHave you killed an innocent soul, who killed no one? You have certainly done a horrible thing.‚ÄĚ

18:75.text: He answered, ‚ÄúDid I not tell you that you cannot have patience with me?‚ÄĚ

18:76.text: Moses replied, ‚ÄúIf I ever question you about anything after this, then do not keep me in your company, for by then I would have given you enough of an excuse.‚ÄĚ

18:77.text: So they moved on until they came to the people of a town. They asked them for food, but the people refused to give them hospitality. There they found a wall ready to collapse, so the man set it right. Moses protested, ‚ÄúIf you wanted, you could have demanded a fee for this.‚ÄĚ

18:78.text: He replied, ‚ÄúThis is the parting of our ways. I will explain to you what you could not bear patiently.

18:79.text: ‚ÄúAs for the ship, it belonged to some poor people, working at sea. So I intended to damage it, for there was a ňĻtyrantňļ king ahead of them who seizes every ňĻgoodňļ ship by force.

18:80.text: ‚ÄúAnd as for the boy, his parents were ňĻtrueňļ believers, and we<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†feared that he would pressure them into defiance and disbelief.
footnotes: ¬†This is the royal ‚Äėwe‚Äô.

18:81.text: So we hoped that their Lord would give them another, more virtuous and caring in his place.

18:82.text: ‚ÄúAnd as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and under the wall was a treasure that belonged to them, and their father had been a righteous man. So your Lord willed that these children should come of age and retrieve their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord. I did not do it ňĻallňļ on my own. This is the explanation of what you could not bear patiently.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) commented on the story, ‚ÄúI wish Moses had been more patient so we can learn more from that wise man.‚ÄĚ

18:83.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ about ‚Īęul-Qarnain. Say, ‚ÄúI will relate to you something of his narrative.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This is the story of a righteous king who travelled far and wide. He is titled ‚Īęul-Qarnain (lit., the man of the two horns) because of his journeys to the far east and far west (or the two horns/points of sunrise and sunset). Some believe ‚Īęul-Qarnain was Alexander the Great, but this is not sound since Alexander the Great was a polytheist. Many scholars believe it was Abu Kuraib Al-Šł§amiri, a righteous king from Yemen.

18:84.text: Surely We established him in the land, and gave him the means to all things.

18:86.text: until he reached the setting ňĻpointňļ of the sun, which appeared to him to be setting in a spring of murky water, where he found some people. We said, ‚ÄúO ‚Īęul-Qarnain! Either punish them or treat them kindly.‚ÄĚ

18:87.text: He responded, ‚ÄúWhoever does wrong will be punished by us, then will be returned to their Lord, Who will punish them with a horrible torment.

18:88.text: As for those who believe and do good, they will have the finest reward, and we will assign them easy commands.‚ÄĚ

18:89.text: Then he travelled a ňĻdifferentňļ course

18:90.text: until he reached the rising ňĻpointňļ of the sun. He found it rising on a people for whom We had provided no shelter from it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†They either had no clothes or homes to protect them from the sun.

18:91.text: So it was. And We truly had full knowledge of him.

18:92.text: Then he travelled a ňĻthirdňļ course

18:93.text: until he reached ňĻa passňļ between two mountains. He found in front of them a people who could hardly understand ňĻhisňļ language.

18:94.text: They pleaded, ‚ÄúO ‚Īęul-Qarnain! Surely Gog and Magog<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†are spreading corruption throughout the land. Should we pay you tribute, provided that you build a wall between us and them?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Gog and Magog are two wild peoples who were sealed off by a barrier built by ‚Īęul-Qarnain to stop them from raiding their peaceful neighbours. There are many speculations as to their location, but none is supported by reliable sources. Their coming out is mentioned in 21:96-97 as one of the major signs of the final Hour.

18:95.text: He responded, ‚ÄúWhat my Lord has provided for me is far better. But assist me with resources, and I will build a barrier between you and them.

18:96.text: Bring me blocks of iron!‚ÄĚ Then, when he had filled up ňĻthe gapňļ between the two mountains, he ordered, ‚ÄúBlow!‚ÄĚ When the iron became red hot, he said, ‚ÄúBring me molten copper to pour over it.‚ÄĚ

18:97.text: And so the enemies could neither scale nor tunnel through it.

18:98.text: He declared, ‚ÄúThis is a mercy from my Lord. But when the promise of my Lord comes to pass, He will level it to the ground. And my Lord‚Äôs promise is ever true.‚ÄĚ

18:99.text: On that Day, We will let them<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†surge ňĻlike wavesňļ over one another. Later, the Trumpet will be blown,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and We will gather all ňĻpeopleňļ together.
footnotes: ¬†Gog and Magog.

¬†On the Day of Judgment, the Trumpet will be blown by an angel‚ÄĒcausing all to die. When it is blown a second time, everyone will be raised from the dead to be assembled for the Judgment (see¬†39:68).

18:100.text: On that Day We will display Hell clearly for the disbelievers,

18:101.text: those who turned a blind eye to My Reminder<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and could not stand listening ňĻto itňļ.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Quran.

18:102.text: Do the disbelievers think they can ňĻsimplyňļ take My servants<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as lords instead of Me? We have surely prepared Hell as an accommodation for the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Jesus, Ezra, and the angels.

18:103.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShall we inform you of who will be the biggest losers of deeds?

18:104.text: ňĻThey areňļ those whose efforts are in vain in this worldly life, while they think they are doing good!‚ÄĚ

18:105.text: It is they who reject the signs of their Lord and their meeting with Him, rendering their deeds void, so We will not give their deeds any weight on Judgment Day.

18:106.text: That is their reward: Hell, for their disbelief and mockery of My signs and messengers.

18:107.text: Indeed, those who believe and do good will have the Gardens of Paradise<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as an accommodation,
footnotes: ¬†Al-Firdaws, the highest place in Paradise.

18:108.text: where they will be forever, never desiring anywhere else.

18:109.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf the ocean were ink for ňĻwritingňļ the Words of my Lord, it would certainly run out before the Words of my Lord were finished, even if We refilled it with its equal.‚ÄĚ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†

18:110.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI am only a man like you, ňĻbutňļ it has been revealed to me that your God is only One God. So whoever hopes for the meeting with their Lord, let them do good deeds and associate none in the worship of their Lord.‚ÄĚ

19:2.text: ňĻThis isňļ a reminder of your Lord‚Äôs mercy to His servant Zachariah,

19:3.text: when he cried out to his Lord privately,

19:4.text: saying, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Surely my bones have become brittle, and grey hair has spread across my head, but I have never been disappointed in my prayer to You, my Lord!

19:5.text: And I am concerned about ňĻthe faith ofňļ my relatives after me, since my wife is barren. So grant me, by Your grace, an heir,

19:6.text: who will inherit ňĻprophethoodňļ from me and the family of Jacob, and make him, O ¬†Lord, pleasing ňĻto Youňļ!‚ÄĚ

19:7.text: ňĻThe angels announced,ňļ ‚ÄúO Zachariah! Indeed, We give you the good news of ňĻthe birth ofňļ a son, whose name will be John‚ÄĒa name We have not given to anyone before.‚ÄĚ

19:8.text: He wondered, ‚ÄúMy Lord! How can I have a son when my wife is barren, and I have become extremely old?‚ÄĚ

19:9.text: An angel replied, ‚ÄúSo will it be! Your Lord says, ‚ÄėIt is easy for Me, just as I created you before, when you were nothing!‚Äô‚ÄĚ

19:10.text: Zachariah said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me a sign.‚ÄĚ He responded, ‚ÄúYour sign is that you will not ňĻbe able toňļ speak to people for three nights, despite being healthy.‚ÄĚ

19:11.text: So he came out to his people from the sanctuary, signalling to them to glorify ňĻAllahňļ morning and evening.

19:12.text: ňĻIt was later said,ňļ ‚ÄúO John! Hold firmly to the Scriptures.‚ÄĚ And We granted him wisdom while ňĻhe was stillňļ a child,

19:13.text: as well as purity and compassion from Us. And he was God-fearing,

19:14.text: and kind to his parents. He was neither arrogant nor disobedient.

19:15.text: Peace be upon him the day he was born, and the day of his death, and the day he will be raised back to life!

19:16.text: And mention in the Book ňĻO Prophet, the story ofňļ Mary when she withdrew from her family to a place in the east,

19:17.text: screening herself off from them. Then We sent to her Our angel, ňĻGabriel,ňļ appearing before her as a man, perfectly formed.

19:18.text: She appealed, ‚ÄúI truly seek refuge in the Most Compassionate from you! ňĻSo leave me aloneňļ if you are God-fearing.‚ÄĚ

19:19.text: He responded, ‚ÄúI am only a messenger from your Lord, ňĻsentňļ to bless you with a pure son.‚ÄĚ

19:20.text: She wondered, ‚ÄúHow can I have a son when no man has ever touched me, nor am I unchaste?‚ÄĚ

19:21.text: He replied, ‚ÄúSo will it be! Your Lord says, ‚ÄėIt is easy for Me. And so will We make him a sign for humanity and a mercy from Us.‚Äô It is a matter ňĻalreadyňļ decreed.‚ÄĚ

19:22.text: So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a remote place.

19:23.text: Then the pains of labour drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She cried, ‚ÄúAlas! I wish I had died before this, and was a thing long forgotten!‚ÄĚ

19:24.text: So a voice<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†reassured her from below her, ‚ÄúDo not grieve! Your Lord has provided a stream at your feet.
footnotes: ¬†This was the voice of baby Jesus. Some say it was Gabriel.

19:25.text: And shake the trunk of this palm tree towards you, it will drop fresh, ripe dates upon you.

19:26.text: So eat and drink, and put your heart at ease. But if you see any of the people, say, ‚ÄėI have vowed silence<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to the Most Compassionate, so I am not talking to anyone today.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., fast ňĻfrom speechňļ. Linguistically, ŠĻ£awm¬†means to abstain from something, such as food, speaking, and playing.

19:27.text: Then she returned to her people, carrying him. They said ňĻin shockňļ, ‚ÄúO Mary! You have certainly done a horrible thing!

19:28.text: O ¬†sister of Aaron!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Your father was not an indecent man, nor was your mother unchaste.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., his twin in virtue. They meant that they did not expect her to do something like that because they held her in high esteem like Aaron, Moses‚Äô brother (perhaps because she was his descendant).

19:29.text: So she pointed to the baby. They exclaimed, ‚ÄúHow can we talk to someone who is an infant in the cradle?‚ÄĚ

19:30.text: Jesus declared, ‚ÄúI am truly a servant of Allah. He has destined me to be given the Scripture and to be a prophet.

19:31.text: He has made me a blessing wherever I go, and bid me to establish prayer and give alms-tax as long as I live,

19:32.text: and to be kind to my mother. He has not made me arrogant or defiant.

19:33.text: Peace be upon me the day I was born, the day I die, and the day I will be raised back to life!‚ÄĚ

19:34.text: That is Jesus, son of Mary. ňĻAnd this isňļ a word of truth, about which they dispute.

19:35.text: It is not for Allah to take a son! Glory be to Him. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And it is!

19:36.text: ňĻJesus also declared,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ňĻaloneňļ. This is the Straight Path.‚ÄĚ

19:37.text: Yet their ňĻvariousňļ groups have differed among themselves ňĻabout himňļ, so woe to the disbelievers when they face a tremendous Day!

19:38.text: How clearly will they hear and see on the Day they will come to Us! But today the wrongdoers are clearly astray.

19:39.text: And warn them ňĻO Prophetňļ of the Day of Regret, when all matters will be settled, while they are ňĻengrossedňļ in heedlessness and disbelief.

19:40.text: Indeed, it is We Who will succeed the earth and whoever is on it. And to Us they will ňĻallňļ be returned.

19:41.text: And mention in the Book ňĻO Prophet, the story ofňļ Abraham. He was surely a man of truth and a prophet.

19:42.text: ňĻRememberňļ when he said to his father, ‚ÄúO dear father! Why do you worship what can neither hear nor see, nor benefit you at all?

19:43.text: O ¬†dear father! I have certainly received some knowledge which you have not received, so follow me and I will guide you to the Straight Path.

19:44.text: O ¬†dear father! Do not worship Satan. Surely Satan is ever rebellious against the Most Compassionate.

19:45.text: O ¬†dear father! I truly fear that you will be touched by a torment from the Most Compassionate, and become Satan‚Äôs companion ňĻin Hellňļ.‚ÄĚ

19:46.text: He threatened, ‚ÄúHow dare you reject my idols, O ¬†Abraham! If you do not desist, I will certainly stone you ňĻto deathňļ. So be gone from me for a long time!‚ÄĚ

19:47.text: Abraham responded, ‚ÄúPeace be upon you! I will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness. He has truly been Most Gracious to me.

19:48.text: As I distance myself from ňĻall ofňļ you and from whatever you invoke besides Allah, I will ňĻcontinue toňļ call upon my Lord ňĻaloneňļ, trusting that I will never be disappointed in invoking my Lord.‚ÄĚ

19:49.text: So after he had left them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and made each of them a prophet.

19:50.text: We showered them with Our mercy, and blessed them with honourable mention.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†On a daily basis, Muslims invoke Allah‚Äôs blessings upon Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) and his family and Prophet Abraham (Ô∑ļ) and his family, in both obligatory and optional prayers.

19:51.text: And mention in the Book ňĻO Prophet, the story ofňļ Moses. He was truly a chosen man, and was a messenger and a prophet.

19:52.text: We called him from the right side of Mount ŠĻ¨√Ľr, and drew him near, speaking ňĻwith himňļ directly.

19:53.text: And We appointed for him‚ÄĒout of Our grace‚ÄĒhis brother, Aaron, as a prophet.

19:54.text: And mention in the Book ňĻO Prophet, the story ofňļ Ishmael. He was truly a man of his word, and was a messenger and a prophet.

19:55.text: He used to urge his people to pray and give alms-tax. And his Lord was well pleased with him.

19:56.text: And mention in the Book ňĻO Prophet, the story ofňļ Enoch. He was surely a man of truth and a prophet.

19:57.text: And We elevated him to an honourable status.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Prophet Enoch (Idr√ģs) (Ô∑ļ) is said to be in the fourth heaven.

19:58.text: Those were ňĻsome ofňļ the prophets who Allah has blessed from among the descendants of Adam, and of those We carried with Noah ňĻin the Arkňļ, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and of those We ňĻrightlyňļ guided and chose. Whenever the revelations of the Most Compassionate were recited to them, they fell down, prostrating and weeping.
footnotes: ¬†Israel is another name for Jacob (Ô∑ļ).

19:59.text: But they were succeeded by generations who neglected prayer and followed their lusts and so will soon face the evil consequences.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The word ghai¬†can either mean the evil consequences or a valley in Hell where they will be punished.

19:60.text: As for those who repent, believe, and do good, it is they who will be admitted into Paradise, never being denied any reward.

19:61.text: ňĻThey will be inňļ the Gardens of Eternity, promised in trust by the Most Compassionate to His servants. Surely His promise will be fulfilled.

19:62.text: There they will never hear any idle talk‚ÄĒonly ňĻgreetings ofňļ peace. And there they will have their provisions morning and evening.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†There is no day or night in Paradise, only light. The verse says that the residents of Paradise will either be receiving provisions around these worldly times or without failure. Words such as morning and evening are used to make it easier for the people to understand.

19:63.text: That is Paradise, which We will grant to whoever is devout among Our servants.

19:64.text: ‚ÄúWe only descend by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us, and whatever is behind us, and everything in between. And your Lord is never forgetful.

19:65.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Lord of the heavens, and the earth, and everything in between. So worship Him ňĻaloneňļ, and be steadfast in His worship. Do you know of anyone equal to Him ňĻin His attributesňļ?‚ÄĚ

19:66.text: Yet ňĻsomeňļ people ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúAfter I die, will I really be raised to life again?‚ÄĚ

19:67.text: Do ňĻsuchňļ people not remember that We created them before, when they were nothing?

19:68.text: By your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ! We will surely gather them along with the devils, and then set them around Hell on their knees.

19:69.text: Then We will certainly begin by dragging out of every group the ones most defiant to the Most Compassionate.

19:70.text: And We truly know best who is most deserving of burning in it.

19:71.text: There is none of you who will not pass over it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻThis isňļ a decree your Lord must fulfil.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the bridge (known as AŠĻ£-ŠĻĘir√ĘŠĻ≠) which is extended over Hell. Both the believers and disbelievers will be made to cross that bridge. The believers will cross it safely, each at a different speed according to the strength of their faith, whereas the disbelievers and the hypocrites will plunge into the Fire.

19:72.text: Then We will deliver those who were devout, leaving the wrongdoers there on their knees.

19:73.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, the disbelievers ask the believers ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhich of the two of us is better in status and superior in assembly?‚ÄĚ

19:74.text: ňĻImagine, O ¬†Prophetňļ how many peoples We have destroyed before them, who were far better in luxury and splendour!

19:75.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhoever is ňĻentrenchedňļ in misguidance, the Most Compassionate will allow them plenty of time, until‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒthey face what they are threatened with: either the torment or the Hour. Only then will they realize who is worse in position and inferior in manpower.‚ÄĚ

19:76.text: And Allah increases in guidance those who are ňĻrightlyňļ guided. And the everlasting good deeds are far better with your Lord in reward and in outcome.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 18:46.

19:77.text: Have you seen ňĻO Prophetňļ the one<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who rejects Our revelations yet boasts, ‚ÄúI will definitely be granted ňĻplenty ofňļ wealth and children ňĻif there is an afterlifeňļ.‚ÄĚ?
footnotes: ¬†This statement was made by Al-‚Äô»āŠĻ£ ibn W√Ę'il, a Meccan pagan who staunchly disbelieved in resurrection.

19:78.text: Has he looked into the unseen or taken a pledge from the Most Compassionate?

19:79.text: Not at all! We certainly record whatever he claims and will increase his punishment extensively.

19:80.text: And We will inherit what he boasts of, and he will come before Us all by himself.

19:81.text: They have taken other gods, instead of Allah, seeking strength ňĻand protectionňļ through them.

19:82.text: But no! Those ňĻgodsňļ will deny their worship and turn against them.

19:83.text: Do you ňĻO Prophetňļ not see that We have sent the devils against the disbelievers, constantly inciting them?

19:84.text: So do not be in haste against them, for indeed We are ňĻcloselyňļ counting down their days.

19:85.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day We will gather the righteous before the Most Compassionate as an honoured delegation,

19:86.text: and drive the wicked to Hell like a thirsty herd.

19:87.text: None will have the right to intercede, except those who have taken a covenant from the Most Compassionate.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†No one will be allowed to intercede except those who firmly believe in Almighty Allah.

19:88.text: They<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†say, ‚ÄúThe Most Compassionate has offspring.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The pagans who claimed that the angels are Allah‚Äôs daughters, the Christians who claim that Jesus is the son of Allah, etc.

19:89.text: You have certainly made an outrageous claim,

19:90.text: by which the heavens are about to burst, the earth to split apart, and the mountains to crumble to pieces

19:91.text: in protest of attributing children to the Most Compassionate.

19:92.text: It does not befit ňĻthe majesty ofňļ the Most Compassionate to have children.

19:93.text: There is none in the heavens or the earth who will not return to the Most Compassionate in full submission.

19:94.text: Indeed, He fully knows them and has counted them precisely.

19:95.text: And each of them will return to Him on the Day of Judgment all alone.

19:96.text: As for those who believe and do good, the Most Compassionate will ňĻcertainlyňļ bless them with ňĻgenuineňļ love.

19:97.text: Indeed, We have made this ňĻQuranňļ easy in your own language ňĻO Prophetňļ so with it you may give good news to the righteous and warn those who are contentious.

19:98.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many peoples We have destroyed before them! Do you ňĻstillňļ see any of them, or ňĻevenňļ hear from them the slightest sound?

footnotes: ¬†See¬†Stylistic Features for information about these letters.

2:2.text: This is the Book! There is no doubt about it<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒa guide for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., there is no doubt regarding its authenticity or consistency.
¬†The word muttaqi (plural¬†muttaq√Ľn) can be translated as one who is mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, devout, pious, God-fearing, righteous, or God-conscious.

2:3.text: who believe in the unseen,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†establish prayer, and donate from what We have provided for them,
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the belief in Allah, the angels, and the Day of Judgment.

2:4.text: and who believe in what has been revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and what was revealed before you, and have sure faith in the Hereafter.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†Notes on Translation for information about the use of ňĻO Prophetňļ.

2:5.text: It is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ guided by their Lord, and it is they who will be successful.

2:6.text: As for those who persist in disbelief, it is the same whether you warn them or not‚ÄĒthey will never believe.

2:7.text: Allah has sealed their hearts and their hearing, and their sight is covered. They will suffer a tremendous punishment.

2:8.text: And there are some who say, ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah and the Last Day,‚ÄĚ yet they are not ňĻtrueňļ believers.

2:9.text: They seek to deceive Allah and the believers, yet they only deceive themselves, but they fail to perceive it.

2:10.text: There is sickness in their hearts, and Allah ňĻonlyňļ lets their sickness increase. They will suffer a painful punishment for their lies.

2:11.text: When they are told, ‚ÄúDo not spread corruption in the land,‚ÄĚ they reply, ‚ÄúWe are only peace-makers!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†When they were criticized for their close association with the pagans who were hostile to Muslims, they would argue that they only wanted to make peace between Muslims and their enemies.

2:12.text: Indeed, it is they who are the corruptors, but they fail to perceive it.

2:13.text: And when they are told, ‚ÄúBelieve as others believe,‚ÄĚ they reply, ‚ÄúWill we believe as the fools believe?‚ÄĚ Indeed, it is they who are fools, but they do not know.

2:14.text: When they meet the believers they say, ‚ÄúWe believe.‚ÄĚ But when alone with their evil associates they say, ‚ÄúWe are definitely with you; we were only mocking.‚ÄĚ

2:15.text: Allah will throw their mockery back at them, leaving them to continue wandering blindly in their defiance.

2:16.text: They are the ones who trade guidance for misguidance. But this trade is profitless, and they are not ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

2:17.text: Their example is that of someone who kindles a fire, but when it lights up all around them, Allah takes away their light, leaving them in complete darkness‚ÄĒunable to see.

2:18.text: They are ňĻwilfullyňļ deaf, dumb, and blind, so they will never return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.

2:19.text: Or ňĻthose caught inňļ a rainstorm from the sky with darkness, thunder, and lightning. They press their fingers into their ears at the sound of every thunder-clap for fear of death. And Allah encompasses the disbelievers ňĻby His mightňļ.

2:20.text: It is as if the lightning were about to snatch away their sight. Whenever lightning strikes, they walk in its light, but when darkness covers them, they stand still. Had Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and sight. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

2:21.text: O humanity! Worship your Lord, Who created you and those before you, so that you may become mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

2:22.text: ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who has made the earth a place of settlement for you and the sky a canopy; and sends down rain from the sky, causing fruits to grow as a provision for you. So do not knowingly set up equals to Allah ňĻin worshipňļ.

2:23.text: And if you are in doubt about what We have revealed to Our servant,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then produce a s√Ľrah¬†like it and call your helpers other than Allah, if what you say is true.
footnotes: ¬†MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

2:24.text: But if you are unable to do so‚ÄĒand you will never be able to do so‚ÄĒthen fear the Fire fuelled with people and stones, which is prepared for the disbelievers.

2:25.text: Give good news ňĻO Prophetňļ to those who believe and do good that they will have Gardens under which rivers flow. Whenever provided with fruit, they will say, ‚ÄúThis is what we were given before,‚ÄĚ for they will be served fruit that looks similar ňĻbut tastes differentňļ. They will have pure spouses,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and they will be there forever.
footnotes: ¬†Residents of Paradise will be in a perfect condition. There will be neither physical impurities such as illness, urination, defecation, or menstruation; nor spiritual blemishes such as jealousy, envy, or hatred.

2:26.text: Surely Allah does not shy away from using the parable of a mosquito or what is even smaller. As for the believers, they know that it is the truth from their Lord. And as for the disbelievers, they argue, ‚ÄúWhat does Allah mean by such a parable?‚ÄĚ Through this ňĻtestňļ, He leaves many to stray, and guides many. And He leaves none to stray except the rebellious‚ÄĒ

2:27.text: those who violate Allah‚Äôs covenant after it has been affirmed, break whatever ňĻtiesňļ Allah has ordered to be maintained, and spread corruption in the land. It is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ losers.

2:28.text: How can you deny Allah? You were lifeless and He gave you life, then He will cause you to die and again bring you to life, and then to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

2:29.text: He is the One Who created everything in the earth for you. Then He turned towards the heaven, forming it into seven heavens. And He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

2:30.text: ňĻRememberňļ when your Lord said to the angels, ‚ÄúI am going to place a successive ňĻhumanňļ authority on earth.‚ÄĚ They asked ňĻAllahňļ, ‚ÄúWill You place in it someone who will spread corruption there and shed blood while we glorify Your praises and proclaim Your holiness?‚ÄĚ Allah responded, ‚ÄúI know what you do not know.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Allah knew that there would be many righteous people who would do good, make peace, and stand up for justice. Since humans have free choice, whoever chooses to believe and do good is better in the sight of Allah than all other beings, and whoever chooses to disbelieve and do evil is worse than all other beings. See¬†98:6-8.

2:31.text: He taught Adam the names of all things, then He presented them to the angels and said, ‚ÄúTell Me the names of these, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†If you are true that Allah will never create anyone who is more honourable and knowledgeable than you.

2:32.text: They replied, ‚ÄúGlory be to You! We have no knowledge except what You have taught us. You are truly the All-Knowing, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ

2:33.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúO Adam! Inform them of their names.‚ÄĚ Then when Adam did, Allah said, ‚ÄúDid I not tell you that I know the secrets of the heavens and the earth, and I know what you reveal and what you conceal?‚ÄĚ

2:34.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We said to the angels, ‚ÄúProstrate before Adam,‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so they all did‚ÄĒbut not Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†who refused and acted arrogantly,<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†becoming unfaithful.
footnotes: ¬†Prostration in this verse does not mean worship, but an act of respect. A similar occurrence can be found in S√Ľrah¬†12, where Jacob (Ô∑ļ), his wife, and his eleven children knelt down before Joseph (Ô∑ļ).

¬†Ibl√ģs was the name of Satan before his fall from grace. Ibl√ģs¬†was not an angel, but one of the jinn (see¬†18:50). Jinn are another creation of Allah, similar to humans in that they‚ÄĒunlike angels‚ÄĒhave free will, but are made of smokeless fire and live in another plane of existence.

¬†The command to prostrate was a test of obedience. Satan arrogantly refused to comply because he believed he was superior to Adam‚ÄĒarguing that Adam was made of mud while Satan was made of fire (see¬†7:12).

2:35.text: We cautioned, ‚ÄúO Adam! Live with your wife in Paradise and eat as freely as you please, but do not approach this tree, or else you will be wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

2:36.text: But Satan deceived them‚ÄĒleading to their fall from the ňĻblissfulňļ state they were in,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and We said, ‚ÄúDescend from the heavens ňĻto the earthňļ as enemies to each other.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†You will find in the earth a residence and provision for your appointed stay.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Both Adam and Eve were deceived, both sinned, and both were later forgiven. There is no concept of original sin in Islam.
¬†i.e., humans and Satan.

2:37.text: Then Adam was inspired with words ňĻof prayerňļ by his Lord,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so He accepted his repentance. Surely He is the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†The prayer that Adam and Eve were inspired to say is mentioned in 7:23, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will certainly be losers.‚ÄĚ

2:38.text: We said, ‚ÄúDescend all of you! Then when guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows it, there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.

2:39.text: But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.‚ÄĚ

2:40.text: O children of Israel!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Remember My favours upon you. Fulfil your covenant and I will fulfil Mine, and stand in awe of Me ňĻaloneňļ.
footnotes: ¬†Israel is another name for Prophet Jacob.

2:41.text: Believe in My revelations which confirm your Scriptures.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Do not be the first to deny them or trade them for a fleeting gain.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†And be mindful of Me.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the passages in the Torah and the Gospel that call for the worship of the One True God, doing what is good, and avoiding what is evil.

¬†Trading Allah‚Äôs revelations for a fleeting gain is a recurring theme in the first few s√Ľrahs¬†of the Quran. This refers to the practice of some Jewish authorities in Medina who contradicted certain rulings in the Torah by giving people lenient opinions only to please them in exchange for money.

2:42.text: Do not mix truth with falsehood or hide the truth knowingly.

2:43.text: Establish prayer, pay alms-tax,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and bow down with those who bow down.
footnotes: ¬†The alms-tax (zak√Ęh) is the payment of 2.5% of someone‚Äôs savings only if the amount is equivalent to or greater than 85 g of gold, if that amount remains untouched for a whole Islamic year‚ÄĒaround 355 days.

2:44.text: Do you preach righteousness and fail to practice it yourselves, although you read the Scripture? Do you not understand?

2:45.text: And seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, it is a burden except for the humble‚ÄĒ

2:46.text: those who are certain that they will meet their Lord and to Him they will return.

2:47.text: O Children of Israel! Remember ňĻallňļ the favours I granted you and how I honoured you above the others.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, I chose you above all peoples of your time.

2:48.text: Guard yourselves against the Day on which no soul will be of help to another. No intercession<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†will be accepted, no ransom taken, and no help will be given.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúIntercession‚ÄĚ is the act of pleading with Allah on behalf of another person on the Day of Judgment.

2:49.text: ňĻRememberňļ how We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with dreadful torment, slaughtering your sons and keeping your women. That was a severe test from your Lord.

2:50.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We parted the sea, rescued you, and drowned Pharaoh‚Äôs people before your very eyes.

2:51.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We appointed forty nights for Moses, then you worshipped the calf in his absence, acting wrongfully.

2:52.text: Even then We ňĻstillňļ forgave you so perhaps you would be grateful.

2:53.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We gave Moses the Scripture‚ÄĒthe decisive authority‚Äé‚ÄĒ‚Äéthat perhaps you would be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

2:54.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Moses said to his people, ‚ÄúO my people! Surely you have wronged yourselves by worshipping the calf, so turn in repentance to your Creator and execute ňĻthe calf-worshippers amongňļ yourselves. That is best for you in the sight of your Creator.‚ÄĚ Then He accepted your repentance. Surely He is the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.

2:55.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when you said, ‚ÄúO Moses! We will never believe you until we see Allah with our own eyes,‚ÄĚ so a thunderbolt struck you while you were looking on.

2:56.text: Then We brought you back to life after your death, so that perhaps you would be grateful.

2:57.text: And ňĻremember whenňļ We shaded you with clouds and sent down to you manna and quails,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúEat from the good things We have provided for you.‚ÄĚ The evildoers ňĻcertainlyňļ did not wrong Us, but wronged themselves.
footnotes: ¬†Manna (heavenly bread) and quails (chicken-like birds) sustained the children of Israel in the wilderness after they left Egypt.

2:58.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We said, ‚ÄúEnter this city and eat freely from wherever you please; enter the gate with humility, saying, ‚ÄėAbsolve us.‚Äô We will forgive your sins and multiply the reward for the good-doers.‚ÄĚ

2:59.text: But the wrongdoers changed the words they were commanded to say. So We sent down a punishment from the heavens upon them for their rebelliousness.

2:60.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Moses prayed for water for his people, We said, ‚ÄúStrike the rock with your staff.‚ÄĚ Then twelve springs gushed out, ňĻandňļ each tribe knew its drinking place. ňĻWe then said,ňļ ‚ÄúEat and drink of Allah‚Äôs provisions, and do not go about spreading corruption in the land.‚ÄĚ

2:61.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when you said, ‚ÄúO Moses! We cannot endure the same meal ňĻevery dayňļ. So ňĻjustňļ call upon your Lord on our behalf, He will bring forth for us some of what the earth produces of herbs, cucumbers, garlic, lentils, and onions.‚ÄĚ Moses scolded ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúDo you exchange what is better for what is worse? ňĻYou canňļ go down to any village and you will find what you have asked for.‚ÄĚ They were stricken with disgrace and misery, and they invited the displeasure of Allah for rejecting Allah‚Äôs signs and unjustly killing the prophets. This is ňĻa fair rewardňļ for their disobedience and violations.

2:62.text: Indeed, the believers, Jews, Christians, and Sabians<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒwhoever ňĻtrulyňļ believes in Allah and the Last Day and does good will have their reward with their Lord. And there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The Sabians are an indigenous group that believes in a supreme being and lives mostly in Iraq.
¬†This verse should be understood in light of 3:19 and 3:85. For more details, see the Introduction.

2:63.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We took a covenant from you and raised the mountain above you ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúHold firmly to that ňĻScriptureňļ which We have given you and observe its teachings so perhaps you will become mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.‚ÄĚ

2:64.text: Yet you turned away afterwards. Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, you would have certainly been of the losers.

2:65.text: You are already aware of those of you who broke the Sabbath. We said to them, ‚ÄúBe disgraced apes!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Although many scholars believe that these individuals were turned into real apes, others interpret this verse in a metaphorical sense. This style is not uncommon in the Quran. See¬†62:5 regarding the donkey that carries books and 2:18 regarding the deaf, dumb, and blind.

2:66.text: So We made their fate an example to present and future generations, and a lesson to the God-fearing.

2:67.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Moses said to his people, ‚ÄúAllah commands you to sacrifice a cow.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They replied, ‚ÄúAre you mocking us?‚ÄĚ Moses responded, ‚ÄúI seek refuge in Allah from acting foolishly!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This s√Ľrah¬†is named after the cow in this story, which happened at the time of Moses (Ô∑ļ). A rich man was killed by his nephew, his only heir, and the body was thrown at the door of an innocent man. After a long investigation, no one was identified as the killer. Moses (Ô∑ļ) prayed for guidance and was told that the only way to find the killer was to sacrifice a cow and strike the victim with a piece of it. When this was done, the victim spoke miraculously and said who the killer was.

2:68.text: They said, ‚ÄúCall upon your Lord to clarify for us what type ňĻof cowňļ it should be!‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúAllah says, ‚ÄėThe cow should neither be old nor young but in between. So do as you are commanded!‚Äô‚ÄĚ

2:69.text: They said, ‚ÄúCall upon your Lord to specify for us its colour.‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúAllah says, ‚ÄėIt should be a bright yellow cow‚ÄĒpleasant to see.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

2:70.text: Again they said, ‚ÄúCall upon your Lord so that He may make clear to us which cow, for all cows look the same to us. Then, Allah willing, we will be guided ňĻto the right oneňļ.‚ÄĚ

2:71.text: He replied, ‚ÄúAllah says, ‚ÄėIt should have been used neither to till the soil nor water the fields; wholesome and without blemish.‚Äô‚ÄĚ They said, ‚ÄúNow you have come with the truth.‚ÄĚ Yet they still slaughtered it hesitantly!

2:72.text: ňĻThis isňļ when a man was killed and you disputed who the killer was, but Allah revealed what you concealed.

2:73.text: So We instructed, ‚ÄúStrike the dead body with a piece of the cow.‚ÄĚ This is how ňĻeasilyňļ Allah brings the dead to life, showing you His signs so that you may understand.

2:74.text: Even then your hearts became hardened like a rock or even harder, for some rocks gush rivers; others split, spilling water; while others are humbled in awe of Allah. And Allah is never unaware of what you do.

2:75.text: Do you ňĻbelievers stillňļ expect them to be true to you, though a group of them would hear the word of Allah then knowingly corrupt it after understanding it?

2:76.text: When they meet the believers they say, ‚ÄúWe believe.‚ÄĚ But in private they say ňĻto each otherňļ, ‚ÄúWill you disclose to the believers the knowledge Allah has revealed to you,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so that they may use it against you before your Lord? Do you not understand?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., verses prophesizing the coming of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) in the Torah (including Deuteronomy 18:15-18 and 33:2).

2:77.text: Do they not know that Allah is aware of what they conceal and what they reveal?

2:78.text: And among them are the illiterate who know nothing about the Scripture except lies, and ňĻsoňļ they ňĻwishfullyňļ speculate.

2:79.text: So woe<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to those who distort the Scripture with their own hands then say, ‚ÄúThis is from Allah‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒseeking a fleeting gain! So woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for what they have earned.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., warning of a curse or punishment.

2:80.text: ňĻSome ofňļ the Jews claim, ‚ÄúThe Fire will not touch us except for a number of days.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHave you taken a pledge from Allah‚ÄĒfor Allah never breaks His word‚ÄĒor are you ňĻjustňļ saying about Allah what you do not know?‚ÄĚ

2:81.text: But no! Those who commit evil and are engrossed in sin will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.

2:82.text: And those who believe and do good will be the residents of Paradise. They will be there forever.

2:83.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We took a covenant from the children of Israel ňĻstatingňļ, ‚ÄúWorship none but Allah; be kind to parents, relatives, orphans and the needy; speak kindly to people; establish prayer; and pay alms-tax.‚ÄĚ But you ňĻIsraelitesňļ turned away‚ÄĒexcept for a few of you‚ÄĒand were indifferent.

2:84.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We took your covenant that you would neither shed each other‚Äôs blood nor expel each other from their homes, you gave your pledge and bore witness.

2:85.text: But here you are, killing each other and expelling some of your people from their homes, aiding one another in sin and aggression; and when those ňĻexpelledňļ come to you as captives, you still ransom them‚ÄĒthough expelling them was unlawful for you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Do you believe in some of the Scripture and reject the rest? Is there any reward for those who do so among you other than disgrace in this worldly life and being subjected to the harshest punishment on the Day of Judgment? For Allah is never unaware of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†The indigenous people of Medina were divided into two warring groups: Al-Aws¬†and Al-Khazraj. Some Jewish tribes were allied with the former and others with the latter, which meant that in times of war each of these Jewish tribes had to fight along with their allies against their enemies, including other Jews. When the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) migrated to Medina, he brought about a lasting peace in the city.

2:86.text: These are the ones who trade the Hereafter for the life of this world. So their punishment will not be reduced, nor will they be helped.

2:87.text: Indeed, We gave Moses the Book and sent after him successive messengers. And We gave Jesus, son of Mary, clear proofs and supported him with the holy spirit.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Why is it that every time a messenger comes to you ňĻIsraelitesňļ with something you do not like, you become arrogant, rejecting some and killing others?
footnotes: ¬†The holy spirit is Gabriel, a mighty angel created from light, whose main duty is to communicate Allah‚Äôs messages to prophets.

2:88.text: They say, ‚ÄúOur hearts are unreceptive!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†In fact, Allah has condemned them for their disbelief. They have but little faith.
footnotes: ¬†Their hearts are unreceptive because they claim they have enough knowledge already.

2:89.text: Although they used to pray for victory ňĻby means of the Prophetňļ over the polytheists,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†when there came to them a Book from Allah which they recognized,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†confirming the Scripture they had ňĻin their handsňļ, they rejected it. So may Allah‚Äôs condemnation be upon the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†lit., the disbelievers.
¬†i.e., the Quran.

2:90.text: Miserable is the price they have sold their souls for‚ÄĒdenying Allah‚Äôs revelation and resenting Allah for granting His grace to whoever He wills of His servants! They have earned wrath upon wrath. And such disbelievers will suffer a humiliating punishment.

2:91.text: When it is said to them: ‚ÄúBelieve in what Allah has revealed,‚ÄĚ they reply, ‚ÄúWe only believe in what was sent down to us,‚ÄĚ and they deny what came afterwards, though it is the truth confirming their own Scriptures! Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWhy then did you kill Allah‚Äôs prophets before, if you are ňĻtrulyňļ believers?‚ÄĚ

2:92.text: Indeed, Moses came to you with clear proofs, then you worshipped the calf in his absence, acting wrongfully.

2:93.text: And when We took your covenant and raised the mountain above you ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúHold firmly to that ňĻScriptureňļ which We have given you and obey,‚ÄĚ they answered, ‚ÄúWe hear and disobey.‚ÄĚ The love of the calf was rooted in their hearts because of their disbelief. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHow evil is what your ňĻso-calledňļ belief prompts you to do, if you ňĻactuallyňļ believe ňĻin the Torahňļ!‚ÄĚ

2:94.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf the ňĻeternalňļ Home of the Hereafter with Allah is exclusively for you ňĻIsraelitesňļ out of all humanity, then wish for death if what you say is true!‚ÄĚ

2:95.text: But they will never wish for that because of what their hands have done.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of the wrongdoers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., disobeying Allah, killing some of the prophets (including Zachariah and John the Baptist), claiming to have killed Jesus, accusing Mary of adultery, and dealing with usury. See¬†4:153-158.

2:96.text: You will surely find them clinging to life more eagerly than any other people, even more than polytheists. Each one of them wishes to live a thousand years. But even if they were to live that long, it would not save them from the punishment. And Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

2:97.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhoever is an enemy of Gabriel should know that he revealed this ňĻQuranňļ to your heart by Allah‚Äôs Will, confirming what came before it‚ÄĒa guide and good news for the believers.‚ÄĚ

2:98.text: Whoever is an enemy of Allah, His angels, His messengers, Gabriel, and Michael, then ňĻlet them know thatňļ Allah is certainly the enemy of the disbelievers.

2:99.text: Indeed, We have sent down to you ňĻO Prophetňļ clear revelations. ňĻButňļ none will deny them except the rebellious.

2:100.text: Why is it that every time they make a covenant, a group of them casts it aside? In fact, most of them do not believe.

2:101.text: Now, when a messenger from Allah has come to them‚ÄĒconfirming their own Scriptures‚ÄĒsome of the People of the Book cast the Book of Allah behind their backs as if they did not know.

2:102.text: They ňĻinsteadňļ followed the magic promoted by the devils during the reign of Solomon. Never did Solomon disbelieve, rather the devils disbelieved. They taught magic to the people, along with what had been revealed to the two angels, H√Ęr√Ľt and M√Ęr√Ľt, in Babylon.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†The two angels never taught anyone without saying, ‚ÄúWe are only a test ňĻfor youňļ, so do not abandon ňĻyourňļ faith.‚ÄĚ Yet people learned ňĻmagicňļ that caused a rift ňĻevenňļ between husband and wife; although their magic could not harm anyone except by Allah‚Äôs Will. They learned what harmed them and did not benefit them‚ÄĒalthough they already knew that whoever buys into magic would have no share in the Hereafter. Miserable indeed was the price for which they sold their souls, if only they knew!
footnotes: ¬†The two angels, H√Ęr√Ľt and M√Ęr√Ľt, were sent to enlighten the people in Babylon so they would not confuse magic tricks with miracles. Still some people abused this knowledge, causing mischief in the land. These practices persisted until the time of Solomon, who himself was falsely accused of utilizing magic to run his kingdom, subdue the jinn, and control the wind.

2:103.text: If only they were faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, there would have been a better reward from Allah, if only they knew!

2:104.text: O believers! Do not say, ‚ÄúR√Ę‚Äôina.‚ÄĚ [Herd us!] But say, ‚ÄúUnŠļďurna,‚ÄĚ [Tend to us!] and listen ňĻattentivelyňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And the disbelievers will suffer a painful punishment.
footnotes:
¬†Some of the disbelievers used to ¬†play with words when they addressed the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) in order to ridicule him. So instead of saying, r√Ę‚Äôina ‚Äúlisten to us,‚ÄĚ they would say, r√Ę‚Äô√ģna¬†‚Äúour shepherd‚ÄĚ or ‚Äúthe foolish among us.‚ÄĚ They would say loudly, ‚ÄúWe listen,‚ÄĚ then whisper, ‚Äúbut we disobey!‚ÄĚ and say, ‚ÄúHear us,‚ÄĚ then, ‚Äúmay you never hear!‚ÄĚ They used to say to each other, ‚ÄúIf he had truly been a prophet of Allah, he would have known that we are mocking him.‚ÄĚ Therefore, this verse (along with

¬†4:46) was revealed commanding the believers to avoid such words altogether. Better words are recommended.

2:105.text: The disbelievers from the People of the Book and the polytheists would not want you to receive any blessing from your Lord, but Allah selects whoever He wills for His mercy. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

2:106.text: If We ever abrogate<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†a verse or cause it to be forgotten, We replace it with a better or similar one. Do you not know that Allah is Most Capable of everything?
footnotes: ¬†The Quran was revealed over a period of twenty-three years. New rules were introduced when the believers were ready to accept and apply them. Replacing a ruling with another is called abrogation (naskh). For example, alcohol consumption was forbidden over three stages (see¬†2:219, 4:43, and 5:90, respectively). Some of the Prophet‚Äôs companions said, ‚ÄúIf drinking had been forbidden from day one, no one would have accepted Islam!‚ÄĚ

2:107.text: Do you not know that the kingdom of the heavens and the earth belongs ňĻonlyňļ to Allah, and you have no guardian or helper besides Allah?

2:108.text: Or do you ňĻbelieversňļ intend to ask of your Messenger as Moses was asked before?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But whoever trades belief for disbelief has truly strayed from the Right Way.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., asking to make Allah visible to them and make the angels talk to them.

2:109.text: Many among the People of the Book wish they could turn you ňĻbelieversňļ back to disbelief because of their envy, after the truth has been made clear to them. Pardon and bear with them until Allah delivers His decision. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

2:110.text: Establish prayer, and pay alms-tax. Whatever good you send forth for yourselves, you will ňĻcertainlyňļ find ňĻits rewardňļ with Allah. Surely Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.

2:111.text: The Jews and Christians each claim that none will enter Paradise except those of their own faith. These are their desires. Reply, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShow ňĻmeňļ your proof if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

2:112.text: But no! Whoever submits themselves to Allah and does good will have their reward with their Lord. And there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.

2:113.text: The Jews say, ‚ÄúThe Christians have nothing to stand on‚ÄĚ and the Christians say, ‚ÄúThe Jews have nothing to stand on,‚ÄĚ although both recite the Scriptures. And those ňĻpagansňļ who have no knowledge say the same ňĻabout people of faithňļ. Surely Allah will judge between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their dispute.

2:114.text: Who does more wrong than those who prevent Allah‚Äôs Name from being mentioned in His places of worship and strive to destroy them? Such people have no right to enter these places except with fear.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†For them is disgrace in this world, and they will suffer a tremendous punishment in the Hereafter.
footnotes: ¬†Or ‚ÄúSuch people should not have even entered these places except with reverence.‚ÄĚ

2:115.text: To Allah belong the east and the west, so wherever you turn you are facing ňĻtowardsňļ Allah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah is All-Encompassing,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†All-Knowing.
footnotes: ¬†lit., wherever you turn, there is the Face of Allah.
¬†His mercy and knowledge covers all.

2:116.text: They<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†say, ‚ÄúAllah has offspring.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Glory be to Him! In fact, to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth‚ÄĒall are subject to His Will.
footnotes: ¬†The Christians, pagans, etc.
¬†Jesus in Christianity, the angels in pagan Arab mythology, etc.

2:117.text: ňĻHe isňļ the Originator of the heavens and the earth! When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And it is!

2:118.text: Those who have no knowledge say, ‚ÄúIf only Allah would speak to us or a sign would come to us!‚ÄĚ The same was said by those who came before. Their hearts are all alike. Indeed, We have made the signs clear for people of sure faith.

2:119.text: We have surely sent you with the truth ňĻO Prophetňļ as a deliverer of good news and a warner. And you will not be accountable for the residents of the Hellfire.

2:120.text: Never will the Jews or Christians be pleased with you, until you follow their faith. Say, ‚ÄúAllah‚Äôs guidance is the only ňĻtrueňļ guidance.‚ÄĚ And if you were to follow their desires after ňĻallňļ the knowledge that has come to you, there would be none to protect or help you against Allah.

2:121.text: Those We have given the Book follow it as it should be followed. It is they who ňĻtrulyňļ believe in it. As for those who reject it, it is they who are the losers.

2:122.text: O Children of Israel! Remember My favours upon you and how I honoured you above the others.

2:123.text: And guard yourselves against the Day when no soul will be of any help to another. No ransom will be taken, no intercession accepted, and no help will be given.

2:124.text: ňĻRememberňļ when Abraham was tested by his Lord with ňĻcertainňļ commandments, which he fulfilled. Allah said, ‚ÄúI will certainly make you into a role model for the people.‚ÄĚ Abraham asked, ‚ÄúWhat about my offspring?‚ÄĚ Allah replied, ‚ÄúMy covenant is not extended to the wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

2:125.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We made the Sacred House<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†a centre and a sanctuary for the people ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúňĻYou mayňļ take the standing-place of Abraham<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†as a site of prayer.‚ÄĚ And We entrusted Abraham and Ishmael to purify My House for those who circle it, who meditate in it, and who bow and prostrate themselves ňĻin prayerňļ.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúThe Sacred House‚ÄĚ (Ka‚Äôbah) is a cube-shaped building in Mecca, Islam‚Äôs holiest sanctuary, which Muslims face when they pray five times a day.
¬†‚ÄúThe standing-place of Abraham‚ÄĚ is the stone on which Abraham stood while he was building the Ka‚Äôbah.

2:126.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Abraham said, ‚ÄúMy Lord, make this city ňĻof Meccaňļ secure and provide fruits to its people‚ÄĒthose among them who believe in Allah and the Last Day.‚ÄĚ He answered, ‚ÄúAs for those who disbelieve, I will let them enjoy themselves for a little while, then I will condemn them to the torment of the Fire. What an evil destination!‚ÄĚ

2:127.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Abraham raised the foundation of the House with Ishmael, ňĻboth praying,ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! Accept ňĻthisňļ from us. You are indeed the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

2:128.text: Our Lord! Make us both ňĻfullyňļ submit to You<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and from our descendants a nation that will submit to You. Show us our rituals, and turn to us in grace. You are truly the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†lit., make both of us Muslims. The word ‚ÄúMuslim‚ÄĚ means ‚Äúone who submits to Allah.‚ÄĚ All of the prophets submitted to Allah and were, therefore, Muslims.

2:129.text: Our Lord! Raise from among them a messenger who will recite to them Your revelations, teach them the Book and wisdom, and purify them. Indeed, You ňĻaloneňļ are the Almighty, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ

2:130.text: And who would reject the faith of Abraham except a fool! We certainly chose him in this life, and in the Hereafter he will surely be among the righteous.

2:131.text: When his Lord ordered him, ‚ÄúSubmit ňĻto My Willňļ,‚ÄĚ he responded, ‚ÄúI submit to the Lord of all worlds.‚ÄĚ

2:132.text: This was the advice of Abraham‚ÄĒas well as Jacob‚ÄĒto his children, ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúIndeed, Allah has chosen for you this faith; so do not die except in ňĻa state of fullňļ submission.‚ÄĚ

2:133.text: Or did you witness when death came to Jacob? He asked his children, ‚ÄúWho will you worship after my passing?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúWe will ňĻcontinue toňļ worship your God, the God of your forefathers‚ÄĒAbraham, Ishmael, and Isaac‚ÄĒthe One God. And to Him we ňĻallňļ submit.‚ÄĚ

2:134.text: That was a community that had already gone before. For them is what they earned and for you is what you have earned. And you will not be accountable for what they have done.

2:135.text: The Jews and Christians each say, ‚ÄúFollow our faith to be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúNo! We follow the faith of Abraham, the upright‚ÄĒwho was not a polytheist.‚ÄĚ

2:136.text: Say, ňĻO believers,ňļ ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah and what has been revealed to us; and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and his descendants; and what was given to Moses, Jesus, and other prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them. And to Allah we all submit.‚ÄĚ

2:137.text: So if they believe in what you believe, then they will indeed be ňĻrightlyňļ guided. But if they turn away, they are simply opposed ňĻto the truthňļ. But Allah will spare you their evil. For He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

2:138.text: This is the ňĻnaturalňļ Way of Allah. And who is better than Allah in ordaining a way? And we worship ňĻnone butňļ Him.

2:139.text: Say, ‚ÄúWould you dispute with us about Allah, while He is our Lord and your Lord? We are accountable for our deeds and you for yours. And we are devoted to Him ňĻaloneňļ.

2:140.text: Do you claim that Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and his descendants were all Jews or Christians?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúWho is more knowledgeable: you or Allah?‚ÄĚ Who does more wrong than those who hide the testimony they received from Allah? And Allah is never unaware of what you do.

2:141.text: That was a community that had already gone before. For them is what they earned and for you is what you have earned. And you will not be accountable for what they have done.

2:142.text: The foolish among the people will ask, ‚ÄúWhy did they turn away from the direction of prayer they used to face?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThe east and west belong ňĻonlyňļ to Allah. He guides whoever He wills to the Straight Path.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†For about sixteen months after daily prayers became obligatory, Muslims used to face towards Jerusalem before the order came to face a new qiblah¬†(direction of prayer)‚ÄĒthe Sacred Mosque in Mecca.

2:143.text: And so We have made you ňĻbelieversňļ an upright<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†community so that you may be witnesses over humanity and that the Messenger may be a witness over you. We assigned your former direction of prayer only to distinguish those who would remain faithful to the Messenger from those who would lose faith. It was certainly a difficult test except for those ňĻrightlyňļ guided by Allah. And Allah would never discount your ňĻprevious acts ofňļ faith. Surely Allah is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to humanity.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., moderate, balanced, and outstanding.

2:144.text: Indeed, We see you ňĻO Prophetňļ turning your face towards heaven. Now We will make you turn towards a direction ňĻof prayerňļ that will please you. So turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque ňĻin Meccaňļ‚ÄĒwherever you are, turn your faces towards it. Those who were given the Scripture certainly know this to be the truth from their Lord. And Allah is never unaware of what they do.

2:145.text: Even if you were to bring every proof to the People of the Book, they would not accept your direction ňĻof prayerňļ, nor would you accept theirs; nor would any of them accept the direction ňĻof prayerňļ of another. And if you were to follow their desires after ňĻallňļ the knowledge that has come to you, then you would certainly be one of the wrongdoers.

2:146.text: Those We have given the Scripture recognize this ňĻProphetňļ as they recognize their own children. Yet a group of them hides the truth knowingly.

2:147.text: ňĻThis isňļ the truth from your Lord, so do not ever be one of those who doubt.

2:148.text: Everyone turns to their own direction ňĻof prayerňļ. So compete with one another in doing good. Wherever you are, Allah will bring you all together ňĻfor judgmentňļ. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

2:149.text: Wherever you are ňĻO Prophetňļ, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque. This is certainly the truth from your Lord. And Allah is never unaware of what you ňĻallňļ do.

2:150.text: Wherever you are ňĻO Prophetňļ, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque.
