21:36.text: When the disbelievers see you ňĻO Prophetňļ, they only make fun of you, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúIs this the one who speaks ňĻillňļ of your gods?‚ÄĚ while they disbelieve at the mention of the Most Compassionate.

21:37.text: Humankind is made of haste. I will soon show you My signs, so do not ask Me to hasten them.

21:38.text: They ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

21:39.text: If only the disbelievers knew that a time will come when they will not be able to keep the Fire off their faces or backs, nor will they be helped.

21:40.text: In fact, the Hour will take them by surprise, leaving them stunned. So they will not be able to avert it, nor will it be delayed from them.

21:41.text: ňĻOtherňļ messengers had already been ridiculed before you ňĻO Prophetňļ, but those who mocked them were overtaken by what they used to ridicule.

21:42.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWho can defend you by day or by night against the Most Compassionate?‚ÄĚ Still they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.

21:43.text: Or do they have gods‚ÄĒother than Us‚ÄĒthat can protect them? They cannot ňĻevenňļ protect themselves, nor will they be aided against Us.

21:44.text: In fact, We have allowed enjoyment for these ňĻMeccansňļ and their forefathers for such a long time ňĻthat they took it for grantedňļ. Do they not see that We gradually reduce ňĻtheirňļ land from its borders? Is it they who will then prevail?

21:45.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI warn you only by revelation.‚ÄĚ But the deaf cannot hear the call when they are warned!

21:46.text: If they were touched by even a breath of your Lord‚Äôs torment, they would certainly cry, ‚ÄúWoe to us! We have really been wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

21:47.text: We set up the scales of justice for the Day of Judgment, so no soul will be wronged in the least. And ňĻevenňļ if a deed is the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as a ňĻvigilantňļ Reckoner.

21:48.text: Indeed, We granted Moses and Aaron the decisive authority‚ÄĒa light and a reminder for the righteous,

21:49.text: who are in awe of their Lord without seeing Him,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and are fearful of the Hour.
footnotes: ¬†This can also mean that they are in awe of their Lord as much in private as they are in public.

21:50.text: And this ňĻQuranňļ is a blessed reminder which We have revealed. Will you ňĻpagansňļ then deny it?

21:51.text: And indeed, We had granted Abraham sound judgment early on, for We knew him well ňĻto be worthy of itňļ.

21:52.text: ňĻRememberňļ when he questioned his father and his people, ‚ÄúWhat are these statues to which you are so devoted?‚ÄĚ

21:53.text: They replied, ‚ÄúWe found our forefathers worshipping them.‚ÄĚ

21:54.text: He responded, ‚ÄúIndeed, you and your forefathers have been clearly astray.‚ÄĚ

21:55.text: They asked, ‚ÄúHave you come to us with the truth, or is this a joke?‚ÄĚ

21:56.text: He replied, ‚ÄúIn fact, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them ňĻbothňļ. And to that I bear witness.‚ÄĚ

21:57.text: ňĻThen he said to himself,ňļ ‚ÄúBy Allah! I will surely plot against your idols after you have turned your backs and gone away.‚ÄĚ

21:58.text: So he smashed them into pieces, except the biggest of them, so they might turn to it ňĻfor answersňļ.

21:59.text: They protested, ‚ÄúWho dared do this to our gods? It must be an evildoer!‚ÄĚ

21:60.text: Some said, ‚ÄúWe heard a young man, called Abraham, speaking ňĻillňļ of them.‚ÄĚ

21:61.text: They demanded, ‚ÄúBring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness ňĻhis trialňļ.‚ÄĚ

21:62.text: They asked, ‚ÄúWas it you who did this to our gods, O ¬†Abraham?‚ÄĚ

21:63.text: He replied ňĻsarcasticallyňļ, ‚ÄúNo, this one‚ÄĒthe biggest of them‚ÄĒdid it! So ask them, if they can talk!‚ÄĚ

21:64.text: So they came back to their senses, saying ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúYou yourselves are truly the wrongdoers!‚ÄĚ

21:65.text: Then they ňĻquicklyňļ regressed to their ňĻoriginalňļ mind-set, ňĻarguing,ňļ ‚ÄúYou already know that those ňĻidolsňļ cannot talk.‚ÄĚ

21:66.text: He rebuked ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúDo you then worship‚ÄĒinstead of Allah‚ÄĒwhat can neither benefit nor harm you in any way?

21:67.text: Shame on you and whatever you worship instead of Allah! Do you not have any sense?‚ÄĚ

21:68.text: They concluded, ‚ÄúBurn him up to avenge your gods, if you must act.‚ÄĚ

21:69.text: We ordered, ‚ÄúO fire! Be cool and safe for Abraham!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Bukh√Ęri that Abraham (Ô∑ļ) said, while being thrown into the fire, ‚ÄúAllah ňĻaloneňļ is sufficient ňĻas an aidňļ for us and ňĻHeňļ is the best Protector.‚ÄĚ

21:70.text: They had sought to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.

21:71.text: Then We delivered him, along with Lot, to the land We had showered with blessings for all people.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Abraham and his nephew, Lot, migrated from Babel, Iraq to Jerusalem.

21:72.text: And We blessed him with Isaac ňĻas a sonňļ and Jacob ňĻas a grandsonňļ, as an additional favour‚ÄĒmaking all of them righteous.

21:73.text: We ňĻalsoňļ made them leaders, guiding by Our command, and inspired them to do good deeds, establish prayer, and pay alms-tax. And they were devoted to Our worship.

21:74.text: And to Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge, and delivered him from the society engrossed in shameful practices. They were certainly an evil, rebellious people.

21:75.text: And We admitted him into Our mercy, ňĻforňļ he was truly one of the righteous.

21:76.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Noah had cried out to Us earlier, so We responded to him and delivered him and his family<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from the great distress.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the believers in his family.

21:77.text: And We made him prevail over those who had rejected Our signs. They were truly an evil people, so We drowned them all.

21:78.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when David and Solomon passed judgment regarding the crops ruined ňĻat nightňļ by someone‚Äôs sheep, and We were witness to their judgments.

21:79.text: We guided ňĻyoungňļ Solomon to a fairer settlement,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and granted each of them wisdom and knowledge. We subjected the mountains as well as the birds to hymn ňĻOur praisesňļ along with David. It is We Who did ňĻit allňļ.
footnotes: ¬†A man‚Äôs flock of sheep strayed into another man‚Äôs vineyard, eating and destroying all his produce. When the two men came to David for judgment, he ruled that the shepherd must give his animals to the vineyard owner in compensation for the damage. On their way out, the two men met young Solomon and the shepherd complained to him. Solomon discussed the case with his father, and suggested that the sheep should be kept with the man who lost his produce so he may benefit from their milk and wool, while the shepherd worked on the farm to restore it to its original state. Eventually the famer would take back his farm in perfect condition, and the sheep would be returned to the shepherd. David was impressed by his son‚Äôs insight and approved his fair judgment immediately.

21:80.text: We taught him the art of making body armour to protect you in battle. Will you then be grateful?

21:81.text: And to Solomon We subjected the raging winds, blowing by his command to the land We had showered with blessings. It is We Who know everything.

21:82.text: And ňĻWe subjectedňļ some jinn<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†that dived for him,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and performed other duties. It is We Who kept them in check.
footnotes: ¬†lit., devils.
¬†The jinn dived to bring him pearls.

21:83.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Job cried out to his Lord, ‚ÄúI have been touched with adversity,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to his loss of health, wealth, and children.

21:84.text: So We answered his prayer and removed his adversity, and gave him back his family, twice as many, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for the ňĻdevotedňļ worshippers.

21:85.text: And ňĻrememberňļ Ishmael, Enoch, and ‚Īęul-Kifl.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They were all steadfast.
footnotes: ¬†Scholars are in disagreement as to whether ‚Īęul-Kifl was a prophet or just a righteous man. Those who maintain that he was a prophet identify him with various Biblical prophets such as Ezekiel, Isaiah, and Obadiah.

21:86.text: We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were truly of the righteous.

21:87.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when the Man of the Whale stormed off ňĻfrom his cityňļ in a rage, thinking We would not restrain him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Then in the ňĻveils ofňļ darkness<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†he cried out, ‚ÄúThere is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except You. Glory be to You! I have certainly done wrong.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Or ‚Äúthinking We will let him get away with it.‚ÄĚ Jonah (Ô∑ļ) had been met with denial for many years. When he sensed the coming of Allah‚Äôs torment, he abandoned his city without Allah‚Äôs permission. Eventually, his people repented before the coming of the torment, and Allah accepted their repentance (see¬†10:98), whereas Jonah ended up in the belly of the whale (see¬†37:140-148).
¬†The darkness of the night, the sea, and the belly of the whale.

21:88.text: So We answered his prayer and rescued him from anguish. And so do We save the ňĻtrueňļ believers.

21:89.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Zachariah cried out to his Lord, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Do not leave me childless, though You are the Best of Successors.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, ‚ÄúYou will be there for eternity after all pass away.‚ÄĚ

21:90.text: So We answered his prayer, granted him John, and made his wife fertile. Indeed, they used to race in doing good, and call upon Us with hope and fear, totally humbling themselves before Us.

21:91.text: And ňĻrememberňļ the one who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her through Our angel, ňĻGabriel,ňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†making her and her son a sign for all peoples.
footnotes: ¬†Gabriel breathed into the sleeves of Mary‚Äôs garment so she conceived Jesus.

21:92.text: ňĻO prophets!ňļ Indeed, this religion of yours is ňĻonlyňļ one, and I am your Lord, so worship Me ňĻaloneňļ.

21:93.text: Yet the people have divided it into sects. But to Us they will all return.

21:94.text: So whoever does good and is a believer will never be denied ňĻthe reward forňļ their striving, for We are recording it all.

21:95.text: It is impossible for a society which We have destroyed to ever rise again,

21:96.text: until ňĻafterňļ Gog and Magog have broken loose ňĻfrom the barrierňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†swarming down from every hill,

21:97.text: ushering in the True Promise.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒthe disbelievers will stare ňĻin horror, crying,ňļ ‚ÄúOh, woe to us! We have truly been heedless of this. In fact, we have been wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Final Hour.

21:98.text: Certainly you ňĻdisbelieversňļ and whatever you worship instead of Allah will be the fuel of Hell.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†You are ňĻallňļ bound to enter it.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 43:57-58.

21:99.text: Had those idols been ňĻtrueňļ gods, they would not have entered it. And they will be there forever.

21:100.text: In it they will groan, and will not be able to hear.

21:101.text: Surely those for whom We have destined the finest reward will be kept far away from Hell,

21:102.text: not even hearing the slightest hissing from it. And they will delight forever in what their souls desire.

21:103.text: The Supreme Horror ňĻof that Dayňļ will not disturb them, and the angels will greet them, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is your Day, which you have been promised.‚ÄĚ

21:104.text: On that Day We will roll up the heavens like a scroll of writings. Just as We produced the first creation, ňĻsoňļ shall We reproduce it. That is a promise binding on Us. We truly uphold ňĻOur promisesňļ!

21:105.text: Surely, following the ňĻheavenlyňļ Record, We decreed in the Scriptures: ‚ÄúMy righteous servants shall inherit the land.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This refers to Psalm 37:29: ‚ÄúThe righteous shall inherit the land.‚ÄĚ See¬†footnote for 39:47.

21:106.text: Surely this ňĻQuranňļ is sufficient ňĻas a reminderňļ for those devoted to worship.

21:107.text: We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ only as a mercy for the whole world.

21:108.text: Say, ‚ÄúWhat has been revealed to me is this: ‚ÄėYour God is only One God.‚Äô Will you then submit?‚ÄĚ

21:109.text: If they turn away, then say, ‚ÄúI have warned you all equally. I do not know if what you are threatened with is near or far.

21:110.text: Allah surely knows what you say openly and whatever you hide.

21:111.text: I do not know if this ňĻdelayňļ is possibly a test for you and an enjoyment for a while.‚ÄĚ

21:112.text: ňĻIn the end,ňļ the Prophet said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Judge ňĻbetween usňļ in truth. And our Lord is the Most Compassionate, Whose help is sought against what you claim.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, ‚ÄúWe seek Allah‚Äôs help against your claims that He has partners ňĻin worshipňļ.‚ÄĚ

22:1.text: O humanity! Fear your Lord, for the ňĻviolentňļ quaking at the Hour is surely a dreadful thing.

22:2.text: The Day you see it, every nursing mother will abandon what she is nursing, and every pregnant woman will deliver her burden ňĻprematurelyňļ. And you will see people ňĻas if they wereňļ drunk, though they will not be drunk; but the torment of Allah is ňĻterriblyňļ severe.

22:3.text: ňĻStillňļ there are some who dispute about Allah without knowledge, and follow every rebellious devil.

22:4.text: It has been decreed for such devils that whoever takes them as a guide will be misguided and led by them into the torment of the Blaze.

22:5.text: O humanity! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, then ňĻknow thatňļ We did create you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from dust, then from a sperm-drop,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†then ňĻdeveloped you intoňļ a clinging clot,<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†then a lump of flesh<sup foot_note=4>4</sup>‚ÄĒfully formed or unformed<sup foot_note=5>5</sup>‚ÄĒin order to demonstrate ňĻOur powerňļ to you. ňĻThenňļ We settle whatever ňĻembryoňļ We will in the womb for an appointed term, then bring you forth as infants, so that you may reach your prime. Some of you ňĻmayňļ die ňĻyoungňļ, while others are left to reach the most feeble stage of life so that they may know nothing after having known much. And you see the earth lifeless, but as soon as We send down rain upon it, it begins to stir ňĻto lifeňļ and swell, producing every type of pleasant plant.
footnotes: ¬†Your father, Adam.
¬†NuŠĻ≠fah¬†refers to the union of male and female gametes (sperm and egg) which results in the zygote after fertilization.
¬†‚ÄôAlaqah, meaning the embryo resembles a leech.
¬†MuŠłćghah, meaning it resembles a chewed morsel.
¬†Fully formed or defected, evolving into a healthy embryo or ending in miscarriage.

22:6.text: That is because Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Truth, He ňĻaloneňļ gives life to the dead, and He ňĻaloneňļ is Most Capable of everything.

22:7.text: And certainly the Hour is coming, there is no doubt about it. And Allah will surely resurrect those in the graves.

22:8.text: ňĻStillňļ there are some who dispute about Allah without knowledge, guidance, or an enlightening scripture,

22:9.text: turning away ňĻin prideňļ to lead ňĻothersňļ astray from Allah‚Äôs Way. They will suffer disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Judgment We will make them taste the torment of burning.

22:10.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is ňĻthe rewardňļ for what your hands have done. And Allah is never unjust to ňĻHisňļ creation.‚ÄĚ

22:11.text: And there are some who worship Allah on the verge ňĻof faithňļ: if they are blessed with something good, they are content with it; but if they are afflicted with a trial, they relapse ňĻinto disbeliefňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†losing this world and the Hereafter. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the clearest loss.
footnotes: ¬†lit., they tumble on their faces.

22:12.text: They call besides Allah what can neither harm nor benefit them. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the farthest one can stray.

22:13.text: They invoke those whose worship leads to harm, not benefit. What an evil patron and what an evil associate!

22:14.text: Indeed, Allah will admit those who believe and do good into Gardens, under which rivers flow. Surely Allah does what He wills.

22:15.text: Whoever thinks that Allah will not help His Prophet in this world and the Hereafter, let them stretch out a rope to the ceiling and strangle themselves, then let them see if this plan will do away with ňĻthe cause ofňļ their rage.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, no matter what the deniers do, Allah will never stop supporting His Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

22:16.text: And so We revealed this ňĻQuranňļ as clear verses. And Allah certainly guides whoever He wills.

22:17.text: Indeed, the believers, Jews, Sabians,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Christians, Magi,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and the polytheists‚ÄĒAllah will judge between them ňĻallňļ on Judgment Day. Surely Allah is a Witness over all things.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:62.
¬†Magi (singular: magus): Zoroastrians; fire-worshippers.

22:18.text: Do you not see that to Allah bow down ňĻin submissionňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†all those in the heavens and all those on the earth, as well as the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, and ňĻallňļ living beings, as well as many humans, while many are deserving of punishment. And whoever Allah disgraces, none can honour. Surely Allah does what He wills.
footnotes: ¬†lit., prostrate. Meaning, all beings submit to His Will.

22:19.text: These are two opposing groups that disagree about their Lord: as for the disbelievers, garments of Fire will be cut out for them and boiling water will be poured over their heads,

22:20.text: melting whatever is in their bellies, along with their skin.

22:21.text: And awaiting them are maces of iron.

22:22.text: Whenever they try to escape from Hell‚ÄĒout of anguish‚ÄĒthey will be forced back into it, ňĻand will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste the torment of burning!‚ÄĚ

22:23.text: ňĻButňļ Allah will surely admit those who believe and do good into Gardens, under which rivers flow, where they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing will be silk,

22:24.text: for they have been guided to the best of speech, and they have been guided to the Commendable Path.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚Äú‚Ä¶ and they have been guided to the Path of the Praiseworthy ňĻGodňļ.‚ÄĚ

22:25.text: Indeed, those who persist in disbelief and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah and from the Sacred Mosque<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒwhich We have appointed for all people, residents and visitors alike‚ÄĒalong with whoever intends to deviate by doing wrong in it,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†We will cause them to taste a painful punishment.
footnotes: ¬†The Sacred Mosque is in Mecca.

¬†Generally, intending to do wrong does not necessitate a punishment, unless the person acts upon it. But the Sacred Mosque is unique in that intending to sin in it is sufficient to bring punishment from Allah, unless the person repents.

22:26.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We assigned to Abraham the site of the House, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúDo not associate anything with Me ňĻin worshipňļ and purify My House for those who circle ňĻthe Ka‚Äôbahňļ, stand ňĻin prayerňļ, and bow and prostrate themselves.

22:27.text: Call ňĻallňļ people to the pilgrimage.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel from every distant path,
footnotes: ¬†According to Ibn Kath√ģr, Abraham (Ô∑ļ) said to Allah, ‚ÄúBut my voice cannot reach all peoples.‚ÄĚ Allah responded, ‚ÄúYou make the call, and We will deliver the invitation to all.‚ÄĚ

22:28.text: so they may obtain the benefits ňĻin storeňļ for them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and pronounce the Name of Allah on appointed days over the sacrificial animals He has provided for them. So eat from their meat and feed the desperately poor.
footnotes: ¬†Spiritual benefits such as prayers, pilgrimage, and forgiveness of sins, as well as worldly benefits such as meeting Muslims from everywhere and trading.

22:29.text: Then let them groom themselves,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†fulfil their vows, and circle the Ancient House.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Shaving their hair, clipping nails, and taking a shower.

22:30.text: That is so. And whoever honours the rituals of Allah, it is best for them in the sight of their Lord. The ňĻmeat ofňļ cattle has been made lawful for you, except what has ňĻalreadyňļ been recited to you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So shun the impurity of idolatry, and shun words of falsehood.

22:31.text: Be upright ňĻin devotionňļ to Allah, associating none with Him ňĻin worshipňļ. For whoever associates ňĻothersňļ with Allah is like someone who has fallen from the sky and is either snatched away by birds or swept by the wind to a remote place.

22:32.text: That is so. And whoever honours the symbols of Allah, it is certainly out of the piety of the heart.

22:33.text: You may benefit from sacrificial animals for an appointed term,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then their place of sacrifice is at the Ancient House.
footnotes: ¬†Milking or riding them until the time for sacrifice.

22:34.text: For every community We appointed a rite of sacrifice so that they may pronounce the Name of Allah over the sacrificial animals He has provided for them. For your God is only One God, so submit yourselves to Him ňĻaloneňļ. And give good news ňĻO Prophetňļ to the humble:

22:35.text: those whose hearts tremble at the remembrance of Allah, who patiently endure whatever may befall them, and who establish prayer and donate from what We have provided for them.

22:36.text: We have made sacrificial camels ňĻand cattleňļ among the symbols of Allah, in which there is ňĻmuchňļ good for you. So pronounce the Name of Allah over them when they are lined up ňĻfor sacrificeňļ. Once they have fallen ňĻlifelessňļ on their sides, you may eat from their meat, and feed the needy‚ÄĒthose who do not beg, and those who do. In this way We have subjected these ňĻanimalsňļ to you so that you may be grateful.

22:37.text: Neither their meat nor blood reaches Allah. Rather, it is your piety that reaches Him. This is how He has subjected them to you so that you may proclaim the greatness of Allah for what He has guided you to, and give good news to the good-doers.

22:38.text: Indeed, Allah defends those who believe. Surely Allah does not like whoever is deceitful, ungrateful.

22:39.text: Permission ňĻto fight backňļ is ňĻherebyňļ granted to those being fought, for they have been wronged.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah is truly Most Capable of helping them ňĻprevailňļ.
footnotes: ¬†For over thirteen years, Muslims had not been allowed to fight back against the brutality of the Meccan pagans, leading to the emigration of the Prophet and many of his companions to Medina. As the hostilities continued, this verse was later revealed allowing Muslims to fight back in self-defence.

22:40.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who have been expelled from their homes for no reason other than proclaiming: ‚ÄúOur Lord is Allah.‚ÄĚ Had Allah not repelled ňĻthe aggression ofňļ some people by means of others, destruction would have surely claimed monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques in which Allah‚Äôs Name is often mentioned. Allah will certainly help those who stand up for Him. Allah is truly All-Powerful, Almighty.

22:41.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who, if established in the land by Us, would perform prayer, pay alms-tax, encourage what is good, and forbid what is evil. And with Allah rests the outcome of all affairs.

22:42.text: If they deny you ňĻO Prophetňļ, so did the people of Noah before them, as well as ňĻthe tribes ofňļ ‚Äô»ād and Tham√Ľd,

22:43.text: the people of Abraham, the people of Lot,

22:44.text: and the residents of Midian. And Moses was denied ňĻtooňļ. But I delayed ňĻthe fate ofňļ the disbelievers ňĻuntil their appointed timeňļ then seized them. And how severe was My response!

22:45.text: Many are the societies We have destroyed for persisting in wrongdoing, leaving them in total ruin. ňĻMany areňļ also the abandoned wells and lofty palaces!

22:46.text: Have they not travelled throughout the land so their hearts may reason, and their ears may listen? Indeed, it is not the eyes that are blind, but it is the hearts in the chests that grow blind.

22:47.text: They challenge you ňĻO Prophetňļ to hasten the torment. And Allah will never fail in His promise. But a day with your Lord is indeed like a thousand years by your counting.

22:48.text: Many are the societies whose end We delayed while they did wrong, then seized them. And to Me is the final return.

22:49.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO humanity! I am only sent to you with a clear warning.

22:50.text: So those who believe and do good will have forgiveness and an honourable provision.

22:51.text: But those who strive to discredit Our revelations, they will be the residents of the Hellfire.‚ÄĚ

22:52.text: Whenever We sent a messenger or a prophet before you ňĻO Prophetňļ and he recited ňĻOur revelationsňļ, Satan would influence ňĻpeople‚Äôs understanding ofňļ his recitation. But ňĻeventuallyňļ Allah would eliminate Satan‚Äôs influence. Then Allah would ňĻfirmlyňļ establish His revelations. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

22:53.text: All that so He may make Satan‚Äôs influence a trial for those ňĻhypocritesňļ whose hearts are sick and those ňĻdisbelieversňļ whose hearts are hardened. Surely the wrongdoers are totally engrossed in opposition.

22:54.text: ňĻThis isňļ also so that those gifted with knowledge would know that this ňĻrevelationňļ is the truth from your Lord, so they have faith in it, and so their hearts would submit humbly to it. And Allah surely guides the believers to the Straight Path.

22:55.text: Yet the disbelievers will persist in doubt about this ňĻrevelationňļ until the Hour takes them by surprise, or the torment of a terminating<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Day comes to them.
footnotes: ¬†The Day ňĻof Judgmentňļ is described as ‚Äôaq√ģm¬†(lit., barren) because it will terminate all forms of life on earth and no new day will be born.

22:56.text: All authority on that Day is for Allah ňĻaloneňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He will judge between them. So those who believe and do good will be in the Gardens of Bliss.
footnotes: ¬†Allah grants authority to some of His servants in this world, but none will have authority on Judgment Day except Him. See¬†3:26.

22:57.text: But those who disbelieve and deny Our revelations, it is they who will suffer a humiliating punishment.

22:58.text: As for those who emigrate in the cause of Allah and then are martyred or die, Allah will indeed grant them a good provision. Surely Allah is the Best Provider.

22:59.text: He will certainly admit them into a place they will be pleased with. For Allah is truly All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.

22:60.text: That is so. And whoever retaliates in equivalence to the injury they have received, and then are wronged ňĻagainňļ, Allah will certainly help them. Surely Allah is Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving.

22:61.text: That is because Allah causes the night to merge into the day, and the day into the night. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

22:62.text: That is because Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Truth and what they invoke besides Him is falsehood, and Allah ňĻaloneňļ is truly the Most High, All-Great.

22:63.text: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky, then the earth becomes green? Surely Allah is Most Subtle, All-Aware.

22:64.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Allah ňĻaloneňļ is truly the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

22:65.text: Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†whatever is in the earth as well as the ships ňĻthatňļ sail through the sea by His command? He keeps the sky from falling down on the earth except by His permission. Surely Allah is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to humanity.
footnotes: ¬†For your service.

22:66.text: And He is the One Who gave you life, then will cause you to die, and then will bring you back to life. ňĻButňļ surely humankind is ever ungrateful.

22:67.text: For every community We appointed a code<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†of life to follow.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†So do not let them dispute with you ňĻO Prophetňļ in this matter. And invite ňĻallňļ to your Lord, for you are truly on the Right Guidance.
footnotes: ¬†All prophets came with the same message: have faith in one God and do good. But each faith-community had their own law.
¬†The Muslim code of life is called Shar√ģ‚Äôah, which means a ‚Äúpath.‚ÄĚ For more information, see¬†the Introduction.

22:68.text: But if they argue with you, then say, ‚ÄúAllah knows best what you do.‚ÄĚ

22:69.text: Allah will judge between you ňĻallňļ on Judgment Day regarding your differences.

22:70.text: Do you not know that Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth? Surely it is all ňĻwrittenňļ in a Record. That is certainly easy for Allah.

22:71.text: Yet they worship besides Allah that for which He has sent down no authority, and of which they have no knowledge. The wrongdoers will have no helper.

22:72.text: Whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, you ňĻO Prophetňļ recognize rage on the faces of the disbelievers, as if they are going to snap at those who recite Our revelations to them. Say, ‚ÄúShall I inform you of something far more enraging than that? ňĻIt isňļ the Fire with which Allah has threatened those who disbelieve. What an evil destination!‚ÄĚ

22:73.text: O humanity! A lesson is set forth, so listen to it ňĻcarefullyňļ: those ňĻidolsňļ you invoke besides Allah can never create ňĻso much asňļ a fly, even if they ňĻallňļ were to come together for that. And if a fly were to snatch anything away from them, they cannot ňĻevenňļ retrieve it from the fly. How powerless are those who invoke and those invoked!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúHow weak are the seekers (i.e., the idols) and the sought (i.e., the fly)!‚ÄĚ

22:74.text: They have not shown Allah the reverence He deserves. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty.

22:75.text: Allah selects messengers from both angels and people, for Allah is truly All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

22:76.text: He knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them. And to Allah ňĻallňļ matters will be returned ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

22:77.text: O believers! Bow down, prostrate yourselves, worship your Lord, and do ňĻwhat isňļ good so that you may be successful.

22:78.text: Strive for ňĻthe cause ofňļ Allah in the way He deserves, for ňĻit isňļ He ňĻWhoňļ has chosen you, and laid upon you no hardship in the religion‚ÄĒthe way of your forefather Abraham. ňĻIt is Allahňļ Who named you ‚Äėthe ones who submit‚Äô<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻin theňļ earlier ňĻScripturesňļ and in this ňĻQuranňļ, so that the Messenger may be a witness over you, and that you may be witnesses over humanity. So establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and hold fast to Allah. He ňĻaloneňļ is your Guardian. What an excellent Guardian, and what an excellent Helper!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Muslims.

23:2.text: those who humble themselves in prayer;

footnotes: ¬†Zak√Ęh¬†means purification and growth. Paying alms-tax and charity are forms of purifying one‚Äôs wealth.

23:5.text: those who guard their chastity<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., private parts.

23:6.text: except with their wives or those ňĻbondwomenňļ in their possession,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for then they are free from blame,
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 4:3.

23:7.text: but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors;

23:8.text: ňĻthe believers are alsoňļ those who are true to their trusts and covenants;

23:9.text: and those who are ňĻproperlyňļ observant of their prayers.

23:10.text: These are the ones who will be awarded

23:11.text: Paradise as their own.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They will be there forever.
footnotes: ¬†Al-Firdaws, the highest place in Paradise.

23:12.text: And indeed, We created humankind<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from an extract of clay,
footnotes: ¬†Adam (Ô∑ļ).

23:13.text: then placed each ňĻhumanňļ as a sperm-drop<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†in a secure place,
footnotes: ¬†See footnote for 22:5.

23:14.text: then We developed the drop into a clinging clot, then developed the clot into a lump ňĻof fleshňļ, then developed the lump into bones, then clothed the bones with flesh, then We brought it into being as a new creation.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So Blessed is Allah, the Best of Creators.
footnotes: ¬†This happens when the spirit is breathed into the embryo.

23:15.text: After that you will surely die,

23:16.text: then on the Day of Judgment you will be resurrected.

23:17.text: And indeed, We created above you seven levels ňĻof heavenňļ. We are never unmindful of ňĻOurňļ creation.

23:18.text: We send down rain from the sky in perfect measure, causing it to soak into the earth. And We are surely able to take it away.

23:19.text: With it We produce for you gardens of palm trees and grapevines, in which there are abundant fruits, and from which you may eat,

23:20.text: as well as ňĻoliveňļ trees which grow at Mount Sinai, providing oil and a condiment to eat.

23:21.text: And there is certainly a lesson for you in cattle, from whose bellies We give you ňĻmilkňļ to drink, and in them are many other benefits for you, and from them you may eat.

23:22.text: And you are carried upon ňĻsome ofňļ them and upon ships.

23:23.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people. He declared, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear ňĻHimňļ?‚ÄĚ

23:24.text: But the disbelieving chiefs of his people said ňĻto the massesňļ, ‚ÄúThis is only a human like you, who wants to be superior to you. Had Allah willed, He could have easily sent down angels ňĻinsteadňļ. We have never heard of this in ňĻthe history ofňļ our forefathers.

23:25.text: He is simply insane, so bear with him for a while.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†So he may recover from his ‚Äúmadness‚ÄĚ or die.

23:26.text: Noah prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Help me, because they have denied ňĻmeňļ.‚ÄĚ

23:27.text: So We inspired him: ‚ÄúBuild the Ark under Our ňĻwatchfulňļ Eyes and directions. Then when Our command comes and the oven bursts ňĻwith waterňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†take on board a pair from every species along with your family‚ÄĒexcept those against whom the decree ňĻto drownňļ has already been passed. And do not plead with Me for those who have done wrong, for they will surely be drowned.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Noah was given a signal that once the water gushes out of a particular oven then the Flood is about to begin. It can also be translated as: ‚ÄúThe fountains of the earth gushed forth.‚ÄĚ

23:28.text: Then when you and those with you have settled in the Ark, say, ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah, Who saved us from the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

23:29.text: And pray, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Allow me a blessed landing, for You are the best accommodator.‚ÄĚ

23:30.text: Surely in this are lessons.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And We ňĻalwaysňļ put ňĻpeopleňļ to the test.
footnotes: ¬†lit., signs.

23:31.text: Then We raised another generation after them,

23:32.text: and sent to them a messenger from among themselves, ňĻdeclaring,ňļ ‚ÄúWorship Allah ňĻaloneňļ. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear ňĻHimňļ?‚ÄĚ

23:33.text: But the chiefs of his people‚ÄĒwho disbelieved, denied the meeting ňĻwith Allahňļ in the Hereafter, and were spoiled by the worldly luxuries We had provided for them‚ÄĒsaid ňĻto the massesňļ, ‚ÄúThis is only a human like you. He eats what you eat, and drinks what you drink.

23:34.text: And if you ňĻeverňļ obey a human like yourselves, then you would certainly be losers.

23:35.text: Does he promise you that once you are dead and reduced to dust and bones, you will be brought forth ňĻaliveňļ?

23:36.text: Impossible, simply impossible is what you are promised!

23:37.text: There is nothing beyond our worldly life. We die, others are born, and none will be resurrected.

23:38.text: He is no more than a man who has fabricated a lie about Allah, and we will never believe in him.‚ÄĚ

23:39.text: The messenger prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Help me, because they have denied ňĻmeňļ.‚ÄĚ

23:40.text: Allah responded, ‚ÄúSoon they will be truly regretful.‚ÄĚ

23:41.text: Then the ňĻmightyňļ blast overtook them with justice, and We reduced them to rubble. So away with the wrongdoing people!

23:42.text: Then We raised other generations after them.

23:43.text: No people can advance their doom, nor can they delay it.

23:44.text: Then We sent Our messengers in succession: whenever a messenger came to his people, they denied him. So We destroyed them, one after the other, reducing them to ňĻcautionaryňļ tales. So away with the people who refuse to believe!

23:45.text: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and compelling proof
footnotes: ¬†The nine signs are: the staff, the hand (both mentioned in 20:17-22), famine, shortage of crops, floods, locusts, lice, frogs, and blood (all mentioned in 7:130-133).

23:46.text: to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they behaved arrogantly and were a tyrannical people.

23:47.text: They argued, ‚ÄúWill we believe in two humans, like ourselves, whose people are slaves to us?‚ÄĚ

23:48.text: So they rejected them both, and ňĻsoňļ were among those destroyed.

23:49.text: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture, so perhaps his people would be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

23:50.text: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, and gave them refuge on high ground‚ÄĒa ňĻsuitableňļ place for rest with flowing water.

23:51.text: O messengers! Eat from what is good and lawful, and act righteously. Indeed, I fully know what you do.

23:52.text: Surely this religion of yours is ňĻonlyňļ one, and I am your Lord, so fear Me ňĻaloneňļ.

23:53.text: Yet the people have divided it into different sects, each rejoicing in what they have.

23:54.text: So leave them ňĻO Prophetňļ in their heedlessness for a while.

23:55.text: Do they think, since We provide them with wealth and children,

23:56.text: that We hasten to ňĻhonourňļ them ňĻwithňļ all kinds of good? No! They are not aware.

23:57.text: Surely those who tremble in awe of their Lord,

23:58.text: and who believe in the revelations of their Lord,

23:59.text: and who associate none with their Lord,

23:60.text: and who do whatever ňĻgoodňļ they do with their hearts fearful, ňĻknowingňļ that they will return to their Lord<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in an authentic narration collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i that ‚Äô»āishah, the Prophet‚Äôs wife, asked him (Ô∑ļ) if this verse refers to those who steal or drink ňĻalcoholňļ. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) answered, ‚ÄúNo, it refers to those who pray, fast, and donate, but are afraid that their deeds are rejected because they are not good enough.‚ÄĚ Then he (Ô∑ļ) recited the last part of the verse: ‚ÄúIt is they who race to do good deeds, always taking the lead.‚ÄĚ

23:61.text: it is they who race to do good deeds, always taking the lead.

23:62.text: We never require of any soul more than what it can afford. And with Us is a record which speaks the truth. None will be wronged.

23:63.text: But the hearts of those ňĻwho disbelieveňļ are oblivious to ňĻall ofňļ this,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and they have other ňĻevilňļ deeds, opposite to this, in which they are engrossed.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the believers‚Äô good deeds mentioned in 23:57-62.

23:64.text: But as soon as We seize their elite with torment, they start to cry for help.

23:65.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúDo not cry for help today. Surely you will never be saved from Us.

23:66.text: Indeed, My revelations were recited to you, but you used to back away ňĻin disgustňļ,

23:67.text: boasting of the Sacred House, and babbling ňĻnonsense about the Quranňļ by night.‚ÄĚ

23:68.text: Is it because they have never contemplated the Word ňĻof Allahňļ? Or ňĻbecauseňļ there has come to them something that did not come to their forefathers?

23:69.text: Or ňĻbecauseňļ they failed to recognize their Messenger, and so they denied him?

23:70.text: Or ňĻbecauseňļ they say, ‚ÄúHe is insane?‚ÄĚ In fact, he has come to them with the truth, but most of them are resentful of the truth.

23:71.text: Had the truth followed their desires,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†the heavens, the earth, and all those in them would have certainly been corrupted. In fact, We have brought them ňĻthe means toňļ their glory, but they turn away from it.
footnotes: ¬†Their desire that the universe has more than one God. See¬†21:22.

23:72.text: Or ňĻis it becauseňļ you ňĻO Prophetňļ are asking them for tribute? But the reward of your Lord is best, for He is the Best Provider.

23:73.text: And surely you are calling them to the Straight Path,

23:74.text: but those who disbelieve in the Hereafter are certainly deviating from that Path.

23:75.text: ňĻEvenňļ if We had mercy on them and removed their affliction,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†they would still persist in their transgression, wandering blindly.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to famine which affected the pagans of Mecca.

23:76.text: And We have already seized them with torment, but they never humbled themselves to their Lord, nor did they ňĻsubmissivelyňļ appeal ňĻto Himňļ.

23:77.text: But as soon as We open for them a gate of severe punishment, they will be utterly desperate.

23:78.text: He is the One Who created for you hearing, sight, and intellect. ňĻYetňļ you hardly give any thanks.

23:79.text: And He is the One Who has dispersed you ňĻallňļ over the earth, and to Him you will ňĻallňļ be gathered.

23:80.text: And He is the One Who gives life and causes death, and to Him belongs the alternation of the day and night. Will you not then understand?

23:81.text: But they ňĻjustňļ say what their predecessors said.

23:82.text: They said, ‚ÄúOnce we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?

23:83.text: We have already been promised this, as well as our forefathers earlier. This is nothing but ancient fables!‚ÄĚ

23:84.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúTo whom belong the earth and all those on it, if you ňĻreallyňļ know?‚ÄĚ

23:85.text: They will reply, ‚ÄúTo Allah!‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúWhy are you not then mindful?‚ÄĚ

23:86.text: ňĻAndňļ ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúWho is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the Mighty Throne?‚ÄĚ

23:87.text: They will reply, ‚ÄúAllah.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúWill you not then fear ňĻHimňļ?‚ÄĚ

23:88.text: Ask ňĻthem also,ňļ ‚ÄúIn Whose Hands is the authority over all things, protecting ňĻallňļ while none can protect against Him, if you ňĻreallyňļ know?‚ÄĚ

23:89.text: They will reply, ‚ÄúAllah.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúHow are you then so deluded?‚ÄĚ

23:90.text: In fact, We have brought them the truth, and they are certainly liars.

23:91.text: Allah has never had ňĻanyňļ offspring, nor is there any god besides Him. Otherwise, each god would have taken away what he created, and they would have tried to dominate one another. Glorified is Allah above what they claim!

23:92.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Knower of the seen and unseen. Exalted is He above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ.

23:93.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúMy Lord! Should You show me what they<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†are threatened with,
footnotes: ¬†The Meccan pagans.

23:94.text: then, my Lord, do not count me among the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

23:95.text: We are indeed able to show you what We have threatened them with.

23:96.text: Respond to evil with what is best. We know well what they claim.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Their claims that there are many gods, and that the Prophet is a liar, a poet, etc.

23:97.text: And say, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I seek refuge in You from the temptations of the devils.

23:98.text: And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, that they ňĻevenňļ come near me.‚ÄĚ

23:99.text: When death approaches any of them, they cry, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Let me go back,

23:100.text: so I may do good in what I left behind.‚ÄĚ Never! It is only a ňĻuselessňļ appeal they make. And there is a barrier behind them until the Day they are resurrected.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, once they are dead, they will never return to the world.

23:101.text: Then, when the Trumpet will be blown,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†there will be no kinship between them on that Day, nor will they ňĻeven care toňļ ask about one another.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†On the Day of Judgment, the Trumpet will be blown by an angel‚ÄĒcausing all to die. When it is blown a second time, everyone will be raised from the dead for judgment (see¬†39:68).
¬†Everyone will be busy with their own situation.

23:102.text: As for those whose scale is heavy ňĻwith good deedsňļ, it is they who will be successful.

23:103.text: But those whose scale is light, they will have doomed themselves, staying in Hell forever.

23:104.text: The Fire will burn their faces, leaving them deformed.

23:105.text: ňĻIt will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúWere My revelations not recited to you, but you used to deny them?‚ÄĚ

23:106.text: They will cry, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Our ill-fate took hold of us, so we became a misguided people.

23:107.text: Our Lord! Take us out of this ňĻFireňļ. Then if we ever return ňĻto denialňļ, we will truly be wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

23:108.text: Allah will respond, ‚ÄúBe despised in there! Do not ňĻeverňļ plead with Me ňĻagainňļ!

23:109.text: Indeed, there was a group of My servants who used to pray, ‚ÄėOur Lord! We have believed, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of those who show mercy,‚Äô

23:110.text: but you were ňĻso busyňļ making fun of them that it made you forget My remembrance. And you used to laugh at them.

23:111.text: Today I have indeed rewarded them for their perseverance: they are certainly the triumphant.‚ÄĚ

23:112.text: He will ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúHow many years did you remain on earth?‚ÄĚ

23:113.text: They will reply, ‚ÄúWe remained ňĻonlyňļ a day or part of a day.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But ask those who kept count.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Compared to the length and misery of their stay in the grave and Hell, their worldly life will seem very short to them.

23:114.text: He will say, ‚ÄúYou only remained for a little while, if only you knew.

23:115.text: Did you then think that We had created you without purpose, and that you would never be returned to Us?‚ÄĚ

23:116.text: Exalted is Allah, the True King! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him, the Lord of the Honourable Throne.

23:117.text: Whoever invokes, besides Allah, another god‚ÄĒfor which they can have no proof‚ÄĒthey will surely find their penalty with their Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will never succeed.

23:118.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúMy Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the best of those who show mercy.‚ÄĚ

24:1.text: ňĻThis isňļ a s√Ľrah¬†which We have revealed and made ňĻits rulingsňļ obligatory, and revealed in it clear commandments so that you may be mindful.

24:2.text: As for female and male fornicators, give each of them one hundred lashes,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and do not let pity for them make you lenient in ňĻenforcingňļ the law of Allah, if you ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a number of believers witness their punishment.
footnotes: ¬†The address here is to legal authorities. Adultery has to be proven either by confession or the testimony of four reliable witnesses.

24:3.text: A male fornicator would only marry a female fornicator or idolatress. And a female fornicator would only be married to a fornicator or idolater. This is ňĻallňļ forbidden to the believers.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i that Marthad ibn Abi Marthad, a companion of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), asked if he could marry ‚ÄôAn√Ęq, a pagan prostitute he befriended in Mecca before he accepted Islam. So this verse was revealed. However, if someone committed a sin then later repented genuinely, they will be accepted by Allah and the rest of the believers.

24:4.text: Those who accuse chaste women ňĻof adulteryňļ and fail to produce four witnesses, give them eighty lashes ňĻeachňļ. And do not ever accept any testimony from them‚ÄĒfor they are indeed the rebellious‚ÄĒ

24:5.text: except those who repent afterwards and mend their ways, then surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

24:6.text: And those who accuse their wives ňĻof adulteryňļ but have no witness except themselves, the accuser must testify,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†swearing four times by Allah that he is telling the truth,
footnotes: ¬†In order to be spared the punishment for false accusation.

24:7.text: and a fifth oath that Allah may condemn him if he is lying.

24:8.text: For her to be spared the punishment, she must swear four times by Allah that he is telling a lie,

24:9.text: and a fifth oath that Allah may be displeased with her if he is telling the truth.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This ruling is called li‚Äô√Ęn¬†in Islamic legal system. Once the husband and the wife swear each five times, as specified in 24:6-9, the marriage is terminated forever‚ÄĒwhich means that they can never be remarried.

24:10.text: ňĻYou would have suffered,ňļ had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, and had Allah not been Accepting of Repentance, All-Wise.

24:11.text: Indeed, those who came up with that ňĻoutrageousňļ slander are a group of you. Do not think this is bad for you. Rather, it is good for you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They will be punished, each according to their share of the sin. As for their mastermind,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†he will suffer a tremendous punishment.
footnotes: ¬†Because now you can tell who is a true believer and who is a hypocrite.
¬†This refers to ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh ibn Ubai, the leader of the hypocrites in Medina.

24:12.text: If only the believing men and women had thought well of one another, when you heard this ňĻrumourňļ, and said, ‚ÄúThis is clearly ňĻan outrageousňļ slander!‚ÄĚ

24:13.text: Why did they not produce four witnesses? Now, since they have failed to produce witnesses, they are ňĻtrulyňļ liars in the sight of Allah.

24:14.text: Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you in this world and the Hereafter, you would have certainly been touched with a tremendous punishment for what you plunged into‚ÄĒ

24:15.text: when you passed it from one tongue to the other, and said with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, taking it lightly while it is ňĻextremelyňļ serious in the sight of Allah.

24:16.text: If only you had said upon hearing it, ‚ÄúHow can we speak about such a thing! Glory be to You ňĻO Lordňļ! This is a heinous slander!‚ÄĚ

24:17.text: Allah forbids you from ever doing something like this again, if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.

24:18.text: And Allah makes ňĻHisňļ commandments clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

24:19.text: Indeed, those who love to see indecency spread among the believers will suffer a painful punishment in this life and the Hereafter. Allah knows and you do not know.

24:20.text: ňĻYou would have suffered,ňļ had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, and had Allah not been Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.

24:21.text: O believers! Do not follow the footsteps of Satan. Whoever follows Satan‚Äôs footsteps, then ňĻlet them know thatňļ he surely bids ňĻall toňļ immorality and wickedness. Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, none of you would have ever been purified. But Allah purifies whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

24:22.text: Do not let the people of virtue and affluence among you swear to suspend donations to their relatives, the needy, and the emigrants in the cause of Allah. Let them pardon and forgive. Do you not love to be forgiven by Allah? And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This verse was revealed when Abu Bakr AŠĻ£-ŠĻĘidd√ģq, ‚Äô»āishah‚Äôs father, swore to end his financial assistance to his poor cousin, MisŠĻ≠aŠł• ibn Athathah, for taking part in the rumours against ‚Äô»āishah. Once the verse was revealed, Abu Bakr resumed his assistance to MisŠĻ≠aŠł•.

24:23.text: Surely those who accuse chaste, unsuspecting,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†believing women are cursed in this life and the Hereafter. And they will suffer a tremendous punishment
footnotes: ¬†Immoral thoughts never cross the minds of these chaste women and, therefore, they never expect slander.

24:24.text: on the Day their tongues, hands, and feet will testify against them for what they used to do.

24:25.text: On that Day, Allah will give them their just penalty in full, and they will ňĻcome toňļ know that Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Ultimate Truth.

24:26.text: Wicked women are for wicked men, and wicked men are for wicked women. And virtuous women are for virtuous men, and virtuous men are for virtuous women. The virtuous are innocent of what the wicked say. They will have forgiveness and an honourable provision.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This passage declares the innocence of ‚Äô»āishah, the Prophet‚Äôs wife.

24:27.text: O believers! Do not enter any house other than your own until you have asked for permission and greeted its occupants. This is best for you, so perhaps you will be mindful.

24:28.text: If you find no one at home, do not enter it until you have been given permission. And if you are asked to leave, then leave. That is purer for you. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of what you do.

24:29.text: There is no blame on you if you enter public places<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†where there is something of benefit for you. And Allah knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
footnotes: ¬†lit., non-residential buildings.

24:30.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their chastity. That is purer for them. Surely Allah is All-Aware of what they do.

24:31.text: And tell the believing women to lower their gaze and guard their chastity, and not to reveal their adornments<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†except what normally appears.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Let them draw their veils over their chests, and not reveal their ňĻhiddenňļ adornments<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†except to their husbands, their fathers, their fathers-in-law, their sons, their stepsons, their brothers, their brothers‚Äô sons or sisters‚Äô sons, their fellow women, those ňĻbondwomenňļ in their possession, male attendants with no desire, or children who are still unaware of women‚Äôs nakedness. Let them not stomp their feet, drawing attention to their hidden adornments. Turn to Allah in repentance all together, O ¬†believers, so that you may be successful.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., hair, body shape, and underclothes.
¬†i.e., the face, hands, outer clothes, rings, kohl, and henna.
¬†i.e., hair, arms, and legs.

24:32.text: Marry off the ňĻfreeňļ singles among you, as well as the righteous of your bondmen and bondwomen. If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His bounty. For Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing.

24:33.text: And let those who do not have the means to marry keep themselves chaste until Allah enriches them out of His bounty. And if any of those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in your possession desires a contract ňĻto buy their own freedomňļ, make it possible for them, if you find goodness in them. And give them some of Allah‚Äôs wealth which He has granted you. Do not force your ňĻslaveňļ girls into prostitution for your own worldly gains while they wish to remain chaste. And if someone coerces them, then after such a coercion Allah is certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful ňĻto themňļ.

24:34.text: Indeed, We have sent down to you clear revelations, along with examples of those who had gone before you, and a lesson to the God-fearing.

24:35.text: Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His light<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is like a niche in which there is a lamp, the lamp is in a crystal, the crystal is like a shining star, lit from ňĻthe oil ofňļ a blessed olive tree, ňĻlocatedňļ neither to the east nor the west,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†whose oil would almost glow, even without being touched by fire. Light upon light! Allah guides whoever He wills to His light. And Allah sets forth parables for humanity. For Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.
footnotes: ¬†This is a metaphor for the light of guidance in the heart of the believer.
¬†Meaning, the olive tree is wholesome because it is located in a central place, so it is hit by sunrays all day-long, and, therefore, the oil is of a premium quality.

24:36.text: ňĻThat light shinesňļ through houses ňĻof worshipňļ which Allah has ordered to be raised, and where His Name is mentioned. He is glorified there morning and evening

24:37.text: by men<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who are not distracted‚ÄĒeither by buying or selling‚ÄĒfrom Allah‚Äôs remembrance, or performing prayer, or paying alms-tax. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will tremble,
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:46.

24:38.text: ňĻhopingňļ that Allah may reward them according to the best of their deeds, and increase them out of His grace. And Allah provides for whoever He wills without limit.

24:39.text: As for the disbelievers, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert, which the thirsty perceive as water, but when they approach it, they find it to be nothing. Instead, they find Allah there ňĻin the Hereafter, readyňļ to settle their account. And Allah is swift in reckoning.

24:40.text: Or ňĻtheir deeds areňļ like the darkness in a deep sea, covered by waves upon waves,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†topped by ňĻdarkňļ clouds. Darkness upon darkness! If one stretches out their hand, they can hardly see it. And whoever Allah does not bless with light will have no light!
footnotes: ¬†This is another scientific fact mentioned in the Quran: the existence of underwater waves, like layers.

24:41.text: Do you not see that Allah is glorified by all those in the heavens and the earth, even the birds as they soar? Each ňĻinstinctivelyňļ knows their manner of prayer and glorification. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all they do.

24:42.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allah is the final return.

24:43.text: Do you not see that Allah gently drives the clouds, then joins them together, piling them up into masses, from which you see raindrops come forth? And He sends down from the sky mountains ňĻof cloudsňļ loaded with hail, pouring it on whoever He wills and averting it from whoever He wills. The flash of the clouds‚Äô lightning nearly takes away eyesight.

24:44.text: Allah alternates the day and night. Surely in this is a lesson for people of insight.

24:45.text: And Allah has created from water every living creature. Some of them crawl on their bellies, some walk on two legs, and some walk on four. Allah creates whatever He wills. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

24:46.text: We have indeed sent down revelations clarifying ňĻthe truthňļ. But Allah ňĻonlyňļ guides whoever He wills to the Straight Path.

24:47.text: And the hypocrites say, ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah and the Messenger, and we obey.‚ÄĚ Then a group of them turns away soon after that. These are not ňĻtrueňļ believers.

24:48.text: And as soon as they are called to Allah and His Messenger so he may judge between them, a group of them<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†turns away.
footnotes: ¬†Those at fault.

24:49.text: But if the truth is in their favour, they come to him, fully submitting.

24:50.text: Is there a sickness in their hearts? Or are they in doubt? Or do they fear that Allah and His Messenger will be unjust to them? In fact, it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.

24:51.text: The only response of the ňĻtrueňļ believers, when they are called to Allah and His Messenger so he may judge between them, is to say, ‚ÄúWe hear and obey.‚ÄĚ It is they who will ňĻtrulyňļ succeed.

24:52.text: For whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, and fears Allah and is mindful of Him, then it is they who will ňĻtrulyňļ triumph.

24:53.text: They swear by Allah their most solemn oaths that if you ňĻO Prophetňļ were to command them, they would certainly march forth ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ. Say, ‚ÄúňĻYouňļ do not ňĻhave toňļ swear; your obedience is well known!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah is All-Aware of what you do.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This is used in a sarcastic way. It means, ‚ÄúSave your oaths! Everyone knows you are only bluffing. It is actions that matter, not words.‚ÄĚ

24:54.text: Say, ‚ÄúObey Allah and obey the Messenger. But if you turn away, then he is only responsible for his duty<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and you are responsible for yours.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†And if you obey him, you will be ňĻrightlyňļ guided. The Messenger‚Äôs duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet‚Äôs only duty is to deliver the message.
¬†Their only duty is to obey the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) by submitting to Allah.

24:55.text: Allah has promised those of you who believe and do good that He will certainly make them successors in the land, as He did with those before them; and will surely establish for them their faith which He has chosen for them; and will indeed change their fear into security‚ÄĒňĻprovided thatňļ they worship Me, associating nothing with Me. But whoever disbelieves after this ňĻpromiseňļ, it is they who will be the rebellious.

24:56.text: Moreover, establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and obey the Messenger, so you may be shown mercy.

24:57.text: Do not think ňĻO Prophetňļ that the disbelievers can escape in the land. The Fire will be their home. Indeed, what an evil destination!

24:58.text: O believers! Let those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in your possession and those of you who are still under age ask for your permission ňĻto come inňļ at three times: before dawn prayer, when you take off your ňĻouterňļ clothes at noon, and after the late evening prayer. ňĻThese areňļ three times of privacy for you. Other than these times, there is no blame on you or them to move freely, attending to one another. This is how Allah makes the revelations clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

24:59.text: And when your children reach the age of puberty, let them seek permission ňĻto come inňļ, as their seniors do. This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

24:60.text: As for elderly women past the age of marriage, there is no blame on them if they take off their ňĻouterňļ garments, without revealing their adornments. But it is better for them if they avoid this ňĻaltogetherňļ. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

24:61.text: There is no restriction on the blind, or the disabled, or the sick.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Nor on yourselves if you eat from your homes,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†or the homes of your fathers, or your mothers, or your brothers, or your sisters, or your paternal uncles, or your paternal aunts, or your maternal uncles, or your maternal aunts, or from the homes in your trust, or ňĻthe homes ofňļ your friends. There is no blame on you eating together or separately. However, when you enter houses, greet one another with a greeting ňĻof peaceňļ from Allah, blessed and good.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so perhaps you will understand.
footnotes: ¬†There is no blame on any of the three if they do not march forth in Allah‚Äôs cause. Moreover, some Muslims would give the keys of their homes to one of those who could not march forth (namely the blind, the disabled, or the sick) or their own relatives and ask them to enter their houses and eat at will but these people were shy to do that.
¬†i.e., the home of your spouse or children.

¬†If someone is in the house, greet them by saying, ‚ÄėAs-sal√Ęmu ‚Äôalaikum‚Äô¬†(peace be upon you).‚ÄĚ But if there is no one there, you greet yourselves by saying, ‚ÄėAsal√Ęmu ‚Äôalaina wa ‚Äôala ‚Äôib√Ęd-ill√Ęhi aŠĻ£-ŠĻ£√ĘliŠł•√ģn‚Äô¬†(peace be upon us and all righteous servants of Allah).‚ÄĚ

24:62.text: The ňĻtrueňļ believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, and when they are with him on a public matter, they do not leave without his permission. Indeed, those who ask your permission ňĻO Prophetňļ are the ones who ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and His Messenger. So when they ask your permission for a private matter, grant permission to whoever you wish and ask Allah‚Äôs forgiveness for them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

24:63.text: Do not treat the Messenger‚Äôs summons to you ňĻas lightlyňļ as your summons to one another.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Allah certainly knows those of you who slip away, hiding behind others.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†So let those who disobey his orders beware, for an affliction may befall them, or a painful torment may overtake them.
footnotes: ¬†There is also a different interpretation: ‚ÄúDo not call the Messenger ňĻby his nameňļ as you call one another.‚ÄĚ In other words, Do not say, ‚ÄėO MuŠł•ammad.‚Äô Rather, say, ‚ÄėO Prophet.‚Äô
¬†Some hypocrites used to sneak out while the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was talking.

24:64.text: Surely to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. He knows well what you stand for. And ňĻonňļ the Day all will be returned to Him, He will inform them of what they did. For Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

25:1.text: Blessed is the One Who sent down the Decisive Authority<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to His servant,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†so that he may be a warner to the whole world.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Al-Furq√Ęn, which is one of names of the Quran, means "the Decisive Authority.‚ÄĚ
¬†Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).
¬†For both humans and jinn.

25:2.text: ňĻAllah isňļ the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, Who has never had ňĻanyňļ offspring, nor does He have a partner in ňĻgoverningňļ the kingdom. He has created everything, ordaining it precisely.

25:3.text: Yet they have taken besides Him gods who cannot create anything but are themselves created. Nor can they protect or benefit themselves. Nor can they control life, death, or resurrection.

25:4.text: The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ is nothing but a fabrication which he<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†made up with the help of others.‚ÄĚ Their claim is totally unjustified and untrue!
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

25:5.text: And they say, ‚ÄúňĻThese revelations are onlyňļ ancient fables which he has had written down, and they are rehearsed to him morning and evening.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Some Arab pagans claimed that the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) had been taught the Bible by some non-Arab Christians. The Quran itself (16:103) refutes this claim by arguing that a non-Arab could not come up with an eloquent Arabic scripture like the Quran when eloquent Arabs failed to produce something like it. For more details, see¬†the Introduction.

25:6.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ has been revealed by the One Who knows the secrets of the heavens and the earth. Surely He is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†He forgives those who repent, and gives a grace period to those who do not.

25:7.text: And they say ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhat kind of messenger is this who eats food and goes about in market-places ňĻfor a livingňļ? If only an angel had been sent down with him to be his co-warner,

25:8.text: or a treasure had been cast down to him, or he had had a garden from which he may eat!‚ÄĚ And the wrongdoers say ňĻto the believersňļ, ‚ÄúYou are only following a bewitched man.‚ÄĚ

25:9.text: See ňĻO Prophetňļ how they call you names!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So they have gone so ňĻfarňļ astray that they cannot find the ňĻRightňļ Way.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., a magician, a poet, a fabricator, and a madman.

25:10.text: Blessed is the One Who‚ÄĒif He wills‚ÄĒcan give you far better than ňĻallňļ that: gardens under which rivers flow, and palaces as well.

25:11.text: In fact, they deny the Hour.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And for the deniers of the Hour, We have prepared a blazing Fire.
footnotes: ¬†What they have said about you are only excuses to justify their denial of the Hereafter.

25:12.text: Once it sees them from a distance, they will hear it fuming and growling.

25:13.text: And when they are tossed into a narrow place inside ňĻHellňļ, chained together, then and there they will cry out for ňĻinstantňļ destruction.

25:14.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúDo not cry only once for destruction Today, but cry many times over!‚ÄĚ

25:15.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIs this better or the Garden of Eternity which the righteous have been promised, as a reward and ňĻan ultimateňļ destination?

25:16.text: There they will have whatever they wish for, forever. That is a promise ňĻto be sought afterňļ, binding on your Lord.‚ÄĚ

25:17.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day He will gather them along with what they used to worship besides Allah, and ask ňĻthe objects of worshipňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúWas it you who misled these servants of Mine, or did they stray from the Way ňĻon their ownňļ?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†e.g., Jesus and the angels.

25:18.text: They will say, ‚ÄúGlory be to You! It was not right for ňĻothers likeňļ us to take any lords besides You,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but You allowed enjoyment for them and their forefathers ňĻfor so longňļ that they forgot ňĻYourňļ remembrance and became a doomed people.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúThey will say, ‚ÄėGlory be to You! We would not dare to take other lords besides You ňĻso how can we claim to be lords?ňļ‚Äô‚ÄĚ

25:19.text: ňĻThe doomed will be toldňļ, ‚ÄúYour gods have clearly denied your claims. So now you can neither ward off ňĻthe punishmentňļ nor get any help.‚ÄĚ And whoever of you does wrong, We will make them taste a horrible punishment.

25:20.text: We never sent any messenger before you ňĻO Prophetňļ, who did not eat food and go about in market-places. We have made some of you a trial for others. Will you ňĻnot thenňļ be patient? And your Lord is All-Seeing.

25:21.text: Those who do not expect to meet Us say, ‚ÄúIf only the angels were sent down to us, or we could see our Lord!‚ÄĚ They have certainly been carried away by their arrogance and have entirely exceeded all limits.

25:22.text: ňĻButňļ on the Day they will see the angels, there will be no good news for the wicked, who will cry, ‚ÄúKeep away! Away ňĻfrom usňļ!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúAnd the angels will say, ‚ÄėAll good is forbidden to you!‚Äô‚ÄĚ

25:23.text: Then We will turn to whatever ňĻgoodňļ deeds they did, reducing them to scattered dust.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The good deeds of the disbelievers (like charity) will have no weight on Judgment Day.

25:24.text: ňĻButňļ on that Day the residents of Paradise will have the best settlement and the finest place to rest.

25:25.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day the heavens will burst with clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive ranks.

25:26.text: True authority on that Day will belong ňĻonlyňļ to the Most Compassionate.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And it will be a hard day for the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†Some people (like kings and rulers) have some sort of authority in this world. But on Judgment Day Allah will be the sole authority.

25:27.text: And ňĻbeware ofňļ the Day the wrongdoer will bite his nails ňĻin regretňļ and say, ‚ÄúOh! I wish I had followed the Way along with the Messenger!

25:28.text: Woe to me! I wish I had never taken so-and-so as a close friend.

25:29.text: It was he who truly made me stray from the Reminder after it had reached me.‚ÄĚ And Satan has always betrayed humanity.

25:30.text: The Messenger has cried, ‚ÄúO my Lord! My people have indeed received this Quran with neglect.‚ÄĚ

25:31.text: Similarly, We made enemies for every prophet from among the wicked, but sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper.

25:32.text: The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúIf only the Quran had been sent down to him all at once!‚ÄĚ ňĻWe have sent itňļ as such ňĻin stagesňļ so We may reassure your heart with it. And We have revealed it at a deliberate pace.

25:33.text: Whenever they bring you an argument, We come to you with the right refutation and the best explanation.

25:34.text: Those who will be dragged into Hell on their faces will be in the worst place, and are ňĻnowňļ farthest from the ňĻRightňļ Way.

25:35.text: We certainly gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron as his helper.

25:36.text: We had ordered ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúGo to the people who would deny Our signs.‚ÄĚ Then We annihilated the deniers entirely.

25:37.text: And when the people of Noah rejected the messengers,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We drowned them, making them an example to humanity. And We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.
footnotes: ¬†lit., messengers, because denying Noah was equal to denying all of Allah‚Äôs messengers.

25:38.text: Also ňĻWe destroyedňļ ‚Äô»ād, Tham√Ľd, and the people of the Water-pit,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as well as many peoples in between.
footnotes: ¬†Ar-Rass¬†means ‚Äúwell‚ÄĚ or ‚Äúwater-pit‚ÄĚ. This refers to a pagan people, along with Midian, to whom Allah sent Prophet Shu‚Äôaib.

25:39.text: For each We set forth ňĻvariousňļ lessons, and We ultimately destroyed each.

25:40.text: They have certainly passed by the city ňĻof Sodomňļ, which had been showered with a dreadful rain ňĻof stonesňļ. Have they not seen its ruins? But they do not expect to be resurrected.

25:41.text: When they see you ňĻO Prophetňļ, they only make fun of you, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúIs this the one that Allah has sent as a messenger?

25:42.text: He would have almost tricked us away from our gods, had we not been so devoted to them.‚ÄĚ ňĻButňļ soon they will know, when they face the punishment, who is far astray from the ňĻRightňļ Way.

25:43.text: Have you seen ňĻO Prophetňļ the one who has taken their own desires as their god? Will you then be a keeper over them?

25:44.text: Or do you think that most of them listen or understand?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They are only like cattle‚ÄĒno, more than that, they are astray from the ňĻRightňļ Way!<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†They neither pay attention, nor think for themselves. They only follow others blindly.

¬†Generally, animals are obedient and loyal to their masters who care for them and they seem to know their way, whereas Meccan pagans are disobedient and ungrateful to their Lord and Sustainer, choosing to stray from the right path.

25:45.text: Have you not seen how your Lord extends the shade‚ÄĒHe could have simply made it ňĻremainňļ still if He so willed‚ÄĒthen We make the sun its guide,

25:46.text: causing the shade to retreat gradually?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This means that one of the great miracles of Allah is that He allows the sun to rise in the morning, causing darkness to retreat. He could have stopped the sun and the earth from rotating, so half of the world would be engulfed in complete darkness, leading to an imbalance in the earth‚Äôs eco-systems and endangering life on the planet.

25:47.text: He is the One Who has made the night for you as a cover, and ňĻmadeňļ sleep for resting, and the day for rising.

25:48.text: And He is the One Who sends the winds ushering in His mercy, and We send down pure rain from the sky,

25:49.text: giving life to a lifeless land, and providing water for countless animals and humans of Our Own creation.

25:50.text: We certainly disperse it among them so they may be mindful, but most people persist in ungratefulness.

25:51.text: Had We willed, We could have easily sent a warner to every society.

25:52.text: So do not yield to the disbelievers, but strive diligently against them with this ňĻQuranňļ.

25:53.text: And He is the One Who merges the two bodies of water: one fresh and palatable and the other salty and bitter, placing between them a barrier they cannot cross.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This refers to estuaries where salt and fresh waters meet forming brackish water. Although both waters mix together, each still keeps its distinctive qualities.

25:54.text: And He is the One Who creates human beings from a ňĻhumbleňļ liquid,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then establishes for them bonds of kinship and marriage. For your Lord is Most Capable.
footnotes: ¬†The mixture of sperm and egg.

25:55.text: Yet they worship besides Allah what can neither benefit nor harm them. And the disbeliever always collaborates against their Lord.

25:56.text: And We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner.

25:57.text: Say, ‚ÄúI do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ, but whoever wishes, let them pursue the Way to their Lord.‚ÄĚ

25:58.text: Put your trust in the Ever-Living, Who never dies, and glorify His praises. Sufficient is He as All-Aware of the sins of His servants.

25:59.text: ňĻHe isňļ the One Who created the heavens and the earth and everything in between in six Days,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then established Himself on the Throne. ňĻHe isňļ the Most Compassionate! Ask ňĻnone other thanňļ the All-Knowledgeable about Himself.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:54.

25:60.text: When it is said to them, ‚ÄúProstrate to the Most Compassionate,‚ÄĚ they ask ňĻin disgustňļ, ‚ÄúWhat is ‚Äėthe Most Compassionate‚Äô? Will we prostrate to whatever you order us to?‚ÄĚ And it only drives them farther away.

25:61.text: Blessed is the One Who has placed constellations in the sky, as well as a ňĻradiantňļ lamp<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and a luminous moon.
footnotes: ¬†The sun.

25:62.text: And He is the One Who causes the day and the night to alternate, ňĻas a signňļ for whoever desires to be mindful or to be grateful.

25:63.text: The ňĻtrueňļ servants of the Most Compassionate are those who walk on the earth humbly, and when the foolish address them ňĻimproperlyňļ, they only respond with peace.

25:64.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who spend ňĻa good portion ofňļ the night, prostrating themselves and standing before their Lord.

25:65.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who pray, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Keep the punishment of Hell away from us, for its punishment is indeed unrelenting.

25:66.text: It is certainly an evil place to settle and reside.‚ÄĚ

25:67.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who spend neither wastefully nor stingily, but moderately in between.

25:68.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who do not invoke any other god besides Allah, nor take a ňĻhumanňļ life‚ÄĒmade sacred by Allah‚ÄĒexcept with ňĻlegalňļ right,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†nor commit fornication. And whoever does ňĻany ofňļ this will face the penalty.
footnotes: ¬†For example, in retaliation for intentional killing through legal channels.

25:69.text: Their punishment will be multiplied on the Day of Judgment, and they will remain in it forever, in disgrace.

25:70.text: As for those who repent, believe, and do good deeds, they are the ones whose evil deeds Allah will change into good deeds. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

25:71.text: And whoever repents and does good has truly turned to Allah properly.
