25:72.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who do not bear false witness, and when they come across falsehood, they pass ňĻitňļ by with dignity.

25:73.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who, when reminded of the revelation of their Lord, do not turn a blind eye or a deaf ear to it.

25:74.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who pray, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Bless us with ňĻpiousňļ spouses and offspring who will be the joy of our hearts, and make us models for the righteous.‚ÄĚ

25:75.text: It is they who will be rewarded with ňĻelevatedňļ mansions ňĻin Paradiseňļ for their perseverance, and will be received with salutations and ňĻgreetings ofňļ peace,

25:76.text: staying there forever. What an excellent place to settle and reside!

25:77.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYou ňĻallňļ would not ňĻevenňļ matter to my Lord were it not for your faith ňĻin Himňļ. But now you ňĻdisbelieversňļ have denied ňĻthe truthňļ, so the torment is bound to come.‚ÄĚ

26:2.text: These are the verses of the clear Book.

26:3.text: Perhaps you ňĻO Prophetňļ will grieve yourself to death over their disbelief.

26:4.text: If We willed, We could send down upon them a ňĻcompellingňļ sign from the heavens, leaving their necks bent in ňĻutterňļ submission to it.

26:5.text: Whatever new reminder comes to them from the Most Compassionate, they always turn away from it.

26:6.text: They have certainly denied ňĻthe truthňļ, so they will soon face the consequences of their ridicule.

26:7.text: Have they failed to look at the earth, ňĻto seeňļ how many types of fine plants We have caused to grow in it?

26:8.text: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:9.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:10.text: ňĻRememberňļ when your Lord called out to Moses, ‚ÄúGo to the wrongdoing people‚ÄĒ

26:11.text: the people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear ňĻAllahňļ?‚ÄĚ

26:12.text: He replied, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I fear that they will reject me.

26:13.text: And ňĻsoňļ my heart will be broken and my tongue will be tied. So send Aaron along ňĻas a messengerňļ.

26:14.text: Also, they have a charge against me,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so I fear they may kill me.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†For killing an Egyptian by mistake before prophethood. See¬†28:15-17.

26:15.text: Allah responded, ‚ÄúCertainly not! So go, both of you, with Our signs. We will be with you, listening.

26:16.text: Go to Pharaoh and say, ‚ÄėWe are messengers from the Lord of all worlds,

26:17.text: ňĻcommanded to say:ňļ ‚ÄėLet the Children of Israel go with us.‚Äô‚Äô‚ÄĚ

26:18.text: Pharaoh protested, ‚ÄúDid we not raise you among us as a child, and you stayed several years of your life in our care?

26:19.text: Then you did what you did,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†being ňĻutterlyňļ ungrateful!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†See¬†previous footnote.

26:20.text: Moses replied, ‚ÄúI did it then, lacking guidance.

26:21.text: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and made me one of the messengers.

26:22.text: How can that be a ‚Äėfavour,‚Äô of which you remind me, when ňĻit was only becauseňļ you ňĻhaveňļ enslaved the Children of Israel?‚ÄĚ

26:23.text: Pharaoh asked, ‚ÄúAnd what is ‚Äėthe Lord of all worlds‚Äô?‚ÄĚ

26:24.text: Moses replied, ‚ÄúňĻHe isňļ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith.‚ÄĚ

26:25.text: Pharaoh said to those around him, ‚ÄúDid you hear ňĻwhat he saidňļ?‚ÄĚ

26:26.text: Moses added, ‚ÄúňĻHe isňļ your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.‚ÄĚ

26:27.text: Pharaoh said ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúYour messenger, who has been sent to you, must be insane.‚ÄĚ

26:28.text: Moses responded: ‚ÄúňĻHe isňļ the Lord of the east and west, and everything in between, if only you had any sense.‚ÄĚ

26:29.text: Pharaoh threatened, ‚ÄúIf you take any other god besides me, I will certainly have you imprisoned.‚ÄĚ

26:30.text: Moses responded, ‚ÄúEven if I bring you a clear proof?‚ÄĚ

26:31.text: Pharaoh demanded, ‚ÄúBring it then, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

26:32.text: So he threw down his staff and‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒit became a real snake.

26:33.text: Then he drew his hand ňĻout of his collarňļ and it was ňĻshiningňļ white for all to see.

26:34.text: Pharaoh said to the chiefs around him, ‚ÄúHe is indeed a skilled magician,

26:35.text: who seeks to drive you from your land by his magic. So what do you propose?‚ÄĚ

26:36.text: They replied, ‚ÄúLet him and his brother wait and dispatch mobilizers to all cities

26:37.text: to bring you every skilled magician.‚ÄĚ

26:38.text: So the magicians were assembled at the set time on the appointed day.

26:39.text: And the people were asked, ‚ÄúWill you join the gathering,

26:40.text: so that we may follow the magicians if they prevail?‚ÄĚ

26:41.text: When the magicians came, they asked Pharaoh, ‚ÄúShall we have a ňĻsuitableňļ reward if we prevail?‚ÄĚ

26:42.text: He replied, ‚ÄúYes, and you will then certainly be among those closest to me.‚ÄĚ

26:43.text: Moses said to them, ‚ÄúCast whatever you wish to cast.‚ÄĚ

26:44.text: So they cast down their ropes and staffs, saying, ‚ÄúBy Pharaoh‚Äôs might, it is we who will surely prevail.‚ÄĚ

26:45.text: Then Moses threw down his staff, and‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒit devoured the objects of their illusion!

26:46.text: So the magicians fell down, prostrating.

26:47.text: They declared, ‚ÄúWe ňĻnowňļ believe in the Lord of all worlds‚ÄĒ

26:48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.‚ÄĚ

26:49.text: Pharaoh threatened, ‚ÄúHow dare you believe in him before I give you permission? He must be your master who taught you magic, but soon you will see. I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then crucify you all.‚ÄĚ

26:50.text: They responded, ‚ÄúňĻThat would beňļ no harm! Surely to our Lord we will ňĻallňļ return.

26:51.text: We really hope that our Lord will forgive our sins, as we are the first to believe.‚ÄĚ

26:52.text: And We inspired Moses, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúLeave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued.‚ÄĚ

26:53.text: Then Pharaoh sent mobilizers to all cities,

26:54.text: ňĻand said,ňļ ‚ÄúThese ňĻoutcastsňļ are just a handful of people,

26:56.text: but we are all on the alert.‚ÄĚ

26:57.text: So We lured the tyrants<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†out of ňĻtheirňļ gardens, springs,
footnotes: ¬†Pharaoh and his soldiers.

26:59.text: So it was. And We awarded it ňĻallňļ to the Children of Israel.

26:60.text: And so they pursued them at sunrise.

26:61.text: When the two groups came face to face, the companions of Moses cried out, ‚ÄúWe are overtaken for sure.‚ÄĚ

26:62.text: Moses reassured ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúAbsolutely not! My Lord is certainly with me‚ÄĒHe will guide me.‚ÄĚ

26:63.text: So We inspired Moses: ‚ÄúStrike the sea with your staff,‚ÄĚ and the sea was split, each part was like a huge mountain.

26:65.text: and delivered Moses and those with him all together.

26:67.text: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:68.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:69.text: Relate to them ňĻO Prophetňļ the story of Abraham,

26:70.text: when he questioned his father and his people, ‚ÄúWhat is that you worship ňĻbesides Allahňļ?‚ÄĚ

26:71.text: They replied, ‚ÄúWe worship idols, to which we are fully devoted.‚ÄĚ

26:72.text: Abraham asked, ‚ÄúCan they hear you when you call upon them?

26:73.text: Or can they benefit or harm you?‚ÄĚ

26:74.text: They replied, ‚ÄúNo! But we found our forefathers doing the same.‚ÄĚ

26:75.text: Abraham responded, ‚ÄúHave you ňĻreallyňļ considered what you have been worshipping‚ÄĒ

26:77.text: They are ňĻallňļ enemies to me, except the Lord of all worlds.

26:78.text: ňĻHe isňļ the One Who created me, and He ňĻaloneňļ guides me.

26:79.text: ňĻHe isňļ the One Who provides me with food and drink.

26:80.text: And He ňĻaloneňļ heals me when I am sick.

26:81.text: And He ňĻis the One Whoňļ will cause me to die, and then bring me back to life.

26:82.text: And He is ňĻthe Oneňļ Who, I hope, will forgive my flaws<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†on Judgment Day.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in an authentic narration collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i that Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) said that Abraham (Ô∑ļ) did not tell the full truth only on three different occasions: when he justified his absence from the pagan festival by saying ‚ÄėI am really sick‚Äô (see¬†37:89), and when he destroyed the idols and blamed it on the biggest one (see¬†21:63), and when he said that Sarah was his sister to save her from a tyrant ruler.

26:83.text: ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me wisdom, and join me with the righteous.

26:84.text: Bless me with honourable mention among later generations.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†On a daily basis, Muslims invoke Allah‚Äôs blessings upon Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) and his family and Prophet Abraham (Ô∑ļ) and his family, in both obligatory and optional prayers.

26:85.text: Make me one of those awarded the Garden of Bliss.

26:86.text: Forgive my father, for he is certainly one of the misguided.

26:87.text: And do not disgrace me on the Day all will be resurrected‚ÄĒ

26:88.text: the Day when neither wealth nor children will be of any benefit.

26:89.text: Only those who come before Allah with a pure heart ňĻwill be savedňļ.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†A pure and sound heart is that of the believer, compared to that of the disbeliever and the hypocrite.

26:90.text: ňĻOn that Dayňļ Paradise will be brought near to the God-fearing,

26:91.text: and the Hellfire will be displayed to the deviant.

26:92.text: And it will be said to them, ‚ÄúWhere are those you used to worship

26:93.text: besides Allah? Can they help you or even help themselves?‚ÄĚ

26:94.text: Then the idols will be hurled headlong into Hell, along with the deviant

26:95.text: and the soldiers of Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†all together.
footnotes: ¬†Ibl√ģs was the name of Satan before his fall from grace. See footnote for 2:34.

26:96.text: There the deviant will cry while disputing with their idols,

26:98.text: when we made you equal to the Lord of all worlds.

26:99.text: And none led us astray other than the wicked.

26:100.text: Now we have none to intercede for us,

26:102.text: If only we could have a second chance, then we would be believers.‚ÄĚ

26:103.text: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:104.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:105.text: The people of Noah rejected the messengers<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., messengers, because denying Noah (Ô∑ļ) was equal to denying all of Allah‚Äôs messengers.

26:106.text: when their brother Noah said to them, ‚ÄúWill you not fear ňĻAllahňļ?

26:107.text: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

26:108.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:109.text: I do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

26:110.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.‚ÄĚ

26:111.text: They argued, ‚ÄúHow can we believe in you, when you are followed ňĻonlyňļ by the lowest of the low?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The pagans were accusing the impoverished believers of following Noah (Ô∑ļ) only for personal gain, not out of sincere faith.

26:112.text: He responded, ‚ÄúAnd what knowledge do I have of what they do?

26:113.text: Their judgment is with my Lord, if you had any sense!

26:114.text: I am not going to expel the believers.

26:115.text: I am only sent with a clear warning.‚ÄĚ

26:116.text: They threatened, ‚ÄúIf you do not desist, O ¬†Noah, you will surely be stoned ňĻto deathňļ.‚ÄĚ

26:117.text: Noah prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! My people have truly rejected me.

26:118.text: So judge between me and them decisively, and save me and the believers with me.‚ÄĚ

26:119.text: So We saved him and those with him in the fully loaded Ark.

26:120.text: Then afterwards We drowned the rest.

26:121.text: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:122.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:123.text: The people of ‚Äô√ād rejected the messengers

26:124.text: when their brother H√Ľd said to them, ‚ÄúWill you not fear ňĻAllahňļ?

26:125.text: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

26:126.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:127.text: I do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

26:128.text: ňĻWhyňļ do you build a landmark on every high place in vanity,

26:129.text: and construct castles, as if you are going to live forever,

26:130.text: and act so viciously when you attack ňĻothersňļ?

26:131.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:132.text: Fear the One Who has provided you with ňĻthe goodňļ things you know:

26:133.text: He provided you with cattle, and children,

26:135.text: I truly fear for you the torment of a tremendous day.‚ÄĚ

26:136.text: They responded, ‚ÄúIt is all the same to us whether you warn ňĻusňļ or not.

26:137.text: This is simply the tradition of our predecessors.

26:138.text: And we will never be punished.‚ÄĚ

26:139.text: So they rejected him, and ňĻsoňļ We destroyed them. Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:140.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:141.text: The people of Tham√Ľd rejected the messengers

26:142.text: when their brother ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• said to them, ‚ÄúWill you not fear ňĻAllahňļ?

26:143.text: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

26:144.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:145.text: I do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

26:146.text: Do you think you will be ňĻforeverňļ left secure in what you have here:

26:148.text: and ňĻvariousňļ crops, and palm trees ňĻloadedňļ with tender fruit;

26:149.text: to carve homes in the mountains with great skill?

26:150.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:151.text: And do not follow the command of the transgressors,

26:152.text: who spread corruption throughout the land, never setting things right.‚ÄĚ

26:154.text: You are only a human being like us, so bring forth a sign if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

26:155.text: ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• said, ‚ÄúHere is a camel. She will have her turn to drink as you have yours, each on an appointed day.

26:156.text: And do not ever touch her with harm, or you will be overtaken by the torment of a tremendous day.‚ÄĚ

26:157.text: But they killed her, becoming regretful.

26:158.text: So the punishment overtook them. Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:159.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:160.text: The people of Lot rejected the messengers

26:161.text: when their brother Lot said to them, ‚ÄúWill you not fear ňĻAllahňļ?

26:162.text: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

26:163.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:164.text: I do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

26:165.text: Why do you ňĻmenňļ lust after fellow men,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., men of the world.

26:166.text: leaving the wives that your Lord has created for you? In fact, you are a transgressing people.‚ÄĚ

26:167.text: They threatened, ‚ÄúIf you do not desist, O ¬†Lot, you will surely be expelled.‚ÄĚ

26:168.text: Lot responded, ‚ÄúI am truly one of those who despise your ňĻshamefulňļ practice.

26:169.text: My Lord! Save me and my family from ňĻthe consequences ofňļ what they do.‚ÄĚ

26:170.text: So We saved him and all of his family,

26:171.text: except an old woman,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who was one of the doomed.
footnotes: ¬†Lot‚Äôs wife, who betrayed her husband.

26:172.text: Then We utterly destroyed the rest,

26:173.text: pouring upon them a rain ňĻof brimstoneňļ. How evil was the rain of those who had been warned!

26:174.text: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:175.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:176.text: The residents of the Forest rejected the messengers

26:177.text: when Shu‚Äôaib said to them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúWill you not fear ňĻAllahňļ?
footnotes: ¬†Unlike other prophets in this s√Ľrah¬†who are identified as the brothers of fellow tribesmen, Shu‚Äôaib is not identified as the brother of the people of the Aykah¬†(which is the name of the forest where they used to worship a particular tree) because they are identified by faith, rather than a tribal connection.

26:178.text: I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

26:179.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

26:180.text: I do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻmessageňļ. My reward is only from the Lord of all worlds.

26:181.text: Give full measure, and cause no loss ňĻto othersňļ.

26:183.text: and do not defraud people of their property. Nor go about spreading corruption in the land.

26:184.text: And fear the One Who created you and ňĻallňļ earlier peoples.‚ÄĚ

26:186.text: Also, you are only a human being like us, and we think you are indeed a liar.

26:187.text: So cause ňĻdeadlyňļ pieces of the sky to fall upon us, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

26:188.text: Shu‚Äôaib responded, ‚ÄúMy Lord knows best whatever you do.‚ÄĚ

26:189.text: So they rejected him, and ňĻsoňļ were overtaken by the torment of the day of the ňĻdeadlyňļ cloud.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†That was really a torment of a tremendous day.
footnotes: ¬†They were targeted by scorching heat, so they did not know where to go. Finally, a soothing cloud appeared in the sky, so they rushed to it for shade, then the cloud rained torment upon them, as they had requested.

26:190.text: Surely in this is a sign. Yet most of them would not believe.

26:191.text: And your Lord is certainly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

26:192.text: This is certainly a revelation from the Lord of all worlds,

26:193.text: which the trustworthy spirit ňĻGabrielňļ brought down

26:194.text: into your heart ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒso that you may be one of the warners‚ÄĒ

26:196.text: And it has indeed been ňĻforetoldňļ in the Scriptures of those before.

26:197.text: Was it not sufficient proof for the deniers that it has been recognized by the knowledgeable among the Children of Israel?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh ibn Sal√Ęm, a Jewish authority, who accepted Islam during the time of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

26:198.text: Had We revealed it to a non-Arab,

26:199.text: who would then recite it to the deniers ňĻin fluent Arabicňļ, still they would not have believed in it!

26:200.text: This is how We allow denial ňĻto steepňļ into the hearts of the wicked.

26:201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment,

26:202.text: which will take them by surprise when they least expect ňĻitňļ.

26:203.text: Then they will cry, ‚ÄúCan we be allowed more time?‚ÄĚ

26:204.text: Do they ňĻreallyňļ seek to hasten Our torment?

26:205.text: Imagine ňĻO Prophetňļ if We allowed them enjoyment for years,

26:206.text: then there came to them what they had been threatened with:

26:207.text: would that enjoyment be of any benefit to them ňĻat allňļ?

26:208.text: We have never destroyed a society without warners

26:209.text: to remind ňĻthemňļ, for We would never wrong ňĻanyoneňļ.

26:210.text: It was not the devils who brought this ňĻQuranňļ down:

26:211.text: it is not for them ňĻto do soňļ, nor can they,

26:212.text: for they are strictly barred from ňĻevenňļ overhearing ňĻitňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The devils can no longer eavesdrop on heaven. See¬†72:8-10.

26:213.text: So do not ever call upon any other god besides Allah, or you will be one of the punished.

26:214.text: And warn ňĻall, starting withňļ your closest relatives,

26:215.text: and be gracious to the believers who follow you.

26:216.text: But if they disobey you, say, ‚ÄúI am certainly free of what you do.‚ÄĚ

26:217.text: Put your trust in the Almighty, Most Merciful,

26:218.text: Who sees you when you rise ňĻfor prayer at nightňļ,

26:219.text: as well as your movements ňĻin prayerňļ along with ňĻfellowňļ worshippers.

26:220.text: He ňĻaloneňļ is indeed the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

26:221.text: Shall I inform you of whom the devils ňĻactuallyňļ descend upon?

26:222.text: They descend upon every sinful liar,

26:223.text: who gives an ňĻattentiveňļ ear ňĻto half-truthsňļ, mostly passing on sheer lies.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This refers to fortune-tellers who listen to Satanic whispers, adding more lies as they pass on the information to people.

26:224.text: As for poets, they are followed ňĻmerelyňļ by deviants.

26:225.text: Do you not see how they rant in every field,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., roam aimlessly in every valley.

26:226.text: only saying what they never do?

26:227.text: Except those who believe, do good, remember Allah often, and ňĻpoeticallyňļ avenge ňĻthe believersňļ after being wrongfully slandered. The wrongdoers will come to know what ňĻevilňļ end they will meet.

27:1.text: ŠĻ¨√Ę-Sń©n. These are the verses of the Quran; the clear Book.

27:2.text: ňĻIt isňļ a guide and good news for the believers:

27:3.text: ňĻthoseňļ who establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and have sure faith in the Hereafter.

27:4.text: As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have certainly made their ňĻevilňļ deeds appealing to them, so they wander blindly.

27:5.text: It is they who will suffer a dreadful torment, and in the Hereafter they will ňĻtrulyňļ be the greatest losers.

27:6.text: And indeed, you ňĻO Prophetňļ are receiving the Quran from the One ňĻWho isňļ All-Wise, All-Knowing.

27:7.text: ňĻRememberňļ when Moses said to his family, ‚ÄúI have spotted a fire. I will either bring you some directions<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from there, or a burning torch so you may warm yourselves.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., information. Moses and his family lost their way in the dark while they were travelling from Midian to Egypt.

27:8.text: But when he came to it, he was called ňĻby Allahňļ, ‚ÄúBlessed is the one at the fire, and whoever is around it!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Glory be to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the angels who were present around the light.

27:9.text: O ¬†Moses! It is truly I. I am Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

27:10.text: Now, throw down your staff!‚ÄĚ But when he saw it slithering like a snake, he ran away without looking back. ňĻAllah reassured him,ňļ ‚ÄúO Moses! Do not be afraid! Messengers should have no fear in My presence.

27:11.text: ňĻFear isňļ only for those who do wrong. But if they later mend ňĻtheirňļ evil ňĻwaysňļ with good, then I am certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

27:12.text: Now put your hand through ňĻthe opening ofňļ your collar, it will come out ňĻshiningňļ white, unblemished.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻThese are twoňļ of nine signs for Pharaoh and his people.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†They have truly been a rebellious people.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 20:22.
¬†See¬†footnote for 23:45.

27:13.text: But when Our enlightening signs came to them, they said, ‚ÄúThis is pure magic.‚ÄĚ

27:14.text: And, although their hearts were convinced the signs were true, they still denied them wrongfully and arrogantly. See then what was the end of the corruptors!

27:15.text: Indeed, We granted knowledge to David and Solomon. And they said ňĻin acknowledgmentňļ, ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah Who has privileged us over many of His faithful servants.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The privilege was their ability to communicate with members of the animal kingdom, control the wind, utilize the jinn for their service, etc.

27:16.text: And David was succeeded by Solomon, who said, ‚ÄúO people! We have been taught the language of birds, and been given everything ňĻwe needňļ. This is indeed a great privilege.‚ÄĚ

27:17.text: Solomon‚Äôs forces of jinn, humans, and birds were rallied for him, perfectly organized.

27:18.text: And when they came across a valley of ants, an ant warned, ‚ÄúO ants! Go quickly into your homes so Solomon and his armies do not crush you, unknowingly.‚ÄĚ

27:19.text: So Solomon smiled in amusement at her words, and prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Inspire me to ňĻalwaysňļ be thankful for Your favours which You have blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please you. Admit me, by Your mercy, into ňĻthe company ofňļ Your righteous servants.‚ÄĚ

27:20.text: ňĻOne dayňļ he inspected the birds, and wondered, ‚ÄúWhy is it that I cannot see the hoopoe? Or could he be absent?

27:21.text: I will surely subject him to a severe punishment, or ňĻevenňļ slaughter him, unless he brings me a compelling excuse.‚ÄĚ

27:22.text: It was not long before the bird came and said, ‚ÄúI have found out something you do not know. I have just come to you from Sheba with sure news.

27:23.text: Indeed, I found a woman ruling over them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who has been given everything ňĻshe needsňļ, and who has a magnificent throne.
footnotes: ¬†The Queen of Sheba is known in Islamic tradition as Bilkis.

27:24.text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah. For Satan has made their deeds appealing to them‚ÄĒhindering them from the ňĻRightňļ Way and leaving them unguided‚ÄĒ

27:25.text: so they do not prostrate to Allah, Who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you ňĻallňļ conceal and what you reveal.

27:26.text: ňĻHe isňļ Allah! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him, the Lord of the Mighty Throne.‚ÄĚ

27:27.text: Solomon said, ‚ÄúWe will see whether you are telling the truth or lying.

27:28.text: Go with this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then stand aside and see how they will respond.‚ÄĚ

27:29.text: The Queen ňĻlaterňļ announced, ‚ÄúO chiefs! Indeed, a noble letter has been delivered to me.

27:30.text: It is from Solomon, and it reads: ‚ÄėIn the Name of Allah‚ÄĒthe Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

27:31.text: Do not be arrogant with me, but come to me, fully submitting ňĻto Allahňļ.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

27:32.text: She said, ‚ÄúO chiefs! Advise me in this matter of mine, for I would never make any decision without you.‚ÄĚ

27:33.text: They responded, ‚ÄúWe are a people of strength and great ňĻmilitaryňļ might, but the decision is yours, so decide what you will command.‚ÄĚ

27:34.text: She reasoned, ‚ÄúIndeed, when kings invade a land, they ruin it and debase its nobles. They really do so!

27:35.text: But I will certainly send him a gift, and see what ňĻresponseňļ my envoys will return with.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†She wanted to test Solomon (Ô∑ļ) to see if he was only a king (so he would be satisfied with a tribute) or a prophet (who would only settle with submission to the True God).

27:36.text: When the chief-envoy came to him, Solomon said, ‚ÄúDo you offer me wealth? What Allah has granted me is far greater than what He has granted you. No! It is you who rejoice in ňĻreceivingňļ gifts.

27:37.text: Go back to them, for we will certainly mobilize against them forces which they can never resist, and we will drive them out from there in disgrace, fully humbled.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†If they refuse to submit.

27:38.text: Solomon asked, ‚ÄúO chiefs! Which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in ňĻfullňļ submission?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†To explain why Prophet Solomon brought the Queen‚Äôs magnificent throne and built the marvellous palace, many traditional commentators cite some folkloric or legendary stories. However, none of these stories is supported by reliable Islamic sources.

27:39.text: One mighty jinn responded, ‚ÄúI can bring it to you before you rise from this council of yours. And I am quite strong and trustworthy for this ňĻtaskňļ.‚ÄĚ

27:40.text: But the one who had knowledge of the Scripture said,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúI can bring it to you in the blink of an eye.‚ÄĚ So when Solomon saw it placed before him, he exclaimed, ‚ÄúThis is by the grace of my Lord to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful, it is only for their own good. But whoever is ungrateful, surely my Lord is Self-Sufficient, Most Generous.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to ‚ÄôAŠĻ£if ibn Barkhiya, a knowledgeable and righteous assistant of Solomon.

27:41.text: ňĻThenňļ Solomon said, ‚ÄúDisguise her throne for her so we may see whether she will recognize ňĻitňļ or she will not be able to.‚ÄĚ

27:42.text: So when she arrived, it was said ňĻto herňļ, ‚ÄúIs your throne like this?‚ÄĚ She replied, ‚ÄúIt looks to be the same. We have ňĻalreadyňļ received knowledge ňĻof Solomon‚Äôs prophethoodňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†before this ňĻmiracleňļ,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and have submitted ňĻto Allahňļ.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Because he had refused her tribute.
¬†Bringing her well-guarded, mighty throne all the way from Sheba, Yemen.

27:43.text: But she had been hindered by what she used to worship instead of Allah, for she was indeed from a disbelieving people.

27:44.text: Then she was told, ‚ÄúEnter the palace.‚ÄĚ But when she saw the hall, she thought it was a body of water, so she bared her legs. Solomon said. ‚ÄúIt is just a palace paved with crystal.‚ÄĚ ňĻAt lastňļ she declared, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have certainly wronged my soul. Now I ňĻfullyňļ submit myself along with Solomon to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.‚ÄĚ

27:45.text: And We certainly sent to the people of Tham√Ľd their brother ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•, proclaiming, ‚ÄúWorship Allah,‚ÄĚ but they suddenly split into two opposing groups.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., believers and disbelievers.

27:46.text: He urged ňĻthe disbelieving groupňļ, ‚ÄúO my people! Why do you ňĻseek toňļ hasten the torment<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†rather than grace?<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†If only you sought Allah‚Äôs forgiveness so you may be shown mercy!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., the evil.
¬†lit., the good.

27:47.text: They replied, ‚ÄúYou and your followers are a bad omen for us.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He responded, ‚ÄúYour omens are destined by Allah. In fact, you are ňĻonlyňļ a people being tested.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†They were struck with famine, which they blamed on ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• and his followers.

27:48.text: And there were in the city nine ňĻeliteňļ men who spread corruption in the land, never doing what is right.

27:49.text: They vowed, ‚ÄúLet us swear by Allah that we will take him and his family down by night. Then we will certainly say to his ňĻclosestňļ heirs, ‚ÄėWe did not witness the murder of his family. We are definitely telling the truth.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

27:50.text: And ňĻsoňļ they made a plan, but We too made a plan, while they were unaware.

27:51.text: See then what the consequences of their plan were: We ňĻutterlyňļ destroyed them and their people all together.

27:52.text: So their homes are there, ňĻbut completelyňļ ruined because of their wrongdoing. Surely in this is a lesson for people of knowledge.

27:53.text: And We delivered those who were faithful and were mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.

27:54.text: And ňĻrememberňļ Lot, when he rebuked ňĻthe men ofňļ his people, ‚ÄúDo you commit that shameful deed while you can see ňĻone anotherňļ?

27:55.text: Do you really lust after men instead of women? In fact, you are ňĻonlyňļ a people acting ignorantly.‚ÄĚ

27:56.text: But his people‚Äôs only response was to say, ‚ÄúExpel Lot‚Äôs followers from your land! They are a people who wish to remain chaste!‚ÄĚ

27:57.text: So We delivered him and his family, except his wife. We had destined her to be one of the doomed.

27:58.text: And We poured upon them a rain ňĻof brimstoneňļ. How evil was the rain of those who had been warned!

27:59.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah, and peace be upon the servants He has chosen.‚ÄĚ ňĻAsk the disbelievers,ňļ ‚ÄúWhich is better: Allah or whatever ňĻgodsňļ they associate ňĻwith Himňļ?‚ÄĚ

27:60.text: Or ňĻask them,ňļ ‚ÄúWho created the heavens and the earth, and sends down rain for you from the sky, by which We cause delightful gardens to grow? You could never cause their trees to grow. Was it another god besides Allah?‚ÄĚ Absolutely not! But they are a people who set up equals ňĻto Allahňļ!

27:61.text: Or ňĻask them,ňļ ‚ÄúWho made the earth a place of settlement, caused rivers to flow through it, placed firm mountains upon it, and set a barrier between ňĻfresh and saltňļ bodies of water?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Was it another god besides Allah?‚ÄĚ Absolutely not! But most of them do not know.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 25:53.

27:62.text: Or ňĻask them,ňļ ‚ÄúWho responds to the distressed when they cry to Him, relieving ňĻtheirňļ affliction, and ňĻWhoňļ makes you successors in the earth? Is it another god besides Allah? Yet you are hardly mindful!‚ÄĚ

27:63.text: Or ňĻask them,ňļ ‚ÄúWho guides you in the darkness of the land and sea,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and sends the winds ushering in His mercy?<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Is it another god besides Allah? Exalted is Allah above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!
footnotes: ¬†Via the stars and constellations.
¬†In the form of rain.

27:64.text: Or ňĻask them,ňļ ‚ÄúWho originates the creation then resurrects it, and gives you provisions from the heavens and the earth? Is it another god besides Allah?‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShow ňĻmeňļ your proof, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

27:65.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúNone in the heavens and the earth has knowledge of the unseen except Allah. Nor do they know when they will be resurrected.

27:66.text: No! Their knowledge of the Hereafter amounts to ignorance. In fact, they are in doubt about it. In truth, they are ňĻtotallyňļ blind to it.

27:67.text: The disbelievers ask, ‚ÄúWhen we and our fathers are reduced to dust, will we really be brought forth ňĻaliveňļ?

27:68.text: We have already been promised this, as well as our forefathers earlier. This is nothing but ancient fables!‚ÄĚ

27:69.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúTravel throughout the land and see the fate of the wicked.‚ÄĚ

27:70.text: Do not grieve for them, nor be distressed by their schemes.

27:71.text: They ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

27:72.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúPerhaps some of what you seek to hasten is close at hand.‚ÄĚ

27:73.text: Surely your Lord is ever Bountiful to humanity,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but most of them are ungrateful.
footnotes: ¬†By delaying their punishment and allowing them time to repent.

27:74.text: And surely your Lord knows what their hearts conceal and what they reveal.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Through their tongues or actions.

27:75.text: For there is nothing hidden in the heavens or the earth without being ňĻwrittenňļ in a perfect Record.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The Record refers to the Preserved Tablet (Al-LawŠł• Al-MaŠł•f√ĽŠļď) in which Allah has written the destiny of His entire creation.

27:76.text: Indeed, this Quran clarifies for the Children of Israel most of what they differ over.

27:77.text: And it is truly a guide and mercy for the believers.

27:78.text: Your Lord will certainly judge between them by His justice, for He is the Almighty, All-Knowing.

27:79.text: So put your trust in Allah, for you are surely upon the ňĻPath ofňļ clear truth.

27:80.text: You certainly cannot make the dead hear ňĻthe truthňļ. Nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs and walk away.

27:81.text: Nor can you lead the blind out of their misguidance. You can make none hear ňĻthe truthňļ except those who believe in Our revelations, ňĻfullyňļ submitting ňĻto Allahňļ.

27:82.text: And when the decree ňĻof the Hourňļ comes to pass against them, We will bring forth for them a beast from the earth,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†telling them that the people had no sure faith in Our revelations.
footnotes: ¬†The beast is believed to be one of the major signs of the Day of Judgment. No further details are given in reliable resources about this beast.

27:83.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day We will gather from every faith-community a group of those who denied Our revelations, and they will be driven in ranks.

27:84.text: When they ňĻfinallyňļ come before their Lord, He will ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúDid you deny My revelations without ňĻevenňļ comprehending them? Or what ňĻexactlyňļ did you do?‚ÄĚ

27:85.text: And the decree ňĻof tormentňļ will be justified against them for their wrongdoing, leaving them speechless.

27:86.text: Do they not see that We made the night for them to rest in and the day bright?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely in this are signs for those who believe.
footnotes: ¬†To demonstrate His ability to bring people back to life, Allah usually refers to some wonders of His creation, such as the planets, the alternation of the day and night, the mountains, and the development of the human embryo.

27:87.text: And ňĻbeware ofňļ the Day the Trumpet will be blown, and all those in the heavens and all those on the earth will be horrified ňĻto the point of deathňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†except those Allah wills ňĻto spareňļ. And all will come before Him, fully humbled.
footnotes: ¬†The Trumpet will be blown at two different occasions, signalling the beginning of Judgment Day. The first blow will cause all creation to die, except whoever Allah spares, and the second one will cause them to be raised from the dead.

27:88.text: Now you see the mountains, thinking they are firmly fixed, but they are travelling ňĻjustňļ like clouds. ňĻThat isňļ the design of Allah, Who has perfected everything. Surely He is All-Aware of what you do.

27:89.text: Whoever comes with a good deed will be rewarded with what is better, and they will be secure from the horror on that Day.

27:90.text: And whoever comes with an evil deed will be hurled face-first into the Fire. Are you rewarded except for what you used to do?

27:91.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this city ňĻof Meccaňļ, Who has made it sacred, and to Him belongs everything. And I am commanded to be one of those who ňĻfullyňļ submit ňĻto Himňļ,

27:92.text: and to recite the Quran.‚ÄĚ Then whoever chooses to be guided, it is only for their own good. But whoever chooses to stray, say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI am only a warner.‚ÄĚ

27:93.text: And say, ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah! He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. And your Lord is never unaware of what you do.‚ÄĚ

28:2.text: These are the verses of the clear Book.

28:3.text: We narrate to you ňĻO Prophetňļ part of the story of Moses and Pharaoh in truth for people who believe.

28:4.text: Indeed, Pharaoh ňĻarrogantlyňļ elevated himself in the land and divided its people into ňĻsubservientňļ groups, one of which he persecuted, slaughtering their sons and keeping their women. He was truly one of the corruptors.

28:5.text: But it was Our Will to favour those who were oppressed in the land, making them models ňĻof faithňļ as well as successors;

28:6.text: and to establish them in the land; and through them show Pharaoh, Ham√Ęn,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and their soldiers ňĻthe fulfilment ofňļ what they feared.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†It is commonly believed that Ham√Ęn was in charge of architecture during the reign of Pharaoh at the time of Moses (Ô∑ļ).
¬†The fulfilment of the Pharaoh‚Äôs old dream that his reign would come to an end at the hands of a boy from the Children of Israel.

28:7.text: We inspired the mother of Moses: ‚ÄúNurse him, but when you fear for him, put him then into the river, and do not fear or grieve. We will certainly return him to you, and make him one of the messengers.‚ÄĚ

28:8.text: And ňĻit so happened thatňļ Pharaoh‚Äôs people picked him up, only to become their enemy and source of grief. Surely Pharaoh, Ham√Ęn, and their soldiers were sinful.

28:9.text: Pharaoh‚Äôs wife said ňĻto himňļ, ‚ÄúňĻThis baby isňļ a source of joy for me and you. Do not kill him. Perhaps he may be useful to us or we may adopt him as a son.‚ÄĚ They were unaware ňĻof what was to comeňļ.

28:10.text: And the heart of Moses‚Äô mother ached so much that she almost gave away his identity, had We not reassured her heart in order for her to have faith ňĻin Allah‚Äôs promiseňļ.

28:11.text: And she said to his sister, ‚ÄúKeep track of him!‚ÄĚ So she watched him from a distance, while they were unaware.

28:12.text: And We had caused him to refuse all wet-nurses at first, so his sister suggested, ‚ÄúShall I direct you to a family who will bring him up for you and take good care of him?‚ÄĚ

28:13.text: This is how We returned him to his mother so that her heart would be put at ease, and not grieve, and that she would know that Allah‚Äôs promise is ňĻalwaysňļ true. But most people do not know.

28:14.text: And when he reached full strength and maturity, We gave him wisdom and knowledge. This is how We reward the good-doers.

28:15.text: ňĻOne dayňļ he entered the city unnoticed by its people.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†There he found two men fighting: one of his own people, and the other of his enemies. The man from his people called to him for help against his foe. So Moses punched him, causing his death. Moses cried, ‚ÄúThis is from Satan‚Äôs handiwork. He is certainly a sworn, misleading enemy.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†In his adulthood, Moses and Pharaoh‚Äôs people were not on good terms because he started to question their evil practices.

28:16.text: He pleaded, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have definitely wronged my soul, so forgive me.‚ÄĚ So He forgave him, ňĻforňļ He is indeed the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

28:17.text: Moses pledged, ‚ÄúMy Lord! For all Your favours upon me, I will never side with the wicked.‚ÄĚ

28:18.text: And so Moses became fearful, watching out in the city, when suddenly the one who sought his help the day before cried out to him again for help. Moses rebuked him, ‚ÄúIndeed, you are clearly a trouble-maker.‚ÄĚ

28:19.text: Then when Moses was about to lay his hands on their foe,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†the enemy said, ‚ÄúO Moses! Do you intend to kill me as you killed a man yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land. You do not intend to make peace!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The man assumed that Moses was going to hit him.

28:20.text: And there came a man, rushing from the farthest end of the city. He said, ‚ÄúO Moses! The chiefs are actually conspiring against you to put you to death, so leave ňĻthe cityňļ. I really advise you ňĻto do soňļ.‚ÄĚ

28:21.text: So Moses left the city in a state of fear and caution, praying, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Deliver me from the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

28:22.text: And as he made his way towards Midian, he said, ‚ÄúI trust my Lord will guide me to the right way.‚ÄĚ

28:23.text: When he arrived at the well of Midian, he found a group of people watering ňĻtheir herdsňļ. Apart from them, he noticed two women holding back ňĻtheir herdňļ. He asked ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúWhat is the matter?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúWe cannot water ňĻour animalsňļ until the ňĻotherňļ shepherds are done, for our father is a very old man.‚ÄĚ

28:24.text: So he watered ňĻtheir herdňļ for them, then withdrew to the shade and prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I am truly in ňĻdesperateňļ need of whatever provision You may have in store for me.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Moses left Egypt with no food, money, or even shoes. He was completely worn out by the time he arrived in Midian. After he prayed for help, Allah gave him a wife, a job, and a home on the same day.

28:25.text: Then one of the two women came to him, walking bashfully. She said, ‚ÄúMy father is inviting you so he may reward you for watering ňĻour animalsňļ for us.‚ÄĚ When Moses came to him and told him his whole story, the old man said, ‚ÄúHave no fear! You are ňĻnowňļ safe from the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

28:26.text: One of the two daughters suggested, ‚ÄúO my dear father! Hire him. A strong, trustworthy person is definitely the best to hire.‚ÄĚ

28:27.text: The old man proposed, ‚ÄúI wish to marry one of these two daughters of mine to you, provided that you stay in my service for eight years. If you complete ten, it will be ňĻa favourňļ from you, but I do not wish to make it difficult for you. Allah willing, you will find me an agreeable man.‚ÄĚ

28:28.text: Moses responded, ‚ÄúňĻThenňļ it is ňĻsettledňļ between you and I. Whichever term I fulfill, there will be no ňĻfurtherňļ obligation on me. And Allah is a Witness to what we say.‚ÄĚ

28:29.text: When Moses had completed the term and was travelling with his family, he spotted a fire on the side of Mount ŠĻ¨√Ľr. He said to his family, ‚ÄúStay here, ňĻforňļ I have spotted a fire. Perhaps from there I can bring you some directions<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†or a torch from the fire so you may warm yourselves.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., information. Moses and his family lost their way in the dark while they were travelling from Midian to Egypt.

28:30.text: But when he came to it, he was called from the bush in the sacred ground to the right side of the valley: ‚ÄúO Moses! It is truly I. I am Allah‚ÄĒthe Lord of all worlds.

28:31.text: Now, throw down your staff!‚ÄĚ But when he saw it slithering like a snake, he ran away without looking back. ňĻAllah reassured him,ňļ ‚ÄúO Moses! Draw near, and have no fear. You are perfectly secure.

28:32.text: Now put your hand through ňĻthe opening ofňļ your collar, it will come out ňĻshiningňļ white, unblemished.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And cross your arms tightly to calm your fears.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†These are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his chiefs. They have truly been a rebellious people.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 20:22.
¬†When Moses put his arm through the opening in his collar again, his hand returned to its original colour.

28:33.text: Moses appealed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have indeed killed a man from them, so I fear they may kill me.

28:34.text: And my brother Aaron is more eloquent than I, so send him with me as a helper to support what I say, for I truly fear they may reject me.‚ÄĚ

28:35.text: Allah responded, ‚ÄúWe will assist you with your brother and grant you both authority, so they cannot harm you. With Our signs, you and those who follow you will ňĻcertainlyňļ prevail.‚ÄĚ

28:36.text: But when Moses came to them with Our clear signs, they said ňĻarrogantlyňļ, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but conjured magic ňĻtricksňļ. We have never heard of this in ňĻthe history ofňļ our forefathers.‚ÄĚ

28:37.text: Moses responded, ‚ÄúMy Lord knows best who has come with ňĻtrueňļ guidance from Him and will fare best in the end. Indeed, the wrongdoers will never succeed.‚ÄĚ

28:38.text: Pharaoh declared, ‚ÄúO chiefs! I know of no other god for you but myself. So bake bricks out of clay for me, O ¬†Ham√Ęn, and build a high tower so I may look at the God of Moses, although I am sure he is a liar.‚ÄĚ

28:39.text: And so he and his soldiers behaved arrogantly in the land with no right, thinking they would never be returned to Us.

28:40.text: So We seized him and his soldiers, casting¬†them into the sea. See then what was the end of the wrongdoers!

28:41.text: We made them leaders inviting ňĻothersňļ to the Fire. And on the Day of Judgment they will not be helped.

28:42.text: We caused a curse to follow them in this world. And on the Day of Judgment they will be among the outcasts.

28:43.text: Indeed, We gave Moses the Scripture‚ÄĒafter destroying earlier nations‚ÄĒas an insight for the people, a guide, and mercy so perhaps they would be mindful.

28:44.text: You were not there ňĻO Prophetňļ on the western side of the mountain when We entrusted the Commandments to Moses, nor were you present ňĻin his timeňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The pagans are reminded repeatedly in the Quran that the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) did not witness any of these events that happened centuries before he was born. For example, the conspiracies that were made against Joseph (12:102), the dispute on who should be the guardian of young Mary (3:44), and the drowning of Noah‚Äôs son in the Flood (11:49). These details were not known to Arabs before the revelation of the Quran. So the only logical way that the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) knew about these stories is through divine revelation.

28:45.text: But We ňĻlaterňļ raised ňĻseveralňļ generations, and the ages took their toll on them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Nor were you living among the people of Midian, rehearsing Our revelations with them. But it is We Who have sent ňĻthis¬†revelation to youňļ.
footnotes: ¬†So they neglected the Commandments over time.

28:46.text: And you were not at the side of Mount ŠĻ¨√Ľr when We called out ňĻto Mosesňļ. But ňĻyou have been sentňļ as a mercy from your Lord to warn a people to whom no warner has come before you, so perhaps they may be mindful.

28:47.text: Also so they would not say, if struck by an affliction for what their hands have done: ‚ÄúOur Lord! If only You had sent us a messenger, we would have followed Your revelations and become believers.‚ÄĚ

28:48.text: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, ‚ÄúIf only he was given the like of what Moses had been given.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Did they not deny what had been given to Moses earlier? They claimed, ‚ÄúBoth ňĻScripturesňļ are works of magic, supporting each other!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Adding, ‚ÄúWe truly deny both.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The pagans of Mecca demanded that the Quran should have been revealed all at once like the Torah, and the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) should have had some tangible signs like the staff of Moses.

¬†Some Meccan pagans approached some Jewish authorities to inquire about the Prophet‚Äôs message and they were told that there are references of him in the Torah. So the pagans immediately rejected both the Torah and the Quran as two works of magic.

28:49.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBring then a scripture from Allah which is a better guide than these two so I may follow it, if your claim is true.‚ÄĚ

28:50.text: So if they fail to respond to you, then know that they only follow their desires. And who could be more astray than those who follow their desires with no guidance from Allah? Surely Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

28:51.text: Indeed, We have steadily delivered the Word ňĻof Allahňļ to the people so they may be mindful.

28:52.text: ňĻAs forňļ those ňĻfaithfulňļ to whom We had given the Scripture before this ňĻQuranňļ, they do believe in it.

28:53.text: When it is recited to them, they declare, ‚ÄúWe believe in it. This is definitely the truth from our Lord. We had already submitted<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻevenňļ before this.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., ‚ÄėWe had already been Muslims.‚Äô

28:54.text: These ňĻbelieversňļ will be given a double reward for their perseverance, responding to evil with good, and for donating from what We have provided for them.

28:55.text: When they hear slanderous talk, they turn away from it, saying, ‚ÄúWe are accountable for our deeds and you for yours. Peace ňĻis our only responseňļ to you! We want nothing to do with those who act ignorantly.‚ÄĚ

28:56.text: You surely cannot guide whoever you like ňĻO Prophetňļ, but it is Allah Who guides whoever He wills, and He knows best who are ňĻfit to beňļ guided.

28:57.text: They say ňĻto the Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúIf we were to follow ňĻtrueňļ guidance with you, we would certainly be snatched away from our land.‚ÄĚ Have We not established for them a safe haven ňĻin Meccaňļ to which fruits of all kinds are brought as a provision from Us? But most of them do not know ňĻthis favourňļ.

28:58.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many societies We have destroyed that had been spoiled by their ňĻcomfortableňļ living! Those are their residences, never inhabited after them except passingly.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And We ňĻaloneňļ were the Successor.
footnotes: ¬†By travellers such as the Meccan pagans.

28:59.text: Your Lord would never destroy a society until He had sent to its capital a messenger, reciting Our revelations to them. Nor would We ever destroy a society unless its people persisted in wrongdoing.

28:60.text: Whatever ňĻpleasureňļ you have been given is no more than ňĻa fleetingňļ enjoyment and adornment of this worldly life. But what is with Allah is far better and more lasting. Will you not then understand?

28:61.text: Can those to whom We have made a fine promise‚ÄĒwhich they will see fulfilled‚ÄĒbe like those who We have allowed to enjoy the pleasures of this worldly life, but on the Day of Judgment will be brought ňĻfor punishmentňļ?

28:62.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day He will call to them, ‚ÄúWhere are those you claimed were My associate-gods?‚ÄĚ

28:63.text: Those ňĻmisleadersňļ against whom the decree ňĻof tormentňļ is justified will cry, ‚ÄúOur Lord! These ňĻfollowersňļ are the ones we caused to deviate. We led them into deviance, for we ourselves were deviant. We disassociate ourselves ňĻfrom themňļ before You. It was not us that they used to worship.‚ÄĚ

28:64.text: It will be said ňĻto the disbelieversňļ, ‚ÄúCall upon your associate-gods ňĻfor helpňļ.‚ÄĚ So they will call them, but will receive no response. And they will face the punishment, wishing they had been ňĻrightlyňļ guided!

28:65.text: And ňĻwatch forňļ the Day He will call to them, asking, ‚ÄúWhat response did you give to the messengers?‚ÄĚ

28:66.text: They will be too dumbstruck on that Day to ask one another ňĻfor answersňļ.

28:67.text: As for those who repent, believe, and do good ňĻin this worldňļ, it is right to hope that they will be among the successful.

28:68.text: Your Lord creates and chooses whatever He wills‚ÄĒthe choice is not theirs. Glorified and Exalted is Allah above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!

28:69.text: And your Lord knows what their hearts conceal and what they reveal.

28:70.text: He is Allah. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. All praise belongs to Him in this life and the next. All authority is His. And to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

28:71.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúImagine if Allah were to make the night perpetual for you until the Day of Judgment, which god other than Allah could bring you sunlight? Will you not then listen?‚ÄĚ

28:72.text: Ask ňĻthem alsoňļ, ‚ÄúImagine if Allah were to make the day perpetual for you until the Day of Judgment, which god other than Allah could bring you night to rest in? Will you not then see?‚ÄĚ

28:73.text: It is out of His mercy that He has made for you the day and night so that you may rest ňĻin the latterňļ and seek His bounty ňĻin the formerňļ, and perhaps you will be grateful.

28:74.text: And ňĻwatch forňļ the Day He will call to them, ‚ÄúWhere are those you claimed were My associate-gods?‚ÄĚ

28:75.text: And We will bring forth a witness<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from every faith-community and ask ňĻthe polytheistsňļ, ‚ÄúShow ňĻUsňļ your proof.‚ÄĚ Then they will ňĻcome toňļ know that the truth is with Allah ňĻaloneňļ. And whatever ňĻgodsňļ they fabricated will fail them.
footnotes: ¬†A prophet.

28:76.text: Indeed, Korah<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†was from the people of Moses, but he behaved arrogantly towards them. We had granted him such treasures that even their keys would burden a group of strong men. ňĻSome ofňļ his people advised him, ‚ÄúDo not be prideful! Surely Allah does not like the prideful.
footnotes: ¬†Korah was the cousin of Moses. Because of Korah‚Äôs close association with Pharaoh, he became very rich and started to behave arrogantly towards his own people. When he was asked repeatedly by Moses (Ô∑ļ) to pay his alms-tax to help poor Israelites, Korah refused and eventually conspired with a prostitute to damage the reputation of Moses, but Moses was cleared of any wrongdoing.

28:77.text: Rather, seek the ňĻrewardňļ of the Hereafter by means of what Allah has granted you, without forgetting your share of this world. And be good ňĻto othersňļ as Allah has been good to you. Do not seek to spread corruption in the land, for Allah certainly does not like the corruptors.‚ÄĚ

28:78.text: He replied, ‚ÄúI have been granted all this because of some knowledge I have.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Did he not know that Allah had already destroyed some from the generations before him who were far superior to him in power and greater in accumulating ňĻwealthňļ? There will be no need for the wicked to be asked about their sins.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Or: ‚ÄúI have been granted all this because I know that Allah knows I deserve it.‚ÄĚ
¬†Since their sins are already known to Allah and written in perfect records, they will only be interrogated as a form of punishment.

28:79.text: Then he came out before his people in all his glamour. Those who desired the life of this world wished, ‚ÄúIf only we could have something like what Korah has been given. He is truly a man of great fortune!‚ÄĚ

28:80.text: But those gifted with knowledge said, ‚ÄúShame on you! Allah‚Äôs reward is far better for those who believe and do good. But none will attain this except the steadfast.‚ÄĚ

28:81.text: Then We caused the earth to swallow him up, along with his home. There was no one to help him against Allah, nor could he even help himself.

28:82.text: And those who had craved his position the previous day began to say, ‚ÄúAh! It is certainly Allah Who gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills of His servants. Had it not been for the grace of Allah, He could have surely caused the earth to swallow us up! Oh, indeed! The disbelievers will never succeed.‚ÄĚ

28:83.text: That ňĻeternalňļ Home in the Hereafter We reserve ňĻonlyňļ for those who seek neither tyranny nor corruption on the earth. The ultimate outcome belongs ňĻonlyňļ to the righteous.

28:84.text: Whoever comes with a good deed will be rewarded with what is better. And whoever comes with an evil deed, then the evildoers will only be rewarded for what they used to do.

28:85.text: Most certainly, the One Who has ordained the Quran for you will ňĻultimatelyňļ bring you back home ňĻto Meccaňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ‚ÄúMy Lord knows best who has come with ňĻtrueňļ guidance and who is clearly astray.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This verse was revealed while the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was on the way to Medina, emigrating from Mecca after years of persecution. Eventually the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) returned to Mecca and the majority of its people accepted Islam.

28:86.text: You never expected this Book to be revealed to you, but ňĻit cameňļ only ňĻasňļ a mercy from your Lord. So never side with the disbelievers ňĻin their disbeliefňļ.

28:87.text: Do not let them turn you away from the revelations of Allah after they have been sent down to you. Rather, invite ňĻallňļ to ňĻthe Way ofňļ your Lord, and never be one of the polytheists.

28:88.text: And do not invoke any other god with Allah. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. Everything is bound to perish except He Himself.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†All authority belongs to Him. And to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.
footnotes: ¬†lit., except His Face.

29:2.text: Do people think once they say, ‚ÄúWe believe,‚ÄĚ that they will be left without being put to the test?

29:3.text: We certainly tested those before them. And ňĻin this wayňļ Allah will clearly distinguish between those who are truthful and those who are liars.

29:4.text: Or do the evildoers ňĻsimplyňļ think that they will escape Us? How wrong is their judgment!

29:5.text: Whoever hopes for the meeting with Allah, ňĻlet them know thatňļ Allah‚Äôs appointed time is sure to come. He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

29:6.text: And whoever strives ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ, only does so for their own good. Surely Allah is not in need of ňĻany ofňļ His creation.

29:7.text: As for those who believe and do good, We will certainly absolve them of their sins, and reward them according to the best of what they used to do.

29:8.text: We have commanded people to honour their parents. But if they urge you to associate with Me what you have no knowledge of,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then do not obey them. To Me you will ňĻallňļ return, and then I will inform you of what you used to do.
footnotes: ¬†Other gods.

29:9.text: Those who believe and do good will surely be admitted by Us into ňĻthe company ofňļ the righteous.

29:10.text: There are some who say, ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah,‚ÄĚ but when they suffer in the cause of Allah, they mistake ňĻthisňļ persecution at the hands of people for the punishment of Allah. But when victory comes from your Lord, they surely say ňĻto the believersňļ, ‚ÄúWe have always been with you.‚ÄĚ Does Allah not know best what is in the hearts of all beings?

29:11.text: Allah will certainly distinguish between those who have ňĻsureňļ faith and the hypocrites.

29:12.text: The disbelievers say to the believers, ‚ÄúňĻJustňļ follow our way, and we will bear ňĻthe burden ofňļ your sins.‚ÄĚ But they would never ňĻwant toňļ bear any of the believers‚Äô sins. They are simply lying.

29:13.text: Yet they will certainly ňĻbe made toňļ carry their own burdens, as well as other burdens along with their own.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And they will surely be questioned on Judgment Day about what they used to fabricate.
footnotes: ¬†The burdens of those they misled.

29:14.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them for a thousand years, less fifty. Then the Flood overtook them, while they persisted in wrongdoing.

29:15.text: But We delivered him and those in the Ark, making it a sign for all people.

29:16.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Abraham said to his people, ‚ÄúWorship Allah, and fear Him. This is better for you, if only you knew.

29:17.text: You worship besides Allah nothing but idols, simply creating lies ňĻabout themňļ. Those you worship besides Allah certainly cannot give you any provision. So seek provision from Allah ňĻaloneňļ, worship Him, and be grateful to Him. To Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

29:18.text: If you ňĻMeccansňļ persist in denial, so did ňĻmanyňļ communities before you. The Messenger‚Äôs duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.‚ÄĚ

29:19.text: Have they not seen how Allah originates the creation then resurrects it? That is certainly easy for Allah.

29:20.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúTravel throughout the land and see how He originated the creation, then Allah will bring it into being one more time. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

29:21.text: He punishes whoever He wills, and shows mercy to whoever He wills. And you will ňĻallňļ be returned to Him.

29:22.text: And you cannot escape Him on earth or in heaven. Nor have you any protector or helper besides Allah.‚ÄĚ

29:23.text: As for those who disbelieve in Allah‚Äôs signs and the meeting with Him, it is they who will have no hope in His mercy.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And it is they who will suffer a painful punishment.
footnotes: ¬†lit., My mercy.

29:24.text: But the only response of Abraham‚Äôs people was to say: ‚ÄúKill him or burn him!‚ÄĚ But Allah saved him from the fire.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely in this are signs for people who believe.
footnotes: ¬†The idolaters tried to burn Abraham because he smashed their idols. See¬†21:51-71 for the full story.

29:25.text: He said ňĻto his peopleňļ, ‚ÄúYou have taken idols ňĻfor worshipňļ instead of Allah, only to keep ňĻthe bond ofňļ harmony among yourselves in this worldly life. But on the Day of Judgment you will disown and curse one another. Your home will be the Fire, and you will have no helper!‚ÄĚ

29:26.text: So Lot believed in him. And Abraham said, ‚ÄúI am emigrating ňĻin obedienceňļ to my Lord. He ňĻaloneňļ is indeed the Almighty, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ

29:27.text: We blessed him with Isaac and ňĻlaterňļ Jacob, and reserved prophethood and revelation for his descendants. We gave him his reward in this life,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and in the Hereafter he will certainly be among the righteous.
footnotes: ¬†Through honourable mention, righteous family, etc.

29:28.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Lot rebuked ňĻthe men ofňļ his people: ‚ÄúYou certainly commit a shameful deed that no man has ever done before you.

29:29.text: Do you really lust after ňĻotherňļ men, abuse the travellers, and practice immorality ňĻopenlyňļ in your gatherings?‚ÄĚ His people‚Äôs only response was to say ňĻmockinglyňļ: ‚ÄúBring Allah‚Äôs punishment upon us, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

29:30.text: Lot prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Help me against the people of corruption.‚ÄĚ

29:31.text: When Our messenger-angels came to Abraham with the good news ňĻof the birth of Isaacňļ, they said, ‚ÄúWe are going to destroy the people of this city ňĻof Sodomňļ, for its people have persisted in wrongdoing.‚ÄĚ

29:32.text: He said, ‚ÄúBut Lot is there!‚ÄĚ They responded, ‚ÄúWe know best who is there. We will certainly save him and his family‚ÄĒexcept his wife, who is one of the doomed.‚ÄĚ

29:33.text: And when Our messenger-angels came to Lot, he was distressed and worried by their arrival.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They reassured ňĻhimňļ, ‚ÄúDo not fear, nor grieve. We will surely deliver you and your family‚ÄĒexcept your wife, who is one of the doomed.
footnotes: ¬†Since the angels came in the form of handsome men, Lot was worried for the dignity and safety of his guests among his people‚ÄĒnot knowing that they were angels.

29:34.text: We are certainly bringing down a punishment from heaven upon the people of this city for their rebelliousness.‚ÄĚ

29:35.text: And We did leave ňĻsome ofňļ its ruins as a clear lesson for people of understanding.

29:36.text: And to the people of Midian ňĻWe sentňļ their brother Shu‚Äôaib. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah, and hope for ňĻthe reward ofňļ the Last Day. And do not go about spreading corruption in the land.‚ÄĚ

29:37.text: But they rejected him, so an ňĻoverwhelmingňļ earthquake struck them and they fell lifeless in their homes.

29:38.text: And the people of ‚Äô»ād and Tham√Ľd ňĻmet a similar fateňļ, which must be clear to you ňĻMeccansňļ from their ruins.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Satan made their ňĻevilňļ deeds appealing to them, hindering them from the ňĻRightňļ Way, although they were capable of reasoning.
footnotes: ¬†Meccan caravans always passed by these ruins on the way¬†to Yemen and Syria for business.

29:39.text: ňĻWeňļ also ňĻdestroyedňļ Korah, Pharaoh, and Ham√Ęn. Indeed, Moses had come to them with clear proofs, but they behaved arrogantly in the land. Yet they could not escape ňĻUsňļ.

29:40.text: So We seized each ňĻpeopleňļ for their sin: against some of them We sent a storm of stones, some were overtaken by a ňĻmightyňļ blast, some We caused the earth to swallow, and some We drowned. Allah would not have wronged them, but it was they who wronged themselves.

29:41.text: The parable of those who take protectors other than Allah is that of a spider spinning a shelter. And the flimsiest of all shelters is certainly that of a spider, if only they knew.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Externally, the web is too flimsy to protect the spider against rain and strong wind. Internally, the spider‚Äôs family structure is fragile, since some species are cannibalistic, with the female preying on the male and the young eating their own mother.

29:42.text: Allah surely knows that whatever ňĻgodsňļ they invoke besides Him are ňĻsimplyňļ nothing. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

29:43.text: These are the parables We set forth for humanity, but none will understand them except the people of knowledge.

29:44.text: Allah created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. Surely in this is a sign for the people of faith.

29:45.text: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish prayer. Indeed, ňĻgenuineňļ prayer should deter ňĻoneňļ from indecency and wickedness. The remembrance of Allah is ňĻanňļ even greater ňĻdeterrentňļ. And Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows what you ňĻallňļ do.

29:46.text: Do not argue with the People of the Book unless gracefully, except with those of them who act wrongfully. And say, ‚ÄúWe believe in what has been revealed to us and what was revealed to you. Our God and your God is ňĻonlyňļ One. And to Him we ňĻfullyňļ submit.‚ÄĚ

29:47.text: Similarly ňĻto earlier messengersňļ, We have revealed to you a Book ňĻO Prophetňļ. ňĻThe faithful ofňļ those to whom We gave the Scriptures believe in it, as do some of these ňĻpagan Arabsňļ. And none denies Our revelations except the ňĻstubbornňļ disbelievers.

29:48.text: You ňĻO Prophetňļ could not read any writing ňĻevenňļ before this ňĻrevelationňļ, nor could you write at all. Otherwise, the people of falsehood would have been suspicious.

29:49.text: But this ňĻQuranňļ is ňĻa set ofňļ clear revelations ňĻpreservedňļ in the hearts of those gifted with knowledge. And none denies Our revelations except the ňĻstubbornňļ wrongdoers.

29:50.text: They say, ‚ÄúIf only ňĻsomeňļ signs had been sent down to him from his Lord!‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSigns are only with Allah. And I am only sent with a clear warning.‚ÄĚ

29:51.text: Is it not enough for them that We have sent down to you the Book, ňĻwhich isňļ recited to them. Surely in this ňĻQuranňļ is a mercy and reminder for people who believe.

29:52.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSufficient is Allah as a Witness between me and you. He ňĻfullyňļ knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in Allah, it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ losers.‚ÄĚ

29:53.text: They challenge you ňĻO Prophetňļ to hasten the punishment. Had it not been for a time already set, the punishment would have certainly come to them ňĻat onceňļ. But it will definitely take them by surprise when they least expect it.

29:54.text: They urge you to hasten the punishment. And Hell will certainly encompass the disbelievers

29:55.text: on the Day the punishment will overwhelm them from above them and from below their feet. And it will be said, ‚ÄúReap what you sowed.‚ÄĚ

29:56.text: O My believing servants! My earth is truly spacious, so worship Me ňĻaloneňļ.

29:57.text: Every soul will taste death, then to Us you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

29:58.text: ňĻAs forňļ those who believe and do good, We will certainly house them in ňĻelevatedňļ mansions in Paradise, under which rivers flow, to stay there forever. How excellent is the reward for those who work ňĻrighteousness!ňļ‚ÄĒ

29:59.text: those who patiently endure, and put their trust in their Lord!

29:60.text: How many are the creatures that cannot secure their provisions! ňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ provides for them and you ňĻas wellňļ. He is indeed the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

29:61.text: If you ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ who created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon ňĻfor your benefitňļ, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ How can they then be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

29:62.text: Allah gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills of His servants. Surely Allah has ňĻfullňļ knowledge of everything.

29:63.text: And if you ask them who sends down rain from the sky, giving life to the earth after its death, they will surely say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah!‚ÄĚ In fact, most of them do not understand.

29:64.text: This worldly life is no more than play and amusement. But the Hereafter is indeed the real life, if only they knew.

29:65.text: If they happen to be aboard a ship ňĻcaught in a stormňļ, they cry out to Allah ňĻaloneňļ in sincere devotion. But as soon as He delivers them ňĻsafelyňļ to shore, they associate ňĻothers with Him once againňļ.

29:66.text: So let them be ungrateful for all We have given them, and ňĻlet themňļ enjoy themselves ňĻfor nowňļ! For they will soon know.

29:67.text: Have they not seen how We have made ňĻMeccaňļ a safe haven, whereas people ňĻallňļ around them are snatched away?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†How can they then believe in falsehood<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and deny Allah‚Äôs favours?
footnotes: ¬†Mecca was considered as a sanctuary. Therefore, fighting was not allowed there, and whoever entered Mecca (especially in the neighbourhood of the Ka‚Äôbah) was safe‚ÄĒa privilege that other cities in Arabia did not have.
¬†i.e., the false gods and idols.

29:68.text: And who does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah or reject the truth after it has reached them? Is Hell not a ňĻfittingňļ home for the disbelievers?

29:69.text: As for those who struggle in Our cause, We will surely guide them along Our Way. And Allah is certainly with the good-doers.

3:2.text: Allah! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒthe Ever-Living, All-Sustaining.

3:3.text: He has revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the Book in truth, confirming what came before it, as He revealed the Torah and the Gospel

3:4.text: previously, as a guide for people, and ňĻalsoňļ revealed the Decisive Authority.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely those who reject Allah‚Äôs revelations will suffer a severe torment. For Allah is Almighty, capable of punishment.
footnotes: ¬†The Decisive Authority (Al-Furq√Ęn)¬†is one of the names of the Quran.

3:5.text: Surely nothing on earth or in the heavens is hidden from Allah.

3:6.text: He is the One Who shapes you in the wombs of your mothers as He wills. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

3:7.text: He is the One Who has revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ the Book, of which some verses are precise‚ÄĒthey are the foundation of the Book‚ÄĒwhile others are elusive.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Those with deviant hearts follow the elusive verses seeking ňĻto spreadňļ doubt through their ňĻfalseňļ interpretations‚ÄĒbut none grasps their ňĻfullňļ meaning except Allah. As for those well-grounded in knowledge, they say, ‚ÄúWe believe in this ňĻQuranňļ‚ÄĒit is all from our Lord.‚ÄĚ But none will be mindful ňĻof thisňļ except people of reason.
footnotes: ¬†The precise verses are easy to understand and deal mostly with matters of belief and practice, such as the belief in one God (S√Ľrah¬†112), forbidden foods (see¬†5:3), and the commandments in 6:151-152 and 17:23-39. As for the elusive verses, their full meaning is known only to Allah, such as the meaning of letter combinations at the beginning of some s√Ľrahs, such as Alif-L√£m-Mń©m¬†(see¬†Stylistic Features), and how Allah settles Himself on the Throne.

3:8.text: ňĻThey say,ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! Do not let our hearts deviate after you have guided us. Grant us Your mercy. You are indeed the Giver ňĻof all bountiesňļ.

3:9.text: Our Lord! You will certainly gather all humanity for the ňĻpromisedňļ Day‚ÄĒabout which there is no doubt. Surely Allah does not break His promise.‚ÄĚ

3:10.text: Indeed, neither the wealth nor children<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†of the disbelievers will be of any benefit to them against Allah‚ÄĒand they will be the fuel for the Fire.
footnotes: ¬†Wealth and children were considered the most important things in pre-Islamic Arabia, and so the frequent mention of these things in the Quran is meant to represent all worldly gains.

3:11.text: Their fate will be like that of the people of Pharaoh and those before them‚ÄĒthey all rejected Our signs, so Allah seized them for their sins. And Allah is severe in punishment.

3:12.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Tell the disbelievers, ‚ÄúSoon you will be overpowered and driven to Hell‚ÄĒwhat an evil place to rest!‚ÄĚ

3:13.text: Indeed, there was a sign for you in the two armies that met in battle‚ÄĒone fighting for the cause of Allah and the other in denial. The believers saw their enemy twice their number.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But Allah supports with His victory whoever He wills. Surely in this is a lesson for people of insight.
footnotes: ¬†It can also be understood that the disbelievers were made to think there were twice as many believers in the battlefield.

3:14.text: The enjoyment of ňĻworldlyňļ desires‚ÄĒwomen, children,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†treasures of gold and silver, fine horses, cattle, and fertile land‚ÄĒhas been made appealing to people. These are the pleasures of this worldly life, but with Allah is the finest destination.
footnotes: ¬†Ban√ģn¬†means sons. In the ancient Arab culture, sons were a source of pride for their parents and tribes. This is because they provided for their families and took up arms in defence of their tribes.

3:15.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShall I inform you of what is better than ňĻall ofňļ this? Those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ will have Gardens with their Lord under which rivers flow, to stay there forever, and pure spouses,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†along with Allah‚Äôs pleasure.‚ÄĚ And Allah is All-Seeing of ňĻHisňļ servants,
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:25.

3:16.text: who pray, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We have believed, so forgive our sins and protect us from the torment of the Fire.‚ÄĚ

3:17.text: ňĻIt is theyňļ who are patient, sincere, obedient, and charitable, and who pray for forgiveness before dawn.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Optional prayers before dawn are recommended and are more likely to be accepted.

3:18.text: Allah ňĻHimselfňļ is a Witness that there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒand so are the angels and people of knowledge. He is the Maintainer of justice. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

3:19.text: Certainly, Allah‚Äôs only Way is Islam.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Those who were given the Scripture did not dispute ňĻamong themselvesňļ out of mutual envy until knowledge came to them.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Whoever denies Allah‚Äôs signs, then surely Allah is swift in reckoning.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., full submission to the Will of Allah.
¬†No community split into believers and disbelievers until they received the knowledge given by their prophet.

3:20.text: So if they argue with you ňĻO Prophetňļ, say, ‚ÄúI have submitted myself to Allah, and so have my followers.‚ÄĚ And ask those who were given the Scripture and the illiterate ňĻpeopleňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúHave you submitted yourselves ňĻto Allahňļ?‚ÄĚ If they submit, they will be ňĻrightlyňļ guided. But if they turn away, then your duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ. And Allah is All-Seeing of ňĻHisňļ servants.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the pagans of Arabia before Islam.¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†

3:21.text: Indeed, those who deny Allah‚Äôs signs, kill the prophets unjustly, and kill people who stand up for justice‚ÄĒgive them good news of a painful punishment.

3:22.text: They are the ones whose deeds are wasted in this world and the Hereafter. And they will have no helpers.

3:23.text: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Scriptures?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet when they are invited to the Book of Allah to settle their disputes, some of them turn away heedlessly.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Torah.

3:24.text: This is because they say, ‚ÄúThe Fire will not touch us except for a few days.‚ÄĚ They have been deceived in their faith by their wishful lying.

3:25.text: But how ňĻhorribleňļ will it be when We gather them together on the Day about which there is no doubt‚ÄĒwhen every soul will be paid in full for what it has done, and none will be wronged!

3:26.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO Allah! Lord over all authorities! You give authority to whoever You please and remove it from who You please; You honour whoever You please and disgrace who You please‚ÄĒall good is in Your Hands. Surely You ňĻaloneňļ are Most Capable of everything.

3:27.text: You cause the night to pass into the day and the day into the night. You bring forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living. And You provide for whoever You will without limit.‚ÄĚ
