3:28.text: Believers should not take disbelievers as guardians instead of the believers‚ÄĒand whoever does so will have nothing to hope for from Allah‚ÄĒunless it is a precaution against their tyranny. And Allah warns you about Himself. And to Allah is the final return.

3:29.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhether you conceal what is in your hearts or reveal it, it is known to Allah. For He knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.‚ÄĚ

3:30.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day when every soul will be presented with whatever good it has done. And it will wish that its misdeeds were far off. And Allah warns you about Himself. And Allah is Ever Gracious to ňĻHisňļ servants.

3:31.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf you ňĻsincerelyňļ love Allah, then follow me; Allah will love you and forgive your sins. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ

3:32.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúObey Allah and His Messenger.‚ÄĚ If they still turn away, then truly Allah does not like the disbelievers.

3:33.text: Indeed, Allah chose Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of ‚ÄôImr√Ęn above all people ňĻof their timeňļ.

3:34.text: They are descendants of one another. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

3:35.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the wife of ‚ÄôImr√Ęn said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I dedicate what is in my womb entirely to Your service,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so accept it from me. You ňĻaloneňļ are truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., dedicating the child to service in the temple.

3:36.text: When she delivered, she said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have given birth to a girl,‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒand Allah fully knew what she had delivered‚ÄĒ‚Äúand the male is not like the female.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†I have named her Mary, and I seek Your protection for her and her offspring from Satan, the accursed.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Service at the temple was hosted by males only.

¬†The prayers of Mary‚Äôs mother were answered. In a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Bukh√Ęri and Muslim, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) says, ‚ÄúEvery child is touched by Satan when they are born‚ÄĒand they cry because of this contact‚ÄĒexcept Jesus and his mother.‚ÄĚ

3:37.text: So her Lord accepted her graciously and blessed her with a pleasant upbringing‚ÄĒentrusting her to the care of Zachariah. Whenever Zachariah visited her in the sanctuary, he found her supplied with provisions. He exclaimed, ‚ÄúO Mary! Where did this come from?‚ÄĚ She replied, ‚ÄúIt is from Allah. Surely Allah provides for whoever He wills without limit.‚ÄĚ

3:38.text: Then and there Zachariah prayed to his Lord, saying, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me‚ÄĒby your grace‚ÄĒrighteous offspring. You are certainly the Hearer of ňĻallňļ prayers.‚ÄĚ

3:39.text: So the angels called out to him while he stood praying in the sanctuary, ‚ÄúAllah gives you good news of ňĻthe birth ofňļ John who will confirm the Word of Allah and will be a great leader, chaste, and a prophet among the righteous.‚ÄĚ

3:40.text: Zachariah exclaimed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! How can I have a son when I am very old and my wife is barren?‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúSo will it be. Allah does what He wills.‚ÄĚ

3:41.text: Zachariah said, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me a sign.‚ÄĚ He said, ‚ÄúYour sign is that you will not ňĻbe able toňļ speak to people for three days except through gestures. Remember your Lord often and glorify ňĻHimňļ morning and evening.‚ÄĚ

3:42.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when the angels said, ‚ÄúO Mary! Surely Allah has selected you, purified you, and chosen you over all women of the world.

3:43.text: O ¬†Mary! Be devout to your Lord, prostrate yourself ňĻin prayerňļ and bow along with those who bow down.‚ÄĚ

3:44.text: This is news of the unseen that We reveal to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. You were not with them when they cast lots to decide who would be Mary‚Äôs guardian, nor were you there when they argued ňĻabout itňļ.

3:45.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the angels proclaimed, ‚ÄúO Mary! Allah gives you good news of a Word<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from Him, his name will be the Messiah,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Jesus, son of Mary; honoured in this world and the Hereafter, and he will be one of those nearest ňĻto Allahňļ.
footnotes: ¬†In the Quran, Jesus is called the ‚ÄúWord‚ÄĚ of Allah since he was created with the word ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ
¬†Messiah (Mas√ģŠł•) means the ‚Äúanointed one.‚ÄĚ It is used in the Quran exclusively as a title for Jesus Christ.

3:46.text: And he will speak to people in ňĻhisňļ infancy and adulthood and will be one of the righteous.‚ÄĚ

3:47.text: Mary wondered, ‚ÄúMy Lord! How can I have a child when no man has ever touched me?‚ÄĚ An angel replied, ‚ÄúSo will it be. Allah creates what He wills. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, ‚ÄėBe!‚Äô And it is!

3:48.text: And Allah will teach him writing and wisdom, the Torah and the Gospel,

3:49.text: and ňĻmake himňļ a messenger to the Children of Israel ňĻto proclaim,ňļ ‚ÄėI have come to you with a sign from your Lord: I will make for you a bird from clay, breathe into it, and it will become a ňĻrealňļ bird‚ÄĒby Allah‚Äôs Will. I will heal the blind and the leper and raise the dead to life‚ÄĒby Allah‚Äôs Will. And I will prophesize what you eat and store in your houses. Surely in this is a sign for you if you ňĻtrulyňļ believe.

3:50.text: And I will confirm the Torah revealed before me and legalize some of what had been forbidden to you. I have come to you with a sign from your Lord, so be mindful of Allah and obey me.

3:51.text: Surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him ňĻaloneňļ. This is the Straight Path.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

3:52.text: When Jesus sensed disbelief from his people, he asked, ‚ÄúWho will stand up with me for Allah?‚ÄĚ The disciples replied, ‚ÄúWe will stand up for Allah. We believe in Allah, so bear witness that we have submitted.‚ÄĚ

3:53.text: ňĻThey prayed to Allah,ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! We believe in Your revelations and follow the messenger, so count us among those who bear witness.‚ÄĚ

3:54.text: And the disbelievers made a plan ňĻagainst Jesusňļ, but Allah also planned‚ÄĒand Allah is the best of planners.

3:55.text: ňĻRememberňļ when Allah said, ‚ÄúO Jesus! I will take you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and raise you up to Myself. I will deliver you from those who disbelieve, and elevate your followers above the disbelievers until the Day of Judgment. Then to Me you will ňĻallňļ return, and I will settle all your disputes.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄėMutawaff√ģka‚Äô means literally: ‚ÄúI will make you reach the end of your worldly term.‚ÄĚ The popular Muslim belief is that a conspiracy was made to kill Jesus. Allah made the main culprit who betrayed Jesus look exactly like Jesus, while the culprit was crucified. Similar to Christians, Muslims believe in the second coming of Jesus (Ô∑ļ).

3:56.text: As for those who disbelieve, I will subject them to a severe punishment in this life and the Hereafter, and they will have no helpers.

3:57.text: And as for those who believe and do good, they will be rewarded in full. And Allah does not like the wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

3:58.text: We recite ňĻallňļ this to you ňĻO Prophetňļ as one of the signs<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and ňĻasňļ a wise reminder.
footnotes: ¬†As a testament to your prophethood.

3:59.text: Indeed, the example of Jesus in the sight of Allah is like that of Adam. He created him from dust, then said to him, ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And he was!

3:60.text: This is the truth from your Lord, so do not be one of those who doubt.

3:61.text: Now, whoever disputes with you ňĻO Prophetňļ concerning Jesus after full knowledge has come to you, say, ‚ÄúCome! Let us gather our children and your children, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves‚ÄĒthen let us sincerely invoke Allah‚Äôs curse upon the liars.‚ÄĚ

3:62.text: Certainly, this is the true narrative, and there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah. And indeed, Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Almighty, All-Wise.

3:63.text: If they turn away, then surely Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of the corruptors.

3:64.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Let us come to common terms: that we will worship none but Allah, associate none with Him, nor take one another as lords instead of Allah.‚ÄĚ But if they turn away, then say, ‚ÄúBear witness that we have submitted ňĻto Allah aloneňļ.‚ÄĚ

3:65.text: O People of the Book! Why do you argue about Abraham, while the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until long after him? Do you not understand?

3:66.text: Here you are! You disputed about what you have ňĻlittleňļ knowledge of,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but why do you now argue about what you have no knowledge of?<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Allah knows and you do not know.
footnotes: ¬†The perception of Jesus (Ô∑ļ).
¬†Abraham (Ô∑ļ) being Jewish or Christian.

3:67.text: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian; he submitted in all uprightness<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and was not a polytheist.
footnotes: ¬†Linguistically, ‚ÄúMuslim‚ÄĚ means ‚Äúsomeone who has submitted themselves to Allah.‚ÄĚ

3:68.text: Indeed, those who have the best claim to Abraham are his followers, this Prophet,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and the believers. And Allah is the Guardian of those who believe.
footnotes: ¬†MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

3:69.text: Some of the People of the Book wish to mislead you ňĻbelieversňļ. They mislead none but themselves, yet they fail to perceive it.

3:70.text: O ¬†People of the Book! Why do you reject the signs of Allah while you bear witness ňĻto their truthňļ?

3:71.text: O ¬†People of the Book! Why do you mix the truth with falsehood and hide the truth knowingly?

3:72.text: A group among the People of the Book said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúBelieve in what has been revealed to the believers in the morning and reject it in the evening, so they may abandon their faith.

3:73.text: And only believe those who follow your religion.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely, ňĻthe onlyňļ true guidance is Allah‚Äôs guidance.‚ÄĚ ňĻThey also said,ňļ ‚ÄúDo not believe that someone will receive ňĻrevealedňļ knowledge similar to yours or argue against you before your Lord.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIndeed, all bounty is in the Hands of Allah‚ÄĒHe grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing.‚ÄĚ

3:74.text: He chooses whoever He wills to receive His mercy. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

3:75.text: There are some among the People of the Book who, if entrusted with a stack of gold, will readily return it. Yet there are others who, if entrusted with a single coin, will not repay it unless you constantly demand it. This is because they say, ‚ÄúWe are not accountable for ňĻexploitingňļ the Gentiles.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And ňĻsoňļ they attribute lies to Allah knowingly.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúGentiles‚ÄĚ here refers to the pagans of Arabia.

3:76.text: Absolutely! Those who honour their trusts and shun evil‚ÄĒsurely Allah loves those who are mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

3:77.text: Indeed, those who trade Allah‚Äôs covenant and their oaths for a fleeting gain will have no share in the Hereafter. Allah will neither speak to them, nor look at them, nor purify them on the Day of Judgment. And they will suffer a painful punishment.

3:78.text: There are some among them who distort the Book with their tongues to make you think this ňĻdistortionňļ is from the Book‚ÄĒbut it is not what the Book says. They say, ‚ÄúIt is from Allah‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒbut it is not from Allah. And ňĻsoňļ they attribute lies to Allah knowingly.

3:79.text: It is not appropriate for someone who Allah has blessed with the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood to say to people, ‚ÄúWorship me instead of Allah.‚ÄĚ Rather, he would say, ‚ÄúBe devoted to the worship of your Lord ňĻaloneňļ‚ÄĒby virtue of what you read in the Scripture and what you teach.‚ÄĚ

3:80.text: And he would never ask you to take angels and prophets as lords. Would he ask you to disbelieve after you have submitted?

3:81.text: ňĻRememberňļ when Allah made a covenant with the prophets, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúNow that I have given you the Book and wisdom, if there comes to you a messenger<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†confirming what you have, you must believe in him and support him.‚ÄĚ He added, ‚ÄúDo you affirm this covenant and accept this commitment?‚ÄĚ They said, ‚ÄúYes, we do.‚ÄĚ Allah said, ‚ÄúThen bear witness, and I too am a Witness.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

3:82.text: Whoever turns back after this, they will be the rebellious.

3:83.text: Do they desire a way other than Allah‚Äôs‚ÄĒknowing that all those in the heavens and the earth submit to His Will, willingly or unwillingly, and to Him they will ňĻallňļ be returned?

3:84.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah and what has been revealed to us and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and his descendants; and what was given to Moses, Jesus, and other prophets from their Lord‚ÄĒwe make no distinction between any of them, and to Him we ňĻfullyňļ submit.‚ÄĚ

3:85.text: Whoever seeks a way other than Islam,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†it will never be accepted from them, and in the Hereafter they will be among the losers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., full submission to the Will of Allah.

3:86.text: How will Allah guide a people who chose to disbelieve after they had believed, acknowledged the Messenger to be true, and received clear proofs? For Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

3:87.text: Their reward is that they will be condemned by Allah, the angels, and all of humanity.

3:88.text: They will be in Hell forever. Their punishment will not be lightened, nor will they be delayed ňĻfrom itňļ.

3:89.text: As for those who repent afterwards and mend their ways, then surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

3:90.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve after having believed then increase in disbelief, their repentance will never be accepted.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It is they who are astray.
footnotes: ¬†Their repentance will not be accepted if they die as disbelievers.

3:91.text: Indeed, if each of those who disbelieve then die as disbelievers were to offer a ransom of enough gold to fill the whole world, it would never be accepted from them. It is they who will suffer a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.

3:92.text: You will never achieve righteousness until you donate some of what you cherish. And whatever you give is certainly well known to Allah.

3:93.text: All food was lawful for the children of Israel, except what Israel<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†made unlawful for himself before the Torah was revealed.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBring the Torah and read it, if your claims are true.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Jacob (Ô∑ļ).
¬†When Jacob fell sick, he made camel meat unlawful for himself, but not for the rest of his people.

3:94.text: Then whoever still fabricates lies about Allah, they will be the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.

3:95.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah has declared the truth. So follow the Way of Abraham, the upright‚ÄĒwho was not a polytheist.‚ÄĚ

3:96.text: Surely the first House ňĻof worshipňļ established for humanity is the one at Bakkah‚ÄĒa blessed sanctuary and a guide for ňĻallňļ people.

3:97.text: In it are clear signs and the standing-place of Abraham. Whoever enters it should be safe. Pilgrimage to this House is an obligation by Allah upon whoever is able among the people.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And whoever disbelieves, then surely Allah is not in need of ňĻany of Hisňļ creation.
footnotes: ¬†Pilgrimage is obligatory on every Muslim at least once in their lifetime if the person is physically and financially able.

3:98.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Why do you deny the revelations of Allah, when Allah is a Witness to what you do?‚ÄĚ

3:99.text: Say, ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Why do you turn the believers away from the Way of Allah‚ÄĒstriving to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked, while you are witnesses ňĻto its truthňļ? And Allah is never unaware of what you do.‚ÄĚ

3:100.text: O believers! If you were to yield to a group of those who were given the Scripture, they would turn you back from belief to disbelief.

3:101.text: How can you disbelieve when Allah‚Äôs revelations are recited to you and His Messenger is in your midst? Whoever holds firmly to Allah is surely guided to the Straight Path.

3:102.text: O believers! Be mindful of Allah in the way He deserves,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and do not die except in ňĻa state of fullňļ submission ňĻto Himňļ.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, always be conscious of Him; obedient and grateful to Him.
¬†lit., except as Muslims.

3:103.text: And hold firmly together to the rope of Allah<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and do not be divided. Remember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you when you were enemies, then He united your hearts, so you‚ÄĒby His grace‚ÄĒbecame brothers. And you were at the brink of a fiery pit and He saved you from it. This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so that you may be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Allah‚Äôs covenant.

3:104.text: Let there be a group among you who call ňĻothersňļ to goodness, encourage what is good, and forbid what is evil‚ÄĒit is they who will be successful.

3:105.text: And do not be like those who split ňĻinto sectsňļ and differed after clear proofs had come to them. It is they who will suffer a tremendous punishment.

3:106.text: On that Day some faces will be bright while others gloomy. To the gloomy-faced it will be said, ‚ÄúDid you disbelieve after having believed? So taste the punishment for your disbelief.‚ÄĚ

3:107.text: As for the bright-faced, they will be in Allah‚Äôs mercy, where they will remain forever.

3:108.text: These are Allah‚Äôs revelations We recite to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in truth. And Allah desires no injustice to ňĻHisňļ creation.

3:109.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to Allah ňĻallňļ matters will be returned ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

3:110.text: You are the best community ever raised for humanity‚ÄĒyou encourage good, forbid evil, and believe in Allah. Had the People of the Book believed, it would have been better for them. Some of them are faithful, but most are rebellious.

3:111.text: They can never inflict harm on you, except a little annoyance.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But if they meet you in battle, they will flee and they will have no helpers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they can only annoy you with their words.

3:112.text: They will be stricken with disgrace wherever they go, unless they are protected by a covenant with Allah or a treaty with the people. They have invited the displeasure of Allah and have been branded with misery for rejecting Allah‚Äôs revelations and murdering ňĻHisňļ prophets unjustly. This is ňĻa fair rewardňļ for their disobedience and violations.

3:113.text: Yet they are not all alike: there are some among the People of the Book who are upright, who recite Allah‚Äôs revelations throughout the night, prostrating ňĻin prayerňļ.

3:114.text: They believe in Allah and the Last Day, encourage good and forbid evil, and race with one another in doing good. They are ňĻtrulyňļ among the righteous.

3:115.text: They will never be denied the reward for any good they have done. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of those mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

3:116.text: Indeed, neither the wealth nor children of the disbelievers will be of any benefit to them against Allah. It is they who will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.

3:117.text: The good they do in this worldly life is like the harvest of an evil people struck by a bitter wind, destroying it ňĻcompletelyňļ. Allah never wronged them, but they wronged themselves.

3:118.text: O believers! Do not associate closely with others who would not miss a chance to harm you. Their only desire is to see you suffer. Their prejudice has become evident from what they say‚ÄĒand what their hearts hide is far worse. We have made Our revelations clear to you, if only you understood.

3:119.text: Here you are! You love them but they do not love you, and you believe in all Scriptures. When they meet you they say, ‚ÄúWe believe.‚ÄĚ But when alone, they bite their fingertips in rage. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻMay youňļ die of your rage!‚ÄĚ Surely Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

3:120.text: When you ňĻbelieversňļ are touched with good, they grieve; but when you are afflicted with evil, they rejoice. ňĻYet,ňļ if you are patient and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, their schemes will not harm you in the least. Surely Allah is Fully Aware of what they do.

3:121.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when you left your home in the early morning to position the believers in the battlefield. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

3:122.text: ňĻRememberňļ when two groups among you ňĻbelieversňļ were about to cower, then Allah reassured them. So in Allah let the believers put their trust.

3:123.text: Indeed, Allah made you victorious at Badr when you were ňĻvastlyňļ outnumbered. So be mindful of Allah, perhaps you will be grateful.

3:124.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when you said to the believers, ‚ÄúIs it not enough that your Lord will send down a reinforcement of three thousand angels for your aid?‚ÄĚ

3:125.text: Most certainly, if you ňĻbelieversňļ are firm and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ and the enemy launches a sudden attack on you, Allah will reinforce you with five thousand angels designated ňĻfor battleňļ.

3:126.text: Allah ordained this ňĻreinforcementňļ only as good news for you and reassurance for your hearts. And victory comes only from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise‚ÄĒ

3:127.text: to destroy a group of the disbelievers and humble the rest, causing them to withdraw in disappointment.

3:128.text: You ňĻO Prophetňļ have no say in the matter.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It is up to Allah to turn to them in mercy or punish them, for indeed they are wrongdoers.
footnotes: ¬†When the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was injured at the Battle of UŠł•ud, some of his companions suggested that he should pray against the pagans of Mecca. He responded, ‚ÄúI have not come to condemn people, but as a mercy to pray for their guidance.‚ÄĚ The verse here says that it is not up to the Prophet whether they believe or disbelieve. A prophet‚Äôs only duty is to convey the message. Eventually, almost all Meccans accepted Islam before the death of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

3:129.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He forgives whoever He wills, and punishes whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

3:130.text: O believers! Do not consume interest, multiplying it many times over. And be mindful of Allah, so you may prosper.

3:131.text: Guard yourselves against the Fire prepared for the disbelievers.

3:132.text: Obey Allah and the Messenger, so you may be shown mercy.

3:133.text: And hasten towards forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.

3:134.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who donate in prosperity and adversity, control their anger, and pardon others. And Allah loves the good-doers.

3:135.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who, upon committing an evil deed or wronging themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins‚ÄĒand who forgives sins except Allah?‚ÄĒand they do not knowingly persist in wrongdoing?

3:136.text: Their reward is forgiveness from their Lord and Gardens under which rivers flow, staying there forever. How excellent is the reward for those who work ňĻrighteousnessňļ!

3:137.text: Similar situations came to pass before you, so travel throughout the land and see the fate of the deniers.

3:138.text: This<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is an insight to humanity‚ÄĒa guide and a lesson to the God-fearing.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúThis‚ÄĚ either refers to how the forces of evil are ultimately destroyed, or that the Quran is a reminder of the destruction of evildoers.

3:139.text: Do not falter or grieve, for you will have the upper hand, if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.

3:140.text: If you have suffered injuries ňĻat UŠł•udňļ, they suffered similarly ňĻat Badrňļ. We alternate these days ňĻof victory and defeatňļ among people so that Allah may reveal the ňĻtrueňļ believers, choose martyrs from among you‚ÄĒand Allah does not like the wrongdoers‚ÄĒ

3:141.text: and distinguish the ňĻtrueňļ believers and destroy the disbelievers.

3:142.text: Do you think you will enter Paradise without Allah proving which of you ňĻtrulyňļ struggled ňĻfor His causeňļ and patiently endured?

3:143.text: You certainly wished ňĻfor the opportunityňļ for martyrdom before encountering it, now you have seen it with your own eyes.

3:144.text: MuŠł•ammad is no more than a messenger; other messengers have gone before him. If he were to die or to be killed, would you regress into disbelief? Those who do so will not harm Allah whatsoever. And Allah will reward those who are grateful.

3:145.text: No soul can ever die without Allah‚Äôs Will at the destined time. Those who desire worldly gain, We will let them have it, and those who desire heavenly reward, We will grant it to them. And We will reward those who are grateful.

3:146.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many devotees fought along with their prophets and never faltered despite whatever ňĻlossesňļ they suffered in the cause of Allah, nor did they weaken or give in! Allah loves those who persevere.

3:147.text: And all they said was, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Forgive our sins and excesses, make our steps firm, and grant us victory over the disbelieving people.‚ÄĚ

3:148.text: So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the excellent reward of the Hereafter. For Allah loves the good-doers.

3:149.text: O believers! If you yield to the disbelievers, they will drag you back to disbelief‚ÄĒand you will become losers.

3:150.text: But no! Allah is your Guardian, and He is the best Helper.

3:151.text: We will cast horror into the hearts of the disbelievers for associating ňĻfalse godsňļ with Allah‚ÄĒa practice He has never authorized. The Fire will be their home‚ÄĒwhat an evil place for the wrongdoers to stay!

3:152.text: Indeed, Allah fulfilled His promise to you when you ňĻinitiallyňļ swept them away by His Will, then your courage weakened and you disputed about the command and disobeyed,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†after Allah had brought victory within your reach. Some of you were after worldly gain while others desired a heavenly reward. He denied you victory over them as a test, yet He has pardoned you. And Allah is Gracious to the believers.
footnotes: ¬†The archers disputed whether to keep their positions after the initial victory. Eventually, most of them decided to go in pursuit of the spoils of war, disobeying the Prophet‚Äôs direct orders not to leave their position no matter what happened. Defeat became inevitable.

3:153.text: ňĻRememberňļ when you were running far away ňĻin panicňļ‚ÄĒnot looking at anyone‚ÄĒwhile the Messenger was calling to you from behind! So Allah rewarded your disobedience with distress upon distress. Now, do not grieve over the victory you were denied or the injury you suffered. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

3:154.text: Then after distress, He sent down serenity in the form of drowsiness overcoming some of you, while others were disturbed by evil thoughts about Allah‚ÄĒthe thoughts of ňĻpre-Islamicňļ ignorance. They ask, ‚ÄúDo we have a say in the matter?‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAll matters are destined by Allah.‚ÄĚ They conceal in their hearts what they do not reveal to you. They say ňĻto themselvesňļ, ‚ÄúIf we had any say in the matter, none of us would have come to die here.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEven if you were to remain in your homes, those among you who were destined to be killed would have met the same fate.‚ÄĚ Through this, Allah tests what is within you and purifies what is in your hearts. And Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

3:155.text: Indeed, those ňĻbelieversňļ who fled on the day when the two armies met were made to slip by Satan because of their misdeeds. But Allah has pardoned them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Forbearing.

3:156.text: O believers! Do not be like the unfaithful<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who say about their brothers who travel throughout the land or engage in battle, ‚ÄúIf they had stayed with us, they would not have died or been killed.‚ÄĚ Allah makes such thinking a cause of agony in their hearts. It is Allah who gives life and causes death. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the hypocrites.

3:157.text: Should you be martyred or die in the cause of Allah, then His forgiveness and mercy are far better than whatever ňĻwealthňļ those ňĻwho stay behindňļ accumulate.

3:158.text: Whether you die or are martyred‚ÄĒall of you will be gathered before Allah.

3:159.text: It is out of Allah‚Äôs mercy that you ňĻO Prophetňļ have been lenient with them. Had you been cruel or hard-hearted, they would have certainly abandoned you. So pardon them, ask Allah‚Äôs forgiveness for them, and consult with them in ňĻconductingňļ matters. Once you make a decision, put your trust in Allah. Surely Allah loves those who trust in Him.

3:160.text: If Allah helps you, none can defeat you. But if He denies you help, then who else can help you? So in Allah let the believers put their trust.

3:161.text: It is not appropriate for a prophet to illegally withhold spoils of war. And whoever does so, it will be held against them on the Day of Judgment. Then every soul will be paid in full for what it has done, and none will be wronged.

3:162.text: Are those who seek Allah‚Äôs pleasure like those who deserve Allah‚Äôs wrath? Hell is their home. What an evil destination!

3:163.text: They ňĻeachňļ have varying degrees in the sight of Allah. And Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

3:164.text: Indeed, Allah has done the believers a ňĻgreatňļ favour by raising a messenger from among them‚ÄĒreciting to them His revelations, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom. For indeed they had previously been clearly astray.

3:165.text: Why is it when you suffered casualties ňĻat UŠł•udňļ‚ÄĒalthough you had made your enemy suffer twice as much ňĻat Badrňļ‚ÄĒyou protested, ‚ÄúHow could this be?‚ÄĚ? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIt is because of your disobedience.‚ÄĚ Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

3:166.text: So what you suffered on the day the two armies met was by Allah‚Äôs Will, so that He might distinguish the ňĻtrueňļ believers

3:167.text: and expose the hypocrites. When it was said to them, ‚ÄúCome fight in the cause of Allah or ňĻat leastňļ defend yourselves,‚ÄĚ they replied, ‚ÄúIf we had known there was fighting, we would have definitely gone with you.‚ÄĚ They were closer to disbelief than to belief on that day‚ÄĒfor saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts. Allah is All-Knowing of what they hide.

3:168.text: Those who sat at home, saying about their brothers, ‚ÄúHad they listened to us, they would not have been killed.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúTry not to die<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†if what you say is true!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., try not to die when your time comes.

3:169.text: Never think of those martyred in the cause of Allah as dead. In fact, they are alive with their Lord, well provided for‚ÄĒ

3:170.text: rejoicing in Allah‚Äôs bounties and being delighted for those yet to join them. There will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.

3:171.text: They are joyful for receiving Allah‚Äôs grace and bounty, and that Allah does not deny the reward of the believers.

3:172.text: ňĻAs forňļ those who responded to the call of Allah and His Messenger after their injury,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†those of them who did good and were mindful ňĻof Allahňļ will have a great reward.
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) realized that the city of Medina became vulnerable after the Muslim loss at UŠł•ud. So on the next day of the battle he decided to lead a small force of his companions‚ÄĒmany of whom had been wounded at UŠł•ud‚ÄĒto chase away the Meccan army which was camping at a place called Šł§amr√Ę' Al-Asad‚ÄĒnot far from Medina. Abu Sufy√Ęn, commander of the Meccan army, sent a man to discourage the Muslims from following the Meccans. Although the man falsely claimed that the Meccans were mobilizing to launch a decisive attack on Medina, the Prophet became more determined to chase them away. Eventually, the Meccans decided to flee and not waste their victory after the Prophet sent a revert to Islam‚ÄĒwho was friends with Abu Sufy√Ęn‚ÄĒto convince him to withdraw; otherwise Muslims were going to avenge their loss at UŠł•ud.

3:173.text: Those who were warned, ‚ÄúYour enemies have mobilized their forces against you, so fear them,‚ÄĚ the warning only made them grow stronger in faith and they replied, ‚ÄúAllah ňĻaloneňļ is sufficient ňĻas an aidňļ for us and ňĻHeňļ is the best Protector.‚ÄĚ

3:174.text: So they returned with Allah‚Äôs favours and grace, suffering no harm. For they sought to please Allah. And surely Allah is ňĻtheňļ Lord of infinite bounty.

3:175.text: That ňĻwarningňļ was only ňĻfromňļ Satan, trying to prompt you to fear his followers.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So do not fear them; fear Me if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the warning mentioned in 3:173.

3:176.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Do not grieve for those who race to disbelieve‚ÄĒsurely they will not harm Allah in the least. It is Allah‚Äôs Will to disallow them a share in the Hereafter, and they will suffer a tremendous punishment.

3:177.text: Those who trade belief for disbelief will never harm Allah in the least, and they will suffer a painful punishment.

3:178.text: Those who disbelieve should not think that living longer is good for them. They are only given more time to increase in sin, and they will suffer a humiliating punishment.

3:179.text: Allah would not leave the believers in the condition you were in, until He distinguished the good from the evil ňĻamong youňļ. Nor would Allah ňĻdirectlyňļ reveal to you the unseen,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but He chooses whoever He wills as a messenger. So believe in Allah and His messengers. And if you are faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, you will receive a great reward.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the distinction between the true believers and hypocrites.

3:180.text: And do not let those who ňĻgreedilyňļ withhold Allah‚Äôs bounties think it is good for them‚ÄĒin fact, it is bad for them! They will be leashed ňĻby their necksňļ on the Day of Judgment with whatever ňĻwealthňļ they used to withhold. And Allah is the ňĻsoleňļ inheritor of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

3:181.text: Indeed, Allah has heard those ňĻamong the Jewsňļ who said, ‚ÄúAllah is poor; we are rich!‚ÄĚ We have certainly recorded their slurs and their killing of prophets unjustly. Then We will say, ‚ÄúTaste the torment of burning!

3:182.text: This is ňĻthe rewardňļ for what your hands have done. And Allah is never unjust to ňĻHisňļ creation.‚ÄĚ

3:183.text: Those ňĻare the same peopleňļ who say, ‚ÄúAllah has commanded us not to believe in any messenger unless he brings us an offering to be consumed by fire ňĻfrom the skyňļ.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúOther prophets did in fact come to you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†before me with clear proofs and ňĻevenňļ what you demanded‚ÄĒwhy then did you kill them, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., your forefathers.

3:184.text: If you are rejected by them, so too were messengers before you who came with clear proofs, divine Books, and enlightening Scriptures.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the original Torah of Moses and the Gospel of Jesus.

3:185.text: Every soul will taste death. And you will only receive your full reward on the Day of Judgment. Whoever is spared from the Fire and is admitted into Paradise will ňĻindeedňļ triumph, whereas the life of this world is no more than the delusion of enjoyment.

3:186.text: You ňĻbelieversňļ will surely be tested in your wealth and yourselves,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and you will certainly hear many hurtful words from those who were given the Scripture before you and ňĻfromňļ the polytheists. But if you are patient and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ‚ÄĒsurely this is a resolve to aspire to.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, a believer is always tested through the loss of wealth as well as sickness, injuries, and the loss of life.

3:187.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when Allah took the covenant of those who were given the Scripture to make it known to people and not hide it, yet they cast it behind their backs and traded it for a fleeting gain. What a miserable profit!

3:188.text: Do not let those who rejoice in their misdeeds and love to take credit for what they have not done think they will escape torment. They will suffer a painful punishment.

3:189.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

3:190.text: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the day and night there are signs for people of reason.

3:191.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who remember Allah while standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth ňĻand prayňļ, ‚ÄúOur Lord! You have not created ňĻall ofňļ this without purpose. Glory be to You! Protect us from the torment of the Fire.

3:192.text: Our Lord! Indeed, those You commit to the Fire will be ňĻcompletelyňļ disgraced! And the wrongdoers will have no helpers.

3:193.text: Our Lord! We have heard the caller<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to ňĻtrueňļ belief, ňĻproclaiming,ňļ ‚ÄėBelieve in your Lord ňĻaloneňļ,‚Äô so we believed. Our Lord! Forgive our sins, absolve us of our misdeeds, and join us with the virtuous when we die‚Äé.
footnotes: ¬†The caller is MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

3:194.text: Our Lord! Grant us what You have promised us through Your messengers and do not put us to shame on Judgment Day‚ÄĒfor certainly You never fail in Your promise.‚ÄĚ

3:195.text: So their Lord responded to them: ‚ÄúI will never deny any of you‚ÄĒmale or female‚ÄĒthe reward of your deeds. Both are equal in reward. Those who migrated or were expelled from their homes, and were persecuted for My sake and fought and ňĻsomeňļ were martyred‚ÄĒI will certainly forgive their sins and admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, as a reward from Allah. And with Allah is the finest reward!‚ÄĚ

3:196.text: Do not be deceived by the prosperity of the disbelievers throughout the land.

3:197.text: It is only a brief enjoyment. Then Hell will be their home‚ÄĒwhat an evil place to rest!

3:198.text: But those who are mindful of their Lord will be in Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever‚ÄĒas an accommodation from Allah. And what is with Allah is best for the virtuous.

3:199.text: Indeed, there are some among the People of the Book who truly believe in Allah and what has been revealed to you ňĻbelieversňļ and what was revealed to them. They humble themselves before Allah‚ÄĒnever trading Allah‚Äôs revelations for a fleeting gain. Their reward is with their Lord. Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.

3:200.text: O believers! Patiently endure, persevere, stand on guard,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and be mindful of Allah, so you may be successful.
footnotes: ¬†R√ĘbiŠĻ≠o¬†refers to watching over the borders of one‚Äôs homeland. It can also mean keeping up one‚Äôs prayers.

30:3.text: in a nearby land.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet following their defeat, they will triumph
footnotes: ¬†This can also mean ‚Äúin the lowest part of the earth.‚ÄĚ

30:4.text: within three to nine years.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†The ňĻwholeňļ matter rests with Allah before and after ňĻvictoryňļ. And on that day the believers will rejoice
footnotes: ¬†This Meccan s√Ľrah¬†takes its name from the reference to the Romans in verse 2. The world‚Äôs superpowers in the early 7th century were the Roman Byzantine and Persian Empires. When they went to war in 614 C.E., the Romans suffered a devastating defeat. The Meccan pagans rejoiced at the defeat of the Roman Christians at the hands of the Persian pagans. Soon verses 30:1-5 were revealed, stating that the Romans would be victorious in three to nine years. Eight years later, the Romans won a decisive battle against the Persians, reportedly on the same day the Muslims vanquished the Meccan army at the Battle of Badr.

30:5.text: at the victory willed by Allah. He gives victory to whoever He wills. For He is the Almighty, Most Merciful.

30:6.text: ňĻThis isňļ the promise of Allah. ňĻAndňļ Allah never fails in His promise. But most people do not know.

30:7.text: They ňĻonlyňļ know the worldly affairs of this life, but are ňĻtotallyňļ oblivious to the Hereafter.

30:8.text: Have they not reflected upon their own being? Allah only created the heavens and the earth and everything in between for a purpose and an appointed term. Yet most people are truly in denial of the meeting with their Lord!

30:9.text: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them? They were far superior in might; they cultivated the land and developed it more than these ňĻMeccansňļ ever have. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs. Allah would have never wronged them, but it was they who wronged themselves.

30:10.text: Then most evil was the end of the evildoers for denying and mocking the signs of Allah.

30:11.text: It is Allah Who originates the creation, and will resurrect it. And then to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

30:12.text: On the Day the Hour will arrive, the wicked will be dumbstruck.

30:13.text: There will be no intercessors for them from among their associate-gods, and they will ňĻtotallyňļ deny their associate-gods.

30:14.text: And on the Day the Hour will arrive, the people will then be split ňĻinto two groupsňļ.

30:15.text: As for those who believed and did good, they will be rejoicing in a Garden.

30:16.text: And as for those who disbelieved, and denied Our signs and the meeting ňĻwith Allahňļ in the Hereafter, they will be confined in punishment.

30:17.text: So glorify Allah in the evening and in the morning‚ÄĒ

30:18.text: all praise is for Him in the heavens and the earth‚ÄĒas well as in the afternoon, and at noon.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This verse outlines the times of the five daily prayers. The evening refers to Maghrib¬†and ‚ÄôIsh√Ę' prayers, the morning refers to Fajr, the afternoon refers to ‚ÄôAŠĻ£r, and noon refers to Šļíuhr.

30:19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living. And He gives life to the earth after its death. And so will you be brought forth ňĻfrom the graveňļ.

30:20.text: One of His signs is that He created you from dust, then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒyou are human beings spreading over ňĻthe earthňļ.

30:21.text: And one of His signs is that He created for you spouses from among yourselves so that you may find comfort in them. And He has placed between you compassion and mercy. Surely in this are signs for people who reflect.

30:22.text: And one of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the diversity of your languages and colours. Surely in this are signs for those of ňĻsoundňļ knowledge.

30:23.text: And one of His signs is your sleep by night and by day ňĻfor restňļ as well as your seeking His bounty ňĻin bothňļ. Surely in this are signs for people who listen.

30:24.text: And one of His signs is that He shows you lightning, inspiring ňĻyou withňļ hope and fear.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And He sends down rain from the sky, reviving the earth after its death. Surely in this are signs for people who understand.
footnotes: ¬†Hope of rain and fear of torment.

30:25.text: And one of His signs is that the heavens and the earth persist by His command. Then when He calls you out of the earth just once, you will instantly come forth.

30:26.text: And to Him belong all those in the heavens and the earth‚ÄĒall are subject to His Will.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., to Him.

30:27.text: And He is the One Who originates the creation then will resurrect it‚ÄĒwhich is even easier for Him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†To Him belong the finest attributes in the heavens and the earth. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†This is from a human perspective. Otherwise, both the creation of the universe and the resurrection of humans are easy for Allah.

30:28.text: He sets forth for you an example ňĻdrawnňļ from your own lives: would you allow some of those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in your possession to be your equal partners in whatever ňĻwealthňļ We have provided you, keeping them in mind as you are mindful of your peers?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is how We make the signs clear for people who understand.
footnotes: ¬†The passage says that since humans would not allow those who are inferior to them to become their partners in wealth, how can they set up helpless partners with Allah in His kingdom?

30:29.text: In fact, the wrongdoers merely follow their desires with no knowledge. Who then can guide those Allah has left to stray? They will have no helpers.

30:30.text: So be steadfast in faith in all uprightness ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒthe natural Way of Allah which He has instilled in ňĻallňļ people. Let there be no change in this creation of Allah. That is the Straight Way, but most people do not know.

30:31.text: ňĻO believers!ňļ Always turn to Him ňĻin repentanceňļ, be mindful of Him, and establish prayers. And do not be polytheists‚ÄĒ

30:32.text: ňĻlikeňļ those who have divided their faith and split into sects, each rejoicing in what they have.

30:33.text: When people are touched with hardship, they cry out to their Lord, turning to Him ňĻaloneňļ. But as soon as He gives them a taste of His mercy, a group of them associates ňĻothersňļ with their Lord ňĻin worshipňļ,

30:34.text: becoming ungrateful for whatever ňĻfavoursňļ We have given them. So enjoy yourselves, for soon you will know.

30:35.text: Or have We sent down to them an authority which attests to what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ?

30:36.text: If We give people a taste of mercy, they become prideful ňĻbecauseňļ of it. But if they are afflicted with an evil for what their hands have done, they instantly fall into despair.

30:37.text: Have they not seen that Allah gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills? Surely in this are signs for people who believe.

30:38.text: So give your close relatives their due, as well as the poor and the ňĻneedyňļ traveller. That is best for those who seek the pleasure of Allah,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and it is they who will be successful.
footnotes: ¬†lit., seek the Face of Allah.

30:39.text: Whatever loans you give, ňĻonlyňļ seeking interest at the expense of people‚Äôs wealth<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†will not increase with Allah. But whatever charity you give, ňĻonlyňļ seeking the pleasure of Allah‚ÄĒit is they whose reward will be multiplied.
footnotes: ¬†Another meaning is the gift given to someone with the intention of getting a more expensive gift in return‚ÄĒa common ancient practice.

30:40.text: It is Allah Who created you, then gives you provisions, then will cause you to die, and then will bring you back to life. Can any of your associate-gods do any of this? Glorified and Exalted is He above what they associate with Him ňĻin worshipňļ!

30:41.text: Corruption has spread on land and sea as a result of what people‚Äôs hands have done, so that Allah may cause them to taste ňĻthe consequences ofňļ some of their deeds and perhaps they might return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.

30:42.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúTravel throughout the land and see what was the end of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before ňĻyouňļ‚ÄĒmost of them were polytheists.‚ÄĚ

30:43.text: So be steadfast in the Upright Faith ňĻO Prophetňļ, before the coming of a Day from Allah that cannot be averted. On that Day the people will be divided:

30:44.text: those who disbelieved will bear ňĻthe burden ofňļ their own disbelief; and those who did good will have prepared for themselves ňĻeternal homesňļ,

30:45.text: so that He may ňĻgenerouslyňļ reward those who believe and do good, out of His grace. He truly does not like the disbelievers.

30:46.text: And one of His signs is that He sends the winds, ushering in good news ňĻof rainňļ so that He may give you a taste of His mercy, and that ships may sail by His command, and that you may seek His bounty, and perhaps you will be grateful.

30:47.text: Indeed, We sent before you ňĻO Prophetňļ messengers, each to their own people, and they came to them with clear proofs. Then We inflicted punishment upon those who persisted in wickedness. For it is Our duty to help the believers.

30:48.text: It is Allah Who sends the winds, which then stir up ňĻvapour, formingňļ clouds, which He then spreads out in the sky or piles up into masses as He wills, from which you see rain come forth. Then as soon as He causes it to fall on whoever He wills of His servants, they rejoice,

30:49.text: although they had utterly lost hope just before it was sent down to them.

30:50.text: See then the impact of Allah‚Äôs mercy: how He gives life to the earth after its death! Surely That ňĻsame Godňļ can raise the dead. For He is Most Capable of everything.

30:51.text: Then if We send a ňĻharshňļ wind which they see withering ňĻtheirňļ crops, they will definitely deny ňĻold favoursňļ right after.

30:52.text: So you ňĻO Prophetňļ certainly cannot make the dead hear ňĻthe truthňļ. Nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs and walk away.

30:53.text: Nor can you lead the blind out of their misguidance. You can make none hear ňĻthe truthňļ except those who believe in Our revelations, ňĻfullyňļ submitting ňĻto Allahňļ.

30:54.text: It is Allah Who created you in a state of weakness, then developed ňĻyourňļ weakness into strength, then developed ňĻyourňļ strength into weakness and old age.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He creates whatever He wills. For He is the All-Knowing, Most Capable.
footnotes: ¬†lit., grey hair.

30:55.text: And on the Day the Hour will arrive, the wicked will swear that they did not stay ňĻin this worldňļ more than an hour. In this way they were always deluded ňĻin the worldňļ.

30:56.text: But those gifted with knowledge and faith will say ňĻto themňļ, ‚ÄúYou did actually stay‚ÄĒas destined by Allah‚ÄĒuntil the Day of Resurrection. So here is the Day of Resurrection ňĻwhich you deniedňļ! But you did not know ňĻit was trueňļ.‚ÄĚ

30:57.text: So on that Day the wrongdoers‚Äô excuses will not benefit them, nor will they be allowed to appease ňĻtheir Lordňļ.

30:58.text: We have certainly set forth every ňĻkind ofňļ lesson for people in this Quran. And no matter what sign you bring to them ňĻO Prophetňļ, the disbelievers will definitely say ňĻto the believersňļ, ‚ÄúYou are only a people of falsehood.‚ÄĚ

30:59.text: This is how Allah seals the hearts of those unwilling to know ňĻthe truthňļ.

30:60.text: So be patient, for the promise of Allah certainly is true. And do not be disturbed by those who have no sure faith.

31:2.text: These are the verses of the Book, rich in wisdom.

31:3.text: ňĻIt isňļ a guide and mercy for the good-doers‚ÄĒ

31:4.text: those who establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and have sure faith in the Hereafter.

31:5.text: It is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ guided by their Lord, and it is they who will be successful. ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†

31:6.text: But there are some who employ theatrics,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†only to lead others away from Allah‚Äôs Way‚ÄĒwithout any knowledge‚ÄĒand to make a mockery of it. They will suffer¬†a humiliating punishment.
footnotes: ¬†Some pagan Arabs used to utilize singing, dancing, chanting, etc. to distract people‚Äôs attention from listening to the recitation of the Quran.

31:7.text: Whenever Our revelations are recited to them, they turn away in arrogance as if they did not hear them, as if there is deafness in their ears. So give them good news ňĻO Prophetňļ of a painful punishment.

31:8.text: Surely those who believe and do good will have the Gardens of Bliss,

31:9.text: staying there forever. Allah‚Äôs promise is true. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

31:10.text: He created the heavens without pillars‚ÄĒas you can see‚ÄĒand placed firm mountains upon the earth so it does not shake with you, and scattered throughout it all types of creatures. And We send down rain from the sky, causing every type of fine plant to grow on earth.

31:11.text: This is Allah‚Äôs creation. Now show Me what those ňĻgodsňļ other than Him have created. In fact, the wrongdoers are clearly astray.

31:12.text: Indeed, We blessed Luqm√Ęn with wisdom, ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúBe grateful to Allah, for whoever is grateful, it is only for their own good. And whoever is ungrateful, then surely Allah is Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Luqm√Ęn is believed to have been a righteous, wise man who lived around the time of Prophet David (Ô∑ļ).

31:13.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Luqm√Ęn said to his son, while advising him, ‚ÄúO my dear son! Never associate ňĻanythingňļ with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ, for associating ňĻothers with Himňļ is truly the worst of all wrongs.‚ÄĚ

31:14.text: And We have commanded people to ňĻhonourňļ their parents. Their mothers bore them through hardship upon hardship, and their weaning takes two years. So be grateful to Me and your parents.¬†To Me is the final return.

31:15.text: But if they pressure you to associate with Me what you have no knowledge of,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†do not obey them. Still keep their company in this world courteously, and follow the way of those who turn to Me ňĻin devotionňļ. Then to Me you will ňĻallňļ return, and then I will inform you of what you used to do.
footnotes: ¬†Other gods.

31:16.text: ňĻLuqm√Ęn ¬†added,ňļ ‚ÄúO my dear son! ňĻEvenňļ if a deed were the weight of a mustard seed‚ÄĒbe it ňĻhiddenňļ in a rock or in the heavens or the earth‚ÄĒAllah will bring it forth. Surely Allah is Most Subtle, All-Aware.

31:17.text: ‚ÄúO my dear son! Establish prayer, encourage what is good and forbid what is evil, and endure patiently whatever befalls you. Surely this is a resolve to aspire to.

31:18.text: ‚ÄúAnd do not turn your nose up to people, nor walk pridefully upon the earth. Surely Allah does not like whoever is arrogant, boastful.

31:19.text: Be moderate in your pace. And lower your voice, for the ugliest of all voices is certainly the braying of donkeys.‚ÄĚ

31:20.text: Have you not seen that Allah has subjected for you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and has lavished His favours upon you, both seen and unseen? ňĻStillňļ there are some who dispute about Allah without knowledge, or guidance, or an enlightening scripture.

31:21.text: When it is said to them, ‚ÄúFollow what Allah has revealed,‚ÄĚ they reply, ‚ÄúNo! We ňĻonlyňļ follow what we found our forefathers practicing.‚ÄĚ ňĻWould they still do soňļ even if Satan is inviting them to the torment of the Blaze?

31:22.text: Whoever fully submits themselves to Allah and is a good-doer, they have certainly grasped the firmest hand-hold. And with Allah rests the outcome of ňĻallňļ affairs.

31:23.text: But whoever disbelieves, do not let their disbelief grieve you ňĻO Prophetňļ. To Us is their return, and We will inform them of all they did. Surely Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

31:24.text: We allow them enjoyment for a little while, then ňĻin timeňļ We will force them into a harsh torment.

31:25.text: And if you ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they will definitely say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah!‚ÄĚ In fact, most of them do not know.

31:26.text: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Allah is truly the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

31:27.text: If all the trees on earth were pens and the ocean ňĻwere inkňļ, refilled by seven other oceans, the Words of Allah would not be exhausted. Surely Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

31:28.text: The creation and resurrection of you ňĻallňļ is as simple ňĻfor Himňļ as that of a single soul. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

31:29.text: Do you not see that Allah causes the night to merge into the day and the day into the night, and has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term, and that Allah is All-Aware of what you do?

31:30.text: That is because Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Truth and what they invoke besides Him is falsehood, and ňĻbecauseňļ Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Most High, All-Great.

31:31.text: Do you not see that the ships sail ňĻsmoothlyňļ through the sea by the grace of Allah so that He may show you some of His signs? Surely in this are signs for whoever is steadfast, grateful.

31:32.text: And as soon as they are overwhelmed by waves like mountains, they cry out to Allah ňĻaloneňļ in sincere devotion. But when He delivers them ňĻsafelyňļ to shore, only some become relatively grateful. And none rejects Our signs except whoever is deceitful, ungrateful.

31:33.text: O humanity! Be mindful of your Lord, and beware of a Day when no parent will be of any benefit to their child, nor will a child be of any benefit to their parent. Surely Allah‚Äôs promise is true. So do not let the life of this world deceive you, nor let the Chief Deceiver<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†deceive you about Allah.
footnotes: ¬†Satan.

31:34.text: Indeed, Allah ňĻaloneňļ has the knowledge of the Hour. He sends down the rain,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and knows what is in the wombs.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†No soul knows what it will earn for tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die. Surely Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.
footnotes: ¬†He knows precisely when, where, and how much rain falls, and whether it is going to be drunk by people and animals or used for irrigation, or absorbed by the earth, etc.

¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Bukh√Ęri and Muslim to have said, ‚ÄúIndeed, the creation of each one of you is brought together in the womb of one‚Äôs mother as a drop of ňĻmale and femaleňļ discharges for forty days, then one becomes a clinging clot for a similar period, then a lump of flesh for a similar period. Then an angel is sent to blow the breath of life into the embryo. The angel is then commanded to write four things: one‚Äôs destined provisions, lifespan, actions, and whether one will be happy or miserable ňĻin the Hereafterňļ.‚ÄĚ

32:2.text: The revelation of this Book is‚ÄĒbeyond doubt‚ÄĒfrom the Lord of all worlds.

32:3.text: Or do they say, ‚ÄúHe has fabricated it!‚ÄĚ? No! It is the truth from your Lord in order for you to warn a people to whom no warner has come before you, so they may be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

32:4.text: It is Allah Who has created the heavens and the earth and everything in between in six Days,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then established Himself on the Throne. You have no protector or intercessor besides Him. Will you not then be mindful?
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:54.

32:5.text: He conducts every affair from the heavens to the earth, then it all ascends to Him on a Day whose length is a thousand years by your counting.

32:6.text: That is the Knower of the seen and unseen‚ÄĒthe Almighty, Most Merciful,

32:7.text: Who has perfected everything He created. And He originated the creation of humankind from clay.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Adam (Ô∑ļ).

32:8.text: Then He made his descendants from an extract of a humble fluid,

32:9.text: then He fashioned them and had a spirit of His Own ňĻcreationňļ breathed into them. And He gave you hearing, sight, and intellect. ňĻYetňļ you hardly give any thanks.

32:10.text: ňĻStillňļ they ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen we are disintegrated into the earth, will we really be raised as a new creation?‚ÄĚ In fact, they are in denial of the meeting with their Lord.

32:11.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYour soul will be taken by the Angel of Death, who is in charge of you. Then to your Lord you will ňĻallňļ be returned.‚ÄĚ

32:12.text: If only you could see the wicked hanging their heads ňĻin shameňļ before their Lord, ňĻcrying:ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! We have now seen and heard, so send us back and we will do good. We truly have sure faith ňĻnowňļ!‚ÄĚ

32:13.text: Had We willed, We could have easily imposed guidance on every soul. But My Word will come to pass: I will surely fill up Hell with jinn and humans all together.

32:14.text: So taste ňĻthe punishmentňļ for neglecting the meeting of this Day of yours. We ňĻtooňļ will certainly neglect you. And taste the torment of eternity for what you used to do!

32:15.text: The only ňĻtrueňļ believers in Our revelation are those who‚ÄĒwhen it is recited to them‚ÄĒfall into prostration and glorify the praises of their Lord and are not too proud.

32:16.text: They abandon their beds, invoking their Lord with hope and fear, and donate from what We have provided for them.

32:17.text: No soul can imagine what delights are kept in store for them as a reward for what they used to do.

32:18.text: Is the one who is a believer equal ňĻbefore Allahňļ to the one who is rebellious? They are not equal!

32:19.text: As for those who believe and do good, they will have the Gardens of ňĻEternalňļ Residence‚ÄĒas an accommodation for what they used to do.

32:20.text: But as for those who are rebellious, the Fire will be their home. Whenever they try to escape from it, they will be forced back into it, and will be told, ‚ÄúTaste the Fire‚Äôs torment, which you used to deny.‚ÄĚ

32:21.text: We will certainly make them taste some of the minor torment ňĻin this lifeňļ before the major torment ňĻof the Hereafterňļ, so perhaps they will return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.

32:22.text: And who does more wrong than the one who is reminded of Allah‚Äôs revelations then turns away from them? We will surely inflict punishment upon the wicked.

32:23.text: Indeed, We gave the Scripture to Moses‚ÄĒso let there be no doubt ňĻO Prophetňļ that you ňĻtooňļ are receiving revelations‚ÄĒ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>and We made it a guide for the Children of Israel.
footnotes: ¬†Other possible translations: 1. ‚ÄúDo not be in doubt that Moses received it.‚ÄĚ 2. ‚ÄúDo not be in doubt of your meeting with Moses.‚ÄĚ

32:24.text: We raised from among them leaders,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†guiding by Our command, when they patiently endured and firmly believed in Our signs.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., prophets.

32:25.text: Indeed, your Lord will decide between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their differences.

32:26.text: Is it not yet clear to them how many peoples We destroyed before them, whose ruins they still pass by? Surely in this are signs. Will they not then listen?

32:27.text: Do they not see how We drive rain to parched land, producing ňĻvariousňļ crops from which they and their cattle eat? Will they not then see?

32:28.text: They ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen is this ňĻDay of finalňļ Decision, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

32:29.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúOn the Day of Decision it will not benefit the disbelievers to believe then, nor will they be delayed ňĻfrom punishmentňļ.‚ÄĚ

32:30.text: So turn away from them, and wait! They too are waiting.

33:1.text: O Prophet! ňĻAlwaysňļ be mindful of Allah, and do not yield to the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

33:2.text: Follow what is revealed to you from your Lord. Surely Allah is All-Aware of what you ňĻallňļ do.

33:3.text: And put your trust in Allah, for Allah is sufficient as a Trustee of Affairs.

33:4.text: Allah does not place two hearts in any person‚Äôs chest. Nor does He regard your wives as ňĻunlawful for you likeňļ your real mothers, ňĻevenňļ if you say they are.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Nor does He regard your adopted children as your real children.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†These are only your baseless assertions. But Allah declares the truth, and He ňĻaloneňļ guides to the ňĻRightňļ Way.
footnotes: ¬†This divorce (called Šļďih√Ęr) was commonly practiced in Arabia before the Prophet (Ô∑ļ). If a man declared his wife as unlawful for him as the Šļďahr¬†(back) of his mother, his wife would be divorced. Islam abolished this type of divorce (see¬†58:3-4).

¬†The following distinction should be made:

  1. Sponsorship (which is permissible): a person can sponsor a child or host them in their home and care for them as they care for their own children, except for legal matters. For example, adopted children keep their last names, are allowed to marry the children of their adopted parent, adopted and biological children of the opposite sex should dress modestly in front of each other, and do not have a share in the estate of their adopted parents, but can get a share of inheritance (up to one-third of the estate) through bequest (waŠĻ£iyah).
  2. Adoption: a person is not allowed to take an orphan and give them his/her last name, give them a share of inheritance similar to their own children, etc.

33:5.text: Let your adopted children keep their family names. That is more just in the sight of Allah. But if you do not know their fathers, then they are ňĻsimplyňļ your fellow believers and close associates. There is no blame on you for what you do by mistake, but ňĻonlyňļ for what you do intentionally. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

33:6.text: The Prophet has a stronger affinity to the believers than they do themselves. And his wives are their mothers. As ordained by Allah, blood relatives are more entitled ňĻto inheritanceňļ than ňĻotherňļ believers and immigrants, unless you ňĻwant toňļ show kindness to your ňĻcloseňļ associates ňĻthrough bequestňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is decreed in the Record.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This verse confirms a ruling mentioned in 8:75 which ended a previous ruling that allowed inheritance between Muslims from Mecca (Al-Muh√Ęjir√Ľn, the Emigrants) and Muslims from Medina (Al-AnŠĻ£√Ęr, the Helpers). Now, only relatives can inherit from one another, whereas non-heirs can receive a share through bequest, up to one third of the estate. See¬†4:7, 11-13, 32-33, and 176.
¬†See¬†footnote for 27:75.

33:7.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We took a covenant from the prophets, as well as from you ňĻO Prophetňļ, and from Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus, son of Mary. We did take a solemn covenant from ňĻall ofňļ them

33:8.text: so that He may question these men of truth about their ňĻdelivery of theňļ truth. And He has prepared a painful punishment for the disbelievers.

33:9.text: O believers! Remember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you when ňĻenemyňļ forces came to ňĻbesiegeňļ you ňĻin Medinaňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so We sent against them a ňĻbitterňļ wind and forces you could not see.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†In 5 A.H./627 C.E., the Meccan pagans along with some Arab and Jewish tribes (totalling around 10 000 soldiers) laid a siege around Medina, where the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was positioned with 3000 soldiers. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) had anticipated the offence and, upon an advice from one of his companions, dug a trench around the city to protect it from invaders. After several unsuccessful attempts to cross the trench, the allied enemies quickly lost morale, and were forced to end the siege due to severe weather conditions. This encounter is commonly known as the Battle of the Trench or the Enemy Alliance.
¬†The angels.

33:10.text: ňĻRememberňļ when they came at you from east and west,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†when your eyes grew wild ňĻin horrorňļ and your hearts jumped into your throats, and you entertained ňĻconflictingňļ thoughts about Allah.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., from above and below you.
¬†The believers were reassured while the doubts of the hypocrites grew more fierce.

33:11.text: Then and there the believers were put to the test, and were violently shaken.

33:12.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when the hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts said, ‚ÄúAllah and His Messenger have promised us nothing but delusion!‚ÄĚ

33:13.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when a group of them said, ‚ÄúO people of Yathrib!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†There is no point in you staying ňĻhereňļ, so retreat!‚ÄĚ Another group of them asked the Prophet‚Äôs permission ňĻto leaveňļ, saying, ‚ÄúOur homes are vulnerable,‚ÄĚ while ňĻin factňļ they were not vulnerable. They only wished to flee.
footnotes: ¬†Yathrib was the name of Medina before the arrival of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and Muslim emigrants.

33:14.text: Had their city been sacked from all sides and they had been asked to abandon faith, they would have done so with little hesitation.

33:15.text: They had already pledged to Allah earlier never to turn their backs ňĻin retreatňļ. And a pledge to Allah must be answered for.

33:16.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúFleeing will not benefit you if you ňĻtry toňļ escape a natural or violent death. ňĻIf it is not your time,ňļ you will only be allowed enjoyment for a little while.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Until the end of your short life on earth.

33:17.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWho can put you out of Allah‚Äôs reach if He intends to harm you or show you mercy?‚ÄĚ They can never find any protector or helper besides Allah.

33:18.text: Allah knows well those among you who discourage ňĻothers from fightingňļ, saying ňĻsecretlyňļ to their brothers, ‚ÄúStay with us,‚ÄĚ and who themselves hardly take part in fighting.

33:19.text: ňĻThey areňļ totally unwilling to assist you. When danger comes, you see them staring at you with their eyes rolling like someone in the throes of death. But once the danger is over, they slash you with razor-sharp tongues, ravenous for ňĻworldlyňļ gains. Such people have not ňĻtrulyňļ believed, so Allah has rendered their deeds void. And that is easy for Allah.

33:20.text: They ňĻstillňļ think that the enemy alliance has not ňĻyetňļ withdrawn. And if the allies were to come ňĻagainňļ, the hypocrites would wish to be away in the desert among nomadic Arabs, ňĻonlyňļ asking for news about you ňĻbelieversňļ. And if the hypocrites were in your midst, they would hardly take part in the fight.

33:21.text: Indeed, in the Messenger of Allah you have an excellent example for whoever has hope in Allah and the Last Day, and remembers Allah often.

33:22.text: When the believers saw the enemy alliance, they said, ‚ÄúThis is what Allah and His Messenger had promised us. The promise of Allah and His Messenger has come true.‚ÄĚ And this only increased them in faith and submission.

33:23.text: Among the believers are men who have proven true to what they pledged to Allah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Some of them have fulfilled their pledge ňĻwith their livesňļ, others are waiting ňĻtheir turnňļ. They have never changed ňĻtheir commitmentňļ in the least.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:46.

33:24.text: ňĻIt all happenedňļ so Allah may reward the faithful for their faithfulness, and punish the hypocrites if He wills or turn to them ňĻin mercyňļ. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

33:25.text: And Allah drove back the disbelievers in their rage, totally empty-handed. And Allah spared the believers from fighting. For Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty.

33:26.text: And He brought down those from the People of the Book<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who supported the enemy alliance from their own strongholds, and cast horror into their hearts. You ňĻbelieversňļ killed some, and took others captive.
footnotes: ¬†The Jews of Banu QuraiŠļďah who had violated their treaty with the Muslims and sided with the enemy alliance. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) asked how they wished to be judged, and they chose to be judged by their book, the Torah‚ÄĒin which the penalty for treason is death.

33:27.text: He has also caused you to take over their lands, homes, and wealth, as well as lands you have not yet set foot on. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

33:28.text: O Prophet! Say to your wives, ‚ÄúIf you desire the life of this world and its luxury, then come, I will give you a ňĻsuitableňļ compensation ňĻfor divorceňļ and let you go graciously.

33:29.text: But if you desire Allah and His Messenger and the ňĻeverlastingňļ Home of the Hereafter, then surely Allah has prepared a great reward for those of you who do good.‚ÄĚ

33:30.text: O wives of the Prophet! If any of you were to commit a blatant misconduct, the punishment would be doubled for her. And that is easy for Allah.

33:31.text: And whoever of you devoutly obeys Allah and His Messenger and does good, We will grant her double the reward, and We have prepared for her an honourable provision.

33:32.text: O wives of the Prophet! You are not like any other women: if you are mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, then do not be overly effeminate in speech ňĻwith menňļ or those with sickness in their hearts may be tempted, but speak in a moderate tone.

33:33.text: Settle in your homes, and do not display yourselves as women did in the days of ňĻpre-Islamicňļ ignorance. Establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and obey Allah and His Messenger. Allah only intends to keep ňĻthe causes ofňļ evil away from you and purify you completely, O ¬†members of the ňĻProphet‚Äôsňļ family!

33:34.text: ňĻAlwaysňļ remember what is recited in your homes of Allah‚Äôs revelations and ňĻpropheticňļ wisdom.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah is Most Subtle, All-Aware.
footnotes: ¬†Allah‚Äôs revelations refers to verses of the Quran whereas prophetic wisdom refers to the sayings and teachings of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), known collectively as Šł•ad√ģth.

33:35.text: Surely ňĻforňļ Muslim men and women, believing men and women,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†devout men and women, truthful men and women, patient men and women, humble men and women, charitable men and women, fasting men and women, men and women who guard their chastity, and men and women who remember Allah often‚ÄĒfor ňĻall ofňļ them Allah has prepared forgiveness and a great reward.
footnotes: ¬†Though Islam and √ém√Ęn¬†are sometimes used interchangeably in the Quran, √ém√Ęn is a higher state of Islam. A Muslim is someone who observes the five pillars of Islam, but a believer is someone with strong faith, who does everything purely for the sake of Allah, and is mindful of Allah in everything they say or do. Every Mu'min¬†(believer/faithful) is a Muslim, but not every Muslim is a Mu'min. See¬†49:14.

33:36.text: It is not for a believing man or woman‚ÄĒwhen Allah and His Messenger decree a matter‚ÄĒto have any other choice in that matter.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Indeed, whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger has clearly gone ňĻfarňļ astray.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, if Allah makes a ruling in the Quran or the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) makes a ruling, then a Muslim is not allowed to seek judgment from anyone else, nor follow their own desires.

33:37.text: And ňĻremember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when you said to the one<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for whom Allah has done a favour and you ňĻtooňļ have done a favour,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†‚ÄúKeep your wife and fear Allah,‚ÄĚ while concealing within yourself what Allah was going to reveal. And ňĻsoňļ you were considering the people, whereas Allah was more worthy of your consideration. So when Zaid totally lost interest in ňĻkeepingňļ his wife, We gave her to you in marriage, so that there would be no blame on the believers for marrying the ex-wives of their adopted sons after their divorce. And Allah‚Äôs command is totally binding.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to Zaid ibn Šł§√Ęrithah, the Prophet‚Äôs adopted son before adoption was made unlawful. His wife, Zainab bint JaŠł•sh was from a prestigious family. Because Zaid and Zainab came from two different social classes, the marriage was not successful. Eventually, Zaid insisted on divorcing his wife, despite several appeals from the Prophet (Ô∑ļ). Since one‚Äôs adopted son was no longer considered to be one‚Äôs own son, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was later allowed to marry Zainab.
¬†Allah‚Äôs favour to Zaid was to guide him to Islam, and the Prophet‚Äôs favour was to free him from slavery.

33:38.text: There is no blame on the Prophet for doing what Allah has ordained for him. That has been the way of Allah with those ňĻprophetsňļ who had gone before. And Allah‚Äôs command has been firmly decreed.

33:39.text: ňĻThat is His way withňļ those ňĻprophetsňļ who deliver the messages of Allah, and consider Him, and none but Allah. And sufficient is Allah as a ňĻvigilantňļ Reckoner.

33:40.text: MuŠł•ammad is not the father of any of your men,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but is the Messenger of Allah and the seal of the prophets. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.
footnotes: ¬†He (Ô∑ļ) is not the father of Zaid (mentioned in 33:37) or any other man. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) had three biological sons, who all died in childhood.

33:41.text: O believers! Always remember Allah often,

33:42.text: and glorify Him morning and evening.

33:43.text: He is the One Who showers His blessings upon you‚ÄĒand His angels pray for you‚ÄĒso that He may bring you out of darkness and into light. For He is ever Merciful to the believers.

33:44.text: Their greeting on the Day they meet Him will be, ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ And He has prepared for them an honourable reward.

33:45.text: O Prophet! We have sent you as a witness, and a deliverer of good news, and a warner,

33:46.text: and a caller to ňĻthe Way ofňļ Allah by His command, and a beacon of light.

33:47.text: Give good news to the believers that they will have a great bounty from Allah.

33:48.text: Do not yield to the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Overlook their annoyances, and put your trust in Allah. For Allah is sufficient as a Trustee of Affairs.

33:49.text: O believers! If you marry believing women and then divorce them before you touch them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†they will have no waiting period for you to count,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†so give them a ňĻsuitableňļ compensation, and let them go graciously.
footnotes: ¬†Before the marriage is consummated.

¬†If divorce is pronounced after the consummation of marriage, a waiting period of three months is observed to give the couple a chance to get back together and to see if the wife is pregnant (see¬†65:4). But if the divorce happens before the marriage is consummated, then there is no waiting period.

33:50.text: O Prophet! We have made lawful for you your wives to whom you have paid their ňĻfullňļ dowries as well as those ňĻbondwomenňļ in your possession, whom Allah has granted you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And ňĻyou are allowed to marryňļ the daughters of your paternal uncles and aunts, and the daughters of your maternal uncles and aunts, who have emigrated like you. Also ňĻallowed for marriage isňļ a believing woman who offers herself to the Prophet ňĻwithout dowryňļ if he is interested in marrying her‚ÄĒňĻthis isňļ exclusively for you, not for the rest of the believers.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†We know well what ňĻrulingsňļ We have ordained for the believers in relation to their wives and those ňĻbondwomenňļ in their possession. As such, there would be no blame on you. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 4:3.

¬†This ruling was exclusively for the Prophet (which he never took advantage of), but Muslims have to pay a dowry to their wives. Some women did offer themselves to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) in marriage, but he declined.

33:51.text: It is up to you ňĻO Prophetňļ to delay or receive whoever you please of your wives.
