There is no blame on you if you call back any of those you have set aside.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†That is more likely that they will be content, not grieved, and satisfied with what you offer them all. Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows what is in your hearts. And Allah is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.
footnotes:
¬†The Prophet‚Äôs married life can be classified as follows:

  • Until the age of 25: he was single.
  • Age 25 ‚Äď 50: he was married only to Khad√ģjah.
  • Age 50 ‚Äď 52: he was single after Khad√ģjah‚Äôs death.
  • Age 53 ‚Äď his death at the age of 63: he had a total of ten wives. Many of these marriages were to widows (who had been left with their children without a provider) and in some cases to foster stronger ties with some of his companions and neighbouring tribes. Of all the women he married, ‚Äô»āishah was the only virgin.

33:52.text: It is not lawful for you ňĻO Prophetňļ to marry more women after this, nor can you replace any of your present wives with another, even if her beauty may attract you‚ÄĒexcept those ňĻbondwomenňļ in your possession. And Allah is ever Watchful over all things.

33:53.text: O believers! Do not enter the homes of the Prophet without permission ňĻand if invitedňļ for a meal, do not ňĻcome too early andňļ linger until the meal is ready. But if you are invited, then enter ňĻon timeňļ. Once you have eaten, then go on your way, and do not stay for casual talk. Such behaviour is truly annoying to the Prophet, yet he is too shy to ask you to leave. But Allah is never shy of the truth. And when you ňĻbelieversňļ ask his wives for something, ask them from behind a barrier. This is purer for your hearts and theirs. And it is not right for you to annoy the Messenger of Allah, nor ever marry his wives after him. This would certainly be a major offence in the sight of Allah.

33:54.text: Whether you reveal something or conceal it, surely Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

33:55.text: There is no blame on the Prophet‚Äôs wives ňĻif they appear unveiledňļ before their fathers,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†their sons, their brothers, their brothers‚Äô sons, their sisters‚Äô sons, their fellow ňĻMuslimňļ women, and those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in their possession. And be mindful of Allah ňĻO wives of the Prophet!ňļ Surely Allah is a Witness over all things.
footnotes: ¬†Paternal and maternal uncles are similar to the fathers.

33:56.text: Indeed, Allah showers His blessings upon the Prophet, and His angels pray for him. O ¬†believers! Invoke Allah‚Äôs blessings upon him, and salute him with worthy greetings of peace.

33:57.text: Surely those who offend Allah<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and His Messenger<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†are condemned by Allah in this world and the Hereafter. And He has prepared for them a humiliating punishment.
footnotes: ¬†By attributing children to Him or associating gods with Him in worship.
¬†By calling him a liar or speaking ill of him and his family.

33:58.text: As for those who abuse believing men and women<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†unjustifiably, they will definitely bear the guilt of slander and blatant sin.
footnotes: ¬†By harming them physically or accusing them falsely.

33:59.text: O Prophet! Ask your wives, daughters, and believing women to draw their cloaks over their bodies. In this way it is more likely that they will be recognized ňĻas virtuousňļ and not be harassed. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†He has forgiven what Muslim women did in the past because they were not obligated to cover up.

33:60.text: If the hypocrites, and those with sickness in their hearts, and rumour-mongers in Medina do not desist, We will certainly incite you ňĻO Prophetňļ against them, and then they will not be your neighbours there any longer.

33:61.text: ňĻThey deserve to beňļ condemned. ňĻIf they were to persist,ňļ they would get themselves seized and killed relentlessly wherever they are found!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This verse is intended to deter the hypocrites. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) never killed any hypocrite as long as they did not join the enemy to attack and kill Muslims.

33:62.text: That was Allah‚Äôs way with those ňĻhypocritesňļ who have gone before. And you will find no change in Allah‚Äôs way.

33:63.text: People ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ about the Hour. Say, ‚ÄúThat knowledge is only with Allah. You never know, perhaps the Hour is near.‚ÄĚ

33:64.text: Surely Allah condemns the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a blazing Fire,

33:65.text: to stay there for ever and ever‚ÄĒnever will they find any protector or helper.

33:66.text: On the Day their faces are ňĻconstantlyňļ flipped in the Fire, they will cry, ‚ÄúOh! If only we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger!‚ÄĚ

33:67.text: And they will say, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We obeyed our leaders and elite, but they led us astray from the ňĻRightňļ Way.

33:68.text: Our Lord! Give them double ňĻourňļ punishment, and condemn them tremendously.‚ÄĚ

33:69.text: O believers! Do not be like those who slandered Moses, but Allah cleared him of what they said. And he was honourable in the sight of Allah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The Quran does not specify how Moses was slandered by some of his people. Some narrations suggest that he was either falsely accused of adultery (see¬†footnote for 28:76), killing his brother Aaron (who died a natural death), or having a skin disease since he, unlike others, used to bathe with his clothes on.

33:70.text: O ¬†believers! Be mindful of Allah, and say what is right.

33:71.text: He will bless your deeds for you, and forgive your sins. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, has truly achieved a great triumph.

33:72.text: Indeed, We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they ňĻallňļ declined to bear it, being fearful of it. But humanity assumed it, ňĻforňļ they are truly wrongful ňĻto themselvesňļ and ignorant ňĻof the consequencesňļ,

33:73.text: so that Allah will punish hypocrite men and women and polytheistic men and women, and Allah will turn in mercy to believing men and women. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This is a guarantee from Allah that if the hypocrites and polytheists ever turn to Him in repentance, He is always willing to forgive them.

34:1.text: All praise is for Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And praise be to Him in the Hereafter. He is the All-Wise, All-Aware.

34:2.text: He knows whatever goes into the earth and whatever comes out of it, and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends into it. And He is the Most Merciful, All-Forgiving.

34:3.text: The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúThe Hour will never come to us.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYes‚ÄĒby my Lord, the Knower of the unseen‚ÄĒit will certainly come to you!‚ÄĚ Not ňĻevenňļ an atom‚Äôs weight is hidden from Him in the heavens or the earth; nor anything smaller or larger than that, but is ňĻwrittenňļ in a perfect Record.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 33:6.

34:4.text: So He may reward those who believe and do good. It is they who will have forgiveness and an honourable provision.

34:5.text: As for those who strive to discredit Our revelations, it is they who will suffer the ňĻworstňļ torment of agonizing pain.

34:6.text: Those gifted with knowledge ňĻclearlyňļ see that what has been revealed to you from your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ is the truth, and that it guides to the Path of the Almighty, the Praiseworthy.

34:7.text: The disbelievers say ňĻmockingly to one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúShall we show you a man who claims that when you have been utterly disintegrated you will be raised as a new creation?

34:8.text: Has he fabricated a lie against Allah or is he insane?‚ÄĚ In fact, those who do not believe in the Hereafter are bound for torment and have strayed farthest ňĻfrom the truthňļ.

34:9.text: Have they not then seen all that surrounds them of the heavens and the earth? If We willed, We could cause the earth to swallow them up, or cause ňĻdeadlyňļ pieces of the sky to fall upon them. Surely in this is a sign for every servant who turns ňĻto Allahňļ.

34:10.text: Indeed, We granted David a ňĻgreatňļ privilege from Us, ňĻcommanding:ňļ ‚ÄúO mountains! Echo his hymns! And the birds as well.‚ÄĚ We made iron mouldable for him,

34:11.text: instructing: ‚ÄúMake full-length armour, ňĻperfectlyňļ balancing the links. And work righteousness ňĻO family of David!ňļ. Indeed, I am All-Seeing of what you do.‚ÄĚ

34:12.text: And to Solomon ňĻWe subjectedňļ the wind: its morning stride was a month‚Äôs journey and so was its evening stride. And We caused a stream of molten copper to flow for him, and ňĻWe subjectedňļ some of the jinn to work under him by his Lord‚Äôs Will. And whoever of them deviated from Our command, We made them taste the torment of the blaze.

34:13.text: They made for him whatever he desired of sanctuaries, statues,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†basins as large as reservoirs, and cooking pots fixed ňĻinto the groundňļ. ňĻWe ordered:ňļ ‚ÄúWork gratefully, O ¬†family of David!‚ÄĚ ňĻOnlyňļ a few of My servants are ňĻtrulyňļ grateful.
footnotes: ¬†Which was permissible at the time of Solomon (Ô∑ļ).

34:14.text: When We decreed Solomon‚Äôs death, nothing indicated to the ňĻsubjectedňļ jinn that he was dead except the termites eating away his staff.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So when he collapsed, the jinn realized that if they had ňĻreallyňļ known the unseen, they would not have remained in ňĻsuchňļ humiliating servitude.
footnotes: ¬†Which he died while leaning on.

34:15.text: Indeed, there was a sign for ňĻthe tribe ofňļ Sheba in their homeland: two orchards‚ÄĒone to the right and the other to the left. ňĻThey were told:ňļ ‚ÄúEat from the provision of your Lord, and be grateful to Him. ňĻYours isňļ a good land and a forgiving Lord.‚ÄĚ

34:16.text: But they turned away. So We sent against them a devastating flood, and replaced their orchards with two others producing bitter fruit, fruitless bushes,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and a few ňĻsparseňļ thorny trees.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., tamarisks.¬†
¬†lit., lote trees.

34:17.text: This is how We rewarded them for their ingratitude. Would We ever punish ňĻanyone in such a wayňļ except the ungrateful?

34:18.text: We had also placed between them and the cities<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We showered with blessings ňĻmany smallňļ towns within sight of one another. And We set moderate travel distances in between, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúTravel between them by day and night safely.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Mecca and Jerusalem.

34:19.text: But they said, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Make ňĻthe distances ofňļ our journeys longer,‚ÄĚ wronging themselves.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So We reduced them to ňĻcautionaryňļ tales, and scattered them utterly. Surely in this are lessons for whoever is steadfast, grateful.
footnotes: ¬†They became bored of living comfortably and traveling easily. A similar example can be found in 2:61, where the Children of Israel became bored with the manna and quails and wanted to eat other things such as onions and garlic.

34:20.text: Indeed, Ibl√ģs‚Äô assumption about them has come true,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so they ňĻallňļ follow him, except a group of ňĻtrueňļ believers.
footnotes: ¬†Ibl√ģs was the name of Satan before his fall from grace (see footnote for 2:33). His assumption (or vow) was that he could easily mislead the majority of people (see¬†7:16-17, 15:39-40, and 38:82-83).

34:21.text: He does not have any authority over them, but ňĻOur Will isňļ only to distinguish those who believe in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt about it. And your Lord is a ňĻvigilantňļ Keeper over all things.

34:22.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúCall upon those you claim ňĻto be divineňļ besides Allah. They do not possess ňĻevenňļ an atom‚Äôs weight either in the heavens or the earth, nor do they have any share in ňĻgoverningňļ them. Nor is any of them a helper to Him.‚ÄĚ

34:23.text: No intercession will be of any benefit with Him, except by those granted permission by Him. ňĻAt last,ňļ when the dread ňĻof Judgment Dayňļ is relieved from their hearts ňĻbecause they are permitted to intercedeňļ, they will ňĻexcitedlyňļ ask ňĻthe angelsňļ, ‚ÄúWhat has your Lord ňĻjustňļ said?‚ÄĚ The angels will reply, ‚ÄúThe truth! And He is the Most High, All-Great.‚ÄĚ

34:24.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWho provides for you from the heavens and the earth?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAllah! Now, certainly one of our two groups<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is ňĻrightlyňļ guided; the other is clearly astray.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The believers and polytheists.

34:25.text: Say, ‚ÄúYou will not be accountable for our misdeeds, nor will we be accountable for your deeds.‚ÄĚ

34:26.text: Say, ‚ÄúOur Lord will gather us together, then He will judge between us with the truth. For He is the All-Knowing Judge.‚ÄĚ

34:27.text: Say, ‚ÄúShow me those ňĻidolsňļ you have joined with Him as partners. No! In fact, He ňĻaloneňļ is Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ

34:28.text: We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner to all of humanity, but most people do not know.

34:29.text: And they ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

34:30.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúA Day has ňĻalreadyňļ been appointed for you, which you can neither delay nor advance by a ňĻsingleňļ moment.‚ÄĚ

34:31.text: The disbelievers vow, ‚ÄúWe will never believe in this Quran, nor in those ňĻScripturesňļ before it.‚ÄĚ If only you could see when the wrongdoers will be detained before their Lord, throwing blame at each other! The lowly ňĻfollowersňļ will say to the arrogant ňĻleadersňļ, ‚ÄúHad it not been for you, we would certainly have been believers.‚ÄĚ

34:32.text: The arrogant will respond to the lowly, ‚ÄúDid we ever hinder you from guidance after it came to you? In fact, you were wicked ňĻon your ownňļ.‚ÄĚ

34:33.text: The lowly will say to the arrogant, ‚ÄúNo! It was your plotting by day and night‚ÄĒwhen you ordered us to disbelieve in Allah and to set up equals with Him.‚ÄĚ They will ňĻallňļ hide ňĻtheirňļ remorse when they see the torment. And We will put shackles around the necks of the disbelievers. Will they be rewarded except for what they used to do?

34:34.text: Whenever We sent a warner to a society, its elite would say, ‚ÄúWe truly reject what you have been sent with.‚ÄĚ

34:35.text: Adding, ‚ÄúWe are far superior ňĻto the believersňļ in wealth and children, and we will never be punished.‚ÄĚ

34:36.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely ňĻit isňļ my Lord ňĻWhoňļ gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. But most people do not know.‚ÄĚ

34:37.text: It is not your wealth or children that bring you closer to Us. But those who believe and do good‚ÄĒit is they who will have a multiplied reward for what they did, and they will be secure in ňĻelevatedňļ mansions.

34:38.text: As for those who strive to discredit Our revelations, it is they who will be confined in punishment.

34:39.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely ňĻit isňļ my Lord ňĻWhoňļ gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills of His servants. And whatever you spend in charity, He will compensate ňĻyouňļ for it. For He is the Best Provider.‚ÄĚ

34:40.text: And ňĻconsiderňļ the Day He will gather them all together, and then ask the angels, ‚ÄúWas it you that these ňĻpolytheistsňļ used to worship?‚ÄĚ

34:41.text: They will say, ‚ÄúGlory be to You! Our loyalty is to You, not them. In fact, they ňĻonlyňļ followed the ňĻtemptations of evilňļ jinn,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†in whom most of them had faith.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the devils.

34:42.text: So Today neither of you can benefit or protect each other. And We will say to the wrongdoers, ‚ÄúTaste the torment of the Fire, which you used to deny.‚ÄĚ

34:43.text: When Our clear revelations are recited to them, they say, ‚ÄúThis is only a man who wishes to hinder you from what your forefathers used to worship.‚ÄĚ They also say, ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ is no more than a fabricated lie.‚ÄĚ And the disbelievers say of the truth when it has come to them, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but pure magic.‚ÄĚ

34:44.text: ňĻThey say so even thoughňļ We had never given them any scriptures to study, nor did We ever send them a warner before you ňĻO Prophetňļ.

34:45.text: Those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them denied as well‚ÄĒand these ňĻMeccansňļ have not attained even one-tenth of what We had given their predecessors.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet ňĻwhenňļ they denied My messengers, how severe was My response!
footnotes: ¬†Like the people of Pharaoh, ‚Äô»ād, and Tham√Ľd.

34:46.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI advise you to do ňĻonlyňļ one thing: stand up for ňĻthe sake ofňļ Allah‚ÄĒindividually or in pairs‚ÄĒthen reflect. Your fellow man<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is not insane. He is only a warner to you before ňĻthe coming ofňļ a severe punishment.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

34:47.text: Say, ‚ÄúIf I had ever asked you for a reward, you could keep it. My reward is only from Allah. And He is a Witness over all things.‚ÄĚ

34:48.text: Say, ‚ÄúSurely my Lord hurls the truth ňĻagainst falsehoodňļ. ňĻHe isňļ the Knower of all unseen.‚ÄĚ

34:49.text: Say, ‚ÄúThe truth has come, and falsehood will vanish, never to return.‚ÄĚ

34:50.text: Say, ‚ÄúIf I am astray, the loss is only mine. And if I am guided, it is ňĻonlyňļ because of what my Lord reveals to me. He is indeed All-Hearing, Ever Near.‚ÄĚ

34:51.text: If only you could see when they will be horrified with no escape ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ! And they will be seized from a nearby place.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†From the place of Judgment to Hell.

34:52.text: They will ňĻthenňļ cry, ‚ÄúWe do ňĻnowňļ believe in it ňĻallňļ.‚ÄĚ But how could they ňĻpossiblyňļ attain faith from a place so far-off ňĻfrom the worldňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†When it is already too late.

34:53.text: while they had already rejected it before, guessing blindly from a place ňĻequallyňļ far-away ňĻfrom the Hereafterňļ?

34:54.text: They will be sealed off from whatever they desire, as was done to their counterparts before. Indeed, they were ňĻallňļ in alarming doubt.

35:1.text: All praise is for Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, Who made angels ňĻas Hisňļ messengers with wings‚ÄĒtwo, three, or four. He increases in creation whatever He wills. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.

35:2.text: Whatever mercy Allah opens up for people, none can withhold it. And whatever He withholds, none but Him can release it. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

35:3.text: O humanity! Remember Allah‚Äôs favours upon you. Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the heavens and the earth? There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. How can you then be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

35:4.text: If you are rejected by them, so too were messengers before you. And to Allah ňĻallňļ matters will be returned ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

35:5.text: O humanity! Indeed, Allah‚Äôs promise is true. So do not let the life of this world deceive you, nor let the Chief Deceiver<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†deceive you about Allah.
footnotes: ¬†Satan.

35:6.text: Surely Satan is an enemy to you, so take him as an enemy. He only invites his followers to become inmates of the Blaze.

35:7.text: Those who disbelieve will have a severe punishment. But those who believe and do good will have forgiveness and a great reward.

35:8.text: Are those whose evil-doing is made so appealing to them that they deem it good ňĻlike those who are rightly guidedňļ? ňĻIt isňļ certainly Allah ňĻWhoňļ leaves to stray whoever He wills, and guides whoever He wills. So do not grieve yourself to death over them ňĻO Prophetňļ. Surely Allah is All-Knowing of what they do.

35:9.text: And it is Allah Who sends the winds, which then stir up ňĻvapour, formingňļ clouds, and then We drive them to a lifeless land, giving life to the earth after its death. Similar is the Resurrection.

35:10.text: Whoever seeks honour and power, then ňĻlet them know thatňļ all honour and power belongs to Allah. To Him ňĻaloneňļ good words ascend, and righteous deeds are raised up by Him. As for those who plot evil, they will suffer a severe punishment. And the plotting of such ňĻpeopleňļ is doomed ňĻto failňļ.

35:11.text: And ňĻit isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ created you from dust,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then ňĻdeveloped youňļ from a sperm-drop, then made you into pairs.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†No female ever conceives or delivers without His knowledge. And no one‚Äôs life is made long or cut short but is ňĻwrittenňļ in a Record.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†That is certainly easy for Allah.
footnotes: ¬†Created your father, Adam, from dust.
¬†Males and females.
¬†See¬†footnote for 33:6.

35:12.text: The two bodies of water are not alike: one is fresh, palatable, and pleasant to drink and the other is salty and bitter. Yet from them both you eat tender seafood and extract ornaments to wear. And you see the ships ploughing their way through both, so you may seek His bounty and give thanks ňĻto Himňļ.

35:13.text: He merges the night into the day and the day into the night, and has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term. That is Allah‚ÄĒyour Lord! All authority belongs to Him. But those ňĻidolsňļ you invoke besides Him do not possess even the skin of a date stone.

35:14.text: If you call upon them, they cannot hear your calls. And if they were to hear, they could not respond to you. On the Day of Judgment they will disown your worship ňĻof themňļ. And no one can inform you ňĻO Prophetňļ like the All-Knowledgeable.

35:15.text: O humanity! It is you who stand in need of Allah, but Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

35:16.text: If He willed, He could eliminate you and produce a new creation.

35:17.text: And that is not difficult for Allah ňĻat allňļ.

35:18.text: No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. And if a sin-burdened soul cries for help with its burden, none of it will be carried‚ÄĒeven by a close relative. You ňĻO Prophetňļ can only warn those who stand in awe of their Lord without seeing Him<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and establish prayer. Whoever purifies themselves, they only do so for their own good. And to Allah is the final return.
footnotes: ¬†This can also mean that they are in awe of their Lord as much in private as they are in public.

35:19.text: Those blind ňĻto the truthňļ and those who can see are not equal,

35:20.text: nor are the darkness and the light,

35:21.text: nor the ňĻscorchingňļ heat and the ňĻcoolňļ shade.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This implies Hell and Paradise.

35:22.text: Nor are the dead and the living equal. Indeed, Allah ňĻaloneňļ makes whoever He wills hear, but you ňĻO Prophetňļ can never make those in the graves hear ňĻyour callňļ.

35:24.text: We have surely sent you with the truth as a deliverer of good news and a warner. There is no community that has not had a warner.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†According to a Prophetic narration collected by Ibn Šł§ibb√Ęn, the total number of prophets sent around the world, from Adam (Ô∑ļ) to MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ), is 124 000‚ÄĒof which only twenty-five are mentioned in the Quran.

35:25.text: If they deny you, so did those before them. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, divine Books, and enlightening Scriptures.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Torah, the Gospel, and Psalms.

35:26.text: Then I seized those who persisted in disbelief. How severe was My response!

35:27.text: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky with which We bring forth fruits of different colours? And in the mountains are streaks of varying shades of white, red, and raven black;

35:28.text: just as people, living beings, and cattle are of various colours as well. Of all of Allah‚Äôs servants, only the knowledgeable ňĻof His mightňļ are ňĻtrulyňļ in awe of Him. Allah is indeed Almighty, All-Forgiving.

35:29.text: Surely those who recite the Book of Allah, establish prayer, and donate from what We have provided for them‚ÄĒsecretly and openly‚ÄĒňĻcanňļ hope for an exchange that will never fail,

35:30.text: so that He will reward them in full and increase them out of His grace. He is truly All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative.

35:31.text: The Book We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ is the truth, confirming what came before it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah is All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†previous footnote.

35:32.text: Then We granted the Book to those We have chosen from Our servants. Some of them wrong themselves, some follow a middle course, and some are foremost in good deeds by Allah‚Äôs Will. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the greatest bounty.

35:33.text: They will enter the Gardens of Eternity, where they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing will be silk.

35:34.text: And they will say, ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah, Who has kept away from us all ňĻcauses ofňļ sorrow. Our Lord is indeed All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative.

35:35.text: ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who‚ÄĒout of His grace‚ÄĒhas settled us in the Home of Everlasting Stay, where we will be touched by neither fatigue nor weariness.‚ÄĚ

35:36.text: As for the disbelievers, they will have the Fire of Hell, where they will not be ňĻallowed to beňļ finished by death, nor will its torment be lightened for them. This is how We reward every ňĻstubbornňļ disbeliever.

35:37.text: There they will be ňĻferventlyňļ screaming, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Take us out ňĻand send us backňļ. We will do good, unlike what we used to do.‚ÄĚ ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúDid We not give you lives long enough so that whoever wanted to be mindful could have done so? And the warner came to you. So taste ňĻthe punishmentňļ, for the wrongdoers have no helper.‚ÄĚ

35:38.text: Indeed, Allah is the Knower of the unseen of the heavens and the earth. He surely knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

35:39.text: He is the One Who has placed you as successors on earth. So whoever disbelieves will bear ňĻthe burden ofňļ their own disbelief. The disbelievers‚Äô denial only increases them in contempt in the sight of their Lord, and it will only contribute to their loss.

35:40.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúHave you considered your associate-gods which you invoke besides Allah? Show me what they have created on earth! Or do they have a share in ňĻthe creation ofňļ the heavens? Or have We given the polytheists a Book, which serves as a clear proof for them? In fact, the wrongdoers promise each other nothing but delusion.‚ÄĚ

35:41.text: Indeed, Allah ňĻaloneňļ keeps the heavens and the earth from falling apart. If they were to fall apart, none but Him could hold them up. He is truly Most Forbearing, All-Forgiving.

35:42.text: They swore by Allah their most solemn oaths that if a warner were to come to them, they would certainly be better guided than any other community. Yet when a warner did come to them, it only drove them farther away‚ÄĒ

35:43.text: behaving arrogantly in the land and plotting evil. But evil plotting only backfires on those who plot. Are they awaiting anything but the fate of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before? You will find no change in the way of Allah, nor will you find it diverted ňĻto someone elseňļ.

35:44.text: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them? They were far superior in might. But there is nothing that can escape Allah in the heavens or the earth. He is certainly All-Knowing, Most Capable.

35:45.text: If Allah were to punish people ňĻimmediatelyňļ for what they have committed, He would not have left a single living being on earth. But He delays them for an appointed term. And when their time arrives, then surely Allah is All-Seeing of His servants.

36:2.text: By the Quran, rich in wisdom!

36:3.text: You ňĻO Prophetňļ are truly one of the messengers

36:5.text: ňĻThis isňļ a revelation from the Almighty, Most Merciful,

36:6.text: so that you may warn a people whose forefathers were not warned, and so are heedless.

36:7.text: The decree ňĻof tormentňļ has already been justified against most of them, for they will never believe.

36:8.text: ňĻIt is as ifňļ We have put shackles around their necks up to their chins, so their heads are forced up,

36:9.text: and have placed a barrier before them and a barrier behind them and covered them ňĻallňļ up, so they fail to see ňĻthe truthňļ.

36:10.text: It is the same whether you warn them or not‚ÄĒthey will never believe.

36:11.text: You can only warn those who follow the Reminder<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and are in awe of the Most Compassionate without seeing Him.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†So give them good news of forgiveness and an honourable reward.
footnotes: ¬†The Reminder is another name for the Quran.
¬†See¬†footnote for 35:18.

36:12.text: It is certainly We Who resurrect the dead, and write what they send forth and what they leave behind. Everything is listed by Us in a perfect Record.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 35:11.

36:13.text: Give them an example ňĻO Prophetňļ of the residents of a town, when the messengers came to them.

36:14.text: We sent them two messengers, but they rejected both. So We reinforced ňĻthe twoňļ with a third, and they declared, ‚ÄúWe have indeed been sent to you ňĻas messengersňļ.‚ÄĚ

36:15.text: The people replied, ‚ÄúYou are only humans like us, and the Most Compassionate has not revealed anything. You are simply lying!‚ÄĚ

36:16.text: The messengers responded, ‚ÄúOur Lord knows that we have truly been sent to you.

36:17.text: And our duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.‚ÄĚ

36:18.text: The people replied, ‚ÄúWe definitely see you as a bad omen for us. If you do not desist, we will certainly stone you ňĻto deathňļ and you will be touched with a painful punishment from us.‚ÄĚ

36:19.text: The messengers said, ‚ÄúYour bad omen lies within yourselves. Are you saying this because you are reminded ňĻof the truthňļ? In fact, you are a transgressing people.‚ÄĚ

36:20.text: Then from the farthest end of the city a man came, rushing. He advised, ‚ÄúO my people! Follow the messengers.

36:21.text: Follow those who ask no reward of you, and are ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

36:22.text: And why should I not worship the One Who has originated me, and to Whom you will be returned.

36:23.text: How could I take besides Him other gods whose intercession would not be of any benefit to me, nor could they save me if the Most Compassionate intended to harm me?

36:24.text: Indeed, I would then be clearly astray.

36:25.text: I do believe in your Lord, so listen to me.‚ÄĚ

36:26.text: ňĻBut they killed him, thenňļ he was told ňĻby the angelsňļ, ‚ÄúEnter Paradise!‚ÄĚ He said, ‚ÄúIf only my people knew

36:27.text: of how my Lord has forgiven me, and made me one of the honourable.‚ÄĚ

36:28.text: We did not send any soldiers from the heavens against his people after his death, nor did We need to.

36:29.text: All it took was one ňĻmightyňļ blast, and they were extinguished at once.

36:30.text: Oh pity, such beings! No messenger ever came to them without being mocked.

36:31.text: Have the deniers not considered how many peoples We destroyed before them who never came back to life again?

36:32.text: Yet they will all be brought before Us.

36:33.text: There is a sign for them in the dead earth: We give it life, producing grain from it for them to eat.

36:34.text: And We have placed in it gardens of palm trees and grapevines, and caused springs to gush forth in it,

36:35.text: so that they may eat from its fruit, which they had no hand in making. Will they not then give thanks?

36:36.text: Glory be to the One Who created all ňĻthings inňļ pairs‚ÄĒňĻbe itňļ what the earth produces, their genders, or what they do not know!

36:37.text: There is also a sign for them in the night: We strip from it daylight, then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒthey are in darkness.

36:38.text: The sun travels for its fixed term. That is the design of the Almighty, All-Knowing.

36:39.text: As for the moon, We have ordained ňĻpreciseňļ phases for it, until it ends up ňĻlookingňļ like an old, curved palm stalk.

36:40.text: It is not for the sun to catch up with the moon,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†nor does the night outrun the day. Each is travelling in an orbit of their own.
footnotes: ¬†The moon completes a cycle every month (waxing and waning), while the sun takes a whole year to complete its cycle (resulting in the spring, summer, fall, and winter seasons).

36:41.text: Another sign for them is that We carried their ancestors ňĻwith Noahňļ in the fully loaded Ark,

36:42.text: and created for them similar things to ride in.

36:43.text: If We willed, We could drown them: then no one would respond to their cries, nor would they be rescued‚ÄĒ

36:44.text: except by mercy from Us, allowing them enjoyment for a ňĻlittleňļ while.

36:45.text: ňĻStill they turn awayňļ when it is said to them, ‚ÄúBeware of what is ahead of you ňĻin the Hereafterňļ and what is behind you ňĻof destroyed nationsňļ so you may be shown mercy.‚ÄĚ

36:46.text: Whenever a sign comes to them from their Lord, they turn away from it.

36:47.text: And when it is said to them, ‚ÄúDonate from what Allah has provided for you,‚ÄĚ the disbelievers say to the believers, ‚ÄúWhy should we feed those whom Allah could have fed if He wanted to? You are clearly astray!‚ÄĚ

36:48.text: And they ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

36:49.text: They must be awaiting a single Blast,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†which will seize them while they are ňĻentrenchedňļ in ňĻworldlyňļ disputes.
footnotes: ¬†On the Day of Judgment, the Trumpet will be blown by an angel‚ÄĒcausing all to die. When it is blown a second time, everyone will be raised from the dead for judgment (see¬†39:68).

36:50.text: Then they will not be able to make a ňĻlastňļ will, nor can they return to their own people.

36:51.text: The Trumpet will be blown ňĻa second timeňļ, then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒthey will rush from the graves to their Lord.

36:52.text: They will cry, ‚ÄúWoe to us! Who has raised us up from our place of rest? This must be what the Most Compassionate warned us of; the messengers told the truth!‚ÄĚ

36:53.text: It will only take one Blast, then at once they will all be brought before Us.

36:54.text: On that Day no soul will be wronged in the least, nor will you be rewarded except for what you used to do.

36:55.text: Indeed, on that Day the residents of Paradise will be busy enjoying themselves.

36:56.text: They and their spouses will be in ňĻcoolňļ shade, reclining on ňĻcanopiedňļ couches.

36:57.text: There they will have fruits and whatever they desire.

36:58.text: And ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ will be ňĻtheirňļ greeting from the Merciful Lord.

36:59.text: ňĻThen the disbelievers will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúStep away ňĻfrom the believersňļ this Day, O ¬†wicked ones!

36:60.text: Did I not command you, O ¬†Children of Adam, not to follow Satan, for he is truly your sworn enemy,

36:61.text: but to worship Me ňĻaloneňļ? This is the Straight Path.

36:62.text: Yet he already misled great multitudes of you. Did you not have any sense?

36:63.text: This is the Hell you were warned of.

36:64.text: Burn in it Today for your disbelief.‚ÄĚ

36:65.text: On this Day We will seal their mouths, their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify to what they used to commit.

36:66.text: Had We willed, We could have easily blinded their eyes, so they would struggle to find their way. How then could they see?

36:67.text: And had We willed, We could have transfigured them on the spot,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so they could neither progress forward nor turn back.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, We have the ability to turn them into stone or any other inanimate object.

36:68.text: And whoever We grant a long life, We reverse them in development.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Will they not then understand?
footnotes: ¬†Human beings are born weak, then they reach their prime, then they grow weak after a certain age. See¬†30:54.

36:69.text: We have not taught him poetry, nor is it fitting for him. This ňĻBookňļ is only a Reminder and a clear Quran

36:70.text: to warn whoever is ňĻtrulyňļ alive and fulfil the decree ňĻof tormentňļ against the disbelievers.

36:71.text: Do they not see that We singlehandedly<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†created for them, among other things, cattle which are under their control?
footnotes: ¬†lit., ‚Äėwhat Our Own Hands have created.‚Äô

36:72.text: And We have subjected these ňĻanimalsňļ to them, so they may ride some and eat others.

36:73.text: And they derive from them other benefits and drinks. Will they not then give thanks?

36:74.text: Still they have taken other gods besides Allah, hoping to be helped ňĻby themňļ.

36:75.text: They cannot help the pagans, even though they serve the idols as dedicated guards.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another meaning is that those idols will be assembled to witness the torment of their worshippers on Judgment Day.

36:76.text: So do not let their words grieve you ňĻO Prophetňļ. Indeed, We ňĻfullyňļ know what they conceal and what they reveal.

36:77.text: Do people not see that We have created them from a sperm-drop, then‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒthey openly challenge ňĻUsňļ?

36:78.text: And they argue with Us‚ÄĒforgetting they were created‚ÄĒsaying, ‚ÄúWho will give life to decayed bones?‚ÄĚ

36:79.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThey will be revived by the One Who produced them the first time, for He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of every created being.

36:80.text: ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who gives you fire from green trees, and‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒyou kindle ňĻfireňļ from them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The verse refers to two types of Arabian trees, markh¬†and ‚Äôaf√Ęr, which produce fire when their green branches are rubbed together.

36:81.text: Can the One Who created the heavens and the earth not ňĻeasilyňļ resurrect these ňĻdeniersňļ?‚ÄĚ Yes ňĻHe canňļ! For He is the Master Creator, All-Knowing.

36:82.text: All it takes, when He wills something ňĻto beňļ, is simply to say to it: ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And it is!

36:83.text: So glory be to the One in Whose Hands is the authority over all things, and to Whom ňĻaloneňļ you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

37:1.text: By those ňĻangelsňļ lined up in ranks,

37:2.text: and those who diligently drive ňĻthe cloudsňļ,

37:3.text: and those who recite the Reminder!

37:5.text: ňĻHe isňļ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, and the Lord of all points of sunrise.

37:6.text: Indeed, We have adorned the lowest heaven with the stars for decoration

37:7.text: and ňĻforňļ protection from every rebellious devil.

37:8.text: They cannot listen to the highest assembly ňĻof angelsňļ for they are pelted from every side,

37:9.text: ňĻfiercelyňļ driven away. And they will suffer an everlasting torment.

37:10.text: But whoever manages to stealthily eavesdrop is ňĻinstantlyňļ pursued by a piercing flare.

37:11.text: So ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ, which is harder to create: them or other marvels of Our creation?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Indeed, We created them from a sticky clay.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the angels, the sun, the moon, the stars, and the mountains.
¬†Their father Adam (Ô∑ļ).

37:12.text: In fact, you are astonished ňĻby their denialňļ, while they ridicule ňĻyouňļ.

37:13.text: When they are reminded, they are never mindful.

37:14.text: And whenever they see a sign, they make fun of it,

37:15.text: saying, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but pure magic.

37:16.text: When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?

37:18.text: Say, ‚ÄúYes! And you will be fully humbled.‚ÄĚ

37:19.text: It will only take one Blast,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then at once they will see ňĻit allňļ.
footnotes: ¬†This is when the Trumpet will be blown the second time.

37:20.text: They will cry, ‚ÄúOh, woe to us! This is the Day of Judgment!‚ÄĚ

37:21.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision which you used to deny.‚ÄĚ

37:22.text: ňĻAllah will say to the angels,ňļ ‚ÄúGather ňĻallňļ the wrongdoers along with their peers, and whatever they used to worship

37:23.text: instead of Allah, then lead them ňĻallňļ to the path of Hell.

37:24.text: And detain them, for they must be questioned.‚ÄĚ

37:25.text: ňĻThen they will be asked,ňļ ‚ÄúWhat is the matter with you that you can no longer help each other?‚ÄĚ

37:26.text: In fact, on that Day they will be ňĻfullyňļ submissive.

37:27.text: They will turn on each other, throwing blame.

37:28.text: The misled will say, ‚ÄúIt was you who deluded us away from what is right.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Other possible translations: 1. ‚ÄúIt was you who used to pressure us.‚ÄĚ 2. ‚ÄúIt was you who tricked us in the name of the truth.‚ÄĚ 3. ‚ÄúIt was you who dissuaded us from doing good.‚ÄĚ 4. ‚ÄúIt was you who deceived us with false oaths.‚ÄĚ

37:29.text: The misleaders will reply, ‚ÄúNo! You disbelieved on your own.

37:30.text: We had no authority over you. In fact, you yourselves were a transgressing people.

37:31.text: The decree of our Lord has come to pass against us ňĻallňļ: we will certainly taste ňĻthe punishmentňļ.

37:32.text: We caused you to deviate, for we ourselves were deviant.‚ÄĚ

37:33.text: Surely on that Day they will ňĻallňļ share in the punishment.

37:34.text: That is certainly how We deal with the wicked.

38:1.text: ŠĻĘ√£d. By the Quran, full of reminders!

38:2.text: ňĻThis is the truth,ňļ yet the disbelievers are ňĻentrenchedňļ in arrogance and opposition.

38:3.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many peoples We destroyed before them, and they cried out when it was too late to escape.

footnotes: ¬†In other words, they do not question the honesty of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), but question the Quran itself. This verse is similar to 6:33.

38:23.text: This is my brother.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He has ninety-nine sheep while I have ňĻonlyňļ one. ňĻStillňļ he asked me to give it up to him, overwhelming me with ňĻhisňļ argument.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Brother in faith or business partner.

38:24.text: David ňĻeventuallyňļ ruled, ‚ÄúHe has definitely wronged you in demanding ňĻto addňļ your sheep to his. And certainly many partners wrong each other, except those who believe and do good‚ÄĒbut how few are they!‚ÄĚ Then David realized that We had tested him so he asked for his Lord‚Äôs forgiveness, fell down in prostration, and turned ňĻto Him in repentanceňļ.

38:26.text: ňĻWe instructed him:ňļ ‚ÄúO David! We have surely made you an authority in the land, so judge between people with truth. And do not follow ňĻyourňļ desires or they will lead you astray from Allah‚Äôs Way. Surely those who go astray from Allah‚Äôs Way will suffer a severe punishment for neglecting the Day of Reckoning.‚ÄĚ

38:27.text: We have not created the heavens and earth and everything in between without purpose‚ÄĒas the disbelievers think. So woe to the disbelievers because of the Fire!

38:29.text: ňĻThis isňļ a blessed Book which We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ so that they may contemplate its verses, and people of reason may be mindful.

38:31.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the well-trained, swift horses were paraded before him in the evening.

footnotes: ¬†Some commentators have a different interpretation, which is not supported by reliable sources. They maintain that Solomon kept watching these fine horses until the sun set, then he realized that he had missed the noon prayer because of his love for horses, so he killed them, striking at their legs and necks.

footnotes: ¬†It is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Bukh√Ęri that one night Solomon said that each of his wives would conceive a boy who would struggle in Allah‚Äôs cause. He forgot to say ‚ÄėAllah willing,‚ÄĚ so only one of them gave birth to a deformed, dead baby. So Solomon prayed to Allah for forgiveness.

38:44.text: ňĻAnd We said to him,ňļ ‚ÄúTake in your hand a bundle of grass, and strike ňĻyour wifeňļ with it, and do not break your oath.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We truly found him patient. What an excellent servant ňĻhe wasňļ! Indeed, he ňĻconstantlyňļ turned ňĻto Allahňļ.

He once became angry with his wife and vowed to give her one hundred lashes if he recovered. When Job‚Äôs affliction was lifted, he became remorseful at what he had said. To help Job fulfil his vow without harming his wife, Allah ordered him to strike her with a bundle of one hundred blades of grass.

38:45.text: And remember Our servants: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob‚ÄĒthe men of strength and insight.

38:47.text: And in Our sight they are truly among the chosen and the finest.

38:48.text: Also remember Ishmael, Elisha, and ‚Īęul-Kifl.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†All are among the best.
footnotes: ¬†Scholars are in disagreement as to whether ‚Īęul-Kifl was a prophet or just a righteous man. Those who maintain that he was a prophet identify him with various Biblical prophets such as Ezekiel, Isaiah, and Obadiah.

38:49.text: This is ňĻallňļ a reminder. And the righteous will certainly have an honourable destination:

38:50.text: the Gardens of Eternity, whose gates will be open for them.

38:51.text: There they will recline, calling for abundant fruit and drink.

38:59.text: ňĻThe misleaders will say to one another,ňļ ‚ÄúHere is a crowd ňĻof followersňļ being thrown in with us. They are not welcome, ňĻforňļ they ňĻtooňļ will burn in the Fire.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, they are not welcome since their presence in Hell with us will not benefit us in anyway.

38:60.text: The followers will respond, ‚ÄúNo! You are not welcome! You brought this upon us. What an evil place for settlement!‚ÄĚ

38:61.text: Adding, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, double their punishment in the Fire.‚ÄĚ

38:62.text: The tyrants will ask ňĻone anotherňļ, ‚ÄúBut why do we not see those we considered to be lowly?

38:63.text: Were we wrong in mocking them ňĻin the worldňļ?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Or do our eyes ňĻjustňļ fail to see them ňĻin the Fireňļ?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†In other words, did we underestimate them?

38:64.text: This dispute between the residents of the Fire will certainly come to pass.

38:65.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI am only a warner. And there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme.

38:66.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between‚ÄĒthe Almighty, Most Forgiving.‚ÄĚ

38:68.text: from which you ňĻpagansňļ are turning away.‚ÄĚ

38:69.text: ňĻAnd say,ňļ ‚ÄúI had no knowledge of the highest assembly ňĻin heavenňļ when they differed ňĻconcerning Adamňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†When the angels obeyed Allah‚Äôs orders to prostrate before Adam, whereas Satan refused to comply.

38:70.text: What is revealed to me is that I am only sent with a clear warning.‚ÄĚ

38:71.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophetňļ when your Lord said to the angels, ‚ÄúI am going to create a human being from clay.

38:72.text: So when I have fashioned him and had a spirit of My Own ňĻcreationňļ breathed into him, fall down in prostration to him.‚ÄĚ

38:73.text: So the angels prostrated all together‚ÄĒ

38:74.text: but not Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who acted arrogantly,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†becoming unfaithful.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:34.
¬†The command to bow down was a test of obedience. Satan arrogantly refused to comply because he believed he was superior to Adam (Ô∑ļ).

38:75.text: Allah asked, ‚ÄúO Ibl√ģs! What prevented you from prostrating to what I created with My Own Hands? Did you ňĻjustňļ become proud? Or have you always been arrogant?‚ÄĚ

38:76.text: He replied, ‚ÄúI am better than he is: You created me from fire and him from clay.‚ÄĚ

38:77.text: Allah commanded, ‚ÄúThen get out of Paradise, for you are truly cursed.

38:78.text: And surely upon you is My condemnation until the Day of Judgment.‚ÄĚ

38:79.text: Satan appealed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Then delay my end until the Day of their resurrection.‚ÄĚ

38:82.text: Satan said, ‚ÄúBy Your Glory! I will certainly mislead them all,

38:83.text: except Your chosen servants among them.‚ÄĚ

38:84.text: Allah concluded, ‚ÄúThe truth is‚ÄĒand I ňĻonlyňļ say the truth‚ÄĒ:

38:85.text: I will surely fill up Hell with you and whoever follows you from among them, all together.‚ÄĚ

38:86.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻQuranňļ, nor do I pretend to be someone I am not.

38:87.text: It is only a reminder to the whole world.

38:88.text: And you will certainly know its truth before long.‚ÄĚ

39:1.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

39:2.text: Indeed, We have sent down the Book to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in truth, so worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ, being sincerely devoted to Him.

39:3.text: Indeed, sincere devotion is due ňĻonlyňļ to Allah. As for those who take other lords besides Him, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúWe worship them only so they may bring us closer to Allah,‚ÄĚ surely Allah will judge between all<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†regarding what they differed about. Allah certainly does not guide whoever persists in lying and disbelief.
footnotes: ¬†Those who are devoted to Allah alone and those who associate other gods with Him.

39:4.text: Had it been Allah‚Äôs Will to have offspring, He could have chosen whatever He willed of His creation. Glory be to Him! He is Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme.

39:5.text: He created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. He wraps the night around the day, and wraps the day around the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term. He is truly the Almighty, Most Forgiving.

39:6.text: He created you ňĻallňļ from a single soul,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then from it He made its mate.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†And He produced for you four pairs of cattle.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†He creates you in the wombs of your mothers ňĻin stagesňļ, one development after another, in three layers of darkness.<sup foot_note=4>4</sup>¬†That is Allah‚ÄĒyour Lord! All authority belongs to Him. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. How can you then be turned away?
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Adam (Ô∑ļ).
¬†i.e., Eve.
¬†The four pairs (males and females), as listed in 6:143-144, are: a pair of sheep, a pair of goats, a pair of camels, and a pair of oxen.
¬†The three layers of darkness are: the belly, the womb, and the amniotic sac.

39:7.text: If you disbelieve, then ňĻknow thatňļ Allah is truly not in need of you, nor does He approve of disbelief from His servants. But if you become grateful ňĻthrough faithňļ, He will appreciate that from you. No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of what you used to do. He certainly knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

39:8.text: When one is touched with hardship, they cry out to their Lord, turning to Him ňĻaloneňļ. But as soon as He showers them with blessings from Him, they ňĻtotallyňļ forget the One they had cried to earlier, and set up equals to Allah to mislead ňĻothersňļ from His Way. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEnjoy your disbelief for a little while! You will certainly be one of the inmates of the Fire.‚ÄĚ

39:9.text: ňĻAre they betterňļ or those who worship ňĻtheir Lordňļ devoutly in the hours of the night, prostrating and standing, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of their Lord? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAre those who know equal to those who do not know?‚ÄĚ None will be mindful ňĻof thisňļ except people of reason.

39:10.text: Say ňĻO Prophet, that Allah saysňļ, ‚ÄúO My servants who believe! Be mindful of your Lord. Those who do good in this world will have a good reward. And Allah‚Äôs earth is spacious. Only those who endure patiently will be given their reward without limit.‚ÄĚ

39:11.text: Say, ‚ÄúI am commanded to worship Allah, being sincerely devoted to Him ňĻaloneňļ.

39:12.text: And I am commanded to be the first of those who submit ňĻto His Willňļ.‚ÄĚ

39:13.text: Say, ‚ÄúI truly fear‚ÄĒif I were to disobey my Lord‚ÄĒthe torment of a tremendous Day.‚ÄĚ

39:14.text: Say, ‚ÄúIt is ňĻonlyňļ Allah that I worship, being sincere in my devotion to Him.

39:15.text: Worship then whatever ňĻgodsňļ you want instead of Him.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúThe ňĻtrueňļ losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on Judgment Day. That is indeed the clearest loss.‚ÄĚ

39:16.text: They will have layers of fire above and below them. That is what Allah warns His servants with. So fear Me, O ¬†My servants!

39:17.text: And those who shun the worship of false gods, turning to Allah ňĻaloneňļ, will have good news. So give good news to My servants ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒ

39:18.text: those who listen to what is said and follow the best of it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†These are the ones ňĻrightlyňļ guided by Allah, and these are ňĻtrulyňļ the people of reason.
footnotes: ¬†This verse can apply to anything that a person listens to, and it can also apply specifically to the Quran‚ÄĒmeaning, for example, those who read verses about retaliation and other verses about forgiveness, then they opt for forgiveness.

39:19.text: What about those against whom the decree of torment has been justified? Is it you ňĻO Prophetňļ who will then save those bound for the Fire?

39:20.text: But those mindful of their Lord will have ňĻelevatedňļ mansions, built one above the other, under which rivers flow. ňĻThat isňļ the promise of Allah. ňĻAndňļ Allah never fails in ňĻHisňļ promise.

39:21.text: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky‚ÄĒchannelling it through streams in the earth‚ÄĒthen produces with it crops of various colours, then they dry up and you see them wither, and then He reduces them to chaff? Surely in this is a reminder for people of reason.

39:22.text: Can ňĻthe misguided be likeňļ those whose hearts Allah has opened to Islam, so they are enlightened by their Lord? So woe to those whose hearts are hardened at the remembrance of Allah! It is they who are clearly astray.

39:23.text: ňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ has sent down the best message‚ÄĒa Book of perfect consistency and repeated lessons‚ÄĒwhich causes the skin ňĻand heartsňļ of those who fear their Lord to tremble, then their skin and hearts soften at the mention of ňĻthe mercy ofňļ Allah. That is the guidance of Allah, through which He guides whoever He wills. But whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.

39:24.text: Are those who will only have their ňĻbareňļ faces to shield themselves from the awful torment on Judgment Day ňĻbetter than those in Paradiseňļ? It will ňĻthenňļ be said to the wrongdoers: ‚ÄúReap what you sowed!‚ÄĚ

39:25.text: Those before them ňĻalsoňļ rejected ňĻthe truthňļ, then the torment came upon them from where they least expected.

39:26.text: So Allah made them taste humiliation in this worldly life, but far worse is the punishment of the Hereafter, if only they knew.

39:27.text: We have certainly set forth every ňĻkind ofňļ lesson for people in this Quran, so perhaps they will be mindful.

39:28.text: ňĻIt isňļ a Quran ňĻrevealedňļ in Arabic without any crookedness, so perhaps they will be conscious ňĻof Allahňļ.

39:29.text: Allah sets forth the parable of a slave owned by several quarrelsome masters, and a slave owned by only one master. Are they equal in condition?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Praise be to Allah! In fact, most of them do not know.
footnotes: ¬†The slave owned by several quarrelsome masters will always be confused because their masters will always give them conflicting orders‚ÄĒsimilarly, one who worships multiple gods will never find peace between them. This parable is a logical argument against the existence of multiple gods, since each god would try to covet what it created. See¬†23:91 for a similar argument.

39:30.text: You ňĻO Prophetňļ will certainly die, and they will die too.

39:31.text: Then on the Day of Judgment you will ňĻall settle yourňļ dispute before your Lord.

39:32.text: Who then does more wrong than those who lie about Allah and reject the truth after it has reached them? Is Hell not a ňĻfittingňļ home for the disbelievers?

39:33.text: And the one who has brought the truth and those who embrace it‚ÄĒit is they who are the righteous.

39:34.text: They will have whatever they desire with their Lord. That is the reward of the good-doers.

39:35.text: As such, Allah will absolve them of ňĻevenňļ the worst of what they did and reward them according to the best of what they used to do.

39:36.text: Is Allah not sufficient for His servant? Yet they threaten you with other ňĻpowerlessňļ gods besides Him! Whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.

39:37.text: And whoever Allah guides, none can lead astray. Is Allah not Almighty, capable of punishment?

39:38.text: If you ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ who created the heavens and the earth, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ Ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúConsider then whatever ňĻidolsňļ you invoke besides Allah: if it was Allah‚Äôs Will to harm me, could they undo that harm? Or if He willed ňĻsomeňļ mercy for me, could they withhold His mercy?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAllah is sufficient for me. In Him ňĻaloneňļ the faithful put their trust.‚ÄĚ

39:39.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO my people! Persist in your ways, for I ňĻtooňļ will persist in mine. You will soon come to know

39:40.text: who will be visited by a humiliating torment ňĻin this lifeňļ and overwhelmed by an everlasting punishment ňĻin the nextňļ.‚ÄĚ

39:41.text: Surely We have revealed to you the Book ňĻO Prophetňļ with the truth for humanity. So whoever chooses to be guided, it is for their own good. And whoever chooses to stray, it is only to their own loss. You are not a keeper over them.

39:42.text: ňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ calls back the souls ňĻof peopleňļ upon their death as well as ňĻthe soulsňļ of the living during their sleep. Then He keeps those for whom He has ordained death, and releases the others until ňĻtheirňļ appointed time. Surely in this are signs for people who reflect.

39:43.text: Or have they taken others besides Allah as intercessors? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻWould they do so,ňļ even though those ňĻidolsňļ have neither authority nor intelligence?‚ÄĚ

39:44.text: Say, ‚ÄúAll intercession belongs to Allah ňĻaloneňļ. To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. Then to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.‚ÄĚ

39:45.text: Yet when Allah alone is mentioned, the hearts of those who disbelieve in the Hereafter are filled with disgust. But as soon as those ňĻgodsňļ other than Him are mentioned, they are filled with joy.

39:46.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO Allah‚ÄĒOriginator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the seen and unseen! You will judge between Your servants regarding their differences.‚ÄĚ

39:47.text: Even if the wrongdoers were to possess everything in the world twice over, they would certainly offer it to ransom themselves from the horrible punishment on Judgment Day, for they will see from Allah what they had never expected.

39:48.text: And the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds will unfold before them, and they will be overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.

39:49.text: When one is touched with hardship, they cry out to Us ňĻaloneňļ. Then when We shower Our blessings upon them, they say, ‚ÄúI have been granted all this only because of ňĻmyňļ knowledge.‚ÄĚ Not at all! It is ňĻno more thanňļ a test. But most of them do not know.

39:50.text: The same had already been said by those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but their ňĻworldlyňļ gains were of no benefit to them.
footnotes: ¬†Like Korah (see¬†28:76-81).

39:51.text: So the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds overtook them. And the wrongdoers among these ňĻpagansňļ will be overtaken by the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds. And they will have no escape.

39:52.text: Do they not know that Allah gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills? Surely in this are signs for people who believe.

39:53.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet, that Allah says,ňļ ‚ÄúO My servants who have exceeded the limits against their souls! Do not lose hope in Allah‚Äôs mercy, for Allah certainly forgives all sins.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He is indeed the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†No matter how big someone‚Äôs sins are, they cannot be bigger than Allah‚Äôs mercy. Based on 4:48, the only unforgivable sin in Islam is if someone dies while disbelieving in Allah or associating others with Him in worship. In an authentic narration collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) reports that Almighty Allah says, "O children of Adam! As long as you call upon Me, putting your hope in Me, I will forgive you for what you have done, and I will not mind. O ¬†children of Adam! If your sins were to reach the clouds of the sky and then you sought My forgiveness, I would ňĻstillňļ forgive you. O ¬†children of Adam! If you were to come to Me with sins filling the whole world and then you came to Me without associating other gods with Me, I would certainly match your sins with forgiveness."

39:54.text: Turn to your Lord ňĻin repentanceňļ, and ňĻfullyňļ submit to Him before the punishment reaches you, ňĻforňļ then you will not be helped.

39:55.text: Follow ňĻthe Quran,ňļ the best of what has been revealed to you from your Lord, before the punishment takes you by surprise while you are unaware,

39:56.text: so that no ňĻsinfulňļ soul will say ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ, ‚ÄėWoe to me for neglecting ňĻmy duties towardsňļ Allah, while ridiculing ňĻthe truthňļ.‚Äô

39:57.text: Or ňĻa soul willňļ say, ‚ÄėIf only Allah had guided me, I would have certainly been one of the righteous.‚Äô

39:58.text: Or say, upon seeing the torment, ‚ÄėIf only I had a second chance, I would have been one of the good-doers.‚Äô

39:59.text: Not at all! My revelations had already come to you, but you rejected them, acted arrogantly, and were one of the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ

39:60.text: On the Day of Judgment you will see those who lied about Allah with their faces gloomy. Is Hell not a ňĻfittingňļ home for the arrogant?

39:61.text: And Allah will deliver those who were mindful ňĻof Himňļ to their place of ňĻultimateňļ triumph. No evil will touch them, nor will they grieve.

39:62.text: Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is the Maintainer of everything.

39:63.text: To Him belong the keys ňĻof the treasuriesňļ¬†of the heavens and the earth. As for those who rejected the signs of Allah, it is they who will be the ňĻtrueňļ losers.

39:64.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAre you urging me to worship ňĻanyoneňļ other than Allah, O ¬†ignorant ones?‚ÄĚ

39:65.text: It has already been revealed to you‚ÄĒand to those ňĻprophetsňļ before you‚ÄĒthat if you associate others ňĻwith Allahňļ, your deeds will certainly be void and you will truly be one of the losers.

39:66.text: Rather, worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ and be one of the grateful.

39:67.text: They have not shown Allah His proper reverence‚ÄĒwhen on the Day of Judgment the ňĻwholeňļ earth will be in His Grip, and the heavens will be rolled up in His Right Hand. Glorified and Exalted is He above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!

39:68.text: The Trumpet will be blown and all those in the heavens and all those on the earth will fall dead, except those Allah wills ňĻto spareňļ. Then it will be blown again and they will rise up at once, looking on ňĻin anticipationňļ.

39:69.text: The earth<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†will shine with the light of its Lord, the record ňĻof deedsňļ will be laid ňĻopenňļ, the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forward‚ÄĒand judgment will be passed on all with fairness. None will be wronged.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the place where the judgment will be held. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by AŠĻ≠-ŠĻ¨abar√Ęni to have said, ‚ÄúThe Judgment will be passed on an earth where neither blood has been shed nor any sin committed.‚ÄĚ

39:70.text: Every soul will be paid in full for its deeds, for Allah knows best what they have done.

39:71.text: Those who disbelieved will be driven to Hell in ňĻsuccessiveňļ groups. When they arrive there, its gates will be opened and its keepers will ask them: ‚ÄúDid messengers not come to you from among yourselves, reciting to you the revelations of your Lord and warning you of the coming of this Day of yours?‚ÄĚ The disbelievers will cry, ‚ÄúYes ňĻindeedňļ! But the decree of torment has come to pass against the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ

39:72.text: It will be said to them, ‚ÄúEnter the gates of Hell, to stay there forever.‚ÄĚ What an evil home for the arrogant!

39:73.text: And those who were mindful of their Lord will be led to Paradise in ňĻsuccessiveňļ groups. When they arrive at its ňĻalreadyňļ open gates, its keepers will say, ‚ÄúPeace be upon you! You have done well, so come in, to stay forever.‚ÄĚ

39:74.text: The righteous will say, ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah Who has fulfilled His promise to us, and made us inherit the ňĻeverlastingňļ land<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to settle in Paradise wherever we please.‚ÄĚ How excellent is the reward of those who work ňĻrighteousnessňļ!
footnotes: ¬†This is emphasized in 21:105. The meaning is that Allah will award Paradise to the believers to be its residents forever. Some commentators say that the word ‚Äúinherit‚ÄĚ is used because of the understanding that when Allah created Paradise and Hell, He made places for all of humanity in both. Those who will go to Paradise will inherit the spots of the disbelievers who chose misguidance (which leads to Hell), and those who will go to Hell will inherit the spots of those who chose guidance (which leads to Paradise).

39:75.text: You will see the angels all around the Throne, glorifying the praises of their Lord, for judgment will have been passed on all with fairness. And it will be said,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúPraise be to Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The believers will praise Him for His grace, and the disbelievers will praise Him for His justice.

4:1.text: O humanity! Be mindful of your Lord Who created you from a single soul, and from it He created its mate,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and through both He spread countless men and women. And be mindful of Allah‚ÄĒin Whose Name you appeal to one another‚ÄĒand ňĻhonourňļ family ties. Surely Allah is ever Watchful over you.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Adam and Eve.

4:2.text: Give orphans their wealth ňĻwhen they reach maturityňļ, and do not exchange your worthless possessions for their valuables, nor cheat them by mixing their wealth with your own. For this would indeed be a great sin.

4:3.text: If you fear you might fail to give orphan women their ňĻdueňļ rights ňĻif you were to marry themňļ, then marry other women of your choice‚ÄĒtwo, three, or four. But if you are afraid you will fail to maintain justice, then ňĻcontent yourselves withňļ one<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†or those ňĻbondwomenňļ in your possession.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†This way you are less likely to commit injustice.
footnotes: ¬†The Quran is the only scripture that says marry only one. Unlike any previous faith, Islam puts a limit on the number of wives a man can have. Under certain circumstances, a Muslim man may marry up to four wives as long as he is able to provide for them and maintain justice among them‚ÄĒotherwise it is unlawful. With the exception of Jesus Christ and John the Baptist (neither of whom were married), almost all religious figures in the Bible had more than one wife. According to the Bible, Solomon (Ô∑ļ) had 700 wives and 300 concubines (1 Kings 11:3) and his father, David (Ô∑ļ), had many wives and concubines (2 Samuel 5:13).

¬†A bondwoman is a female slave that a man owned either through purchase or taking her captive in war‚ÄĒa common ancient practice in many parts of the world. Islam opened the door for ending slavery by making it an act of charity to free slaves. Many sins (such as breaking one‚Äôs oath, unintentional killing, and intercourse with one‚Äôs wife during the day of fasting in RamaŠłć√Ęn) can be atoned by freeing a slave. According to Islamic teachings, no free person can be enslaved. Islam also improved the condition of slaves. It was unlawful to separate a mother from her child. Children born to a slave-master were deemed free, and their mother would gain her freedom upon the death of her master. With regards to slaves, Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) says, ‚ÄúFeed them from what you eat, clothe them from what you wear, and do not overwhelm them with work unless you assist them.‚ÄĚ He (Ô∑ļ) also says, ‚ÄúWhoever kills his slave will be killed and whoever injures his slave will be injured.‚ÄĚ In recent times, slavery has been outlawed in all countries‚ÄĒincluding the Muslim world.

4:4.text: Give women ňĻyou wedňļ their due dowries graciously. But if they waive some of it willingly, then you may enjoy it freely with a clear conscience.

4:5.text: Do not entrust the incapable ňĻamong your dependantsňļ with your wealth which Allah has made a means of support for you‚ÄĒbut feed and clothe them from it, and speak to them kindly.

4:6.text: Test ňĻthe competence ofňļ the orphans until they reach a marriageable age. Then if you feel they are capable of sound judgment, return their wealth to them. And do not consume it wastefully and hastily before they grow up ňĻto demand itňļ. If the guardian is well-off, they should not take compensation; but if the guardian is poor, let them take a reasonable provision. When you give orphans back their property, call in witnesses. And sufficient is Allah as a ňĻvigilantňļ Reckoner.

4:7.text: For men there is a share in what their parents and close relatives leave, and for women there is a share in what their parents and close relatives leave‚ÄĒwhether it is little or much. ňĻThese areňļ obligatory shares.

4:8.text: If ňĻnon-inheritingňļ relatives, orphans,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†or the needy are present at the time of distribution, offer them a ňĻsmallňļ provision from it and speak to them kindly.
footnotes: ¬†For example, in the case of orphaned children whose grandfather then dies, leaving behind other children, some scholars rule that an obligatory bequest should be made to them despite not having a share in inheritance.

4:9.text: Let the guardians be as concerned ňĻfor the orphansňļ as they would if they were to ňĻdie andňļ leave ňĻtheir ownňļ helpless children behind. So let them be mindful of Allah and speak equitably.

4:10.text: Indeed, those who unjustly consume orphans‚Äô wealth ňĻin factňļ consume nothing but fire into their bellies. And they will be burned in a blazing Hell!

4:11.text: Allah commands you regarding your children: the share of the male will be twice that of the female.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†If you leave only two ňĻor moreňļ females, their share is two-thirds of the estate. But if there is only one female, her share will be one-half. Each parent is entitled to one-sixth if you leave offspring.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†But if you are childless and your parents are the only heirs, then your mother will receive one-third.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†But if you leave siblings, then your mother will receive one-sixth<sup foot_note=4>4</sup>‚ÄĒafter the fulfilment of bequests and debts.<sup foot_note=5>5</sup>¬†ňĻBe fair toňļ your parents and children, as you do not ňĻfullyňļ know who is more beneficial to you.<sup foot_note=6>6</sup>¬†ňĻThis isňļ an obligation from Allah. Surely Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
footnotes:
¬†According to Islamic law of inheritance, a female‚ÄĒwhether she is a mother, a wife, a sister, a daughter, etc.‚ÄĒgets one of three shares, depending on her closeness to the deceased:

  1. Less than a male‚Äôs share. If she is a daughter, she will get half of the share of her brother, since he‚ÄĒunlike his sister‚ÄĒhas to provide for the family and pay a dowry at the time of his marriage.
  2. More than a male‚Äôs share. For example, if a man leaves $24 000 and six sons, two brothers, a wife, and a mother. The wife will get one-eighth ($3000), the mother one-sixth ($4000), each of the sons will receive about $2 833 and the two brothers will not get anything.
  3. An equal share. This is in the case of siblings from the mother‚Äôs side (mentioned in verse 12 of this s√Ľrah).
¬†"Offspring‚ÄĚ here means any number of children‚ÄĒmale or female.
¬†And the father will take the rest of the estate.
¬†Although the existence of siblings reduces the mother‚Äôs share from one-third to one-sixth, siblings themselves do not receive a share of inheritance and the rest of the estate goes to the father.

¬†Debts as well as any other financial obligations (such as funeral expenses, unfulfilled vows, or unpaid dowry) have to be repaid before the fulfilment of bequests, then the estate can be distributed among the heirs. A person can bequest up to one-third of their estate for charities as well as individuals who are not entitled to a share in inheritance.
¬†You do not know who will benefit you by looking after you during your life or doing good deeds on your behalf after your death.

4:12.text: You will inherit half of what your wives leave if they are childless. But if they have children, then ňĻyour share isňļ one-fourth of the estate‚ÄĒafter the fulfilment of bequests and debts. And your wives will inherit one-fourth of what you leave if you are childless. But if you have children, then your wives will receive one-eighth of your estate‚ÄĒafter the fulfilment of bequests and debts. And if a man or a woman leaves neither parents nor children but only a brother or a sister ňĻfrom their mother‚Äôs sideňļ, they will each inherit one-sixth, but if they are more than one, they ňĻallňļ will share one-third of the estate<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒafter the fulfilment of bequests and debts without harm ňĻto the heirsňļ.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†ňĻThis isňļ a commandment from Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.
footnotes: ¬†If there are two or more brothers and sisters from the mother‚Äôs side, they share one-third equally‚ÄĒthe female will receive the same share of the male.
¬†Harming the heirs includes giving away more than one-third of the estate as a bequest.

4:13.text: These ňĻentitlementsňļ are the limits set by Allah. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger will be admitted into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever. That is the ultimate triumph!

4:14.text: But whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger and exceeds their limits will be cast into Hell, to stay there forever. And they will suffer a humiliating punishment.

4:15.text: ňĻAs forňļ those of your women who commit illegal intercourse‚ÄĒcall four witnesses from among yourselves. If they testify, confine the offenders to their homes until they die or Allah ordains a ňĻdifferentňļ way for them.

4:16.text: And the two among you who commit this sin‚ÄĒdiscipline them. If they repent and mend their ways, relieve them. Surely Allah is ever Accepting of Repentance, Most Merciful.

4:17.text: Allah only accepts the repentance of those who commit evil ignorantly ňĻor recklesslyňļ then repent soon after<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒAllah will pardon them. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†A person‚Äôs repentance will be accepted as long as they repent any time before their death. But one should not procrastinate because they do not know when they will die.

4:18.text: However, repentance is not accepted from those who knowingly persist in sin until they start dying, and then cry, ‚ÄúNow I repent!‚ÄĚ nor those who die as disbelievers. For them We have prepared a painful punishment.

4:19.text: O believers! It is not permissible for you to inherit women against their will<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†or mistreat them to make them return some of the dowry ňĻas a ransom for divorceňļ‚ÄĒunless they are found guilty of adultery.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Treat them fairly. If you happen to dislike them, you may hate something which Allah turns into a great blessing.
footnotes: ¬†For example, a man would prevent a female relative (such as his sister or mother) from getting married so he can secure her estate for himself.
¬†lit., blatant misconduct. If someone‚Äôs wife has been found guilty of adultery, he has the right to ask for his dowry back.

4:20.text: If you desire to replace a wife with another and you have given the former ňĻevenňļ a stack of gold ňĻas a dowryňļ, do not take any of it back. Would you ňĻstillňļ take it unjustly and very sinfully?

4:21.text: And how could you take it back after having enjoyed each other intimately and she has taken from you a firm commitment?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the promise to live with her in kindness or divorce her with dignity.

4:22.text: Do not marry former wives of your fathers‚ÄĒexcept what was done previously. It was indeed a shameful, despicable, and evil practice.

4:23.text: ňĻAlsoňļ forbidden to you for marriage are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal and maternal aunts, your brother‚Äôs daughters, your sister‚Äôs daughters, your foster-mothers, your foster-sisters, your mothers-in-law, your stepdaughters under your guardianship if you have consummated marriage with their mothers‚ÄĒbut if you have not, then you can marry them‚ÄĒnor the wives of your own sons, nor two sisters together at the same time‚ÄĒexcept what was done previously. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

4:24.text: Also ňĻforbidden areňļ married women‚ÄĒexcept ňĻfemaleňļ captives in your possession.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is Allah‚Äôs commandment to you. Lawful to you are all beyond these‚ÄĒas long as you seek them with your wealth in a legal marriage, not in fornication. Give those you have consummated marriage with their due dowries. It is permissible to be mutually gracious regarding the set dowry. Surely Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†A man was not allowed to have a relationship with a bondwoman who he had taken captive in war unless he made sure she was not pregnant. This was verified by her having at least one monthly cycle. See footnote for 4:3.

4:25.text: But if any of you cannot afford to marry a free believing woman, then ňĻlet him marryňļ a believing bondwoman possessed by one of you. Allah knows best ňĻthe state ofňļ your faith ňĻand theirsňļ. You are from one another.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So marry them with the permission of their owners,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†giving them their dowry in fairness, if they are chaste, neither promiscuous nor having secret affairs. If they commit indecency after marriage, they receive half the punishment of free women.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†This is for those of you who fear falling into sin. But if you are patient, it is better for you. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†Do not be ashamed to marry a bondwoman since you are all part of the same human family and Allah knows you are doing so to avoid illegal relationships.
¬†A married slave-woman is forbidden from having a relationship with her master.
¬†i.e., fifty lashes.

4:26.text: It is Allah‚Äôs Will to make things clear to you, guide you to the ňĻnobleňļ ways of those before you, and turn to you in mercy. For Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

4:27.text: And it is Allah‚Äôs Will to turn to you in grace, but those who follow their desires wish to see you deviate entirely ňĻfrom Allah‚Äôs Wayňļ.

4:28.text: And it is Allah‚Äôs Will to lighten your burdens, for humankind was created weak.
