4:29.text: O believers! Do not devour one another‚Äôs wealth illegally, but rather trade by mutual consent. And do not kill ňĻeach other orňļ yourselves. Surely Allah is ever Merciful to you.

4:30.text: And whoever does this sinfully and unjustly, We will burn them in the Fire. That is easy for Allah.

4:31.text: If you avoid the major sins forbidden to you, We will absolve you of your ňĻlesserňļ misdeeds and admit you into a place of honour.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Paradise.

4:32.text: And do not crave what Allah has given some of you over others. Men will be rewarded according to their deeds and women ňĻequallyňļ according to theirs. Rather, ask Allah for His bounties. Surely Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

4:33.text: And We have appointed heirs to what has been left by parents and next of kin. As for those you have made a pledge to, give them their share.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah is a Witness over all things.
footnotes: ¬†This pledge was a common practice before Islam between friends and allies‚ÄĒtaking oaths to inherit each other. This practice came to an end with the revelation of verse 8:75 of the Quran. Although friends and allies do not have a share in inheritance anymore, they still can get some of the estate through bequest.

4:34.text: Men are the caretakers of women, as men have been provisioned by Allah over women and tasked with supporting them financially. And righteous women are devoutly obedient and, when alone, protective of what Allah has entrusted them with.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And if you sense ill-conduct from your women, advise them ňĻfirstňļ, ňĻif they persist,ňļ do not share their beds, ňĻbut if they still persist,ňļ then discipline them ňĻgentlyňļ.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†But if they change their ways, do not be unjust to them. Surely Allah is Most High, All-Great.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., their husbands‚Äô honour and wealth.

¬†Disciplining one‚Äôs wife gently is the final resort. The earliest commentators understood that this was to be light enough not to leave a mark, should be done with nothing bigger than a tooth stick, and should not be on the face. Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) said to his companions ‚ÄúDo not beat the female servants of Allah.‚ÄĚ He said that honourable husbands do not beat their wives, and he himself never hit a woman or a servant. If a woman feels her husband is ill-behaved, then she can get help from her guardian or seek divorce.

4:35.text: If you anticipate a split between them, appoint a mediator from his family and another from hers. If they desire reconciliation, Allah will restore harmony between them. Surely Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.

4:36.text: Worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ and associate none with Him. And be kind to parents, relatives, orphans, the poor, near and distant neighbours, close friends, ňĻneedyňļ travellers, and those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in your possession. Surely Allah does not like whoever is arrogant, boastful‚ÄĒ

4:37.text: those who are stingy, promote stinginess among people, and withhold Allah‚Äôs bounties. We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment.

4:38.text: Likewise for those who spend their wealth to show off and do not believe in Allah or the Last Day. And whoever takes Satan as an associate‚ÄĒwhat an evil associate they have!

4:39.text: What harm could have come to them if they had believed in Allah and the Last Day and donated from what Allah has provided for them? And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of them.

4:40.text: Indeed, Allah never wrongs ňĻanyoneňļ‚ÄĒeven by an atom‚Äôs weight.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And if it is a good deed, He will multiply it many times over and will give a great reward out of His grace.
footnotes: ¬†lit., the smallest particle of dust (‚Ī¨arrah).

4:41.text: So how will it be when We bring a witness from every faith-community and bring you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a witness against yours?

4:42.text: On that Day, those who denied ňĻAllahňļ and disobeyed the Messenger will wish they were reduced to dust. And they will never be able to hide anything from Allah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes:
¬†Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) asked one of his companions, ‚ÄôAbdullah ibn Mas‚Äô√Ľd, to recite some verses of the Quran for him because he loved to hear it from someone other than himself. ‚ÄôAbdullah recited from the beginning of this s√Ľrah¬†until he reached verse

¬†42. When he was told he may stop, ‚ÄôAbdullah said he looked at the face of Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and saw tears rolling down his cheeks.

4:43.text: O believers! Do not approach prayer while intoxicated<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†until you are aware of what you say, nor in a state of ňĻfullňļ impurity<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>‚ÄĒunless you merely pass through ňĻthe mosqueňļ‚ÄĒuntil you have bathed. But if you are ill, on a journey, or have relieved yourselves, or been intimate with your wives and cannot find water, then purify yourselves with clean earth, wiping your faces and hands.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†And Allah is Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving.
footnotes: ¬†Intoxicants were prohibited in the Quran in three stages: 2:219, 4:43, and finally 5:90-91.
¬†i.e., after sexual intercourse or a wet dream.

¬†This ruling is called tayammum¬†or ‚Äėdry ablution.‚Äô If someone cannot find water or is unable to use it because of illness or cold weather, then they are allowed to touch clean earth, sand, etc. with the palms of their hands once then blow in their hands and wipe over their face and hands.

4:44.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ not seen those who were given a portion of the Scriptures yet trade it for misguidance and wish to see you deviate from the ňĻRightňļ Path?

4:45.text: Allah knows best who your enemies are! And Allah is sufficient as a Guardian, and He is sufficient as a Helper.

4:46.text: Some Jews take words out of context and say, ‚ÄúWe listen and we disobey,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúHear! May you never hear,‚ÄĚ and ‚ÄúR√Ę‚Äôina!‚ÄĚ [Herd us!]‚ÄĒplaying with words and discrediting the faith. Had they said ňĻcourteouslyňļ, ‚ÄúWe hear and obey,‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúListen to us,‚ÄĚ and ‚ÄúUnŠļďurna,‚ÄĚ [Tend to us!] it would have been better for them and more proper. Allah has condemned them for their disbelief, so they do not believe except for a few.

4:47.text: O you who were given the Book! Believe in what We have revealed‚ÄĒconfirming your own Scriptures‚ÄĒbefore We wipe out ňĻyourňļ faces, turning them backwards, or We condemn the defiant as We did to the Sabbath-breakers.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah‚Äôs command is always executed!

4:48.text: Indeed, Allah does not forgive associating others with Him ňĻin worshipňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but forgives anything else of whoever He wills. And whoever associates others with Allah has indeed committed a grave sin.
footnotes: ¬†A person will never be forgiven if they die as a disbeliever. But if they repent before their death and mend their ways, then their repentance will be accepted (see¬†25:68-70).

4:49.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ not seen those who ňĻfalselyňļ elevate themselves? It is Allah who elevates whoever He wills. And none will be wronged ňĻeven by the width ofňļ the thread of a date stone.

4:50.text: See how they fabricate lies against Allah‚ÄĒthis alone is a blatant sin.

4:51.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ not seen those who were given a portion of the Scriptures yet believe in idols and false gods and reassure the disbelievers<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†that they are better guided than the believers?
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the polytheists.

4:52.text: It is they who have been condemned by Allah. And whoever is condemned by Allah will have no helper.

4:53.text: Do they have control over shares of the kingdom? If so, they would not have given anyone so much as the speck on a date stone.

4:54.text: Or do they envy the people for Allah‚Äôs bounties? Indeed, We have given the descendants of Abraham the Book and wisdom, along with great authority.

4:55.text: Yet some believed in him while others turned away from him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Hell is sufficient as a torment!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Abraham (Ô∑ļ) or MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

4:56.text: Surely those who reject Our signs, We will cast them into the Fire. Whenever their skin is burnt completely, We will replace it so they will ňĻconstantlyňļ taste the punishment. Indeed, Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

4:57.text: As for those who believe and do good, We will admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. There they will have pure spouses,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and We will place them under a vast shade.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:25.

4:58.text: Indeed, Allah commands you to return trusts to their rightful owners;<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and when you judge between people, judge with fairness. What a noble commandment from Allah to you! Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
footnotes: ¬†This verse was revealed right after Mecca had peacefully surrendered to the Muslims. ‚ÄôAli ibn Abi ŠĻ¨√Ęlib, the Prophet‚Äôs cousin and son-in-law, took the Ka‚Äôbah‚Äôs key by force from Othm√Ęn ibn ŠĻ¨alŠł•a, the non-Muslim keeper of the Ka‚Äôbah. The verse was revealed to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) inside the Ka‚Äôbah with the order to give the key back to its rightful owner. He (Ô∑ļ) ordered Ali to return the key to the keeper immediately along with an apology. Othm√Ęn was also promised that his family would keep the key forever. The keeper was so touched by the Quranic sense of justice that he decided to accept Islam.

4:59.text: O believers! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger and those in authority among you. Should you disagree on anything, then refer it to Allah and His Messenger, if you ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and the Last Day. This is the best and fairest resolution.

4:60.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ not seen those who claim they believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed before you? They seek the judgment of false judges, which they were commanded to reject. And Satan ňĻonlyňļ desires to lead them farther away.

4:61.text: When it is said to them, ‚ÄúCome to Allah‚Äôs revelations and to the Messenger,‚ÄĚ you see the hypocrites turn away from you stubbornly.

4:62.text: How ňĻhorribleňļ will it be if a disaster strikes them because of what their hands have done, then they come to you swearing by Allah, ‚ÄúWe intended nothing but goodwill and reconciliation.‚ÄĚ

4:63.text: ňĻOnlyňļ Allah knows what is in their hearts. So turn away from them, caution them, and give them advice that will shake their very souls.

4:64.text: We only sent messengers to be obeyed by Allah‚Äôs Will. If only those ňĻhypocritesňļ came to you ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒafter wronging themselves‚ÄĒseeking Allah‚Äôs forgiveness and the Messenger prayed for their forgiveness, they would have certainly found Allah ever Accepting of Repentance, Most Merciful.

4:65.text: But no! By your Lord, they will never be ňĻtrueňļ believers until they accept you ňĻO Prophetňļ as the judge in their disputes, and find no resistance within themselves against your decision and submit wholeheartedly.

4:66.text: If We had commanded them to sacrifice themselves or abandon their homes, none would have obeyed except for a few. Had they done what they were advised to do,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†it would have certainly been far better for them and more reassuring,
footnotes: ¬†i.e., obeying Allah and His Messenger.

4:67.text: and We would have granted them a great reward by Our grace

4:68.text: and guided them to the Straight Path.

4:69.text: And whoever obeys Allah and the Messenger will be in the company of those blessed by Allah: the prophets, the people of truth, the martyrs, and the righteous‚ÄĒwhat honourable company!

4:70.text: This is Allah‚Äôs favour, and Allah fully knows ňĻwho deserves itňļ.

4:71.text: O believers! Take your precautions and go forth either in groups or together.

4:72.text: There will be some among you who will lag behind so that if you face a disaster, they will say, ‚ÄúAllah has blessed us for not being there among them.‚ÄĚ

4:73.text: But if you return with Allah‚Äôs bounties, they will say‚ÄĒas if there had been no bond between you‚ÄĒ‚ÄúWe wish we had been there with them to share the great gain!‚ÄĚ

4:74.text: Let those who would sacrifice this life for the Hereafter fight in the cause of Allah. And whoever fights in Allah‚Äôs cause‚ÄĒwhether they achieve martyrdom or victory‚ÄĒWe will honour them with a great reward.

4:75.text: And what is it with you? You do not fight in the cause of Allah and for oppressed men, women, and children who cry out, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Deliver us from this land of oppressors! Appoint for us a saviour; appoint for us a helper‚ÄĒall by Your grace.‚ÄĚ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†

4:76.text: Believers fight for the cause of Allah, whereas disbelievers fight for the cause of the Devil. So fight against Satan‚Äôs ňĻevilňļ forces. Indeed, Satan‚Äôs schemes are ever weak.

4:77.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ not seen those who had been told, ‚ÄúDo not fight! Rather, establish prayer and pay alms-tax.‚ÄĚ? Then once the order came to fight, a group of them feared those ňĻhostileňļ people as Allah should be feared‚ÄĒor even more. They said, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Why have You ordered us to fight? If only You had delayed ňĻthe order forňļ us for a little while!‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThe enjoyment of this world is so little, whereas the Hereafter is far better for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ. And none of you will be wronged ňĻeven by the width ofňļ the thread of a date stone.

4:78.text: Wherever you may be, death will overcome you‚ÄĒeven if you were in fortified towers.‚ÄĚ When something good befalls them, they say, ‚ÄúThis is from Allah,‚ÄĚ but when something evil befalls them, they say, ‚ÄúThis is from you.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBoth have been destined by Allah.‚ÄĚ So what is the matter with these people? They can hardly comprehend anything!

4:79.text: Whatever good befalls you is from Allah and whatever evil befalls you is from yourself.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a messenger to ňĻallňļ people. And Allah is sufficient as a Witness.
footnotes: ¬†Both good and evil are destined by Allah. The good comes as Allah‚Äôs reward for good deeds, whereas the bad comes as Allah‚Äôs punishment for evil deeds. In some cases, bad things happen to good people to test their faith, or as an atonement for their sins, or as part of a process of replacing something with what is better (e.g., a job or a spouse).

4:80.text: Whoever obeys the Messenger has truly obeyed Allah. But whoever turns away, then ňĻknow thatňļ We have not sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a keeper over them.

4:81.text: And they say, ‚ÄúWe obey,‚ÄĚ but when they leave you, a group of them would spend the night contradicting what they said. Allah records all their schemes. So turn away from them, and put your trust in Allah. And Allah is sufficient as a Trustee of Affairs.

4:82.text: Do they not then reflect on the Quran? Had it been from anyone other than Allah, they would have certainly found in it many inconsistencies.

4:83.text: And when they hear news of security or fear,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†they publicize it. Had they referred it to the Messenger or their authorities, those with sound judgment among them would have validated it. Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy, you would have followed Satan‚ÄĒexcept for a few.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., when they get the news of victory or a threat.

4:84.text: So fight in the cause of Allah ňĻO Prophetňļ. You are accountable for none but yourself. And motivate the believers ňĻto fightňļ, so perhaps Allah will curb the disbelievers‚Äô might. And Allah is far superior in might and in punishment.

4:85.text: Whoever intercedes for a good cause will have a share in the reward, and whoever intercedes for an evil cause will have a share in the burden. And Allah is Watchful over all things.

4:86.text: And when you are greeted, respond with a better greeting or at least similarly. Surely Allah is a ňĻvigilantňļ Reckoner of all things.

4:87.text: Allah, there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. He will certainly gather ňĻall ofňļ you together on the Day of Judgment‚ÄĒabout which there is no doubt. And whose word is more truthful than Allah‚Äôs?

4:88.text: Why are you ňĻbelieversňļ divided into two groups regarding the hypocrites while Allah allowed them to regress ňĻto disbeliefňļ because of their misdeeds? Do you wish to guide those left by Allah to stray? And whoever Allah leaves to stray, you will never find for them a way.

4:89.text: They wish you would disbelieve as they have disbelieved, so you may all be alike. So do not take them as allies unless they emigrate in the cause of Allah. But if they turn away, then seize them and kill them wherever you find them, and do not take any of them as allies or helpers,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The verse discusses a group of people who nominally became Muslims and secretly supported the enemies of Muslims. For those hypocrites to prove their loyalty, they were commanded to emigrate and join the ranks of the believers, or they would be considered enemies.

4:90.text: except those who are allies of a people you are bound with in a treaty or those wholeheartedly opposed to fighting either you or their own people. If Allah had willed, He would have empowered them to fight you. So if they refrain from fighting you and offer you peace, then Allah does not permit you to harm them.

4:91.text: You will find others who wish to be safe from you and their own people. Yet they cannot resist the temptation ňĻof disbelief or hostilityňļ. If they do not keep away, offer you peace, or refrain from attacking you, then seize them and kill them wherever you find them. We have given you full permission over such people.

4:92.text: It is not lawful for a believer to kill another except by mistake. And whoever kills a believer unintentionally must free a believing slave and pay blood-money to the victim‚Äôs family‚ÄĒunless they waive it charitably. But if the victim is a believer from a hostile people, then a believing slave must be freed. And if the victim is from a people bound with you in a treaty, then blood-money must be paid to the family along with freeing a believing slave. Those who are unable, let them fast two consecutive months‚ÄĒas a means of repentance to Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

4:93.text: And whoever kills a believer intentionally, their reward will be Hell‚ÄĒwhere they will stay indefinitely.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Allah will be displeased with them, condemn them, and will prepare for them a tremendous punishment.
footnotes: ¬†If a Muslim commits a major sin (such as intentional killing or adultery) and dies without repenting, they will be punished in Hell according to the severity or magnitude of the sin, then eventually be taken out of Hell. No Muslim will stay in Hell forever.

4:94.text: O believers! When you struggle in the cause of Allah, be sure of who you fight. And do not say to those who offer you ňĻgreetings ofňļ peace, ‚ÄúYou are no believer!‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒseeking a fleeting worldly gain.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Instead, Allah has infinite bounties ňĻin storeňļ. You were initially like them then Allah blessed you ňĻwith Islamňļ. So be sure! Indeed, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

4:95.text: Those believers who stay at home‚ÄĒexcept those with valid excuses<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒare not equal to those who strive in the cause of Allah with their wealth and their lives. Allah has elevated in rank those who strive with their wealth and their lives above those who stay behind ňĻwith valid excusesňļ. Allah has promised each a fine reward, but those who strive will receive a far better reward than others‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†This includes women, the elderly, the sick, etc.

4:96.text: far superior ranks, forgiveness, and mercy from Him. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

4:97.text: When the angels seize the souls of those who have wronged themselves<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒscolding them, ‚ÄúWhat was wrong with you?‚ÄĚ they will reply, ‚ÄúWe were oppressed in the land.‚ÄĚ The angels will respond, ‚ÄúWas Allah‚Äôs earth not spacious enough for you to emigrate?‚ÄĚ It is they who will have Hell as their home‚ÄĒwhat an evil destination!
footnotes: ¬†This verse refers to some of those who had accepted Islam secretly in Mecca but refused to emigrate to Medina along with the rest of the believers. Some of them were killed in the Battle of Badr when they were rallied by the Meccans to fight against the Muslims. The verse also applies to any Muslim who accepts abuse and refuses to move to another place where they can live with dignity and practice their faith freely.

4:98.text: Except helpless men, women, and children who cannot afford a way out‚ÄĒ

4:99.text: it is right to hope that Allah will pardon them. For Allah is Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving.

4:100.text: Whoever emigrates in the cause of Allah will find many safe havens and bountiful resources throughout the earth. Those who leave their homes and die while emigrating to Allah and His Messenger‚ÄĒtheir reward has already been secured with Allah. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

4:101.text: When you travel through the land, it is permissible for you to shorten the prayer<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒňĻespeciallyňļ if you fear an attack by the disbelievers. Indeed, the disbelievers are your sworn enemies.
footnotes: ¬†Generally, it is permissible for Muslims who are travelling a distance of 85 km or more to shorten their prayers. A four-unit prayer is reduced to two.

4:102.text: When you ňĻO Prophetňļ are ňĻcampaigningňļ with them and you lead them in prayer, let one group of them pray with you‚ÄĒwhile armed. When they prostrate themselves, let the other group stand guard behind them. Then the group that has not yet prayed will then join you in prayer‚ÄĒand let them be vigilant and armed.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†The disbelievers would wish to see you neglect your weapons and belongings, so they could launch a sweeping assault on you. But there is no blame if you lay aside your weapons when overcome by heavy rain or illness‚ÄĒbut take precaution. Indeed, Allah has prepared a humiliating punishment for the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†When the first group prays, the second group stands guard behind them, then the first group withdraws to the back when they finish their prayers and stand guard when the second group moves up to pray.

4:103.text: When the prayers are over, remember Allah‚ÄĒwhether you are standing, sitting, or lying down. But when you are secure, establish regular prayers. Indeed, performing prayers is a duty on the believers at the appointed times.

4:104.text: Do not falter in pursuit of the enemy‚ÄĒif you are suffering, they too are suffering. But you can hope to receive from Allah what they can never hope for. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

4:105.text: Indeed, We have sent down the Book to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in truth to judge between people by means of what Allah has shown you. So do not be an advocate for the deceitful.

4:106.text: And seek Allah‚Äôs forgiveness‚ÄĒindeed, Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

4:107.text: Do not advocate for those who wrong themselves.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah does not like those who are deceitful, sinful.
footnotes: ¬†lit., deceive themselves.

4:108.text: They try to hide ňĻtheir deceptionňļ from people, but they can never hide it from Allah‚ÄĒin Whose presence they plot by night what is displeasing to Him. And Allah is Fully Aware of what they do.

4:109.text: Here you are! You ňĻbelieversňļ are advocating for them in this life, but who will ňĻdare toňļ advocate for them before Allah on the Day of Judgment? Or who will come to their defence?

4:110.text: Whoever commits evil or wrongs themselves then seeks Allah‚Äôs forgiveness will certainly find Allah All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

4:111.text: And whoever commits a sin‚ÄĒit is only to their own loss. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

4:112.text: And whoever commits an evil or sinful deed then blames it on an innocent person, they will definitely bear the guilt of slander and blatant sin.

4:113.text: Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy, a group of them would have sought to deceive you ňĻO Prophetňļ. Yet they would deceive none but themselves, nor can they harm you in the least. Allah has revealed to you the Book and wisdom and taught you what you never knew. Great ňĻindeedňļ is Allah‚Äôs favour upon you!

4:114.text: There is no good in most of their secret talks‚ÄĒexcept those encouraging charity, kindness, or reconciliation between people. And whoever does this seeking Allah‚Äôs pleasure, We will grant them a great reward.

4:115.text: And whoever defies the Messenger after guidance has become clear to them and follows a path other than that of the believers, We will let them pursue what they have chosen, then burn them in Hell‚ÄĒwhat an evil end!

4:116.text: Surely Allah does not forgive associating ňĻothersňļ with Him ňĻin worshipňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but forgives anything else of whoever He wills. Indeed, whoever associates ňĻothersňļ with Allah has clearly gone far astray.
footnotes: ¬†This applies to those who die in a state of disbelief in Allah. But those who repent before their death and mend their ways, their repentance will be accepted (see¬†25:68-70).

4:117.text: Instead of Allah, they only invoke female gods<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and they ňĻactuallyňļ invoke none but a rebellious Satan‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†The pagans of Arabia used to shape their idols as females and give them feminine names such as Al-L√Ęt, Al-‚ÄôUzza, and Man√Ęt.

4:118.text: cursed by Allah‚ÄĒwho said, ‚ÄúI will surely take hold of a certain number of Your servants.

4:119.text: I will certainly mislead them and delude them with empty hopes. Also, I will order them and they will slit the ears of cattle<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and alter Allah‚Äôs creation.‚ÄĚ And whoever takes Satan as a guardian instead of Allah has certainly suffered a tremendous loss.
footnotes: ¬†Slitting the ears of cattle dedicated to idols was a superstitious practice before Islam.

4:120.text: Satan only makes them ňĻfalseňļ promises and deludes them with ňĻemptyňļ hopes. Truly Satan promises them nothing but delusion.

4:121.text: It is they who will have Hell as their home, and they will find no escape from it!

4:122.text: And those who believe and do good, We will soon admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. Allah‚Äôs promise is ňĻalwaysňļ true. And whose word is more truthful than Allah‚Äôs?

4:123.text: ňĻDivine grace isňļ neither by your wishes nor those of the People of the Book! Whoever commits evil will be rewarded accordingly, and they will find no protector or helper besides Allah.

4:124.text: But those who do good‚ÄĒwhether male or female‚ÄĒand have faith will enter Paradise and will never be wronged ňĻeven as much asňļ the speck on a date stone.

4:125.text: And who is better in faith than those who ňĻfullyňļ submit themselves to Allah, do good, and follow the Way of Abraham, the upright? Allah chose Abraham as a close friend.

4:126.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is Fully Aware of everything.

4:127.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ regarding women. Say, ‚ÄúIt is Allah Who instructs you regarding them. Instruction has ňĻalreadyňļ been revealed in the Book<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†concerning the orphan women you deprive of their due rights<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†but still wish to marry, also helpless children, as well as standing up for orphans‚Äô rights. And whatever good you do is certainly well known to Allah.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to verses 2-11 of this s√Ľrah.
¬†i.e., their inheritance and dowries.

4:128.text: If a woman fears indifference or neglect from her husband, there is no blame on either of them if they seek ňĻfairňļ settlement, which is best. Humans are ever inclined to selfishness.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But if you are gracious and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, surely Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†This implies that many husbands and wives are unwilling to give the other their rights.

4:129.text: You will never be able to maintain ňĻemotionalňļ justice between your wives‚ÄĒno matter how keen you are. So do not totally incline towards one leaving the other in suspense.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And if you do what is right and are mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†Do not leave one hanging‚ÄĒnot fully enjoying the rights of a married woman and not fully divorced.

4:130.text: But if they choose to separate, Allah will enrich both of them from His bounties. And Allah is Ever-Bountiful, All-Wise.

4:131.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Indeed, We have commanded those given the Scripture before you, as well as you, to be mindful of Allah. But if you disobey, then ňĻknow thatňļ to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And Allah is Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

4:132.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is sufficient as a Trustee of Affairs.

4:133.text: If it is His Will, He can remove you altogether, O ¬†humanity, and replace you with others. And Allah is Most Capable to do so.

4:134.text: Whoever desires the reward of this world, then ňĻlet them know thatňļ with Allah are the rewards of this world and the Hereafter. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

4:135.text: O believers! Stand firm for justice as witnesses for Allah even if it is against yourselves, your parents, or close relatives. Be they rich or poor, Allah is best to ensure their interests. So do not let your desires cause you to deviate ňĻfrom justiceňļ. If you distort the testimony or refuse to give it, then ňĻknow thatňļ Allah is certainly All-Aware of what you do.

4:136.text: O believers! Have faith in Allah, His Messenger, the Book He has revealed to His Messenger, and the Scriptures He revealed before. Indeed, whoever denies Allah, His angels, His Books, His messengers, and the Last Day has clearly gone far astray.

4:137.text: Indeed, those who believed then disbelieved, then believed and again disbelieved‚ÄĒňĻonlyňļ increasing in disbelief‚ÄĒAllah will neither forgive them nor guide them to the ňĻRightňļ Way.

4:138.text: Give good news of a painful punishment to hypocrites,

4:139.text: who choose disbelievers as allies instead of the believers. Do they seek honour and power through that company? Surely all honour and power belongs to Allah.

4:140.text: He has already revealed to you in the Book that when you hear Allah‚Äôs revelations being denied or ridiculed, then do not sit in that company unless they engage in a different topic, or else you will be like them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah will gather the hypocrites and disbelievers all together in Hell.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to 6:68.

4:141.text: ňĻThe hypocrites areňļ those who wait to see what happens to you. So if Allah grants you victory, they say ňĻto youňļ, ‚ÄúWere we not on your side?‚ÄĚ But if the disbelievers have a share ňĻof victoryňļ, they say ňĻto themňļ, ‚ÄúDid we not have the advantage over you, yet we protected you from the believers?‚ÄĚ Allah will judge between ňĻall ofňļ you on the Day of Judgment. And Allah will never grant the disbelievers a way over the believers.

4:142.text: Surely the hypocrites seek to deceive Allah, but He outwits them. When they stand up for prayer, they do it half-heartedly only to be seen by people‚ÄĒhardly remembering Allah at all.

4:143.text: Torn between belief and disbelief‚ÄĒbelonging neither to these ňĻbelieversňļ nor those ňĻdisbelieversňļ. And whoever Allah leaves to stray, you will never find for them a way.

4:144.text: O believers! Do not take disbelievers as allies instead of the believers. Would you like to give Allah solid proof against yourselves?

4:145.text: Surely the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire‚ÄĒand you will never find for them any helper‚ÄĒ

4:146.text: except those who repent, mend their ways, hold fast to Allah, and are sincere in their devotion to Allah; they will be with the believers. And Allah will grant the believers a great reward.

4:147.text: Why should Allah punish you if you are grateful and faithful? Allah is ever Appreciative, All-Knowing.

4:148.text: Allah does not like negative thoughts to be voiced‚ÄĒexcept by those who have been wronged.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Allah does not like talking about people behind their backs‚ÄĒexcept when someone who has been wronged seeks counselling or assistance from authorities.

4:149.text: Whether you reveal or conceal a good or pardon an evil‚ÄĒsurely Allah is Ever-Pardoning, Most Capable.

4:150.text: Surely those who deny Allah and His messengers and wish to make a distinction between Allah and His messengers,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†saying, ‚ÄúWe believe in some and disbelieve in others,‚ÄĚ desiring to forge a compromise,
footnotes: ¬†By their claim that they believe in Allah but still reject some of His messengers.

4:151.text: they are indeed the true disbelievers. And We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment.

4:152.text: As for those who believe in Allah and His messengers‚ÄĒaccepting all; rejecting none‚ÄĒHe will surely give them their rewards. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

4:153.text: The People of the Book demand that you ňĻO Prophetňļ bring down for them a revelation in writing from heaven.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They demanded what is even greater than this from Moses, saying, ‚ÄúMake Allah visible to us!‚ÄĚ So a thunderbolt struck them for their wrongdoing. Then they took the calf for worship after receiving clear signs. Still We forgave them for that ňĻafter their repentanceňļ and gave Moses compelling proof.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they demanded the Quran to be revealed all at once in writing similar to the Tablets of Moses. This demand is refuted in 25:32.

4:154.text: We raised the Mount over them ňĻas a warningňļ for ňĻbreakingňļ their covenant and said, ‚ÄúEnter the gate ňĻof Jerusalemňļ with humility.‚ÄĚ We also warned them, ‚ÄúDo not break the Sabbath,‚ÄĚ and took from them a firm covenant.

4:155.text: ňĻThey were condemnedňļ for breaking their covenant, rejecting Allah‚Äôs signs, killing the prophets unjustly, and for saying, ‚ÄúOur hearts are unreceptive!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒit is Allah Who has sealed their hearts for their disbelief, so they do not believe except for a few‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†Their hearts are unreceptive because they claim they have enough knowledge already.

4:156.text: and for their denial and outrageous accusation against Mary,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they slandered Mary by claiming that she conceived Jesus through an illegal relationship.

4:157.text: and for boasting, ‚ÄúWe killed the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, the messenger of Allah.‚ÄĚ But they neither killed nor crucified him‚ÄĒit was only made to appear so.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Even those who argue for this ňĻcrucifixionňļ are in doubt. They have no knowledge whatsoever‚ÄĒonly making¬†assumptions. They certainly did not kill him.
footnotes: ¬†The popular belief among Muslims is that a conspiracy was made to kill Jesus, Allah made the main culprit who betrayed Jesus look exactly like Jesus, then he was crucified in Jesus‚Äô place. Jesus was raised safe and sound to the heavens. Muslims also believe in the second coming of Jesus (Ô∑ļ).

4:158.text: Rather, Allah raised him up to Himself. And Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

4:159.text: Every one of the People of the Book will definitely believe in him before his death.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And on the Day of Judgment Jesus will be a witness against them.
footnotes: ¬†Scholars disagree whether Jews and Christians will come to realize that Jesus is a prophet of Allah at the time of their death or before the death of Jesus after his second coming.

4:160.text: We forbade the Jews certain foods that had been lawful to them for their wrongdoing, and for hindering many from the Way of Allah,

4:161.text: taking interest despite its prohibition, and consuming people‚Äôs wealth unjustly. We have prepared for the disbelievers among them a painful punishment.

4:162.text: But those with solid knowledge among them and those with true faith believe in what has been revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ and what was revealed before you‚ÄĒňĻespeciallyňļ those who establish prayer‚ÄĒand those who pay alms-tax and believe in Allah and the Last Day, to these ňĻpeopleňļ We will grant a great reward.

4:163.text: Indeed, We have sent revelation to you ňĻO Prophetňļ as We sent revelation to Noah and the prophets after him. We also sent revelation to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and his descendants, ňĻas well asňļ Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon. And to David We gave the Psalms.

4:164.text: There are messengers whose stories We have told you already and others We have not. And to Moses Allah spoke directly.

4:165.text: ňĻAll wereňļ messengers delivering good news and warnings so humanity should have no excuse before Allah after ňĻthe coming ofňļ the messengers. And Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

4:166.text: Yet ňĻif you are denied, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you‚ÄĒHe has sent it with His knowledge. The angels too bear witness. And Allah ňĻaloneňļ is sufficient as a Witness.

4:167.text: Those who disbelieve and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah have certainly strayed far away.

4:168.text: Those who disbelieve and wrong themselves‚ÄĒsurely Allah will neither forgive them nor guide them to any path

4:169.text: except that of Hell, to stay there for ever and ever. And that is easy for Allah.

4:170.text: O humanity! The Messenger has certainly come to you with the truth from your Lord, so believe for your own good. But if you disbelieve, then ňĻknow thatňļ to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

4:171.text: O People of the Book! Do not go to extremes regarding your faith; say nothing about Allah except the truth.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†The Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, was no more than a messenger of Allah and the fulfilment of His Word through Mary and a spirit ňĻcreated by a commandňļ from Him.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†So believe in Allah and His messengers and do not say, ‚ÄúTrinity.‚ÄĚ Stop!‚ÄĒfor your own good. Allah is only One God. Glory be to Him! He is far above having a son! To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is sufficient as a Trustee of Affairs.
footnotes: ¬†The Jews are warned against denying Jesus as the Messiah, while Christians are warned against calling Jesus Allah.
¬†i.e., Jesus was created by Allah‚Äôs Word ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ and he was, and life was breathed into Jesus by the holy spirit (the angel Gabriel) at the command of Allah.

4:172.text: The Messiah would never be too proud to be a servant of Allah, nor would the angels nearest to Allah. Those who are too proud and arrogant to worship Him will be brought before Him all together.

4:173.text: As for those who believe and do good, He will reward them in full and increase them out of His grace. But those who are too proud and arrogant, He will subject them to a painful punishment. And besides Allah they will find no protector or helper.

4:174.text: O humanity! There has come to you conclusive evidence from your Lord. And We have sent down to you a brilliant light.

4:175.text: As for those who believe in Allah and hold fast to Him, He will admit them into His mercy and grace and guide them to Himself through the Straight Path.

4:176.text: They ask you ňĻfor a ruling, O ¬†Prophetňļ. Say, ‚ÄúAllah gives you a ruling regarding those who die without children or parents.‚ÄĚ If a man dies childless and leaves behind a sister, she will inherit one-half of his estate, whereas her brother will inherit all of her estate if she dies childless. If this person leaves behind two sisters, they together will inherit two-thirds of the estate. But if the deceased leaves male and female siblings, a male‚Äôs share will be equal to that of two females. Allah makes ňĻthisňļ clear to you so you do not go astray. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This verse is placed at the end of the s√Ľrah¬†and not with similar verses at the beginning to connect the end of this s√Ľrah¬†with the next one or, according to Al-Fakhr Ar-R√Ęzi, either to tie the end of the s√Ľrah¬†with its beginning for emphasis, as found in 20:2 and 124 as well as 23:1 and 117, or to emphasize Allah‚Äôs knowledge, just like the first verse emphasizes His power.

40:2.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Knowing,

40:3.text: the Forgiver of sin and Accepter of repentance, the Severe in punishment, and Infinite in bounty. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. To Him ňĻaloneňļ ¬†is the final return.

40:4.text: None disputes the signs of Allah except the disbelievers, so do not be deceived by their prosperity throughout the land.

40:5.text: Before them, the people of Noah denied ňĻthe truthňļ, as did ňĻotherňļ enemy forces afterwards. Every community plotted against its prophet to seize him, and argued in falsehood, ňĻhopingňļ to discredit the truth with it. So I seized them. And how ňĻhorribleňļ was My punishment!

40:6.text: And so your Lord‚Äôs decree has been proven true against the disbelievers‚ÄĒthat they will be the inmates of the Fire.

40:7.text: Those ňĻangelsňļ who carry the Throne and those around it glorify the praises of their Lord, have faith in Him, and seek forgiveness for the believers, ňĻpraying:ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! You encompass everything in ňĻYourňļ mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your Way, and protect them from the torment of the Hellfire.

40:8.text: Our Lord! Admit them into the Gardens of Eternity which You have promised them, along with the righteous among their parents, spouses, and descendants. You ňĻaloneňļ are truly the Almighty, All-Wise.

40:9.text: And protect them from ňĻthe consequences of theirňļ evil deeds. For whoever You protect from the evil of their deeds on that Day will have been shown Your mercy. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.‚ÄĚ

40:10.text: Indeed, it will be announced to the disbelievers, ‚ÄúAllah‚Äôs contempt for you‚ÄĒas you disbelieved when invited to belief‚ÄĒwas far worse than your contempt for one another ňĻTodayňļ.‚ÄĚ

40:11.text: They will plead, ‚ÄúOur Lord! You made us lifeless twice, and gave us life twice.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Now we confess our sins. So is there any way out?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†You created us from nothing, then gave us life in the wombs of our mothers, then caused us to die at the end of our worldly lives, and finally resurrected us after our death (see¬†2:28).

40:12.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻNo!ňļ This is because when Allah alone was invoked, you ňĻstaunchlyňļ disbelieved. But when others were associated with Him ňĻin worshipňļ, you ňĻreadilyňļ believed. So ňĻTodayňļ judgment belongs to Allah ňĻaloneňļ‚ÄĒthe Most High, All-Great.‚ÄĚ

40:13.text: He is the One Who shows you His signs and sends down ňĻrain asňļ a provision for you from the sky. ňĻButňļ none will be mindful except those who turn ňĻto Himňļ.

40:14.text: So call upon Allah with sincere devotion, even to the dismay of the disbelievers.

40:15.text: ňĻHe isňļ Highly Exalted in rank, Lord of the Throne. He sends down the revelation by His command to whoever He wills of His servants to warn ňĻallňļ of the Day of Meeting‚ÄĒ

40:16.text: the Day all will appear ňĻbefore Allahňļ. Nothing about them will be hidden from Him. ňĻHe will ask,ňļ ‚ÄúWho does all authority belong to this Day? To Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Im√Ęm Muslim that everyone and everything that has ever existed will die on that Day, except for Allah‚ÄĒthe Eternal. He will then ask, ‚ÄúWho does all authority belong to this Day? Where are the kings of the world? I am the King.‚ÄĚ Since no one will be there to answer, Allah will answer Himself, ‚ÄúňĻAll authority belongsňļ to Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme!‚ÄĚ

40:17.text: Today every soul will be rewarded for what it has done. No injustice Today! Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.‚ÄĚ

40:18.text: Warn them ňĻO Prophetňļ of the approaching Day when the hearts will jump into the throats, suppressing distress. The wrongdoers will have neither a close friend nor intercessor to be heard.

40:19.text: Allah ňĻevenňļ knows the sly glances of the eyes and whatever the hearts conceal.

40:20.text: And Allah judges with the truth, while those ňĻidolsňļ they invoke besides Him cannot judge at all. Indeed, Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

40:21.text: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them? They were far superior in might and ňĻricher inňļ monuments throughout the land. But Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no protector from Allah.

40:22.text: That was because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs, but they persisted in disbelief. So Allah seized them. Surely He is All-Powerful, severe in punishment.

40:23.text: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs and compelling proof

40:24.text: to Pharaoh, Ham√Ęn, and Korah. But they responded: ‚ÄúMagician! Total liar!‚ÄĚ

40:25.text: Then, when he came to them with the truth from Us, they said, ‚ÄúKill the sons of those who believe with him and keep their women.‚ÄĚ But the plotting of the disbelievers was only in vain.

40:26.text: And Pharaoh said, ‚ÄúLet me kill Moses, and let him call upon his Lord! I truly fear that he may change your traditions or cause mischief in the land.‚ÄĚ

40:27.text: Moses replied, ‚ÄúI seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant person who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.‚ÄĚ

40:28.text: A believing man from Pharaoh‚Äôs people, who was hiding his faith, argued, ‚ÄúWill you kill a man<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻonlyňļ for saying: ‚ÄėMy Lord is Allah,‚Äô while he has in fact come to you with clear proofs from your Lord? If he is a liar, it will be to his own loss. But if he is truthful, then you will be afflicted with some of what he is threatening you with. Surely Allah does not guide whoever is a transgressor, a total liar.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Moses (Ô∑ļ).

40:29.text: O ¬†my people! Authority belongs to you today, reigning supreme in the land. But who would help us against the torment of Allah, if it were to befall us?‚ÄĚ Pharaoh assured ňĻhis peopleňļ, ‚ÄúI am telling you only what I believe, and I am leading you only to the way of guidance.‚ÄĚ

40:30.text: And the man who believed cautioned, ‚ÄúO my people! I truly fear for you the doom of ňĻearlierňļ enemy forces‚ÄĒ

40:31.text: like the fate of the people of Noah, ‚Äô»ād, Tham√Ľd, and those after them. For Allah would never will to wrong ňĻHisňļ servants.

40:32.text: O ¬†my people! I truly fear for you the Day all will be crying out ňĻto each otherňļ‚ÄĒ

40:33.text: the Day you will ňĻtry in vain toňļ turn your backs and run away, with no one to protect you from Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.

40:34.text: Joseph already came to you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†earlier with clear proofs, yet you never ceased to doubt what he came to you with. When he died you said, ‚ÄėAllah will never send a messenger after him.‚Äô This is how Allah leaves every transgressor and doubter to stray‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, your ancestors, because Joseph (Ô∑ļ) died over 400 hundred years before Moses (Ô∑ļ).

40:35.text: those who dispute Allah‚Äôs signs with no proof given to them. How despicable is that for Allah and the believers! This is how Allah seals the heart of every arrogant tyrant.‚ÄĚ

40:36.text: Pharaoh ordered, ‚ÄúO Ham√Ęn! Build me a high tower so I may reach the pathways

40:37.text: leading up to the heavens and look for the God of Moses, although I am sure he is a liar.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And so Pharaoh‚Äôs evil deeds were made so appealing to him that he was hindered from the ňĻRightňļ Way. But the plotting of Pharaoh was only in vain.

40:38.text: And the man who believed urged, ‚ÄúO my people! Follow me, ňĻandňļ I will lead you to the Way of Guidance.

40:39.text: O ¬†my people! This worldly life is only ňĻa fleetingňļ enjoyment, whereas the Hereafter is truly the home of settlement.

40:40.text: Whoever does an evil deed will only be paid back with its equivalent. And whoever does good, whether male or female, and is a believer, they will enter Paradise, where they will be provided for without limit.

40:41.text: O ¬†my people! How is it that I invite you to salvation, while you invite me to the Fire!

40:42.text: You invite me to disbelieve in Allah and associate with Him what I have no knowledge of, while I invite you to the Almighty, Most Forgiving.

40:43.text: There is no doubt that whatever ňĻidolsňļ you invite me to ňĻworshipňļ are not worthy to be invoked either in this world or the Hereafter.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻUndoubtedly,ňļ our return is to Allah, and the transgressors will be the inmates of the Fire.
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúWithout a doubt, whatever ňĻgodsňļ you are calling me to ňĻworshipňļ have no claim ňĻto divinityňļ in this world or the Hereafter.‚ÄĚ

40:44.text: You will remember what I say to you, and I entrust my affairs to Allah. Surely Allah is All-Seeing of all ňĻHisňļ servants.‚ÄĚ

40:45.text: So Allah protected him from the evil of their schemes. And Pharaoh‚Äôs people were overwhelmed by an evil punishment:

40:46.text: they are exposed to the Fire ňĻin their gravesňļ morning and evening. And on the Day the Hour will be established ňĻit will be saidňļ, ‚ÄúAdmit Pharaoh‚Äôs people into the harshest punishment ňĻof Hellňļ.‚ÄĚ

40:47.text: ňĻConsider the Dayňļ when they will dispute in the Fire, and the lowly ňĻfollowersňļ will appeal to the arrogant ňĻleadersňļ, ‚ÄúWe were your ňĻdedicatedňļ followers, will you then shield us from a portion of the Fire?‚ÄĚ

40:48.text: The arrogant will say, ‚ÄúWe are all in it! ňĻForňļ Allah has already passed judgment over ňĻHisňļ servants.‚ÄĚ

40:49.text: And those in the Fire will cry out to the keepers of Hell,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúPray to your Lord to lighten the torment for us ňĻevenňļ for one day!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The verse does not say ‚ÄúThose in the Fire will cry out to its keepers,‚ÄĚ simply because there are levels in the Fire: those in higher levels will plead to the keepers of the depths of Hell to appeal to Allah to lighten the punishment even for one day, since they think they are better than those at the bottom.

40:50.text: The keepers will reply, ‚ÄúDid your messengers not ňĻconstantlyňļ come to you with clear proofs?‚ÄĚ They will say, ‚ÄúYes ňĻthey didňļ.‚ÄĚ The keepers will say, ‚ÄúThen pray! Though the prayer of the disbelievers is only in vain.‚ÄĚ

40:51.text: We certainly help Our messengers and the believers, ňĻbothňļ in this worldly life and on the Day the witnesses will stand forth ňĻfor testimonyňļ‚ÄĒ

40:52.text: the Day the wrongdoers‚Äô excuses will be of no benefit to them. They will be condemned, and will have the worst outcome.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Hellfire.

40:53.text: And indeed, We gave Moses ňĻtrueňļ guidance, and made the Children of Israel inherit the Scripture‚ÄĒ

40:54.text: a guide and a reminder to people of reason.

40:55.text: So be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ, ňĻforňļ Allah‚Äôs promise is certainly true. Seek forgiveness for your shortcomings.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And glorify the praises of your Lord morning and evening.
footnotes: ¬†Like other prophets, MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) was infallible of sin. The verse here refers to misjudgments, such as the Prophet‚Äôs response to the blind man (80:1-10), Jonah‚Äôs departure from his city without Allah‚Äôs permission (21:87-88), and David‚Äôs suspicions of the two men who scaled the wall of his sanctuary (38:21-25). If the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) himself is urged to seek forgiveness, then the believers are even more in need of praying for Allah‚Äôs forgiveness.

40:56.text: Surely those who dispute Allah‚Äôs signs‚ÄĒwith no proof given to them‚ÄĒhave nothing in their hearts but greed for dominance, which they will never attain. So seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He alone is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

40:57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is certainly greater than the re-creation of humankind, but most people do not know.

40:58.text: Those blind ňĻto the truthňļ and those who can see are not equal, nor are those who believe and do good ňĻequalňļ to those who do evil. Yet you are hardly mindful.

40:59.text: The Hour is certainly coming, there is no doubt about it. But most people do not believe.

40:60.text: Your Lord has proclaimed, ‚ÄúCall upon Me, I will respond to you. Surely those who are too proud to worship Me will enter Hell, fully humbled.‚ÄĚ

40:61.text: It is Allah Who has made the night for you to rest in and the day bright. Surely Allah is ever Bountiful to humanity, but most people are ungrateful.

40:62.text: That is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. How can you then be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

40:63.text: This is how those who used to reject Allah‚Äôs signs were ňĻalsoňļ deluded.

40:64.text: It is Allah Who made the earth a place of settlement for you and the sky a canopy. He shaped you ňĻin the wombňļ, perfecting your form. And He has provided you with what is good and lawful. That is Allah‚ÄĒyour Lord. So Blessed is Allah, Lord of all worlds.

40:65.text: He is the Ever-Living. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. So call upon Him with sincere devotion, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds.‚ÄĚ

40:66.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI have been forbidden to worship those ňĻidolsňļ you worship besides Allah, since clear proofs have come to me from my Lord. And I have been commanded to ňĻfullyňļ submit to the Lord of all worlds.‚ÄĚ

40:67.text: He is the One Who created you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†from dust, then from a sperm-drop,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†then ňĻdeveloped you intoňļ a clinging clot,<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†then He brings you forth as infants, so that you may reach your prime, and become old‚ÄĒthough some of you ňĻmayňļ die sooner‚ÄĒreaching an appointed time, so perhaps you may understand ňĻAllah‚Äôs powerňļ.
footnotes: ¬†Your father, Adam.
¬†NuŠĻ≠fah¬†refers to the union of male and female gametes (sperm and egg) which results in the zygote after fertilization.
¬†‚ÄôAlaqah, meaning the embryo resembles a leech.

40:68.text: He is the One Who gives life and causes death. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, ‚ÄúBe!‚ÄĚ And it is!

40:69.text: Have you not seen how those who dispute Allah‚Äôs signs are turned away?

40:70.text: ňĻThey areňļ the ones who reject this Book and all ňĻscripturesňļ We sent Our messengers with. So they will know ňĻthe consequencesňļ

40:71.text: when shackles will be around their necks and chains ňĻon their legsňļ. They will be dragged

40:72.text: through boiling water, then burned in the Fire ňĻas fuelňļ.

40:73.text: Then they will be asked, ‚ÄúWhere are those ňĻidolsňļ you used to associate

40:74.text: with Allah?‚ÄĚ They will cry, ‚ÄúThey have ňĻallňļ failed us. In fact, we did not invoke anything ňĻrealňļ before.‚ÄĚ This is how Allah leaves the disbelievers to stray.

40:75.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúThis ňĻpunishmentňļ is for being prideful on earth unjustly and for acting arrogantly.

40:76.text: Enter the gates of Hell, to stay there forever. What an evil home for the arrogant!‚ÄĚ

40:77.text: So be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ. Surely Allah‚Äôs promise is true. Whether We show you some of what We threaten them with, or cause you to die ňĻbefore thatňļ, to Us they will ňĻallňļ be returned.

40:78.text: We already sent messengers before you. We have told you the stories of some of them, while others We have not. It was not for any messenger to bring a sign without Allah‚Äôs permission. But when Allah‚Äôs decree comes, judgment will be passed with fairness, and the people of falsehood will then be in ňĻtotalňļ loss.

40:79.text: It is Allah Who made cattle for you so that you may ride some and eat others.

40:80.text: Also, you find in them ňĻotherňļ benefits.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And by means of them you may reach destinations you desire. And you are carried upon ňĻsome ofňļ them and upon ships.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., milk, wool, and hide.

40:81.text: And He shows you His signs. Now which of Allah‚Äôs signs will you deny?

40:82.text: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those who were ňĻdestroyedňļ before them? They were far superior in number and might and ňĻricher inňļ monuments throughout the land, but their ňĻworldlyňļ gains were of no benefit to them.

40:83.text: When their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they were prideful in whatever ňĻworldlyňļ knowledge they had,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and were ňĻultimatelyňļ overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.
footnotes: ¬†And ridiculed the divine knowledge brought to them by their messengers.

40:84.text: When they saw Our punishment, they cried, ‚ÄúňĻNowňļ we believe in Allah alone and reject what we had been associating with Him!‚ÄĚ

40:85.text: But their faith was of no benefit to them when they saw Our torment. This has ňĻalwaysňļ been Allah‚Äôs way ňĻof dealingňļ with His ňĻwickedňļ servants. Then and there the disbelievers were in ňĻtotalňļ loss.

41:2.text: ňĻThis isňļ a revelation from the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

41:3.text: ňĻIt isňļ a Book whose verses are perfectly explained‚ÄĒa Quran in Arabic for people who know,

41:4.text: delivering good news and warning. Yet most of them turn away, so they do not hear.

41:5.text: They say, ‚ÄúOur hearts are veiled against what you are calling us to, there is deafness in our ears, and there is a barrier between us and you. So do ňĻwhatever you wantňļ and so shall we!‚ÄĚ

41:6.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI am only a man like you, ňĻbutňļ it has been revealed to me that your God is only One God. So take the Straight Way towards Him, and seek His forgiveness. And woe to the polytheists‚ÄĒ

41:7.text: those who do not pay alms-tax and are in denial of the Hereafter.

41:8.text: ňĻButňļ those who believe and do good will certainly have a never-ending reward.

41:9.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúHow can you disbelieve in the One Who created the earth in two Days? And how can you set up equals with Him? That is the Lord of all worlds.

41:10.text: He placed on the earth firm mountains, standing high, showered His blessings upon it, and ordained ňĻallňļ its means of sustenance‚ÄĒtotaling four Days exactly<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒfor all who ask.
footnotes: ¬†These four Days include the first two, so the total period of creation is six heavenly Days.

41:11.text: Then He turned towards the heaven when it was ňĻstill likeňļ smoke, saying to it and to the earth, ‚ÄėSubmit, willingly or unwillingly.‚Äô They both responded, ‚ÄėWe submit willingly.‚Äô

41:12.text: So He formed the heaven into seven heavens in two Days, assigning to each its mandate. And We adorned the lowest heaven with ňĻstars likeňļ lamps ňĻfor beautyňļ and for protection. That is the design of the Almighty, All-Knowing.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:54.

41:13.text: If they turn away, then say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI warn you of a ňĻmightyňļ blast, like the one that befell ‚Äô»ād and Tham√Ľd.‚ÄĚ

41:14.text: The messengers had come to them from all angles, ňĻproclaiming,ňļ ‚ÄúWorship none but Allah.‚ÄĚ They responded, ‚ÄúHad our Lord willed, He could have easily sent down angels ňĻinsteadňļ. So we totally reject what you have been sent with.‚ÄĚ

41:15.text: As for ‚Äô»ād, they acted arrogantly throughout the land with no right, boasting, ‚ÄúWho is superior to us in might?‚ÄĚ Did they not see that Allah ňĻHimselfňļ, Who created them, was far superior to them in might? Still they persisted in denying Our signs.

41:16.text: So We sent against them a furious wind,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for ňĻseveralňļ miserable days, to make them taste a humiliating punishment in this worldly life. But far more humiliating will be the punishment of the Hereafter. And they will not be helped.
footnotes: ¬†lit., a bitter and screaming wind.

41:17.text: As for Tham√Ľd, We showed them guidance, but they preferred blindness over guidance. So the blast of a disgracing punishment overtook them for what they used to commit.

41:18.text: And We delivered those who were faithful and were mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.

41:19.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the enemies of Allah will be gathered for the Fire, all driven in ranks.

41:20.text: When they reach it, their ears, eyes, and skin will testify against what they used to do.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It is reported in Ibn Kath√ģr‚Äôs commentary that, on the Day of Judgment, the wicked will deny the evil deeds in their records‚ÄĒas a desperate attempt to escape the horrible punishment in Hell. They will be asked if they accept Allah, the angels, or even their own families and neighbours as witnesses, but they will refuse. So Allah will make their organs testify against them.

41:21.text: They will ask their skin ňĻfuriouslyňļ, ‚ÄúWhy have you testified against us?‚ÄĚ It will say, ‚ÄúWe have been made to speak by Allah, Who causes all things to speak. He ňĻis the One Whoňļ created you the first time, and to Him you were bound to return.

41:22.text: You did not ňĻbother toňļ hide yourselves from your ears, eyes, and skin to prevent them from testifying against you. Rather, you assumed that Allah did not know much of what you used to do.

41:23.text: It was that ňĻfalseňļ assumption you entertained about your Lord that has brought about your doom, so you have become losers.‚ÄĚ

41:24.text: Even if they endure patiently, the Fire will ňĻalwaysňļ be their home. And if they ňĻbeg toňļ appease ňĻtheir Lordňļ, they will never be allowed to.

41:25.text: We placed at their disposal ňĻevilňļ associates who made their past and future ňĻmisdeedsňļ appealing to them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻSoňļ the fate of earlier communities of jinn and humans has been justified against them ňĻas wellňļ, ňĻforňļ they were truly losers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., associating others with Allah in worship and denying the Hereafter. See¬†43:36-37.

41:26.text: The disbelievers advised ňĻone anotherňļ, ‚ÄúDo not listen to this Quran but drown it out so that you may prevail.‚ÄĚ

41:27.text: So We will certainly make the disbelievers taste a severe punishment, and We will surely repay them according to the worst of their deeds.

41:28.text: That is the reward of Allah‚Äôs enemies: the Fire, which will be their eternal home‚ÄĒa ňĻfittingňļ reward for their denial of Our revelations.

41:29.text: The disbelievers will ňĻthenňļ cry, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Show us those jinn and humans who led us astray: we will put them under our feet so that they will be among the lowest ňĻin Hellňļ.‚ÄĚ

41:30.text: Surely those who say, ‚ÄúOur Lord is Allah,‚ÄĚ and then remain steadfast, the angels descend upon them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúDo not fear, nor grieve. Rather, rejoice in the good news of Paradise, which you have been promised.
footnotes: ¬†Especially at the time of their death.

41:31.text: We are your supporters in this worldly life and in the Hereafter. There you will have whatever your souls desire, and there you will have whatever you ask for:

41:32.text: an accommodation from the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful ňĻLordňļ.‚ÄĚ

41:33.text: And whose words are better than someone who calls ňĻothersňļ to Allah, does good, and says, ‚ÄúI am truly one of those who submit.‚ÄĚ?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., Muslims.

41:34.text: Good and evil cannot be equal. Respond ňĻto evilňļ with what is best, then the one you are in a feud with will be like a close friend.

41:35.text: But this cannot be attained except by those who are patient and who are truly fortunate.

41:36.text: And if you are tempted by Satan, then seek refuge with Allah. Indeed, He ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

41:37.text: Among His signs are the day and the night, the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun or the moon, but prostrate to Allah, Who created them ňĻallňļ, if you ňĻtrulyňļ worship Him ňĻaloneňļ.

41:38.text: But if the pagans are too proud, then ňĻlet them know thatňļ those ňĻangelsňļ nearest to your Lord glorify Him day and night, and never grow weary.

41:39.text: And among His signs is that you see the earth devoid of life, but as soon as We send down rain upon it, it begins to stir ňĻto lifeňļ and swell. Indeed, the One Who revives it can easily revive the dead. He is certainly Most Capable of everything.

41:40.text: Indeed, those who abuse Our revelations<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†are not hidden from Us. Who is better: the one who will be cast into the Fire or the one who will be secure on Judgment Day? Do whatever you want. He is certainly All-Seeing of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the noise the disbelievers used to make when the Quran was recited (see 41:26), or those who twist the meaning of Quranic passages and take them out of context.

41:41.text: Indeed, those who deny the Reminder<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†after it has come to them ňĻare doomedňļ, for it is truly a mighty Book.
footnotes: ¬†The Reminder is one of the names of the Quran.

41:42.text: It cannot be proven false from any angle. ňĻIt isňļ a revelation from the ňĻOne Who isňļ All-Wise, Praiseworthy.

41:43.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Nothing is said to you ňĻby the deniersňļ except what was already said to the messengers before you. Surely your Lord is ňĻthe Lordňļ of forgiveness and painful punishment.

41:44.text: Had We revealed it as a non-Arabic Quran, they would have certainly argued, ‚ÄúIf only its verses were made clear ňĻin our languageňļ. What! A non-Arabic revelation for an Arab audience!‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIt is a guide and a healing to the believers. As for those who disbelieve, there is deafness in their ears and blindness to it ňĻin their heartsňļ. It is as if they are being called from a faraway place.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†So they neither hear nor understand the call.

41:45.text: Indeed, We had given Moses the Scripture, but differences arose regarding it. Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†their differences would have been settled ňĻat onceňļ. They are truly in alarming doubt about it.
footnotes: ¬†That He will delay their judgment until the Hereafter.

41:46.text: Whoever does good, it is to their own benefit. And whoever does evil, it is to their own loss. Your Lord is never unjust to ňĻHisňļ creation.

41:47.text: With Him ňĻaloneňļ is the knowledge of the Hour. No fruit comes out of its husk, nor does a female conceive or deliver without His knowledge. And ňĻconsiderňļ the Day He will call to them, ‚ÄúWhere are My ňĻso-calledňļ associate-gods?‚ÄĚ They will cry, ‚ÄúWe declare before you that none of us testifies to that ňĻany longerňļ.‚ÄĚ

41:48.text: Whatever ňĻidolsňļ they used to invoke besides Allah will fail them. And they will realize that they will have no escape.

41:49.text: One never tires of praying for good. And if touched with evil, they become desperate and hopeless.

41:50.text: And if We let them taste a mercy from Us after being touched with adversity, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúThis is what I deserve. I do not think the Hour will ňĻeverňļ come. And if in fact I am returned to my Lord, the finest reward with Him will definitely be mine.‚ÄĚ But We will surely inform the disbelievers of what they used to do. And We will certainly make them taste a harsh torment.

41:51.text: When We show favour to someone, they turn away, acting arrogantly. And when touched with evil, they make endless prayers ňĻfor goodňļ.

41:52.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúImagine if this ňĻQuranňļ is ňĻtrulyňļ from Allah and you deny it: who can be more astray than those who have gone too far in opposition ňĻto the truthňļ?‚ÄĚ

41:53.text: We will show them Our signs in the universe and within themselves until it becomes clear to them that this ňĻQuranňļ is the truth. Is it not enough that your Lord is a Witness over all things?

41:54.text: They are truly in doubt of the meeting with their Lord! ňĻButňļ He is indeed Fully Aware of everything.

42:3.text: And so you ňĻO Prophetňļ are sent revelation, just like those before you, by Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

42:4.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And He is the Most High, the Greatest.

42:5.text: The heavens¬†nearly burst, one above the other, ňĻin awe of Himňļ. And the angels glorify the praises of their Lord, and seek forgiveness for those on earth. Surely Allah alone is the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

42:6.text: As for those who take other protectors besides Him, Allah is Watchful over them. And you ňĻO Prophetňļ are not a keeper over them.

42:7.text: And so We have revealed to you a Quran in Arabic, so you may warn the Mother of Cities<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and everyone around it, and warn of the Day of Gathering‚ÄĒabout which there is no doubt‚ÄĒňĻwhenňļ a group will be in Paradise and another in the Blaze.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúThe Mother of Cities‚ÄĚ is an honorary title given to the city of Mecca because of its great religious significance as the home of Allah‚Äôs first house of worship ever built on earth, and perhaps because of its central location as well.

42:8.text: Had Allah willed, He could have easily made all ňĻhumanityňļ into a single community ňĻof believersňļ. But He admits into His mercy whoever He wills. And the wrongdoers will have no protector or helper.

42:9.text: How can they take protectors besides Him? Allah alone is the Protector. He ňĻaloneňļ gives life to the dead. And He ňĻaloneňļ is Most Capable of everything.

42:10.text: ňĻSay to the believers, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhatever you may differ about, its judgment rests with Allah. That is Allah‚ÄĒmy Lord. In Him I put my trust, and to Him I ňĻalwaysňļ turn.‚ÄĚ

42:11.text: ňĻHe isňļ the Originator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you spouses from among yourselves, and ňĻmadeňļ mates for cattle ňĻas wellňļ‚ÄĒmultiplying you ňĻbothňļ. There is nothing like Him, for He ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

42:12.text: To Him belong the keys ňĻof the treasuriesňļ of the heavens and the earth. He gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. Indeed, He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

42:13.text: He has ordained for you ňĻbelieversňļ the Way which He decreed for Noah, and what We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ and what We decreed for Abraham, Moses, and Jesus,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻcommanding:ňļ ‚ÄúUphold the faith, and make no divisions in it.‚ÄĚ What you call the polytheists to is unbearable for them. Allah chooses for Himself whoever He wills, and guides to Himself whoever turns ňĻto Himňļ.
footnotes: ¬†Like 33:7, this verse names the five greatest prophets of Islam: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and MuŠł•ammad.

42:14.text: They did not split ňĻinto sectsňļ out of mutual envy until knowledge came to them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord for an appointed term,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†the matter would have certainly been settled between them ňĻat onceňļ. And surely those who were made to inherit the Scripture<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†after them are truly in alarming doubt about this ňĻQuranňļ.
footnotes: ¬†No community split into believers and disbelievers until they received the knowledge given by their prophet.
¬†That He will delay their judgment until the Hereafter.
¬†i.e., the Jews and Christians.

42:15.text: Because of that, you ňĻO Prophetňļ will invite ňĻallňļ. Be steadfast as you are commanded, and do not follow their desires. And say, ‚ÄúI believe in every Scripture Allah has revealed. And I am commanded to judge fairly among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. We will be accountable for our deeds and you for yours. There is no ňĻneed forňļ contention between us. Allah will gather us together ňĻfor judgmentňļ. And to Him is the final return.‚ÄĚ

42:16.text: As for those who dispute about Allah after He is ňĻalreadyňļ acknowledged ňĻby manyňļ, their argument is futile in the sight of their Lord. Upon them is wrath, and they will suffer a severe punishment.

42:17.text: It is Allah Who has revealed the Book with the truth and the balance ňĻof justiceňļ. You never know, perhaps the Hour is near.

42:18.text: Those who disbelieve in it ňĻask toňļ hasten it ňĻmockinglyňļ. But the believers are fearful of it, knowing that it is the truth. Surely those who dispute about the Hour have gone far astray.

42:19.text: Allah is Ever Kind to His servants. He provides ňĻabundantlyňļ to whoever He wills. And He is the All-Powerful, Almighty.

42:20.text: Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We will increase their harvest. And whoever desires ňĻonlyňļ the harvest of this world, We will give them some of it, but they will have no share in the Hereafter.

42:21.text: Or do they have associate-gods who have ordained for them some ňĻpolytheisticňļ beliefs, which Allah has not authorized? Had it not been for ňĻpriorňļ decree on Judgment, the matter would have certainly been settled between them ňĻat onceňļ. And surely the wrongdoers will suffer a painful punishment.

42:22.text: You will see the wrongdoers fearful ňĻof the punishmentňļ for what they committed but it will be inevitable for them, whereas those who believe and do good will be in the lush Gardens of Paradise. They will have whatever they desire from their Lord. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the greatest bounty.

42:23.text: That ňĻrewardňļ is the good news which Allah gives to His servants who believe and do good. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI do not ask you for a reward for this ňĻmessageňļ‚ÄĒonly honour for ňĻourňļ kinship.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Whoever earns a good deed, We will increase it in goodness for them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative.
footnotes: ¬†This could also be a call to all believers to love and respect the immediate family of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

42:24.text: Or do they say, ‚ÄúHe has fabricated a lie about Allah!‚ÄĚ? ňĻIf you had,ňļ Allah would have sealed your heart, if He willed. And Allah wipes out falsehood and establishes the truth by His Words. He certainly knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

42:25.text: He is the One Who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons ňĻtheirňļ sins. And He knows whatever you do.

42:26.text: He responds to those who believe and do good, and increases their reward out of His grace. As for the disbelievers, they will suffer a severe punishment.

42:27.text: Had Allah given abundant provisions to ňĻallňļ His servants, they would have certainly transgressed throughout the land. But He sends down whatever He wills in perfect measure. He is truly All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.

42:28.text: He is the One Who sends down rain after people have given up hope, spreading out His mercy. He is the Guardian, the Praiseworthy.

42:29.text: And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and all living beings He dispersed throughout both. And He is Most Capable of bringing all together whenever He wills.

42:30.text: Whatever affliction befalls you is because of what your own hands have committed. And He pardons much.

42:31.text: You can never escape ňĻHimňļ on earth, nor do you have any protector or helper besides Allah.

42:32.text: And among His signs are the ships like mountains ňĻsailingňļ in the sea.

42:33.text: If He wills, He can calm the wind, leaving the ships motionless on the water. Surely in this are signs for whoever is steadfast, grateful.

42:34.text: Or He can wreck the ships for what the people have committed‚ÄĒthough He forgives much‚ÄĒ

42:35.text: so those who dispute about Our signs may know that they have no refuge.

42:36.text: Whatever ňĻpleasureňļ you have been given is ňĻno more than a fleetingňļ enjoyment of this worldly life. But what is with Allah is far better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord;

42:37.text: who avoid major sins and shameful deeds, and forgive when angered;

42:38.text: who respond to their Lord, establish prayer, conduct their affairs by mutual consultation, and donate from what We have provided for them;

42:39.text: and who enforce justice when wronged.

42:40.text: The reward of an evil deed is its equivalent. But whoever pardons and seeks reconciliation, then their reward is with Allah. He certainly does not like the wrongdoers.

42:41.text: There is no blame on those who enforce justice after being wronged.

42:42.text: Blame is only on those who wrong people and transgress in the land unjustly. It is they who will suffer a painful punishment.

42:43.text: And whoever endures patiently and forgives‚ÄĒsurely this is a resolve to aspire to.

42:44.text: And whoever Allah leaves to stray will have no guide after Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they face the torment, pleading, ‚ÄúIs there any way back ňĻto the worldňļ?‚ÄĚ

42:45.text: And you will see them exposed to the Fire, fully humbled out of disgrace, stealing glances ňĻat itňļ. And the believers will say, ‚ÄúThe ňĻtrueňļ losers are those who have lost themselves and their families on Judgment Day.‚ÄĚ The wrongdoers will certainly be in everlasting torment.

42:46.text: They will have no protectors to help them against Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray, for them there is no way.

42:47.text: Respond to your Lord before the coming of a Day from Allah that cannot be averted. There will be no refuge for you then, nor ňĻgrounds forňļ denial ňĻof sinsňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Other possible translations: 1. ‚ÄúYou will have no refuge then, nor will you have an advocate ňĻagainst punishmentňļ.‚ÄĚ 2. ‚ÄúYou will have no refuge then, nor ňĻgroundsňļ to protest ňĻthe tormentňļ.‚ÄĚ

42:48.text: But if they turn away, We have not sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a keeper over them. Your duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ. And indeed, when We let someone taste a mercy from Us, they become prideful ňĻbecauseňļ of it. But when afflicted with evil because of what their hands have done, then one becomes totally ungrateful.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†They totally deny previous favours.

42:49.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He creates whatever He wills. He blesses whoever He wills with daughters, and blesses whoever He wills with sons,

42:50.text: or grants both, sons and daughters, ňĻto whoever He willsňļ, and leaves whoever He wills infertile. He is indeed All-Knowing, Most Capable.

42:51.text: It is not ňĻpossibleňļ for a human being to have Allah communicate with them, except through inspiration, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger-angel to reveal whatever He wills by His permission. He is surely Most High, All-Wise.
