42:52.text: And so We have sent to you ňĻO Prophetňļ a revelation by Our command. You did not know of ňĻthisňļ Book and faith ňĻbeforeňļ. But We have made it a light, by which We guide whoever We will of Our servants. And you are truly leading ňĻallňļ to the Straight Path‚ÄĒ

42:53.text: the Path of Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Surely to Allah all matters will return ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

43:3.text: Certainly, We have made it a Quran in Arabic so perhaps you will understand.

43:4.text: And indeed, it is‚ÄĒin the Master Record<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†with Us‚ÄĒhighly esteemed, rich in wisdom.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the Preserved Tablet, Al-LawŠł• Al-MaŠł•f√ĽŠļď, in which Allah has written the destiny of His entire creation.

43:5.text: Should We then turn the ňĻQuranicňļ Reminder away from you ňĻsimplyňļ because you have been a transgressing people?

43:6.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many prophets We sent to those ňĻdestroyedňļ before!

43:7.text: But no prophet ever came to them without being mocked.

43:8.text: So We destroyed those who were far mightier than these ňĻMeccansňļ. The examples of ňĻtheirňļ predecessors have ňĻalreadyňļ been related.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†For example, in the stories of the people of Noah, Lot, Shu‚Äôaib and other nations that were destroyed, found throughout the Quran.

43:9.text: If you ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ who created the heavens and the earth, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúThe Almighty, All-Knowing did.‚ÄĚ

43:10.text: ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who has laid out the earth for you, and set in it pathways for you so that you may find your way.

43:11.text: And ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who sends down rain from the sky in perfect measure, with which We give life to a lifeless land. And so will you be brought forth ňĻfrom the graveňļ.

43:12.text: And ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who created all ňĻthings inňļ pairs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and made for you ships and animals to ride
footnotes: ¬†For example, male and female, sweet and bitter, day and night, plains and mountains, heat and cold, light and darkness.

43:13.text: so that you may sit firmly on their backs, and remember your Lord‚Äôs blessings once you are settled on them, saying, ‚ÄúGlory be to the One Who has subjected these for us, for we could have never done so ňĻon our ownňļ.

43:14.text: And surely to our Lord we will ňĻallňļ return.‚ÄĚ

43:15.text: Still the pagans have made some of His creation out to be a part of Him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Indeed, humankind is clearly ungrateful.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 37:149.

43:16.text: Has He taken ňĻangels as Hisňļ daughters from what He created, and favoured you ňĻO pagansňļ with sons?

43:17.text: Whenever one of them is given the good news of what they attribute to the Most Compassionate,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†his face grows gloomy, as he suppresses his rage.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the birth of a baby girl.

43:18.text: ňĻDo they attribute to Himňļ those who are brought up in fineries and are not commanding in disputes?

43:19.text: Still they have labelled the angels, who are servants of the Most Compassionate, as female. Did they witness their creation? Their statement will be recorded, and they will be questioned!

43:20.text: And they argue, ‚ÄúHad the Most Compassionate willed, we would have never worshipped them.‚ÄĚ They have no knowledge ňĻin supportňļ of this ňĻclaimňļ. They do nothing but lie.

43:21.text: Or have We given them a Book ňĻfor proofňļ, before this ňĻQuranňļ, to which they are holding firm?

43:22.text: In fact, they say, ‚ÄúWe found our forefathers following a ňĻparticularňļ way, and we are following in their footsteps.‚ÄĚ

43:23.text: Similarly, whenever We sent a warner to a society before you ňĻO Prophetňļ, its ňĻspoiledňļ elite would say, ‚ÄúWe found our forefathers following a ňĻparticularňļ way, and we are walking in their footsteps.‚ÄĚ

43:24.text: Each ňĻwarnerňļ asked, ‚ÄúEven if what I brought you is better guidance than what you found your forefathers practicing?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúWe totally reject whatever you have been sent with.‚ÄĚ

43:25.text: So We inflicted punishment upon them. See then what was the fate of the deniers!

43:26.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophetňļ when Abraham declared to his father and his people, ‚ÄúI am totally free of whatever ňĻgodsňļ you worship,

43:27.text: except the One Who originated me, and He will surely guide me.‚ÄĚ

43:28.text: And he left this enduring declaration among his descendants, so they may ňĻalwaysňļ turn back ňĻto Allahňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†

43:29.text: In fact, I had allowed enjoyment for these ňĻMeccansňļ and their forefathers, until the truth came to them along with a messenger making things clear.

43:30.text: ňĻButňļ when the truth came to them, they said, ‚ÄúThis is magic, and we totally reject it.‚ÄĚ

43:31.text: And they exclaimed, ‚ÄúIf only this Quran was revealed to a great man from ňĻone ofňļ the two cities!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This refers to Mecca and ŠĻ¨√Ęif, two cities in Arabia, around 100 km apart.

43:32.text: Is it they who distribute your Lord‚Äôs mercy? We ňĻaloneňļ have distributed their ňĻveryňļ livelihood among them in this worldly life and raised some of them in rank above others so that some may employ others in service. ňĻButňļ your Lord‚Äôs mercy is far better than whatever ňĻwealthňļ they amass.

43:33.text: Were it not that people might ňĻbe tempted toňļ become one community ňĻof disbelieversňļ, We would have supplied the homes of ňĻonlyňļ those who disbelieve in the Most Compassionate with silver roofs and ňĻsilverňļ stairways to ascend,

43:34.text: as well as ňĻsilverňļ gates and thrones to recline on,

43:35.text: and ornaments ňĻof goldňļ. Yet all this is no more than a ňĻfleetingňļ enjoyment in this worldly life. ňĻButňļ the Hereafter with your Lord is ňĻonlyňļ for those mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

43:36.text: And whoever turns a blind eye to the Reminder of the Most Compassionate, We place at the disposal of each a devilish one as their close associate,

43:37.text: who will certainly hinder them from the ňĻRightňļ Way while they think they are ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

43:38.text: But when such a person comes to Us, one will say ňĻto their associateňļ, ‚ÄúI wish you were as distant from me as the east is from the west! What an evil associate ňĻyou wereňļ!‚ÄĚ

43:39.text: ňĻIt will be said to both,ňļ ‚ÄúSince you all did wrong, sharing in the punishment will be of no benefit to you this Day.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†From a worldly perspective, many people are comforted when they hear of others who went through similar trials as them. But this will not be the case on Judgment Day. Everyone will be desperate to save themselves from the punishment, regardless of others.

43:40.text: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind or those clearly astray?

43:41.text: Even if We take you away ňĻfrom this worldňļ, We will surely inflict punishment upon them.

43:42.text: Or if We show you what We threaten them with, We certainly have full power over them.

43:43.text: So hold firmly to what has been revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. You are truly on the Straight Path.

43:44.text: Surely this ňĻQuranňļ is a glory for you and your people. And you will ňĻallňļ be questioned ňĻabout itňļ.

43:45.text: Ask ňĻthe followers ofňļ the messengers that We already sent before you if We ňĻeverňļ appointed ňĻotherňļ gods to be worshipped besides the Most Compassionate.

43:46.text: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, and he said: ‚ÄúI am a messenger of the Lord of all worlds.‚ÄĚ

43:47.text: But as soon as he came to them with Our signs, they laughed at them,

43:48.text: although every sign We showed them was greater than the one before.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Ultimately, We seized them with torments so that they might return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the hand and the staff (see¬†20:17-22).
¬†The torments were: famine, shortage of crops, floods, locusts, lice, frogs, and blood (see¬†7:130-133).

43:49.text: ňĻThenňļ they pleaded, ‚ÄúO ňĻmightyňļ magician! Pray to your Lord on our behalf, by virtue of the covenant He made with you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We will certainly accept guidance.‚ÄĚ

43:50.text: But as soon as We removed the torments from them, they broke their promise.

43:51.text: And Pharaoh called out to his people, boasting, ‚ÄúO my people! Am I not sovereign over Egypt as well as ňĻallňļ these streams<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†flowing at my feet? Can you not see?
footnotes: ¬†Branches of the Nile.

43:52.text: Am I not better than this nobody who can hardly express himself?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Moses used to have a speech impediment. But when he became a prophet he prayed to Allah to help him speak clearly, and his prayer was answered (see¬†20:25-36).

43:53.text: Why then have no golden bracelets ňĻof kingshipňļ been granted to him or angels come with him as escorts!‚ÄĚ

43:54.text: And so he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. They were truly a rebellious people.

43:55.text: So when they enraged Us, We inflicted punishment upon them, drowning them all.

43:56.text: And We made them an example and a lesson for those after them.

43:57.text: When the son of Mary was cited as an example ňĻin argumentňļ, your people ňĻO Prophetňļ broke into ňĻjoyfulňļ applause.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†When 21:98 was revealed, warning the polytheists that all false gods will be in Hell, ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh ibn Az-Ziba‚Äôra, a poet who always attacked Islam, argued with the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) that if what the verse says is true, then Jesus will be in Hell as well! The polytheists, who were present, laughed in agreement with this argument. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) replied that the verse was talking exclusively about idols, adding that Jesus himself did not ask anyone to worship him. Verse 21:101 was later revealed in support of the Prophet‚Äôs argument. Eventually, ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh accepted Islam.

43:58.text: They exclaimed, ‚ÄúWhich is better: our gods or Jesus?‚ÄĚ They cite him only to argue. In fact, they are a people prone to dispute.

43:59.text: He was only a servant We showed favour to, and made as an example for the Children of Israel.

43:60.text: Had We willed, We could have easily replaced you ňĻallňļ with angels,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†succeeding one another on earth.
footnotes: ¬†Or ‚ÄúWe could have easily produced angels from you ‚Ä¶‚ÄĚ

43:61.text: And his ňĻsecondňļ coming is truly a sign for the Hour. So have no doubt about it, and follow me. This is the Straight Path.

43:62.text: And do not let Satan hinder you, ňĻforňļ he is certainly your sworn enemy.

43:63.text: When Jesus came with clear proofs, he declared, ‚ÄúI have come to you with wisdom, and to clarify to you some of what you differ about. So fear Allah, and obey me.

43:64.text: Surely Allah ňĻaloneňļ is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ňĻaloneňļ. This is the Straight Path.‚ÄĚ

43:65.text: Yet their ňĻvariousňļ groups have differed among themselves ňĻabout himňļ, so woe to the wrongdoers when they face the torment of a painful Day!

43:66.text: Are they waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise when they least expect ňĻitňļ?

43:67.text: Close friends will be enemies to one another on that Day, except the righteous,

43:68.text: ňĻwho will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúO My servants! There is no fear for you Today, nor will you grieve‚ÄĒ

43:69.text: ňĻthoseňļ who believed in Our signs and ňĻfullyňļ submitted ňĻto Usňļ.

43:70.text: Enter Paradise, you and your spouses, rejoicing.‚ÄĚ

43:71.text: Golden trays and cups will be passed around to them. There will be whatever the souls desire and the eyes delight in. And you will be there forever.

43:72.text: That is the Paradise which you will be awarded for what you used to do.

43:73.text: There you will have abundant fruit<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to eat from.
footnotes: ¬†Fruit also implies delicacies.

43:74.text: Indeed, the wicked will be in the torment of Hell forever.

43:75.text: It will never be lightened for them, and there they will be overwhelmed with despair.

43:76.text: We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers.

43:77.text: They will cry, ‚ÄúO M√Ęlik!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Let your Lord finish us off.‚ÄĚ He will answer, ‚ÄúYou are definitely here to stay.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Angel M√Ęlik is the chief keeper of Hell.

43:78.text: We certainly brought the truth to you, but most of you were resentful of the truth.

43:79.text: Or have they mastered some ňĻevilňļ plan? Then We ňĻtooňļ are surely planning.

43:80.text: Or do they think that We do not hear their ňĻevilňļ thoughts and secret talks? Yes ňĻWe doňļ! And Our messenger-angels are in their presence, recording ňĻit allňļ.

43:81.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf the Most Compassionate ňĻreallyňļ had offspring, I would be the first worshipper.‚ÄĚ

43:82.text: Glorified is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, far above what they claim.

43:83.text: So let them indulge ňĻin falsehoodňļ and amuse ňĻthemselvesňļ until they face their Day, which they have been warned of.

43:84.text: It is He Who is ňĻthe onlyňļ God in the heavens and ňĻthe onlyňļ God on the earth. For He is the All-Wise, All-Knowing.

43:85.text: And Blessed is the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between! With Him ňĻaloneňļ is the knowledge of the Hour. And to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

43:86.text: ňĻButňļ those ňĻobjects of worshipňļ they invoke besides Him have no power to intercede, except those who testify to the truth knowingly.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Jesus, Ezra, and the angels.

43:87.text: If you ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ who created them, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ How can they then be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

43:88.text: ňĻAllah is Aware ofňļ the Prophet‚Äôs cry: ‚ÄúO my Lord! Indeed, these are a people who persist in disbelief.‚ÄĚ

43:89.text: So bear with them and respond with peace. They will soon come to know.

44:3.text: Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for We always warn ňĻagainst evilňļ.
footnotes: ¬†The revelation of the Quran started on the 27th¬†night of RamaŠłć√Ęn‚ÄĒthe 9th¬†month of the Islamic calendar (see¬†97:1 and 2:185)

44:4.text: On that night every matter of wisdom is ordained

44:5.text: by a command from Us, for We have always sent ňĻmessengersňļ

44:6.text: as a mercy from your Lord. He ňĻaloneňļ is truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing‚ÄĒ

44:7.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith.

44:8.text: There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. He ňĻaloneňļ gives life and causes death. ňĻHe isňļ your Lord, and the Lord of your forefathers.

44:9.text: In fact, they are in doubt, amusing themselves.

44:10.text: Wait then ňĻO Prophetňļ for the day ňĻwhenňļ the sky will be veiled in haze,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†clearly visible,
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the drought that affected the Meccan pagans so badly that they came to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), begging him to pray to Allah to remove the affliction from them.

44:11.text: overwhelming the people. ňĻThey will cry,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is a painful torment.

44:12.text: Our Lord! Remove ňĻthisňļ torment from us, ňĻandňļ we will certainly believe.‚ÄĚ

44:13.text: How can they be reminded when a messenger has already come to them, making things clear,

44:14.text: then they turned away from him, saying, ‚ÄúA madman, taught by others!‚ÄĚ?

44:15.text: Indeed, We will remove ňĻthatňļ torment for a while, and you ňĻMeccansňļ will return ňĻto disbeliefňļ.

44:16.text: ňĻThenňļ on the Day We will deal ňĻyouňļ the fiercest blow, We will surely inflict punishment.

44:17.text: Indeed, before them We tested Pharaoh‚Äôs people: a noble messenger came to them,

44:18.text: ňĻproclaiming,ňļ ‚ÄúHand over the servants of Allah to me.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, let my people go.

44:19.text: And do not be arrogant with Allah. I have certainly come to you with a compelling proof.

44:20.text: And indeed, I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord so you do not stone me ňĻto deathňļ.

44:21.text: ňĻButňļ if you do not believe me, then let me be.‚ÄĚ

44:22.text: Ultimately, he cried out to his Lord, ‚ÄúThese are a wicked people!‚ÄĚ

44:23.text: ňĻAllah responded,ňļ ‚ÄúLeave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued.

44:24.text: And leave the sea parted, for they are certainly an army bound to drown.‚ÄĚ

44:25.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many gardens and springs the tyrants<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†left behind,
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Pharaoh and his soldiers.

44:26.text: as well as ňĻvariousňļ crops and splendid residences,

44:27.text: and luxuries which they fully enjoyed.

44:28.text: So it was. And We awarded it ňĻallňļ to another people.

44:29.text: Neither heaven nor earth wept over them, nor was their fate delayed.

44:30.text: And We certainly delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment

44:31.text: of Pharaoh. He was truly a tyrant, a transgressor.

44:32.text: And indeed, We chose the Israelites knowingly above the others.

44:33.text: And We showed them signs in which there was a clear test.

44:35.text: ‚ÄúThere is nothing beyond our first death, and we will never be resurrected.

44:36.text: Bring ňĻbackňļ our forefathers, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

44:37.text: Are they superior to the people of Tubba‚Äô<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and those before them? We destroyed them ňĻallňļ, ňĻforňļ they were truly wicked.
footnotes: ¬†Tubba‚Äô Al-Šł§imiari was an ancient righteous Yemeni king whose people persisted in disbelief and were destroyed, although they were superior to Meccans in strength and manpower.

44:38.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything in between for sport.

44:39.text: We only created them for a purpose, but most of these ňĻpagansňļ do not know.

44:40.text: Surely the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision is the time appointed for all‚ÄĒ

44:41.text: the Day no kith or kin will be of benefit to another whatsoever, nor will they be helped,

44:42.text: except those shown mercy by Allah. He is truly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

44:43.text: Surely ňĻthe fruit ofňļ the tree of Zaqq√Ľm<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Zaqq√Ľm¬†is a tree that grows in the depths of Hell as mentioned in 37:62-65. The pagans of Mecca used to make fun of the Prophet and say, ‚ÄúHow can a tree grow in Hell?‚ÄĚ

44:44.text: will be the food of the evildoer.

44:45.text: Like molten metal, it will boil in the bellies

44:46.text: like the boiling of hot water.

44:47.text: ňĻIt will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúSeize them and drag them into the depths of the Hellfire.

44:48.text: Then pour over their heads the torment of boiling water.‚ÄĚ

44:49.text: ňĻThe wicked will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste this. You mighty, noble one!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Abu Jahl, a Meccan pagan leader, met the warning of punishment in the Hereafter by exclaiming, ‚ÄúHow can I be punished when I am the mighty, noble one!‚ÄĚ

44:50.text: This is truly what you ňĻallňļ used to doubt.‚ÄĚ

44:51.text: Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure place,

44:53.text: dressed in fine silk and rich brocade, facing one another.

44:54.text: So it will be. And We will pair them to maidens with gorgeous eyes.

44:55.text: There they will call for every fruit in serenity.

44:56.text: There they will never taste death, beyond the first death. And He will protect them from the punishment of the Hellfire‚ÄĒ

44:57.text: as ňĻan act ofňļ grace from your Lord. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.

44:58.text: Indeed, We have made this ňĻQuranňļ easy in your own language ňĻO Prophetňļ so perhaps they will be mindful.

44:59.text: Wait then! They too are certainly waiting.

45:2.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

45:3.text: Surely in ňĻthe creation ofňļ the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers.

45:4.text: And in your own creation, and whatever living beings He dispersed, are signs for people of sure faith.

45:5.text: And ňĻinňļ the alternation of the day and the night, the provision<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†sent down from the skies by Allah‚ÄĒreviving the earth after its death‚ÄĒand the shifting of the winds, are signs for people of understanding.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., rain.

45:6.text: These are Allah‚Äôs revelations which We recite to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in truth. So what message will they believe in after ňĻdenyingňļ Allah and His revelations?

45:8.text: They hear Allah‚Äôs revelations recited to them, then persist ňĻin denialňļ arrogantly as if they did not hear them. So give them good news of a painful punishment.

45:9.text: And whenever they learn anything of Our revelations, they make a mockery of it. It is they who will suffer a humiliating punishment.

45:10.text: Awaiting them is Hell. Their ňĻworldlyňļ gains will not be of any benefit to them whatsoever, nor will those protectors they have taken besides Allah. And they will suffer a tremendous punishment.

45:11.text: This ňĻQuranňļ is ňĻtrueňļ guidance. And those who deny their Lord‚Äôs revelations will suffer the ňĻworstňļ torment of agonizing pain.

45:12.text: Allah is the One Who has subjected the sea for you so that ships may sail upon it by His command, and that you may seek His bounty, and that perhaps you will be grateful.

45:13.text: He ňĻalsoňļ subjected for you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth‚ÄĒall by His grace. Surely in this are signs for people who reflect.

45:14.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Tell the believers to forgive those who do not fear Allah‚Äôs days ňĻof tormentňļ, so that He will reward each group for what they used to commit.

45:15.text: Whoever does good, it is to their own benefit. And whoever does evil, it is to their own loss. Then to your Lord you will ňĻallňļ be returned.

45:16.text: Indeed, We gave the Children of Israel the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood; granted them good, lawful provisions; and favoured them above the others.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†By sending many prophets, and showing them unique miracles such as the splitting of the sea.

45:17.text: We ňĻalsoňļ gave them clear commandments regarding ňĻtheirňļ faith. But it was not until knowledge came to them that they differed out of mutual envy.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Judgment regarding their differences.
footnotes: ¬†No community split into believers and disbelievers until they received the knowledge given by their prophet.

45:18.text: Now We have set you ňĻO Prophetňļ on the ňĻclearňļ Way of faith. So follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know ňĻthe truthňļ.

45:19.text: They certainly can be of no benefit to you against Allah whatsoever. Surely the wrongdoers are patrons of each other, whereas Allah is the Patron of the righteous.

45:20.text: This ňĻQuranňļ is an insight for humanity‚ÄĒa guide and mercy for people of sure faith.

45:21.text: Or do those who commit evil deeds ňĻsimplyňļ think that We will make them equal‚ÄĒin their life and after their death‚ÄĒto those who believe and do good? How wrong is their judgment!

45:22.text: For Allah created the heavens and the earth for a purpose, so that every soul may be paid back for what it has committed. And none will be wronged.

45:23.text: Have you seen ňĻO Prophetňļ those who have taken their own desires as their god? ňĻAnd soňļ Allah left them to stray knowingly, sealed their hearing and hearts, and placed a cover on their sight. Who then can guide them after Allah? Will you ňĻallňļ not then be mindful?

45:24.text: And they argue, ‚ÄúThere is nothing beyond our worldly life. We die;¬†others are born. And nothing destroys us but ňĻthe passage ofňļ time.‚ÄĚ Yet they have no knowledge ňĻin supportňļ of this ňĻclaimňļ. They only speculate.

45:25.text: And whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, their only argument is to say: ‚ÄúBring our forefathers back, if what you say is true!‚ÄĚ

45:26.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ gives you life, then causes you to die, then will gather you ňĻallňļ on the Day of Judgment, about which there is no doubt. But most people do not know.‚ÄĚ

45:27.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. On the Day the Hour will be established, the people of falsehood will then be in ňĻtotalňļ loss.

45:28.text: And you will see every faith-community on its knees. Every community will be summoned to its record ňĻof deedsňļ. ňĻThey all will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúThis Day you will be rewarded for what you used to do.

45:29.text: This record of Ours speaks the truth about you. Indeed, We always had your deeds recorded ňĻby the angelsňļ.‚ÄĚ

45:30.text: As for those who believed and did good, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the absolute triumph.

45:31.text: And as for those who disbelieved, ňĻthey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúWere My revelations not recited to you, yet you acted arrogantly and were a wicked people?

45:32.text: And whenever it was said ňĻto youňļ, ‚ÄėSurely Allah‚Äôs promise ňĻof judgmentňļ is true and there is no doubt about the Hour,‚Äô you said ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄėWe do not know what the Hour is! We think it is no more than speculation, and we are not convinced ňĻthat it will ever comeňļ.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

45:33.text: And the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds will unfold before them, and they will be overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.

45:34.text: It will be said, ‚ÄúThis Day We will neglect you as you neglected the meeting of this Day of yours! Your home will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers.

45:35.text: This is because you made a mockery of Allah‚Äôs revelations, and were deluded by ňĻyourňļ worldly life.‚ÄĚ So ňĻfromňļ that Day ňĻonňļ they will not be taken out of the Fire, nor will they be allowed to appease ňĻtheir Lordňļ.

45:36.text: So all praise is for Allah‚ÄĒLord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of all worlds.

45:37.text: To Him belongs ňĻallňļ Majesty in the heavens and the earth. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

46:2.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

46:3.text: We only created the heavens and the earth and everything in between for a purpose and an appointed term. Yet the disbelievers are turning away from what they have been warned about.

46:4.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúHave you considered whatever ňĻidolsňļ you invoke besides Allah? Show me what they have created on earth! Or do they have a share in ňĻthe creation ofňļ the heavens? Bring me a scripture ňĻrevealedňļ before this ňĻQuranňļ or a shred of knowledge, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

46:5.text: And who could be more astray than those who call upon others besides Allah‚ÄĒňĻothersňļ that cannot respond to them until the Day of Judgment, and are ňĻevenňļ unaware of their calls?

46:6.text: And when ňĻsuchňļ people will be gathered together, those ňĻgodsňļ will be their enemies and will disown their worship.

46:7.text: Whenever Our clear revelations are recited to them, the disbelievers say of the truth when it has come to them, ‚ÄúThis is pure magic.‚ÄĚ

46:8.text: Or do they say, ‚ÄúHe<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†has fabricated this ňĻQuranňļ!‚ÄĚ? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf I have done so, then there is nothing whatsoever you can do to save me from Allah. He knows best what ňĻslursňļ you indulge about it. Sufficient is He as a Witness between you and me. And He is the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

46:9.text: Say, ‚ÄúI am not the first messenger ever sent, nor do I know what will happen to me or you. I only follow what is revealed to me. And I am only sent with a clear warning.‚ÄĚ

46:10.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúConsider if this ňĻQuranňļ is ňĻtrulyňļ from Allah and you deny it, and a witness from the Children of Israel attests to it and then believes,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†whereas you act arrogantly. Surely Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh ibn Sal√Ęm, a Jewish scholar, who embraced Islam when the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) emigrated to Medina.

46:11.text: The disbelievers say of the believers, ‚ÄúHad it been ňĻsomethingňļ good, they<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†would not have beaten us to it.‚ÄĚ Now since they reject its guidance, they will say, ‚ÄúňĻThis isňļ an ancient fabrication!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Poor and powerless Muslims.

46:12.text: And before this ňĻQuranňļ the Book of Moses was ňĻrevealed asňļ a guide and mercy. And this Book is a confirmation, in the Arabic tongue, to warn those who do wrong, and as good news to those who do good.

46:13.text: Surely those who say, ‚ÄúOur Lord is Allah,‚ÄĚ and then remain steadfast‚ÄĒthere will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.

46:14.text: It is they who will be the residents of Paradise, staying there forever, as a reward for what they used to do.

46:15.text: We have commanded people to honour their parents. Their mothers bore them in hardship and delivered them in hardship. Their ňĻperiod ofňļ bearing and weaning is thirty months. In time, when the child reaches their prime at the age of forty, they pray, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Inspire me to ňĻalwaysňļ be thankful for Your favours which You blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please You. And instil righteousness in my offspring. I truly repent to You, and I truly submit ňĻto Your Willňļ.‚ÄĚ

46:16.text: It is from these ňĻpeopleňļ that We will accept the good they did, and overlook their misdeeds‚ÄĒalong with the residents of Paradise, ňĻin fulfilment ofňļ the true promise they have been given.

46:17.text: But some scold their parents, ‚ÄúEnough with you! Are you warning me that I will be brought forth ňĻfrom the graveňļ, while many generations had already perished before me ňĻfor goodňļ?‚ÄĚ The parents cry to Allah for help, ňĻand warn their child,ňļ ‚ÄúPity you. Have faith! Surely Allah‚Äôs promise is true.‚ÄĚ But the deniers insist, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but ancient fables.‚ÄĚ

46:18.text: These are the ones against whom the fate of earlier communities of jinn and humans has been justified, ňĻforňļ they were truly losers.

46:19.text: Each ňĻof the two groupsňļ will be ranked according to what they have done so He may fully reward all. And none will be wronged.

46:20.text: ňĻWatch forňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the disbelievers will be exposed to the Fire. ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúYou ňĻalreadyňļ exhausted your ňĻshare ofňļ pleasures during your worldly life, and ňĻfullyňļ enjoyed them. So Today you will be rewarded with the torment of disgrace for your arrogance throughout the land with no right, and for your rebelliousness.‚ÄĚ

46:21.text: And remember the brother of ‚Äô»ād, when he warned his people, who inhabited the sand-hills<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒthere were certainly warners before and after him‚ÄĒňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúWorship none but Allah. I truly fear for you the torment of a tremendous day.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The people of ‚Äô»ād were the residents of the hills in the southern part of the Arabian Peninsula.

46:22.text: They argued, ‚ÄúHave you come to turn us away from our gods? Bring us then whatever you threaten us with, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

46:23.text: He responded, ‚ÄúThe knowledge ňĻof its timeňļ is only with Allah. I only convey to you what I have been sent with. But I can see that you are a people acting ignorantly.‚ÄĚ

46:24.text: Then when they saw the torment as a ňĻdenseňļ cloud approaching their valleys, they said ňĻhappilyňļ, ‚ÄúThis is a cloud bringing us rain.‚ÄĚ ňĻBut H√Ľd replied,ňļ ‚ÄúNo, it is what you sought to hasten: a ňĻfierceňļ wind carrying a painful punishment!‚ÄĚ

46:25.text: It destroyed everything by the command of its Lord, leaving nothing visible except their ruins. This is how We reward the wicked people.

46:26.text: Indeed, We had established them<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†in a way We have not established you ňĻMeccansňļ. And We gave them hearing, sight, and intellect. But neither their hearing, sight, nor intellect were of any benefit to them whatsoever, since they persisted in denying Allah‚Äôs signs. And ňĻsoňļ they were overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.
footnotes: ¬†The people of H√Ľd.

46:27.text: We certainly destroyed the societies around you after having varied the signs so perhaps they would return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.

46:28.text: Why then were they not helped by those ňĻidolsňļ they took as gods besides Allah, hoping to get closer ňĻto Himňļ? Instead, they failed them. That is ňĻthe result ofňļ their lies and their fabrications.

46:29.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when We sent a group of jinn your way to listen to the Quran. Then, upon hearing it, they said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúListen quietly!‚ÄĚ Then when it was over, they returned to their fellow jinn as warners.

46:30.text: They declared, ‚ÄúO our fellow jinn! We have truly heard a scripture revealed after Moses, confirming what came before it. It guides to the truth and the Straight Way.

46:31.text: O ¬†our fellow jinn! Respond to the caller of Allah and believe in him, He will forgive your sins and protect you from a painful punishment.

46:32.text: And whoever does not respond to the caller of Allah will have no escape on earth, nor will they have any protectors against Him. It is they who are clearly astray.‚ÄĚ

46:33.text: Do they not realize that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth and did not tire in creating them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is able to give life to the dead? Yes ňĻindeedňļ! He is certainly Most Capable of everything.
footnotes: ¬†Or never failed in creating them.

46:34.text: And on the Day the disbelievers will be exposed to the Fire, ňĻthey will be asked,ňļ ‚ÄúIs this ňĻHereafterňļ not the truth?‚ÄĚ They will cry, ‚ÄúAbsolutely, by our Lord!‚ÄĚ It will be said, ‚ÄúThen taste the punishment for your disbelief.‚ÄĚ

46:35.text: So endure patiently, as did the Messengers of Firm Resolve.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And do not ňĻseek toňļ hasten ňĻthe tormentňļ for the deniers. On the Day they see what they have been threatened with, it will be as if they had only stayed ňĻin this worldňļ for an hour of a day.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†ňĻThis isňļ a ňĻsufficientňļ warning! Then, will anyone be destroyed except the rebellious people?
footnotes: ¬†Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and MuŠł•ammad are called the Messengers of Firm Resolve.
¬†i.e., only for a very short period of time.

47:1.text: Those who disbelieve and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, He will render their deeds void.

47:2.text: As for those who believe, do good, and have faith in what has been revealed to MuŠł•ammad‚ÄĒwhich is the truth from their Lord‚ÄĒHe will absolve them of their sins and improve their condition.

47:3.text: This is because the disbelievers follow falsehood, while the believers follow the truth from their Lord. This is how Allah shows people their true state ňĻof faithňļ.

47:4.text: So when you meet the disbelievers ňĻin battleňļ, strike ňĻtheirňļ necks until you have thoroughly subdued them, then bind them firmly. Later ňĻfree them either asňļ an act of grace or by ransom until the war comes to an end. So will it be. Had Allah willed, He ňĻHimselfňļ could have inflicted punishment on them. But He does ňĻthis only toňļ test some of you by means of others. And those who are martyred in the cause of Allah,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He will never render their deeds void.
footnotes: ¬†Another possible interpretation: ‚ÄúAnd those who fight in the cause of Allah.‚ÄĚ

47:5.text: He will guide them ňĻto their rewardňļ, improve their condition,

47:6.text: and admit them into Paradise, having made it known to them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Once in Paradise, the believers will recognize their mansions just like they recognized their homes in the world.

47:7.text: O believers! If you stand up for Allah, He will help you and make your steps firm.

47:8.text: As for the disbelievers, may they be doomed and may He render their deeds void.

47:9.text: That is because they detest what Allah has revealed, so He has rendered their deeds void.

47:10.text: Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those before them? Allah annihilated them, and a similar fate awaits the disbelievers.

47:11.text: This is because Allah is the Patron of the believers while the disbelievers have no patron.

47:12.text: Surely Allah will admit those who believe and do good into Gardens under which rivers flow. As for the disbelievers, they enjoy themselves and feed like cattle.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But the Fire will be their home.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, the disbelievers enjoy their life to the fullest while they are heedless of what will happen to them in the Hereafter, just like the cattle that graze in the pasture are unaware that they will probably end up in the slaughter house.

47:13.text: ňĻImagine, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ how many societies We destroyed that were far superior in might than your society‚ÄĒwhich drove you out‚ÄĒand there was none to help them!

47:14.text: Can those ňĻbelieversňļ who stand on clear proof from their Lord be like those whose evil deeds are made appealing to them and ňĻonlyňļ follow their desires?

47:15.text: The description of the Paradise promised to the righteous is that in it are rivers of fresh water, rivers of milk that never changes in taste, rivers of wine delicious to drink, and rivers of pure honey. There they will ňĻalsoňļ have all kinds of fruit, and forgiveness from their Lord. ňĻCan they beňļ like those who will stay in the Fire forever, left to drink boiling water that will tear apart their insides?

47:16.text: There are some of them who listen to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, but when they depart from you, they say ňĻmockinglyňļ to those ňĻbelieversňļ gifted with knowledge, ‚ÄúWhat did he just say?‚ÄĚ These are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed and who ňĻonlyňļ follow their desires.

47:17.text: As for those who are ňĻrightlyňļ guided, He increases them in guidance and blesses them with righteousness.

47:18.text: Are they only waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise? Yet ňĻsome ofňļ its signs have already come.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Once it actually befalls them, will it not be too late to be mindful?
footnotes: ¬†The coming of MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) is one of the signs of the Hour.

47:19.text: So, know ňĻwell, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ that there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah. And seek forgiveness for your shortcomings<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and for ňĻthe sins ofňļ the believing men and women. For Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows your movements and places of rest ňĻO peopleňļ.
footnotes: ¬†Like other prophets, MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) was infallible of sin. The verse here refers to misjudgments, such as the example given in 80:1-10. If the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) himself is urged to seek forgiveness, then the believers are even more in need of praying for Allah‚Äôs forgiveness.

47:20.text: And the believers say, ‚ÄúIf only a s√Ľrah¬†was revealed ňĻallowing self-defenceňļ!‚ÄĚ Yet when a precise s√Ľrah¬†is revealed, in which fighting is ňĻexplicitlyňļ mentioned, you see those with sickness in their hearts staring at you like someone in the throes of death. It would have been better for them

47:22.text: Now if you ňĻhypocritesňļ turn away, perhaps you would then spread corruption throughout the land and sever your ňĻties ofňļ kinship!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†As you did before Islam.

47:25.text: Indeed, those who relapse ňĻinto disbeliefňļ after ňĻtrueňļ guidance has become clear to them, ňĻit isňļ Satan ňĻthatňļ has tempted them, luring them with false hopes.

footnotes: ¬†i.e., your opposition to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

47:27.text: Then how ňĻhorribleňļ will it be when the angels take their souls, beating their faces and backs!

47:28.text: This is because they follow whatever displeases Allah and hate whatever pleases Him, so He has rendered their deeds void.

47:29.text: Or do those with sickness in their hearts think that Allah will not ňĻbe able toňļ expose their malice?

47:30.text: Had We willed, We could have truly shown them to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, and you would have certainly recognized them by their appearance. But you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech. And Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows your doings ňĻO peopleňļ.

47:31.text: We will certainly test you ňĻbelieversňļ until We prove those of you who ňĻtrulyňļ struggle ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ and remain steadfast, and reveal how you conduct yourselves.

47:32.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve, hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, and defy the Messenger after ňĻtrueňļ guidance has become clear to them; they will not harm Allah in the least, but He will render their deeds void.

47:33.text: O believers! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and do not let your deeds be in vain.

47:34.text: Surely those who disbelieve, hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, and then die as disbelievers; Allah will never forgive them.

47:35.text: So do not falter or cry for peace, for you will have the upper hand and Allah is with you. And He will never let your deeds go to waste.

47:36.text: This worldly life is no more than play and amusement. But if you are faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, He will grant you your ňĻfullňļ reward, and will not ask you ňĻto donate allňļ your wealth.

47:37.text: If He were to do so and pressure you, you would withhold and He would bring out your resentment.

47:38.text: Here you are, being invited to donate ňĻa littleňļ in the cause of Allah. Still some of you withhold. And whoever does so, it is only to their own loss. For Allah is the Self-Sufficient, whereas you stand in need ňĻof Himňļ. If you ňĻstillňļ turn away, He will replace you with another people. And they will not be like you.

48:1.text: Indeed, We have granted you a clear triumph ňĻO Prophetňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This Medinian s√Ľrah takes its name from the clear triumph (i.e., Treaty of Šł§udaibiyah) in verse 1. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and 1400 of his companions travelled to Mecca to perform the minor pilgrimage (‚Äôumrah) in 6 A.H./628 C.E. He (Ô∑ļ) sent ‚ÄôUthm√Ęn ibn ‚ÄôAff√Ęn to let the Meccans know that the Muslims had come in peace, only to visit the Sacred House. When the Meccans delayed ‚ÄôUthm√Ęn, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) thought they might have killed his envoy. So he (Ô∑ļ) called upon the faithful to pledge allegiance to him under a tree at Šł§udaibiyah in the outskirts of Mecca. Shortly after, ‚ÄôUthm√Ęn returned safely and a peace agreement was signed by the Muslims and Meccan pagans, stating in part that the Muslims would have to return to Medina and come back next year for ‚Äôumrah. The Treaty of Šł§udaibiyah is described as a clear triumph since it established peace, temporarily diffused the tension between the Muslims and the Meccan pagans, and gave the Muslims plenty of time to spread awareness and understanding of their faith. Thousands from different tribes accepted Islam during that truce.

48:2.text: so that Allah may forgive you for your past and future shortcomings,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†perfect His favour upon you, guide you along the Straight Path,
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 47:19.

48:5.text: So He may admit believing men and women¬†into Gardens under which rivers flow‚ÄĒto stay there forever‚ÄĒand absolve them of their sins. And that is a supreme achievement in the sight of Allah.

48:6.text: Also ňĻso thatňļ He may punish hypocrite men and women and polytheistic men and women, who harbour evil thoughts of Allah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†May ill-fate befall them! Allah is displeased with them. He has condemned them and prepared for them Hell. What an evil destination!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., that Allah will not support His Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and that misfortune will befall the believers.

48:7.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belong the forces of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

48:8.text: Indeed, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ We have sent you as a witness, a deliverer of good news, and a warner,

48:9.text: so that you ňĻbelieversňļ may have faith in Allah and His Messenger, support and honour him, and glorify Allah morning and evening.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚Äú‚Ä¶ so that you ňĻallňļ may believe in Allah and His Messenger, support ňĻHis causeňļ, revere, and glorify Him morning and evening.‚ÄĚ

48:10.text: Surely those who pledge allegiance to you ňĻO Prophetňļ are actually pledging allegiance to Allah. Allah‚Äôs Hand is over theirs. Whoever breaks their pledge, it will only be to their own loss. And whoever fulfils their pledge to Allah, He will grant them a great reward.

48:11.text: The nomadic Arabs, who stayed behind, will say to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWe were preoccupied with our wealth and families, so ask for forgiveness for us.‚ÄĚ They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. 48:14.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whoever He wills, and punishes whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

48:15.text: Those who stayed behind will say, when you ňĻbelieversňļ set out to take the spoils of war, ‚ÄúLet us accompany you.‚ÄĚ They wish to change Allah‚Äôs promise.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYou will not accompany us. This is what Allah has said before.‚ÄĚ They will then say, ‚ÄúIn fact, you are driven by jealousy against us!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†The truth is: they can hardly comprehend.
footnotes: ¬†Those who pledged allegiance at Šł§udaibiyah were promised by Allah that the spoils of war obtained from the Tribe of Khaibar would be exclusively theirs.
¬†Meaning, you only say so to deprive us of our share.

48:16.text: Say to nomadic Arabs, who stayed behind, ‚ÄúYou will be called ňĻto fightňļ against a people of great might,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who you will fight unless they submit. If you then obey, Allah will grant you a fine reward. But if you turn away as you did before, He will inflict upon you a painful punishment.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to Haw√Ęzin and Thaq√ģf, two pagan Arab tribes.

48:18.text: Indeed, Allah was pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you ňĻO Prophetňļ under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down serenity upon them and rewarded them with a victory at hand,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The conquest of Khaibar in 7 A.H./628 C.E.

48:19.text: and many spoils of war they will gain. For Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

48:20.text: Allah has promised you ňĻbelieversňļ abundant spoils, which you will gain, so He hastened this ňĻtruceňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for you. And He has held people‚Äôs hands back from ňĻharmingňļ you, so it may be a sign for the believers, and so He may guide you along the Straight Path.
footnotes: ¬†Or the spoils of Khaibar.

48:21.text: And ňĻthere areňļ other gains which are beyond your reach that Allah is keeping in store ňĻfor youňļ. For Allah is Most Capable of everything.

48:22.text: If the disbelievers were to fight you, they would certainly flee.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Then they would never find any protector or helper.
footnotes: ¬†lit., turn their backs.

48:23.text: ňĻThis isňļ Allah‚Äôs way, already long established ňĻin the pastňļ. And you will find no change in Allah‚Äôs way.

48:24.text: He is the One Who held back their hands from you and your hands from them in the valley of ňĻŠł§udaibiyah, nearňļ Mecca, after giving you the upper hand over ňĻa group ofňļ them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†A group of Meccans wanted to attack the Muslims on their way to Mecca, but were taken captive by the Muslims, then were released.

48:25.text: They are the ones who persisted in disbelief and hindered you from the Sacred Mosque, preventing the sacrificial animals from reaching their destination.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻWe would have let you march through Mecca,ňļ had there not been believing men and women, unknown to you. That was so Allah may admit into His mercy whoever He wills.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Had those ňĻunknownňļ believers stood apart, We would have certainly inflicted a painful punishment on the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†The animals that Muslims had brought along to be sacrificed after completing the rituals of the minor pilgrimage (‚Äôumrah).
¬†By doing so, the Meccan Muslims who were unknown to their fellow believers were safe, along with the pagans who later accepted Islam.

48:26.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the disbelievers had filled their hearts with pride‚ÄĒthe pride of ňĻpre-Islamicňļ ignorance<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒthen Allah sent down His serenity upon His Messenger and the believers, inspiring them to uphold the declaration of faith,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†for they were better entitled and more worthy of it. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.
footnotes: ¬†This is when the pagans prevented the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and the believers from entering the Sacred House for ‚Äôumrah¬†and the arrogance they showed when drafting the peace agreement.
¬†The declaration of faith that there is only one God to be worshipped and that MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) is His Messenger.

48:27.text: Indeed, Allah will fulfil His Messenger‚Äôs vision in all truth: Allah willing, you will surely enter the Sacred Mosque, in security‚ÄĒňĻsome withňļ heads shaved and ňĻothers withňļ hair shortened‚ÄĒwithout fear.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He knew what you did not know, so He first granted you the triumph at hand.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Pilgrims are required to shave or trim their hair upon successfully completing the rituals of pilgrimage.
¬†This refers to the Treaty of Šł§udaibiyah and/or the gains of Khaibar before the fulfilment of the Prophet‚Äôs vision to enter Mecca for ‚Äôumrah.

48:28.text: He is the One Who has sent His Messenger with ňĻrightňļ guidance and the religion of truth, making it prevail over all others. And sufficient is Allah as a Witness.

48:29.text: MuŠł•ammad is the Messenger of Allah. And those with him are firm with the disbelievers<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and compassionate with one another. You see them bowing and prostrating<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†ňĻin prayerňļ, seeking Allah‚Äôs bounty and pleasure. The sign ňĻof brightness can be seenňļ on their faces from the trace of prostrating ňĻin prayerňļ. This is their description in the Torah.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†And their parable in the Gospel is that of a seed that sprouts its ňĻtinyňļ branches, making it strong. Then it becomes thick, standing firmly on its stem, to the delight of the planters<sup foot_note=4>4</sup>‚ÄĒin this way Allah makes the believers a source of dismay for the disbelievers.<sup foot_note=5>5</sup>¬†To those of them who believe and do good, Allah has promised forgiveness and a great reward.
footnotes: ¬†This passage and the next should be understood in their historical context.
