¬†Prostration means lying down on one‚Äôs face (i.e., touching the ground with the forehead and nose while resting on knees and flat palms of both hands) as an act of prayer and submission to Allah.

¬†In Deuteronomy 33:1-3, ňĻMoses proclaimed:ňļ ‚ÄúThe Lord came from Sinai. Rising from Seir upon us, he shone forth from Mount Paran, accompanied by a myriad of his holy ones, with flaming fire from his right hand for them. Indeed, lover of people, all of his holy ones are in your control. They gather at your feet to do as you have instructed. [sic]‚ÄĚ Paran is a mountain in Mecca.

¬†In Matthew 13:3-9, ‚ÄúňĻJesusňļ spoke many things to them in parables, saying, ‚ÄúBehold, the sower went out to sow. ‚Ä¶ ňĻA seedňļ fell on the good soil and yielded a crop, some a hundredfold, some sixty, and some thirty. ‚ÄúHe who has ears, let him hear.‚ÄĚ‚Äô Or the parable of the growing seed in Mark 4:26-28.
¬†See¬†the first footnote for 48:29.

49:1.text: O believers! Do not proceed ňĻin any matterňļ before ňĻa decree fromňļ Allah and His Messenger. And fear Allah. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

49:2.text: O believers! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, nor speak loudly to him as you do to one another,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†or your deeds will become void while you are unaware.
footnotes: ¬†There is also a different interpretation: ‚Äú‚Ä¶ nor call him ňĻby his nameňļ as you call one another.‚ÄĚ In other words, Do not say, ‚ÄėO MuŠł•ammad.‚Äô Rather, say, ‚ÄėO Prophet.‚Äô

49:3.text: Indeed, those who lower their voices in the presence of Allah‚Äôs Messenger are the ones whose hearts Allah has refined for righteousness. They will have forgiveness and a great reward.

49:4.text: Indeed, most of those who call out to you ňĻO Prophetňļ from outside ňĻyourňļ private quarters have no understanding ňĻof mannersňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†A group from the tribe of Bani Tam√ģm came to see the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) while he was taking a nap in the afternoon. They stood outside the homes of his wives and started calling him out loud.

49:5.text: Had they been patient until you could come out to them, it would have certainly been better for them. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

49:6.text: O believers, if an evildoer brings you any news, verify ňĻitňļ so you do not harm people unknowingly, becoming regretful for what you have done.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Al-Wal√ģd ibn ‚ÄôUqbah ibn Abi Mu‚ÄôaiŠĻ≠ was sent by the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) to collect alms-tax from Bani Al-MuŠĻ£ŠĻ≠aliq. They went out to receive Al-Wal√ģd but he assumed they wanted to harm him. Therefore, he quickly returned to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) with the bad news so he would punish them. Shortly after, a delegation from Bani Al-MuŠĻ£ŠĻ≠aliq came to clarify the misunderstanding.

49:7.text: And keep in mind that Allah‚Äôs Messenger is ňĻstillňļ in your midst. If he were to yield to you in many matters, you would surely suffer ňĻthe consequencesňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But Allah has endeared faith to you, making it appealing in your hearts. And He has made disbelief, rebelliousness, and disobedience detestable to you. Those are the ones rightly guided.
footnotes: ¬†Or things would have been difficult for you.

49:8.text: ňĻThis isňļ a bounty and a blessing from Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

49:9.text: And if two groups of believers fight each other, then make peace between them. But if one of them transgresses against the other, then fight against the transgressing group until they ňĻare willing toňļ submit to the rule of Allah. If they do so, then make peace between both ňĻgroupsňļ in all fairness and act justly. Surely Allah loves those who uphold justice.

49:10.text: The believers are but one brotherhood, so make peace between your brothers. And be mindful of Allah so you may be shown mercy.

49:11.text: O believers! Do not let some ňĻmenňļ ridicule others, they may be better than them, nor let ňĻsomeňļ women ridicule other women, they may be better than them. Do not defame one another, nor call each other by offensive nicknames. How evil it is to act rebelliously after having faith! And whoever does not repent, it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.

49:12.text: O ¬†believers! Avoid many suspicions, ňĻforňļ indeed, some suspicions are sinful. And do not spy, nor backbite one another. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of their dead brother? You would despise that!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And fear Allah. Surely Allah is ňĻtheňļ Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†Backbiting people is likened to eating their dead bodies. So if eating someone‚Äôs flesh is detestable, then backbiting them is no different.

49:13.text: O humanity! Indeed, We created you from a male and a female, and made you into peoples and tribes so that you may ňĻget toňļ know one another. Surely the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous among you. Allah is truly All-Knowing, All-Aware.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Im√Ęm AŠł•med to have said, "O humanity! Your Lord is one, and your ancestry is one. No Arab is superior to a non-Arab, nor is any non-Arab superior to any Arab. No white is superior to any black, nor is any black superior to any white except on account of their righteousness."

49:14.text: ňĻSome ofňļ the nomadic Arabs say, ‚ÄúWe believe.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYou have not believed. But say, ‚ÄėWe have submitted,‚Äô for faith has not yet entered your hearts.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But if you obey Allah and His Messenger ňĻwholeheartedlyňļ, He will not discount anything from ňĻthe reward ofňļ your deeds. Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The verse makes a distinction between Islam¬†(the declaration of accepting the faith, then performing Islamic duties such as prayers and fasting) and √ém√Ęn¬†(a higher state of Islam, which means to embrace the faith wholeheartedly). This distinction is very clear in a famous narration from the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), commonly known as the Šł§ad√ģth¬†of Gabriel.

49:15.text: The ňĻtrueňļ believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger‚ÄĒnever doubting‚ÄĒand strive with their wealth and their lives in the cause of Allah. They are the ones true in faith.

49:16.text: Say, ‚ÄúDo you inform Allah of your faith, when Allah ňĻalreadyňļ knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.‚ÄĚ

49:17.text: They regard their acceptance of Islam as a favour to you. Tell ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúDo not regard your Islam as a favour to me. Rather, it is Allah Who has done you a favour by guiding you to the faith, if ňĻindeedňļ you are faithful.

49:18.text: Surely Allah knows the unseen of the heavens and earth. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.‚ÄĚ

5:1.text: O believers! Honour your obligations. All grazing livestock has been made lawful to you‚ÄĒexcept what is hereby announced to you and hunting while on pilgrimage. Indeed, Allah commands what He wills.

5:2.text: O believers! Do not violate Allah‚Äôs rituals ňĻof pilgrimageňļ, the sacred months, the sacrificial animals, the ňĻofferings decorated withňļ garlands, nor those ňĻpilgrimsňļ on their way to the Sacred House seeking their Lord‚Äôs bounty and pleasure. When pilgrimage has ended, you are allowed to hunt. Do not let the hatred of a people who once barred you from the Sacred Mosque provoke you to transgress. Cooperate with one another in goodness and righteousness, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression. And be mindful of Allah. Surely Allah is severe in punishment.

5:3.text: Forbidden to you are carrion, blood, and swine; what is slaughtered in the name of any other than Allah; what is killed by strangling, beating, a fall, or by being gored to death; what is partly eaten by a predator unless you slaughter it; and what is sacrificed on altars. You are also forbidden to draw lots for decisions.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is all evil. Today the disbelievers have given up all hope of ňĻunderminingňļ your faith. So do not fear them; fear Me! Today I have perfected your faith for you, completed My favour upon you, and chosen Islam as your way. But whoever is compelled by extreme hunger‚ÄĒnot intending to sin‚ÄĒthen surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†To make a decision in pre-Islamic Arabia, a person would draw one of three straws: one saying ‚ÄúDo it,‚ÄĚ the other ‚ÄúDo not do it,‚ÄĚ and the third was left blank, whereas in Islam there is a special prayer (called istikh√Ęrah) for guidance when making a decision.

5:4.text: They ask you, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ what is permissible for them ňĻto eatňļ. Say, ‚ÄúWhat is good and lawful. Also what is caught by your hunting animals and birds of prey which you have trained as instructed by Allah. So eat what they catch for you, but mention the Name of Allah over it ňĻfirstňļ.‚ÄĚ And be mindful of Allah. Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.

5:5.text: Today all good, pure foods have been made lawful for you. Similarly, the food of the People of the Book<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is permissible for you and yours is permissible for them. And ňĻpermissible for you in marriageňļ are chaste believing women as well as chaste women of those given the Scripture before you‚ÄĒas long as you pay them their dowries in wedlock, neither fornicating nor taking them as mistresses. And whoever rejects the faith, all their good deeds will be void ňĻin this lifeňļ and in the Hereafter they will be among the losers.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúThe food of the People of the Book‚ÄĚ here means the meat of the animals slaughtered by the Jews and Christians.

5:6.text: O believers! When you rise up for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, wipe your heads, and wash your feet to the ankles. And if you are in a state of ňĻfullňļ impurity,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then take a full bath. But if you are ill, on a journey, or have relieved yourselves, or have been intimate with your wives and cannot find water, then purify yourselves with clean earth by wiping your faces and hands.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†It is not Allah‚Äôs Will to burden you, but to purify you and complete His favour upon you, so perhaps you will be grateful.
footnotes: ¬†For example, following sexual intercourse or a wet dream.
¬†This ruling is called tayammum¬†or ‚Äėdry ablution.‚Äô See¬†footnote for 4:43.

5:7.text: Remember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you and the covenant He made with you when you said, ‚ÄúWe hear and obey.‚ÄĚ And be mindful of Allah. Surely Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

5:8.text: O believers! Stand firm for Allah and bear true testimony. Do not let the hatred of a people lead you to injustice. Be just! That is closer to righteousness. And be mindful of Allah. Surely Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

5:9.text: Allah has promised those who believe and do good ňĻHisňļ forgiveness and a great reward.

5:10.text: As for those who disbelieve and deny Our signs, they are the residents of the Hellfire.

5:11.text: O believers! Remember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you: when a people sought to harm you, but He held their hands back from you. Be mindful of Allah. And in Allah let the believers put their trust.

5:12.text: Allah made a covenant with the Children of Israel and appointed twelve leaders from among them and ňĻthenňļ said, ‚ÄúI am truly with you. If you establish prayer, pay alms-tax, believe in My messengers, support them, and lend to Allah a good loan, I will certainly forgive your sins and admit you into Gardens under which rivers flow. And whoever among you disbelieves afterwards has truly strayed from the Right Way.‚ÄĚ

5:13.text: But for breaking their covenant We condemned them and hardened their hearts. They distorted the words of the Scripture and neglected a portion of what they had been commanded to uphold. You ňĻO Prophetňļ will always find deceit on their part, except for a few. But pardon them and bear with them. Indeed, Allah loves the good-doers.

5:14.text: And from those who say, ‚ÄúWe are Christians,‚ÄĚ We took their covenant, but they neglected a portion of what they had been commanded to uphold. So We let hostility and enmity arise between them until the Day of Judgment, and soon Allah will inform them of all they have done.

5:15.text: O People of the Book! Now Our Messenger has come to you, revealing much of what you have hidden of the Scriptures and disregarding much. There certainly has come to you from Allah a light and a clear Book

5:16.text: through which Allah guides those who seek His pleasure to the ways of peace, brings them out of darkness and into light by His Will, and guides them to the Straight Path.

5:17.text: Indeed, those who say, ‚ÄúAllah is the Messiah, son of Mary,‚ÄĚ have fallen into disbelief. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWho has the power to prevent Allah if He chose to destroy the Messiah, son of Mary, his mother, and everyone in the world all together?‚ÄĚ To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between. He creates whatever He wills. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

5:18.text: The Jews and the Christians each say, ‚ÄúWe are the children of Allah and His most beloved!‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhy then does He punish you for your sins? No! You are only humans like others of His Own making. He forgives whoever He wills and punishes whoever He wills. To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between. And to Him is the final return.‚ÄĚ

5:19.text: O People of the Book! Our Messenger has indeed come to you, making things clear to you after an interval between the messengers so you do not say, ‚ÄúThere has never come to us a deliverer of good news or a warner.‚ÄĚ Now there has come to you a deliverer of good news and a warner. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

5:20.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Moses said to his people, ‚ÄúO my people! Remember Allah‚Äôs favours upon you when He raised prophets from among you, made you sovereign,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and gave you what He had never given anyone in the world.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†He rescued you from Egypt where the Pharaoh treated you like slaves and blessed you with freedom so you can manage your own affairs.
¬†i.e., the miracles of splitting the sea, water coming out of the rock, manna and quails, and clouds shading them.

5:21.text: O ¬†my people! Enter the Holy Land which Allah has destined for you ňĻto enterňļ. And do not turn back or else you will become losers.‚ÄĚ

5:22.text: They replied, ‚ÄúO Moses! There is an enormously powerful people there, so we will never ňĻbe able toňļ enter it until they leave. If they do, then we will enter!‚ÄĚ

5:23.text: Two God-fearing men‚ÄĒwho had been blessed by Allah‚ÄĒsaid, ‚ÄúSurprise them through the gate. If you do, you will certainly prevail. Put your trust in Allah if you are ňĻtrulyňļ believers.‚ÄĚ

5:24.text: ňĻYetňļ they said, ‚ÄúO Moses! ňĻStillňļ we will never enter as long as they remain there. So go‚ÄĒboth you and your Lord‚ÄĒand fight; we are staying right here!‚ÄĚ

5:25.text: Moses pleaded, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have no control over anyone except myself and my brother. So set us apart from the rebellious people.‚ÄĚ

5:26.text: Allah replied, ‚ÄúThen this land is forbidden to them for forty years, during which they will wander through the land. So do not grieve for the rebellious people.‚ÄĚ

5:27.text: Relate to them in truth ňĻO Prophetňļ the story of Adam‚Äôs two sons‚ÄĒhow each offered a sacrifice: one‚Äôs offering was accepted while the other‚Äôs was not, so he threatened ňĻhis brotherňļ, ‚ÄúI will kill you!‚ÄĚ His brother replied, ‚ÄúAllah only accepts ňĻthe offeringňļ of the sincerely devout.

5:28.text: If you raise your hand to kill me, I will not raise mine to kill you, because I fear Allah‚ÄĒthe Lord of all worlds.

5:29.text: I want to let you bear your sin against me along with your other sins, then you will be one of those destined to the Fire. And that is the reward of the wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

5:30.text: Still, the other convinced himself to kill his own brother, so he killed him‚ÄĒbecoming a loser.

5:31.text: Then Allah sent a crow digging ňĻa graveňļ in the ground ňĻfor a dead crowňļ, in order to show him how to bury the corpse of his brother. He cried, ‚ÄúAlas! Have I ňĻevenňļ failed to be like this crow and bury the corpse of my brother?‚ÄĚ So he became regretful.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†In Islam, regret for doing something wrong is essential for repentance. But in the case of Cain, his regret was not for killing his brother, but for failing to bury his corpse to hide the evidence of his crime. Therefore, his regret was not intended as a step towards repentance.

5:32.text: That is why We ordained for the Children of Israel that whoever takes a life‚ÄĒunless as a punishment for murder or mischief in the land‚ÄĒit will be as if they killed all of humanity; and whoever saves a life, it will be as if they saved all of humanity.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻAlthoughňļ Our messengers already came to them with clear proofs, many of them still transgressed afterwards through the land.
footnotes: ¬†Although this is addressed to the Children of Israel, it is applicable to everyone at all times.

5:33.text: Indeed, the penalty for those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger and spread mischief in the land is death, crucifixion, cutting off their hands and feet on opposite sides, or exile from the land. This ňĻpenaltyňļ is a disgrace for them in this world, and they will suffer a tremendous punishment in the Hereafter.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes:
¬†This ruling (called Šł•ir√Ębah) applies to crimes committed by armed individuals or groups against civilians‚ÄĒMuslim or non-Muslim. Different punishments apply depending on the severity of the crime:

  1. In the case of murder or rape, offenders are to be executed.
  2. In the case of armed robbery, offenders‚Äô right hands and left feet are to be cut off.
  3. In the case of terrorizing innocent people, offenders are to be jailed in exile.
  4. Penalties for lesser offences are left for the judge to decide.

5:34.text: As for those who repent before you seize them, then know that Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

5:35.text: O believers! Be mindful of Allah and seek what brings you closer to Him and struggle in His Way, so you may be successful.

5:36.text: As for the disbelievers, even if they were to possess everything in the world twice over ňĻand offer it allňļ to ransom themselves from the punishment of the Day of Judgment, it would never be accepted from them. And they will suffer a painful punishment.

5:37.text: They will be desperate to get out of the Fire but they will never be able to. And they will suffer an everlasting punishment.

5:38.text: As for male and female thieves, cut off their hands for what they have done‚ÄĒa deterrent from Allah. And Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†In Islam, strict conditions must be met for punishments to be carried out. For example, for someone to be punished for theft, the thief must be a sane adult who knows that theft is forbidden, the crime has to be proven either by confession or the testimony of two reliable eyewitnesses, the stolen item has to be of value and taken secretly from a safe place, and the owner has to claim it. Otherwise, the punishment is not applied. Islamic punishments apply neither in non-Muslim countries nor in Muslim countries where Shar√ģ‚Äôah¬†is not fully applied. Moreover, the punishment is only applicable in a society where the needs of the poor are adequately met (i.e., they receive alms-tax, charity, or welfare) and where theft is more out of desire than necessity. It is worth mentioning that ‚ÄôUmar ibn Al-KhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠√Ęb, Islam‚Äôs 2nd¬†Caliph, suspended this punishment for one year due to widespread famine.

5:39.text: But whoever repents after their wrongdoing and mends their ways, Allah will surely turn to them in forgiveness. Indeed, Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

5:40.text: Do you not know that the kingdom of the heavens and the earth belongs to Allah ňĻaloneňļ? He punishes whoever He wills and forgives whoever He wills. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

5:41.text: O Messenger! Do not grieve for those who race to disbelieve‚ÄĒthose who say, ‚ÄúWe believe‚ÄĚ with their tongues, but their hearts are in disbelief. Nor those among the Jews who eagerly listen to lies, attentive to those who are too arrogant to come to you. They distort the Scripture, taking rulings out of context, then say, ‚ÄúIf this is the ruling you get ňĻfrom MuŠł•ammadňļ, accept it. If not, beware!‚ÄĚ Whoever Allah allows to be deluded, you can never be of any help to them against Allah. It is not Allah‚Äôs Will to purify their hearts. For them is disgrace in this world, and they will suffer a tremendous punishment in the Hereafter.

5:42.text: They eagerly listen to falsehood and consume forbidden gain.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So if they come to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, either judge between them or turn away from them. If you turn away from them, they cannot harm you whatsoever. But if you judge between them, then do so with justice. Surely Allah loves those who are just.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., bribes and interest.

5:43.text: But why do they come to you for judgment when they ňĻalreadyňļ have the Torah containing Allah‚Äôs judgment, then they turn away after all? They are not ňĻtrueňļ believers.

5:44.text: Indeed, We revealed the Torah, containing guidance and light, by which the prophets, who submitted themselves to Allah, made judgments for Jews. So too did the rabbis and scholars judge according to Allah‚Äôs Book, with which they were entrusted and of which they were made keepers. So do not fear the people; fear Me! Nor trade my revelations for a fleeting gain. And those who do not judge by what Allah has revealed are ňĻtrulyňļ the disbelievers.

5:45.text: We ordained for them in the Torah, ‚ÄúA life for a life, an eye for an eye, a nose for a nose, an ear for an ear, a tooth for a tooth‚ÄĒand for wounds equal retaliation.‚ÄĚ But whoever waives it charitably, it will be atonement for them. And those who do not judge by what Allah has revealed are ňĻtrulyňļ the wrongdoers.

5:46.text: Then in the footsteps of the prophets, We sent Jesus, son of Mary, confirming the Torah revealed before him. And We gave him the Gospel containing guidance and light and confirming what was revealed in the Torah‚ÄĒa guide and a lesson to the God-fearing.

5:47.text: So let the people of the Gospel judge by what Allah has revealed in it. And those who do not judge by what Allah has revealed are ňĻtrulyňļ the rebellious.

5:48.text: We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ this Book with the truth, as a confirmation of previous Scriptures and a supreme authority on them. So judge between them by what Allah has revealed, and do not follow their desires over the truth that has come to you. To each of you We have ordained a code of law and a way of life. If Allah had willed, He would have made you one community, but His Will is to test you with what He has given ňĻeach ofňļ you. So compete with one another in doing good. To Allah you will all return, then He will inform you ňĻof the truthňļ regarding your differences.

5:49.text: And judge between them ňĻO Prophetňļ by what Allah has revealed, and do not follow their desires. And beware, so they do not lure you away from some of what Allah has revealed to you. If they turn away ňĻfrom Allah‚Äôs judgmentňļ, then know that it is Allah‚Äôs Will to repay them for some of their sins, and that many people are indeed rebellious.

5:50.text: Is it the judgment of ňĻpre-Islamicňļ ignorance they seek? Who could be a better judge than Allah for people of sure faith?

5:51.text: O believers! Take neither Jews nor Christians as guardians‚ÄĒthey are guardians of each other.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Whoever does so will be counted as one of them. Surely Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
footnotes: ¬†Wali¬†means guardian, caretaker, protector, provider, ally, etc. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) for example says, ‚ÄúA woman should not get married without a wali.‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒthat is a guardian such as her father to represent her in the marriage and make sure she receives her full rights. The verse forbids Muslims from allying with the Jews and Christians (or anyone else) conspiring to persecute Muslims. On the other hand, as long as non-Muslims are not at war with Muslims or persecuting them, the Quran (60:8) commands Muslims to be kind and gracious to them.

5:52.text: You see those with sickness in their hearts racing for their guardianship, saying ňĻin justificationňļ, ‚ÄúWe fear a turn of fortune will strike us.‚ÄĚ But perhaps Allah will bring about ňĻyourňļ victory or another favour by His command, and they will regret what they have hidden in their hearts.

5:53.text: ňĻOnly then,ňļ the believers will ask ňĻone anotherňļ, ‚ÄúAre these the ones who swore solemn oaths by Allah that they were with you?‚ÄĚ Their deeds have been in vain, so they have become losers.

5:54.text: O believers! Whoever among you abandons their faith, Allah will replace them with others who love Him and are loved by Him. They will be humble with the believers but firm towards the disbelievers, struggling in the Way of Allah; fearing no blame from anyone. This is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing.

5:55.text: Your only guardians are Allah, His Messenger, and fellow believers‚ÄĒwho establish prayer and pay alms-tax¬†with humility.

5:56.text: Whoever allies themselves with Allah, His Messenger, and fellow believers, then it is certainly Allah‚Äôs party that will prevail.

5:57.text: O believers! Do not seek the guardianship of those given the Scripture before you and the disbelievers who have made your faith a mockery and amusement. And be mindful of Allah if you are ňĻtrulyňļ believers.

5:58.text: When you call to prayer, they mock it in amusement. This is because they are a people without understanding.

5:59.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Do you resent us only because we believe in Allah and what has been revealed to us and what was revealed before‚ÄĒwhile most of you are rebellious?‚ÄĚ

5:60.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShall I inform you of those who deserve a worse punishment from Allah ňĻthan the rebelliousňļ? It is those who earned Allah‚Äôs condemnation and displeasure‚ÄĒsome being reduced to apes and pigs<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and worshippers of false gods. These are far worse in rank and farther astray from the Right Way.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Literally or metaphorically. See¬†footnote for 2:65.

5:61.text: When they come to you ňĻbelieversňļ they say, ‚ÄúWe believe.‚ÄĚ But they are committed to disbelief when they enter and when they leave. And Allah knows what they hide.

5:62.text: You see many of them racing towards sin, transgression, and consumption of forbidden gain. Evil indeed are their actions!

5:63.text: Why do their rabbis and scholars not forbid them from saying what is sinful and consuming what is unlawful? Evil indeed is their inaction!

5:64.text: ňĻSome amongňļ the Jews said, ‚ÄúAllah is tight-fisted.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†May their fists be tied and they be condemned for what they said. Rather, He is open-handed, giving freely as He pleases. That which has been revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ from your Lord will only cause many of them to increase in wickedness and disbelief. We have stirred among them hostility and hatred until the Day of Judgment. Whenever they kindle the fire of war, Allah puts it out. And they strive to spread corruption in the land. And Allah does not like corruptors.
footnotes: ¬†lit., Allah‚Äôs Hand is tied up ňĻwith greedňļ.

5:65.text: Had the People of the Book only been faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, We would have certainly absolved them of their sins and admitted them into the Gardens of Bliss.

5:66.text: And had they observed the Torah, the Gospel, and what has been revealed to them from their Lord, they would have been overwhelmed with provisions from above and below. Some among them are upright, yet many do nothing but evil.

5:67.text: O Messenger! Convey everything revealed to you from your Lord. If you do not, then you have not delivered His message. Allah will ňĻcertainlyňļ protect you from the people. Indeed, Allah does not guide the people who disbelieve.

5:68.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO People of the Book! You have nothing to stand on unless you observe the Torah, the Gospel, and what has been revealed to you from your Lord.‚ÄĚ And your Lord‚Äôs revelation to you ňĻO Prophetňļ will only cause many of them to increase in wickedness and disbelief. So do not grieve for the people who disbelieve.

5:69.text: Indeed, the believers, Jews, Sabians<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and Christians‚ÄĒwhoever ňĻtrulyňļ believes in Allah and the Last Day and does good, there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 2:62.

5:70.text: Indeed, We took a covenant from the Children of Israel and sent them messengers. Whenever a messenger came to them with what they did not desire, they denied some and killed others.

5:71.text: They thought there would be no consequences, so they turned a blind eye and a deaf ear. Yet Allah turned to them in forgiveness ňĻafter their repentanceňļ, but again many became blind and deaf. And Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

5:72.text: Those who say, ‚ÄúAllah is the Messiah, son of Mary,‚ÄĚ have certainly fallen into disbelief. The Messiah ňĻhimselfňļ said, ‚ÄúO Children of Israel! Worship Allah‚ÄĒmy Lord and your Lord.‚ÄĚ Whoever associates others with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ will surely be forbidden Paradise by Allah. Their home will be the Fire. And the wrongdoers will have no helpers.

5:73.text: Those who say, ‚ÄúAllah is one in a Trinity,‚ÄĚ have certainly fallen into disbelief. There is only One God. If they do not stop saying this, those who disbelieve among them will be afflicted with a painful punishment.

5:74.text: Will they not turn to Allah in repentance and seek His forgiveness? And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

5:75.text: The Messiah, son of Mary, was no more than a messenger. ňĻManyňļ messengers had ňĻcome andňļ gone before him. His mother was a woman of truth. They both ate food.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†See how We make the signs clear to them, yet see how they are deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they were in need of food for nourishment. According to some scholars, this implies that Jesus and his mother had to relieve themselves after the food had been digested. Almighty Allah is far above depending on food or having to go to the restroom.

5:76.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHow can you worship besides Allah those who can neither harm nor benefit you? And Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.‚ÄĚ

5:77.text: Say, ‚ÄúO People of the Book! Do not go to extremes in your faith beyond the truth, nor follow the vain desires of those who went astray before ňĻyouňļ. They misled many and strayed from the Right Way.‚ÄĚ

5:78.text: The disbelievers among the Children of Israel were condemned in the revelations of David and Jesus, son of Mary. That was for their disobedience and violations.

5:79.text: They did not forbid one another from doing evil. Evil indeed was what they did!

5:80.text: You see many of them taking the disbelievers<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as allies. Truly wicked are their misdeeds, which have earned them Allah‚Äôs wrath. And they will be in everlasting torment.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the pagans.

5:81.text: Had they believed in Allah, the Prophet, and what has been revealed to him, they would have never taken those ňĻpagansňļ as allies. But most of them are rebellious.

5:82.text: You will surely find the most bitter towards the believers to be the Jews and polytheists and the most gracious to be those who call themselves Christian. That is because there are priests and monks among them and because they are not arrogant.

5:83.text: When they listen to what has been revealed to the Messenger, you see their eyes overflowing with tears for recognizing the truth. They say, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We believe, so count us among the witnesses.

5:84.text: Why should we not believe in Allah and the truth that has come to us? And we long for our Lord to include us in the company of the righteous.‚ÄĚ

5:85.text: So Allah will reward them for what they said with Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever. And that is the reward of the good-doers.

5:86.text: As for those who disbelieve and reject Our signs, they will be the residents of the Hellfire.

5:87.text: O believers! Do not forbid the good things which Allah has made lawful for you, and do not transgress. Indeed, Allah does not like transgressors.

5:88.text: Eat of the good, lawful things provided to you by Allah. And be mindful of Allah in Whom you believe.

5:89.text: Allah will not call you to account for your thoughtless oaths, but He will hold you accountable for deliberate oaths. The penalty for a broken oath is to feed ten poor people from what you normally feed your own family, or to clothe them, or to free a bondsperson. But if none of this is affordable, then you must fast three days. This is the penalty for breaking your oaths. So be mindful of your oaths. This is how Allah makes things clear to you, so perhaps you will be grateful.

5:90.text: O believers! Intoxicants, gambling, idols, and drawing lots for decisions<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†are all evil of Satan‚Äôs handiwork. So shun them so you may be successful.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 5:3.

5:91.text: Satan‚Äôs plan is to stir up hostility and hatred between you with intoxicants and gambling and to prevent you from remembering Allah and praying. Will you not then abstain?

5:92.text: Obey Allah and obey the Messenger and beware! But if you turn away, then know that Our Messenger‚Äôs duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.

5:93.text: There is no blame on those who believe and do good for what they had consumed before ňĻthe prohibitionňļ, as long as they fear Allah, have faith, and do what is good; then they believe and act virtuously, then become fully mindful ňĻof Allahňļ and do righteous deeds. For Allah loves the good-doers.

5:94.text: O believers! Allah will surely test you with game within the reach of your hands and spears to distinguish those who fear Him in secret. Whoever transgresses from now on will suffer a painful punishment.

5:95.text: O ¬†believers! Do not kill game while on pilgrimage. Whoever kills game intentionally must compensate by offering its equivalence‚ÄĒas judged by two just men among you‚ÄĒto be offered at the Sacred House, or by feeding the needy, or by fasting so that they may taste the consequences of their violations. Allah has forgiven what has been done. But those who persist will be punished by Allah. And Allah is Almighty, capable of punishment.

5:96.text: It is lawful for you to hunt and eat seafood, as a provision for you and for travellers. But hunting on land is forbidden to you while on pilgrimage. Be mindful of Allah to Whom you all will be gathered.

5:97.text: Allah has made the Ka‚Äôbah‚ÄĒthe Sacred House‚ÄĒa sanctuary of well-being for all people, along with the sacred months, the sacrificial animals, and the ňĻofferings decorated withňļ garlands. All this so you may know that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and that He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of everything.

5:98.text: Know that Allah is severe in punishment and that He is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

5:99.text: The Messenger‚Äôs duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ. And Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows what you reveal and what you conceal.

5:100.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúGood and evil are not equal, though you may be dazzled by the abundance of evil. So be mindful of Allah, O ¬†people of reason, so you may be successful.‚ÄĚ

5:101.text: O believers! Do not ask about any matter which, if made clear to you, may disturb you. But if you inquire about what is being revealed in the Quran, it will be made clear to you. Allah has forgiven what was done ňĻin the pastňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Forbearing.
footnotes: ¬†This implies that either these minor details have been overlooked by Allah out of His mercy or that Allah has forgiven them for these unnecessary questions.

5:102.text: Some people before you asked such questions then denied their answers.

5:103.text: Allah has never ordained the ňĻso-calledňļ baŠł•√ģrah, s√Ę'ibah, waŠĻ£√ģlah, and Šł•√Ęm¬†camels.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But the disbelievers<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†just fabricate lies about Allah, and most of them lack understanding.
footnotes: ¬†These camels were allowed by pagan Arabs to pasture freely and were not to be used for labour or transportation after fathering or giving birth to a certain number of male or female camels.
¬†i.e., the pagans.

5:104.text: When it is said to them, ‚ÄúCome to Allah‚Äôs revelations and to the Messenger,‚ÄĚ they reply, ‚ÄúWhat we found our forefathers practicing is good enough for us.‚ÄĚ ňĻWould they still do so,ňļ even if their forefathers had absolutely no knowledge or guidance?

5:105.text: O believers! You are accountable only for yourselves.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It will not harm you if someone chooses to deviate‚ÄĒas long as you are ňĻrightlyňļ guided. To Allah you will all return, and He will inform you of what you used to do.
footnotes: ¬†After fulfilling their obligation of delivering the truth, enjoining what is good, and forbidding what is evil.

5:106.text: O believers! When death approaches any of you, call upon two just Muslim men to witness as you make a bequest; otherwise, two non-Muslims if you are afflicted with death while on a journey.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†If you doubt ňĻtheir testimonyňļ, keep them after prayer and let them testify under oath ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúBy Allah! We would never sell our testimony for any price, even in favour of a close relative, nor withhold the testimony of Allah. Otherwise, we would surely be sinful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†If no Muslim witnesses can be found.

5:107.text: If they are found guilty ňĻof false testimonyňļ, let the deceased‚Äôs two closest heirs affected by the bequest replace the witnesses and testify under oath ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúBy Allah! Our testimony is truer than theirs. We have not transgressed. Otherwise, we would surely be wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

5:108.text: In this way it is more likely that witnesses will give true testimony or else fear that their oaths could be refuted by those of the heirs. Be mindful of Allah and obey. For Allah does not guide the rebellious people.

5:109.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day Allah will gather the messengers and say, ‚ÄúWhat response did you receive?‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúWe have no knowledge ňĻcompared to Youňļ! You ňĻaloneňļ are indeed the Knower of all unseen.‚ÄĚ

5:110.text: And ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ Allah will say, ‚ÄúO Jesus, son of Mary! Remember My favour upon you and your mother: how I supported you with the holy spirit<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so you spoke to people in ňĻyourňļ infancy and adulthood. How I taught you writing, wisdom, the Torah, and the Gospel. How you moulded a bird from clay‚ÄĒby My Will‚ÄĒand breathed into it and it became a ňĻrealňļ bird‚ÄĒby My Will. How you healed the blind and the lepers‚ÄĒby My Will. How you brought the dead to life‚ÄĒby My Will. How I prevented the Children of Israel from harming you when you came to them with clear proofs and the disbelievers among them said, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but pure magic.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the angel Gabriel.

footnotes: ¬†lit., Muslims.

5:112.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the disciples asked, ‚ÄúO Jesus, son of Mary! Would your Lord be willing to send down to us a table spread with food from heaven?‚ÄĚ Jesus answered, ‚ÄúFear Allah if you are ňĻtrulyňļ believers.‚ÄĚ

5:113.text: They said, ‚ÄúWe ňĻonlyňļ wish to eat from it to reassure our hearts, to verify you are indeed truthful to us, and to become its witnesses.‚ÄĚ

5:114.text: Jesus, son of Mary, prayed, ‚ÄúO Allah, our Lord! Send us from heaven a table spread with food as a feast for us‚ÄĒthe first and last of us‚ÄĒand as a sign from You. Provide for us! You are indeed the Best Provider.‚ÄĚ

5:115.text: Allah answered, ‚ÄúI am sending it down to you. But whoever among you denies afterwards will be subjected to a torment I have never inflicted on anyone of My creation.‚ÄĚ

5:116.text: And ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ Allah will say, ‚ÄúO Jesus, son of Mary! Did you ever ask the people to worship you and your mother as gods besides Allah?‚ÄĚ He will answer, ‚ÄúGlory be to You! How could I ever say what I had no right to say? If I had said such a thing, you would have certainly known it. You know what is ňĻhiddenňļ within me, but I do not know what is within You. Indeed, You ňĻaloneňļ are the Knower of all unseen.

5:117.text: I never told them anything except what You ordered me to say: ‚ÄúWorship Allah‚ÄĒmy Lord and your Lord!‚ÄĚ And I was witness over them as long as I remained among them. But when You took me,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†You were the Witness over them‚ÄĒand You are a Witness over all things.
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the ascension of Jesus Christ. See footnote for 3:54.

5:118.text: If You punish them, they belong to You after all.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But if You forgive them, You are surely the Almighty, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., as Your creation, they cannot escape the punishment.

5:119.text: Allah will declare, ‚ÄúThis is the Day when ňĻonlyňļ the faithful will benefit from their faithfulness. Theirs are Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. That is the ultimate triumph.‚ÄĚ

5:120.text: To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything within. And He is Most Capable of everything.

footnotes: ¬†Meaning, they are confused about the Prophet (Ô∑ļ): some say he is a poet, some say he is a fabricator, while others say he is insane. They also rejected the Quran as poetry, magic, or ancient fables.

footnotes: ¬†The people of Shu‚Äôaib (Ô∑ļ).
¬†Tubba‚Äô Al-Šł§imiari was an ancient righteous Yemeni king whose people persisted in disbelief and were destroyed, although they were superior to the Meccans in strength and manpower.

51:2.text: and ňĻthe cloudsňļ loaded with rain,

footnotes: ¬†Or ‚ÄúBy the universe with its fine-tuning.‚ÄĚ

51:11.text: those who are ňĻsteepedňļ in ignorance, totally heedless.

footnotes: ¬†As mentioned in 11:69-70, the angels came to Abraham in the form of men and when he offered them food, they did not eat. In ancient Middle Eastern culture, if a guest refused to eat the food provided by their host, it was a sign of ill-will.
¬†Prophet Isaac (Ô∑ļ).

51:29.text: Then his wife came forward with a cry, clasping her forehead ňĻin astonishmentňļ, exclaiming, ‚ÄúňĻA baby fromňļ a barren, old woman!‚ÄĚ

51:31.text: ňĻLater,ňļ Abraham asked, ‚ÄúWhat is your mission, O ¬†messenger-angels?‚ÄĚ

footnotes: ¬†Lot (Ô∑ļ) and his two daughters.

51:44.text: Still they persisted in defying the commands of their Lord, so they were overtaken by a ňĻmightyňļ blast while they were looking on.

footnotes: ¬†Where Moses (Ô∑ļ) communicated with Allah.

52:3.text: on open pages ňĻfor all to readňļ!<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>

footnotes: ¬†Arab pagans were challenged in the Quran to produce its equivalent¬†(52:34), or ten s√Ľrahs¬†(11:13), or at least one s√Ľrah¬†(2:23), but the challenge has never been met.

56:3.text: It will debase ňĻsomeňļ and elevate ňĻothersňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Unlike this world of sheer injustice, the Hereafter will put everyone in their right place. The righteous will be elevated in Paradise, while the wicked will be humiliated in Hell.

56:4.text: When the earth will be violently shaken,

56:5.text: and the mountains will be crushed to pieces,

56:7.text: you will ňĻallňļ be ňĻdivided intoňļ three groups:

56:8.text: the people of the right, how ňĻblessedňļ will they be;

56:9.text: the people of the left, how ňĻmiserableňļ will they be;

56:10.text: and the foremost ňĻin faithňļ will be the foremost ňĻin Paradiseňļ.

56:11.text: They are the ones nearest ňĻto Allahňļ,

56:13.text: ňĻThey will beňļ a multitude from earlier generations

56:14.text: and a few from later generations.

56:15.text: ňĻAll will beňļ on jewelled thrones,

56:17.text: They will be waited on by eternal youths

56:18.text: with cups, pitchers, and a drink ňĻof pure wineňļ from a flowing stream,

56:19.text: that will cause them neither headache nor intoxication.

56:20.text: ňĻThey will also be servedňļ any fruit they choose

56:21.text: and meat from any bird they desire.

56:22.text: And ňĻthey will haveňļ maidens with gorgeous eyes,

56:24.text: ňĻallňļ as a reward for what they used to do.

56:25.text: There they will never hear any idle or sinful talk‚ÄĒ

56:26.text: only good and virtuous speech.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚Äúonly exchanging greetings of peace.‚ÄĚ

56:27.text: And the people of the right‚ÄĒhow ňĻblessedňļ will they be!

56:28.text: ňĻThey will beňļ amid thornless lote trees,

56:33.text: never out of season nor forbidden‚ÄĒ

56:35.text: Indeed, We will have perfectly created their mates,

56:38.text: for the people of the right,

56:39.text: ňĻwho will beňļ a multitude from earlier generations

56:40.text: and a multitude from later generations.

56:41.text: And the people of the left‚ÄĒhow ňĻmiserableňļ will they be!

56:42.text: ňĻThey will beňļ in scorching heat and boiling water,

56:43.text: in the shade of black smoke,

56:45.text: Indeed, before this ňĻtormentňļ they were spoiled by luxury,

56:46.text: and persisted in the worst of sin.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., associating others with Allah in worship and denying the resurrection and judgment.

56:47.text: They used to ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?

56:49.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúMost certainly, earlier and later generations

56:50.text: will surely be gathered ňĻtogetherňļ for the appointed Day.

56:52.text: will certainly eat from ňĻthe fruit ofňļ the trees of Zaqq√Ľm,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†See footnote for 44:43.

56:53.text: filling up ňĻyourňļ bellies with it.

56:54.text: Then on top of that you will drink boiling water‚ÄĒ

56:55.text: and you will drink ňĻitňļ like thirsty camels do.‚ÄĚ

56:56.text: This will be their accommodation on the Day of Judgment.

56:57.text: It is We Who created you. Will you not then believe ňĻin resurrectionňļ?

56:58.text: Have you considered what you ejaculate?

56:59.text: Is it you who create ňĻa child out ofňļ it, or is it We Who do so?

56:60.text: We have ordained death for ňĻall ofňļ you, and We cannot be prevented

56:61.text: from transforming and recreating you in forms unknown to you.

56:62.text: You already know how you were first created. Will you not then be mindful?

56:63.text: Have you considered what you sow?

56:64.text: Is it you who cause it to grow, or is it We Who do so?

56:65.text: If We willed, We could simply reduce this ňĻharvestňļ to chaff, leaving you to lament,

56:66.text: ‚ÄúWe have truly suffered a ňĻgreatňļ loss.

56:67.text: In fact, we have been deprived ňĻof our livelihoodňļ.‚ÄĚ

56:68.text: Have you considered the water you drink?

56:69.text: Is it you who bring it down from the clouds, or is it We Who do so?

56:70.text: If We willed, We could make it salty. Will you not then give thanks?

56:71.text: Have you considered the fire you kindle?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This verse refers to two types of Arabian trees, markh¬†and ‚Äôaf√Ęr, which produce fire when their green branches are rubbed together.

56:72.text: Is it you who produce its trees, or is it We Who do so?

56:73.text: We have made it ňĻasňļ a reminder ňĻof the Hellfireňļ and a provision for the travellers.

56:74.text: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.

56:75.text: So I do swear by the positions of the stars‚ÄĒ

56:76.text: and this, if only you knew, is indeed a great oath‚ÄĒ

56:77.text: that this is truly a noble Quran,

56:79.text: touched by none except the purified ňĻangelsňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This verse refutes the pagan claim that the Quran was sent down by devils (see¬†26:210-212). Based on this verse, many jurists maintain that a Muslim should perform ablutions before reading the Quran.

56:80.text: ňĻIt isňļ a revelation from the Lord of all worlds.

56:81.text: How can you then take this message lightly,

56:82.text: and repay ňĻAllah forňļ your provisions with denial?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Some pagans believed that rain was caused by some stars.

56:83.text: Why then ňĻare you helplessňļ when the soul ňĻof a dying personňļ reaches ňĻtheirňļ throat,

56:85.text: And We are nearer to such a person than you, but you cannot see.

56:86.text: Now, if you are not subject to Our Will ňĻas you claimňļ,

56:87.text: bring that soul back, if what you say is true.

56:88.text: So, if the deceased is one of those brought near ňĻto Usňļ,

56:89.text: then ňĻsuch a person will haveňļ serenity, fragrance,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and a Garden of Bliss.
footnotes: ¬†lit., fragrant basil. Throughout the Quran, Paradise is described as being pleasant to the eye, the heart, and the ear. This verse emphasizes the scent as well.

56:90.text: And if the deceased is one of the people of the right,

56:91.text: then ňĻthey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúGreetings to you from the people of the right.‚ÄĚ

56:92.text: But if such person is one of the misguided deniers,

56:93.text: then their accommodation will be boiling water ňĻto drinkňļ

56:95.text: Indeed, this is the absolute truth.

56:96.text: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.

57:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, for He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

57:2.text: To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And He is Most Capable of everything.

57:3.text: He is the First and the Last, the Most High and Most Near,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚Äúthe Manifest ňĻthrough His signsňļ and the Hidden ňĻfrom His creationňļ.‚ÄĚ

57:4.text: He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then established Himself on the Throne. He knows whatever goes into the earth and whatever comes out of it, and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends into it. And He is with you wherever you are.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†For Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:54.
¬†By His knowledge.

57:5.text: To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allah all matters are returned.

57:6.text: He merges the night into day and the day into night. And He knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

57:7.text: Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and donate from what He has entrusted you with. So those of you who believe and donate will have a mighty reward.

57:8.text: Why do you not believe in Allah while the Messenger is inviting you to have faith in your Lord, although He has already taken your covenant,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†if you will ever believe.

57:9.text: He is the One Who sends down clear revelations to His servant to bring you out of darkness and into light. For indeed Allah is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to you.

57:10.text: And why should you not spend in the cause of Allah, while Allah is the ňĻsoleňļ inheritor of the heavens and the earth? Those of you who donated and fought before the victory ňĻover Meccaňļ are unparalleled. They are far greater in rank than those who donated and fought afterwards.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet Allah has promised each a fine reward. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†Prior to the victory over Mecca, Muslims were outnumbered and perceived as weak. Once Mecca surrendered to the Muslims, many tribes either accepted Islam or made peace agreements with the Muslim victors. Therefore, the believers who donated and strived at the time of hardship and weakness deserve more rewards than those who did so at the time of ease and prominence.

57:11.text: Who is it that will lend to Allah a good loan which Allah will multiply ňĻmany times overňļ for them, and they will have an honourable reward?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Paradise.

57:12.text: On that Day you will see believing men and women with their light shining ahead of them and on their right. ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúToday you have good news of Gardens, under which rivers flow, ňĻfor youňļ to stay in forever. This is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.‚ÄĚ

57:13.text: On that Day hypocrite men and women will beg the believers, ‚ÄúWait for us so that we may have some of your light.‚ÄĚ It will be said ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúGo back ňĻto the worldňļ and seek a light ňĻthereňļ!‚ÄĚ Then a ňĻseparatingňļ wall with a gate will be erected between them. On the near side will be grace and on the far side will be torment.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This wall is said to be the barrier of Al-A‚Äôr√Ęf¬†(or the heights) between Paradise and Hell. See¬†7:46-49.

57:14.text: The tormented will cry out to those graced, ‚ÄúWere we not with you?‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúYes ňĻyou wereňļ. But you chose to be tempted ňĻby hypocrisyňļ, ňĻeagerlyňļ awaited ňĻour demiseňļ, doubted ňĻthe truthňļ, and were deluded by false hopes until Allah‚Äôs decree ňĻof your deathňļ came to pass. And ňĻsoňļ the Chief Deceiver<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†deceived you about Allah.
footnotes: ¬†Satan.

57:15.text: So Today no ransom will be accepted from you ňĻhypocritesňļ, nor from the disbelievers. Your home is the Fire‚ÄĒit is the ňĻonlyňļ fitting place for you. What an evil destination!‚ÄĚ

57:16.text: Has the time not yet come for believers‚Äô hearts to be humbled at the remembrance of Allah and what has been revealed of the truth, and not be like those given the Scripture before‚ÄĒňĻthoseňļ who were spoiled for so long that their hearts became hardened. And many of them are ňĻstillňļ rebellious.

57:17.text: Know that Allah revives the earth after its death.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We have certainly made the signs clear for you so perhaps you will understand.
footnotes: ¬†So it is easy for Him to soften your hearts as well.

57:18.text: Indeed, those men and women who give in charity and lend to Allah a good loan will have it multiplied for them, and they will have an honourable reward.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Paradise.

57:19.text: ňĻAs forňļ those who believe in Allah and His messengers, it is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ the people of truth. And the martyrs, with their Lord, will have their reward and their light. But ňĻas forňļ those who disbelieve and reject Our signs, it is they who will be the residents of the Hellfire.

57:20.text: Know that this worldly life is no more than play, amusement, luxury, mutual boasting, and competition in wealth and children. This is like rain that causes plants to grow, to the delight of the planters. But later the plants dry up and you see them wither, then they are reduced to chaff. And in the Hereafter there will be either severe punishment or forgiveness and pleasure of Allah, whereas the life of this world is no more than the delusion of enjoyment.

57:21.text: ňĻSoňļ compete with one another for forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who believe in Allah and His messengers. This is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

57:22.text: No calamity ňĻor blessingňļ occurs on earth or in yourselves without being ňĻwrittenňļ in a Record before We bring it into being. This is certainly easy for Allah.

57:23.text: ňĻWe let you know thisňļ so that you neither grieve over what you have missed nor boast over what He has granted you. For Allah does not like whoever is arrogant, boastful‚ÄĒ

57:24.text: those who are stingy and promote stinginess among people. And whoever turns away ňĻshould know thatňļ Allah ňĻaloneňļ is truly the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

57:25.text: Indeed, We sent Our messengers with clear proofs, and with them We sent down the Scripture and the balance ňĻof justiceňļ so that people may administer justice. And We sent down iron with its great might, benefits for humanity, and means for Allah to prove who ňĻis willing toňļ stand up for Him and His messengers without seeing Him. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty.

57:26.text: And indeed, We sent Noah and Abraham and reserved prophethood and revelation for their descendants. Some of them are ňĻrightlyňļ guided, while most are rebellious.

57:27.text: Then in the footsteps of these ňĻprophetsňļ, We sent Our messengers, and ňĻafter themňļ We sent Jesus, son of Mary, and granted him the Gospel, and instilled compassion and mercy into the hearts of his followers. As for monasticism, they made it up‚ÄĒWe never ordained it for them‚ÄĒonly seeking to please Allah, yet they did not ňĻevenňļ observe it strictly. So We rewarded those of them who were faithful. But most of them are rebellious.

57:28.text: O people of faith! Fear Allah and believe in His Messenger. ňĻAndňļ He will grant you a double share of His mercy, provide you with a light to walk in ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ, and forgive you. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

57:29.text: ňĻThis is soňļ that the People of the Book ňĻwho deny the Prophetňļ may know that they do not have any control over Allah‚Äôs grace, and that all grace is in Allah‚Äôs Hands. He grants it to whoever He wills. For Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

58:1.text: Indeed, Allah has heard the argument of the woman who pleaded with you ňĻO Prophetňļ concerning her husband, and appealed to Allah. Allah has heard your exchange.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
footnotes: ¬†A companion named Khawlah bint Tha‚Äôlabah had a disagreement with her husband, Aws ibn AŠĻ£-ŠĻĘ√Ęmit, who then told her that she was as unlawful for him as the Šļďahr (back) of his mother. This statement had been considered to be a form of divorce (known as Šļďih√Ęr) in Arabia. Khawlah came to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) to ask for his opinion. He (Ô∑ļ) told her that he had not received any revelation in this regard, and that, based on tradition, she was divorced. She argued that she and her husband had children together who would suffer if their parents were separated. Then she started to plead to Allah as the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) repeated the same answer. Eventually, this Medinian s√Ľrah¬†was revealed in response to her pleas, thereby abolishing this ancient practice.

58:2.text: Those of you who ňĻsinfullyňļ divorce their wives by comparing them to their mothers ňĻshould know thatňļ their wives are in no way their mothers. None can be their mothers except those who gave birth to them. What they say is certainly detestable and false. Yet Allah is truly Ever-Pardoning, All-Forgiving.

58:3.text: Those who divorce their wives in this manner, then ňĻwish toňļ retract what they said, must free a slave before they touch each other. This ňĻpenaltyňļ is meant to deter you. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

58:4.text: But if the husband cannot afford this, let him then fast two consecutive months before the couple touch each other. But if he is unable ňĻto fastňļ, then let him feed sixty poor people. This is to re-affirm your faith in Allah and His Messenger. These are the limits set by Allah. And the disbelievers will suffer a painful punishment.

58:5.text: Indeed, those who defy Allah and His Messenger will be debased, just like those before them. We have certainly sent down clear revelations. And the disbelievers will suffer a humiliating punishment.

58:6.text: On the Day Allah resurrects them all together, He will then inform them of what they have done. Allah has kept account of it all, while they have forgotten it. For Allah is a Witness over all things.

58:7.text: Do you not see that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? If three converse privately, He is their fourth. If five, He is their sixth. Whether fewer or more, He is with them<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†wherever they may be. Then, on the Day of Judgment, He will inform them of what they have done. Surely Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.
footnotes: ¬†By His knowledge.

58:8.text: Have you not seen those who were forbidden from secret talks, yet they ňĻalwaysňļ return to what they were forbidden from, conspiring in sin, aggression, and disobedience to the Messenger? And when they come to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, they greet you not as Allah greets you,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and say to one another, ‚ÄúWhy does Allah not punish us for what we say?‚ÄĚ Hell is enough for them‚ÄĒthey will burn in it. And what an evil destination!
footnotes: ¬†Some Jews used to ¬†play with words when they addressed the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) to ridicule him, saying for example ‚ÄėAs√Ęmu ‚Äôalaikum‚Äô¬†(death be upon you) instead of ‚ÄėAsal√Ęmu ‚Äôalaikum‚Äô (peace be upon you)‚ÄĒwhich is the greeting of Islam, inspired by Allah.

58:9.text: O believers! When you converse privately, let it not be for sin, aggression, or disobedience to the Messenger, but let it be for goodness and righteousness. And fear Allah, to Whom you will ňĻallňļ be gathered.

58:10.text: Secret talks are only inspired by Satan to grieve the believers. Yet he cannot harm them whatsoever except by Allah‚Äôs Will. So in Allah let the believers put their trust.

58:11.text: O believers! When you are told to make room in gatherings, then do so. Allah will make room for you ňĻin His graceňļ. And if you are told to rise, then do so. Allah will elevate those of you who are faithful, and ňĻraiseňļ those gifted with knowledge in rank. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

58:12.text: O believers! When you consult the Messenger privately, give something in charity before your consultation. That is better and purer for you. But if you lack the means, then Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

58:13.text: Are you afraid of spending in charity before your private consultations ňĻwith himňļ? Since you are unable to do so, and Allah has turned to you in mercy, then ňĻcontinue toňļ establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and obey Allah and His Messenger. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

58:14.text: Have you not seen those ňĻhypocritesňļ who ally themselves with a people with whom Allah is displeased? They are neither with you nor with them. And they swear to lies knowingly.

58:15.text: Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. Evil indeed is what they do.

58:16.text: They have made their ňĻfalseňļ oaths as a shield, hindering ňĻothersňļ from the cause of Allah. So they will suffer a humiliating punishment.

58:17.text: Neither their wealth nor children will be of any help to them against Allah whatsoever. It is they who will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.

58:18.text: On the Day Allah resurrects them all, they will ňĻfalselyňļ swear to Him as they swear to you, thinking they have something to stand on. Indeed, it is they who are the ňĻtotalňļ liars.

58:19.text: Satan has taken hold of them, causing them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are the party of Satan. Surely Satan‚Äôs party is bound to lose.

58:20.text: ňĻAs forňļ those who defy Allah and His Messenger, they will definitely be among the most debased.

58:21.text: Allah has decreed, ‚ÄúI and My messengers will certainly prevail.‚ÄĚ Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty.

58:22.text: You will never find a people who ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and the Last Day loyal to those who defy Allah and His Messenger, even if they were their parents, children, siblings, or extended family. For those ňĻbelieversňļ, Allah has instilled faith in their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He will admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever. Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. They are the party of Allah. Indeed, Allah‚Äôs party is bound to succeed.
footnotes: ¬†Spirit here can mean revelation, light, or help.

59:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

59:2.text: He is the One Who expelled the disbelievers of the People of the Book from their homes for ňĻtheirňļ first banishment ňĻeverňļ. You never thought they would go. And they thought their strongholds would put them out of Allah‚Äôs reach. But ňĻthe decree ofňļ Allah came upon them from where they never expected. And He cast horror into their hearts so they destroyed their houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So take a lesson ňĻfrom thisňļ, O ¬†people of insight!
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) allowed Banu An-NaŠłć√ģr to carry whatever they could on their camels, so they removed the wooden pillars of their homes, causing them to collapse.

59:3.text: Had Allah not decreed exile for them, He would have certainly punished them in this world. And in the Hereafter they will suffer the punishment of the Fire.

59:4.text: This is because they defied Allah and His Messenger. And whoever defies Allah, then Allah is truly severe in punishment.

59:5.text: Whatever palm trees you ňĻbelieversňļ cut down or left standing intact, it was ňĻallňļ by Allah‚Äôs Will, so that He might disgrace the rebellious.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) had instructed the Muslim army not to cut down trees, but some soldiers had to remove some palm trees to be able to enter the fort, to the dismay of Banu An-NaŠłć√ģr. When Muslims disagreed on that act, this verse was revealed.

59:6.text: As for the gains Allah has turned over to His Messenger from them‚ÄĒyou did not ňĻevenňļ spur on any horse or camel for such gains. But Allah gives authority to His messengers over whoever He wills. For Allah is Most Capable of everything.

59:7.text: As for gains granted by Allah to His Messenger from the people of ňĻotherňļ lands, they are for Allah and the Messenger, his close relatives, orphans, the poor, and ňĻneedyňļ travellers so that wealth may not merely circulate among your rich. Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it. And whatever he forbids you from, leave it. And fear Allah. Surely Allah is severe in punishment.

59:8.text: ňĻSome of the gains will beňļ for poor emigrants who were driven out of their homes and wealth, seeking Allah‚Äôs bounty and pleasure, and standing up for Allah and His Messenger. They are the ones true in faith.

59:9.text: As for those who had settled in the city and ňĻembracedňļ the faith before ňĻthe arrival ofňļ the emigrants, they love whoever immigrates to them, never having a desire in their hearts for whatever ňĻof the gainsňļ is given to the emigrants. They give ňĻthe emigrantsňļ preference over themselves even though they may be in need. And whoever is saved from the selfishness of their own souls, it is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ successful.

59:10.text: And those who come after them will pray, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Forgive us and our fellow believers who preceded us in faith, and do not allow bitterness into our hearts towards those who believe. Our Lord! Indeed, You are Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ

59:11.text: Have you ňĻO Prophetňļ not seen the hypocrites who say to their fellow disbelievers from the People of the Book,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúIf you are expelled, we will certainly leave with you, and We will never obey anyone against you. And if you are fought against, we will surely help you.‚ÄĚ? But Allah bears witness that they are truly liars.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, Banu An-NaŠłć√ģr.

59:12.text: Indeed, if they are expelled, the hypocrites will never leave with them. And if they are fought against, the hypocrites will never help them. And even if the hypocrites did so, they would certainly flee, then the disbelievers would be left with no help.

59:13.text: Indeed, there is more fear in their hearts for you ňĻbelieversňļ than for Allah. That is because they are a people who do not comprehend.

59:14.text: Even united, they would not ňĻdareňļ fight against you except ňĻfromňļ within fortified strongholds or from behind walls. Their malice for each other is intense: you think they are united, yet their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people with no ňĻrealňļ understanding.

59:15.text: They are ňĻboth justňļ like those who recently went down before them:<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†they tasted the evil consequences of their doings. And they will suffer a painful punishment.
footnotes: ¬†This either refers to the defeat of the Meccan pagans at the Battle of Badr or the exile of Banu Qainuq√Ę‚Äô.

59:16.text: ňĻThey areňļ like Satan when he lures someone to disbelieve. Then after they have done so, he will say ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ, ‚ÄúI have absolutely nothing to do with you. I truly fear Allah‚ÄĒthe Lord of all worlds.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>

59:17.text: So they will both end up in the Fire, staying there forever. That is the reward of the wrongdoers.

59:18.text: O believers! Be mindful of Allah and let every soul look to what ňĻdeedsňļ it has sent forth for tomorrow.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And fear Allah, ňĻforňļ certainly Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Hereafter.

59:19.text: And do not be like those who forgot Allah, so He made them forget themselves. It is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ rebellious.

59:20.text: The residents of the Fire cannot be equal to the residents of Paradise. ňĻOnlyňļ the residents of Paradise will be successful.

59:21.text: Had We sent down this Quran upon a mountain, you would have certainly seen it humbled and torn apart in awe of Allah. We set forth such comparisons for people, ňĻsoňļ perhaps they may reflect.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, that the hearts of the disbelievers are harder than the mountains concerning the Quran.

59:22.text: He is Allah‚ÄĒthere is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him: Knower of the seen and unseen. He is the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

59:23.text: He is Allah‚ÄĒthere is no god except Him: the King, the Most Holy, the All-Perfect, the Source of Serenity, the Watcher ňĻof allňļ, the Almighty, the Supreme in Might,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†the Majestic. Glorified is Allah far above what they associate with Him ňĻin worshipňļ!
footnotes: ¬†Jabb√Ęr¬†comes from the root word ja-ba-ra¬†which means to impose, support, or console. For example, Jab√ģrah¬†means the cast that supports a broken bone. Hence, Allah is the One Whose Will cannot be resisted, and Who comforts those who are broken or oppressed.

59:24.text: He is Allah: the Creator, the Inventor, the Shaper. He ňĻaloneňļ has the Most Beautiful Names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth ňĻconstantlyňļ glorifies Him. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

6:1.text: All praise is for Allah Who created the heavens and the earth and made darkness and light.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet the disbelievers set up equals to their Lord ňĻin worshipňļ.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúLight‚ÄĚ is always used in the Quran in the singular (n√Ľr), whereas ‚Äúdarkness‚ÄĚ is used in the plural (Šļďulum√Ęt). N√Ľr¬†is usually used in a metaphorical sense to refer to true guidance, whereas Šļďulum√Ęt refers to different forms of misguidance.

6:2.text: He is the One Who created you from clay,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then appointed a term ňĻfor your deathňļ and another known only to Him ňĻfor your resurrectionňļ‚ÄĒyet you continue to doubt!
footnotes: ¬†i.e., created your father, Adam, from clay. Some scholars suggest that if we examine all the minerals found in the earth (potassium, nitrogen, and carbon), we find that these are the same minerals that make up our body. Furthermore when we die, our bodies are absorbed back into the ground. And when we are resurrected, we will be resurrected from the ground (see¬†20:55).

6:3.text: He is the Only True God in the heavens and the earth. He knows whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal, and knows whatever you do.

6:4.text: Whenever a sign comes to them from their Lord, they turn away from it.

6:5.text: They have indeed rejected the truth when it came to them, so they will soon face the consequences of their ridicule.

6:6.text: Have they not seen how many ňĻdisbelievingňļ peoples¬†We destroyed before them? We had made them more established in the land than you. We sent down abundant rain for them and made rivers flow at their feet. Then We destroyed them for their sins and replaced them with other peoples.

6:7.text: Had We sent down to you ňĻO Prophetňļ a revelation in writing and they were to touch it with their own hands, the disbelievers would still have said, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but pure magic!‚ÄĚ

6:8.text: They say, ‚ÄúWhy has no ňĻvisibleňļ angel come with him?‚ÄĚ Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have certainly been settled ňĻat onceňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and they would have never been given more time ňĻto repentňļ.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they would have been destroyed immediately upon denying the angel.

6:9.text: And if We had sent an angel, We would have certainly made it ňĻassume the form ofňļ a man‚ÄĒleaving them more confused than they already are.

6:10.text: ňĻOtherňļ messengers had already been ridiculed before you ňĻO Prophetňļ, but those who mocked them were overtaken by what they used to ridicule.

6:11.text: Say, ‚ÄúTravel throughout the land and see the fate of the deniers.‚ÄĚ

6:12.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúTo whom belongs everything in the heavens and the earth?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúTo Allah!‚ÄĚ He has taken upon Himself to be Merciful. He will certainly gather ňĻall ofňļ you together for the Day of Judgment‚ÄĒabout which there is no doubt. But those who have ruined themselves will never believe.

6:13.text: To Him belongs whatever exists in the day and night. And He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

6:14.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWill I take any guardian other than Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, Who provides for all and is not in need of provision?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúI have been commanded to be the first to submit and not be one of the polytheists.‚ÄĚ

6:15.text: Say, ‚ÄúI truly fear‚ÄĒif I were to disobey my Lord‚ÄĒthe torment of a tremendous Day.‚ÄĚ

6:16.text: Whoever is spared the torment of that Day will have certainly been shown Allah‚Äôs mercy. And that is the absolute triumph.

6:17.text: If Allah touches you with harm, none can undo it except Him. And if He touches you with a blessing, He is Most Capable of everything.

6:18.text: He reigns supreme over His creation. And He is the All-Wise, All-Aware.

6:19.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWho is the best witness?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAllah is! He is a Witness between me and you. And this Quran has been revealed to me so that, with it, I may warn you and whoever it reaches. Do you ňĻpagansňļ testify that there are other gods besides Allah?‚ÄĚ ňĻThenňļ say, ‚ÄúI will never testify ňĻto thisňļ!‚ÄĚ ňĻAndňļ say, ‚ÄúThere is only One God. And I totally reject whatever ňĻidolsňļ you associate with Him.‚ÄĚ

6:20.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture recognize him ňĻto be a true prophetňļ as they recognize their own children. Those who have ruined themselves will never believe.

6:21.text: Who does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah or deny His signs? Indeed, the wrongdoers will never succeed.

6:22.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day We will gather them all together then ask those who associated others ňĻwith Allah in worshipňļ, ‚ÄúWhere are those gods you used to claim?‚ÄĚ

6:23.text: Their only argument will be: ‚ÄúBy Allah, our Lord! We were never polytheists.‚ÄĚ

6:24.text: See how they will lie about themselves and how those ňĻgodsňļ they fabricated will fail them!

6:25.text: There are some of them who ňĻpretend toňļ listen to your recitation ňĻof the Quranňļ, but We have cast veils over their hearts‚ÄĒleaving them unable to comprehend it‚ÄĒand deafness in their ears. Even if they were to see every sign, they still would not believe in them. The disbelievers would ňĻevenňļ come to argue with you, saying, ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ is nothing but ancient fables!‚ÄĚ

6:26.text: They turn others away from the Prophet and distance themselves as well. They ruin none but themselves, yet they fail to perceive it.

6:27.text: If only you could see when they will be detained before the Fire! They will cry, ‚ÄúOh! If only we could be sent back, we would never deny the signs of our Lord and we would ňĻsurelyňļ be of the believers.‚ÄĚ

6:28.text: But no! ňĻThey only say thisňļ because the truth they used to hide will become all too clear to them. Even if they were to be sent back, they would certainly revert to what they were forbidden. Indeed they are liars!

6:29.text: They insisted, ‚ÄúThere is nothing beyond this worldly life and we will never be resurrected.‚ÄĚ

6:30.text: But if only you could see when they will be detained before their Lord! He will ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúIs this ňĻHereafterňļ not the truth?‚ÄĚ They will cry, ‚ÄúAbsolutely, by our Lord!‚ÄĚ He will say, ‚ÄúThen taste the punishment for your disbelief.‚ÄĚ

6:31.text: Losers indeed are those who deny the meeting with Allah until the Hour takes them by surprise, then they will cry, ‚ÄúWoe to us for having ignored this!‚ÄĚ They will bear ňĻthe burden ofňļ their sins on their backs. Evil indeed is their burden!

6:32.text: This worldly life is no more than play and amusement, but far better is the ňĻeternalňļ Home of the Hereafter for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ. Will you not then understand?

6:33.text: We certainly know that what they say grieves you ňĻO Prophetňļ. It is not your honesty they question‚ÄĒit is Allah‚Äôs signs that the wrongdoers deny.

6:34.text: Indeed, messengers before you were rejected but patiently endured rejection and persecution until Our help came to them. And Allah‚Äôs promise ňĻto helpňļ is never broken. And you have already received some of the narratives of these messengers.

6:35.text: If you find their denial unbearable, then build‚ÄĒif you can‚ÄĒa tunnel through the earth or stairs to the sky to bring them a ňĻmore compellingňļ sign. Had Allah so willed, He could have guided them all. So do not be one of those ignorant ňĻof this factňļ.

6:36.text: Only the attentive will respond ňĻto your callňļ. As for the dead, Allah will raise them up, then to Him they will ňĻallňļ be returned.

6:37.text: They ask, ‚ÄúWhy has no ňĻotherňļ sign been sent down to him from his Lord?‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah certainly has the power to send down a sign‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒthough most of them do not know.

6:38.text: All living beings roaming the earth and winged birds soaring in the sky are communities like yourselves.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We have left nothing out of the Record.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Then to their Lord they will be gathered all together.
footnotes: ¬†Allah has created living beings (like animals, birds, and fish) just like He created human beings. He provides for all and is merciful to all. They all belong to communities and have their own systems of living.
