¬†The Record refers to the Preserved Tablet (Al-LawŠł• Al-MaŠł•f√ĽŠļď) in which Allah has written the destiny of His entire creation.

6:39.text: Those who deny Our signs are ňĻwilfullyňļ deaf and dumb‚ÄĒlost in darkness. Allah leaves whoever He wills to stray and guides whoever He wills to the Straight Way.

6:40.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúImagine if you were overwhelmed by Allah‚Äôs torment or the Hour‚ÄĒwould you call upon any other than Allah ňĻfor helpňļ? ňĻAnswer meňļ if your claims are true!

6:41.text: No! He is the only One you would call. And if He willed, He could remove the affliction that made you invoke Him. Only then will you forget whatever you associate with Him ňĻin worshipňļ.‚ÄĚ

6:42.text: Indeed, We have sent messengers before you ňĻO Prophetňļ to other people who We put through suffering and adversity ňĻfor their denialňļ, so perhaps they would be humbled.

6:43.text: Why did they not humble themselves when We made them suffer? Instead, their hearts were hardened, and Satan made their misdeeds appealing to them.

6:44.text: When they became oblivious to warnings, We showered them with everything they desired. But just as they became prideful of what they were given, We seized them by surprise, then they instantly fell into despair!

6:45.text: So the wrongdoers were utterly uprooted. And all praise is for Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds.

6:46.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúImagine if Allah were to take away your hearing or sight, or seal your hearts‚ÄĒwho else other than Allah could restore it?‚ÄĚ See ňĻO Prophetňļ how We vary the signs, yet they still turn away.

6:47.text: Ask, ‚ÄúImagine if Allah‚Äôs punishment were to overwhelm you with or without warning‚ÄĒwho would be destroyed other than the wrongdoers?‚ÄĚ

6:48.text: We have sent messengers only as deliverers of good news and as warners. Whoever believes and does good, there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.

6:49.text: But those who deny Our signs will be afflicted with punishment for their rebelliousness.

6:50.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI do not say to you that I possess Allah‚Äôs treasuries or know the unseen, nor do I claim to be an angel. I only follow what is revealed to me.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAre those blind ňĻto the truthňļ equal to those who can see? Will you not then reflect?‚ÄĚ

6:51.text: Warn with this ňĻQuranňļ those who are awed by the prospect of being gathered before their Lord‚ÄĒwhen they will have no protector or intercessor besides Him‚ÄĒso perhaps they will be mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

6:52.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Do not dismiss those ňĻpoor believersňļ who invoke their Lord morning and evening, seeking His pleasure.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†You are not accountable for them whatsoever, nor are they accountable for you. So do not dismiss them, or you will be one of the wrongdoers.
footnotes: ¬†lit., seeking His Face.

6:53.text: In this way We have tested some by means of others, so those ňĻdisbelieversňļ may say, ‚ÄúHas Allah favoured these ňĻpoor believersňļ out of all of us?‚ÄĚ Does Allah not best recognize the grateful?

6:54.text: When the believers in Our revelations come to you, say, ‚ÄúPeace be upon you! Your Lord has taken upon Himself to be Merciful. Whoever among you commits evil ignorantly ňĻor recklesslyňļ then repents afterwards and mends their ways, then Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ

6:55.text: This is how We make Our signs clear, so the way of the wicked may become distinct.

6:56.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI have been forbidden to worship those you invoke besides Allah.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúI will not follow your desires, for I then would certainly be astray and not one of those ňĻrightlyňļ guided.‚ÄĚ

6:57.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIndeed, I stand on a clear proof from my Lord‚ÄĒyet you have denied it. That ňĻtormentňļ you seek to hasten is not within my power. It is only Allah Who decides ňĻits timeňļ. He declares the truth. And He is the Best of Judges.‚ÄĚ

6:58.text: Say ňĻalsoňļ, ‚ÄúIf what you seek to hasten were within my power, the matter between us would have already been settled. But Allah knows the wrongdoers best.‚ÄĚ

6:59.text: With Him are the keys of the unseen‚ÄĒno one knows them except Him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And He knows what is in the land and sea. Not even a leaf falls without His knowledge, nor a grain in the darkness of the earth or anything‚ÄĒgreen or dry‚ÄĒbut is ňĻwrittenňļ in a perfect Record.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†The five keys of the unseen are mentioned in 31:34.
¬†See¬†footnote for 6:38.

6:60.text: He is the One Who calls back your souls by night and knows what you do by day, then revives you daily to complete your appointed term. To Him is your ňĻultimateňļ return, then He will inform you of what you used to do.

6:61.text: He reigns supreme over all of His creation, and sends recording-angels, watching over you. When death comes to any of you, Our angels take their soul, never neglecting this duty.

6:62.text: Then they are ňĻallňļ returned to Allah‚ÄĒtheir True Master. Judgment is His ňĻaloneňļ. And He is the Swiftest Reckoner.

6:63.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWho rescues you from the darkest times on land and at sea? He ňĻaloneňļ you call upon with humility, openly and secretly: ‚ÄúIf You rescue us from this, we will be ever grateful.‚ÄĚ

6:64.text: Say, ‚ÄúňĻOnlyňļ Allah rescues you from this and any other distress, yet you associate others with Him ňĻin worshipňļ.‚ÄĚ

6:65.text: Say, ‚ÄúHe ňĻaloneňļ has the power to unleash upon you a torment from above or below you or split you into ňĻconflictingňļ factions and make you taste the violence of one another.‚ÄĚ See how We vary the signs, so perhaps they will comprehend.

6:66.text: Still your people ňĻO Prophetňļ have rejected this ňĻQuranňļ, although it is the truth. Say, ‚ÄúI am not a keeper over you.‚ÄĚ

6:67.text: Every ňĻdestinedňļ matter has a ňĻsetňļ time to transpire. And you will soon come to know.

6:68.text: And when you come across those who ridicule Our revelations, do not sit with them unless they engage in a different topic. Should Satan make you forget, then once you remember, do not ňĻcontinue toňļ sit with the wrongdoing people.

6:69.text: Those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ will not be accountable for those ňĻwho ridicule itňļ whatsoever‚ÄĒtheir duty is to advise, so perhaps the ridiculers will abstain.

6:70.text: And leave those who take this<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†faith ňĻof Islamňļ as mere play and amusement and are deluded by ňĻtheirňļ worldly life. Yet remind them by this ňĻQuranňļ, so no one should be ruined for their misdeeds. They will have no protector or intercessor other than Allah. Even if they were to offer every ňĻpossibleňļ ransom, none will be accepted from them. Those are the ones who will be ruined for their misdeeds. They will have a boiling drink and painful punishment for their disbelief.
footnotes: ¬†lit., their faith (which they are supposed to follow).

6:71.text: Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúShould we invoke, other than Allah, those ňĻidolsňļ which cannot benefit or harm us, and turn back to disbelief after Allah has guided us? ňĻIf we do so, we will beňļ like those disoriented by devils in the wilderness, while their companions call them to guidance, ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄėCome to us!‚Äô Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah‚Äôs guidance is the ňĻonlyňļ true guidance. And we are commanded to submit to the Lord of all worlds,

6:72.text: establish prayer, and be mindful of Him. To Him you will all be gathered together.

6:73.text: He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth in truth. On the Day ňĻof Judgmentňļ He will say, ‚ÄėBe!‚Äô And there will be! His command is truth. All authority is His ňĻaloneňļ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†on the Day the Trumpet will be blown.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†He is the Knower of all‚ÄĒseen or unseen. And He is the All-Wise, All-Aware.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Allah grants authority to some of His servants in this world, but none will have authority on Judgment Day except Him. See¬†3:26.
¬†On the Day of Judgment, the Trumpet will be blown by an angel‚ÄĒcausing all to die. When it is blown a second time, everyone will be raised from the dead for judgment (see¬†39:68).

6:74.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Abraham said to his father, »āzar, ‚ÄúDo you take idols as gods? It is clear to me that you and your people are entirely misguided.‚ÄĚ

6:75.text: We also showed Abraham the wonders of the heavens and the earth, so he would be sure in faith.

6:76.text: When the night grew dark upon him, he saw a star and said, ‚ÄúThis is my Lord!‚ÄĚ But when it set, he said, ‚ÄúI do not love things that set.‚ÄĚ

6:77.text: Then when he saw the moon rising, he said, ‚ÄúThis one is my Lord!‚ÄĚ But when it disappeared, he said, ‚ÄúIf my Lord does not guide me, I will certainly be one of the misguided people.‚ÄĚ

6:78.text: Then when he saw the sun shining, he said, ‚ÄúThis must be my Lord‚ÄĒit is the greatest!‚ÄĚ But again when it set, he declared, ‚ÄúO my people! I totally reject whatever you associate ňĻwith Allah in worshipňļ.

6:79.text: I have turned my face towards the One Who has originated the heavens and the earth‚ÄĒbeing upright‚ÄĒand I am not one of the polytheists.‚ÄĚ

6:80.text: And his people argued with him. He responded, ‚ÄúAre you arguing with me about Allah, while He has guided me? I am not afraid of whatever ňĻidolsňļ you associate with Him‚ÄĒňĻnone can harm me,ňļ unless my Lord so wills. My Lord encompasses everything in ňĻHisňļ knowledge. Will you not be mindful?

6:81.text: And how should I fear your associate-gods, while you have no fear in associating ňĻothersňļ with Allah‚ÄĒa practice He has never authorized? Which side has more right to security? ňĻTell meňļ if you really know!‚ÄĚ

6:82.text: It is ňĻonlyňļ those who are faithful and do not tarnish their faith with falsehood<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who are guaranteed security and are ňĻrightlyňļ guided.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., associating false gods in worship with the Almighty.

6:83.text: This was the argument We gave Abraham against his people. We elevate in rank whoever We please. Surely your Lord is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

6:84.text: And We blessed him with Isaac and Jacob. We guided them all as We previously guided Noah and those among his descendants: David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron. This is how We reward the good-doers.

6:85.text: Likewise, ňĻWe guidedňļ Zachariah, John, Jesus, and Elias, who were all of the righteous.

6:86.text: ňĻWe also guidedňļ Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot, favouring each over other people ňĻof their timeňļ.

6:87.text: And ňĻWe favouredňļ some of their forefathers, their descendants, and their brothers. We chose them and guided them to the Straight Path.

6:88.text: This is Allah‚Äôs guidance with which He guides whoever He wills of His servants. Had they associated others with Him ňĻin worshipňļ, their ňĻgoodňļ deeds would have been wasted.

6:89.text: Those were the ones to whom We gave the Scripture, wisdom, and prophethood. But if these ňĻpagansňļ disbelieve in this ňĻmessageňļ, then We have already entrusted it to a people who will never disbelieve in it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the companions of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

6:90.text: These ňĻprophetsňļ were ňĻrightlyňļ guided by Allah, so follow their guidance. Say, ‚ÄúI ask no reward of you for this ňĻQuranňļ‚ÄĒit is a reminder to the whole world.‚ÄĚ

6:91.text: And they<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†have not shown Allah His proper reverence when they said, ‚ÄúAllah has revealed nothing to any human being.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWho then revealed the Book brought forth by Moses as a light and guidance for people, which you split into separate sheets‚ÄĒrevealing some and hiding much? You have been taught ňĻthrough this Quranňļ what neither you nor your forefathers knew.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah ňĻrevealed itňļ!‚ÄĚ Then leave them to amuse themselves with falsehood.
footnotes: ¬†Some Jews.

6:92.text: This is a blessed Book which We have revealed‚ÄĒconfirming what came before it‚ÄĒso you may warn the Mother of Cities<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and everyone around it. Those who believe in the Hereafter ňĻtrulyňļ believe in it and guard their prayers.
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄúThe Mother of Cities‚ÄĚ is an honorary title given to the city of Mecca because of its great religious significance as the home of Allah‚Äôs first house of worship ever built on earth, and perhaps because of its central location as well.

6:93.text: Who does more wrong than the one who fabricates lies against Allah or claims, ‚ÄúI have received revelations!‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒalthough nothing was revealed to them‚ÄĒor the one who says, ‚ÄúI can reveal the like of Allah‚Äôs revelations!‚ÄĚ? If you ňĻO Prophetňļ could only see the wrongdoers in the throes of death while the angels are stretching out their hands ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúGive up your souls! Today you will be rewarded with the torment of disgrace for telling lies about Allah and for being arrogant towards His revelations!‚ÄĚ

6:94.text: ňĻTodayňļ you have come back to Us all alone as We created you the first time‚ÄĒleaving behind everything We have provided you with. We do not see your intercessors with you‚ÄĒthose you claimed were Allah‚Äôs partners ňĻin worshipňļ. All your ties have been broken and all your claims have let you down.

6:95.text: Indeed, Allah is the One Who causes seeds and fruit stones to sprout. He brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living. That is Allah! How can you then be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

6:96.text: He causes the dawn to break, and has made the night for rest and ňĻmadeňļ the sun and the moon ňĻto travelňļ with precision. That is the design of the Almighty, All-Knowing.

6:97.text: And He is the One Who has made the stars as your guide through the darkness of land and sea. We have already made the signs clear for people who know.

6:98.text: And He is the One Who originated you all from a single soul,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then assigned you a place to live and another to ňĻbe laid toňļ rest. We have already made the signs clear for people who comprehend.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Adam (Ô∑ļ).

6:99.text: And He is the One Who sends down rain from the sky‚ÄĒcausing all kinds of plants to grow‚ÄĒproducing green stalks from which We bring forth clustered grain. And from palm trees come clusters of dates hanging within reach. ňĻThere areňļ also gardens of grapevines, olives, and pomegranates, similar ňĻin shapeňļ but dissimilar ňĻin tasteňļ. Look at their fruit as it yields and ripens! Indeed, in these are signs for people who believe.

6:100.text: Yet they associate the jinn<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ, even though He created them, and they falsely attribute to Him sons<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†and daughters<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†out of ignorance. Glorified and Exalted is He above what they claim!
footnotes: ¬†Jinn are another creation of Allah, made of ‚Äúsmokeless fire,‚ÄĚ and inhabit a realm parallel to our own. Like us, they have free will and can choose guidance or disobedience.
¬†i.e., ‚ÄúJesus‚ÄĚ in Christian belief.
¬†i.e., ‚Äúthe angels‚ÄĚ in pre-Islamic Arab tradition.

6:101.text: ňĻHe isňļ the Originator of the heavens and earth. How could He have children when He has no mate? He created all things and has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of everything.

6:102.text: That is Allah‚ÄĒyour Lord! There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. ňĻHe isňļ the Creator of all things, so worship Him ňĻaloneňļ. And He is the Maintainer of everything.

6:103.text: No vision can encompass Him, but He encompasses all vision. For He is the Most Subtle, All-Aware.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†No one is able to see Allah in this world, but there is extensive evidence in the Quran and the teachings of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) that the believers will be able to see their Lord on the Day of Judgment.

6:104.text: Indeed, there have come to you insights from your Lord. So whoever chooses to see, it is for their own good. But whoever chooses to be blind, it is to their own loss. And I am not a keeper over you.

6:105.text: And so We vary our signs to the extent that they will say, ‚ÄúYou have studied ňĻprevious scripturesňļ,‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and We make this ňĻQuranňļ clear for people who know.
footnotes: ¬†Allegations have always been made that the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) copied the Quran from the Bible, mostly because of similar narratives in both Scriptures (such as the stories of Adam, Joseph, Moses, and others). Historically, the Bible was not translated into Arabic until centuries after the Prophet (Ô∑ļ). Even if an Arabic translation of the Bible existed at his time, he (Ô∑ļ) could not have copied it because he could not read or write. From an Islamic point of view, similarities stem from the fact that both scriptures came originally from the same source‚ÄĒdivine revelation. For more details, see¬†the Introduction.

6:106.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Follow what is revealed to you from your Lord‚ÄĒthere is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him‚ÄĒand turn away from the polytheists.

6:107.text: Had Allah willed, they would not have been polytheists. We have not appointed you as their keeper, nor are you their maintainer.

6:108.text: ňĻO believers!ňļ Do not insult what they invoke besides Allah or they will insult Allah spitefully out of ignorance. This is how We have made each people‚Äôs deeds appealing to them. Then to their Lord is their return, and He will inform them of what they used to do.

6:109.text: They swear by Allah their most solemn oaths that if a sign were to come to them, they would certainly believe in it. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSigns are only with Allah.‚ÄĚ What will make you ňĻbelieversňļ realize that even if a sign were to come to them, they still would not believe?

6:110.text: We turn their hearts and eyes away ňĻfrom the truthňļ as they refused to believe at first, leaving them to wander blindly in their defiance.

6:111.text: Even if We had sent them the angels, made the dead speak to them, and assembled before their own eyes every sign ňĻthey demandedňļ, they still would not have believed‚ÄĒunless Allah so willed. But most of them are ignorant ňĻof thisňļ.

6:112.text: And so We have made for every prophet enemies‚ÄĒdevilish humans and jinn‚ÄĒwhispering to one another with elegant words of deception. Had it been your Lord‚Äôs Will, they would not have done such a thing. So leave them and their deceit,

6:113.text: so that the hearts of those who disbelieve in the Hereafter may be receptive to it, be pleased with it, and be persistent in their evil pursuits.

6:114.text: ňĻSay, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShould I seek a judge other than Allah while He is the One Who has revealed for you the Book ňĻwith the truthňļ perfectly explained?‚ÄĚ Those who were given the Scripture know that it has been revealed ňĻto youňļ from your Lord in truth. So do not be one of those who doubt.

6:115.text: The Word of your Lord has been perfected in truth and justice. None can change His Words. And He is the All-Hearing, All- Knowing.

6:116.text: ňĻO Prophet!ňļ If you were to obey most of those on earth, they would lead you away from Allah‚Äôs Way. They follow nothing but assumptions and do nothing but lie.

6:117.text: Indeed, your Lord knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

6:118.text: So eat only of what is slaughtered in Allah‚Äôs Name if you truly believe in His revelations.

6:119.text: Why should you not eat of what is slaughtered in Allah‚Äôs Name when He has already explained to you what He has forbidden to you‚ÄĒexcept when compelled by necessity? Many ňĻdeviantsňļ certainly mislead others by their whims out of ignorance. Surely your Lord knows the transgressors best.

6:120.text: Shun all sin‚ÄĒopen and secret. Indeed, those who commit sin will be punished for what they earn.

6:121.text: Do not eat of what is not slaughtered in Allah‚Äôs Name. For that would certainly be ňĻan act ofňļ disobedience. Surely the devils whisper to their ňĻhumanňļ associates to argue with you.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†If you were to obey them, then you ňĻtooňļ would be polytheists.
footnotes: ¬†Because Muslims are allowed to eat the meat of animals slaughtered properly and not carrion, the pagans of Mecca used to argue, ‚ÄúWhy are you only allowed to eat what you yourselves kill but not what Allah causes to die?‚ÄĚ

6:122.text: Can those who had been dead, to whom We gave life and a light with which they can walk among people, be compared to those in complete darkness from which they can never emerge?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†That is how the misdeeds of the disbelievers have been made appealing to them.
footnotes: ¬†The Quran often compares disbelief to death and blindness whereas belief is compared to life and the ability to see.

6:123.text: And so We have placed in every society the most wicked to conspire in it. Yet they plot only against themselves, but they fail to perceive it.

6:124.text: Whenever a sign comes to them, they say, ‚ÄúWe will never believe until we receive what Allah‚Äôs messengers received.‚ÄĚ Allah knows best where to place His message. The wicked will soon be overwhelmed by humiliation from Allah and a severe punishment for their evil plots.

6:125.text: Whoever Allah wills to guide, He opens their heart to Islam.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But whoever He wills to leave astray, He makes their chest tight and constricted as if they were climbing up into the sky. This is how Allah dooms those who disbelieve.
footnotes: ¬†Islam literally means ‚Äėsubmission to the Will of Allah.‚Äô

6:126.text: That is your Lord‚Äôs Path‚ÄĒperfectly straight. We have already made the signs clear to those who are mindful.

6:127.text: They will have the Home of Peace with their Lord, Who will be their Guardian because of what they used to do.

6:128.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day He will gather them ňĻallňļ together and say, ‚ÄúO assembly of jinn! You misled humans in great numbers.‚ÄĚ And their human associates will say, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We benefited from each other‚Äôs company,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but now we have reached the term which You appointed for us.‚ÄĚ ňĻThenňļ He will say, ‚ÄúThe Fire is your home, yours to stay in forever, except whoever Allah wills to spare.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Surely your Lord is All-Wise, All-Knowing.
footnotes: ¬†For example, the jinn helped humans with magic, while the jinn had a feeling of importance when they had a human following.
¬†i.e., disobedient Muslims. They will be punished according to the severity of their sins, but eventually no Muslim will stay in Hell forever.

6:129.text: This is how We make the wrongdoers ňĻdestructiveňļ allies of one another because of their misdeeds.

6:130.text: ňĻAllah will ask,ňļ ‚ÄúO assembly of jinn and humans! Did messengers not come from among you, proclaiming My revelations and warning you of the coming of this Day of yours?‚ÄĚ They will say, ‚ÄúWe confess against ourselves!‚ÄĚ For they have been deluded by ňĻtheirňļ worldly life. And they will testify against themselves that they were disbelievers.

6:131.text: This ňĻsending of the messengersňļ is because your Lord would never destroy a society for their wrongdoing while its people are unaware ňĻof the truthňļ.

6:132.text: They will each be assigned ranks according to their deeds. And your Lord is not unaware of what they do.

6:133.text: Your Lord is the Self-Sufficient, Full of Mercy. If He wills, He can do away with you and replace you with whoever He wills, just as He produced you from the offspring of other people.

6:134.text: Indeed, what you have been promised will certainly come to pass. And you will have no escape.

6:135.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO my people! Persist in your ways, for I ňĻtooňļ will persist in mine. You will soon know who will fare best in the end. Indeed, the wrongdoers will never succeed.‚ÄĚ

6:136.text: The pagans set aside for Allah a share of the crops and cattle He created, saying, ‚ÄúThis ňĻportionňļ is for Allah,‚ÄĚ so they claim, ‚Äúand this ňĻoneňļ for our associate-gods.‚ÄĚ Yet the portion of their associate-gods is not shared with Allah while Allah‚Äôs portion is shared with their associate-gods. What unfair judgment!

6:137.text: Likewise, the pagans‚Äô evil associates have made it appealing to them to kill their own children‚ÄĒonly leading to their destruction as well as confusion in their faith. Had it been Allah‚Äôs Will, they would not have done such a thing. So leave them and their falsehood.

6:138.text: They say, ‚ÄúThese cattle and crops are reserved‚ÄĒnone may eat them except those we permit,‚ÄĚ so they claim. Some other cattle are exempted from labour and others are not slaughtered in Allah‚Äôs Name‚ÄĒfalsely attributing lies to Him. He will repay them for their lies.

6:139.text: They ňĻalsoňļ say, ‚ÄúThe offspring of this cattle is reserved for our males and forbidden to our females; but if it is stillborn, they may all share it.‚ÄĚ He will repay them for their falsehood. Surely He is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

6:140.text: Lost indeed are those who have murdered their own children foolishly out of ignorance and have forbidden what Allah has provided for them‚ÄĒfalsely attributing lies to Allah. They have certainly strayed and are not ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

6:141.text: He is the One Who produces gardens‚ÄĒboth cultivated and wild<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒand palm trees, crops of different flavours, olives, and pomegranates‚ÄĒsimilar ňĻin shapeňļ, but dissimilar ňĻin tasteňļ. Eat of the fruit they bear and pay the dues at harvest, but do not waste. Surely He does not like the wasteful.
footnotes: ¬†Or trellised and untrellised.

6:142.text: Some cattle are fit for labour, others are too small.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Eat of what Allah has provided for you and do not follow Satan‚Äôs footsteps. Certainly, he is your sworn enemy.
footnotes: ¬†Strong cattle like camels and oxen can be used for labour, whereas smaller ones like goats and sheep are not suitable for labour but are good for their meat, milk, hide, etc.

6:143.text: ňĻAllah has createdňļ four pairs: a pair of sheep and a pair of goats‚ÄĒask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúHas He forbidden ňĻto youňļ the two males or the two females or what is in the wombs of the two females? Tell me with knowledge, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒ

6:144.text: and a pair of camels and a pair of oxen. Ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúHas He forbidden ňĻto youňļ the two males or the two females or what is in the wombs of the two females? Or were you present when Allah gave you this commandment?‚ÄĚ Who does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah to mislead others without ňĻanyňļ knowledge? Surely Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

6:145.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI do not find in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden to eat except carrion, running blood, swine‚ÄĒwhich is impure‚ÄĒor a sinful offering in the name of any other than Allah. But if someone is compelled by necessity‚ÄĒneither driven by desire nor exceeding immediate need‚ÄĒthen surely your Lord is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ

6:146.text: For those who are Jewish, We forbade every animal with undivided hoofs and the fat of oxen and sheep except what is joined to their backs or intestines or mixed with bone. In this way We rewarded them for their violations. And We are certainly truthful.

6:147.text: But if they deny you ňĻO Prophetňļ, say, ‚ÄúYour Lord is infinite in mercy, yet His punishment will not be averted from the wicked people.‚ÄĚ

6:148.text: The polytheists will argue, ‚ÄúHad it been Allah‚Äôs Will, neither we nor our forefathers would have associated others with Him ňĻin worshipňļ or made anything unlawful.‚ÄĚ Likewise, those before them rejected the truth until they tasted Our punishment. Ask ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúDo you have any knowledge that you can produce for us? Surely you follow nothing but ňĻfalseňļ assumptions and you do nothing but lie.‚ÄĚ

6:149.text: Say, ‚ÄúAllah has the most conclusive argument. Had it been His Will, He would have easily imposed guidance upon all of you.‚ÄĚ

6:150.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBring your witnesses who can testify that Allah has forbidden this.‚ÄĚ If they ňĻfalselyňļ testify, do not testify with them. And do not follow the desires of those who deny Our proofs, disbelieve in the Hereafter, and set up equals with their Lord.

6:151.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúCome! Let me recite to you what your Lord has forbidden to you: do not associate others with Him ňĻin worshipňļ. ňĻDo not fail toňļ honour your parents. Do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for you and for them. Do not come near indecencies, openly or secretly. Do not take a ňĻhumanňļ life‚ÄĒmade sacred by Allah‚ÄĒexcept with ňĻlegalňļ right.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is what He has commanded you, so perhaps you will understand.
footnotes: ¬†For example, in retaliation for intentional killing through legal channels.

6:152.text: And do not come near the wealth of the orphan‚ÄĒunless intending to enhance it‚ÄĒuntil they attain maturity. Give full measure and weigh with justice. We never require of any soul more than what it can afford. Whenever you speak,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†maintain justice‚ÄĒeven regarding a close relative. And fulfil your covenant with Allah. This is what He has commanded you, so perhaps you will be mindful.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., when you testify or judge.

6:153.text: Indeed, that is My Path‚ÄĒperfectly straight. So follow it and do not follow other ways, for they will lead you away from His Way. This is what He has commanded you, so perhaps you will be conscious ňĻof Allahňļ.‚ÄĚ

6:154.text: Additionally, We gave Moses the Scripture, completing the favour upon those who do good, detailing everything, and as a guide and a mercy, so perhaps they would be certain of the meeting with their Lord.

6:155.text: This is a blessed Book We have revealed. So follow it and be mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, so you may be shown mercy.

6:156.text: You ňĻpagansňļ can no longer say, ‚ÄúScriptures were only revealed to two groups before us and we were unaware of their teachings.‚ÄĚ

6:157.text: Nor can you say, ‚ÄúIf only the Scriptures had been revealed to us, we would have been better guided than they.‚ÄĚ Now there has come to you from your Lord a clear proof<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒa guide and mercy. Who then does more wrong than those who deny Allah‚Äôs revelations and turn away from them? We will reward those who turn away from Our revelations with a dreadful punishment for turning away.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Quran.

6:158.text: Are they awaiting the coming of the angels, or your Lord ňĻHimselfňļ, or some of your Lord‚Äôs ňĻmajorňļ signs? On the Day your Lord‚Äôs signs arrive, belief will not benefit those who did not believe earlier or those who did no good through their faith.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Say, ‚ÄúKeep waiting! We too are waiting.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This refers to disobedient believers who neither did any good nor repented before the time of their death or the arrival of the Day of Judgment. This is supported by authentic narrations from the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

6:159.text: Indeed, you ňĻO Prophetňļ are not responsible whatsoever for those who have divided their faith and split into sects. Their judgment rests only with Allah. And He will inform them of what they used to do.

6:160.text: Whoever comes with a good deed will be rewarded tenfold. But whoever comes with a bad deed will be punished for only one. None will be wronged.

6:161.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely my Lord has guided me to the Straight Path, a perfect way, the faith of Abraham, the upright, who was not one of the polytheists.‚ÄĚ

6:162.text: Say, ‚ÄúSurely my prayer, my sacrifice, my life, and my death are all for Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds.

6:163.text: He has no partner. So I am commanded, and so I am the first to submit.‚ÄĚ

6:164.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúShould I seek a lord other than Allah while He is the Lord of everything?‚ÄĚ No one will reap except what they sow. No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of your differences.

6:165.text: He is the One Who has placed you as successors on earth and elevated some of you in rank over others, so He may test you with what He has given you. Surely your Lord is swift in punishment, but He is certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

60:1.text: O believers! Do not take My enemies and yours as trusted allies, showing them affection even though they deny what has come to you of the truth. They drove the Messenger and yourselves out ňĻof Meccaňļ, simply for your belief in Allah, your Lord. If you ňĻtrulyňļ emigrated<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to struggle in My cause and seek My pleasure, ňĻthen do not take them as allies,ňļ disclosing secrets ňĻof the believersňļ to the pagans out of affection for them, when I know best whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal. And whoever of you does this has truly strayed from the Right Way.
footnotes: ¬†lit., come out ňĻof Meccaňļ.

60:2.text: If they gain the upper hand over you, they would be your ňĻopenňļ enemies, unleashing their hands and tongues to harm you, and wishing that you would abandon faith.

60:3.text: Neither your relatives nor children will benefit you on Judgment Day‚ÄĒHe will decide between you ňĻallňļ. For Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.

60:4.text: You already have an excellent example in Abraham and those with him, when they said to their people, ‚ÄúWe totally dissociate ourselves from you and ňĻshunňļ whatever ňĻidolsňļ you worship besides Allah. We reject you. The enmity and hatred that has arisen between us and you will last until you believe in Allah alone.‚ÄĚ The only exception is when Abraham said to his father, ‚ÄúI will seek forgiveness for you,ňĻ‚ÄĚ adding, ‚Äúbutňļ I cannot protect you from Allah at all.‚ÄĚ ňĻThe believers prayed,ňļ ‚ÄúOur Lord! In You we trust. And to You we ňĻalwaysňļ turn. And to You is the final return.

60:5.text: Our Lord! Do not subject us to the persecution of the disbelievers. Forgive us, our Lord! You ňĻaloneňļ are truly the Almighty, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ

60:6.text: You certainly have an excellent example in them for whoever has hope in Allah and the Last Day. But whoever turns away, then surely Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

60:7.text: ňĻIn time,ňļ Allah may bring about goodwill between you and those of them you ňĻnowňļ hold as enemies. For Allah is Most Capable. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

60:8.text: Allah does not forbid you from dealing kindly and fairly with those who have neither fought nor driven you out of your homes. Surely Allah loves those who are fair.

60:9.text: Allah only forbids you from befriending those who have fought you for ňĻyourňļ faith, driven you out of your homes, or supported ňĻothersňļ in doing so. And whoever takes them as friends, then it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.

60:10.text: O believers! When the believing women come to you as emigrants,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†test their intentions‚ÄĒtheir faith¬†is best known to Allah‚ÄĒand if you find them to be believers, then do not send them back to the disbelievers. These ňĻwomenňļ are not lawful ňĻwivesňļ for the disbelievers, nor are the disbelievers lawful ňĻhusbandsňļ for them. ňĻButňļ repay the disbelievers whatever ňĻdowriesňļ they had paid. And there is no blame on you if you marry these ňĻwomenňļ as long as you pay them their dowries. And do not hold on to marriage with polytheistic women.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†ňĻButňļ demand ňĻrepayment ofňļ whatever ňĻdowriesňļ you had paid, and let the disbelievers do the same. That is the judgment of Allah‚ÄĒHe judges between you. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†According to the Treaty of Šł§udaibiyah (see¬†footnote for 48:1-3), Muslims who chose to move to Mecca would not be returned to Muslims in Medina, and Meccan pagans who accepted Islam and chose to move to Medina would be returned to Mecca (except for women).
¬†Those who moved to Mecca to marry pagans.

60:11.text: And if any of your wives desert you to the disbelievers, and later you take spoils from them, then pay those whose wives have gone, the equivalent of whatever ňĻdowryňļ they had paid. And be mindful of Allah, in Whom you believe.

60:12.text: O Prophet! When the believing women come to you, pledging to you that they will neither associate anything with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ, nor steal, nor fornicate, nor kill their children, nor falsely attribute ňĻillegitimateňļ children to their husbands,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†nor disobey you in what is right, then accept their pledge, and ask Allah to forgive them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†lit., nor come up with a falsehood they have forged between their own hands and legs.

60:13.text: O believers! Do not ally yourselves with a people Allah is displeased with. They already have no hope for the Hereafter, just like the disbelievers lying in ňĻtheirňļ graves.

61:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah. For He ňĻaloneňļ is the Almighty, All-Wise.

61:2.text: O believers! Why do you say what you do not do?

61:3.text: How despicable it is in the sight of Allah that you say what you do not do!

61:4.text: Surely Allah loves those who fight in His cause in ňĻsolidňļ ranks as if they were one concrete structure.

61:5.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when Moses said to his people, ‚ÄúO my people! Why do you hurt me when you already know I am Allah‚Äôs messenger to you?‚ÄĚ So when they ňĻpersistentlyňļ deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate. For Allah does not guide the rebellious people.

61:6.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Jesus, son of Mary, said, ‚ÄúO children of Israel! I am truly Allah‚Äôs messenger to you, confirming the Torah which came before me, and giving good news of a messenger after me whose name will be AŠł•mad.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet when the Prophet came to them with clear proofs, they said, ‚ÄúThis is pure magic.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†AŠł•mad is another name for Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ). Both are derived from Šł•a-ma-da¬†which means ‚Äėpraise.‚Äô Some Muslim scholars believe this verse refers to John 14:16, where Jesus says: ‚ÄúAnd I will ask the Father, and he will give you another periklytos, to be with you forever.‚ÄĚ Periklytos is a Greek word that means ‚Äėthe praised one.‚Äô The name of Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) appears several times in the Gospel of Barnabas, which is deemed apocryphal by Christian authorities.

61:7.text: Who does more wrong than the one who fabricates lies about Allah when invited to submit ňĻto Himňļ?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†For Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
footnotes: ¬†Submission to Allah means ‚ÄėIslam‚Äô in Arabic.

61:8.text: They wish to extinguish Allah‚Äôs light with their mouths, but Allah will ňĻcertainlyňļ perfect His light, even to the dismay of the disbelievers.

61:9.text: He is the One Who has sent His Messenger with ňĻtrueňļ guidance and the religion of truth, making it prevail over all others, even to the dismay of the polytheists.

61:10.text: O believers! Shall I guide you to an exchange that will save you from a painful punishment?

61:11.text: ňĻIt is toňļ have faith in Allah and His Messenger, and strive in the cause of Allah with your wealth and your lives. That is best for you, if only you knew.

61:12.text: He will forgive your sins, and admit you into Gardens under which rivers flow, and ňĻhouse you inňļ splendid homes in the Gardens of Eternity. That is the ultimate triumph.

61:13.text: ňĻHe will also give youňļ another favour that you long for: help from Allah and an imminent victory. ňĻSoňļ give good news ňĻO Prophetňļ to the believers.

61:14.text: O believers! Stand up for Allah, as Jesus, son of Mary, asked the disciples, ‚ÄúWho will stand up with me for Allah?‚ÄĚ The disciples replied, ‚ÄúWe will stand up for Allah.‚ÄĚ Then a group from the Children of Israel believed while another disbelieved. We then supported the believers against their enemies, so they prevailed.

62:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth ňĻconstantlyňļ glorifies Allah‚ÄĒthe King, the Most Holy, the Almighty, the All-Wise.

62:2.text: He is the One Who raised for the illiterate ňĻpeopleňļ a messenger from among themselves‚ÄĒreciting to them His revelations, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom, for indeed they had previously been clearly astray‚ÄĒ

62:3.text: along with others of them who have not yet joined them ňĻin faithňļ. For He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

62:4.text: This is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

62:5.text: The example of those who were entrusted with ňĻobservingňļ the Torah but failed to do so, is that of a donkey carrying books.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†How evil is the example of those who reject Allah‚Äôs signs! For Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, they can carry the physical burden of the books, but not understand a word of them.

62:6.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO Jews! If you claim to be Allah‚Äôs chosen ňĻpeopleňļ out of all humanity, then wish for death, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

62:7.text: But they will never wish for that because of what their hands have done.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of the wrongdoers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., disobeying Allah, killing some of the prophets (including Zachariah and John the Baptist), claiming to have killed Jesus, accusing Mary of adultery, and dealing with usury. See¬†4:153-158.

62:8.text: Say, ‚ÄúThe death you are running away from will inevitably come to you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the seen and unseen, and He will inform you of what you used to do.‚ÄĚ

62:9.text: O believers! When the call to prayer is made on Friday, then proceed ňĻdiligentlyňļ to the remembrance of Allah and leave off ňĻyourňļ business. That is best for you, if only you knew.

62:10.text: Once the prayer is over, disperse throughout the land and seek the bounty of Allah. And remember Allah often so you may be successful.

62:11.text: When they saw the fanfare along with the caravan, they ňĻalmost allňļ flocked to it, leaving you ňĻO Prophetňļ standing ňĻon the pulpitňļ. Say, ‚ÄúWhat is with Allah is far better than amusement and merchandise. And Allah is the Best Provider.‚ÄĚ

63:1.text: When the hypocrites come to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, they say, ‚ÄúWe bear witness that you are certainly the Messenger of Allah‚ÄĚ‚ÄĒand surely Allah knows that you are His Messenger‚ÄĒbut Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are truly liars.

63:2.text: They have made their ňĻfalseňļ oaths as a shield, hindering ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah. Evil indeed is what they do!

63:3.text: This is because they believed and then abandoned faith. Therefore, their hearts have been sealed, so they do not comprehend.

63:4.text: When you see them, their appearance impresses you. And when they speak, you listen to their ňĻimpressiveňļ speech. But they are ňĻjustňļ like ňĻworthlessňļ planks of wood leaned ňĻagainst a wallňļ. They think every cry is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allah condemn them! How can they be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

63:5.text: When it is said to them, ‚ÄúCome! The Messenger of Allah will pray for you to be forgiven,‚ÄĚ they turn their heads ňĻin disgustňļ, and you see them ňĻO Prophetňļ turn away in arrogance.

63:6.text: It is the same whether you pray for their forgiveness or not, Allah will not forgive them. Surely Allah does not guide the rebellious people.

63:7.text: They are the ones who say ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúDo not spend ňĻanythingňļ on those ňĻemigrantsňļ with the Messenger of Allah so that they will break away ňĻfrom himňļ.‚ÄĚ But to Allah ňĻaloneňļ belong the treasuries of the heavens and the earth, yet the hypocrites do not comprehend.

63:8.text: They say, ‚ÄúIf we return to Medina, the honourable will definitely expel the inferior.‚ÄĚ But all honour and power belongs to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, yet the hypocrites do not know.

63:9.text: O believers! Do not let your wealth or your children divert you from the remembrance of Allah. For whoever does so, it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ losers.

63:10.text: And donate from what We have provided for you before death comes to one of you, and you cry, ‚ÄúMy Lord! If only You delayed me for a short while, I would give in charity and be one of the righteous.‚ÄĚ

63:11.text: But Allah never delays a soul when its appointed time comes. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

64:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth ňĻconstantlyňļ glorifies Allah. The kingdom is His, and all praise is for Him. For He is Most Capable of everything.

64:2.text: He is the One Who created you, yet some of you are disbelievers while some are believers. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.

64:3.text: He created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. He shaped you ňĻin the wombňļ, perfecting your form. And to Him is the final return.

64:4.text: He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And He knows whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal. For Allah knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

64:5.text: Have the stories of those who disbelieved before not reached you ňĻpagansňļ? They tasted the evil consequences of their doings, and they will suffer a painful punishment.

64:6.text: That was because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs, but they said ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúHow can humans be our guides?‚ÄĚ So they persisted in disbelief and turned away. And Allah was not in need ňĻof their faithňļ. For Allah is Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

64:7.text: The disbelievers claim they will not be resurrected. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYes, by my Lord, you will surely be resurrected, then you will certainly be informed of what you have done. And that is easy for Allah.‚ÄĚ

64:8.text: So believe in Allah and His Messenger and in the Light<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We have revealed. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Quran.

64:9.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day He will gather you ňĻallňļ for the Day of Gathering‚ÄĒthat will be the Day of mutual loss and gain. So whoever believes in Allah and does good, He will absolve them of their sins and admit them into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. That is the ultimate triumph.

64:10.text: As for those who disbelieve and reject Our revelations, they will be the residents of the Fire, staying there forever. What an evil destination!

64:11.text: No calamity befalls ňĻanyoneňļ except by Allah‚Äôs Will. And whoever has faith in Allah, He will ňĻrightlyňļ guide their hearts ňĻthrough adversityňļ. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

64:12.text: Obey Allah and obey the Messenger! But if you turn away, then Our Messenger‚Äôs duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.

64:13.text: Allah‚ÄĒthere is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. So in Allah let the believers put their trust.

64:14.text: O believers! Indeed, some of your spouses and children are enemies to you,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so beware of them. But if you pardon, overlook, and forgive ňĻtheir faultsňļ, then Allah is truly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†This verse was revealed when some early Muslims were prevented from emigrating to Medina by their own spouses and children, so they could not join the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and the rest of the believers to practice their faith freely.

64:15.text: Your wealth and children are only a test, but Allah ňĻaloneňļ has a great reward.

64:16.text: So be mindful of Allah to the best of your ability, hear and obey, and spend in charity‚ÄĒthat will be best for you. And whoever is saved from the selfishness of their own souls, it is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ successful.

64:17.text: If you lend to Allah a good loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you. For Allah is Most Appreciative, Most Forbearing.

64:18.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Knower of the seen and unseen‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.

65:1.text: O Prophet! ňĻInstruct the believers:ňļ When you ňĻintend toňļ divorce women, then divorce them with concern for their waiting period,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and count it accurately. And¬†fear Allah, your Lord. Do not force them out of their homes, nor should they leave‚ÄĒunless they commit a blatant misconduct. These are the limits set by Allah. And whoever transgresses Allah‚Äôs limits has truly wronged his own soul. You never know, perhaps Allah will bring about a change ňĻof heartňļ later.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, when a husband intends to divorce his wife‚ÄĒafter the consummation of marriage‚ÄĒhe should divorce her outside her monthly cycle, provided that he has not touched her after her period. This makes it easy for the wife to observe her ‚Äôiddah¬†(waiting period for around three months, see¬†65:4). Otherwise, things will be complicated for her. For example, if divorce happens after sexual intercourse, she might get pregnant, which delays the end of ‚Äôiddah¬†until the end of her pregnancy. If divorce happens during a monthly cycle, scholars are in disagreement as to whether the divorce counts or not. If it does not, she has to wait until her menstruation is over to see if her husband still wants to divorce her.
¬†Perhaps the husband who has divorced his wife may change his mind and restore the marriage before the end of her waiting period (after the first or second count of revocable divorce).

65:2.text: Then when they have ňĻalmostňļ reached the end of their waiting period, either retain them honourably or separate from them honourably.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And call two of your reliable men to witness ňĻeither wayňļ‚ÄĒand ňĻlet the witnessesňļ bear true testimony for ňĻthe sake ofňļ Allah. This is enjoined on whoever has faith in Allah and the Last Day. And whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make a way out for them,
footnotes: ¬†A husband may separate from his wife after each of the first two counts of divorce or at the end of her waiting period with dignity. If he chooses to stay with her after the first two counts of divorce then divorces her a third time, the marriage is terminated at the end of her third waiting period. The wife will have to marry and divorce another man before she can be remarried to her ex-husband (see¬†2:230). However, a woman marrying someone with the intention of getting divorced, in order to return to her first husband, is forbidden.

65:3.text: and provide for them from sources they could never imagine. And whoever puts their trust in Allah, then He ňĻaloneňļ is sufficient for them. Certainly Allah achieves His Will. Allah has already set a destiny for everything.

65:4.text: As for your women past the age of menstruation, in case you do not know, their waiting period is three months, and those who have not menstruated as well. As for those who are pregnant, their waiting period ends with delivery.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make their matters easy for them.
footnotes: ¬†This applies to pregnant women who are divorced or widowed.

65:5.text: This is the commandment of Allah, which He has revealed to you. And whoever is mindful of Allah, He will absolve them of their sins and reward them immensely.

65:6.text: Let them live where you live ňĻduring their waiting periodňļ, according to your means. And do not harass them to make their stay unbearable. If they are pregnant, then maintain them until they deliver. And if they nurse your child,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†compensate them, and consult together courteously. But if you fail to reach an agreement, then another woman will nurse ňĻthe childňļ for the father.
footnotes: ¬†After the divorce is finalized.

65:7.text: Let the man of wealth provide according to his means. As for the one with limited resources, let him provide according to whatever Allah has given him. Allah does not require of any soul beyond what He has given it. After hardship, Allah will bring about ease.

65:8.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many societies rebelled against the commandments of their Lord and His messengers, so We called each ňĻsocietyňļ to a severe account and subjected them to a horrible punishment.

65:9.text: So they tasted the evil consequences of their doings, and the outcome of their doings was ňĻtotalňļ loss.

65:10.text: Allah has ňĻalsoňļ prepared for them a severe punishment. So fear Allah, O ¬†people of reason and faith. Allah has indeed revealed to you a Reminder,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This is one of the names of the Quran.

65:11.text: ňĻand sentňļ a messenger reciting to you Allah‚Äôs revelations, making things clear so that He may bring those who believe and do good out of darkness and into light. And whoever believes in Allah and does good will be admitted by Him into Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. Allah will have indeed granted them an excellent provision.

65:12.text: Allah is the One Who created seven heavens ňĻin layersňļ, and likewise for the earth. The ňĻdivineňļ command descends between them so you may know that Allah is Most Capable of everything and that Allah certainly encompasses all things in ňĻHisňļ knowledge.

66:1.text: O Prophet! Why do you prohibit ňĻyourselfňļ from what Allah has made lawful to you, seeking to please your wives? And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

66:2.text: Allah has already ordained for you ňĻbelieversňļ the way to absolve yourselves from your oaths.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†For Allah is your Guardian. And He is the All-Knowing, All-Wise.

66:3.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the Prophet had ňĻonceňļ confided something to one of his wives, then when she disclosed it ňĻto another wifeňļ and Allah made it known to him, he presented ňĻto herňļ part of what was disclosed and overlooked a part. So when he informed her of it, she exclaimed, ‚ÄúWho told you this?‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúI was informed by the All-Knowing, All-Aware.‚ÄĚ

66:4.text: ňĻIt will be betterňļ if you ňĻwivesňļ both turn to Allah in repentance, for your hearts have certainly faltered. But if you ňĻcontinue toňļ collaborate against him, then ňĻknow thatňļ Allah Himself is his Guardian. And Gabriel, the righteous believers, and the angels are ňĻallňļ his supporters as well.

66:5.text: Perhaps, if he were to divorce you ňĻallňļ, his Lord would replace you with better wives who are submissive ňĻto Allahňļ, faithful ňĻto Himňļ, devout, repentant, dedicated to worship and fasting‚ÄĒpreviously married or virgins.

66:6.text: O believers! Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, overseen by formidable and severe angels, who never disobey whatever Allah orders‚ÄĒalways doing as commanded.

66:7.text: ňĻThe deniers will then be told,ňļ ‚ÄúO disbelievers! Make no excuses this Day! You are only rewarded for what you used to do.‚ÄĚ

66:8.text: O believers! Turn to Allah in sincere repentance, so your Lord may absolve you of your sins and admit you into Gardens, under which rivers flow, on the Day Allah will not disgrace the Prophet or the believers with him. Their light will shine ahead of them and on their right. They will say, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Perfect our light for us, and forgive us. ňĻForňļ You are truly Most Capable of everything.‚ÄĚ

66:9.text: O Prophet! Struggle against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be firm with them. Hell will be their home. What an evil destination!

66:10.text: Allah sets forth an example for the disbelievers: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. Each was married to one of Our righteous servants, yet betrayed them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So their husbands were of no benefit to them against Allah whatsoever. Both were told, ‚ÄúEnter the Fire, along with the others!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Both wives were disbelievers. Noah‚Äôs wife used to give away the identity of the new believers so the disbelievers could persecute them. And Lot‚Äôs wife used to tell the men about her husband‚Äôs handsome male visitors so they could approach them.

66:11.text: And Allah sets forth an example for the believers: the wife of Pharaoh, who prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Build me a house in Paradise near You, deliver me from Pharaoh and his ňĻevilňļ doing, and save me from the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

66:12.text: ňĻThere isňļ also ňĻthe example ofňļ Mary, the daughter of ‚ÄôImr√Ęn, who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her ňĻwombňļ through Our angel ňĻGabrielňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†She testified to the words of her Lord and His Scriptures, and was one of the ňĻsincerelyňļ devout.
footnotes: ¬†Gabriel breathed into the sleeves of Mary‚Äôs garment so she conceived Jesus (Ô∑ļ).

67:1.text: Blessed is the One in Whose Hands rests all authority. And He is Most Capable of everything.

67:2.text: ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who created death and life in order to test which of you is best in deeds. And He is the Almighty, All-Forgiving.

67:3.text: ňĻHe is the Oneňļ Who created seven heavens, one above the other. You will never see any imperfection in the creation of the Most Compassionate.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So look again: do you see any flaws?
footnotes: ¬†Some may point to birth defects, genetic mutations, etc. and say these are flaws. This verse means that, in the grand scheme of creation, everything created has its place, form, and purpose, and that it does exactly what Allah ordained for it.

67:4.text: Then look again and again‚ÄĒyour sight will return frustrated and weary.

67:5.text: And indeed, We adorned the lowest heaven with ňĻstars likeňļ lamps, and made them ňĻas missilesňļ for stoning ňĻeavesdroppingňļ devils, for whom We have also prepared the torment of the Blaze.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†

67:6.text: Those who disbelieve in their Lord will suffer the punishment of Hell. What an evil destination!

67:7.text: When they are tossed into it, they will hear its roaring as it boils over,

67:8.text: almost bursting in fury. Every time a group is cast into it, its keepers will ask them, ‚ÄúDid a warner not come to you?‚ÄĚ

67:9.text: They will reply, ‚ÄúYes, a warner did come to us, but we denied and said, ‚ÄėAllah has revealed nothing. You are extremely astray.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

67:10.text: And they will lament, ‚ÄúIf only we had listened and reasoned, we would not be among the residents of the Blaze!‚ÄĚ

67:11.text: And so they will confess their sins. So away with the residents of the Blaze!

67:12.text: Indeed, those in awe of their Lord without seeing Him<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†will have forgiveness and a mighty reward.
footnotes: ¬†This can also mean that they are in awe of their Lord as much in private as they are in public.

67:13.text: Whether you speak secretly or openly‚ÄĒHe surely knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

67:14.text: How could He not know His Own creation? For He ňĻaloneňļ is the Most Subtle, All-Aware.

67:15.text: He is the One Who smoothed out the earth for you, so move about in its regions and eat from His provisions. And to Him is the resurrection ňĻof allňļ.

67:16.text: Do you feel secure that the One Who is in heaven will not cause the earth to swallow you up as it quakes violently?

67:17.text: Or do you feel secure that the One Who is in heaven will not unleash upon you a storm of stones. Only then would you know how ňĻseriousňļ My warning was!

67:18.text: And certainly those before them denied ňĻas wellňļ, then how severe was My response!

67:19.text: Have they not seen the birds above them, spreading and folding their wings? None holds them up except the Most Compassionate. Indeed, He is All-Seeing of everything.

67:20.text: Also, which ňĻpowerlessňļ force will come to your help instead of the Most Compassionate? Indeed, the disbelievers are only ňĻlostňļ in delusion.

67:21.text: Or who is it that will provide for you if He withholds His provision? In fact, they persist in arrogance and aversion ňĻto the truthňļ.

67:22.text: Who is ňĻrightlyňļ guided: the one who crawls facedown or the one who walks upright on the Straight Path?

67:23.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHe is the One Who brought you into being and gave you hearing, sight, and intellect. ňĻYetňļ you hardly give any thanks.‚ÄĚ

67:24.text: ňĻAlsoňļ say, ‚ÄúHe is the One Who has dispersed you ňĻallňļ over the earth, and to Him you will ňĻallňļ be gathered.‚ÄĚ

67:25.text: ňĻStillňļ they ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ

67:26.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThat knowledge is with Allah alone, and I am only sent with a clear warning.‚ÄĚ

67:27.text: Then when they see the torment drawing near, the faces of the disbelievers will become gloomy, and it will be said ňĻto themňļ, ‚ÄúThis is what you claimed would never come.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúThis is what you were calling for.‚ÄĚ

67:28.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúConsider this: whether Allah causes me and those with me to die or shows us mercy, who will save the disbelievers from a painful punishment?‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, whether Allah takes our lives (as you wish) or grants us a long life, it is all good for us. But who will protect you from Allah if you disbelieve in Him.

67:29.text: Say, ‚ÄúHe is the Most Compassionate‚ÄĒin Him ňĻaloneňļ we believe, and in Him ňĻaloneňļ we trust. You will soon know who is clearly astray.‚ÄĚ

67:30.text: Say, ‚ÄúConsider this: if your water were to sink ňĻinto the earthňļ, then who ňĻelseňļ could bring you flowing water?‚ÄĚ

68:1.text: NŇ©n. By the pen and what everyone writes!

68:2.text: By the grace of your Lord, you ňĻO Prophetňļ are not insane.

68:3.text: You will certainly have a never-ending reward.

68:4.text: And you are truly ňĻa manňļ of outstanding character.

68:5.text: Soon you and the pagans will see,

68:7.text: Surely your Lord ňĻaloneňļ knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

68:8.text: So do not give in to the deniers.

68:9.text: They wish you would compromise so they would yield ňĻto youňļ.

68:10.text: And do not obey the despicable, vain oath-taker,

68:13.text: brute, and‚ÄĒon top of all that‚ÄĒan illegitimate child.

68:14.text: Now, ňĻsimplyňļ because he has been blessed with ňĻabundantňļ wealth and children,

68:15.text: whenever Our revelations are recited to him, he says, ‚ÄúAncient fables!‚ÄĚ

68:16.text: We will soon mark his snout.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Like 53:33-35 and 74:11-26, these verses refer to Al-Wal√ģd ibn Al-Mugh√ģrah, a leader of the Meccan opposition against Islam. He rejected the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) as a madman, so the Quran responded by listing ten of his qualities‚ÄĒtwo of which were unknown to him: the fact that he was born out of wedlock and that his nose would be chopped off several years later at the Battle of Badr. At least three of his ten sons accepted Islam‚ÄĒincluding Kh√Ęlid ibn Al-Wal√ģd.

68:17.text: Indeed, We have tested those ňĻMeccansňļ as We tested the owners of the garden‚ÄĒwhen they swore they would surely harvest ňĻallňļ its fruit in the early morning,

68:18.text: leaving no thought for Allah‚Äôs Will.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚Äú‚Ä¶ without leaving some for the poor.‚ÄĚ

68:19.text: Then it was struck by a torment from your Lord while they slept,

68:20.text: so it was reduced to ashes.

68:21.text: Then by daybreak they called out to each other,

68:22.text: ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúGo early to your harvest, if you want to pick ňĻallňļ the fruit.‚ÄĚ

68:23.text: So they went off, whispering to one another,

68:24.text: ‚ÄúDo not let any poor person enter your garden today.‚ÄĚ

68:25.text: And they proceeded early, totally fixated on their purpose.

68:26.text: But when they saw it ňĻdevastatedňļ, they cried, ‚ÄúWe must have lost ňĻourňļ way!

68:27.text: In fact, we have been deprived ňĻof our livelihoodňļ.‚ÄĚ

68:28.text: The most sensible of them said, ‚ÄúDid I not urge you to say, ‚ÄėAllah willing.‚Äô?‚ÄĚ

68:29.text: They replied, ‚ÄúGlory be to our Lord! We have truly been wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

68:30.text: Then they turned on each other, throwing blame.

68:31.text: They said, ‚ÄúWoe to us! We have certainly been transgressors.

68:32.text: We trust our Lord will give us a better garden than this, ňĻforňļ we are indeed turning to our Lord with hope.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It is believed that Allah accepted their repentance and replaced their garden with a better one.

68:33.text: That is the ňĻway of Ourňļ punishment ňĻin this worldňļ. But the punishment of the Hereafter is certainly far worse, if only they knew.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†i.e., if only the Meccan pagans knew.

68:34.text: Indeed, the righteous will have the Gardens of Bliss with their Lord.

68:35.text: Should We then treat those who have submitted like the wicked?

68:36.text: What is the matter with you? How do you judge?

68:37.text: Or do you have a scripture, in which you read

68:38.text: that you will have whatever you choose?

68:39.text: Or do you have oaths binding on Us until the Day of Judgment that you will have whatever you decide?

68:40.text: Ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ which of them can guarantee all that.

68:41.text: Or do they have associate-gods ňĻsupporting this claimňļ? Then let them bring forth their associate-gods, if what they say is true.

68:42.text: ňĻBeware ofňļ the Day the Shin ňĻof Allahňļ will be bared,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and the wicked will be asked to prostrate, but they will not be able to do so,
footnotes: ¬†Like the Face and the Hands, the Shin is believed by many to be one of the qualities of Allah, in a way befitting His Majesty and Greatness. Since baring the shin in the Arab culture is associated with the heat of battle, some interpret the verse metaphorically, so the meaning would be: ‚ÄúňĻBeware ofňļ the Day when horror sets in.‚ÄĚ

68:43.text: with eyes downcast, totally covered with disgrace. For they were ňĻalwaysňļ called to prostrate ňĻin the worldňļ when they were fully capable ňĻbut they chose not toňļ.

68:44.text: So leave to Me ňĻO Prophetňļ those who reject this message. We will gradually draw them to destruction in ways they cannot comprehend.

68:45.text: I ňĻonlyňļ delay their end for a while, but My planning is flawless.

68:46.text: Or are you asking them for a reward ňĻfor the messageňļ so that they are overburdened by debt?

68:47.text: Or do they have access to ňĻthe Record inňļ the unseen, so they copy it ňĻfor all to seeňļ?

68:48.text: So be patient with your Lord‚Äôs decree, and do not be like ňĻJonah,ňļ the Man of the Whale, who cried out ňĻto Allahňļ, in total distress.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†While inside the whale.¬†See¬†21:87 and 37:139-148.

68:49.text: Had he not been shown grace by his Lord, he would have certainly been cast onto the open ňĻshoreňļ, still blameworthy.

68:50.text: Then his Lord chose him, making him one of the righteous.

68:51.text: The disbelievers would almost cut you down with their eyes when they hear ňĻyou reciteňļ the Reminder,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and say, ‚ÄúHe is certainly a madman.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†This is one of the names of the Quran.

68:52.text: But it is simply a reminder to the whole world.

69:3.text: And what will make you realize what the Inevitable Hour is?

69:4.text: ňĻBothňļ Tham√Ľd and ‚Äô»ād denied the Striking Disaster.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The Striking Disaster is another name for the Day of Judgment.

69:5.text: As for Tham√Ľd, they were destroyed by an overwhelming blast.

69:6.text: And as for ‚Äô»ād, they were destroyed by a furious, bitter wind

69:7.text: which Allah unleashed on them non-stop for seven nights and eight days, so that you would have seen its people lying dead like trunks of uprooted palm trees.

69:8.text: Do you see any of them left alive?

69:9.text: Also, Pharaoh and those before him, and ňĻthe people ofňļ the overturned cities ňĻof Lotňļ indulged in sin,

69:10.text: each disobeying their Lord‚Äôs messenger, so He seized them with a crushing grip.

69:11.text: Indeed, when the floodwater had overflowed, We carried you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†in the floating Ark ňĻwith Noahňļ,
footnotes: ¬†i.e., your ancestors.

69:12.text: so that We may make this a reminder to you, and that attentive ears may grasp it.

69:13.text: At last, when the Trumpet will be blown with one blast,

69:14.text: and the earth and mountains will be lifted up and crushed with one blow,

69:15.text: on that Day the Inevitable Event will have come to pass.

69:16.text: The sky will then be so torn that it will be frail,

69:17.text: with the angels on its sides. On that Day eight ňĻmighty angelsňļ will bear the Throne of your Lord above them.

69:18.text: You will then be presented ňĻbefore Him for judgmentňļ, and none of your secrets will stay hidden.

69:19.text: As for those given their records in their right hand, they will cry ňĻhappilyňļ, ‚ÄúHere ňĻeveryoneňļ! Read my record!

69:20.text: I surely knew I would face my reckoning.‚ÄĚ

69:21.text: They will be in a life of bliss,

69:23.text: whose fruit will hang within reach.

69:24.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúEat and drink joyfully for what you did in the days gone by.‚ÄĚ

69:25.text: And as for those given their record in their left hand, they will cry ňĻbitterlyňļ, ‚ÄúI wish I had not been given my record,

69:26.text: nor known anything of my reckoning!

69:27.text: I wish death was the end!

69:28.text: My wealth has not benefited me!

69:29.text: My authority has been stripped from me.‚ÄĚ

69:30.text: ňĻIt will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúSeize and shackle them,

69:32.text: then tie them up with chains seventy arms long.

69:33.text: For they never had faith in Allah, the Greatest,

69:34.text: nor encouraged the feeding of the poor.

69:35.text: So this Day they will have no close friend here,

69:36.text: nor any food except ňĻoozingňļ pus,

69:37.text: which none will eat except the evildoers.‚ÄĚ

69:38.text: Now, I do swear by whatever you see,

69:40.text: Indeed, this ňĻQuranňļ is the recitation of a noble Messenger.

69:41.text: It is not the prose of a poet ňĻas you claimňļ, ňĻyetňļ you hardly have any faith.

69:42.text: Nor is it the mumbling of a fortune-teller, ňĻyetňļ you are hardly mindful.

69:43.text: ňĻIt isňļ a revelation from the Lord of all worlds.

69:44.text: Had the Messenger made up something in Our Name,

69:45.text: We would have certainly seized him by his right hand,

69:47.text: and none of you could have shielded him ňĻfrom Usňļ!

69:48.text: Indeed, this ňĻQuranňļ is a reminder to those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.

69:49.text: And We certainly know that some of you will persist in denial,

69:50.text: and it will surely be a source of regret for the disbelievers.

69:51.text: And indeed, this ňĻQuranňļ is the absolute truth.

69:52.text: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Greatest.

7:2.text: ňĻThis isňļ a Book sent down to you ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒdo not let anxiety into your heart regarding it‚ÄĒso with it you may warn ňĻthe disbelieversňļ, and as a reminder to the believers.

7:3.text: Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not take others as guardians besides Him. How seldom are you mindful!

7:4.text: ňĻImagineňļ how many societies We have destroyed! Our torment took them by surprise ňĻwhile sleepingňļ at night or midday.

7:5.text: Their only cry‚ÄĒwhen overwhelmed by Our torment‚ÄĒwas, ‚ÄúWe have indeed been wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

7:6.text: We will surely question those who received messengers and We will question the messengers ňĻthemselvesňļ.

7:7.text: Then We will give them a full account with sure knowledge‚ÄĒfor We were never absent.

7:8.text: The weighing on that Day will be just. As for those whose scale will be heavy ňĻwith good deedsňļ, ňĻonlyňļ they will be successful.

7:9.text: But those whose scale is light, they have doomed themselves for wrongfully denying Our signs.

7:10.text: We have indeed established you on earth and provided you with a means of livelihood. ňĻYetňļ you seldom give any thanks.

7:11.text: Surely We created you,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then shaped you, then said to the angels, ‚ÄúProstrate before Adam,‚ÄĚ so they all did‚ÄĒbut not Ibl√ģs,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†who refused to prostrate with the others.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., your father, Adam (Ô∑ļ).
¬†See¬†footnote for 2:34.

7:12.text: Allah asked, ‚ÄúWhat prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúI am better than he is: You created me from fire and him from clay.‚ÄĚ

7:13.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúThen get down from Paradise! It is not for you to be arrogant here. So get out! You are truly one of the disgraced.‚ÄĚ

7:14.text: He appealed, ‚ÄúThen delay my end until the Day of their resurrection.‚ÄĚ

7:15.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúYou are delayed ňĻuntil the appointed Dayňļ.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Satan asked to be allowed to live until humans are resurrected in order to escape death at the end of time. He was told he was going to live only until the time appointed by Allah Almighty.

7:16.text: He said, ‚ÄúFor leaving me to stray I will lie in ambush for them on Your Straight Path.

7:17.text: I will approach them from their front, their back, their right, their left, and then You will find most of them ungrateful.‚ÄĚ

7:18.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúGet out of Paradise! You are disgraced and rejected! I will certainly fill up Hell with you and your followers all together.‚ÄĚ

7:19.text: ňĻAllah said,ňļ ‚ÄúO Adam! Live with your wife in Paradise and eat from wherever you please, but do not approach this tree, or else you will be wrongdoers.‚ÄĚ

7:20.text: Then Satan tempted them in order to expose what was hidden of their nakedness. He said, ‚ÄúYour Lord has forbidden this tree to you only to prevent you from becoming angels or immortals.‚ÄĚ

7:21.text: And he swore to them, ‚ÄúI am truly your sincere advisor.‚ÄĚ

7:22.text: So he brought about their fall through deception. And when they tasted of the tree, their nakedness was exposed to them, prompting them to cover themselves with leaves from Paradise. Then their Lord called out to them, ‚ÄúDid I not forbid you from that tree and ňĻdid I notňļ tell you that Satan is your sworn enemy?‚ÄĚ

7:23.text: They replied, ‚ÄúOur Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will certainly be losers.‚ÄĚ

7:24.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúDescend as enemies to each other.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†You will find in the earth a residence and provision for your appointed stay.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†There will be enmity between humans and Satan.

7:25.text: He added, ‚ÄúThere you will live, there you will die, and from there you will be resurrected.‚ÄĚ

7:26.text: O children of Adam! We have provided for you clothing to cover your nakedness and as an adornment. However, the best clothing is righteousness. This is one of Allah‚Äôs bounties, so perhaps you will be mindful.

7:27.text: O children of Adam! Do not let Satan deceive you as he tempted your parents out of Paradise and caused their cover to be removed in order to expose their nakedness. Surely he and his soldiers watch you from where you cannot see them. We have made the devils allies of those who disbelieve.

7:28.text: Whenever they commit a shameful deed,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†they say, ‚ÄúWe found our forefathers doing it and Allah has commanded us to do it.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúNo! Allah never commands what is shameful. How can you attribute to Allah what you do not know?‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†It was customary for the pagans of Mecca to circle the Ka‚Äôbah while naked. So verses 26-31 of this s√Ľrah¬†were revealed commanding the believers to cover themselves properly when praying to their Lord.

7:29.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúMy Lord has commanded uprightness and dedication ňĻto Him aloneňļ in worship, calling upon Him with sincere devotion. Just as He first brought you into being, you will be brought to life again.‚ÄĚ

7:30.text: He has guided some, while others are destined to stray. They have taken devils as their masters instead of Allah‚ÄĒthinking they are ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

7:31.text: O Children of Adam! Dress properly whenever you are at worship. Eat and drink, but do not waste. Surely He does not like the wasteful.

7:32.text: Ask, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWho has forbidden the adornments and lawful provisions Allah has brought forth for His servants?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúThey are for the enjoyment of the believers in this worldly life, but they will be exclusively theirs on the Day of Judgment.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†This is how We make Our revelations clear for people of knowledge.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The pleasures of this worldly life are shared by believers and disbelievers, whereas the pleasures of the Hereafter will be enjoyed exclusively by the believers.

7:33.text: Say, ‚ÄúMy Lord has only forbidden open and secret indecencies, sinfulness, unjust aggression, associating ňĻothersňļ with Allah ňĻin worshipňļ‚ÄĒa practice He has never authorized‚ÄĒand attributing to Allah what you do not know.‚ÄĚ

7:34.text: For each community there is an appointed term. When their time arrives, they can neither delay it for a moment, nor could they advance it.

7:35.text: O children of Adam! When messengers from among yourselves come to you reciting My revelations‚ÄĒwhoever shuns evil and mends their ways, there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve.

7:36.text: But those who receive Our revelations with denial and arrogance will be the residents of the Fire. They will be there forever.

7:37.text: Who does more wrong than those who fabricate lies against Allah or deny His revelations? They will receive what is destined for them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†until Our messenger-angels arrive to take their souls, asking them, ‚ÄúWhere are those ňĻfalse godsňļ you used to invoke besides Allah?‚ÄĚ They will cry, ‚ÄúThey have failed us,‚ÄĚ and they will confess against themselves that they were indeed disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., they will receive all provisions and bounties destined for them by their Lord.

7:38.text: Allah will say, ‚ÄúEnter the Fire along with the ňĻevilňļ groups of jinn and humans that preceded you.‚ÄĚ Whenever a group enters Hell, it will curse the preceding one until they are all gathered inside, the followers will say about their leaders, ‚ÄúOur Lord! They have misled us, so multiply their torment in the Fire.‚ÄĚ He will answer, ‚ÄúIt has already been multiplied for all, but you do not know.‚ÄĚ

7:39.text: Then the leaders will say to their followers, ‚ÄúYou were no better than us! So taste the torment for what you used to commit.‚ÄĚ

7:40.text: Surely those who receive our revelations with denial and arrogance, the gates of heaven will not be opened for them, nor will they enter Paradise until a camel passes through the eye of a needle. This is how We reward the wicked.

7:41.text: Hell will be their bed; flames will be their cover. This is how We reward the wrongdoers.

7:42.text: As for those who believe and do good‚ÄĒWe never require of any soul more than what it can afford‚ÄĒit is they who will be the residents of Paradise. They will be there forever.

7:43.text: We will remove whatever bitterness they had in their hearts.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Rivers will flow under their feet. And they will say, ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah for guiding us to this. We would have never been guided if Allah had not guided us.
