The messengers of our Lord had certainly come with the truth.‚ÄĚ It will be announced to them, ‚ÄúThis is Paradise awarded to you for what you used to do.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the bitterness they had in their hearts towards other believers who wronged them in the worldly life.

7:44.text: The residents of Paradise will call out to the residents of the Fire, ‚ÄúWe have certainly found our Lord‚Äôs promise to be true. Have you too found your Lord‚Äôs promise to be true?‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúYes, we have!‚ÄĚ Then a caller will announce to both, ‚ÄúMay Allah‚Äôs condemnation be upon the wrongdoers,

7:45.text: those who hindered ňĻothersňļ from Allah‚Äôs Way, strived to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked, and disbelieved in the Hereafter.‚ÄĚ

7:46.text: There will be a barrier between Paradise and Hell. And on the heights ňĻof that barrierňļ will be people<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†who will recognize ňĻthe residents ofňļ both by their appearance.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†They will call out to the residents of Paradise, ‚ÄúPeace be upon you!‚ÄĚ They will have not yet entered Paradise, but eagerly hope to.
footnotes: ¬†Although the word ‚Äúrij√Ęl‚ÄĚ generally means ‚Äúmen,‚ÄĚ some Quran commentators believe that the word ‚Äúrij√Ęl‚ÄĚ here can also mean ‚Äúpeople,‚ÄĚ but they are called men since men make up the majority in the group. In some Arabic dialects, ‚Äúrij√Ęl‚ÄĚ is the plural of ‚Äúrajul‚ÄĚ (man) and ‚Äúrajulah‚ÄĚ (woman).
¬†The faces of the residents of Paradise will be bright, whereas those of the residents of Hell will be gloomy.

7:47.text: When their eyes will turn towards the residents of Hell, they will pray, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Do not join us with the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

7:48.text: Those on the heights will call out to some ňĻtyrants in the Fireňļ, who they will recognize by their appearance, saying, ‚ÄúYour large numbers and arrogance are of no use ňĻtodayňļ!

7:49.text: Are these ňĻhumble believersňļ the ones you swore would never be shown Allah‚Äôs mercy?‚ÄĚ ňĻFinally, those on the heights will be told:ňļ ‚ÄúEnter Paradise! There will be no fear for you, nor will you grieve.‚ÄĚ

7:50.text: The residents of the Fire will then cry out to the residents of Paradise, ‚ÄúAid us with some water or any provision Allah has granted you.‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúAllah has forbidden both to the disbelievers,

7:51.text: those who took this<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†faith ňĻof Islamňļ as mere amusement and play and were deluded by ňĻtheirňļ worldly life.‚ÄĚ ňĻAllah will say,ňļ ‚ÄúToday We will ignore them just as they ignored the coming of this Day of theirs and for rejecting Our revelations.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†lit., their faith (which they are supposed to follow).

7:52.text: We have certainly brought them a Book which We explained with knowledge‚ÄĒa guide and mercy for those who believe.

7:53.text: Do they only await the fulfilment ňĻof its warningňļ? The Day it will be fulfilled, those who ignored it before will say, ‚ÄúThe messengers of our Lord certainly came with the truth. Are there any intercessors who can plead on our behalf? Or can we be sent back so we may do ňĻgood,ňļ unlike what we used to do?‚ÄĚ They will have certainly ruined themselves, and whatever ňĻgodsňļ they fabricated will fail them.

7:54.text: Indeed your Lord is Allah Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then established Himself on the Throne. He makes the day and night overlap in rapid succession. He created the sun, the moon, and the stars‚ÄĒall subjected by His command. The creation and the command belong to Him ňĻaloneňļ. Blessed is Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds!
footnotes: ¬†The word day is not always used in the Quran to mean a 24-hour period. According to 22:47, a heavenly Day is 1000 years of our time. The Day of Judgment will be 50 000 years of our time (see¬†70:4). Hence, the six Days of creation refer to six eons of time, known only by Allah.

7:55.text: Call upon your Lord humbly and secretly. Surely He does not like the transgressors.

7:56.text: Do not spread corruption in the land after it has been set in order. And call upon Him with hope and fear. Indeed, Allah‚Äôs mercy is always close to the good-doers.

7:57.text: He is the One Who sends the winds ushering in His mercy. When they bear heavy clouds, We drive them to a lifeless land and then cause rain to fall, producing every type of fruit. Similarly, We will bring the dead to life, so perhaps you will be mindful.

7:58.text: The fertile land produces abundantly by the Will of its Lord, whereas the infertile land hardly produces anything. This is how We vary ňĻOurňļ lessons to those who are thankful.

7:59.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah‚ÄĒyou have no other god except Him. I truly fear for you the torment of a tremendous Day.‚ÄĚ

7:60.text: But the chiefs of his people said, ‚ÄúWe surely see that you are clearly misguided.‚ÄĚ

7:61.text: He replied, ‚ÄúO my people! I am not misguided! But I am a messenger from the Lord of all worlds,

7:62.text: conveying to you my Lord‚Äôs messages and giving you ňĻsincereňļ advice. And I know from Allah what you do not know.

7:63.text: Do you find it astonishing that a reminder should come to you from your Lord through one of your own, warning you, so you may beware and perhaps be shown mercy?‚ÄĚ

7:64.text: But they rejected him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and drowned those who rejected Our signs. They were certainly a blind people.

7:65.text: And to the people of ‚Äô√ād We sent their brother H√Ľd. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah‚ÄĒyou have no other god except Him. Will you not then fear Him?‚ÄĚ

7:66.text: The disbelieving chiefs of his people responded, ‚ÄúWe surely see you as a fool, and we certainly think you are a liar.‚ÄĚ

7:67.text: H√Ľd replied, ‚ÄúO my people! I am no fool! But I am a messenger from the Lord of all worlds,

7:68.text: conveying to you my Lord‚Äôs messages. And I am your sincere advisor.

7:69.text: Do you find it astonishing that a reminder should come to you from your Lord through one of your own so he may warn you? Remember that He made you successors after the people of Noah and increased you greatly in stature. So remember Allah‚Äôs favours, so you may be successful.‚ÄĚ

7:70.text: They said, ‚ÄúHave you come to us so that we would worship Allah alone and abandon what our forefathers used to worship? Then bring us what you threaten us with, if what you say is true!‚ÄĚ

7:71.text: He said, ‚ÄúYou will certainly be subjected to your Lord‚Äôs torment and wrath. Do you dispute with me regarding the so-called gods which you and your forefathers have made up‚ÄĒa practice Allah has never authorized? Then wait! I too am waiting with you.‚ÄĚ

7:72.text: So We saved him and those with him by Our mercy and uprooted those who denied Our signs. They were not believers.

7:73.text: And to the people of Tham√Ľd We sent their brother ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah‚ÄĒyou have no other god except Him. A clear proof has come to you from your Lord: this is Allah‚Äôs she-camel as a sign to you. So leave her to graze ňĻfreelyňļ on Allah‚Äôs land and do not harm her, or else you will be overcome by a painful punishment.

7:74.text: Remember when He made you successors after ‚Äô√ād and established you in the land‚ÄĒňĻandňļ you built palaces on its plains and carved homes into mountains. So remember Allah‚Äôs favours, and do not go about spreading corruption in the land.‚ÄĚ

7:75.text: The arrogant chiefs of his people asked the lowly who believed among them, ‚ÄúAre you certain that ŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł• has been sent by his Lord?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúWe certainly believe in what he has been sent with.‚ÄĚ

7:76.text: The arrogant said, ‚ÄúWe surely reject what you believe in.‚ÄĚ

7:77.text: Then they killed the she-camel‚ÄĒdefying their Lord‚Äôs command‚ÄĒand challenged ňĻŠĻĘ√ĘliŠł•ňļ, ‚ÄúBring us what you threaten us with, if you are ňĻtrulyňļ one of the messengers.‚ÄĚ

7:78.text: Then an ňĻoverwhelmingňļ earthquake struck them, and they fell lifeless in their homes.

7:79.text: So he turned away from them, saying, ‚ÄúO my people! Surely I conveyed to you my Lord‚Äôs message and gave you ňĻsincereňļ advice, but you do not like ňĻsincereňļ advisors.‚ÄĚ

7:80.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Lot scolded ňĻthe men ofňļ his people, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúDo you commit a shameful deed that no man has ever done before?

7:81.text: You lust after men instead of women! You are certainly transgressors.‚ÄĚ

7:82.text: But his people‚Äôs only response was to say, ‚ÄúExpel them from your land! They are a people who wish to remain chaste!‚ÄĚ

7:83.text: So We saved him and his family except his wife, who was one of the doomed.

7:84.text: We poured upon them a rain ňĻof brimstoneňļ. See what was the end of the wicked!

7:85.text: And to the people of Midian We sent their brother Shu‚Äôaib. He said, ‚ÄúO my people! Worship Allah‚ÄĒyou have no other god except Him. A clear proof has already come to you from your Lord. So give just measure and weight, do not defraud people of their property, nor spread corruption in the land after it has been set in order. This is for your own good, if you are ňĻtrulyňļ believers.

7:86.text: And do not lie in ambush on every road‚ÄĒthreatening and hindering those who believe in Allah from His Path and striving to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked. Remember when you were few, then He increased you in number. And consider the fate of the corruptors!

7:87.text: If some of you do believe in what I have been sent with while others do not, then be patient until Allah judges between us. He is the Best of Judges.‚ÄĚ

7:88.text: The arrogant chiefs of his people threatened, ‚ÄúO Shu‚Äôaib! We will certainly expel you and your fellow believers from our land, unless you return to our faith.‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúEven if we hate it?

7:89.text: We would surely be fabricating a lie against Allah if we were to return to your faith after Allah has saved us from it. It does not befit us to return to it unless it is the Will of Allah, our Lord. Our Lord has encompassed everything in ňĻHisňļ knowledge. In Allah we trust. Our Lord! Judge between us and our people with truth. You are the best of those who judge.‚ÄĚ

7:90.text: The disbelieving chiefs of his people threatened, ‚ÄúIf you follow Shu‚Äôaib, you will surely be losers!‚ÄĚ

7:91.text: Then an ňĻoverwhelmingňļ earthquake struck them and they fell lifeless in their homes.

7:92.text: Those who rejected Shu‚Äôaib were ňĻwiped outňļ as if they had never lived there. Those who rejected Shu‚Äôaib were the true losers.

7:93.text: He turned away from them, saying, ‚ÄúO my people! Indeed, I have delivered to you the messages of my Lord and gave you ňĻsincereňļ advice. How can I then grieve for those who chose to disbelieve?‚ÄĚ

7:94.text: Whenever We sent a prophet to a society, We afflicted its ňĻdisbelievingňļ people with suffering and adversity, so perhaps they would be humbled.

7:95.text: Then We changed their adversity to prosperity until they flourished and argued ňĻfalselyňļ, ‚ÄúOur forefathers ňĻtooňļ had been visited by adversity and prosperity.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So We seized them by surprise, while they were unaware.
footnotes: ¬†They argued that life has its ups and downs and, therefore, they did not perceive adversity as a punishment or prosperity as a test‚ÄĒarguing that the same thing happened to their ancestors.

7:96.text: Had the people of those societies been faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, We would have overwhelmed them with blessings from heaven and earth. But they disbelieved, so We seized them for what they used to commit.

7:97.text: Did the people of those societies feel secure that Our punishment would not come upon them by night while they were asleep?

7:98.text: Or did they feel secure that Our punishment would not come upon them by day while they were at play?

7:99.text: Did they feel secure against Allah‚Äôs planning? None would feel secure from Allah‚Äôs planning except the losers.

7:100.text: Is it not clear to those who take over the land after ňĻthe destruction ofňļ its former residents that‚ÄĒif We will‚ÄĒWe can punish them ňĻtooňļ for their sins and seal their hearts so they will not hear ňĻthe truthňļ?

7:101.text: We have narrated to you ňĻO Prophetňļ some of the stories of those societies. Surely, their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but still they would not believe in what they had already denied. This is how Allah seals the hearts of the disbelievers.

7:102.text: We did not find most of them true to their covenant. Rather, We found most of them truly rebellious.

7:103.text: Then after them We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they wrongfully rejected them. See what was the end of the corruptors!

7:104.text: And Moses said, ‚ÄúO Pharaoh! I am truly a messenger from the Lord of all worlds,

7:105.text: obliged to say nothing about Allah except the truth. Indeed, I have come to you with clear proof from your Lord, so let the children of Israel go with me.‚ÄĚ

7:106.text: Pharaoh said, ‚ÄúIf you have come with a sign, then bring it if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ

7:107.text: So Moses threw down his staff and‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒit became a real snake.

7:108.text: Then he drew his hand ňĻout of his collarňļ and it was ňĻshiningňļ white for all to see.

7:109.text: The chiefs of Pharaoh‚Äôs people said, ‚ÄúHe is indeed a skilled magician,

7:110.text: who seeks to drive you from your land.‚ÄĚ ňĻSo Pharaoh asked,ňļ ‚ÄúWhat do you propose?‚ÄĚ

7:111.text: They replied, ‚ÄúLet him and his brother wait and send mobilizers to all cities

7:112.text: to bring you every clever magician.‚ÄĚ

7:113.text: The magicians came to Pharaoh, saying, ‚ÄúShall we receive a ňĻsuitableňļ reward if we prevail?‚ÄĚ

7:114.text: He replied, ‚ÄúYes, and you will certainly be among those closest to me.‚ÄĚ

7:115.text: They asked, ‚ÄúO Moses! Will you cast, or shall we be the first to cast?‚ÄĚ

7:116.text: Moses said, ‚ÄúYou first.‚ÄĚ So when they did, they deceived the eyes of the people, stunned them, and made a great display of magic.

7:117.text: Then We inspired Moses, ‚ÄúThrow down your staff,‚ÄĚ and‚ÄĒbehold!‚ÄĒit devoured the objects of their illusion!

7:118.text: So the truth prevailed and their illusions failed.

7:119.text: So Pharaoh and his people were defeated right there and put to shame.

7:120.text: And the magicians fell down, prostrating.

7:121.text: They declared, ‚ÄúWe ňĻnowňļ believe in the Lord of all worlds‚ÄĒ

7:122.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.‚ÄĚ

7:123.text: Pharaoh threatened, ‚ÄúHow dare you believe in him before I give you permission? This must be a conspiracy you devised in the city to drive out its people, but soon you will see.

7:124.text: I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then crucify you all.‚ÄĚ

7:125.text: They responded, ‚ÄúSurely to our Lord we will ňĻallňļ return.

7:126.text: Your rage towards us is only because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord! Shower us with perseverance, and let us die while submitting ňĻto Youňļ.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†lit., as Muslims.

7:127.text: The chiefs of Pharaoh‚Äôs people protested, ‚ÄúAre you going to leave Moses and his people free to spread corruption in the land and abandon you and your gods?‚ÄĚ He responded, ‚ÄúWe will kill their sons and keep their women.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We will completely dominate them.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†As we did before the birth of Moses.

7:128.text: Moses reassured his people, ‚ÄúSeek Allah‚Äôs help and be patient. Indeed, the earth belongs to Allah ňĻaloneňļ. He grants it to whoever He chooses of His servants. The ultimate outcome belongs ňĻonlyňļ to the righteous.‚ÄĚ

7:129.text: They complained, ‚ÄúWe have always been oppressed‚ÄĒbefore and after you came to us ňĻwith the messageňļ.‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúPerhaps your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land to see what you will do.‚ÄĚ

7:130.text: Indeed, We afflicted Pharaoh‚Äôs people with famine and shortage of crops so they might come back ňĻto their sensesňļ.

7:131.text: In times of prosperity, they said, ‚ÄúThis is what we deserve,‚ÄĚ but in adversity, they blamed it on Moses and those with him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Surely all is destined by Allah. Yet most of them did not know.
footnotes: ¬†In psychology, this is called self-serving bias and scapegoating.

7:132.text: They said, ‚ÄúNo matter what sign you may bring to deceive us, we will never believe in you.‚ÄĚ

7:133.text: So We plagued them with floods, locusts, lice, frogs, and blood‚ÄĒall as clear signs, but they persisted in arrogance and were a wicked people.

7:134.text: When tormented, they pleaded, ‚ÄúO Moses! Pray to your Lord on our behalf, by virtue of the covenant He made with you. If you help remove this torment from us, we will certainly believe in you and let the Children of Israel go with you.‚ÄĚ

7:135.text: But as soon as We removed the torment from them‚ÄĒuntil they met their inevitable fate<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>‚ÄĒthey broke their promise.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., their drowning in the sea.

7:136.text: So We inflicted punishment upon them, drowning them in the sea for denying Our signs and being heedless of them.

7:137.text: And ňĻsoňļ We made the oppressed people successors of the eastern and western lands, which We had showered with blessings. ňĻIn this wayňļ the noble Word of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel for what they had endured. And We destroyed what Pharaoh and his people constructed and what they established.

7:138.text: We brought the Children of Israel across the sea and they came upon a people devoted to idols. They demanded, ‚ÄúO Moses! Make for us a god like their gods.‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúIndeed, you are a people acting ignorantly!

7:139.text: What they follow is certainly doomed to destruction and their deeds are in vain.‚ÄĚ

7:140.text: He added, ‚ÄúShall I seek for you a god other than Allah, while He has honoured you above the others?‚ÄĚ

7:141.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We rescued you from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with dreadful torment‚ÄĒkilling your sons and keeping your women. That was a severe test from your Lord.

7:142.text: We appointed for Moses thirty nights then added another ten‚ÄĒcompleting his Lord‚Äôs term of forty nights. Moses commanded his brother Aaron, ‚ÄúTake my place among my people, do what is right, and do not follow the way of the corruptors.‚ÄĚ

7:143.text: When Moses came at the appointed time and his Lord spoke to him, he asked, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Reveal Yourself to me so I may see You.‚ÄĚ Allah answered, ‚ÄúYou cannot see Me! But look at the mountain. If it remains firm in its place, only then will you see Me.‚ÄĚ When his Lord appeared to the mountain, He levelled it to dust and Moses collapsed unconscious. When he recovered, he cried, ‚ÄúGlory be to You! I turn to You in repentance and I am the first of the believers.‚ÄĚ

7:144.text: Allah said, ‚ÄúO Moses! I have ňĻalreadyňļ elevated you above all others by My messages and speech.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So hold firmly to what I have given you and be grateful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Allah reminds Moses that even though his request to see Allah was denied, he has already been favoured by Allah over the people of his time through prophethood and direct communication with the Almighty.

7:145.text: We wrote for him on the Tablets ňĻthe fundamentalsňļ of everything; commandments and explanations of all things. ňĻWe commanded,ňļ ‚ÄúHold to this firmly and ask your people to take the best of it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†I will soon show ňĻall ofňļ you the home of the rebellious.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†To follow the commandments that generate more rewards than others, to put grace before justice, etc.
¬†This could either mean the ruins of destroyed nations or the Hellfire which is the home of the wicked.

7:146.text: I will turn away from My signs those who act unjustly with arrogance in the land. And even if they were to see every sign, they still would not believe in them. If they see the Right Path, they will not take it. But if they see a crooked path, they will follow it. This is because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.

7:147.text: The deeds of those who deny Our signs and the meeting ňĻwith Allahňļ in the Hereafter will be in vain. Will they be rewarded except for what they have done?‚ÄĚ

7:148.text: In the absence of Moses, his people made from their ňĻgoldenňļ jewellery an idol of a calf that made a lowing sound. Did they not see that it could neither speak to them nor guide them to the ňĻRightňļ Path? Still they took it as a god and were wrongdoers.

7:149.text: Later, when they were filled with remorse and realized they had gone astray, they cried, ‚ÄúIf our Lord does not have mercy on us and forgive us, we will certainly be losers.‚ÄĚ

7:150.text: When Moses returned to his people, ňĻtotallyňļ furious and sorrowful, he said, ‚ÄúWhat an evil thing you committed in my absence! Did you want to hasten your Lord‚Äôs torment?‚ÄĚ Then he threw down the Tablets and grabbed his brother by the hair, dragging him closer. Aaron pleaded, ‚ÄúO son of my mother! The people overpowered me and were about to kill me. So do not ňĻhumiliate me andňļ make my enemies rejoice, nor count me among the wrongdoing people.‚ÄĚ

7:151.text: Moses prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Forgive me and my brother! And admit us into Your mercy. You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.‚ÄĚ

7:152.text: Those who worshipped the calf will certainly be afflicted with wrath from their Lord as well as disgrace in the life of this world. This is how We reward those who invent falsehood.

7:153.text: But those who commit evil, then repent and become ňĻtrueňļ believers, your Lord will certainly be All-Forgiving, Most-Merciful.

7:154.text: When Moses‚Äô anger subsided, he took up the Tablets whose text contained guidance and mercy for those who stand in awe of their Lord.

7:155.text: Moses chose seventy men from among his people for Our appointment and, when they were seized by an earthquake,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†he cried, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Had You willed, You could have destroyed them long ago, and me as well. Will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? This is only a test from You‚ÄĒby which You allow whoever you will to stray and guide whoever You will. You are our Guardian. So forgive us and have mercy on us. You are the best forgiver.
footnotes: ¬†For asking Moses to make Allah visible to them.

7:156.text: Ordain for us what is good in this life and the next. Indeed, we have turned to You ňĻin repentanceňļ.‚ÄĚ Allah replied, ‚ÄúI will inflict My torment on whoever I will. But My mercy encompasses everything. I will ordain mercy for those who shun evil, pay alms-tax, and believe in Our revelations.

7:157.text: ‚ÄúňĻThey areňļ the ones who follow the Messenger, the unlettered Prophet, whose description they find in their Torah and the Gospel.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He commands them to do good and forbids them from evil, permits for them what is lawful and forbids to them what is impure, and relieves them from their burdens and the shackles that bound them. ňĻOnlyňļ those who believe in him, honour and support him, and follow the light sent down to him will be successful.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Some Muslim scholars cite Deuteronomy 18:15-18 and 33:2, Isaiah 42, and John 14:16 as examples of the description of Prophet MuŠł•ammad in the Bible. However, Bible scholars interpret these verses differently. The name of Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) appears several times in the Gospel of Barnabas, which is deemed apocryphal by Christian authorities.

7:158.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO humanity! I am Allah‚Äôs Messenger to you all. To Him ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. He gives life and causes death.‚ÄĚ So believe in Allah and His Messenger, the unlettered Prophet, who believes in Allah and His revelations. And follow him, so you may be ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

7:159.text: There are some among the people of Moses who guide with the truth and establish justice accordingly.

7:160.text: We divided them into twelve tribes‚ÄĒeach as a community. And We revealed to Moses, when his people asked for water, ‚ÄúStrike the rock with your staff.‚ÄĚ Then twelve springs gushed out. Each tribe knew its drinking place. We shaded them with clouds and sent down to them manna and quails,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻsayingňļ, ‚ÄúEat from the good things We have provided for you.‚ÄĚ They ňĻcertainlyňļ did not wrong Us, but wronged themselves.
footnotes: ¬†Manna (heavenly bread) and quails (chicken-like birds) sustained the children of Israel in the wilderness after they left Egypt.

7:161.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when it was said to them, ‚ÄúEnter this city ňĻof Jerusalemňļ and eat from wherever you please. Say, ‚ÄėAbsolve us,‚Äô and enter the gate with humility. We will forgive your sins, ňĻandňļ We will multiply the reward for the good-doers.‚ÄĚ

7:162.text: But the wrongdoers among them changed the words they were commanded to say. So We sent down a punishment from the heavens upon them for their wrongdoing.

7:163.text: Ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ about ňĻthe people ofňļ the town which was by the sea, who broke the Sabbath.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†During the Sabbath, ňĻabundantňļ fish would come to them clearly visible, but on other days the fish were never seen. In this way We tested them for their rebelliousness.
footnotes: ¬†The people of Aylah, an ancient town by the Red Sea, were forbidden to catch fish on the Sabbath. However, on Saturdays fish were everywhere, whereas on weekdays no fish were seen. To get around the prohibition, some decided to lay their nets on Fridays and collect the fish caught in their nets on Sundays. Those opposed to this practice were divided into two groups: one group tried to convince the offenders to honour the Sabbath, but soon gave up when their advice was not taken seriously. The second group was persistent in giving advice to the Sabbath-breakers. Eventually, the Sabbath-breakers were punished whereas the other two groups were saved.

7:164.text: When some of ňĻthe righteous amongňļ them questioned ňĻtheir fellow Sabbath-keepersňļ, ‚ÄúWhy do you ňĻbother toňļ warn those ňĻSabbath-breakersňļ who will either be destroyed or severely punished by Allah?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúJust to be free from your Lord‚Äôs blame, and so perhaps they may abstain.‚ÄĚ

7:165.text: When they ignored the warning they were given, We rescued those who used to warn against evil and overtook the wrongdoers with a dreadful punishment for their rebelliousness.

7:166.text: But when they stubbornly persisted in violation, We said to them, ‚ÄúBe disgraced apes!‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†Literally or metaphorically. See¬†footnote for 2:65.

7:167.text: And ňĻremember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when your Lord declared that He would send against them others who would make them suffer terribly until the Day of Judgment. Indeed, your Lord is swift in punishment, but He is certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

7:168.text: We dispersed them through the land in groups‚ÄĒsome were righteous, others were less so. We tested them with prosperity and adversity, so perhaps they would return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.

7:169.text: Then they were succeeded by other generations who inherited the Scripture. They indulged in unlawful gains, claiming, ‚ÄúWe will be forgiven ňĻafter allňļ.‚ÄĚ And if similar gain came their way, they would seize it. Was a covenant not taken from them in the Scripture that they would not say anything about Allah except the truth? And they were already well-versed in its teachings. But the ňĻeternalňļ Home of the Hereafter is far better for those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ. Will you not then understand?

7:170.text: As for those who firmly abide by the Scripture and establish prayer‚ÄĒsurely We never discount the reward of those acting righteously.

7:171.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We raised the mountain over them as if it were a cloud and they thought it would fall on them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻWe said,ňļ ‚ÄúHold firmly to that ňĻScriptureňļ which¬†We have given you and observe its teachings so perhaps you will become mindful ňĻof Allahňļ.‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†The mountain was raised over their heads when Moses came to them with the teachings of the Torah and was met with defiance. So the mountain was raised as a miracle and a display of Allah‚Äôs infinite power over them.

7:172.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when your Lord brought forth from the loins of the children of Adam their descendants and had them testify regarding themselves. ňĻAllah asked,ňļ ‚ÄúAm I not your Lord?‚ÄĚ They replied, ‚ÄúYes, You are! We testify.‚ÄĚ ňĻHe cautioned,ňļ ‚ÄúNow you have no right to say on Judgment Day, ‚ÄėWe were not aware of this.‚Äô

7:173.text: Nor say, ‚ÄėIt was our forefathers who had associated others ňĻwith Allah in worshipňļ and we, as their descendants, followed in their footsteps. Will you then destroy us for the falsehood they invented?‚Äô‚ÄĚ

7:174.text: This is how We make our signs clear, so perhaps they will return ňĻto the Right Pathňļ.

7:175.text: And relate to them ňĻO Prophetňļ the story of the one to whom We gave Our signs, but he abandoned them, so Satan took hold of him, and he became a deviant.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Bal‚Äôam ibn Ba‚Äô√Ľr√Ę' was a scholar who lived at the time of Moses (Ô∑ļ) but later deviated from the truth.

7:176.text: If We had willed, We would have elevated him with Our signs, but he clung to this life‚ÄĒfollowing his evil desires. His example is that of a dog: if you chase it away, it pants, and if you leave it, it ňĻstillňļ pants. This is the example of the people who deny Our signs.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So narrate ňĻto themňļ stories ňĻof the pastňļ, so perhaps they will reflect.
footnotes: ¬†Regardless of whether they are warned or not, still they will not believe.

7:177.text: What an evil example of those who denied Our signs! They ňĻonlyňļ wronged their own souls.

7:178.text: Whoever Allah guides is truly guided. And whoever He leaves to stray, they are the ňĻtrueňļ losers.

7:179.text: Indeed, We have destined many jinn and humans for Hell. They have hearts they do not understand with, eyes they do not see with, and ears they do not hear with. They are like cattle. In fact, they are even less guided! Such ňĻpeopleňļ are ňĻentirelyňļ heedless.

7:180.text: Allah has the Most Beautiful Names. So call upon Him by them, and keep away from those who abuse His Names.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They will be punished for what they used to do.
footnotes: ¬†Those who twist Allah‚Äôs Names then use them to call false gods. For example, All√Ęt, a name of one of the idols was derived from Allah¬†(the One God), Al-‚ÄôUzza¬†was derived from Al-‚ÄôAz√ģz¬†(the Almighty), and Man√Ęt¬†was derived from Al-Mann√Ęn¬†(the Bestower).

7:181.text: And among those We created is a group that guides with the truth and establishes justice accordingly.

7:182.text: As for those who deny Our signs, We will gradually draw them to destruction in ways they cannot comprehend.

7:183.text: I ňĻonlyňļ delay their end for a while, but My planning is flawless.

7:184.text: Have they not ever given it a thought? Their fellow man<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is not insane. He is only sent with a clear warning.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

7:185.text: Have they ever reflected on the wonders of the heavens and the earth, and everything Allah has created, and that perhaps their end is near? So what message after this ňĻQuranňļ would they believe in?

7:186.text: Whoever Allah allows to stray, none can guide, leaving them to wander blindly in their defiance.

7:187.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ regarding the Hour, ‚ÄúWhen will it be?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúThat knowledge is only with my Lord. He alone will reveal it when the time comes. It is too tremendous for the heavens and the earth and will only take you by surprise.‚ÄĚ They ask you as if you had full knowledge of it. Say, ‚ÄúThat knowledge is only with Allah, but most people do not know.‚ÄĚ

7:188.text: Say, ‚ÄúI have no power to benefit or protect myself, except by the Will of Allah. If I had known the unknown, I would have benefited myself enormously, and no harm would have ever touched me. I am only a warner and deliverer of good news for those who believe.‚ÄĚ

7:189.text: He is the One Who created you from a single soul, then from it made its spouse so he may find comfort in her. After he had been united with her, she carried a light burden that developed gradually. When it grew heavy, they prayed to Allah, their Lord, ‚ÄúIf you grant us good offspring, we will certainly be grateful.‚ÄĚ

7:190.text: But when He granted their descendants good offspring, they associated false gods in what He has given them. Exalted is Allah above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!

7:191.text: Do they associate ňĻwith Allahňļ those ňĻidolsňļ which cannot create anything, but are in fact created;

7:192.text: which cannot help them, or even help themselves?

7:193.text: And if you ňĻidolatersňļ call upon them for guidance, they cannot respond to you. It is all the same whether you call them or remain silent.

7:194.text: Those ňĻidolsňļ you invoke besides Allah are created beings like yourselves. So call upon them and see if they will answer you, if your claims are true!

7:195.text: Do they have feet to walk with? Or hands to hold with? Or eyes to see with? Or ears to hear with? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúCall upon your associate-gods and conspire against me without delay!

7:196.text: ‚ÄúIndeed, my Protector is Allah Who has revealed this Book. For He ňĻaloneňļ protects the righteous.

7:197.text: But those ňĻfalse godsňļ you call besides Him can neither help you nor even themselves.‚ÄĚ

7:198.text: If you ňĻidolatersňļ call them to guidance, they cannot hear. And you ňĻO Prophetňļ may see them facing towards you, but they cannot see.

7:199.text: Be gracious, enjoin what is right, and turn away from those who act ignorantly.

7:200.text: If you are tempted by Satan, then seek refuge with Allah. Surely He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

7:201.text: Indeed, when Satan whispers to those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, they remember ňĻtheir Lordňļ then they start to see ňĻthingsňļ clearly.

7:202.text: But the devils persistently plunge their ňĻhumanňļ associates<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†deeper into wickedness, sparing no effort.
footnotes: ¬†lit., their brothers.

7:203.text: If you ňĻO Prophetňļ do not bring them a sign ňĻwhich they demandedňļ, they ask, ‚ÄúWhy do you not make it yourself?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúI only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord. This ňĻQuranňļ is an insight from your Lord‚ÄĒa guide and a mercy for those who believe.‚ÄĚ

7:204.text: When the Quran is recited, listen to it attentively and be silent, so you may be shown mercy.

7:205.text: Remember your Lord inwardly with humility and reverence and in a moderate tone of voice, both morning and evening. And do not be one of the heedless.

7:206.text: Surely those ňĻangelsňļ nearest to your Lord are not too proud to worship Him. They glorify Him. And to Him they prostrate.

70:1.text: A challenger<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†has demanded a punishment bound to come
footnotes: ¬†According to 8:32, An-NaŠłćr ibn Al-Šł§√Ęrith challenged Allah, saying, ‚ÄúIf this is indeed the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from the sky or overcome us with a painful punishment.‚ÄĚ

70:2.text: for the disbelievers‚ÄĒto be averted by none‚ÄĒ

70:3.text: from Allah, Lord of pathways of ňĻheavenlyňļ ascent,

70:4.text: ňĻthrough whichňļ the angels and the ňĻholyňļ spirit<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†will ascend to Him on a Day fifty thousand years in length.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The holy spirit is the angel Gabriel.

¬†Judgment Day will seem like 50 000 years for a disbeliever, but it will seem like a very short period for a believer. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth¬†collected by Im√Ęm AŠł•med to have said that, for the believer, this long period will be like the time they took to perform a single prayer in the world.

70:5.text: So endure ňĻthis denial, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ with beautiful patience.

70:6.text: They truly see this ňĻDayňļ as impossible,

70:7.text: but We see it as inevitable.

70:8.text: On that Day the sky will be like molten brass

70:9.text: and the mountains like ňĻtufts ofňļ wool.

70:10.text: And no close friend will ask ňĻaboutňļ their friends,

70:11.text: ňĻalthoughňļ they will be made to see each other. The wicked will wish to ransom themselves from the punishment of that Day by their children,

70:14.text: and everyone on earth altogether, just to save themselves.

70:15.text: But no! There will certainly be a raging Flame

70:17.text: It will summon whoever turned their backs ňĻon Allahňļ and turned away ňĻfrom the truthňļ,

70:21.text: and withholding when touched with good‚ÄĒ

70:24.text: and who give the rightful share of their wealth

70:25.text: to the beggar and the poor;

70:26.text: and who ňĻfirmlyňļ believe in the Day of Judgment;

70:27.text: and those who fear the punishment of their Lord‚ÄĒ

70:28.text: ňĻknowing thatňļ none should feel secure from their Lord‚Äôs punishment‚ÄĒ

70:29.text: and those who guard their chastity<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., private parts.

70:30.text: except with their wives or those ňĻbondwomenňļ in their possession,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†for then they are free from blame,
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 4:3.

70:31.text: but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors.

70:32.text: ňĻThe faithful areňļ also those who are true to their trusts and covenants;

70:33.text: and who are honest in their testimony;

70:34.text: and who are ňĻproperlyňļ observant of their prayers.

70:35.text: These will be in Gardens, held in honour.

70:36.text: So what is the matter with the disbelievers that they rush ňĻhead-longňļ towards you ňĻO Prophetňļ,

70:37.text: from the right and the left, in groups ňĻto mock youňļ?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Some Meccan pagans used to gather around the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) to mock him and the believers, saying that if there is in fact a Hereafter, they are better entitled to Paradise than the poor believers.

70:38.text: Does every one of them expect to be admitted into a Garden of Bliss?

70:39.text: But no! Indeed, they ňĻalreadyňļ know what We created them from.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†i.e., from a humble fluid. See¬†32:8.

70:40.text: So, I do swear by the Lord of ňĻallňļ the points of sunrise and sunset<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†that We are truly capable
footnotes: ¬†The daily points of sunrise and sunset, caused by the earth‚Äôs rotation around its own axis.

70:41.text: of replacing them with ňĻothersňļ better than them, and We cannot be prevented ňĻfrom doing soňļ.

70:42.text: So let them indulge ňĻin falsehoodňļ and amuse ňĻthemselvesňļ until they face their Day, which they have been threatened with‚ÄĒ

70:43.text: the Day they will come forth from the graves swiftly, as if racing to an idol ňĻfor a blessingňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Or ‚Äú‚Ä¶ as if rushing towards a target.‚ÄĚ

70:44.text: with eyes downcast, utterly covered with disgrace. That is the Day they have ňĻalwaysňļ been warned of.

71:1.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people ňĻsaying to himňļ, ‚ÄúWarn your people before a painful punishment comes to them.‚ÄĚ

71:2.text: Noah proclaimed, ‚ÄúO my people! I am truly sent to you with a clear warning:

71:3.text: worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ, fear Him, and obey me.

71:4.text: He will forgive your sins, and delay your end until the appointed time.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Indeed, when the time set by Allah comes, it cannot be delayed, if only you knew!‚ÄĚ
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, it is out of Allah‚Äôs mercy that He does not hasten your punishment but allows you more time to repent.

71:5.text: He cried, ‚ÄúMy Lord! I have surely called my people day and night,

71:6.text: but my calls only made them run farther away.

71:7.text: And whenever I invite them to be forgiven by You, they press their fingers into their ears, cover themselves with their clothes, persist ňĻin denialňļ, and act very arrogantly.

71:8.text: Then I certainly called them openly,

71:9.text: then I surely preached to them publicly and privately,

71:10.text: saying, ‚ÄėSeek your Lord‚Äôs forgiveness, ňĻforňļ He is truly Most Forgiving.

71:11.text: He will shower you with abundant rain,

71:12.text: supply you with wealth and children, and give you gardens as well as rivers.

71:13.text: What is the matter with you that you are not in awe of the Majesty of Allah,

71:14.text: when He truly created you in stages ňĻof developmentňļ?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>

71:15.text: Do you not see how Allah created seven heavens, one above the other,

71:16.text: placing the moon within them as a ňĻreflectedňļ light, and the sun as a ňĻradiantňļ lamp?

71:17.text: Allah ňĻaloneňļ caused you<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†to grow from the earth like a plant.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., your father Adam, who was created from clay.

71:18.text: Then He will return you to it, and then simply bring you forth ňĻagainňļ.

71:19.text: And Allah ňĻaloneňļ spread out the earth for you

71:20.text: to walk along its spacious pathways.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

71:21.text: ňĻEventually,ňļ Noah cried, ‚ÄúMy Lord! They have certainly persisted in disobeying me, and followed ňĻinsteadňļ those ňĻeliteňļ whose ňĻabundantňļ wealth and children only increase them in loss,

71:22.text: and who have devised a tremendous plot,

71:23.text: urging ňĻtheir followersňļ, ‚ÄėDo not abandon your idols‚ÄĒespecially Wadd, Suw√Ę‚Äô, Yagh√Ľth, Ya‚Äô√Ľq, and Nasr.‚Äô<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†These are the names of their main idols. Initially, these idols had been erected to commemorate some righteous people before the time of Noah. After many generations, however, the idols were taken as objects of worship.

71:24.text: Those ňĻeliteňļ have already led many astray. So ňĻO Lordňļ, only allow the wrongdoers to stray farther away.‚ÄĚ

71:25.text: So because of their sins, they were drowned, then admitted into the Fire. And they found none to help them against Allah.

71:26.text: Noah had prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Do not leave a single disbeliever on earth.

71:27.text: For if You spare ňĻany ofňļ them, they will certainly mislead Your servants, and give birth only to ňĻwickedňļ sinners, staunch disbelievers.

71:28.text: My Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and whoever enters my house in faith, and ňĻallňļ believing men and women. And increase the wrongdoers only in destruction.‚ÄĚ

72:1.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIt has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened ňĻto the Quran,ňļ and said ňĻto their fellow jinnňļ: ‚ÄėIndeed, we have heard a wondrous recitation.

72:2.text: It leads to Right Guidance so we believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord ňĻin worshipňļ.

72:3.text: ňĻNow, we believe thatňļ our Lord‚ÄĒExalted is His Majesty‚ÄĒhas neither taken a mate nor offspring,

72:4.text: and that the foolish of us used to utter ňĻoutrageousňļ falsehoods about Allah.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, evil jinn tempted some pagan Arabs to believe that the angels are Allah‚Äôs daughters through female jinn.

72:5.text: We certainly thought that humans and jinn would never speak lies about Allah.

72:6.text: And some men used to seek refuge with some jinn‚ÄĒso they increased each other in wickedness.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 6:128.

72:7.text: And those ňĻhumansňļ thought, just like you ňĻjinnňļ, that Allah would not resurrect anyone ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

72:8.text: ňĻEarlierňļ we tried to reach heaven ňĻfor newsňļ, only to find it filled with stern guards and shooting stars.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Some jinn used to eavesdrop on the heaven, then pass on what they heard to fortune-tellers. But this practice came to an end once MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) was sent as a messenger with the Quran.

72:9.text: We used to take up positions there for eavesdropping, but whoever dares eavesdrop now will find a flare¬†lying in wait for them.

72:10.text: Now, we have no clue whether evil is intended for those on earth, or their Lord intends for them what is right.

72:11.text: Among us are those who are righteous and those who are less so. We have been of different factions.

72:12.text: ňĻNow,ňļ we truly know that we cannot frustrate Allah on earth, nor can we escape from Him ňĻinto heavenňļ.

72:13.text: When we heard the guidance ňĻof the Quranňļ, we ňĻreadilyňļ believed in it. For whoever believes in their Lord will have no fear of being denied ňĻa rewardňļ or wronged.

72:14.text: And among us are those who have submitted ňĻto Allahňļ and those who are deviant. So ňĻas forňļ those who submitted, it is they who have attained Right Guidance.

72:15.text: And as for the deviant, they will be fuel for Hell.‚Äô‚ÄĚ

72:16.text: Had the deniers followed the Right Way, We would have certainly granted them abundant rain to drink‚ÄĒ

72:17.text: as a test for them.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And whoever turns away from the remembrance of their Lord will be admitted by Him into an overwhelming punishment.
footnotes: ¬†Meaning, to see if they will be grateful to Allah for rain and other provisions.

72:18.text: The places of worship are ňĻonlyňļ for Allah, so do not invoke anyone besides Him.

72:19.text: Yet when the servant of Allah<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†stood up calling upon Him ňĻaloneňļ, the pagans almost swarmed over him.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ).

72:20.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI call only upon my Lord, associating none with Him ňĻin worshipňļ.‚ÄĚ

72:21.text: Say, ‚ÄúIt is not in my power to harm or benefit you.‚ÄĚ

72:22.text: Say, ‚ÄúNo one can protect me from Allah ňĻif I were to disobey Himňļ, nor can I find any refuge other than Him.

72:23.text: ňĻMy duty isňļ only to convey ňĻthe truthňļ from Allah and ňĻdeliverňļ His messages.‚ÄĚ And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger will certainly be in the Fire of Hell, to stay there for ever and ever.

72:24.text: Only when they see what they have been threatened with will they know who is weaker in helpers and inferior in manpower.

72:25.text: Say, ‚ÄúI do not know if what you are promised is near or my Lord has set a distant time for it.

72:26.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Knower of the unseen, disclosing none of it to anyone,

72:27.text: except messengers of His choice. Then He appoints angel-guards before and behind them

72:28.text: to ensure that the messengers fully deliver the messages of their Lord‚ÄĒthough He ňĻalreadyňļ knows all about them, and keeps account of everything.‚ÄĚ

73:1.text: O you wrapped ňĻin your clothesňļ!

73:2.text: Stand all night ňĻin prayerňļ except a little‚ÄĒ

73:3.text: ňĻprayňļ half the night, or a little less,

73:4.text: or a little more‚ÄĒand recite the Quran ňĻproperlyňļ in a measured way.

73:5.text: ňĻForňļ We will soon send upon you a weighty revelation.

73:6.text: Indeed, worship in the night is more impactful and suitable for recitation.

73:7.text: For during the day you are over-occupied ňĻwith worldly dutiesňļ.

73:8.text: ňĻAlwaysňļ remember the Name of your Lord, and devote yourself to Him wholeheartedly.

73:9.text: ňĻHe is theňļ Lord of the east and the west. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him, so take Him ňĻaloneňļ as a Trustee of Affairs.

73:10.text: Be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ with what they say, and depart from them courteously.

73:11.text: And leave to Me the deniers‚ÄĒthe people of luxury‚ÄĒand bear with them for a little while.

73:12.text: ňĻForňļ We certainly have shackles, a ňĻragingňļ Fire,

73:13.text: choking food, and a painful punishment ňĻin store for themňļ

73:14.text: on the Day the earth and mountains will shake ňĻviolentlyňļ, and mountains will be ňĻreduced toňļ dunes of shifting sand.

73:15.text: Indeed, We have sent to you a messenger as a witness over you, just as We sent a messenger to Pharaoh.

73:16.text: But Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a stern grip.

73:17.text: If you ňĻpagansňļ persist in disbelief, then how will you guard yourselves against ňĻthe horrors ofňļ a Day which will turn children‚Äôs hair grey?

73:18.text: It will ňĻevenňļ cause the sky to split apart. His promise ňĻof judgmentňļ must be fulfilled.

73:19.text: Surely this is a reminder. So let whoever wills take the ňĻRightňļ Way to their Lord.

73:20.text: Surely your Lord knows that you ňĻO Prophetňļ stand ňĻin prayerňļ for nearly two-thirds of the night, or ňĻsometimesňļ half of it, or a third, as do some of those with you. Allah ňĻaloneňļ keeps a ňĻpreciseňļ measure of the day and night. He knows that you ňĻbelieversňļ are unable to endure this, and has turned to you in mercy.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So recite ňĻin prayerňļ whatever you can from the Quran. He knows that some of you will be sick, some will be travelling throughout the land seeking Allah‚Äôs bounty, and some fighting in the cause of Allah. So recite whatever you can from it. And ňĻcontinue toňļ perform ňĻregularňļ prayers, pay alms-tax, and lend to Allah a good loan.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†Whatever good you send forth for yourselves, you will find it with Allah far better and more rewarding.<sup foot_note=3>3</sup>¬†And seek Allah‚Äôs forgiveness. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†This passage lightens the ruling mentioned in 73:2-4. So from now on, the believers do not have to stick to any of the portions specified at the beginning of this s√Ľrah. Rather, they can pray whatever they can at night.
¬†i.e., giving for charity and good causes.

¬†The reward for your charity will be far better than what you left behind or what you have given in charity because it will be rewarded ten-fold, or even 700-fold, and Allah gives more to whoever He wills. See¬†2:261.

74:1.text: O you covered up ňĻin your clothesňļ!

74:6.text: Do not do a favour expecting more ňĻin returnňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†It was a common practice to give someone a gift, hoping to receive a more valuable gift in return. This practice is disliked in Islam.

74:7.text: And persevere for ňĻthe sake ofňļ your Lord.

74:8.text: ňĻForňļ when the Trumpet will be sounded,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This is when the Trumpet will be blown for the second time, causing everyone to rise from the dead for judgment. See¬†39:68.

74:9.text: that will ňĻtrulyňļ be a difficult Day‚ÄĒ

74:10.text: far from easy for the disbelievers.

74:11.text: And leave to me ňĻO Prophetňļ the one I created all by Myself,

74:13.text: and children always by his side,

74:14.text: and made life very¬†easy for him.

74:15.text: Yet he is hungry for more.

74:16.text: But no! ňĻForňļ he has been truly stubborn with Our revelations.

74:17.text: I will make his fate unbearable,

74:18.text: for he contemplated and determined ňĻa degrading label for the Quranňļ.

74:19.text: May he be condemned! How evil was what he determined!

74:20.text: May he be condemned even more! How evil was what he determined!

74:23.text: then turned his back ňĻon the truthňļ and acted arrogantly,

74:24.text: saying, ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ is nothing but magic from the ancients.

74:25.text: This is no more than the word of a man.‚ÄĚ

74:26.text: Soon I will burn him in Hell!

74:27.text: And what will make you realize what Hell is?

74:28.text: It does not let anyone live or die,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúIt spares none and leaves nothing.‚ÄĚ

74:30.text: It is overseen by nineteen ňĻkeepersňļ.

74:31.text: We have appointed only ňĻsternňļ angels as wardens of the Fire. And We have made their number only as a test for the disbelievers,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so that the People of the Book will be certain, and the believers will increase in faith, and neither the People of the Book nor the believers will have any doubts, and so that those ňĻhypocritesňļ with sickness in their hearts and the disbelievers will argue, ‚ÄúWhat does Allah mean by such a number?‚ÄĚ In this way Allah leaves whoever He wills to stray and guides whoever He wills. And none knows the forces of your Lord except He. And this ňĻdescription of Hellňļ is only a reminder to humanity.
footnotes: ¬†Some pagans made fun of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) when they were told that the keepers of Hell are nineteen angels. One of them said mockingly to other pagans, ‚ÄúYou take care of two angels and I will vanquish the rest all by myself.‚ÄĚ

74:33.text: and the night as it retreats,

74:34.text: and the day as it breaks!

74:35.text: Surely Hell is one of the mightiest catastrophes‚ÄĒ

74:37.text: to whichever of you chooses to take the lead or lag behind.

74:38.text: Every soul will be detained for what it has done,

74:39.text: except the people of the right,

74:40.text: who will be in Gardens, asking one another

74:41.text: about the wicked ňĻwho will then be askedňļ:

74:43.text: They will reply, ‚ÄúWe were not of those who prayed,

74:44.text: nor did we feed the poor.

74:45.text: We used to indulge ňĻin falsehoodňļ along with others,

74:46.text: and deny the Day of Judgment,

74:47.text: until the inevitable came to us.‚ÄĚ

74:48.text: So the pleas of intercessors will be of no benefit to them.

74:49.text: Now, what is the matter with them that they are turning away from the reminder,

74:50.text: as if they were spooked zebras

74:52.text: In fact, each one of them wishes to be given a ňĻpersonalňļ letter ňĻfrom Allahňļ for all ňĻto readňļ.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúIn fact, each of them wishes to be given a scripture open ňĻfor all to readňļ.‚ÄĚ See¬†6:124.

74:53.text: But no! In fact, they do not fear the Hereafter.

74:54.text: Enough! Surely this ňĻQuranňļ is a reminder.

74:55.text: So let whoever wills be mindful of it.

74:56.text: But they cannot do so unless Allah wills. He ňĻaloneňļ is worthy to be feared and entitled to forgive.

75:1.text: I do swear by the Day of Judgment!

75:2.text: And I do swear by the self-reproaching soul!

75:3.text: Do people think We cannot reassemble their bones?

75:4.text: Yes ňĻindeedňļ! We are ňĻmostňļ capable of restoring ňĻevenňļ their very fingertips.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This refers to the fact that every human has a unique set of fingerprints.

75:5.text: Still people want to deny what is yet to come,

75:6.text: asking ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen is this Day of Judgment?‚ÄĚ

75:7.text: But when the sight is stunned,

75:9.text: and the sun and the moon are brought together,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†They both will rise from the west and lose their light.

75:10.text: on that Day one will cry, ‚ÄúWhere is the escape?‚ÄĚ

75:11.text: But no! There will be no refuge.
