75:13.text: All will then be informed of what they have sent forth and left behind.

75:14.text: In fact, people will testify against their own souls,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Their organs will testify against them. See¬†41:19-24.

75:15.text: despite the excuses they come up with.

75:16.text: Do not rush your tongue trying to memorize ňĻa revelation ofňļ the Quran.

75:17.text: It is certainly upon Us to ňĻmake youňļ memorize and recite it.

75:18.text: So once We have recited a revelation ňĻthrough Gabrielňļ, follow its recitation ňĻcloselyňļ.

75:19.text: Then it is surely upon Us to make it clear ňĻto youňļ.

75:20.text: But no! In fact, you love this fleeting world,

75:22.text: On that Day ňĻsomeňļ faces will be bright,

75:24.text: And ňĻotherňļ faces will be gloomy,

75:25.text: anticipating something devastating to befall them.

75:26.text: But no! ňĻBeware of the dayňļ when the soul reaches the collar bone ňĻas it leavesňļ,

75:27.text: and it will be said, ‚ÄúIs there any healer ňĻwho can save this lifeňļ?‚ÄĚ

75:28.text: And the dying person realizes it is ňĻtheirňļ time to depart,

75:29.text: and ňĻthenňļ their feet are tied together ňĻin a shroudňļ.

75:30.text: On that day they will be driven to your Lord ňĻaloneňļ.

75:31.text: This denier neither believed nor prayed,

75:32.text: but persisted in denial and turned away,

75:33.text: then went to their own people, walking boastfully.

75:34.text: Woe to you, and more woe!

75:35.text: Again, woe to you, and even more woe!

75:36.text: Do people think they will be left without purpose?

75:37.text: Were they not ňĻonceňļ a sperm-drop emitted?

75:38.text: Then they became a clinging clot,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then He developed and perfected their form,
footnotes: ¬†‚ÄôAlaqah, meaning the embryo resembles a leech.

75:39.text: producing from it both sexes, male and female.

75:40.text: Is such ňĻa Creatorňļ unable to bring the dead back to life?

76:1.text: Is there not a period of time when each human is nothing yet worth mentioning?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This is the time before a human comes into being and as an embryo.

76:2.text: ňĻForňļ indeed, We ňĻaloneňļ created humans from a drop of mixed fluids,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†ňĻin orderňļ to test them, so We made them hear and see.
footnotes: ¬†The¬†mixture of male and female gametes (sperm and egg) which form the zygote after fertilization.

76:3.text: We already showed them the Way, whether they ňĻchoose toňļ be grateful or ungrateful.

76:4.text: Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains, shackles, and a blazing Fire.

76:5.text: Indeed, the virtuous will have a drink ňĻof pure wineňļ‚ÄĒflavoured with camphor‚ÄĒ

76:6.text: ňĻfromňļ a spring where Allah‚Äôs servants will drink, flowing at their will.

76:7.text: They ňĻare those whoňļ fulfil ňĻtheirňļ vows and fear a Day of sweeping horror,

76:8.text: and give food‚ÄĒdespite their desire for it‚ÄĒto the poor, the orphan, and the captive,

76:9.text: ňĻsaying to themselves,ňļ ‚ÄúWe feed you only for the sake of Allah, seeking neither reward nor thanks from you.

76:10.text: We fear from our Lord a horribly distressful Day.‚ÄĚ

76:11.text: So Allah will deliver them from the horror of that Day, and grant them radiance and joy,

76:12.text: and reward them for their perseverance with a Garden ňĻin Paradiseňļ and ňĻgarments ofňļ silk.

76:13.text: There they will be reclining on ňĻcanopiedňļ couches, never seeing scorching heat or bitter cold.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†There will be no day or night, sun or moon, winter or summer in Paradise. It will always be bright and temperate.

76:14.text: The Garden‚Äôs shade will be right above them, and its fruit will be made very easy to reach.

76:15.text: They will be waited on with silver vessels and cups of crystal‚ÄĒ

76:16.text: crystalline silver, filled precisely as desired.

76:17.text: And they will be given a drink ňĻof pure wineňļ flavoured with ginger

76:18.text: from a spring there, called Salsab√ģl.

76:19.text: They will be waited on by eternal youths. If you saw them, you would think they were scattered pearls.

76:20.text: And if you looked around, you would see ňĻindescribableňļ bliss and a vast kingdom.

76:21.text: The virtuous will be ňĻdressedňļ in garments of fine green silk and rich brocade, and adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a purifying drink.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The word ŠĻ≠ah√Ľr¬†implies that once the believers take that pure drink, there will be no bad feelings in their hearts or ailments in their bodies.

76:22.text: ňĻAnd they will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúAll this is surely a reward for you. Your striving has been appreciated.‚ÄĚ

76:23.text: Indeed, it is We Who have revealed the Quran to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in stages.

76:24.text: So be patient with your Lord‚Äôs decree, and do not yield to any evildoer or ňĻstaunchňļ disbeliever from among them.

76:25.text: ňĻAlwaysňļ remember the Name of your Lord morning and evening,

76:26.text: and prostrate before Him during part of the night,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and glorify Him long at night.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†
footnotes: ¬†This verse refers to the five daily prayers: the dawn prayer, then noon and afternoon prayers, then sunset and late evening prayers, respectively.
¬†Extra prayers offered at night. See¬†73:20.

76:27.text: Surely those ňĻpagansňļ love this fleeting world, ňĻtotallyňļ neglecting a weighty Day ahead of them.

76:28.text: It is We Who created them and perfected their ňĻphysicalňļ form. But if We will, We can easily replace them with others.

76:29.text: Surely this is a reminder. So let whoever wills take the ňĻRightňļ Way to their Lord.

76:30.text: But you cannot will ňĻto do soňļ unless Allah wills. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

76:31.text: He admits whoever He wills into His mercy. As for the wrongdoers, He has prepared for them a painful punishment.

77:1.text: By those ňĻwindsňļ sent forth successively,

77:4.text: And ňĻbyňļ those ňĻangelsňļ fully distinguishing ňĻtruth from falsehoodňļ,

77:7.text: Surely, what you are promised will come to pass.

77:8.text: So when the stars are put out,

77:9.text: and the sky is torn apart,

77:10.text: and the mountains are blown away,

77:11.text: and the messengers‚Äô time ňĻto testifyňļ comes up‚ÄĒ

77:12.text: for which Day has all this been set?

77:13.text: For the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision!

77:14.text: And what will make you realize what the Day of Decision is?

77:15.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:16.text: Did We not destroy earlier disbelievers?

77:17.text: And We will make the later disbelievers follow them.

77:18.text: This is how We deal with the wicked.

77:19.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:20.text: Did We not create you from a humble fluid,

77:21.text: placing it in a secure place

77:23.text: We ňĻperfectlyňļ ordained ňĻits developmentňļ. How excellent are We in doing so!

77:24.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:25.text: Have We not made the earth a lodging

77:26.text: for the living and the dead,

77:27.text: and placed upon it towering, firm mountains, and given you fresh water to drink?

77:28.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:29.text: ňĻThe disbelievers will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúProceed into that ňĻFireňļ which you used to deny!

77:30.text: Proceed into the shade ňĻof smokeňļ which rises in three columns,

77:31.text: providing neither coolness nor shelter from the flames.

77:32.text: Indeed, it hurls sparks ňĻas bigňļ as huge castles,

77:33.text: and ňĻas darkňļ as black camels.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†lit., yellow camels. Black camels‚Äô hair glistens yellow in sunlight.

77:34.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:35.text: On that Day¬†they will not ňĻbe in a position toňļ speak,

77:36.text: nor will they be permitted to offer excuses.

77:37.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:38.text: ňĻThey will be told by Allah,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision: We have gathered you along with earlier disbelievers ňĻfor punishmentňļ.

77:39.text: So if you have a scheme ňĻto save yourselvesňļ, then use it against Me.‚ÄĚ

77:40.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:41.text: Indeed, the righteous will be amid ňĻcoolňļ shade and springs

77:43.text: ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúEat and drink happily for what you used to do.‚ÄĚ

77:44.text: Surely this is how We reward the good-doers.

77:45.text: ňĻButňļ woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:46.text: ‚ÄúEat and enjoy yourselves for a little while, ňĻforňļ you are truly wicked.‚ÄĚ

77:47.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:48.text: When it is said to them, ‚ÄúBow down ňĻbefore Allah,‚ÄĚ they do not bow.

77:49.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers!

77:50.text: So what message after this ňĻQuranňļ would they believe in?

78:1.text: What are they asking one another about?

footnotes: ¬†The pagans used to mock the teachings of the Quran (e.g., resurrection and judgment). They disagreed whether the Quran was magic, poetry, or fortune-telling.

78:4.text: But no! They will come to know.

78:5.text: Again, no! They will come to know.

78:6.text: Have We not smoothed out the earth ňĻlike a bedňļ,

78:7.text: and ňĻmadeňļ the mountains as ňĻitsňļ pegs,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Just like tent pegs and icebergs, mountains go deep below the surface, slowing tectonic movement.

78:9.text: and made your sleep for rest,

78:10.text: and made the night as a cover,

78:11.text: and made the day for livelihood,

78:12.text: and built above you seven mighty ňĻheavensňļ,

78:13.text: and placed ňĻin themňļ a shining lamp,

78:14.text: and sent down from rainclouds pouring water,

78:15.text: producing by it grain and ňĻvariousňļ plants,

78:17.text: Indeed, the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision is an appointed time‚ÄĒ

78:18.text: ňĻit isňļ the Day the Trumpet will be blown, and you will ňĻallňļ come forth in crowds.

78:19.text: The sky will be ňĻsplitňļ open, becoming ňĻmanyňļ gates,

78:20.text: and the mountains will be blown away, becoming ňĻlikeňļ a mirage.

78:21.text: Indeed, Hell is lying in ambush

78:22.text: as a home for the transgressors,

78:23.text: where they will remain for ňĻendlessňļ ages.

78:24.text: There they will not taste any coolness or drink,

78:25.text: except boiling water and ňĻoozingňļ pus‚ÄĒ

78:27.text: For they never expected any reckoning,

78:29.text: And We have everything recorded precisely.

78:30.text: ňĻSo the deniers will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste ňĻthe punishmentňļ, for all you will get from Us is more torment.‚ÄĚ

78:31.text: Indeed, the righteous will have salvation‚ÄĒ

78:33.text: and full-bosomed maidens of equal age,

78:34.text: and full cups ňĻof pure wineňļ,

78:35.text: never to hear any idle talk or lying therein‚ÄĒ

78:36.text: a ňĻfittingňļ reward as a generous gift from your Lord,

78:37.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, the Most Compassionate. No one will dare speak to Him

78:38.text: on the Day the ňĻholyňļ spirit<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and the angels will stand in ranks. None will talk, except those granted permission by the Most Compassionate and whose words are true.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the angel Gabriel.

78:39.text: That Day is the ňĻultimateňļ truth. So let whoever wills take the path leading back to their Lord.

78:40.text: Indeed, We have warned you of an imminent punishment‚ÄĒthe Day every person will see ňĻthe consequences ofňļ what their hands have done, and the disbelievers will cry, ‚ÄúI wish I were dust.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†In the Hereafter, Allah will judge between all of His creation including animals, which will eventually become dust. The wicked will wish that they too were turned into dust so they do not have to go to the Fire, as reported in an authentic narration collected by Al-Šł§akim.

79:1.text: By those ňĻangelsňļ stripping out ňĻevil soulsňļ harshly,

79:2.text: and those pulling out ňĻgood soulsňļ gently,

79:3.text: and those gliding ňĻthrough heavensňļ swiftly,

79:4.text: and those taking the lead vigorously,

79:6.text: ňĻConsiderňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the quaking Blast will come to pass,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The first Blast will cause everyone to die except those spared by Allah. Then the second one will cause the dead to come to life for judgment. See¬†39:68.

79:8.text: ňĻThe deniers‚Äôňļ hearts on that Day will be trembling ňĻin horrorňļ,

79:10.text: ňĻBut nowňļ they ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWill we really be restored to our former state,

79:11.text: even after we have been reduced to decayed bones?‚ÄĚ

79:12.text: Adding, ‚ÄúThen such a return would be a ňĻtotalňļ loss ňĻfor usňļ!‚ÄĚ

79:13.text: But indeed, it will take only one ňĻmightyňļ Blast,

79:14.text: and at once they will be above ground.

79:15.text: Has the story of Moses reached you ňĻO Prophetňļ?

79:16.text: His Lord called him in the sacred valley of ŠĻ¨uwa,

79:17.text: ňĻcommanding,ňļ ‚ÄúGo to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ňĻall boundsňļ.

79:18.text: And say, ‚ÄėWould you ňĻbe willing toňļ purify yourself,

79:19.text: and let me guide you to your Lord so that you will be in awe ňĻof Himňļ?‚Äô‚ÄĚ

79:20.text: Then Moses showed him the great sign,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The staff turning into a snake. See¬†20:17-23.

79:21.text: but he denied and disobeyed ňĻAllahňļ,

79:22.text: then turned his back, striving ňĻagainst the truthňļ.

79:23.text: Then he summoned ňĻhis peopleňļ and called out,

79:24.text: saying, ‚ÄúI am your lord, the most high!‚ÄĚ

79:25.text: So Allah overtook him, making him an example in this life and the next.

79:26.text: Surely in this is a lesson for whoever stands in awe of ňĻAllahňļ.

79:27.text: Which is harder to create: you or the sky?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†He built it,
footnotes: ¬†This is from a human perspective. Otherwise, both the creation of the universe and the resurrection of humans are easy for Allah.

79:28.text: raising it high and forming it flawlessly.

79:29.text: He dimmed its night, and brought forth its daylight.

79:30.text: As for the earth, He spread it out as well,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The Arabic verb daŠł•a¬†also suggests that the earth is egg-shaped. The noun form diŠł•ya¬†is still used in some Arab dialects to mean an egg. Based on 2:29, 41:9-12, and 79:27-33, Allah ordained both realms, then formed the earth and created its provisions, then formed the heaven into seven heavens‚ÄĒall in six ňĻheavenlyňļ Days. See footnote for 7:54.

79:31.text: bringing forth its water and pastures

79:32.text: and setting the mountains firmly ňĻupon itňļ‚ÄĒ

79:33.text: all as ňĻa means ofňļ sustenance for you and your animals.

79:34.text: But, when the Supreme Disaster<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†comes to pass‚ÄĒ
footnotes: ¬†This is the second Blast which will cause the dead to come to life for judgment.¬†See¬†39:68.

79:35.text: the Day every person will remember all ňĻtheirňļ striving,

79:36.text: and the Hellfire will be displayed for all to see‚ÄĒ

79:37.text: then as for those who transgressed

79:38.text: and preferred the ňĻfleetingňļ life of this world,

79:39.text: the Hellfire will certainly be ňĻtheirňļ home.

79:40.text: And as for those who were in awe of standing before their Lord and restrained themselves from ňĻevilňļ desires,

79:41.text: Paradise will certainly be ňĻtheirňļ home.

79:42.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ regarding the Hour, ‚ÄúWhen will it be?‚ÄĚ

79:43.text: But it is not for you to tell its time.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Another possible translation: ‚ÄúWhat is this question? You ňĻO Prophetňļ are one of its signs.‚ÄĚ It is believed‚ÄĒbased on authentic narrations from the Prophet‚ÄĒthat he (Ô∑ļ) is one of the signs of the Day of Judgment.

79:44.text: That knowledge rests with your Lord ňĻaloneňļ.

79:45.text: Your duty is only to warn whoever is in awe of it.

79:46.text: On the Day they see it, it will be as if they had stayed ňĻin the worldňļ no more than one evening or its morning.

8:1.text: They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ regarding the spoils of war. Say, ‚ÄúTheir distribution is decided by Allah and His Messenger. So be mindful of Allah, settle your affairs, and obey Allah and His Messenger if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.‚ÄĚ

8:2.text: The ňĻtrueňļ believers are only those whose hearts tremble at the remembrance of Allah, whose faith increases when His revelations are recited to them, and who put their trust in their Lord.

8:3.text: ňĻThey areňļ those who establish prayer and donate from what We have provided for them.

8:4.text: It is they who are the true believers. They will have elevated ranks, forgiveness, and an honourable provision from their Lord.

8:5.text: Similarly, when your Lord brought you ňĻO Prophetňļ out of your home for a just cause, a group of believers was totally against it.

8:6.text: They disputed with you about the truth after it had been made clear, as if they were being driven to death with their eyes wide open.

8:7.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†believers,ňļ when Allah promised ňĻto giveňļ you the upper hand over either target, you wished to capture the unarmed party.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†But it was Allah‚Äôs Will¬†to establish the truth by His Words and uproot the disbelievers;
footnotes: ¬†After many years of persecution in Mecca, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and many of his early followers decided to emigrate secretly to Medina, about 250 miles to the north, leaving behind their homes and valuables, which were soon taken over by the pagans of Mecca. To avenge ¬†this financial loss, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) decided to capture an unarmed Meccan trade caravan headed by Abu Sufy√Ęn, a Meccan chief. Eventually, the caravan escaped, but the Meccans mobilized an army of over 1000 well-armed soldiers, more than three times the size of the Muslim force. Many Muslims had hoped to capture the caravan without having to meet the Meccan army in battle. Although the Muslims were vastly outnumbered and lightly-armed, they still won this decisive battle.

8:8.text: to firmly establish the truth and wipe out falsehood‚ÄĒeven to the dismay of the wicked.

8:9.text: ňĻRememberňļ when you cried out to your Lord for help, He answered, ‚ÄúI will reinforce you with a thousand angels‚ÄĒfollowed by many others.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†Five thousand angels in total, as mentioned in 3:125.

8:10.text: And Allah made this a sign of victory and reassurance to your hearts. Victory comes only from Allah. Surely Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

8:11.text: ňĻRememberňļ when He caused drowsiness to overcome you, giving you serenity.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And He sent down rain from the sky to purify you, free you from Satan‚Äôs whispers, strengthen your hearts, and make ňĻyourňļ steps firm.
footnotes: ¬†This happened the night before the battle.

8:12.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when your Lord revealed to the angels, ‚ÄúI am with you. So make the believers stand firm. I will cast horror into the hearts of the disbelievers. So strike their necks and strike their fingertips.‚ÄĚ

8:13.text: This is because they defied Allah and His Messenger. And whoever defies Allah and His Messenger, then ňĻknow thatňļ Allah is surely severe in punishment.

8:14.text: That ňĻworldly punishmentňļ is yours, so taste it! Then the disbelievers will suffer the torment of the Fire.

8:15.text: O believers! When you face the disbelievers in battle, never turn your backs to them.

8:16.text: And whoever does so on such an occasion‚ÄĒunless it is a manoeuvre or to join their own troops‚ÄĒwill earn the displeasure of Allah, and their home will be Hell. What an evil destination!

8:17.text: It was not you ňĻbelieversňļ who killed them, but it was Allah Who did so. Nor was it you ňĻO Prophetňļ who threw ňĻa handful of sand at the disbelieversňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†but it was Allah Who did so, rendering the believers a great favour. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
footnotes: ¬†Before the battle, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) threw a handful of sand at the disbelievers and prayed for their defeat.

8:18.text: As such, Allah frustrates the evil plans of the disbelievers.

8:19.text: If you ňĻMeccansňļ sought judgment, now it has come to you. And if you cease, it will be for your own good. But if you persist, We will persist. And your forces‚ÄĒno matter how numerous they might be‚ÄĒwill not benefit you whatsoever. For Allah is certainly with the believers.

8:20.text: O believers! Obey Allah and His Messenger and do not turn away from him while you hear ňĻhis callňļ.

8:21.text: Do not be like those who say, ‚ÄúWe hear,‚ÄĚ but in fact they are not listening.

8:22.text: Indeed, the worst of all beings in the sight of Allah are the ňĻwilfullyňļ deaf and dumb, who do not understand.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This refers metaphorically to the disbelievers who fail to see and hear the truth.

8:23.text: Had Allah found any goodness in them, He would have certainly made them hear. ňĻButňļ even if He had made them hear, they would have surely turned away heedlessly.

8:24.text: O believers! Respond to Allah and His Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life. And know that Allah stands between a person and their heart, and that to Him you will all be gathered.

8:25.text: Beware of a trial that will not only affect the wrongdoers among you. And know that Allah is severe in punishment.

8:26.text: Remember when you had been vastly outnumbered and oppressed in the land,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†constantly in fear of attacks by your enemy, then He sheltered you, strengthened you with His help, and provided you with good things so perhaps you would be thankful.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Mecca.

8:27.text: O believers! Do not betray Allah and the Messenger, nor betray your trusts knowingly.

8:28.text: And know that your wealth and your children are only a test and that with Allah is a great reward.

8:29.text: O believers! If you are mindful of Allah, He will grant you a decisive authority, absolve you of your sins, and forgive you. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.

8:30.text: And ňĻremember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when the disbelievers conspired to capture, kill, or exile you. They planned, but Allah also planned. And Allah is the best of planners.

8:31.text: Whenever Our revelations are recited to them, they challenge ňĻyouňļ, ‚ÄúWe have already heard ňĻthe recitationňļ. If we wanted, we could have easily produced something similar. This ňĻQuranňļ is nothing but ancient fables!‚ÄĚ

8:32.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when they prayed, ‚ÄúO Allah! If this is indeed the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from the sky or overcome us with a painful punishment.‚ÄĚ

8:33.text: But Allah would never punish them while you ňĻO Prophetňļ were in their midst. Nor would He ever punish them if they prayed for forgiveness.

8:34.text: And why should Allah not punish them while they hinder pilgrims from the Sacred Mosque, claiming to be its rightful guardians? None has the right to guardianship except those mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, but most pagans do not know.

8:35.text: Their prayer at the Sacred House was nothing but whistling and clapping. So taste the punishment for your disbelief.

8:36.text: Surely the disbelievers spend their wealth to hinder others from the Path of Allah. They will continue to spend to the point of regret. Then they will be defeated and the disbelievers will be driven into Hell,

8:37.text: so Allah may separate the evil from the good. He will pile up the evil ones all together and then cast them into Hell. They are the ňĻtrueňļ losers.

8:38.text: Tell the disbelievers that if they desist, their past will be forgiven. But if they persist, then they have an example in those destroyed before them.

8:39.text: Fight against them until there is no more persecution‚ÄĒand ňĻyourňļ devotion will be entirely to Allah. But if they desist, then surely Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

8:40.text: And if they do not comply, then know that Allah is your Protector. What an excellent Protector, and what an excellent Helper!

8:41.text: Know that whatever spoils you take, one-fifth is for Allah and the Messenger, his close relatives, orphans, the poor, and ňĻneedyňļ travellers, if you ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and what We revealed to Our servant on that decisive day when the two armies met ňĻat Badrňļ. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

8:42.text: ňĻRememberňļ when you were on the near side of the valley, your enemy on the far side, and the caravan was below you. Even if the two armies had made an appointment ňĻto meetňļ, both would have certainly missed it.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Still it transpired so Allah may establish what He had destined‚ÄĒthat those who were to perish and those who were to survive might do so after the truth had been made clear to both. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
footnotes: ¬†The battle was not planned since the Muslim force was after the caravan. Had the two armies made plans to meet in battle at that time and place, they both probably would have missed each other.

8:43.text: ňĻRemember, O ¬†Prophet,ňļ when Allah showed them in your dream as few in number. Had He shown them to you as many, you ňĻbelieversňļ would have certainly faltered and disputed in the matter. But Allah spared you ňĻfrom thatňļ. Surely He knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

8:44.text: Then when your armies met, Allah made them appear as few in your eyes, and made you appear as few in theirs, so Allah may establish what He had destined. And to Allah ňĻallňļ matters will be returned ňĻfor judgmentňļ.

8:45.text: O believers! When you face an enemy, stand firm and remember Allah often so you may triumph.

8:46.text: Obey Allah and His Messenger and do not dispute with one another, or you would be discouraged and weakened. Persevere! Surely Allah is with those who persevere.

8:47.text: Do not be like those ňĻpagansňļ who left their homes arrogantly, only to be seen by people and to hinder others from Allah‚Äôs Path. And Allah is Fully Aware of what they do.

8:48.text: And ňĻrememberňļ when Satan made their ňĻevilňļ deeds appealing to them, and said, ‚ÄúNo one can overcome you today. I am surely by your side.‚ÄĚ But when the two forces faced off, he cowered and said, ‚ÄúI have absolutely nothing to do with you. I certainly see what you do not see. I truly fear Allah, for Allah is severe in punishment.‚ÄĚ

8:49.text: ňĻRememberňļ when the hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts said, ‚ÄúThese ňĻbelieversňļ are deluded by their faith.‚ÄĚ But whoever puts their trust in Allah, surely Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

8:50.text: If only you could see when the angels take the souls of the disbelievers, beating their faces and backs, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste the torment of burning!

8:51.text: This is ňĻthe rewardňļ for what your hands have done. And Allah is never unjust to ňĻHisňļ creation.‚ÄĚ

8:52.text: Their fate is that of the people of Pharaoh and those before them‚ÄĒthey all disbelieved in Allah‚Äôs signs, so Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed, Allah is All-Powerful, severe in punishment.

8:53.text: This is because Allah would never discontinue His favour to a people until they discontinue their faith. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

8:54.text: That was the case with Pharaoh‚Äôs people and those before them‚ÄĒthey all rejected the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins and drowned Pharaoh‚Äôs people. They were all wrongdoers.

8:55.text: Indeed, the worst of all beings in the sight of Allah are those who persist in disbelief, never to have faith‚ÄĒ

8:56.text: ňĻnamelyňļ those with whom you ňĻO Prophetňļ have entered into treaties, but they violate them every time, not fearing the consequences.

8:57.text: If you ever encounter them in battle, make a fearsome example of them, so perhaps those who would follow them may be deterred.

8:58.text: And if you ňĻO Prophetňļ see signs of betrayal by a people, respond by openly terminating your treaty with them. Surely Allah does not like those who betray.

8:59.text: Do not let those disbelievers<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†think they are not within reach. They will have no escape.
footnotes: ¬†Who survived the Battle of Badr.

8:60.text: Prepare against them what you ňĻbelieversňļ can of ňĻmilitaryňļ power and cavalry to deter Allah‚Äôs enemies and your enemies as well as other enemies unknown to you but known to Allah. Whatever you spend in the cause of Allah will be paid to you in full and you will not be wronged.

8:61.text: If the enemy is inclined towards peace, make peace with them. And put your trust in Allah. Indeed, He ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

8:62.text: But if their intention is only to deceive you, then Allah is certainly sufficient for you. He is the One Who has supported you with His help and with the believers.

8:63.text: He brought their hearts together. Had you spent all the riches in the earth, you could not have united their hearts. But Allah has united them. Indeed, He is Almighty, All-Wise.

8:64.text: O ¬†Prophet! Allah is sufficient for you and for the believers who follow you.

8:65.text: O Prophet! Motivate the believers to fight. If there are twenty steadfast among you, they will overcome two hundred. And if there are one hundred of you, they will overcome one thousand of the disbelievers, for they are a people who do not comprehend.

8:66.text: Now Allah has lightened your burden, for He knows that there is weakness in you. So if there are a hundred steadfast among you, they will overcome two hundred. And if there be one thousand, they will overcome two thousand, by Allah‚Äôs Will. And Allah is with the steadfast.

8:67.text: It is not fit for a prophet that he should take captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land. You ňĻbelieversňļ settled with the fleeting gains of this world,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†while Allah‚Äôs aim ňĻfor youňļ is the Hereafter. Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

The believers are urged to march forth with the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) for the Battle of Tab√Ľk in the summer of 9 A.H./631 C.E. Hypocrites are exposed and their false excuses are refuted. Muslims are reminded of how Allah turned the believers‚Äô initial defeat into sweeping victory at the Battle of Šł§unain and how Allah saved His Messenger (Ô∑ļ) from the pagans during his migration to Medina. Allah‚Äôs acceptance of repentance is echoed throughout the s√Ľrah, hence its title.

9:2.text: ‚ÄúYou ňĻpolytheistsňļ may travel freely through the land for four months, but know that you will have no escape from Allah, and that Allah will disgrace the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†This is the only s√Ľrah¬†in the Quran that does not begin with: ‚ÄúIn the Name of Allah‚ÄĒthe Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.‚ÄĚ The reason is that this s√Ľrah¬†is considered to be a continuation of the previous one, and that it begins with an open termination of treaties not fully respected by the polytheists. Since this constitutes a declaration of war, it would not be fitting to open with the mention of Allah‚Äôs compassion and mercy.

9:3.text: A declaration from Allah and His Messenger ňĻis madeňļ to all people on the day of the greater pilgrimage<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†that Allah and His Messenger are free of the polytheists. So if you ňĻpagansňļ repent, it will be better for you. But if you turn away, then know that you will have no escape from Allah. And give good news ňĻO Prophetňļ to the disbelievers of a painful punishment.
footnotes: ¬†The 10th¬†of ‚Īęul-Šł§ijjah, a significant day in the greater pilgrimage (Šł•ajj). The lesser pilgrimage is known as¬†‚Äôumrah.

9:4.text: As for the polytheists who have honoured every term of their treaty with you and have not supported an enemy against you, honour your treaty with them until the end of its term. Surely Allah loves those who are mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

9:5.text: But once the Sacred Months have passed, kill the polytheists ňĻwho violated their treatiesňļ wherever you find them,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†capture them, besiege them, and lie in wait for them on every way. But if they repent, perform prayers, and pay alms-tax, then set them free. Indeed, Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., inside or outside the sanctuary of the Sacred House in Mecca.

9:6.text: And if anyone from the polytheists asks for your protection ňĻO Prophetňļ, grant it to them so they may hear the Word of Allah, then escort them to a place of safety, for they are a people who have no knowledge.

9:7.text: How can such polytheists have a treaty with Allah and His Messenger, except those you have made a treaty with at the Sacred Mosque?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†So, as long as they are true to you, be true to them. Indeed Allah loves those who are mindful ňĻof Himňļ.
footnotes: ¬†This is known as the Treaty of Šł§udaibiyah, which was signed by the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and the pagans of Mecca in 6 A.H./628 C.E., diffusing the tension between Muslims and the Meccans and affirming a 10-year peace truce. The treaty was violated by the Meccans in 8 A.H/630 C.E.

9:8.text: How ňĻcan they have a treatyňļ? If they were to have the upper hand over you, they would have no respect for kinship or treaty. They only flatter you with their tongues, but their hearts are in denial, and most of them are rebellious.

9:9.text: They chose a fleeting gain over Allah‚Äôs revelations, hindering ňĻothersňļ from His Way. Evil indeed is what they have done!

9:10.text: They do not honour the bonds of kinship or treaties with the believers. It is they who are the transgressors.

9:11.text: But if they repent, perform prayer, and pay alms-tax, then they are your brothers in faith. This is how We make the revelations clear for people of knowledge.

9:12.text: But if they break their oaths after making a pledge and attack your faith, then fight the champions of disbelief‚ÄĒwho never honour their oaths‚ÄĒso perhaps they will desist.

9:13.text: Will you not fight those who have broken their oaths, conspired to expel the Messenger ňĻfrom Meccaňļ, and attacked you first? Do you fear them? Allah is more deserving of your fear, if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.

9:14.text: ňĻSoňļ fight them and Allah will punish them at your hands, put them to shame, help you overcome them, and soothe the hearts of the believers‚ÄĒ

9:15.text: removing rage from their hearts. And Allah pardons whoever He wills. For Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:16.text: Do you ňĻbelieversňļ think that you will be left without Allah proving who among you ňĻtrulyňļ struggles ňĻin His causeňļ and never takes trusted allies other than Allah, His Messenger, or the believers? And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

9:17.text: It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of Allah while they openly profess disbelief. Their deeds are void, and they will be in the Fire forever.

9:18.text: The mosques of Allah should only be maintained by those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and fear none but Allah. It is right to hope that they will be among the ňĻtrulyňļ guided.

9:19.text: Do you ňĻpagansňļ consider providing the pilgrims with water and maintaining the Sacred Mosque as equal to believing in Allah and the Last Day and struggling in the cause of Allah? They are not equal in Allah‚Äôs sight. And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

9:20.text: Those who have believed, emigrated, and strived in the cause of Allah with their wealth and their lives are greater in rank in the sight of Allah. It is they who will triumph.

9:21.text: Their Lord gives them good news of His mercy, pleasure, and Gardens with everlasting bliss,

9:22.text: to stay there for ever and ever. Surely with Allah is a great reward.

9:23.text: O believers! Do not take your parents and siblings as trusted allies if they choose disbelief over belief. And whoever of you does so, they are the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.

9:24.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf your parents and children and siblings and spouses and extended family and the wealth you have acquired and the trade you fear will decline and the homes you cherish‚ÄĒňĻif all theseňļ are more beloved to you than Allah and His Messenger and struggling in His Way, then wait until Allah brings about His Will. Allah does not guide the rebellious people.‚ÄĚ

9:25.text: Indeed Allah has given you ňĻbelieversňļ victory on many battlefields, even at the Battle of Šł§unain<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†when you took pride in your great numbers, but they proved of no advantage to you. The earth, despite its vastness, seemed to close in on you, then you turned back in retreat.
footnotes: ¬†After the Muslims‚Äô victory in Mecca in 8 A.H/630 C.E., almost all Arab tribes pledged allegiance to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) except some tribes such as Haw√Ęzin and Thaq√ģf. Both tribes decided to attack the Muslims, so the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) led an army of 12 000 soldiers to attack the two tribes. Since that was the largest Muslim army ever mobilized, some Muslims expressed their conviction that such an army could not be defeated. However, on the way to battle, the Muslim army was ambushed and most soldiers fled, except for the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and a few loyalists. Eventually, the Muslims were re-organized and won a decisive battle.

9:26.text: Then Allah sent down His reassurance<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†upon His Messenger and the believers, and sent down forces<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†you could not see, and punished those who disbelieved. Such was the reward of the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†lit., tranquility.
¬†i.e., angels.

9:27.text: Then afterwards Allah will turn in grace to whoever He wills.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., Allah will accept those who embrace Islam. Almost all of Arabia became Muslim before the death of Prophet MuŠł•ammad (Ô∑ļ) in 11 A.H./632 C.E.

9:28.text: O believers! Indeed, the polytheists are ňĻspirituallyňļ impure,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†so they should not approach the Sacred Mosque after this year.<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†If you fear poverty, Allah will enrich you out of His bounty, if He wills. Surely, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., impure spiritually because of idolatry, not physically.
¬†The 9th¬†year of the Prophet‚Äôs migration from Mecca to Medina.

9:29.text: Fight those who do not believe in Allah and the Last Day, nor comply with what Allah and His Messenger have forbidden, nor embrace the religion of truth from among those who were given the Scripture,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†until they pay the tax,<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>¬†willingly submitting, fully humbled.
footnotes: ¬†To fully understand this verse we need to bear in mind that Quranic verses are of two types. General verses talk about belief in Allah, good manners, and acts of worship. Specific verses, such¬†as this verse, were revealed in regards to particular situations. This s√Ľrah¬†came at a time when the pagans of Arabia (and their allies) repeatedly violated treaties they had signed with the Prophet (Ô∑ļ). Muslims had to fight for the survival of their newly established state in Medina. So this verse discusses dealing with those who violated their agreements and attacked the Muslims. Offenders were fought, unless they stopped their aggression. If they chose not to accept Islam, they were obligated to pay Jizya-tax.

¬†Taxation systems have existed since ancient times. There are several references in the Old Testament (e.g., Ezra 4:20). In the New Testament, Jesus allows paying taxes to Caesar (Luke 20:25). Under Islamic rule, all individuals had financial obligations‚ÄĒMuslims paid zak√Ęh¬†(2.5% of their savings) and non-Muslims (‚Ī¨imm√ģs) were required to pay jizyah¬†(tax for protection from foreign enemies). The jizyah¬†was an average of one dinar (4.25 g of gold) annually. Women, children, the elderly, the clergy, the poor, and those who were unable to work were exempt. Those who opted to join the army were also exempt. Poor ‚Ī¨imm√ģs¬†were supported financially by the state. Muslim rulers refunded the jizyah¬†if they failed to protect their non-Muslim subjects.

9:30.text: The Jews say, ‚ÄúEzra is the son of Allah,‚ÄĚ while the Christians say, ‚ÄúThe Messiah is the son of Allah.‚ÄĚ Such are their baseless assertions, only parroting the words of earlier disbelievers. May Allah condemn them! How can they be deluded ňĻfrom the truthňļ?

9:31.text: They have taken their rabbis and monks as well as the Messiah, son of Mary, as lords besides Allah,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†even though they were commanded to worship none but One God. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. Glorified is He above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!
footnotes: ¬†When ‚ÄôAdi ibn Šł§√Ętim, a companion of the Prophet (Ô∑ļ), heard this verse, he said, ‚ÄúBut the Jews and Christians do not worship their rabbis and monks!‚ÄĚ The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) replied, ‚ÄúDo the rabbis and monks not forbid the permissible and permit the forbidden, and they obey them?‚ÄĚ ‚ÄôAdi answered, ‚ÄúYes, they do.‚ÄĚ The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) concluded, ‚ÄúThis is how they worship them.‚ÄĚ Collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i.

9:32.text: They wish to extinguish Allah‚Äôs light<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†with their mouths, but Allah will only allow His light to be perfected, even to the dismay of the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., religion of truth.

9:33.text: He is the One Who has sent His Messenger with ňĻtrueňļ guidance and the religion of truth, making it prevail over all others, even to the dismay of the polytheists.

9:34.text: O believers! Indeed, many rabbis and monks consume people‚Äôs wealth wrongfully and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah. Give good news of a painful torment to those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it in Allah‚Äôs cause.

9:35.text: The Day ňĻwill comeňļ when their treasure will be heated up in the Fire of Hell, and their foreheads, sides, and backs branded with it. ňĻIt will be said to them,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is the treasure you hoarded for yourselves. Now taste what you hoarded!‚ÄĚ

9:36.text: Indeed, the number of months ordained by Allah is twelve‚ÄĒin Allah‚Äôs Record<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†since the day He created the heavens and the earth‚ÄĒof which four are sacred. That is the Right Way. So do not wrong one another during these months. And fight the polytheists together as they fight together against you. And know that Allah is with those mindful ňĻof Himňļ.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the Preserved Tablet in which everything is written.

9:37.text: Reallocating the sanctity of ňĻtheseňļ months<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†is an increase in disbelief, by which the disbelievers are led ňĻfarňļ astray. They adjust the sanctity one year and uphold it in another, only to maintain the number of months sanctified by Allah, violating the very months Allah has made sacred. Their evil deeds have been made appealing to them. And Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.
footnotes: ¬†The sacred months are the 11th, 12th, 1st, and 7th¬†of the Islamic calendar. Since the polytheists knew that it was forbidden to fight in the sacred months, they used to transfer the sanctity to other months, say for example, the 3rd, 4th, 8th, and 10th¬†as long as they maintained the sanctity of any four months throughout the year.

9:38.text: O believers! What is the matter with you that when you are asked to march forth in the cause of Allah, you cling firmly to ňĻyourňļ land?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Do you prefer the life of this world over the Hereafter? The enjoyment of this worldly life is insignificant compared to that of the Hereafter.
footnotes: ¬†The fast pace of the spread of Islam in Arabia in the 7th¬†century was intimidating to the world‚Äôs two superpowers of that time: the Romans and Persians. The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) received the news that a Roman army was being mobilized to launch an attack on the newly established Muslim state in Medina, so he announced that he was going to march to Tab√Ľk, located over 700 miles to the north, to meet the Romans in the summer of 9 A.H./631 C.E. It was a time of hardship because of the scorching heat, the long distance, and the financial situation of the Muslims. Although the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was able to mobilize over 30 000 Muslims for battle, many others did not join the army with or without valid excuses. Eventually, the Roman forces were discouraged from fighting and fled to Damascus and other cities under Roman rule. Therefore, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) returned to Medina with a feeling of triumph. With a new power now emerging in Arabia, many tribes started to switch their alliances from Caesar to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ).

9:39.text: If you do not march forth, He will afflict you with a painful torment and replace you with other people. You are not harming Him in the least. And Allah is Most Capable of everything.

9:40.text: ňĻIt does not matterňļ if you ňĻbelieversňļ do not support him, for Allah did in fact support him when the disbelievers drove him out ňĻof Meccaňļ and he was only one of two. While they both were in the cave, he reassured his companion,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†‚ÄúDo not worry; Allah is certainly with us.‚ÄĚ So Allah sent down His serenity upon the Prophet, supported him with forces you ňĻbelieversňļ did not see, and made the word of the disbelievers lowest, while the Word of Allah is supreme. And Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†Abu Bakr AŠĻ£-ŠĻĘidd√ģq, Islam‚Äôs first Caliph and a prominent figure in Islamic history. He accompanied the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) during his emigration from Mecca to Medina after years of persecution at the hands of the Meccan pagans.

9:41.text: ňĻO believers!ňļ March forth whether it is easy or difficult for you, and strive with your wealth and your lives in the cause of Allah. That is best for you, if only you knew.

9:42.text: Had the gain been within reach and the journey shorter, they would have followed you, but the distance seemed too long for them. And they will swear by Allah, ‚ÄúHad we been able, we would have certainly joined you.‚ÄĚ They are ruining themselves. And Allah knows that they are surely lying.

9:43.text: May Allah pardon you ňĻO Prophetňļ! Why did you give them permission ňĻto stay behindňļ before those who told the truth were distinguished from those who were lying?

9:44.text: Those who believe in Allah and the Last Day do not ask for exemption from striving with their wealth and their lives. And Allah has perfect knowledge of those who are mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

9:45.text: No one would ask for exemption except those who have no faith in Allah or the Last Day, and whose hearts are in doubt, so they are torn by their doubts.

9:46.text: Had they ňĻreallyňļ intended to march forth, they would have made preparations for it. But Allah disliked that they should go, so He let them lag behind, and it was said ňĻto themňļ, ‚ÄúStay with those ňĻhelplessňļ who remain behind.‚ÄĚ

9:47.text: Had they gone forth with you ňĻbelieversňļ, they would have been nothing but trouble for you, and would have scrambled around, seeking to spread discord in your midst. And some of you would have eagerly listened to them. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of the wrongdoers.

9:48.text: They had already sought to spread discord before<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and devised every ňĻpossibleňļ plot against you ňĻO Prophetňļ, until the truth came and Allah‚Äôs Will prevailed‚ÄĒmuch to their dismay.
footnotes: ¬†For example, ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh ibn Ubai ibn Sal√Ľl, a chief hypocrite, marched with the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) for the Battle of UŠł•ud in the outskirts of Medina, then decided not to join the fight and returned to Medina with his followers who made up around one third of the Muslim army.

9:49.text: There are some of them who say, ‚ÄúExempt me and do not expose me to temptation.‚ÄĚ<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They have already fallen into temptation. And Hell will surely engulf the disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†Another hypocrite by the name of Jadd ibn Qais came to the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) asking to be exempt from joining the army because he had a weakness for women and he was afraid that he would be tempted by Roman women.

9:50.text: If a blessing befalls you ňĻO Prophetňļ, they grieve, but if a disaster befalls you, they say, ‚ÄúWe took our precaution in advance,‚ÄĚ and turn away, rejoicing.

9:51.text: Say, ‚ÄúNothing will ever befall us except what Allah has destined for us. He is our Protector.‚ÄĚ So in Allah let the believers put their trust.

9:52.text: Say, ‚ÄúAre you awaiting anything to befall us except one of the two best things: ňĻvictory or martyrdomňļ? But¬†We are awaiting Allah to afflict you with torment either from Him or at our hands. So keep waiting! We too are waiting with you.‚ÄĚ

9:53.text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻWhether youňļ donate willingly or unwillingly, it will never be accepted from you, for you have been a rebellious people.‚ÄĚ

9:54.text: And what prevented their donations from being accepted is that they have lost faith in Allah and His Messenger, they never come to prayer except half-heartedly, and they never donate except resentfully.

9:55.text: So let neither their wealth nor children impress you ňĻO Prophetňļ. Allah only intends to torment them through these things in this worldly life, then their souls will depart while they are disbelievers.

9:56.text: They swear by Allah that they are part of you, but they are not. They only say so out of fear.

9:57.text: If only they could find a refuge, or a cave, or any hiding-place, they would rush headlong towards it.

9:58.text: There are some of them who are critical of your distribution of alms ňĻO Prophetňļ. If they are given some of it they are pleased, but if not they are enraged.

9:59.text: If only they had been content with what Allah and His Messenger had given them and said, ‚ÄúAllah is sufficient for us! Allah will grant us out of His bounty, and so will His Messenger. To Allah ňĻaloneňļ we turn with hope.‚ÄĚ

9:60.text: Alms-tax is only for the poor and the needy, for those employed to administer it, for those whose hearts are attracted ňĻto the faithňļ, for ňĻfreeingňļ slaves, for those in debt, for Allah‚Äôs cause, and for ňĻneedyňļ travellers. ňĻThis isňļ an obligation from Allah. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:61.text: And there are others who hurt the Prophet by saying, ‚ÄúHe listens to anyone.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHe listens to what is best for you. He believes in Allah, has faith in the believers, and is a mercy for those who believe among you.‚ÄĚ Those who hurt Allah‚Äôs Messenger will suffer a painful punishment.

9:62.text: They swear by Allah to you ňĻbelieversňļ in order to please you, while it is the pleasure of Allah and His Messenger they should seek, if they are ňĻtrueňļ believers.

9:63.text: Do they not know that whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger will be in the Fire of Hell forever? That is the ultimate disgrace.

9:64.text: The hypocrites fear that a s√Ľrah¬†should be revealed about them, exposing what is in their hearts. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúKeep mocking! Allah will definitely bring to light what you fear.‚ÄĚ

9:65.text: If you question them, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúWe were only talking idly and joking around.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúWas it Allah, His revelations, and His Messenger that you ridiculed?‚ÄĚ

9:66.text: Make no excuses! You have lost faith after your belief. If We pardon a group of you,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†We will punish others for their wickedness.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., those who will repent.

9:67.text: The hypocrites, both men and women, are all alike: they encourage what is evil, forbid what is good, and withhold ňĻwhat is inňļ their hands. They neglected Allah, so He neglected them. Surely the hypocrites are the rebellious.

9:68.text: Allah has promised the hypocrites, both men and women, and the disbelievers an everlasting stay in the Fire of Hell‚ÄĒit is sufficient for them. Allah has condemned them, and they will suffer a never-ending punishment.

9:69.text: ňĻYou hypocrites areňļ like those ňĻdisbelieversňļ before you. They were far superior to you in might and more abundant in wealth and children. They enjoyed their share in this life. You have enjoyed your share, just as they did. And you have engaged in idle talk, just as they did. Their deeds have become void in this world and the Hereafter. And it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ losers.

9:70.text: Have they not received the stories of those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them: the people of Noah, ‚Äô√ād, and Tham√Ľd, the people of Abraham, the residents of Midian, and the overturned cities ňĻof Lotňļ?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Their messengers came to them with clear proofs. Allah would have never wronged them, but it was they who wronged themselves.
footnotes: ¬†The overturned cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.

9:71.text: The believers, both men and women, are guardians of one another. They encourage good and forbid evil, establish prayer and pay alms-tax, and obey Allah and His Messenger. It is they who will be shown Allah‚Äôs mercy. Surely Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

9:72.text: Allah has promised the believers, both men and women, Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever, and splendid homes in the Gardens of Eternity, and‚ÄĒabove all‚ÄĒthe pleasure of Allah. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.

9:73.text: O Prophet! Struggle against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be firm with them. Hell will be their home. What an evil destination!

9:74.text: They swear by Allah that they never said anything ňĻblasphemousňļ, while they did in fact utter a blasphemy, lost faith after accepting Islam, and plotted what they could not carry out.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It is only through resentment that they pay Allah and His Messenger back for enriching them out of His bounty! If they repent, it will be better for them. But if they turn away, Allah will torment them with a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter, and they will have no one on earth to protect or help them.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., some of them tried to assassinate the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) on the way back from Tab√Ľk.

9:75.text: And there are some who had made a vow to Allah: ‚ÄúIf He gives us from His bounty, we will surely spend in charity and be of the righteous.‚ÄĚ

9:76.text: But when He gave them out of His bounty, they withheld it and turned away indifferently.

9:77.text: So He caused hypocrisy to plague their hearts until the Day they will meet Him, for breaking their promise to Allah and for their lies.

9:78.text: Do they not know that Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows their ňĻevilňļ thoughts and secret talks, and that Allah is the Knower of all unseen?

9:79.text: ňĻThere areňļ those who slander ňĻsome ofňļ the believers for donating liberally and mock others for giving only the little they can afford. Allah will throw their mockery back at them, and they will suffer a painful punishment.

9:80.text: ňĻIt does not matterňļ whether you ňĻO Prophetňļ pray for them to be forgiven or not. Even if you pray for their forgiveness seventy times, Allah will never forgive them. That is because they have lost faith in Allah and His Messenger. And Allah does not guide the rebellious people.

9:81.text: Those ňĻhypocritesňļ who remained behind rejoiced for doing so in defiance of the Messenger of Allah and hated ňĻthe prospect ofňļ striving with their wealth and their lives in the cause of Allah. They said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúDo not march forth in the heat.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúThe Fire of Hell is far hotter!‚ÄĚ If only they could comprehend!

9:82.text: So let them laugh a little‚ÄĒthey will weep much as a reward for what they have committed.

9:83.text: If Allah returns you ňĻO Prophetňļ to a group of them and they ask to go forth with you, say, ‚ÄúYou will not ever go forth or fight an enemy along with me. You preferred to stay behind the first time, so stay with those ňĻhelplessňļ who remain behind.‚ÄĚ

9:84.text: And do not ever offer ňĻfuneralňļ prayers for any of their dead, nor stand by their grave ňĻat burialňļ, for they have lost faith in Allah and His Messenger and died rebellious.

9:85.text: And let neither their wealth nor children impress you ňĻO Prophetňļ. Allah only intends to torment them through these things in this world, and ňĻthenňļ their souls will depart while they are disbelievers.

9:86.text: Whenever a s√Ľrah¬†is revealed stating, ‚ÄúBelieve in Allah and struggle along with His Messenger,‚ÄĚ the rich among them would ask to be exempt, saying, ‚ÄúLeave us with those who remain behind.‚ÄĚ

9:87.text: They preferred to stay behind with the helpless, and their hearts have been sealed so they do not comprehend.

9:88.text: But the Messenger and the believers with him strived with their wealth and their lives. They will have all the best, and it is they who will be successful.

9:89.text: Allah has prepared for them Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there forever. That is the ultimate triumph.

9:90.text: Some nomadic Arabs ňĻalsoňļ came with excuses, seeking exemption. And those who were untrue to Allah and His Messenger remained behind ňĻwith no excuseňļ. The unfaithful among them will be afflicted with a painful punishment.

9:91.text: There is no blame on the weak, the sick, or those lacking the means ňĻif they stay behindňļ, as long as they are true to Allah and His Messenger. There is no blame on the good-doers. And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

9:92.text: Nor ňĻis there any blame onňļ those who came to you ňĻO Prophetňļ for mounts, then when you said, ‚ÄúI can find no mounts for you,‚ÄĚ they left with eyes overflowing with tears out of grief that they had nothing to contribute.

9:93.text: Blame is only on those who seek exemption from you although they have the means. They preferred to stay behind with the helpless, and Allah has sealed their hearts so they do not realize ňĻthe consequencesňļ.

9:94.text: They will make excuses to you ňĻbelieversňļ when you return to them. Say, ‚ÄúMake no excuses, ňĻforňļ we will not believe you. Allah has already informed us about your ňĻtrueňļ state ňĻof faithňļ. Your ňĻfutureňļ deeds will be observed by Allah and His Messenger as well. And you will be returned to the Knower of the seen and unseen, then He will inform you of what you used to do.‚ÄĚ

9:95.text: When you return, they will swear to you by Allah so that you may leave them alone. So leave them alone‚ÄĒthey are truly evil. Hell will be their home as a reward for what they have committed.

9:96.text: They will swear to you in order to please you. And even if you are pleased with them, Allah will never be pleased with the rebellious people.

9:97.text: The nomadic Arabs ňĻaround Medinaňļ are far worse in disbelief and hypocrisy, and less likely to know the laws revealed by Allah to His Messenger. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:98.text: And among the nomads are those who consider what they donate to be a loss and await your misfortune. May ill-fortune befall them! And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

9:99.text: However, among the nomadic Arabs are those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, and consider what they donate as a means of coming closer to Allah and ňĻreceivingňļ the prayers of the Messenger. It will certainly bring them closer. Allah will admit them into His mercy. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

9:100.text: As for the foremost‚ÄĒthe first of the Emigrants<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†and the Helpers<sup foot_note=2>2</sup>‚ÄĒand those who follow them in goodness, Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. And He has prepared for them Gardens under which rivers flow, to stay there for ever and ever. That is the ultimate triumph.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., the early Muslims who emigrated from Mecca to Medina, known as Al-Muh√Ęjir√Ľn.
¬†i.e., those from Medina, known as Al-AnŠĻ£√Ęr, who accepted Islam and sheltered Muslim emigrants from Mecca.

9:101.text: Some of the nomads around you ňĻbelieversňļ are hypocrites, as are some of the people of Medina. They have mastered hypocrisy. They are not known to you ňĻO Prophetňļ; they are known to Us. We will punish them twice ňĻin this worldňļ,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†then they will be brought back ňĻto their Lordňļ for a tremendous punishment.
footnotes: ¬†Through disgrace in this world and bad ending of their lives.

9:102.text: Some others have confessed their wrongdoing: they have mixed goodness with evil.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†It is right to hope that Allah will turn to them in mercy. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
footnotes: ¬†They have mixed their adherence to Islam with their disobedience to march forth with the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) to Tab√Ľk.

9:103.text: Take from their wealth ňĻO Prophetňļ charity to purify and bless them, and pray for them‚ÄĒsurely your prayer is a source of comfort for them. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

9:104.text: Do they not know that Allah alone accepts the repentance of His servants and receives ňĻtheirňļ charity, and that Allah alone is the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful?

9:105.text: Tell ňĻthem, O ¬†Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúDo as you will. Your deeds will be observed by Allah, His Messenger, and the believers. And you will be returned to the Knower of the seen and unseen, then He will inform you of what you used to do.‚ÄĚ

9:106.text: And some others are left for Allah‚Äôs decision, either to punish them or turn to them in mercy.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
footnotes: ¬†This verse refers to Ka‚Äôb ibn M√Ęlik, Murarah ibn Rabi‚Äô, and Hil√Ęl ibn Umaiyah, the three companions who remained in Medina with no excuse, but were honest about what they did. They were boycotted by Muslims for about fifty days until verses 118-119 of this chapter were revealed declaring the acceptance of their repentance.

9:107.text: There are also those ňĻhypocritesňļ who set up a mosque ňĻonlyňļ to cause harm, promote disbelief, divide the believers, and as a base for those who had previously fought against Allah and His Messenger.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†They will definitely swear, ‚ÄúWe intended nothing but good,‚ÄĚ but Allah bears witness that they are surely liars.
footnotes: ¬†Abu ‚Äô√āmer Ar-R√Ęhib was a monk who fought against Muslims at the Battle of Badr. He ordered a group of twelve hypocrites to build a mosque near the Mosque of Qub√Ę', the first mosque built by Muslims and also where the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and his companions would pray. The new mosque, commonly referred to as Masjid AŠłć-ŠłĆir√Ęr¬†(Mosque of Harm), was intended to attract other hypocrites and reduce the number of Muslims who prayed at Qub√Ę'. The hypocrites who built the mosque also anticipated the arrival of Abu ‚Äô√āmer with Roman forces to expel the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and Muslims out of Medina. Upon a request from these hypocrites, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) had planned to visit the new mosque once he returned from Tab√Ľk, but verses 107-110 of this s√Ľrah¬†were revealed, warning the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) against that mosque. According to some narrations, the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) ordered this mosque to be burned down.

9:108.text: Do not ňĻO Prophetňļ ever pray in it. Certainly, a mosque founded on righteousness from the first day is more worthy of your prayers. In it are men who love to be purified.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†And Allah loves those who purify themselves.
footnotes: ¬†See¬†footnote for 7:46.

9:109.text: Which is better: those who laid the foundation of their building on the fear and pleasure of Allah, or those who did so on the edge of a crumbling cliff that tumbled down with them into the Fire of Hell? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

9:110.text: The building which they erected will never cease to fuel hypocrisy in their hearts until their hearts are torn apart. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:111.text: Allah has indeed purchased from the believers their lives and wealth in exchange for Paradise. They fight in the cause of Allah and kill or are killed. This is a true promise binding on Him in the Torah, the Gospel, and the Quran. And whose promise is truer than Allah‚Äôs? So rejoice in the exchange you have made with Him. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.

9:112.text: ňĻIt is the believersňļ who repent, who are devoted to worship, who praise ňĻtheir Lordňļ, who fast, who bow down and prostrate themselves, who encourage good and forbid evil, and who observe the limits set by Allah. And give good news to the believers.

9:113.text: It is not ňĻproperňļ for the Prophet and the believers to seek forgiveness for the polytheists, even if they were close relatives, after it has become clear to the believers that they are bound for the Hellfire.

9:114.text: As for Abraham‚Äôs prayer for his father‚Äôs forgiveness, it was only in fulfilment of a promise he had made to him. But when it became clear to Abraham that his father was an enemy of Allah, he broke ties with him.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Abraham was truly tender-hearted, forbearing.
footnotes: ¬†Abraham promised to pray for his father‚Äôs forgiveness in 19:47 and 60:4, but when his father died as a disbeliever he discontinued praying for him.

9:115.text: Allah would never consider a people deviant after He has guided them, until He makes clear to them what they must avoid. Surely Allah has ňĻfullňļ knowledge of everything.

9:116.text: Indeed, to Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And besides Allah you have no guardian or helper.

9:117.text: Allah has certainly turned in mercy to the Prophet<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†as well as the Emigrants and the Helpers who stood by him in the time of hardship, after the hearts of a group of them had almost faltered. He then accepted their repentance. Surely He is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to them.
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet (Ô∑ļ) is forgiven for exempting all those who came to him with excuses regardless of their honesty, or for seeking forgiveness for the polytheists.

9:118.text: And ňĻAllah has also turned in mercy toňļ the three who had remained behind, ňĻwhose guilt distressed themňļ until the earth, despite its vastness, seemed to close in on them, and their souls were torn in anguish. They knew there was no refuge from Allah except in Him. Then He turned to them in mercy so that they might repent. Surely Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Accepter of Repentance, Most Merciful.

9:119.text: O ¬†believers! Be mindful of Allah and be with the truthful.

9:120.text: It was not ňĻproperňļ for the people of Medina and the nomadic Arabs around them to avoid marching with the Messenger of Allah or to prefer their own lives above his. That is because whenever they suffer from thirst, fatigue, or hunger in the cause of Allah; or tread on a territory, unnerving the disbelievers; or inflict any loss on an enemy‚ÄĒit is written to their credit as a good deed. Surely Allah never discounts the reward of the good-doers.

9:121.text: And whenever they make a donation, small or large, or cross a valley ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ‚ÄĒit is written to their credit, so that Allah may grant them the best reward for what they used to do.

9:122.text: ňĻHowever,ňļ it is not necessary for the believers to march forth all at once. Only a party from each group should march forth, leaving the rest to gain religious knowledge then enlighten their people when they return to them, so that they ňĻtooňļ may beware ňĻof evilňļ.

9:123.text: O believers! Fight the disbelievers around you and let them find firmness in you. And know that Allah is with those mindful ňĻof Himňļ.

9:124.text: Whenever a s√Ľrah¬†is revealed, some of them ask ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhich of you has this increased in faith?‚ÄĚ As for the believers, it has increased them in faith and they rejoice.

9:125.text: But as for those with sickness in their hearts,<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†it has increased them only in wickedness upon their wickedness, and they die as disbelievers.
footnotes: ¬†i.e., hypocrites.

9:126.text: Do they not see that they are tried once or twice every year?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>¬†Yet they neither repent nor do they learn a lesson.
footnotes: ¬†Their hypocrisy is exposed over and over again.

9:127.text: Whenever a s√Ľrah¬†is revealed, they look at one another, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúIs anyone watching you?‚ÄĚ Then they slip away. ňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ has turned their hearts away because they are a people who do not comprehend.

9:128.text: There certainly has come to you a messenger from among yourselves. He is concerned by your suffering, anxious for your well-being, and gracious and merciful to the believers.

9:129.text: But if they turn away, then say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAllah is sufficient for me. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. In Him I put my trust. And He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.‚ÄĚ

90:20.text: The Fire will be sealed over them.

92:6.text: and ňĻfirmlyňļ believes in the finest reward,

93:2.text: and the night when it falls still!

94:1.text: Have We not uplifted your heart for you ňĻO Prophetňļ,

94:3.text: which weighed so heavily on your back,

94:4.text: and elevated your renown for you?<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†The Prophet‚Äôs name is always mentioned in the call to prayer, along with Allah‚Äôs Name. Close to the end of each of the five daily prayers, worshippers invoke Allah‚Äôs blessings upon the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and his family. When someone reverts to Islam, they testify that Allah is their Lord and MuŠł•ammad is His Messenger. Muslims, even those who are not practicing, love the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) and many name their children after him.

94:5.text: So, surely with hardship comes ease.

94:6.text: Surely with ňĻthatňļ hardship comes ňĻmoreňļ ease.<sup foot_note=1>1</sup>
footnotes: ¬†If a definite noun is repeated in Arabic, it means that we are referring to the same thing. For example, ‚ÄúI met the man and I gave the man some money.‚ÄĚ But if an indefinite noun is repeated, it means we are referring to two different things. For example, ‚ÄúI met a man and I gave a man some money.‚ÄĚ With this in mind, we realize that 94:5-6 refers to one hardship (a definite noun) and two different eases (an indefinite noun). Hence, the Arabic saying: ‚ÄúTwo eases cannot be overcome by one hardship.‚ÄĚ

94:8.text: turning to your Lord ňĻaloneňļ with hope.

95:1.text: By the fig and the olive ňĻof Jerusalemňļ,

footnotes: ¬†This passage refers to the land of figs and olives where Jesus (Ô∑ļ) lived, Mount Sinai where Moses (Ô∑ļ) communicated with Allah, and the city of Mecca where the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) started his mission. A similar reference can be found in Deuteronomy 33:1, ňĻMoses proclaimed:ňļ ‚ÄúThe Lord came from Sinai. Rising from Seir upon us, he [sic] shone forth from Mount Paran.‚ÄĚ Seir is a mountain near Jerusalem, and Paran is a mountain near Mecca.

Since the Prophet (Ô∑ļ) was unlettered, he responded, ‚ÄúI cannot read.‚ÄĚ Ultimately, Gabriel taught him: ‚ÄúRead in the Name of your Lord ‚Ä¶‚ÄĚ Some scholars believe that this encounter is the fulfilment of Isaiah 29:12, which states, ‚ÄúThen the book will be given to the one who is illiterate, saying, ‚ÄėRead this.‚Äô And he will say, ‚ÄėI cannot read.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
